text: kimaan daanɔ nba kur tuun toonbiit, u nân yentu maŋe, ki ki loon wuu nakiiɔ, kimaan u ki loon nan u toonbiit na-n tan nyi paanu.

ref: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
text: Ŋaan wunba waa Yennu barmɔnii wannu ki bia tuun li biaŋ, ŋɔɔe baat yentu maŋ boor, a sɔɔ kur-n bann nan u toona kur, Yennu be li ni. Jɔɔnn nba want Yisa po biaŋinba

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
text: Li poorpoe ki Yisa nan u poorpoweiteeb din nyii leŋ ki saan Judea siar po, ki saa be leŋ kaman nan daa ŋaŋŋa nna; ki bi din wuur niib Yennu nyunwuru.

ref: John had not yet been put in jail. He was at Aenon near Salim, where there was a lot of water, and people were coming there for John to baptize them.
text: Li yoo na ki sɔɔ ki Jɔɔnn wunba wuur niib Yennu nyunwuru na daa ki kɔɔ dansariki, ki be Enon, siaminba ki nyun din yab na, ki niib din tuu baat u boor, ki u wuurib nyun. Enon din ki fɔk nan Salimi.

ref: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
text: Ŋanne ki mɔniɔk tan fiir Jɔɔnn maŋ poorpoweiteeb nan Juu nirɔ nna sinsuuk ni ki jikit saa Yennu nyunwuru po.

ref: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
text: Ki Jɔɔnn poorpoweiteeb na saan u boor, ki saa betɔ “Chanbaa, nirɔ nba ki a daan be nanɔ na Jɔɔdann mɔk-pootir, ki yaa a u tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ na, ti gbat ki u wuur niib Yennu nyunwuru, ki sɔɔ kur saa u boor ki u wuurib.”

ref: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
text: Ki Jɔɔnn maŋ betib a “Yennu nyint sɔɔ kur nan u toona. Nirɔ kan fit tun siar ki li ki tee Yennu nyinnɔ.

ref: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
text: I mɔŋ poŋ mi gbat ki n yet ma min kaa tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia, ŋaan min ŋarin, Yennu dɔŋ tun mine, a n tan teen u baaru siir.

ref: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
text: Poosɔrɔe tee wunba yen pooyookir na. Wunba tee poosɔrɔ na yɔɔk ki be u boor ki gbiintirɔ, ŋɔɔe mɔk parpeenn poosɔrɔ na kunkɔr paak. Nnee ki n mun set mɔk parpeenn bonchiann Masia na paak.

ref: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
text: “Li kpaa talas nan ŋɔɔ Masia-ii yabitir, ŋaan ki min-ii waabit.” Wunba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni labaar

ref: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
text: Wunba nyii sanpaapo, ŋɔɔe chee sɔɔ kura; ŋaan binba nyii tingbouŋ na ni, ŋamm tee tingbouŋ na teebe, ki bia piak nisaarii maan. Wunba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni, ŋɔɔe chee sɔɔ kur,

ref: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
text: ki bia jenna ki beer niib linba ki u la ki bia gbat, ŋaan sɔɔ kur kaa gaar u maami.

ref: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
text: Ki bia jenna, ki wunba gaar u maan maŋ, li want nan ŋɔɔe saak nan Yennu maan maŋ tee barmɔnii.

ref: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
text: Yennu Bija nba ki u tumɔ na, wa piak maan nba na, li tee Yennu maame, kimaan Yennu tur u Bija maŋ Seyeeŋ nba yab ki kaa bikinu.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
text: Yennu loon u Bija, ki kubinɔ bona kur u nuu ni.

ref: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
text: Daanɔ nba tee Yennu Bija yada, u sii mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu, ki daanɔ nba mun ki saak Yennu Bija na mɔb kan la manfoor maŋi, ŋaan Yennu wutoor sa do u paak ki kan gbenni.

ref: But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself. Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
text: Barmɔnii ŋarime Yisa din ki wuur niib u mɔŋi, ŋaan u poorpoweiteebe din wuurib.

ref: This time he had to go through Samaria,
text: Ki u sɔnu maŋ ni, ki li tee talase nan wun gar Samaria tiŋ ni.

ref: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
text: Ki Juu teeb na din tuu siŋ Samaria niibe, ki ki loon bii taant nammi. Li paak, ki poo na ji boiɔ a “A tee Juu nirɔe, ki n mun tee Samaria pooe, ki li teen nleewa ki a tan mein nyumi?”

ref: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
text: Ki poo na yetɔ a “Chanbaa, bunbunn na sumii hei, ki a mun bia ki mɔk gooka. Lee ki a sa luun manfoor nyun maŋi?

ref: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
text: Ti yeja Jakɔb din lukir bunbunn na, ki ŋɔɔ nan u waas nan u bonkobit kura din nyu leŋe. A dukii nan a gar Jakɔb amii?”

ref: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
text: ŋaan mi-i tur wunba n nyun na ki u nyuu, nyunyukiru kan mi ki soorɔ jiki, kimaan mi-i tur sɔɔ n nyun na, li sii bunii manfoor nyume u ni, ki bia sa turɔ manfoor nba kaa gbennu.”

ref: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
text: Ki poo na yetɔ a “Chanbaa, turimin li nyun, ki nyunyukiru ji daa mi ki somi, ki n bia ji daa mi ki baat nna ki tan lu nyumi.”

ref: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
text: Ki Yisa betɔ a “I saa ki saa yiin a sɔrɔ ki jen nanɔ na.”

ref: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
text: kimaan a poŋ kun jab baŋŋmuua, ki jɔɔ nba ki a daa be nanɔ mɔtana na, u ki tee a sɔrɔ nan mɔnii kaa. A set pak mɔniie.”

ref: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
text: Ki poo na yetɔ a “Chanbaa, n ji bann nan a tee Yennu sɔkiniie.

ref: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
text: Timm Samaria teeb yejamm din jiantir Yennu jɔɔr na paake, ŋaan ki yimm Juu teeb ŋarin yaa a Jerusaleme tee siaminba ŋan nan Yenjiantu.”

ref: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
text: Ki Yisa betɔ, “N diamm, teentin yada, yoo baat ki niib tan kii jiantir ti Baa Yennu jɔɔr na paaki, ki bia tan kii jiantirɔ Jerusalem mun biaki.

ref: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
text: Yimm Samaria teeb ki mi ya jiantir wunba, ŋaan timm Juu teeb ŋarin mi ta jiantir wunba, kimaan niib tinnu nyii Juu teeb nie.

ref: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
text: Ŋaan yoo baat, ki li poŋ set baar na, ki binba jiantir ti Baa Yennu nan barmɔnii na sii jiantirɔ u Seyeeŋ paŋ paak nan bi para kur, kimaan li niboorue ki Yennu loon bii jiantirɔ nna.

ref: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
text: Yennu tee Seeke, ki binba jiantirɔ na, li tee talase bii jiantirɔ nan barmɔnii, u Seyeeŋ paŋ paak.”

ref: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
text: Ki poo na yetɔ, “N mi nan Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia nba ki bi yiu Kristo na tan sa baar na. U-i taŋŋi baar yoonba, u sa betit barii kura.”

ref: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
text: Ki Yisa jiinɔ a “Mine na, min nba piak nana na.”

ref: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
text: Li yoo maŋe ki Yisa poorpoweiteeb jen, ki li teemm bakitnauŋ nan Yisa nba piak nan pooe na, ŋaan bi sɔɔ ki boi poo na a u loon bee, ki bia ki boi Yisa a bee ki u piak nanɔ.

ref: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
text: Ŋanne ki poo na nyik u nyunsɔɔr na, ŋaan ŋmat saan doo na ni, ki saa yiin niib a

ref: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
text: “I baar man, ki tan got jɔɔ nba betin bonsiar kur nba ki n poŋ mi tun. I dukii nan ŋɔɔe tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia-aa?”

ref: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
text: Ba gbat nna yoonba, ki bi nyii doo na ni ki saan Yisa peŋ.

ref: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
text: Yoo nba ki poo na daa din piak nan niib doo na ni na, ki Yisa poorpoweiteeb barimɔ, ki yaa a “Chanbaa, dimin jeet.”

ref: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
text: Ki Yisa jiimm yaa a “Min mɔk jeet nba ki n sa di, ŋaan ki i ki mi li po.”

ref: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
text: Ki u poorpoweiteeb ji boi bi leeb a “I mi sɔɔ tan sii turɔ jeeti?”

ref: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
text: Ki Yisa jiimm, “Min yaa jeet tee ki mii tuun Yennu nba tumin na yanbɔɔtoona ki gbenn. Dii jaŋ nan jaamuwa

ref: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
text: “I dukii nan li tenn ŋmaarii ŋannae nan bin jaan dii. Gotir man, niib bonchiann poŋ teen siir nan ki i dakib ki bin teenin yada, kimaan bi tee kaman nan dii nba tuu beer ki jaŋ nan jaamu nee.

ref: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
text: Ki dijaara tan sa gaar bi paauk, ki dii nba ki bi jaan na mun, ŋamme tee niib nba tan sa la manfoor nba kaa gbennu, ki damm nba buur nan binba jaan, bi kur tan sii mɔk parpeenn bi dii maŋ paak.

ref: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
text: Ti mɔk barjouk, ki li yiin a ‘Yenɔe buur, lɔɔe mun tuu tan jaan,’ ki li set tee mɔnii.

ref: I am sending you to harvest crops in fields where others have done all the hard work.
text: N te ki i jaan siaminba ki i ki koo. Siabe poŋ koowa, ki bi konu maŋ sommii ki i laat dii.”

ref: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
text: Ŋanne ki Samaria doo maŋ ni niib bonchiann gbat poo na nba beerib yaa a “Yisa betin bonsiar kur nba ki n poŋ mi ki tun,” ki bi teen Yisa yada,

ref: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
text: ŋaan saan bunbunn na boor ni Yisa peŋ, ki barimɔ a wun biar kii be namm. Ki Yisa sak ki biar be namm daa ŋannlee,

ref: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
text: ki din piak u maan ki teemm. Ba din gbat nna ki bi bonchiann teen yada ki jii bi mɔŋ ki turɔ.

ref: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
text: Li yoo na ki bi bet poo na “Ta teenɔ yada mɔtana na, li ki tee fa betit u po na kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan ta gbat u mɔŋ mɔŋ maan na paake ki ti teenɔ yada, ki bann nan ŋɔɔe set tee tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb Tinntɔɔ.” Yisa nba din te ki yudaansɔɔ bija paak biaŋinba (Matiu

ref: Jesus had said, “Prophets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.” Then two days later he left
text: kimaan u mɔŋ mɔŋ poŋ yet a “Yennu sɔkinii kan la nibaakir u mɔŋ mɔŋ doo ni.”

ref: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
text: Ŋaan wa din baar Galilee yoonba, niib na din gaarɔ, kimaan ba daan saan yukitgar tiaru jaamm Jerusalem na, bi daan la linba kur ki u poŋ tun Jerusalem ni jaamm yoo na.

ref: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
text: Wa din lin Galilee tiŋ ni na, ki u din saan Kana, siaminba ki u din te ki nyun kpant tilɔɔna daan na. Ki yudaansɔɔ din be Kapenaum, ki u bija din yiar.

ref: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
text: Ki u din gbat a Yisa nyii Judea ki baar Galilee tiŋ ni. Ki u baar u peŋ, ki barimɔ a wun baar Kapenaum, a ki tan te ki u bija-n paak, kimaan u yaa wun kpoe.

ref: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
text: Ki Yisa betɔ a “Li-i tee ki i ki la nyina nan bakitnauŋ toonjaanai kaa, i kan teenin yada.”

ref: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
text: Ki yudaanɔ na jiin yetɔ a “Chanbaa, ŋaant tin saan, ki n bik daa ki kpo na.”

ref: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
text: Ki Yisa betɔ “I kun. A bija na paaka.” Ŋanne ki yudaanɔ na teen yada nan Yisa nba pakɔ linba maŋ, ki ji ŋmat kun.

ref: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
text: U sɔnu nie ki u toontunna chetɔ nan labaar a “A bik na paaka.”

ref: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
text: Ki u boib a “Nlee yooe ki bik maŋ paaki?” Ki bi yetɔ a “Wonn, kut youŋ yenlekir, ki bik na gbanu won maak.”

ref: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
text: Ki jɔɔ na tiar nan li yooe tee yoo nba ki Yisa won betɔ a “A bija na paaka na.” Li paak ki ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb kura teen Yisa yada.

ref: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
text: Yisa nba din tun toonjaann maŋ na, li din tee toonjaann nba pukin ŋannlee ki u tun yoo nba ki u nyii Judea tiŋ ni ki baar Galilee tiŋ ni nee.

ref: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
text: Li poorpoe ki Juu teeb jaansiar tan baar, ki Yisa saan Jerusalem.

ref: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
text: Leŋe ki kukintuk nan nyun din be, ki bakit ŋaŋŋmu din chaan linta, ki Juu teeb maan ni, bi din yir Betsata, ki li kpia nan Pei Tammɔb.

ref: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
text: Tɔn, ki jasɔɔ din be li boor, ki din yiar bina piinntaa nan ŋanniin.

ref: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
text: Ki u tumin i boor, ŋaan i ki teenin yada; ŋannii want nan u maan kaa i para ni.

ref: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
text: Ki Yisa tan baar la ki u dɔɔ li boor, ki bann nan jɔɔ maŋ yiaru yukira, ki u boiɔ, “A loon ki a la laafia-aa?”

ref: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
text: Ki baatɔɔ na jiin a “Chanbaa, nyun na-i damm yoonba, n ki mɔk sɔɔ nba sa jiin sikinin li ni. Ki mi-i tuu yaa n sik, sɔɔ tuu dɔŋ ki sike, ŋaan nyikin.”

ref: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
text: Ki Yisa betɔ, “Fiit, ki jii a dɔɔnu kii somm.”

ref: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
text: Li taakpaak ni ki jɔɔ na paak, ki fiir ki jii u dɔɔnu ki somm saa. Daar nba ki jɔɔ na din la laafia na din tee foon daare.

ref: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
text: Li paake teen ki Juu teeb yudamm din betɔ a “Dinna tee foon daare. Ti sennu ki chab nan fan jii a dɔɔnu kii somm nanni.”

ref: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
text: Ki u jiin a “Jɔɔ nba te ki n paak na, ŋɔɔe yetin a n jii n dɔɔnu maŋ kii saa nann.”

ref: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
text: Ki bi boiɔ, “Jalanɔpoe yeta a a jii a dɔɔnu maŋ kii saa nanni?”

ref: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
text: Ŋaan jɔɔ na ji din ki mi wunba din te ki u paak jikii, kimaan Yisa ji din poŋ nyii leŋa, niib bonchiann nba din tikir leŋ na paak.

ref: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
text: Li poorpoe ki Yisa ji lon jɔɔ na, ki tan lau Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni, ki betɔ a “Gotir, a ji paaka, ŋaan a ji daa mi ki tuun toonbiiti, ki siara bonbisiar tan sa kpet baara ki gar na.”

ref: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
text: Yisa nba pak nan jɔɔ na, ki jɔɔ na nyii ki saan Juu teeb yudamm na boor ki saa betib a “Li tee Yisae te ki n paak.”

ref: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
text: Li paak te ki yudamm maŋ ji piin Yisa muksuk, kimaan wa te ki jɔɔ na paak foon daar na paak.

ref: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
text: Li paake te ki Yisa din betib a “N Baa Yennu yɔɔ tuume, ki n mun tuun nnee.”

ref: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
text: Yisa nba yet nna yoonba, ki Juu teeb maŋ ŋamm donn wutoor ki loon bin kpiu ki gar sinsinna. Bi dukin nan li ki tee wa biir foon daar sennu na paak kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan wa bia yaa a Yennu tee u Baa na paak, ki ŋanne te ki u yek nan u mɔŋ nan Yennu. Yisa nba mɔk yikoo biaŋinba

ref: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
text: Ŋanne ki Yisa betib a “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, min Yennu Bija laat n Baa Yennu nba tuun linbae, ki n mun tuun. N kan fit tun siar nan n mɔŋi. N Baa maŋ nba tuun linba, n mun tuun ŋanne,

ref: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
text: kimaan n Baa maŋ loonin, ma tee u Bija na, ki u wantin linba kur ki u mɔŋ mɔŋ tuun. Ki bia jenna, u bia sa wannin toonjaana mii tuun, ki lin gar biaŋinba ki n te ki jɔɔ na paak gbaa, ki li sa teeni bakitnauŋ.

ref: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
text: N Baa nba fiint kpeemm biaŋinba ki teemm manfoa na, nnee mun, ki min nba tee u Bija na mun sa tur binba ki n gann na manfoa.

ref: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
text: “N Baa ki bu sɔɔ buuti, ŋaan u chab min, u Bija, a mii bu niib buut,

ref: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
text: a ki sɔɔ kur-ii tee-n nibaakir kaman nan ba teen n Baa Yennu nibaakir biaŋinba na. Wunba ki tee-n nibaakir, li want nan u ki teen n Baa Yennu nba tumin na nibaakiri.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
text: “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, daanɔ nba gbat n maan na, ki bia teen wunba tumin na yada, ŋɔɔe mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu; ki Yennu kan bu u buut ki biirɔ. U bia poŋ nyii kuun nuu niwa, ki be manfoor maŋ nie.

ref: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
text: “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, yoo baat, ki li poŋ baara, ki kpeemm tan sa gbat min Yennu Bija kunkɔr, ki binba gbat n kunkɔr maŋ sa la manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

ref: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
text: N Baa nba yen manfoor ki teen niib na, nnee mun ki u te ki min u Bija mun yen manfoor ki teen niib,

ref: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
text: ki u bia teenin yikoo a mii bu niib buut, kimaan n tee nisaarik Bike.

ref: Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man,
text: I daa te ki li teei yaarlituku. Yoo baat ki niib kur nba poŋ kpo ki bi piib na, bi tan sa gbat n kunkɔr,

ref: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
text: ki fiir bi kaat ni; ki binba tun bonŋann, bi tan sa fiir ki be bi manfoa ni, ki li kii mɔk gbennu; ki binba mun tun toonbiit na mun, bi tan sa fiir, ki n bu bi buut ki biirib.

ref: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
text: “N kan fit tun siar nan n mɔŋi. Mi-i bu nirɔ buut biaŋinba, Yennue tuu betin ma n bu. Li paak teen ki n buut ŋan, kimaan n ki loon ki n tun n mɔŋ yanbɔɔtoona kaa, ŋaan n tuun Yennu nba tumin na yanbɔɔtoonae. Binba tee Yisa siara

ref: If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth.

text: “Bakitnauŋ toonjaana nba ki n tuun na, ŋannii want n siara ki chee Jɔɔnn siara na. Ki bia jenna, toonjaana nba ki n tuun na, n Baae yaa mii tuun, ki n tuun, ki toona maŋ want n siara nan n Baa Yennue tumin.

text: “Tɔn, ŋaan i lek tuu karin Yennu gbouŋ na, kimaan i tuu dukii mi a manfoor nba kaa gbennu be leŋe, ki Yennu gbouŋ maŋ want n po,

ref: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.

ref: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
text: I setikii bo-i teen Moses sɔbu yada, i bo sa teenin yadawa mun, kimaan u din sɔb pak n poe.

text: Ŋaan ya ki teen wa din sɔb linba maŋ yada na, nlee ki i ji sa teen n mɔmaan yada?”

text: Ba bann nan Yisae na, ki bi jiiu ki kɔɔnɔ burgbetir na ni nan parpeenn. Li taakpaak ni na ki burgbetir na baar siaminba ki bi baat na. Niib bonchiann nba din lon Yisa biaŋinba

text: Ki Yisa ji bann nan bi yaa bin soorɔ ki dinnɔ naame. Li paake te ki u nyikib ŋaan ŋmat jɔɔr paak u kuukɔɔ, ki saa be. Yisa nba din somm nyun paak biaŋinba (Matiu

ref: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.

ref: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”

ref: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
text: Ki u betib a “Mine tee manfoor jeet maŋ. Daanɔ nba baar n boor ni, kon kan mi ki soor li yɔɔ, ki wunba mun teenin yada, nyunyukiru kan mi ki soorɔ.

ref: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
text: “N poŋ beti sinsinn, i poŋ lama, ŋaan i ki teenin yada.

ref: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
text: Ŋaan wunba kur ki n Baa Yennu jiiu ki turin, u sa baar n boor ni ki teenin yadawa. N kan mi yêt wunba baar n boor ni,

text: kimaan n nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki sik na ma n tun Yennu nba tumin na yanbɔɔtoonne, ŋaan li ki tee n mɔŋ mɔŋ yanbɔɔt kaa.

ref: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
text: Ŋɔɔ Yennu nba tumin na, u lomme tee a n daa boont binba ki u turin na nan yenɔ kuukɔɔwa, ŋaan tan fiimm kuun ni dunia gbennu daar.

ref: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Nnee ki n Baa Yennu loon, a sɔɔ kur nba bann min, u Bija, ki teenin yada, u sa la manfoor nba kaa gbennu; ki n tan sa fiimm kuun ni dunia gbennu daar.”

ref: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
text: Ŋanne ki Juu teeb maŋ ji burin burin nan leeb, a kimaan wa yaa a ŋɔɔe tee jeet nba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni na paak.

ref: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
text: Ki bi boi leeb a “U ki tee Yisa wunba tee Josef bija na kaa na-aa? Ti mi u baa nan u naa. Nlee ki u yaa a u nyii Yendɔuŋ ni?”

ref: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
text: Ŋaan sɔɔ daa ki mi la n Baa Yennu maŋi, see min nba din tuu be nanɔ na kuukɔɔe laatɔ.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
text: “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, daanɔ nba teenin yada, ŋɔɔe mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

text: Mine tee manfoor jeet na.

ref: Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died.
text: Yoo nba ki i yejamm din tuu be muuk ni na, bi din tuu di manna maŋ, ŋaan ki bi kpoa.

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
text: Ki Yisa betib, “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, see ki i ŋman min nisaarik Bik gbanant, ki bia nyuu n sɔn. Nnee kaa, i kii mɔk manfoor maŋ i ni.

ref: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
text: Ki daanɔ nba ŋman n gbanant ki bia nyuu n sɔn, u mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu, ki n tan sa fiinɔ kuun ni dunia gbennu daar.

text: Kimaan n gbanante tee jeet nan mɔnii, ki n sɔn mun tee bon-nyukar nan mɔnii;

text: ki daanɔ nba ŋman n gbanant, ki bia nyuu n sɔn, ŋɔɔe be n ni, ki n mun be u ni.

ref: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
text: “N Baa Yennu nba be nee yen manfoor, ŋaan tumin, ki u paak ki n mun mɔk manfoor maŋ ki be. Li mun bia sii tee nnee, li-i tee ki wunba ŋman n gbanant, n paak, u sii mɔk manfoor maŋ.

ref: The bread that comes down from heaven isn't like what your ancestors ate. They died, but whoever eats this bread will live forever.
text: Mine tee jeet nba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki sik. Ki wunba di jeet na, u sii yɔɔ be nan yoo nba kaa gbennu; ki jeet maŋ ki tee kaman nan jeet nba ki i yejamm din di muuk ni ki bia kpoa na kaa.”

ref: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
text: Yisae din pak linba na, wa daa din be Kapenaum, Yenjiantu diiuk ni, ki want niib na. Manfoor nba kaa gbennu labaar

text: Yisa poorpoweiteeb bonchiann nba din gbat u wannu maŋ, ki bi yeen bi leeb a “Wannu na sii paar ti paak. Ŋmee sa sak linba na?”

text: Li-i tee ki i sa la min nisaarik Bik do Yendɔuŋ ni siaminba ki n din tuu be na, i ji sii dukii nlee?

ref: The Spirit is the one who gives life! Human strength can do nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are from that life-giving Spirit.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ kuukɔɔe teen niib manfoor; ŋaan gbanant na tee yanne. Mɔmaan nba ki n pak ki turi nae baat nan Yennu Seyeeŋ nba teen niib manfoor maŋ.

ref: But some of you refuse to have faith in me.” Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
text: Ŋaan i siab lek ki teenin yada.” Yisa din pakib linba na, kimaan u poŋ mi sinsinn binba tan kan teenɔ yada, nan wunba mun tan sa nyi u poor.

ref: Then Jesus said, “You cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.”

ref: We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.”
text: Ki mɔtana ti teena yadawa ki mi nan fine tee Yennu popeendaanɔ.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “I chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!”
text: Ki Yisa yetib, “Yimm jab piik nan bannlee na, mine ganni, ŋaan ki i ni yenɔ tee Sintaanii yɔɔ.”

text: U din yeen Judas wunba tee Simon Iskariot bik na poe; ŋɔɔe din tee u poorpoweiteeb piik nan bannlee na ni yenɔ, ŋaan ki u lek yaa wun nyi u poor.

ref: Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him.
text: Li poorpoe ki Yisa ji din be Galilee tiŋ ni, ŋaan lin leŋ digbana ni. U din ki loon wuu be Judea tiŋ ni, kimaan Juu teeb yudamm din loon bin kpiu.

ref: It was almost time for the Festival of Shelters,
text: Ki Juu teeb jaamm nba ki bi yir lanbont jaamm na tan per.

ref: and Jesus' brothers said to him, “Why don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing.
text: Ŋanne ki Yisa waarii yetɔ a “Nyikin nna, ŋaan saan Judea tiŋ ni, ki a poorpoweiteeb nba be leŋ na-n la toona nba ki a tuun na.

ref: No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!”
text: Li-i tee ki nirɔ loon wun wann u mɔŋ nan u tee sɔɔ, li ki ŋan ki wun bɔr toona nba ki u tuumi. Li-i tee ki a tuun toonjaana, fan wann niib paanu ni nan a set tee li daanɔe.”

ref: Even Jesus' own brothers had not yet become his followers.
text: U waarii na lek din ki teenɔ yada; li paak ki bi din pakɔ nna maŋ.

ref: Jesus answered, “My time hasn't yet come, but your time is always here.
text: Ki Yisa jiimm a “Li daa ki baar nan yoo nba ki n sa saani. Yimm ŋarin, li ki bi nan ki i saani,

ref: The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things.
text: kimaan tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb ki namiini, ŋaan min ŋarin bi namin, kimaan ma beerib a bi sɔnii ki ŋan na paak.

ref: Go on to the festival. My time hasn't yet come, and I am not going.”
text: Tɔn, yimm-ii saa jaamm maŋ, ŋaan min ŋarin daa ki saa, kimaan n sinsaan yoo daa ki baari.”

ref: Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee. Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
text: U din betib nnee, ŋaan biar be Galilee. Yisa nba din saan jaamm maŋ biaŋinba

ref: During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, “Where is he?”
text: Ki Juu teeb yudamm din lin ki loonɔ jaamm maŋ ni, ki boi niib a “U be lee?”

ref: The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, “Jesus is a good man,” while others were saying, “He is lying to everyone.”
text: Ki niib bonchiann nba be leŋ na burin ki piak bonchiann ki jikit saa u po; ki siab yaa a “U tee niŋanɔe,” ki leeb yaa a “Aaii, a u kpann niib nae;”

ref: But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
text: ŋaan sɔɔ din ki pak u po peee peee ni, kimaan bi tiin Juu teeb yudamm jaŋmaaniie.

ref: When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching.
text: Ki jaamm na yoo sinsuuk ni, ki Yisa saan kɔɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni, ki piin ki want niib Yennu maan.

text: Juu teeb nba gbiintirɔ yoonba, ki li teemm bakitnauŋ; ki bi boi a “Nlee ki nirɔ na mantik jia mi gbouŋ nna, ŋaan ki kɔɔ ti sakursaakari?”

ref: Jesus replied: I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me.
text: Ki Yisa yetib a “Ma want wannu nba na, li ki tee n mɔŋ mɔŋ wannu kaa, ŋaan li nyii Yennu nba tumin na boore.

ref: If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me.
text: Wunba loon wun tun Yennu yanbɔɔtoona, ŋɔɔe sa bann linba ki n piak ki want na nan li nyii Yennu boor amii n boor.

ref: If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie.
text: Daanɔ nba piak u mɔŋ mɔŋ wannu, ŋɔɔ loon nibaakir ki teen u mɔŋe. Ŋaan wunba loon a niib-ii baakit wunba tumɔ na, ŋɔɔe tee popeendaanɔ, ki ki mɔk biiti.

ref: Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
text: “Li ki tee Moses kaa din turi ti sennu-uu? Ŋaan i sɔɔ ki dia li sennu maŋi. Ki bee ki i loon ki i kpimi?”

ref: The crowd replied, “You're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?”
text: Ki niib nba na yetɔ a “Narinbiiuk be a ni! Ŋmee yaa wun kpiani?”

ref: Moses commanded you to circumcise your sons. But it wasn't really Moses who gave you this command. It was your ancestors, and even on the Sabbath you circumcise your sons
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki i pot bonjabik foon daar, i mun tuu tuun toonn foon daarii na. Li din tee Mosese din senn yaa a yii porii bonjabis, ŋaan i yejamme din piin punpotu sinsinn.

ref: in order to obey the Law of Moses. Why are you angry with me for making someone completely well on the Sabbath?
text: Tɔn, li-i tee ki i pot bonjabis foon daar, a i daa bot Moses sennue, nlee ki i donn wutoor n paak a ma te ki baatɔɔ paak foon daar na?

ref: Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, “Isn't this the man they want to kill?
text: Ŋanne ki Jerusalem nisiab boi bi leeb a “Nirɔ nba nee ki bi yabii a bin kpiu na-aa?

ref: Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah?
text: Gotir, u ki yeen ki bɔrii niib ni, ki sɔɔ mun bia ki pak siar a wun tiau. Li pasiar ti yudamm dukii a u tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia na.

text: Ŋaan min miu, kimaan n tuu be u boor nie, ki u tan tumin nna.”

ref: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
text: Wunba nyii Yennu peŋ, ŋɔɔe gbia Yennu maan; ŋaan ya ki nyii Yennu peŋ na, li paake te ki i ki gbia Yennu maan maŋi.” Yisa nan Abraham po

text: Ti chanbaa Abraham nba din kpo na, a dukii mi a a cheeu-ee? Yennu sɔkiniinba mun bia din kpoa. I got! Fin dukii mi a a tee ŋmee?”

text: Ki Yisa jiimm a “Mi-i bo-i dontir n mɔŋie, li bo sii tee yanne, ŋaan wunba dontirin tee n Baae. Ŋɔɔe tee daanɔ nba ki i yaa a u tee i Yennu na.

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
text: Ki Yisa jiin yetib a “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, ba din tan maar Abraham na, ŋaan sɔɔ ki min ŋarin poŋ kpan be.”

ref: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
text: Ŋanne ki bi ji tɔkii tana a bin jaatɔ ki kpiu; ki u lir ki nyii Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni.

ref: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
text: Li poorpoe ki Yisa din gɔrin saa ki tan la jɔɔŋ nna, ki u tee jɔɔŋ u maaru niwa.

ref: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
text: Ki u poorpoweiteeb boiɔ a “Chanbaa, ŋmee toonbiite te ki bi mar jɔɔ na jɔɔŋi? Li daa tee u mɔŋ toonbiit amii u damme?”

ref: “No, it wasn't!” Jesus answered. “But because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him.
text: Ki u jiimm a “U toonbiit paak kaa te ki u jɔɔni. Li mun bia ki tee u damm toonbiit paak kaa, ŋaan u jɔɔn a Yennu-n te ki wun nyɔɔnte, ki niib-n la Yennu toonjaann.

ref: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
text: Yoo nba ki yentu daa be na, li kpaa talase ki tii tuun wunba tumin na toona na, kimaan nyiɔk baat, yoo nba ki sɔɔ kan fit tun toonni.

ref: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
text: Ma daa be tingbouŋ nna, n tee tingbouŋ na niib yentue.”

ref: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
text: Wa yet nna maŋ poorpoe ki u siir mɔsant tiŋ ni, ŋaan buun tant, ki pɔr jɔɔŋ na ninbina paak,

ref: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
text: ŋaan betɔ a “I saa Siloam biɔuŋ ni ki saa yibir.” Ki u saan ki saa yibir, ki nyɔɔnt ki kun.

ref: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
text: Ŋanne ki u leeb nan niib nba tuu laatɔ ki u mei likirii na boi bi leeb a “Jajɔɔŋ nba tuu mei na kaa na-aa?”

ref: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
text: Ki siab yaa a “Ŋɔɔe,” ki leeb yaa a “Aaii, li ki tee ŋɔɔ kaa. U naanɔe.” Ŋanne ki jɔɔ maŋ tiauŋ yet a “Mine na.”

ref: “Then how can you see?” they asked.
text: Ki bi boiɔ a “A nunii na teen nlee ki tan nyɔɔnti?”

ref: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
text: Ki u jiimm a “Jɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Yisa na, ŋɔɔe buun tant ki jii ki pɔr n ninbina paak, ŋaan betin a mii saa Siloam biɔuŋ ni ki saa yibir; ki n saan ki saa yibir ki nyɔɔnt.”

ref: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
text: Ki bi boiɔ a “U be lee?” Ki u jiin yetib a u ki mi. Farisiinba nba boi jajɔɔŋ na nyɔɔntu maŋ po biaŋinba

ref: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
text: Daar nba ki Yisa din buun tant ki nyɔɔnt jɔɔŋ na, li daar din tee foon daare.

ref: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
text: Ki Farisiinba na bia ŋamm boi jɔɔ na, a u teen nlee ki nyɔɔnti. Ki u betib a “Yisae buun tant, ki jii ki pɔr n ninbina paak, ki n yibir ki nyɔɔnt.”

ref: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
text: Ki Farisiinba siab yet a “Yisa ki nyii Yennu boor ni kaa, kimaan u tuun toonn foon daar. Ŋannii want nan u ki dia foon daar sennu.” Ki bi siab mun boi a “Nlee ki biitdaanɔ sii fit tuun toonjaana na booru?” Ki bɔkitu baar Farisiinba na sinsuuk ni.

ref: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
text: Li paak ki bi bia boi jɔɔ na a “Jɔɔ na nba nyɔɔnta na, a dukii nlee u po?” Ki u bia pak jiimm a “U tee Yennu sɔkiniie.”

ref: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
text: Juu teeb yudamm na, ki li lek paar nan bin teen yada nan bi marɔ jɔɔŋe ki u tan nyɔɔnt. Ŋanne ki bi yiin u damm,

ref: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
text: ki tan boib a “U tee i bijae-ee? I set yaa a i marɔ jɔɔŋee? Ki li teen nlee ki u ji laat mɔtana na?”

ref: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
text: Ki bi jiin yetib a “Ti mi nan u tee ti bija ŋarime, ki ti bia mi nan ti marɔ jɔɔŋe.

ref: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
text: Ŋaan ti ki mi la teen biaŋinba ki u tan nyɔɔnti, ki ti bia ki mi wunba nyɔɔntɔ. U mɔŋ ji ki tee bik kaa. Boimɔ man. U sa fit pak u mɔŋ paaka.”

ref: The man's parents said this because they were afraid of their leaders. The leaders had already agreed that no one was to have anything to do with anyone who said Jesus was the Messiah.
text: Li paak te ki u damm din yaa a u ki tee bik kaa, a bi mɔŋ-n boiɔ.

ref: The leaders called the man back and said, “Swear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.”
text: Ki li taar munnlee nie ki bi bia ŋamm yiin jɔɔ nba tuu tee jɔɔŋ ŋaan tan nyɔɔnt na, ki tan betɔ a “I teen Yennue nibaakir nan niipoouk; kimaan ti mi nan Yisae tee biitdaanɔ.”

ref: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
text: Ki jɔɔ na yetib a “Wa tee biitdaanɔ, amii wa ki tee, n ki mi. Ŋaan min ŋarin mi bonyenne: n tuu tee jɔɔŋe, ŋaan mɔtana n ji laat.”

ref: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
text: Ki bi ŋamm bia boiɔ a “U teen nlee ki nyɔɔntani?”

ref: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text: Ki u jiimm a “N poŋ betiiwa, ŋaan ki i ki gbati. Bee ki i jin loon ki n betiini? I mun loon ki i teen u poorpoweiteebee?”

ref: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
text: Ŋanne ki bi wutoor ji doo, ki bi sukiiɔ, ŋaan betɔ a “Fine tee nirɔ maŋ poorpoweitɔɔ, ŋaan timm ŋarin tee Moses poorpoweiteebe.

ref: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
text: Ti mi nan Yennu din piak nan Moses, ŋaan nirɔ na ŋarin, ti ki mi wa nyii siami.”

ref: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
text: Ki jɔɔ nba nyɔɔnt maŋ yetib a “Bee paruko mani, ki i tan yaa a i ki mi wa nyii siami, ŋaan ki u nyɔɔnt n nunii.

ref: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
text: Ti mi nan Yennu ki gbia biitdamm miaru, ŋaan u gbia damm nba jiantirɔ ki tuun wa loon biaŋinbae.

ref: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
text: La nyii dunia piinu niwa, ti daa ki mi gbat a sɔɔ nyɔɔnt wunba ki bi marɔ jɔɔŋi.

ref: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
text: Li-i bo-i tee ki jɔɔ maŋ ki nyii Yennu peŋi, u bo kan fit ki tun bakitnauŋ toonn na booru.”

ref: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
text: Ki bi jiin ki yetɔ a “Fin nba ki bi mara nan biit na po, a tee ŋmee ki want timmi?” Ki bi nyinnɔ bi Yenjiantu ni. Ninyentir labaar

ref: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
text: Yisa nba gbat a bi nyinnɔ bi Yenjiantu ni na, ki u tan lomɔ ki lau, ki boiɔ a “A teen nisaarik Bik na yada-aa?”

ref: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
text: Ki u jiin boiɔ, “Chanbaa, ŋmee tee nisaarik Bik na? Betirin wa tee wunba, ki n teenɔ yada.”

ref: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
text: Ki Yisa betɔ, “A poŋ mi lauwa. Ŋɔɔe bia tee min nba piak nana na.”

ref: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
text: Ki jɔɔ na yetɔ a “N Yomdaanɔ, n teena yadawa,” ŋaan gbaan u tɔɔnn po ki pukiiɔ.

ref: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
text: Ŋanne ki Yisa yet a “N baar tingbouŋ na ni a n bu niib buut, ki bia yent binba ki mɔk ninyentir numm po, ŋaan dɔkin binba mɔk ninyentir numm po.”

ref: When the Pharisees heard Jesus say this, they asked, “Are we blind?”
text: Ki Farisiinba siab nba be leŋ na gbat ki u yet nna, ki bi boiɔ “A yaa a ti mun ki mɔk ninyentiree?”

ref: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
text: Ki u jiimm a “Li-i bo-i tee ki i ki mɔk ninyentiri, i bo kii mɔk biiti, ŋaan ya sat yaa a i mɔk ninyentir na, i mɔk biit.”

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
text: Ki Yisa bia yetib a “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, nirɔ nba ki tɔkin pelook tammɔb ki kɔɔ, ŋaan doo joonn ki kɔɔ, li yɔɔe tee nanyukɔɔ.

ref: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
text: U-i taŋŋi baat a wun kɔɔ, tammɔguurɔ na sa loote, wun kɔɔ; ki u yi u mɔŋ mɔŋ pei na sana, ki bi gbia u mɔb, ki u nyintib.

ref: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
text: U-i nyinn u mɔŋ pei kur yoonba, u tuu gar bi tɔɔnn nie, ki bi waa u poor, kimaan bi mi u kunkɔr.

ref: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
text: Ŋaan bi kan wei saamɔ ŋarimi, kimaan bi ki mi u kunkɔri. See ki bi chiar.”

ref: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
text: Yisa din yimm barjouk na, ŋaan ki bi ki bann li paaki.

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep.
text: Li paak, ki u bia ŋamm ki betib a “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, mine tee pelook na tammɔb.

ref: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
text: Ŋaan binba kur gar n tɔɔnn ki baar na tee nanyukibe nan fatiteeb, ŋaan pei na ki gbia bi mɔbu.

ref: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
text: Mine tee tammɔb na, ki daanɔ nba wein ki kɔɔ, ŋɔɔe sa la tinnu, ki sii yɔɔ gaar kɔɔ, ki ŋmat nyi, ki laat jeŋant.

ref: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
text: Nanyukɔɔ ŋarin baat a wun tan jan pei nae, ki bia kpib, ki bia biirib. Mine baar a niib-n la manfoor nba tee manfoor nan barmɔnii.

ref: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
text: “Mine tee pekpaaŋaŋ, ki pekpaaŋaŋe kpenn u pei paak.

ref: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
text: Ŋaan wunba kpaar pei likirii paak, ŋaan ki ki tee pekpaarik nan mɔnii, ki bia ki yen pei na, li-i tee ki u la ki kinkatir tan baat yoonba, see ki u chiar ŋaan nyik pei na; ki kinkatir-n ji baar ki soorib, ki pelei na-n yat.

ref: Hired workers run away because they don't care about the sheep.
text: U tuu chiare, kimaan u kpaar pei na likirii paake, ki u baka kaa pei na ni.

ref: Just as the Father knows me, I know the Father, and I give up my life for my sheep.
text: Mine tee pekpaaŋaŋ. N Baa Yennu nba min, ki n mun miu na, li mun bia tee nnee ki n mi n pei, ki bi mun min, ki n sa lu n miar bi paak.

ref: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
text: Pelei be ki tee n yab, ŋaan ŋamm ki tee pelook maŋ ni yabi. See ki n baar nan ŋamm mun biak, ki bi sa sak n mɔb, ki bi kur ji sii tee pebooryeŋ, ki mɔk pekpaaryeŋ kɔɔ.

ref: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
text: “N Baa Yennu loonin, kimaan n yaa n nyinn n manfoor, ki li poorpo ki n bia sa ŋamm la n manfoor maŋ.

ref: No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
text: Sɔɔ kaa ki sa nyinn n manfoor maŋi, ŋaan n mɔŋ mɔŋe yaa n nyinnir, kimaan n mɔk yikoo ki sa nyinnir, ki bia mɔk yikoo ki tan sa lar. Nnee ki n Baa betin a n tun.”

ref: The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them.
text: Wa yet nna yoonba, ki Juu teeb yudamm bia ŋamm bɔkit;

ref: Many of them said, “He has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?”
text: ki bi siab bonchiann yaa a “Narinbiiuk be u ni, a ki waatuk bia mɔkɔ; ki bee teen ki i gbiintir u maami?”

ref: But others said, “How could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!” Jesus Is Rejected
text: Ki leeb mun yaa a “Nirɔ nba ki narinbiiuk be u ni na kii piak nna. Nlee ki nirɔ nba ki narinbiiuk be u ni na sa fit nyɔɔnt jɔɔŋi?” Juu teeb yudamm nba yêt Yisa biaŋinba

ref: One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch,
text: ki Yisa din somm lin barintir nba ki bi yir Solomon barintir na ni, ki li din be Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni.

ref: and the people gathered all around him. They said, “How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!”
text: Ki Juu teeb yudamm din baar ki tikir ki lintɔ, ŋaan boiɔ a “A yaa lin yukir nleewa, ki tin tan bann nan a tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia? Li-i tee ki a tee ŋɔɔie, fan betit barmɔnii.”

ref: Jesus answered: I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am.
text: Ki Yisa jiimm, “N poŋ daan betiiwa, ŋaan ki i ki teenin yada. Toona nba ki n tuun nan n Baa Yennu yaak na ni, ŋannii want ma tee wunba.

ref: But since you are not my sheep, you don't believe me.
text: Ŋaan ya ki tee n pei na paak, ŋannii te ki i ki teenin yada.

ref: My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me,
text: N pei saak n mɔb, ki n mib, ki bi waan,

ref: and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand.
text: ki n teemm manfoa nba kaa gbennu, ki bi kan mi ki kpo, ki sɔɔ bia kaa ki sa fit fatib n nuu ni.

ref: My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands,
text: N Baa nba jiib ki teemm n nuu ni na, ŋɔɔe chee gar sɔɔ kur. Li paak teen ki sɔɔ kan fit fatib n Baa maŋ nuu ni.

ref: and I am one with the Father.
text: Min nan n Baa maŋ tee yomme.”

ref: Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus.
text: Li yoo na ki Juu teeb yudamm na tɔkii tana a bin jaatɔ ki kpiu.

ref: But he said, “I have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?”
text: Ki Yisa yetib a “I poŋ la ki n tun toonŋana bonchiann, linba ki n Baa Yennu yaa a n tun, ki toonŋana maŋ ni, lann paake te ki i ji yaa i jaatimi?”

ref: They answered, “We are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!”
text: Ki bi jiin ki yetɔ a “Ti ki loon ki tin jaata tana toonŋansiar paak kaa, ŋaan fa jikit a mɔŋ ki yek nan Yennu na paak. A kpan tee nirɔe, ŋaan yi a mɔŋ Yennu, ki li tee bonbilankante.”

ref: You can't argue with the Scriptures, and God spoke to those people and called them gods.
text: ki ti mun mi nan Yennu gbouŋ kan faar faak fabaa; ki ŋɔɔ Yennu yi niib u waas, kimaan ŋamme tee niib nba ki u din pak u maan ki turib na.

ref: So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world.
text: Ŋaan min ŋarin, n Baa Yennue gannin ki tumin tingbouŋ na ni; ki ma yet yaa a n tee Yennu Bija na, nlee ki i yaa a li tee bonbilankanti?

ref: If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me.
text: Li-i tee ki n ki tuun n Baa Yennu na toonaie, ŋann i daa tee-n yada.

ref: But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki n tuun ŋɔɔ Yennu toona maŋie, li-i lekii tee ki i ki teenin yada, ŋaan yin teen n toona maŋ yada. Ŋannii ki i sa bann nan barmɔnii nan n Baa Yennu be n ni, ki n mun bia be u ni.”

ref: Again they wanted to arrest Jesus. But he escaped
text: Ŋanne ki Juu teeb na tan yaa bin soorɔ; ki u buut nyii,

ref: and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there,
text: ki ŋmat poot Jɔɔdann mɔk-pootir, ki saa be siaminba ki Jɔɔnn din tuu wuur niib Yennu nyunwuru na.

ref: many people came to him. They were saying, “John didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.”
text: Ki niib bonchiann din baar Yisa peŋ, ki din piak nan bi leeb a “Jɔɔnn ŋarin din ki tun toonjaansiari, ŋaan linba kur ki u din piak ki jikit saa Yisa po, li tee barmɔniie.”

ref: A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.
text: Ki niib bonchiann din teen Yisa yada leŋ.

ref: The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
text: ki u niipoob Mari nan Maata tun toomii a wun saan ki saa bet Yisa a “Ti Yomdaanɔ, a naasinyɔɔk nba ki a mantik loonɔ na yiare.”

ref: But he stayed where he was for two more days.
text: Wa gbat a Lasarus yiar na, ŋaan u din teen daa ŋannlee ki daa be leŋ.

ref: Then he said to his disciples, “Now we will go back to Judea.”
text: Li poorpoe ki u din tan bet u poorpoweiteeb na a “Ŋaant man ki tin ŋmat saan Judea.”

ref: “Teacher,” they said, “the people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?”
text: Ki bi yetɔ a “Chanbaa, daa ŋannlelee na, ki Juu teeb yudamm na daan yaa bin jaata tana, ŋaan ki a bia yaa ti ŋmat leŋii?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Aren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble.
text: Ki Yisa betib a “Yentu tuu be yendo po mɔkmɔk nan yenbaa poe. Li-i tee ki nirɔ somm yennu ni, u kan gbiati, kimaan u laat tingbouŋ na yentu na.

ref: But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.”
text: Ŋaan u-i somm nyiɔk, u gbiatire, kimaan yentu kaa.”

ref: Then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.”
text: Yisa din yet nnee, ŋaan bia yet a “Ti yɔɔk Lasarus gɔɔnte, ŋaan n sa saan fiinɔwa.”

ref: They replied, “Lord, if he is asleep, he will get better.”
text: Ki u poorpoweiteeb na yetɔ a “Ti Yomdaanɔ, li-i tee ki u gɔɔntie, u sa la laafiawa.”

ref: Jesus really meant that Lazarus was dead, but they thought he was talking only about sleep.
text: Yisa din want ki li tee a Lasarus kpoe, ŋaan ŋamm din mi a u want a Lasarus gɔɔnte.

ref: I am glad I wasn't there, because now you will have a chance to put your faith in me. Let's go to him.”
text: ŋaan i paak n mɔk parpeenn nan ma daan kaa u boor ni na, kimaan ŋannii sa te ki i fit lian teenin yada. Ŋaant tin saan u peŋ man.”

ref: Thomas, whose nickname was “Twin,” said to the other disciples, “Come on. Let's go, so we can die with him.” Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
text: Ki Tomas, wunba ki bi yiu Jaak na, yet u leeb a “Ŋaant ki ti mun-n saan man. Li-i sii tee kuun, timm nan ŋɔɔ-n kpo.”

ref: When Jesus got to Bethany, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
text: Yoo nba ki bi tan per Betani na, ki u gbat a ba pii Lasarus, li daa ŋannae na.

ref: Bethany was less than three kilometers from Jerusalem,
text: Betani din ki fɔk nan Jerusalemi; li fɔkint din tee kaman nan maar ŋannlee nee.

ref: and many people had come from the city to comfort Martha and Mary because their brother had died.
text: Li paak din teen ki Juu teeb bonchiann din baar Maata nan Mari peŋ, a bin maan bi para, kimaan bi ninja na kuun paak. Wunba fiint kpeemm ki teemm manfoa na labaar

ref: When Martha heard that Jesus had arrived, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed in the house.
text: Maata nba tan gbat a Yisa baat yoonba, ki u nyii chetɔ, ŋaan ki Mari ŋarin biar be ŋaak ni.

ref: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
text: Ki Maata bet Yisa, “N Yomdaanɔ, li-i tee ki a daan bo be nna na, n ninja na daan bo kan kpo.
