text: Ki Piita betɔ, “Min ŋarin poŋ, a kan mi ki fobit n taa fabaa.” Ki Yisa betɔ a “Mi-i kii fobit a taa, a ji kan mi ki teen n yɔɔ.”

ref: Peter said, “Lord, don't wash just my feet. Wash my hands and my head.”
text: Ŋanne jik ki Simon Piita betɔ a “N Yomdaanɔ, daa fobitir n taa kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan fobitir n nii nan n yur kura.”

ref: Jesus answered, “People who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.”
text: Ki Yisa betɔ, “Daanɔ nba poŋ wur nyun, li yɔɔ ji ki mɔk jakinti. Li paak, li ki kpaa talas nan wun ŋamm ki wur biaki, see ki wun fobit u taae. I kura ji ki mɔk jakinti, ŋaan yenɔ kuukɔɔe mɔk jakint.”

ref: Jesus knew who would betray him. That is why he said, “except for one of you.”
text: Yisa poŋ din mi wunba sa nyi u poor na. Li paake te ki u din yet yaa a “Yenɔ kuukɔɔe mɔk jakint.”

ref: After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said: Do you understand what I have done?
text: Yisa nba fobit bi taa ki gbenn yoonba na, ki u ji jii u liatir na ki lia, ŋaan jen kar jeet na boor, ŋaan boib a “I mi ma tun linba ki turi na paakii?

ref: You call me your teacher and Lord, and you should, because that is who I am.
text: I yin a n tee i Wanntɔɔe, a nan i Yomdaanɔ. Li set ŋan nan ya yin biaŋinba na, kimaan n set tee nnee.

ref: I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you.
text: kimaan n wanni nyinne na, ki yii mun-ii tuun nna man kaman nan ma tun biaŋinba ki turi na.

ref: I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them.
text: Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, daabir ki chee u daanɔ, ki toomu mun ki chee wunba tumɔ na.

ref: I am not talking about all of you. I know the ones I have chosen. But what the Scriptures say must come true. And they say, “The man who ate with me has turned against me!”
text: “N ki piak i kura po kaa. N mi yimm nba ki n ganni na para ni. Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a ‘Daanɔ nba ki min nan ŋɔɔ lakin di na, ŋɔɔe yaa wun nyi n poor.’ See ki sɔbu na fabir teen mɔnii ki gbeen.

ref: I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am.
text: N beeri labaar maŋ mɔtana na, ki li set tan sa teen, ki li-i tei teen gbenn yoonba, ki yin teen yada nan n set tee ma tee wunbae.

ref: So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, “Lord, which one of us are you talking about?”
text: Ki u poorpoweitɔɔ maŋ dɔnn nakinɔ, ki boiɔ, “N Yomdaanɔ, li tee ŋmee?”

ref: After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, “I tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.”
text: Yisa nba din yet nna poorpoe, ki parbiir baarɔ bonchiann; ki u betib a “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, i ni yenɔ yaa wun nyi n poore.”

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal,
text: Bi ni yenɔ nba ki Yisa mantik din loonɔ na kar kpiau.

ref: and Simon motioned for this disciple to find out which one Jesus meant.
text: Ki Simon Piita kabintɔ, ki betɔ a “Boimɔ ki laan u yeen ŋmapoe.”

ref: No one at the meal understood what Jesus meant.
text: Ŋaan bi sɔɔ din ki bann linba ki Yisa pakɔ na paaki.

ref: But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor.
text: Kimaan Judas nba din tuu dia likirii pakir na, bi siab din dukii mia a Yisa tumɔ a wun saan saa daa jaamm na jeete, amii u betɔ a wun saan tur talasdamme likirii.

ref: Judas took the piece of bread and went out. It was already night. The New Command
text: Judas nba din gaar boroboro na yoonba, li tonton na ki u fiir nyii. Li mun din tee nyiɔko. Yisa dontir labaar

ref: Then, after God is given glory because of him, God will bring glory to him, and God will do it very soon.
text: Li-i tee ki Yennu la dontir n ni, li kan yukiri ki u mun sa te ki min nisaarik Bik-n la dontir u ni.

ref: My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, “You cannot go where I am going.”
text: “N waas, n ji kan yukir nani bonchianni. I tan sa lomin, ŋaan kan lami, kaman nan ma bet Juu teeb yudamm biaŋinba na, n bia beeri ŋanne na, a i kan fit baar siaminba ki n saa na. Yisa sennpaanu labaar

ref: Simon Peter asked, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “You can't go with me now, but later on you will.”
text: Ŋanne ki Simon Piita boiɔ a “N Yomdaanɔ, a yaa a tan saan lee?” Ki Yisa jiin betɔ a “Ma tan sa saan siaminba, i kan fit wei mɔtana, ŋaan li poorpo i sa weina.”

ref: Peter asked, “Lord, why can't I go with you now? I would die for you!”
text: Ki Piita bia boiɔ, “N Yomdaanɔ, bee teen ki n kan fit weia mɔtana? Min ŋarin poŋ teen siir nan ki n gaar a paak ki kpo.”

ref: “Would you really die for me?” Jesus asked. “I tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.”
text: Ki Yisa boiɔ a “A set teen siir nan mɔnii nan ki a gaar n paak ki kpo-oo? Barmɔniie ki n beera na, kojouk daa tan kan bui daanni, ki a tan sa nɔi taar munntaa a a ki mimi.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “Don't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me.
text: Li yoo na ki Yisa betib a “I daa mɔk parbiiri. Teent Yennu yada, ki mun teenin yada.

ref: There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you.
text: Diit bonchiann be n Baa Yennu ŋaak ni, ki n kun ma n sa teen i diit siir. Li-i bo-i kii tee barmɔnii, n bo kan betiini.

ref: After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together.
text: Ki mi-i kun ki sa teen i diit na siir ki gbenn, n bia sa jenna ki tan jii-i, ki i mun tan sii be siaminba ki n tan sii be na.

ref: Thomas said, “Lord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?”
text: Ki Tomas boiɔ, “N Yomdaanɔ, ti ki mi fa saa siami. Nlee ki ti sa bann sɔnu nba ki ti sa saan leŋi?”

ref: “I am the way, the truth, and the life!” Jesus answered. “Without me, no one can go to the Father.
text: Ki Yisa betɔ, “Mine tee sɔnu na, ki bia tee mɔmɔmm, ki bia tee manfoor. Sɔɔ kaa ki sa baar n Baa Yennu boor ni, ki li ki tee ki u sint baarimi.

ref: If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.”
text: Li-i bo-i tee ki i min, i bia bo sii mi n Baa nawa; ŋaan mɔtana ki jikit saa, i ji miu ki bia laatɔ.”

ref: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father. That is all we need.”
text: Ki Filip yetɔ a “N Yomdaanɔ, wantit a Baa maŋ, ki ti numm-n gboo.”

ref: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
text: Ki Yisa jiin a “Filip, n poŋ be nani ki li yukira, ŋaan ki a daa ki mimii? Wunba lan, u set la n Baa maŋe na. Ki bee ki a yaa a n wanni n Baa maŋi?

ref: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
text: N be n Baa nie, ki n Baa mun be n ni; i ki teen linba na yada-aa? Maan nba ki n pak ki turi na, li ki nyii n ni kaa, ŋaan li nyii n Baa Yennu boore. Ŋɔɔe be n ni ki tuun u toona maŋ.

ref: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
text: Li paak, teentin yada nan n be n Baa Yennu ni, ki n Baa Yennu mun be n ni. Li-i kii tee nna biaki, yin teen bakitnauŋ toonjaana nba ki n tuu tuun na yada.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
text: “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, daanɔ nba teenin yada, u sii tuun toonjaana kaman nan ma tuun biaŋinba na, ki u toonjaana maŋ gbaa sa gar n yara, kimaan n saa n Baa Yennu boore.

ref: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
text: Ki linba kur ki i sii meiɔ li po nan n sann ni, n sa tuma, ki ŋɔɔ Yennu-n la dontir n paak.

ref: I will do whatever you ask me to do. The Holy Spirit Is Promised
text: Ki li-i tee ki i meiɔ linba kur po nan n sann ni, n sa tuma. Yennu Seyeeŋ baaru labaar

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
text: “Li-i tee ki i loonin, i sii saak n mɔb,

ref: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
text: ki n sa miar n Baa Yennu, ki u sa turi Sommtɔlɔɔ. Ŋɔɔe tee u Seyeeŋ nba want barmɔnii wannu, ki sii be nani yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
text: Ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb kan fit gaarɔ, kimaan bi ki miu, ki bia ki mi nan u be. Ŋaan yimm ŋarin miu, kimaan u be nani, ki bia sii yɔɔ be i ni.

ref: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
text: “Mi-i tei yaat saan, ŋaan n kan nyiki yann ki sɔɔ ki goriini, kimaan n sa jen i peŋ na.

ref: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
text: Ki li-i yann waaminna, tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb ji kan lami, ŋaan yimm ŋarin bia sa lama. Tɔn, ma tan sa jen n manfoor ni na, i mun tan sa jen i manfoa niwa.

ref: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: Ki bia jenna, mi-i tei jen n manfoor ni yoonba, i tan sa bann nan n be n Baa Yennu nie, ki i mun bia be n ni, ki n mun be i ni.

ref: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
text: “Daanɔ nba bant n sennii ki bia saakir, li daanɔe loonin. N Baa Yennu sii loon ŋɔɔe, ki n mun sii loonɔ, ki sa fiit n mɔŋ ki wannɔ ma tee wunba.”

ref: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, “Lord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?”
text: Ki Judas, boiɔ a “N Yomdaanɔ, bee teen ki a sa fiit a mɔŋ ki wannit, ŋaan kan fiit a mɔŋ ki tur tingbouŋ na ŋaateebi?”

ref: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
text: Ki Yisa jiin betɔ a “Daanɔ nba loonin, u sii saak n maan, ki n Baa Yennu sii loonɔ, ki min nan n Baa Yennu sa baar u boor ki sii yɔɔ be u ni.

ref: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
text: Ki jenna, daanɔ nba ki loonimi, u ki saak n maami, ki ya gbat maan nba na, li ki tee n yar kaa, ŋaan li tee n Baa Yennu nba tumin na yare.

ref: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
text: “Ma daa be nani na, n beeri maan nba na.

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
text: Ŋaan ki n Baa Yennu sa tun Sommtɔɔ i boor, n sann ni. Li Sommtɔɔe tee u Seyeeŋ na; ŋɔɔe sa wanni barii kur, ki bia sa tiari linba kur ki n poŋ beti.

ref: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
text: “Mi-i yaa n saan, n sa pi-i piinii nba tee n parmaasir na. Li ki tee tingbouŋ na parmaasir na booru kaa. I daa mɔk parbiiri. I bia daa tiin jaŋmaanii.

ref: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
text: I poŋ gbat ki n yeti a ‘N nyikitie, ŋaan n bia sa jen i boor ni na.’ Ki li-i tee ki i loonimie, i sii mɔk parpeenn nan ma saa n Baa Yennu boor ni na, kimaan u cheen.

ref: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
text: “N daa beeri linba tan sa teene, ki yoo nba ki li tan sa teenewa, ki yin teen yada.

ref: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
text: N kan pak nani ki yukiri, kimaan tingbouŋ na yudaanɔ Sintaanii baat, ŋaan u ki mɔk n yaaki.

ref: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
text: N loon tingbouŋ na teeb-n bann nan n loon n Baa Yennu nan mɔnii; li paake teen ki n tuu tuun linba ki u tuu betin a n tun. “Ŋaant man ki tin fiir ki yaat.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
text: Li poorpoe ki Yisa yet a “Mine tee barmɔnii tiik na, ki n Baa Yennu tee li ŋammtɔɔ;

ref: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
text: ki u tuu chɔɔ yiinu kur nba gaa n paak ŋaan ki lôon lɔɔna na. Ŋaan yiinii kur nba ŋarin lôon lɔɔna na, u tuu gbat gbat ŋanne, ki lin ŋamm ki lôn lɔɔna bonchiann.

ref: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
text: Mɔtana, yimme tee nifoyeemm, maan nba ki n pak ki turi na paak.

ref: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
text: Yii gaa n paak man, ki n sii be i ni. Tiyiinu kan fit lon lɔɔna, see ki u gaa tiik paak. Li mun bia tee nnee, i kan fit lon lɔɔna, see ki i gaa n paak.

ref: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
text: “Mine tee tiik maŋ, ki yimme tee li yiinii. Wunba gaa n paak, ki n mun be u ni, ŋɔɔe sii lôon lɔɔna bonchiann, kimaan mi-i kaa i ni, i kan fit tun siari.

ref: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
text: Ki jenna, wunba ki yɔɔ gaa n paaki, Yennu sa chɔɔe ki lu kaman nan ba tuu chɔɔ tiyiinu nba ki lu ki wun koor na. Li tiyiinboorue ki bi tuu tikir ki joo muu.

ref: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
text: Ki bia jenna, li-i tee ki i yɔɔ gaa n paak, ki n maan na be i ni, miat Yennu linba kur ki i loon, ki i sa lawa.

ref: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
text: “N Baa Yennu nba loonin biaŋinba na, n mun looni li biaŋe. Yii gaa n paak, ki n loomm sii be i ni.

ref: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
text: Li-i tee ki i saak n mɔb, n loomm sii be i ni. Li mun bia tee nnee ki n saak n Baa Yennu mɔb, ki u loomm mun be n ni. Leeb loommu labaar

ref: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
text: “N pak na ki turi a n parpeenn na boorue mantik-ii be i ni, ki saa yakir.

ref: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
text: Nee tee n sennu ki n senn ki turi, ma yii loon i leeb kaman nan ma loon yimm biaŋinba na.

ref: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
text: Loonjaansiar kaa ki gar nirɔ-n sak ki gaar u yɔɔsinba paak ki kpo.

ref: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
text: “Li-i tee ki i tuun ma beti biaŋinba na, i tee n yɔɔsinbae.

ref: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
text: N ji kan yiini daaba, kimaan daabir ki mi u yomdaanɔ dudukiti. Ma yi-i n yɔɔsinba na, kimaan labaar kur nba ki n gbat n Baa Yennu peŋ na, ŋanne ki n wanni.

ref: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: Yimm kaa gann mine, ŋaan mine gann yimm, ki turi toona ma yii saa kii lôon lɔɔna bonchiann, lɔɔna nba sii be mɔkmɔk nan mɔkmɔk, a n Baa Yennu-n turi linba ki i miarɔ n sann ni.

ref: So I command you to love each other. The World's Hatred
text: Linba nee ki n pak senn ki turi, ma yii loon i leeb man. Tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb naŋ labaar

ref: If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first.
text: “Tɔn, li-i taŋŋi tee ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb nami, yin bann nan bi poŋ nan minewa.

ref: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
text: Li-i bo-i tee ki i tee tingbouŋ na ŋaaniibie, tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb bo sii looni kaman nan ba loon bi mɔŋ niib biaŋinba nee. Ŋaan n ganni ki nyinni tingbouŋ na niib niwa, ki i ji ki tee tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb yabi; li paake teen ki bi nami.

ref: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
text: Yii tian maan nba ki n paki na man, a ‘Daabir kaa ki chee u yomdaanɔ.’ Tɔn, bi-i mukisin, bi mun sa mukisiiwa; ki bi-i sak n maan, bi mun bia sa sak i maama.

ref: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
text: Bi set sa mukisiiwa, kimaan ya tee n yab na, ki jenna, ba ki mi Yennu wunba tumin na paak.

ref: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
text: “Mi-i diŋŋi kii sik ki tan pak ki turibi, bi bo kii mɔk biiti. Ŋaan ma sat ki sik pak turib na, sɔnsau ji kaa ki bi sa fit bɔr bi biiti.

ref: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
text: Ŋaan wunba namin, u nan n Baa Yennue mun.

ref: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
text: Mi-i bo-i kii tuun toonjaana nba ki sɔɔ daa ki mi tun na bi boor ni, bi bia bo kii mɔk biiti. Bi lek la toonjaana nba ki n tuu tuun nawa, ŋaan nan min nan n Baa Yennu.

ref: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, “People hated me for no reason.”
text: Linba na teen nnee, a ba din sɔb linba n po bi sennu ni na sa fit lian teen mɔnii, ki li sɔb a ‘Bi namin, ki li ki mɔk paaki.’

ref: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
text: “Tɔn, n sa tun Sommtɔɔ i peŋ, wunba tee Yennu Seyeeŋ ki want barmɔnii wannu na. U sa nyi n Baa Yennu boore ki baar i peŋ; ki u baaru maŋ ni, u sii piaki n po.

ref: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
text: Ki i mɔŋ mɔŋ sii piak n po ki teen niib, kimaan i poŋ yɔɔ be nanin, la din nyii yoo nba ki n din piin ki tuun n toonn na.

ref: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
text: “N poŋ mi paki muksuk na poewa a i tan daa nyikit n weiu.

ref: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
text: Bi tan sa yɔɔ nyinni Yenjiantu ni. Ki yoo baat ki wunba tan kpi-i, u sii dukii mi a u tuun nna ki teen Yennue.

ref: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
text: Bi tan sa tun linba na ki turiiwa, kimaan bi ki mimi, ki bia ki mi n Baa.

ref: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
text: Ŋaan n beeri linba na mɔtana, a li yoo-i taŋŋi baar, ki i ji sa lian tiar n maan nba ki n paki na. Yennu Seyeeŋ toona nba sii tee biaŋinba “N din ki beti linba na sinsinni, kimaan min nan yimme tuu be.

ref: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
text: Ŋaan mɔtana n saa Yennu wunba tumin na peŋe. Ŋaan i sɔɔ ki boin ki laan n saa lee.

ref: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
text: Ŋaan ma beti linba mɔtana na, ki i ji mɔk parbiir bonchiann.

ref: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
text: N beeri barmɔniie, nan li ŋan i po nan ki n yaat saan, kimaan mi-i kii saani, Sommtɔɔ na kan baar i peŋi. Ŋaan mi-i taŋŋi saan, n sa tumɔ i peŋ na.

ref: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
text: “U-i taŋŋi baar yoonba, u sa wann tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb a bi mɔk biit. U bia sa wannib yentinu nba tee biaŋinba, ki bia sa wannib Yennu barbuut nba tan sii tee biaŋinba.

ref: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
text: U sa wannib a bi mɔk biit, kimaan bi ki teenin yada.

ref: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
text: Ki jenna, u sa wannib yentinu nba tee biaŋinba, kimaan n sa saan n Baa Yennu boor, ki bi ji kan lami.

ref: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
text: Ki bia jenna, u sa wannib Yennu barbuut nba tan sii tee biaŋinba, kimaan u poŋ bu tingbouŋ na teeb yudaanɔ Sintaanii buut ki biirɔwa.

ref: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
text: “Maan be bonchiann ki n bo sa paki, ŋaan i kan fit gaar mɔtana.

ref: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ nba want barmɔnii na, u-i taŋŋi baar yoonba, u sa wanni barmɔnii kur wannua. U tan kii piak nan u mɔŋ mɔŋ maan kaa, ŋaan u tan sii wanti linba ki u gbia Yennu boore, ki bia sii beeri linba tan sa teen na.

ref: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
text: Ki jenna, u bia tan sii dont n sann kimaan u tan sii jikit n labaare ki fiitir ki wanti.

ref: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
text: Linba kur ki n Baa Yennu mɔk, li tee n yare. Li paake teen ki n yaa ma Yennu Seyeeŋ sii jikit n labaar ki wanti. Parbiir nan parpeenn po

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “For a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.”
text: “Li-i yann waaminna i ji kan lami, ŋaan li-i biakii yann waaminna, i bia sa lama.”

ref: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
text: Ki u poorpoweitesiab boi bi leeb a “Linba na paak tee bee? Ki u yaa a li-i yann waaminna, a ti kan lau, a ŋaan li-i biakii yann waaminna, a ti sa lauwa. Ki u bia yaa a kimaan u saa u Baa peŋe.

ref: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
text: ‘Li-i yann waaminna’ paak tee bee? Ti ki mi wa piak linba na paaki.”

ref: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
text: Ki Yisa bann nan bi yaa bin boiɔ buboite. Li paake te ki u boib a “Ma yaa a li-i yann waaminna i kan lami, ki bia yaa a li-i bia yann waaminna i sa lama na, i boi i leebe a i bann li paakii?

ref: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
text: Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, i tan sii fabin ki mɔ, ŋaan tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb ŋarin tan sii mɔk parpeenne. I sii mɔk parbiir, ŋaan li poorpo ki i parbiir maŋ tan sa tunn ki teen parpeenn.

ref: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
text: “Li tee kaman nan poo nba tuu yaa wun mar, ki mɔk parbiir nee, kimaan u fara yoo baara, ŋaan u-i taŋŋi mar bik yoonba, u ji tuu tamm u fara na poe, ŋaan ji mɔk parpeenn nan wa mar bik tingbouŋ ni na paak.

ref: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
text: Li tan sii tee i mun nnee; mɔtana ya mɔk parbiir na, ŋaan n bia tan sa laiwa, ki i para tan sa penn bonchiann, ki sɔɔ kaa ki tan sa nyinn parpeenn maŋ i ni.

ref: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: “Li yoo-i taŋŋi baar, i tan kan boin buboiti, kimaan i tan sii mi li paak. Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, linba kur ki i mei n Baa Yennu nan n sann ni, u sa turiiwa.

ref: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
text: Ŋaan nan mɔtana, i daa ki mi miarɔ bonsiar nan n sann ni. Yii meiɔ, ki i sa la, ki fit lian la parpeenn ki saa yakir. Linba tan sa baar labaar

ref: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
text: “N poŋ tuu yiini barjokite, ŋaan yoo baat ki n ji tan kii yiini barjokiti, ŋaan n tan sii piak ki yentir i numm poe ki jikit saa n Baa Yennu po.

ref: You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you.
text: Ŋaan li yoo-i taŋŋi baar, i tuu sii meiɔ nan n sann ni. Li bia tan kan kpaa talasi nan ki n gaar i paak ki miarɔ ki turiini,

ref: God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him.
text: kimaan n Baa Yennu mɔŋ mɔŋ looni; ki wa looni maŋ paak tee nnee, kimaan ya loonin ki bia teen yada nan n nyii n Baa maŋ boore na.

ref: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
text: N din nyii n Baa maŋ peŋe ki sik tingbouŋ na ni, ki mɔtana n ji nyikit tingbouŋ maŋe na, ŋaan ŋmat u peŋ.”

ref: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
text: Ŋanne ki u poorpoweiteeb na yetɔ a “Mɔtana a ji kpan piak yentir ti numm poe na. A ji ki yiint barjokit kaa.

ref: At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.”
text: Mɔtana ti ji bann nan a mi bonsiar kur, ki poŋ bia mi ti buboit ŋaan ki ti daa ki boiani. Li paake teen ki ti teen yada nan a nyii Yennu peŋe.”

ref: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
text: Ki Yisa boib a “I ji teenin yada mɔtanawa-aa?

ref: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
text: Yoo baat, ki li poŋ baar gbenna, ki i kur tan sa yat kun, ŋaan ki n tan sa biar n kuukɔɔ. Ŋaan li set ki tee n tan sii kpan be n kuukɔɔ kaa, kimaan min nan n Baa Yennue tan sii be.

ref: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
text: N beti fara na baaru po a i tan fit kii laat parmaasire, kimaan ya gaa n paak na. I tan sii di fara tingbouŋ na ni. Ŋaan yii mɔk parcheenn man, kimaan min ŋarin nyann tingbouŋ nawa.”

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
text: Yisa nba din pak nna maŋ, ŋaan gokit got sanpaapo, ki yet a “N Baa, yoo maŋ baar na. Ŋaant ki min a Bija-n la dontir, ki fit lian te ki a mun-n la dontir,

ref: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
text: kimaan fine turin yikoo niib kur paak, a man fit lian tur niib kur nba ki a turin maŋ manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

ref: Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
text: Ki manfoor maŋe, ki niib sa nakin bann fin nba tee Yennu nan barmɔnii, ki sɔɔ ji kaa; ki bi bia mun sa bann min, Yisa Masia, wunba ki a tumɔ na.

ref: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
text: Toona nba ki a turin a n tun na, n ji tun ki gbenna, ki dont a sann tingbouŋ na ni.

ref: Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
text: N Baa, turimin dontir a boor ni mɔtana, dontbooru nba ki n din laat, ma din be a boor ni ki a daa din ki nan dunia na.

ref: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
text: “Niib nba ki a din gann ki turin na, n te ki bi bannawa. Bi din tee a yabe, ki a din jiib ki turin, ki bi mun sak a maama.

ref: They know that you gave me everything I have.
text: Ki mɔtana bi mi nan bonsiar kur nba ki a turin na, li nyii a boore,

ref: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
text: kimaan n pakib maan nba ki a turin nawa, ki bi mun gaara, ki mɔtana bi ji bann nan n set nyii a peŋe nan mɔnii, ki bi teen yada nan fine tumin.

ref: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
text: “Tɔn, mɔtana n ji meia bi paake na. N ki meia tingbouŋ na niib kur paak kaa, ŋaan mɔtana n meia binba ki a turin na paake, kimaan bi tee a yabe.

ref: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
text: N yab kur tee a yabe; ki a yab kur mun tee n yab; ki bi bia dont n sanne.

ref: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
text: Ki mɔtana n ji baat a boor nie na; n ji kii be tingbouŋ na ni, ŋaan ŋamm ŋarin daa sa biar tingbouŋ na niwa. Tɔn, n Baa, Popeendaanɔ na, i diab fanu nan a sann ni, sann nba ki a din purin na, a bin fit lian taan yomm kaman nan min nan fin nba kpan tee yomm na.

ref: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
text: Ma daa be namm na, n diab fanu nan a sann nba ki a din purin na ni. N baak bi paak, ki bi ni sɔɔ ki teen yanni, see wunba tee Sintaanii yɔɔ na. Wa teen yann na, li tee kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinbae.

ref: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
text: Ki mɔtana n baat a peŋe; ki ma daa be tingbouŋ na ni na, n piak linba na ki teemm, a bin fit lian la n parpeenn na booru ki saa yakir.

ref: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
text: “N pak a maan na turiba. Li paak ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb namm, kimaan bi ki tee tingbouŋ na ni yabi, kaman nan ma mun ki tee tingbouŋ na ni ŋaayɔɔ na.

ref: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
text: N ki meia ma a nyinnib tingbouŋ na ni kaa, ŋaan n meia ma i baak bi paake fanu, ki bonbiiuk daanɔ Sintaanii daa baat ki biiribi.

ref: They don't belong to this world, and neither do I.
text: Ma ki tee tingbouŋ na ŋaayɔɔ na, li mun bia tee nnee, bi ki tee tingbouŋ na ŋaayabi.

ref: Your word is the truth. So let this truth make them completely yours.
text: “Wantib a maan nba set tee barmɔnii na, ki lin teemm popeendamm.

ref: I am sending them into the world, just as you sent me.
text: Fa din tumin tingbouŋ na ni biaŋinba na, nnee ki n mun bia tumm tingbouŋ ni na.

ref: I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
text: Ki bi paake ki n jikit n mɔŋ ki teena na, a bin fit lian teen a tiauŋ yab nan barmɔnii.

ref: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
text: “N ki meia ki teen ŋamm kuukɔɔ kaa; n bia meia ki teen binba tan sa teenin yada bi mɔɔntii paakewa.

ref: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
text: N bia meia ma bi kura-n taan mɔyomme. N Baa, fa be n ni, ki n mun be a ni biaŋinba na, ŋaant ki bi mun-n taan nant mɔyomm ti ni nna; ŋannii sa te ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb-n teen yada nan fine tumin.

ref: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.
text: Popeenn binbeŋ nba ki a turin na, n mun turiba, ki bi sa fit lian taan mɔyomm, kaman nan min nan fin nba tee mɔyomm biaŋinba na.

ref: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
text: N be bi ni, ki a mun be n ni, a bin fit lian taan mɔyomm chib. Ŋannii sa te ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb-n bann nan fine tumin, ki bi sa bann nan a loonib kaman nan fa loon min biaŋinba na.

ref: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
text: “N Baa, a turib mine, ki n loon ki bii taŋŋi be ma tan sii be siaminba, ki bin fit lian la fa dont n sann biaŋinba na, kimaan a din loonime yoo nba ki a daa din ki nan dunia na.

ref: Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me.
text: N Baa, Popeendaanɔ na, tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb ŋarin ki miani, ŋaan min ŋarin mia, ki n poorpoweiteeb na mi nan fine tumin nna.

ref: I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
text: N fiit a binbeŋ ki wanniba, ki bia sii ŋammit ki fiitir a binbeŋ ki wantib, ki jenna, loomm nba ki a loonin na, ki bin fit lian gaar, ki n mun sii fit lian be bi ni.”

ref: One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, “Didn't I see you in the garden with that man?”
text: Ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ daabiyenɔ, ki u ninjae tee wunba ki Piita chɔɔ u tubir ki pot na, ki u boiɔ, “N ki la-a nan Yisa kpaab na ni-ii?”

ref: Jesus again asked, “Who are you looking for?” “We are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,” they answered.
text: Ki Yisa ŋamm boib a “I loon ŋmee?” Ki bi bia yaa a “Yisa nba tee Nasaret nirɔ na.”

ref: This time Jesus replied, “I have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go.
text: Ki Yisa betib a “N poŋ beti a mine na. Li-i tee ki i loonimie, yin ŋaan ki binba be nna na ki bin yaata.”

ref: Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
text: Li yoo maŋ ki Simon Piita din dia jukbanjiak ki nɔɔt, ki chɔɔ Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ daabir niidiitu po tubir ki pot. Daabir maŋ sann din yi Malkus.

ref: The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up.
text: Ŋanne jik ki lanjimanba nan bi yudaanɔ nan Juu teeb yudamm toontunna na soor Yisa, ki lorɔ,

ref: Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in,
text: Ŋaan ki Piita ŋarin biar see nanyer po yaŋŋir tammɔb ni, ki poorpoweitɔlɔɔ maŋ nba ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ din miu na, ki u ŋmat yaŋŋir tammɔb na ni, ki pak nan sapaamɔ nba guu tammɔb na, ki kɔɔn Piita dundɔuŋ na ni.

ref: but asked him, “Aren't you one of that man's followers?” “No, I am not!” Peter answered.
text: Ki sapaamɔ maŋ boi Piita, “A ki tee jɔɔ na poorpoweitɔyenɔ kaa-aa?” Ki Piita jiinɔ a “Aaii! N ki tee u poorpoweitɔyenɔ kaa.”

ref: It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself. Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest (Matthew
text: Waat din be li yoo, ki toontunna na nan lanjimanba na joo sankɔɔna muu, ki see ki nanii, ki Piita tan saan taamm ki bi nanii. Biaŋinba ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ boi Yisa buboit (Matiu

ref: The high priest questioned Jesus about his followers and his teaching.
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ na boi Yisa u poorpoweiteeb nan u wannu na po.

ref: Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.”
text: Ki bee teen ki a boimi? Boin binba gbat linba ki n tuu beerib na. Ŋamm mi ma tuu yeen linba.”

ref: Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus (Matthew
text: Li yooe ki Annas te ki bi jii Yisa ki saan nanɔ Kayafas wunba tee Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ na peŋ; ki Yisa bia lek daa lor nan ŋmiit. Biaŋinba ki Piita bia ŋamm nɔi a u ki mi Yisa (Matiu

ref: It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
text: Kayafas nba din boi Yisa buboit ki gbenn na poorpoe, ki Juu teeb yudamm jiiu ki saan nanɔ tiŋ na yudaanɔ nba ki bi yiu Pailat na ŋaak ni. Li din tee sanyapob nie. Ki Juu teeb yudamm maŋ ki loon ki bin kɔɔ ŋaak maŋ ni, kimaan bi din loon bin dia bi mɔŋe bonŋann Yennu boor ni, a ki bin tan fit lian di yukitgar tiaru jaamm.

ref: Pilate came out and asked, “What charges are you bringing against this man?”
text: Li paak ki Pailat nyii nanyer ki tookib, ki boib a “Jɔɔ na teen bee ki i biirɔ?”

ref: They answered, “He is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.”
text: Ki bi yaa a “U-i bo-i kii tun bonbiiri, ti bo kan baar nanɔ a boor nna.”

ref: And so what Jesus said about his death would soon come true.
text: Yisa nba din yaa a kubooru nba ki u tan sa kpo na, li din set tan tee nnee, ki fit lian gbee.

ref: Pilate then went back inside. He called Jesus over and asked, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
text: Ki Pailat ŋmat ki kɔɔ ŋaak na ni, ŋaan yiin Yisa, ki boiɔ a “A tee Juu teeb kpanbaree?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Are you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?”
text: Ki Yisa jiin ki boiɔ a “Fine daa boin buboit na amii siabe beta n po?”

ref: “You know I'm not a Jew!” Pilate said. “Your own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?”
text: Ki Pailat betɔ, “Mine tee Juu nirɔ-ɔɔ? Li tee a mɔŋ mɔŋ niib nan Yennu jiantu yudamme jiia ki tan turin. A jia biir bee?”

ref: Jesus answered, “My kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.”
text: Ki Yisa yetɔ a “N naan ki tee tingbouŋ na ni yar kaa. N naam-i bo-i tee tingbouŋ nna yarie, n poorpoweiteeb bo sa kɔn n paak, ki Juu teeb yudamm bo kan soorimi. Aaii, n naan ki tee tingbouŋ nna naami.”

ref: “So you are a king,” Pilate replied. “You are saying that I am a king,” Jesus told him. “I was born into this world to tell about the truth. And everyone who belongs to the truth knows my voice.”
text: Ki Pailat boiɔ a “A set tee kpanbare ŋarimii?” Ki Yisa jiin a “Nn, fine yeen a n tee kpanbar. Bi din marin a n wann niib barmɔnii wannue. Na paake ki n baar tingbouŋ na ni; ki sɔɔ kur nba waa barmɔnii wannu maŋ gbia n maan.”

ref: And since I usually set a prisoner free for you at Passover, would you like for me to set free the king of the Jews?”
text: Ŋaan i mɔk siar ki tuu tee binn kur ni, ki n tuu nyinn dansarkɔyenɔ yukitgar tiaru jaamm yoo ki turi. I loon ki n nyinn Juu teeb kpanbar na ki turiinii?”

ref: They shouted, “No, not him! We want Barabbas.” Now Barabbas was a terrorist.
text: Ki bi tian tintianii ki yaa a “Ti ki loon jɔɔ na kaa. Ti loon Barabase, ki a nyinnɔ ki turit.” Ki Barabas din tee fatitɔɔe.

ref: Pilate gave orders for Jesus to be beaten with a whip.
text: Li yoo na ki Pailat te ki bi boo Yisa kpaasir.

ref: The soldiers made a crown out of thorn branches and put it on Jesus. Then they put a purple robe on him.
text: Ki lanjimanba na luu kunkonii fokirik ki yirimɔ, ŋaan gbabinɔ baanmuŋ nna,

ref: They came up to him and said, “Hey, you king of the Jews!” They also hit him with their fists.
text: ki nyi baat ki gbaa u tɔɔnn, ki yeen a “Juu teeb kpanbar, ti foonta,” ŋaan pebitɔ tanpakii.

ref: Once again Pilate went out. This time he said, “I will have Jesus brought out to you again. Then you can see for yourselves that I have not found him guilty.”
text: Ŋanne ki Pailat bia ŋamm ki daa nyii nanyer ki bet yudamm na, “Gotir man, n baat nanɔ i boor, ki yin bann nan n ki la u biiti.”

ref: Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said, “Here is the man!”
text: Ŋanne ki bi baar nan Yisa, niib na boor ni, ki u yir kunkonii fokirik na nan baanmuŋ na. Ki Pailat betib a “Gotir man, nirɔ maŋe na.”

ref: When the chief priests and the temple police saw him, they yelled, “Nail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!” Pilate told them, “You take him and nail him to a cross! I don't find him guilty of anything.”
text: Yennu jiantu yudamm nan bi toontunna nba lau na, ki bi tian tintianii, ki yaa a “Kpaamɔ dapunpunn paak! Kpaamɔ dapunpunn paak!” Ki Pailat betib a “Yimm jiimɔ man ki kpaau dapunpunn paak i mɔŋ, kimaan min ki la u biiti.”

ref: The crowd replied, “He claimed to be the Son of God! Our law says that he must be put to death.”
text: Ki Juu teeb yudamm na jiin yaa a “Ti mɔk sennsau, ki sennu maŋ want nan u kpan lek tee wun kpoe, kimaan u yaa a u tee Yennu Bijae.”

ref: When Pilate heard this, he was terrified.
text: Pailat nba din gbat nna, ki jaŋmaanii ji soorɔ bonchiann;

ref: He went back inside and asked Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus did not answer.
text: ki u ŋmat kɔɔ ŋaan yiin Yisa, ki tan boiɔ a “A nyii lee?” Ŋaan Yisa din ki jiinɔ siari.

ref: “Why won't you answer my question?” Pilate asked. “Don't you know I have the power to let you go free or to nail you to a cross?”
text: Ki Pailat bia boiɔ a “A kan pak jiinimii? A ki mi nan n mɔk yikoo ki sa te ki bin ŋaa-a, ki bia mɔk yikoo ki sa te ki bin kpaa-a dapunpunn paak ki kpianii?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If God had not given you the power, you couldn't do anything at all to me. But the one who handed me over to you did something even worse.”
text: Ki Yisa ji jiinɔ a “Yennu-i bo-i kii chaba yikoo, a bo kii mɔk n yikoo. Li paak, daanɔ nba soorin ki jiin kubina na, u biit yab bonchiann ki gar a yara.”

ref: Then Pilate wanted to set Jesus free. But the crowd again yelled, “If you set this man free, you are no friend of the Emperor! Anyone who claims to be a king is an enemy of the Emperor.”

text: Jɔɔnn ŋanin din wuur niib nan nyume, ŋaan daa ŋannlelee na ni Yennu Seyeeŋ sa gbee yenti.” Biaŋinba ki Yisa doo Yendɔuŋ ni

ref: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”

ref: Jesus said to them, “You don't need to know the time of those events that only the Father controls.
text: Ki Yisa yetib a “Li ki laini nan ki i bann jinjaŋ yoo nba ki n Baa Yennu senn yaa lin tan teeni; ŋɔɔe mɔk u mɔŋ yaak ki senn u yoo.

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
text: Ŋaan yoo nba ki u Seyeeŋ sa sik i paak ki yenti na, i sa la paŋ, ki tan sii tee n siara damm Jerusalem, nan Judea tiŋ kur ni, nan Samaria tiŋ ni, nan tingbouŋ na lokir kur po.”

ref: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
text: Wa pak maan maŋ ki gbenn yoonba, ki bi la ki Yennu te ki u do sanpaapo, ki bi goriiɔ, ki sanpagbann saa dɔkin u paak ki u bot, ki bi ji ki lau.

ref: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
text: Ŋaan ba lek bia see ki kpaa bi nunii ki gorii sanpaapo na, li taakpaak ni ki sɔɔ malakanba bannlee see ki lia tapeenii ki naan nan jab,

ref: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
text: ki boib a “Yimm Galilee teeb, bee ki i see nna ki gorii sanpaapo nna? Yisa na nba nyii i boor ni ki doo Yendɔuŋ ni biaŋinba na, u bia tan sa nyi Yendɔuŋ maŋ ni ki jen sik kaman nan ya goriiɔ ki u do sanpaapo biaŋinba nee.” Wunba gaar Judas yiar labaar

ref: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
text: Ba baar Jerusalem yoonba ki bi saan kɔɔ ŋaak nba ki bi tuu be na ni ki doo ditɔɔtuk paak. text: ki bi kura tuu yɔɔ lakint nan bi leeb ki din pukin poosiab nan Yisa naa, Mari, nan Yisa waarii, ki bi pik mei Yennu nan dudukyent kuukɔɔ ki ki naki.

ref: He said: My friends, long ago by the power of the Holy Spirit, David said something about Judas, and what he said has now happened. Judas was one of us and had worked with us, but he brought the mob to arrest Jesus.
text: Ŋaan Yisa din gann timm nan ŋɔɔe a tii tuun Yennu toonn nba ki ti tuun na, ki u din tee ti kann ni niyenɔ.

ref: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
text: (“Judas toonbiit nyɔɔt likirii nba ki u la nee, ki u jii ki daa kpaab; leŋe ki u bia baa ki put poor, ki u nɔɔt kur nyii.

ref: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
text: “Tɔn, li sɔb yanii gbouŋ nba be Yennu gbouŋ nɔɔk ni a ‘Ŋaant ki u ŋaak-n set langbouŋ ki sɔɔ daa kɔɔ leŋi.’ Ki li bia jen sɔb a ‘Li ŋan ki sɔɔ-n gaar u toonn yiar na.’

ref: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
text: “Li paak amii ti loon ki tin gann niyenɔe ki pukinɔ ti po, ki wun teen Yisa kuunfiiru siara daanɔ, ki li ŋan ki wuu tee timm nba daan tuu yɔɔ lin nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa na ni yenɔ, yoo nba ki Jɔɔnn din wurɔ Yennu nyunwuru mɔkmɔk ki tan baar yoo nba ki Yisa nyii ti boor ni, ki Yennu donnɔ Yendɔuŋ ni na.”

ref: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
text: Ŋanne ki bi nyinn jab bannlee; Josef wunba ki bi din yiu Basabas na , nan Matias,

ref: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
text: ŋaan ji miar Yennu a “Ti Yomdaanɔ, fine mi sɔɔ kur par ni, ki li paak, ti Yomdaanɔ, wantit jab bannlee na ni, wunba ki a gann,

ref: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
text: ki wun teen toomii ki gaar a toonn na yiar nba ki Judas nyik ŋaan saan saa be siaminba ŋan nanɔ na.”

ref: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
text: Tɔn, jaamm nba ki bi yir Pentekost daare baar; daar maŋe ki Yisaweira kura lakin taan leeb boyennkɔɔ ni.

ref: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.

text: ki Yennu Seyeeŋ gbee yent sɔɔ kur ni, ki bi piin piak barganii kaman nan Yennu Seyeeŋ nba wannib biaŋinba.

text: Ki Juu teeb nba tee yentina mun din be Jerusalem li yoo, binba nyii tinii kur po tingbouŋ na ki baar leŋ.

ref: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
text: Ba gbat fuut maŋ yoonba, ki nibur tikir leŋ, ki li teemm yaarlituk, kimaan Yisaweira maŋ piak barganii sɔɔ kur maan ni ki u gbia.

ref: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
text: Ki li teemm bakitnauŋ nan yaarlituk, ki bi boi bi leeb a “Niib na kur nba piak na ki tee Galilee teeb kaa-aa?

ref: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
text: ki li teen nlee ki ti kura tan jia gbia ki bi piak ti mɔŋ mɔŋ maan nna?

ref: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
text: Ti siab nyii Patia nan Media nan Elam nan Mesopotamia nan Judea nan Kapadosia nan Pontus nan Asia,

ref: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
text: ki ti siab tee Juu teeb, ti siab mun bia tee boorganu nba din lebit bi jiantu ki waa Juu teeb Yennu; ki ti yemm mun bia nyii Kreet nan Arabia, ŋaan ki ti kura lek gbia ki bi piak ti mɔŋ mɔŋ maan ni, ki piak dont Yennu sann nan u bakitnauŋ toonjaana nba ki u tun.”

ref: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
text: Ki li bakit ŋaan yaar litib ki ŋmat bi yan, ki bi boi bi leeb a “Linba na paak tee bee?”

ref: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
text: Ki siab mun bia laa ki sarikit Yisaweira maŋ a “Bi nyuu daame ki yib.” Piita mɔɔntii po

text: Ŋanne ki Piita nan u leeb piik nan yenɔkɔɔ na fiir ki set nibur na tɔɔnn ni; ki Piita pak sanpaapowa a “Yimm Juu teeb, nan yimm nba kur be Jerusalem na, gbiintir man ma yaa n paki linba na, kimaan n mɔk siare ki loon ki i bann.

ref: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
text: Ya dukii nan ti yibe na, ŋaan ti ki yib kaa, kimaan li daa tee sanyayendaanne.

ref: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,

text: Ki li yoo maŋ n tan sii sikint n Seek maŋ n daaba nba tee jab nan binba tee poob paak, ki bi mun bia tan sii sɔkint n maan.

ref: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.

text: ki yennu tan sa kpint ki bɔnn, ki ŋmaarik mun tan sa kpint ki mɔnn kaman nan sɔn na. Li tan sa teen nneewa, ki ti Yomdaanɔ-n tan jen sik tingbouŋ na ni ki bu niib buut; ki u jenu daar maŋ tan sii tee dajaanne;

ref: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
text: ki sɔɔ kur nba tan miarɔ nan u sann ni sa la tinnu.’ ”

ref: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
text: Ŋanne ki Piita bia ŋamm yet a “Yimm Israel teeb, gbiintir man linba ki n yaa n beti ki jikit saa Yisa nba tee Nasaret nirɔ na po; i mɔŋ mɔŋ mi nan Yennu te ki u tuu tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana nan nyina i ni, ki li set want nan mɔnii nan Yennue tumɔ.

ref: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
text: Ŋaan Yennu mi linba yaa lin tan teen. Li paak, ki u dɔŋ lor lor ki kubinɔ i nuu ni, ki i te ki biitdamm kpaa tabinɔ dapunpunn paak ki kpiiu, ki li want nan yimme set kpiiu.

ref: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
text: “Wa daan kpo ki daa be kuun nii ni na, ki Yennu fatɔ ki fiinɔ kuun ni, kimaan kuun ki fit diau.

ref: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
text: Kimaan David din bunt buntir ki jikit saa Yisa po ki yaa a ‘N mi nan n Yomdaanɔ Yennu yɔɔ be nanin, ki u bia see n poorpo ki guun ki n ki laat daŋi.

ref: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.

text: kimaan u tan kan nyik n seek ki lin kpakin kpeentiŋi. N tee u popeendaanɔe, ki u kan sak ki n gbanant-n tan bati;

text: ki u bia te ki n bann manfoor sɔnii, ki tan sa baar kii be u tɔɔnn ni kii mɔk parpeenn bonchiann.’ ”

ref: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
text: Ki Piita bia ŋamm yet yaa a “N naa waas, n yaa n pak yent i nummpoe ki jikit saa ti yejasaakɔɔ David po. U din kpo, ki bi piiu, ki u kaauk daa be ti boor nna na.

ref: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
text: U din tee Yennu sɔkiniie, ki din mi nan Yennu senn mɔsonn ki turɔ, ki yet yaa a li-i tee ki u kpo, u tan sa jii u yaaboon-yenɔkɔɔ ki dinnɔ u mɔŋ naan.

ref: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
text: David din mi linba ki Yennu yaa wun tan teen. Li paak ki u din pak Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia kuunfiiru po, ki yet a Yennu Niganntɔɔ tan kan kpakin kpeentiŋ, ki u gbanant bia kan bati.”

ref: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
text: Ki Piita bia ŋamm yet a “Yisa nba nee ki Yennu daan fiinɔ kuun ni, ki ti kura na mun tee u kuunfiiru maŋ siara damm.

ref: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
text: Ki Yennu bia donnɔ a wun kar u niidiitu po. Wa kar yoonba ki u gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki yaa a u tan sa turit na, ki sikin ti ni bonchiann. Tɔn, mɔtana, li Seyeeŋe ki i laat ki bia gbia ti ni na.

ref: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
text: David nba ŋarin din kpo na, u din ki doo Yendɔuŋ ni nan u gbananti. Tɔn, u buntir maŋ din ki want u mɔŋ donnu po kaa, ŋaan u din want Yisa poe, ki din yet a ‘Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu yet n Yomdaanɔ Masia a Karin n niidiitu po

ref: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
text: mɔkmɔk nan ma tan sa jii a datai ki teemm a taakpakta.’ ”

ref: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
text: Niib na nba gbat nna maŋ, ki bi para ni biir bonchiann. Ki bi boi Piita nan u leeb na a “Ei! ti naa waas, ki ti sa teen nlee?”

ref: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
text: Ki Piita betib a “See ki i kura-n nyik i biit, ki tin wuri Yennu nyunwuru nan Yisa Masia sann ni, ki Yennu-n nyik i biit ki chabi, ki sa turi u piinii nba tee u Seyeeŋ na;

ref: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
text: kimaan u poŋ din senn mɔsonn a u sa jii u Seyeeŋ na ki tur yimm nan i waas, nan binba kur be banfɔka, yimm nba kur ki u tan sa yiini ki yin sak u mɔb.”

ref: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
text: Li poorpoe ki Piita pak mɔmaan bonchiann ki nyinn siara, ŋaan bia kpaamm a “Hei! Nyint i mɔŋ man nan Yennu tubdatu nba baat mɔtana nibiit na paak na.”

ref: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
text: Ki bi ŋammit ki lek tumii ki bant toomiinba maŋ wannu, ki bi jii bi yoo ki taan dia leeb nan mɔtaauk, ki lakint ki di jeet, ki bia tikii mei Yennu. Yisaweira benu po
