text: Ki jijeet ji din mɔk sɔɔ kur; ki toomiinba na tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana bonchiann nan Yennu paŋ ni.

ref: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
text: Ki Yisaweira maŋ kura bia lek dia leeb nan mɔtaauk, ki chentir bi bona ki teen bi leeb,

ref: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
text: ki tuu kɔi bi faa ki jikit li likirii ki toor bi leeb, ki jaŋŋit kaman nan sɔɔ kur nba loon biaŋinba.

ref: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
text: Daar kur bi bia lek tuu taan leebe Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni ki jiantir Yennu, ŋaan ji nyi ki lakint baa jedikpaguur bi mɔŋ ŋei ni, ki saa di jeet nan leeb nan parpeenn, ki ninfɔksai kaa nan bi leebi.

ref: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
text: Ki yoo kur ki bi piak dont Yennu sann, ki sɔɔ kur par mei bi paak. Ki daar kur ki ti Yomdaanɔ bia ŋammit ki te ki niib laat tinnu ki pukii bi paak.

ref: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
text: Dasiar yenlekir dinn ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn din tan saa Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni Yenmeiyoo.

ref: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
text: Ŋanne ki bi saa yaa bin kɔɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak tammɔb nba ki bi din yi Tammɔŋaŋ na, ki la jasɔɔ nna ki u kar leŋ, ki poŋ tee wɔbik u maaru niwa; ki daar yejaŋaakur ki bi din tuu jiiu ki baar nanɔ tammɔb maŋ boor ni, a wuu mei likirii niib nba baat Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak maŋ ni na.

ref: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
text: Yoo nba ki u tan la Piita nan Jɔɔnn ki bi yaa bin kɔɔ na, ki u miarib likirii.

ref: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
text: Ki bi jiant ki goriiɔ sirrr; ki Piita tan yetɔ a “I goriit.”

ref: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
text: Ŋanne ki u ji nur u nunii ki goriib, ki daan mi a u sa gaar siare bi peŋ.

ref: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
text: Ŋaan ki Piita betɔ a “Ti lek ki mɔk likirii ki sa turani, ŋaan ta mɔk linbae ki ti yaa tin tura. Mii tura mɔb Yisa Masia nba tee Nasaret nirɔ na sann ni, fiit kii somm,”

ref: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
text: ŋaan soor u niidiitu po nuu ki fiinɔ. Li taakpaak ni ki jɔɔ na taa kakit yiama,

ref: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
text: ki u tab ki fiir set, ki ji piin somm ki lin, ki tan weib ki kɔɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak maŋ ni, ki yuuk ki mɔbit nan parpeenn, ki piak dont Yennu sann.

ref: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
text: Ki niib kur lau ki u somm ki piak dont Yennu sann.

ref: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
text: Ba ji din bann nan ŋɔɔe tee miatɔɔ nba tuu kaar ki mei niib likirii Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak tammɔŋaŋ nna, ki li ji yaar ŋaan litib nan wa paak na paak. Biaŋinba ki Piita mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii

ref: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Ki wɔbik maŋ puur wɔb Piita nan Jɔɔnn; ki li teen niib na kura bakitnauŋ bonchiann, ki bi chiar saan lakin bi boor ni. Ba lakin sian na yi Solomon barintir ni.

ref: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
text: Piita nba la niib na yoonba ki u yet a “Yimm Israel teeb, bee ki li yaar ŋaan liti nan nirɔ na? ki bee ki i barii timm nna? Ta te ki jawɔbik na fiir somm na, i dukii mi a li tee ti mɔŋ paŋ amii ti mɔŋ yentinu paakee?

ref: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
text: Yennu nba din tee Abraham nan Aisak nan Jakɔb nan ti yejaleeb Yennu na, ŋɔɔe tee daanɔ nba donn u Toontunnɔ Yisa ki turɔ u mɔŋ yabint. Yisa maŋe ki i jiiu ki teenɔ ti yudamm nuu ni na, ki Pailat din poŋ bikin sinsinn a wun ŋaau, ki i bia yêtɔ Pailat boor ni.

ref: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
text: Ŋɔɔe lek tee Yennu nirɔ, ki bia jenna tee Popeendaanɔ, ŋaan ki i lek yêtɔ, ŋaan bia miar Pailat a wun nyinn nikpiiruk ki turi.

ref: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
text: Li paak, yimme te ki bi kpii Manfoturtɔɔ maŋ; ŋaan ki Yennu fiinɔ kuun ni, ki timme tee linba na siara damm.

ref: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
text: “Yisa maŋ sann paŋ nba ki ti yi nee te ki jɔɔ na paak. Wɔbik na tee wunba ki i poŋ tuu miu ki bia laatɔe, ŋaan ki u tan paak na, kimaan wa teen Yisa sann yada na paak. Wa teen Yisa yada na paake te ki u paak ki see i numm nna.

ref: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
text: “Tɔn, n naa waas, n mi nan yimm nan i yudamm nba daan te ki bi kpii Yisa na, i daan ki bann ya tun linba maŋ paaki.

ref: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
text: “Li paak, i nyik i biit man, kii waa Yennu sɔnu, ki wun nyik chab i toonbiit,

ref: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
text: ki u sa turi foon, ki bia tan sa tun Yisa Masia i boor ni, wunba ki u poŋ gannɔ ki turi na;

ref: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
text: ŋaan Yisa maŋ lek sii be Yendɔuŋ ni, ki Yennu-n tan ŋamm bona kura, kaman nan wa din te ki u popeenn sɔkiniinba nba din be yaayoo ni na yet biaŋinba na, ki bi mun pak turit na.

ref: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
text: “Ŋann maŋe ki Moses mun din yet a ‘Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu sa turi u sɔkiniisɔɔ, a ki u sii tee i mɔŋ nirɔ, kaman nan wa nyinn min biaŋinba ki teenin u sɔkinii i boor nna na. Gbiintirɔ man ki tun linba kur ki u sa pak turi;

ref: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
text: ki daanɔ nba ki gbat sɔkinii maŋ maami, Yennu sa nyinn li yɔɔ i sinsuuk ni ki biirɔ.’

ref: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
text: “Yennu sɔkinii Samuel nan sɔkiniinba nba wei u paak ki din piak Yennu maan, bi kura din want linba tan sa teen Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia ti yoo nee.

ref: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
text: Yennu mɔsona nba ki u din senn ki tur u sɔkiniinba maŋ, li mɔsona tee i yare; ki lor nba ki u din lor nan ti yejamm na, li lor mun bia tee i yare, kimaan u din yet Abraham a ‘A yaaboonyenɔ nie ki tingbouŋ na teeb kura tan sa la n mɔsonŋann.’

ref: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
text: Li paak ki Yennu gann u Toontunnɔ Masia maŋ ki tumɔ i boor ni sinsinn, a wun te ki i kur-n nyik i biit ki la mɔsonŋann maŋ.”

ref: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
text: Yoo nba ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn daa din see ki piak teen niib maŋ na, ki Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak lanjimanba yudaanɔ nan Sajusiinba nan Yennu jiantu yudamm kpir baar Piita-nba boor ni,

ref: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
text: kimaan bi wutoa doo nan Piita nan Jɔɔnn, a bi want niib a Yisa daan fiir kuun ni paak, ki li want nan kpeemm tan sa fiir kuun ni ki jen bi manfoor ni biak.

ref: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
text: Li paak ki bi soorib ki kɔɔmm dansarik a lin yenta, kimaan yoo poŋ gara.

ref: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
text: Ŋaan ki niib bonchiann nba din gbat Piita maan maŋ teen Yisa yada ki teen Yisaweira. Ki jab nba teen u weira na kann din tee kaman nan tusa ŋaŋŋmu na.

ref: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
text: La tan yent sanyiɔk ni, ki Juu teeb yudamm nan saakab nan sennu wannteeb lakin bi tituk kaanu ni Jersualem;

ref: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
text: ki bi lakin nan Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ Annas, nan Kayafas, nan Jɔɔnn nan Aleksander nan jiantu yudaanɔ maŋ ŋaatesiab,

ref: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
text: ki te ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn fiir ki set bi sinsuuk ni, ŋaan ki bi boib a “Panboor bee ki i mɔki? Ki ŋmee sann nie ki i fiin wɔbik nba na?”

ref: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu Seyeeŋ fiir Piita ni, ki u jiin ki yetib a “Yimm chanbaanba nan saakab, gbiintir man,

ref: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
text: li-i tee ki i boit dinna na ki jikit saa toonŋann nba ki ti tun ki tur wɔbik na, nan wa la laafia na paakie,

ref: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
text: ŋann li ŋan ki i kura-n bann, ki Israel teeb kur-n mun bann nan Nasaret nirɔ Yisa Masia sann paake ki jɔɔ maŋ la laafia ki see i numm ni na. Li Yisae ki i daan te ki bi kpaau dapunpunn paak ki kpiiu, ŋaan ki Yennu fiinɔ kuun ni na.

ref: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
text: Ŋɔɔe tee daanɔ nba ki Yennu gbouŋ piak u po na, a ‘Tann nba ki yimm tanmaara yêtɔ na, a ŋannii set tee lɔtik ni tanŋann.’

ref: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
text: Ki sɔɔ sann ji kaa tingbouŋ na ni ki Yennu tur niib a bin la tinnu, see Yisa kuukɔɔ.”

ref: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
text: Tituk teeb maŋ nba din la Piita nan Jɔɔnn parcheenn nba ki bi mɔk na, ki bia bann nan Piita nan Jɔɔnn tee damm nba ki mi gbouŋ bonŋann na, li din yaar ŋaan litibe, ki bi ji bann nan bi tuu tee Yisa weinanleebe.

ref: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
text: Ba la jɔɔ nba ki bi won te ki u paak na, ki u see Piita nan Jɔɔnn boor ni na, bi ji din ki mɔk mɔb nba sa pak ki biiribi.

ref: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
text: Ŋanne ki bi din ŋaan ki bi daa nyii bi lakinu diiuk maŋ ni daann, ŋaan ki bi biar faa bi leeb labaar,

ref: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
text: ki boi bi leeb, a “Ti sa teen jab na nlee na? kimaan sɔɔ kur nba be Jerusalem poŋ mi yaarlituk toonn nba ki bi tun maŋ na. Li paak nna ti ji kan fit nɔi;

ref: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
text: ŋaan ŋaant man ki tin kpaamm kpaanii, ki bi ji daa mi ki yi Yisa sann ki teen niibi, ki Yisa maan maŋ ji daa bia yat ki saa niib ni.”

ref: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
text: Ŋanne ki bi yiimm, ki bi jen kɔɔ diiuk na ni; ki bi betib a “I mi man ki i ji mi ki jii Yisa sann ni ki pak ki wann sɔɔ jik.”

ref: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
text: Ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn jiin ki betib a “Ki i yaa mɔŋ mɔŋ po ŋarin, i dukin nan lanne tokin Yennu boor ni? ti ji sak i mɔb ŋaan nyik Yennu mɔb amii nlee?

ref: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
text: kimaan timm kan fit ki nyik pinpauk linba po ki ti la ki bia gbati.”

ref: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
text: Jɔɔ nba ki bi te ki u paak na bina din gar kaman nan binpiinna nawa. Biaŋinba ki bi miar Yennu parcheenn po

ref: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
text: Ba ŋaa Piita nan Jɔɔnn yoonba, ki bi ŋmat kun bi leeb peŋ, ki saa yetib linba ki Yennu jiantu yudamm nan saakab maŋ pakib.

ref: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: Ba mun gbat nna maŋ ki bi kur taan leeb nan dudukyent kuukɔɔ, ki mei Yennu a “Ti Yabint Yomdaanɔ, fine din nan sanpaak nan tiŋ na, nan mɔkgbeŋŋa nan linba kur be leŋ,

ref: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
text: ki a Seyeeŋ maŋ nie ki a din wann ti yeja David wunba din tee a toontunnɔ, ki u din bunt buntir ki yet a ‘Binba ki tee Israel teeb na pir nan wutoa, ki Israel teeb lorin lor yanna,

ref: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
text: ki tingbouŋ na kpanbara nan yudamm taan leeb ki took a bin kɔn nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nan u Niganntɔɔ Masia na.’

ref: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
text: “Li tee barmɔniie nan Herod nan Ponshus Pailat nan binba ki tee Israel teeb na nan Israel teeb daan set lakin doo na ni, ki lor lora a bin kpi a popeenn toontunnɔ Yisa, wunba ki a din gann ki sennɔ na.

ref: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
text: Ŋanne ki bi lakin, a bin tun linba kur ki a poŋ din dukin a yanbɔɔt ni nan a paŋ, ki yaa li tan sa teen na.

ref: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
text: Tɔn, mɔtana, ti Yomdaanɔ, gbiintir niib na nba fɔ kɔnfɔɔt nan nunmɔnn ti paak na, ki ŋaan ki timm nba tee a daaba na-ii piak a maan nan parcheenn bonchiann,

ref: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
text: ŋaan tant a nuu ki te ki baatib-n la paaku, ki ŋaan ki tii tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana, nan a popeenn toontunnɔ Yisa sann paŋ ni.”

ref: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
text: Ba gbenn Yenmiaru na yoonba, ki siaminba po ki bi tikir na ji jek, ki bi kura gbee yent nan Yennu Seyeeŋ, ki ji piin piak Yennu maan nan parcheenn. Ba chentir bi bona ki teen leeb biaŋinba

ref: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
text: Li yoo na ki Yisaweira maŋ kura taan leeb nan dudukyent kuukɔɔ, ki chentir bi bona ki teen bi leeb. Bi sɔɔ din ki yaa a u yen siar nan u mɔŋi.

ref: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
text: Li yoo na jik ki toomiinba maŋ din nyint ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa kuunfiiru siara, ki siara maŋ din mɔk paŋ bonchiann, ki Yennu sommir bonchiann din be bi kur ni.

ref: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
text: Ki linba na sɔɔ din kaa bi ni ki pɔt siari, kimaan binba din mɔk bi mɔŋ mɔŋ kpaant nan bi ŋei din tuu kɔie, ki gaar li likirii ki baar nann,

ref: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
text: ki tan teen toomiinba na, ki bi chentir ki teen bi leeb nan ba loon biaŋinba.

ref: Joseph was one of the followers who had sold a piece of property and brought the money to the apostles. He was a Levite from Cyprus, and the apostles called him Barnabas, which means “one who encourages others.”
text: ki u din tan kɔi u kpaab, ki gaar li likirii ki baar nann, ki tan tur toomiinba na.

ref: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
text: ŋaan paat likirii maŋ paak ki bɔr, ki u ŋaapoo mi li po, ŋaan jii linba tenn na, ki baar nann ki tan tur toomiinba maŋ.

ref: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
text: Ki Piita boiɔ a “Ananias, bee ki a ŋaan ki Sintaanii kɔɔ ki yent a par ni, ki a faar faak ki loon ki a kpann Yennu Seyeeŋ, ki paat likirii nba ki a daan kɔi a kpaab na paaki?

ref: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
text: Fa daan daa ki kɔi a kpaab maŋ na, li daan ki tee a yar kaa-aa? Ki fa bia daan kɔir na, li likirii maŋ bia daan ki be a yent ni kaa-aa? Ki bee teen ki a dukin a par ni ki tun linba na? A ki faar faak maŋ ki paan nirɔ kaa, ŋaan a faar ki paan Yennue.”

ref: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
text: Ananias nba gbat nna maŋ yoonba, ki u kpo ki baa li yoo na. Ki jaŋmaanchiauŋ kɔɔ binba kur gbat u kuun maŋ po.

ref: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
text: Ki naasimm fiir ki jiiu ki pɔbinɔ chinchenn ni, ki jiiu nyii nanɔ ki saa piiu.

ref: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
text: Ŋanne ki li yann waaminna, ki u ŋaapoo baar ki kɔɔ, ŋaan daa ki bann linba teeni.

ref: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
text: Ki Piita bia boiɔ a “Betirin, li set tee barmɔniie nan fin nan a sɔrɔ kpaab likirii nba ki i gaar na kurii na-aa?” Ki u jiin a “Nn, li kurii na.”

ref: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
text: Ki Piita boiɔ a “Bee ki fin nan a sɔrɔ taan mɔb, ki lor lor a i bikin ti Yomdaanɔ Seyeeŋi? Tɔn, naasimm nba jii a sɔrɔ ki saan ki saa piiu na, ŋammii kɔɔ tammɔb nna, ki bi mun bia sa jiia nnee ki saan piia.”

ref: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text: Li taakpaak ni, ki u kpo ki baa, ki naasimm na kɔɔ ki la ki u kpoa, ki bi bia jiiu ki nyii nanɔ ki saa piiu ki kpianɔ nan u sɔrɔ.

ref: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
text: Li yoo na ki jaŋmaanchiauŋ soor Yisaweira kur nan binba kur gbat maan maŋ po.

ref: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Li yoo na ki toomiinba na tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana niib na ni bonchiann. Ki Yisaweira kura tuu taan leeb Yennu Yennu ŋasaakak, Solomon barintir boor.

ref: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
text: Binba ki tee Yisaweira na din ki mɔk par nba ki bi sa lakin nammi, ŋaan niib din piak dont Yisaweira na bonchiann,

ref: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
text: ki jab nan poob bonchiann din teen yada ki pukin Yisaweira na po.

ref: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
text: Ŋanne ki niib din nyi nan baatib, ki dɔɔntib gadɔi nan dundɔɔnii paak sanjokpinii ni, a Piita-i taŋŋi baar ki gaar yoonba, ki u yunyuŋ-n lek baa bi paak ki bin la laafia.

ref: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
text: Ki niib nba din be Jerusalem digbanbis ni mun jikit baatib, nan binba ki narinbiit daamiib na, ki baat namm, ki bi kura din laat laafia. Toomiinba na fara dinu po

ref: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
text: Li paak ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ nan u weinanleeb kur, binba tee Sajusiinba na, din fiin punponn bonchiann nan toomiinba na, ki din te ki bi saan

ref: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
text: ki saa soor toomiinba maŋ ki kɔɔmm dansarik.

ref: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
text: Ki li nyiɔk maŋe ki Yennu malaka sik ki loot dansarik tammɔi, ki nyinn toomiinba maŋ, ŋaan betib a

ref: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
text: “I saa man Yennu ŋasaakak ni, ki saa set, ŋaan mɔɔnt ki tur niib manfoor na maan kura.”

ref: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
text: Ba gbat u maan maŋ, ki bi saan ki saa kɔɔ Yennu ŋasaakak maŋ ni, sanyapob ni, ki ji piin ki want niib maan maŋ. Ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ nan u weinanleeb na din yiin Israel teeb yudamm kura, ki lakimm tituk kaanu, ki bi tan tun lanjimanba a bin nyinn toomiinba maŋ ki baar namm na.

ref: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
text: Ŋanne ki bi saan dansarik maŋ ni ki saa got, ki ki labi, ki ŋmat tituk maŋ ni ki saa betib a

ref: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
text: “Ta baar dansarik ni yoonba, ti la ki tammɔi kpare kenkenkema, ŋaan ki dansarguura na be tammɔi ni ki guu, ki ti loot tammɔi na, ŋaan ki ki la sɔɔ dansarik diiuk maŋ ni.”

ref: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
text: Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak lanjimanba yudaanɔ nan jiantu yudamm nba gbat nna maŋ, ki li teemm yaarlituk nan toomiinba na botu maŋ paak, ki bi ji biris linba sa teen.

ref: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
text: Ki taan sɔɔ ki jasɔɔ nna kɔɔ, ki yetib a “Gotir man, jab nba ki i won kɔɔmm dansarik wonn na, bi see Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni ki want niib Yennu maan.”

ref: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
text: Li yoo na ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na jii u jab, ki bi saan ki saa nyinn toomiinba maŋ ki jen namm, ŋaan bi ki nyimm nan paŋ kaa, kimaan bi tiin jaŋmaanii a niib na tan sa mann jaatib tana.

ref: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
text: Ba baar namm yoonba, ki bi te ki bi saan set tituk teeb na sinsuuk ni. Ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ na yetib a

ref: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
text: “Ti daan turi mɔb nan nunmɔnn, ki yaa a i daa want niib Yisa sann ni, ŋaan gotir ya tan tun linba na, ya jii i wannu maŋ ki yat Jerusalem kur po, ŋaan ji loon a i teent Yisa kpiira.”

ref: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
text: Ki Piita nan toomiinba leeb na jiin ki yetib a “See ki ti lek-ii saak Yennu mɔb ki gar nirɔ mɔb.

ref: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
text: Yisa maŋe ki i daan poŋ kpaa tabinɔ dapunpunn paak ki kpiiu na, ŋaan Yennu nba tee ti yejamm Yennu na, ŋɔɔe fiin Yisa kuun ni,

ref: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
text: ki jiiu donnɔ ki kaanɔ u niidiitu po, ki teenɔ Yudaanɔ nan Tinntɔɔ, a Israel teeb-ii nyik bi biit, ki wun nyik bi toonbiit ki chabib.

ref: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
text: Timm nan Yennu Seyeeŋe tee Yisa kuunfiiru maŋ siara damm; ki Yennu jii u Seek maŋ ki tur wunba saak u mɔb.”

ref: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
text: Yudamm maŋ nba gbat nna maŋ, ki bi wutoa fiir, ki bi ji yaa bin kpi toomiinba maŋ.

ref: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
text: Ŋaan Farisiisɔɔ nna din be tituk na ni, ki u sann yi Gamaliel, ki u din tee sennu wanntɔɔe, ki niib kur din teeu nibaakir; ki u din fiir ki set, ŋaan yet a bin daa nyinn toomiinba maŋ nanyer po waan;

ref: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
text: ŋaan bet tituk kaanu damm maŋ a “Hei, Israel teeb, i mi man nan ya yaa i teen niib na linba na fan.

ref: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
text: Ki ŋann tan gar nna, ki Judas, wunba din tee Galilee nirɔ mun din nyii ki set nikant yoo na, ki want u mɔŋ, ki din tunn niib bonchiann yan ki bi waau; ki niib bia mun din tan kpiiu, ki u jɔɔmu na kur yat.

ref: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
text: Mɔtanara, kpan ŋaant Yisaweira maŋ man, ki daa teeb siari; ki li-i tee ki bi dudukit nan bi toona na-i tee nisaarik yarie, li tan sa gbenna.

ref: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki li tee Yennu yarie, i kan fit ki nyannibi. I sa la ki i mann kpet ki kɔn nan Yennue gbaa.” Ŋanne ki tituk teeb maŋ ji sak Gamaliel mɔb na,

ref: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
text: ki yiin toomiinba maŋ ki kɔɔmm, ki te ki bi boob kpaasir, ŋaan ŋaab, ŋaan turib mɔb a “I mi man ki i ji kii yi Yisa sann maŋ.”

ref: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
text: Ŋann maŋe ki toomiinba maŋ nyii tituk kaanu na ni nan parpeenn, Yennu nba la ki bi jaŋ nan feidinu, Yisa sann paak na.

ref: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Ki daar kur ki bi saa Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni, ŋaan bia lin niib ŋei ni, ki want niib a Yisa tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masiae, ki bia mɔɔntir u barŋanii na, ki ki nyikiti.

ref: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
text: Li poorpoe ki Yisaweira ŋammit yabitir ki pukii, ki bunburimii tan baar Juu teeb nba piak Griik maan, nan Juu teeb nba piak bi mɔŋ maan na sinsuuk ni; kimaan binba piak Griik maan na din yet a bi-i toor jeet daar kur, bi pakɔi ki laat bi tora nan leeb na.

ref: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
text: Ŋanne ki toomiinba piik nan bannlee na yiin Yisaweira maŋ kura, ki tan yetib a “N naa waas, li ki ŋan ki tin nyik Yennu maan mɔɔntii ŋaan biar jeet toru ni.

ref: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
text: Li paak, gotir ki gann jab bannlore i sinsuuk ni, binba ki i mi nan bi binbeŋ ŋan, ki bi bia mantik gbee nan Yennu Seyeeŋ nan yanfantir, ki tin teemm toona na yudamm;

ref: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
text: ŋaan ki timm-n jii ti mɔŋ yoo ki teen Yenmiaru ni, nan Yennu maan wannu ni, ki daa naki.”

ref: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
text: Ki mɔtaauk niib na kur ji din mɔk parpeenn nan toomiinba maan maŋ paak. Ŋanne ki bi gann Stiifen, wunba mantik gbee nan Yennu Seyeeŋ nan yada na, nan Filip, nan Prokorus, nan Nikanor, nan Timon, nan Pamenas, nan Nikolaus, wunba din nyii Antiok, ki ki tee Juu nirɔ, ŋaan din kpint u jiantu ki waa Juu teeb jiantu na.

ref: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
text: Mɔtaauk niib na nba gannib yoonba, ki bi te ki bi saan set toomiinba na tɔɔnn po, ki toomiinba maŋ miar Yennu ki turib ŋaan jii bi nii ki yii bi paak.

ref: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
text: Li poorpoe ki Yennu maan ŋammit ki yat kɔɔ niib ni, ki Yisaweira mun bia yabitir ki pukii bonchiann Jerusalem, ki Yennu manteeb niburchiauŋ sak Yisa barŋanii ki teenɔ yada. Stiifen sooru labaar

ref: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
text: Ki Stiifen din la Yennu sommir nan yikoo bonchiann, ki din tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana niib na sinsuuk ni.

ref: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
text: Ŋaan nisiab din ki sakɔ. Binba din nɔi nan Stiifen maŋ din tee Juu teeb nba din tee daaba, ki bi ŋaab bi daabsin ni. Ki bi nyii Sairene nan Aleksandria nan Silisia nan Asia nie. Bi mun din mɔk bi mɔŋ Yenjiantu diiuko.

ref: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
text: Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ din tur Stiifen yanfantir bonchiann, ki bi ki fit nyannɔ nan u maan maŋi.

ref: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
text: Ba din ki fit nyannɔ na, ki bi din kɔɔ nisiab a bin yet a nna, “Ti gbat ki u piak bonbilankant ki biir Moses nan Yennu.”

ref: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
text: Ba yet nna maŋ yoonba, ki niib na nan saakab nan sennu wannteeb wutoa doo, ki bi fiir ki soorɔ, ki saan nanɔ bi yudamm tituk boor ni;

ref: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
text: ki bi tan baar nan nisiab nna biak a bin faar faak, ki yet a “Yoo kur jɔɔ na yɔɔ piak ki biir Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak maŋ, nan Moses sennu nee,

ref: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
text: kimaan ti gbat ki u yet a Yisa nba tee Nasaret nirɔ na sa bet Yennu ŋasaakak na, a ŋaan lebit Moses kaar nba ki u din senn na.”

ref: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
text: Binba kur din lakin kar tituk maŋ ni din kpaa bi ninbina ki gorii Stiifen, ki tan la ki u nummpo lebit ki ji naan nan malaka nummpo na.

ref: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ boi Stiifen a “Li set tee mɔniiee?”

ref: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
text: Ki Stiifen yaa a “Chanbaanba nan n naa waas, gbiintir man, yoo nba ki ti yeja Abraham din be Mesopotamia yakir po, Kaldea tiŋ ni na, li yoo ki u din daa ki saan Haran na, ki paŋ daanɔ Yennu na dɔkit u paak,

ref: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
text: ki yetɔ a ‘Fiit ki nyik a tiŋ nan a niib, ŋaan saan siaminba ki n sa wanna na.’

ref: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
text: Li yoo na ki u fiir ki nyik Kaldea tiŋ maŋ, ŋaan saan ki saa kar Haran doo ni. U baa kuun poorpoe, ki Yennu bia te ki u fiir, ki baar tiŋ nba ki ti be na ni.

ref: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
text: Ŋaan Yennu daa din ki turɔ tiŋ na nan bonmintika a wun yenti, ŋaan senn mɔsonn a u tan sa turɔ tiŋ nna, ki ŋɔɔ nan u yaaboona-ii yen. Yennu nba din senn mɔsonn nna yoonba na, ki sɔɔ ki Abraham daa ki mɔk biki.

ref: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
text: Ki Yennu din yetɔ a ‘A yaaboona tan sa saan ki be tingaŋ niewa, ki tan sii tee daaba leŋ, ki bi tan sa la muksuk leŋ bonchiann, ki tan jaŋ bina kobiinna.

ref: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
text: Ŋaan tiŋ maŋ niib nba ki bi sii tuun ki teemm na, n tan sa dat bi tuba, ki li poorpo, a yaaboona maŋ tan sa nyi tiŋ maŋ ni, ki baar tiŋ na ni, ki jiantin.’

ref: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
text: “Li poorpo ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki tur Abraham, ki bia din wannɔ mɔsonn maŋ nyinn, a ŋɔɔ nan u yaaboona-ii porii puna. Ki Abraham din tan mar Aisak, ki li daa ŋanniin daar ki u potɔ; ki Aisak mun din tan pot u bik Jakɔb; ki Jakɔb mun din tan pot u waas piik nan bannlee binba tee ti yabint yejateeb.

ref: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
text: Ki Jakɔb waas maŋ din tan mɔk punponn nan bi waarɔ Josef, ki din tan soorɔ ki kɔiɔ, ki nisiab daau ki saan nanɔ Ijipt tiŋ ni, ŋaan Yennu din be nanɔ,

ref: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
text: ki din nyinnɔ fara kura ni, ki bia turɔ yan ki te ki Ijipt kpanbar Faro nianɔ ki jiiu teenɔ yudaanɔ Ijipt tiŋ ni, ki bia te ki u tee yudaanɔ u ŋaak ni.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
text: “Li poorpo ki kon tan baa Ijipt tiŋ nan Israel tiŋ Kenann ni kura, ki kon soor niib bonbiir, ki ti yejamm kɔŋ jeet.

ref: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
text: Jakɔb nba din gbat a dii be Ijipt yoonba, ki u tun u waas leŋ. Ŋannii din tee bi sinsinn saanu.

ref: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
text: “Bi saanu taar munnlee nie, ki Josef ji din te ki u naa waas na bannɔ. Ŋanne ki Faro ji din bann Josef booru.

ref: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
text: “Ŋanne ki Jakɔb nan u ŋaateeb maŋ fiir saan Ijipt ki saa kar; ki li poorpo, ŋɔɔ nan u waas din tan kpo gbenn leŋe.

ref: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
text: “Yoo nba din tan per nan Yennu-n gbeen u mɔsonn nba ki u din senn Abraham na, li yoo maŋ, ti niib Israel teeb nba be Ijipt na ji yabit bonchiann.

ref: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
text: Li poorpoe ki sɔɔ din dii Ijipt naan, ŋaan ŋɔɔ din ki mi Josefi.

ref: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
text: Ki kpanbar maŋ din dia ti niib diabiiuk, ki din tuu ton namm bonchiann, ki bia din tuu miab a bin nyinn bi sanpantii bi ŋei ni ki lub ki bin kpo.

ref: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
text: “Ŋanne ki bi din tan mar Moses, ki u fan bonchiann, ki be u baa ŋaak ŋmaarii ŋanntaa.

ref: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
text: Ba din tan nyinnɔ ŋaak ni yoonba, ki kpanbar na sapaamɔ din lau, ki gaarɔ ki jiiu ki wubiiɔ; ki u ji tee u mɔŋ bik.

ref: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
text: Ki bi din wann ŋɔɔ Moses yan kur nba ki Ijipt teeb mɔk, ki u din tan teen wunba mi pinpauk bonchiann, ki bia tuun paŋ toona.

ref: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
text: Wa lin gɔntib na, ki u tan la ki Ijipt nirɔ boo Israel teeb ni yenɔ; ki u baar a wun fatɔ, ki tan kpii Ijipt nirɔ maŋ.

ref: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
text: Ki li yent sanyiɔk, ki u bia la Israel niib bannlee ki bi kɔn; ki u loon ki wun taamm ki bin maŋ nan bi leeb, ki yetib a ‘Gotir man, i tee naa waase, ki bee teen ki i boo i leeb ki teen i leeb danii?’

ref: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
text: Ki wunba tur u lɔɔ daŋ na pii Moses, ŋaan betɔ yaa a ‘Sɔɔ ki ganna a a teen ti yudaanɔ amii ti barbuurɔ.

ref: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
text: A yaan loon ki a kpin kaman nan fa won kpii Ijipt nirɔ na amii?’

ref: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
text: Moses nba gbat nna yoonba, ki u chiar nyii Ijipt tiŋ ni, ki baar Midian tiŋ ni ki be leŋ, ki din tan kɔɔn poo ki mar bonjabis bannlee.

ref: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
text: “Moses nba din la nna maŋ ki li teenɔ yaarlituk. Ki u chat nakin a wun ŋamm got, ki ji gbat ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu kunkɔr ki u beerɔ ki yaa a

ref: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
text: ‘Mine tee Abraham Yennu na, ki bia tee Aisak Yennu nan Jakɔb Yennu. Mine tee a yejamm maŋ Yennu.’ Moses nba gbat nna yoonba, ki jaŋmaanii ji kɔɔu, ki u ji see ki jekir ki ji ki mɔk par nan wun goti.

ref: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
text: Ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu betɔ a ‘Pitir a taasaat nba ki a pir na, kimaan siaminba ki a see na tee boyeenne.

ref: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
text: Tɔn, n got ki la Ijipt teeb nba dint n mɔŋ niib Israel teeb farchiauŋ Ijipt tiŋ ni biaŋinba, ki n gbia bi fabinii. Ŋanne ki n sik ma n fat nyinnib. Li paak n sa tuma Ijipt.’ ”

ref: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
text: Ŋanne ki Stiifen bia lek piak ki saa a “Moses nba nee ki Israel teeb din yêtɔ na, ki bi din betɔ yaa a ‘Sɔɔ ki ganna a a teen ti yudaanɔ amii ti barbuurɔ,’ ŋaan ŋɔɔ nee ki Yennu mun din gannɔ a wuu tee bi yudaanɔ nan bi fatitɔɔ biak; ki ŋɔɔ Yennu din te ki malaka pak nanɔ ŋaan tumɔ. Malaka maŋe ki Moses din lau kunkonjagotuk paak muu na ni na.

ref: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
text: Ŋɔɔ Moses din tee daanɔ nba din nyinnib Ijipt tiŋ nna, ki din bia tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana Ijipt tiŋ ni, nan ba bia din saa poot Mɔkgbeŋmuŋ na, nan ba bia din be muuk ni binpiinna na.

ref: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
text: Li Mosese din yet Israel teeb a ‘Yennu tan sa tun sɔkiniisɔɔ i boor ni kaman nan wa tumin biaŋinba na, ki u sii tee i booru ni yenɔ.’

ref: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
text: Ŋaan ti yejamm din ki loon ki bin sakɔ. Li paak ki bi din yêtɔ, kimaan bi yan tunn Ijipta;

ref: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
text: Ŋanne ki bi maa patir ki nannɔ naajabik, ŋaan mann marouŋ li paak, ki teen jaamm a bin mantik donnɔ, linba ki bi maa nan bi mɔŋ nii na.

ref: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
text: “Li paak ki Yennu ji ki goriibi, ŋaan chabib, ki bi ji jiantir ŋmaabira, kaman nan la sɔb biaŋinba Yennu sɔkiniinba gbouŋ ni na, a Yennu yet a ‘Israel teeb, ya din kpi bonkobit ki mann marouŋ muuk ni bina piinna na, i din ki mann marouŋ maŋ ki tee min kaa.

ref: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
text: I din tuu jikit patir Molok lanbouŋo, ki bia din tuu jikit ŋmaabir Refan naauŋo, ki lii tee patir, ki saa namm. Ŋamm tee yenn-nante ki i maa ki jiantir na. Li paak, n sa te ki i datai-n nyinni i tiŋ ni ki saan nani siaminba fɔk ki gar Babilɔnn.’ ”

ref: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
text: Ŋanne ki Stiifen bia piak a “Ti yejamm din faa Yennu lanbouŋ muuk ni, ki li want a Yennu be namm. Yennue din wann Moses ba sii faa lanbouŋ maŋ biaŋinba, ki bi faa. Li din tee kaman nan Moses nba din lar biaŋinba yerintu ni nee.

ref: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
text: “Li poorpo, ki ti yejamm ji din tan jii lanbouŋ maŋ ki tur bi waas, ki bi gaar ki mun jikit ki saa nann, ki ŋamm nan Joshua din tan baar nna, ki fat tiŋ nba ki niboorganii daa din yen li yoo na. Yennue din ber nyinn niib maŋ ti yejamm tɔɔnn po, ki lanbouŋ na ji din be nna dik dik dik nan David yoo mun.

ref: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
text: David maŋ dudukit din gboot Yennu numm, ki u din tan miar Yennu a wun chabɔ ki wun maa ŋaak ki tur ŋɔɔ Yennu nba din tee u yeja Jakɔb Yennu na.

ref: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
text: Ŋaan ŋɔɔ David bija Solomone din tan maa ŋaak maŋ.

ref: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
text: “Ti lek mi nan Yabint Yennu ki kaar ŋei nba ki niib maa ni kaa. Li tee kaman nan Yennu sɔkinii nba din yet a

ref: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
text: ‘Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu yet a Sanpaak tee n naangbouŋo, a ki tingbouŋ na mun tee n taakpaktir. I kan fit maa ŋaak nba jaŋ ki turin, ki n foi leŋi.

ref: I have made everything.”
text: Min kaa din nan bona na kurii?’ ”

ref: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
text: Yennu sɔkinii sɔɔ din kaa ki i yejamm din ki mukisɔ. Bi bia din kpii binba din poŋ sɔkint a Yennu Popeendaanɔ Masia tan sa baar nawa. Tɔn, ki mɔtana yimme tee damm nba nyii u poor ki bia tee u kpiira.

ref: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
text: Yimme bia tee damm nba din gaar Yennu sennu nba ki i yejamm din gaar malakanba nuu ni, ŋaan bia lek ki sakiri na.”

ref: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
text: Tituk kaanu damm na nba gbiint Stiifen maan maŋ ki bi donn wutoor bonbiir ŋaan gbab mɔgbaba.

ref: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu Seyeeŋ fiir Stiifen ni, ki u yaat ki got Yendɔuŋ ni, ki la Yennu yentsaakar, ki Yisa see ŋɔɔ Yennu niidiitu po;

ref: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
text: ki u yet a “Gotir, n la ki sanpaapo loot ki nisaarik Bik see Yendɔuŋ ni, Yennu niidiitu po.”

ref: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
text: Ŋanne ki tituk teeb na yikin sanpaapo, ŋaan pii bi tuba; ki bi kura tan lakin wuur baar ki kpirɔ,

ref: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
text: ki berɔ ki nyinnɔ doo maŋ ni, ŋaan liat bi liata ki bir naasimɔ nba ki bi yiu Sɔɔl na boor ni, a wuu guu, ŋaan jaat Stiifen tana.

ref: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
text: Bi din jaatɔe dik dik dik; ki u din tan yiin ti Yomdaanɔ a “N Yomdaanɔ Yisa, gaat n seek na;”

ref: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
text: ŋaan gbaan ki yikin sanpaapowa a “N Yomdaanɔ, daa jikit bi biit na ki biiribi.” U yet nnee ŋaan ji kpo.

ref: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
text: Ki Sɔɔl ŋarin din loon wun biir Yisaweirae, ki din lin kɔɔ ŋaak ŋaak, ki so Yisaweira, jab nan poob, ki nyintib ki saa kɔɔntib dansarii. Biaŋinba ki Filip mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii Samaria

ref: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
text: Yisaweira nba din nyii ki yat na ji din saa lokir kur poe ki mɔɔntir Yisa maan maŋ;

ref: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
text: ki Filip nyii ki sik Samaria doo ni, ki saa mɔɔnt Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia po ki tur leŋ niib.

ref: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
text: Ki parpeenn din be doo maŋ ni bonchiann.

ref: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
text: Jasɔɔ nna mun din be doo maŋ ni, ki tee nyɔkluurɔ, ki u sann din yi Simon, ki yosau ki u tuu tan tee u bakitnauŋ toona, ki li din bakitir niib kur nba be doo maŋ yakir po, ki u din yi u mɔŋ a u tee nijaanne.

ref: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
text: Niib kur nba din tee nijaana nan tarii din baakitɔ, ki bi din tuu yaa a “Jɔɔ na mɔk paŋ nba nyii Yennu boore.” Li paak ki bi yiu Panchiauŋ Daanɔ.

ref: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
text: U din tuu tee u bakitnauŋ toona maŋ, ki li bakitirib ki yukira. Li paak ki bi din teeu nibaakir ki bia gbiintir u maan.

ref: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
text: Ŋaan ki Filip nba din tan baar ki mɔɔntir Yennu naan barŋanii nan Yisa sann po na, jab nan poob din sak u mɔɔntii maŋ, ki u wurib Yennu nyunwuru.

ref: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
text: Simon mɔŋ mun din teen Filip maan yada, ki u wurɔ. Ki u teen Filip weinanlɔɔ, ki laat bakitnauŋ toonjaana nba ki u tuun na, ki li tuu yaar ŋaan litɔ.

ref: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
text: Ki Yisa toomiinba nba din be Jerusalem gbat a Samaria teeb gaar Yennu maan maŋ; ki bi din tun Piita nan Jɔɔnn,

ref: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
text: ki bi sik Samaria ki saa miar Yennu bi paak, a bin gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ,

ref: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: kimaan Yennu Seyeeŋ maŋ daa din ki sik bi sɔɔ ni. Bi kpan din wurib Yennu nyunwuru nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann ni kuukɔɔe.

ref: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
text: Ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn yii bi nii bi paak, ki bi gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ.

ref: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
text: Simon nba din la ki toomiinba na nba yiin bi nii niib paak, ki Yennu turib Seyeeŋ na, ki u yaa a wun tur toomiinba na likirii,

ref: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
text: ŋaan yetib a “Chabimin mun yikoo na man, ki n mun tuu yii n nii sɔɔ paak, ki wun gaar Seyeeŋ maŋ.”

ref: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
text: Ŋaan ki Piita betɔ yaa a “A sa kpo yiake nan a likirii maŋ, kimaan a dukii mi a a sa gaar Yennu piinii nan likiriie.

ref: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
text: Li paak na, a lek kan tun siar ti toonn na ni, kimaan a par ki be fanu nan Yennu.

ref: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
text: kimaan n la nan a gbee nan nɔɔntonte, ki bia tee biit yommike.”

ref: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
text: Ki Simon jiin betib a “Miat i Yomdaanɔ ki turin man, ki linba ki i yet na, ki li ji daa baat n paaki.”

ref: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
text: Tɔn, li poorpoe ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn din pak siara ti Yomdaanɔ po ki tur niib, ki bia mɔɔnt u maan, ŋaan ŋmat ki saan Jerusalem. Ba din saa nee, ki bi din mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii Samaria digbanbis ni bonchiann. Filip nan Etiopia jatɔɔkir po

ref: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
text: Ŋanne ki ti Yomdaanɔ malaka tan baar bet Filip a “Fiit saan tɔkin sɔnu nba nyi Jerusalem ki siik Gasa na.” Leŋ din tee tinpere.

ref: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
text: Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ yet Filip a “I saa nakin toruk na.”

ref: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
text: Ki u chiar saan nakin leŋ, ki gbat ki u karin Yennu sɔkinii Aisaya gbouŋ ni. Ki u boiɔ a “A bann linba ki a karin na paakii?”

ref: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
text: Ki u jiin a “Nlee ki n sa bann li paaki, ki li ki tee ki sɔɔ wannin kaa?” ŋaan barin Filip a wun kɔɔ wannɔ. Ki u kɔɔ toruk maŋ ni ki bi lakin kar.

ref: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
text: Yennu gbouŋ maan nba ki jatɔɔkir maŋ din karin na, ŋannii na a “Ki u sak saan kaman nan peeuk na ki bi daarɔ a bin saa kpiu. a ki u ŋmin kaman nan pebik nba ki bi tuu pot pot u kobit ki u ŋmin na, a ki u ki yaat u mɔbi,

ref: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
text: a ki bi sikin u baakir ki yêt bin bu u buut bonŋann, a u din ki mɔk yaaboona, a kimaan u manfoor ji jit tingbouŋ na niwa.”

ref: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
text: Ki jatɔɔkir na boi Filip a “Betirin, ŋmee ki sɔkinii na yeen na? U yeen u mɔŋ amii sɔɔe?”

ref: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
text: Ki Filip piin siaminba ki jatɔɔkir maŋ karin na ki saa, ki piak Yisa barŋanii.

ref: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
text: Ki Filip yetɔ a “Li-i tee ki a teen Yisa yada nan a par kura, n sa wurawa.” Ki u jiin a “N teen yada nan Yisa Masia tee Yennu Bijae.”

ref: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
text: Ki jatɔɔkir maŋ te ki taanii na set, ki ŋɔɔ nan Filip sik nyun na ni, ki Filip wurɔ Yennu nyunwuru.

ref: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
text: Ki bi tan nyii nyun na ni ki doo; ki Yennu Seyeeŋ jii Filip ki yaat nanɔ, ki jatɔɔkir na ji ki lau; ŋaan ŋamm soor u sɔnu ki kun nan parpeenn.

ref: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
text: Ki Filip ji din saa be Asotusewa, ki din lin digbana kur ki mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii mɔkmɔk nan wa din tan baar Siisarea.

ref: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Ŋaan ki Sɔɔl mɔɔntii maŋ bia lek ŋammit ki pukii nan Yennu paŋ, ki u maan maŋ want nan Yisae set tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia ki birsuk kaa; ki Juu teeb nba be Damaskus na ki fit jiinɔ siari.

ref: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
text: Ŋaan fiit ki saan doo na ni, ki bi sa saa beta fa ji sa teen biaŋinba.”

ref: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
text: ki n mɔŋ sa wannɔ muksuk nba kur ki u sa di n paak.”

ref: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
text: Ŋanne ki Sɔɔl din saan Jerusalem ki saa yabir u mɔŋ paak a wun lakin nan Yisaweira na, ŋaan ki bi ki teenɔ yada nan u ji tee Yisaweirɔe, ki tiinɔ jaŋmaanii.

ref: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
text: ki u la jasɔɔ yerintu ni, ki u sann yi Ananias, ki u kɔɔ ki jii u nii ki yii u paak, a wuu jikii laat.”

ref: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
text: Li taakpaak ni ki siar tabit u ninbina ni ki baa, ki naan nan janwɔka na, ki u ji laat jik, ki fiir set; ki bi wurɔ Yennu nyunwuru.

ref: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
text: Tɔn, Sɔɔl lek bia din fɔɔ kɔnfɔɔt, ki yeen a u sii dint ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa poorpoweiteeb biak, ŋaan kpib; ki tan saan Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ boor ni,

ref: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
text: a wun gaar kadaat, a ki saan Damaskus ki saa wann Yenjiantu diit yudamm, a wu sii la wunba waa Yisa sɔnu maŋ, jɔɔ amii poo, see wun soorib ki lorib ki jen namm Jerusalem.

ref: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
text: Tɔn, wa ji din saa yoonbae ki saa per Damaskus, ki yentu nyii sanpaapo, ki sik yent lintɔ.

ref: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
text: Ki u baa tiŋ ni, ki gbat ki kunkɔr yiu a “Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bee ki a mukisirimi?”

ref: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Ki u boi a “N Yomdaanɔ, ŋmee?” Ki kunkɔr na jiin a “Min Yisae, ki a mukisirin.

ref: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
text: Li yoo na, jab nba din chiant Sɔɔl na ji biar set surimm. Bi din gbia kunkɔr na ŋarin, ŋaan ki laat sɔɔ.

ref: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
text: Ki Sɔɔl tan fiir tiŋ ni, ki munt u nunii, ŋaan ki laati. Ŋanne ki bi dat u nuu ki saan nanɔ Damaskus.

ref: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
text: Damunjaa ŋanntaa ki u ki fit laati. Li daa maŋ ni u din ki dii amii ki nyuu siari.

ref: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
text: Ki Yisaweisɔɔ din be Damaskus maŋ, ki u sann yi Ananias, ki ti Yomdaanɔ yiinɔ yerintu ni a “Ananias!” Ki u jiin a “Mine na, n Yomdaanɔ.”

ref: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
text: Ki ti Yomdaanɔ yetɔ a “Fiit ki saan tɔkin sɔnu nba yi Sɔnsun-sonn na ki saa kɔɔ Judas ŋaak ni, ki boi jɔɔ nba nyii Tasus ki u sann yi Sɔɔl na po. U mei Yennue mɔtana,

ref: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
text: Ki Ananias jiin a “N Yomdaanɔ, niib bonchianne piak jɔɔ na po, ki jikit saa biaŋinba kur ki u tuun a mɔŋ niib Jerusalem.

ref: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
text: U daan gaar yikoo jiantu yudamm boor ni, ki baar nna, a wun soor wunba kur yi a sann.”

ref: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
text: Ki ti Yomdaanɔ betɔ a “Fini saawa! a ŋɔɔe tee wunba ki n gannɔ a wuu tuun n toonn, ki te ki boorganu nan bi kpanbara nan Israel teeb-n bann n sann,

ref: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Ŋanne ki Ananias saan kɔɔ ŋaak na ni, ki yii u nii Sɔɔl paak, ŋaan yetɔ a “N naa bik Sɔɔl, n Yomdaanɔ Yisae tumin. Ŋɔɔe tee daanɔ nba ki a daan baat nna ki tan lau sɔnu paak na, ki u tumin a n tan te ki a-ii jikii laat, ki bia gbee yent nan Yennu Seyeeŋ.”

ref: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
text: Ŋanne ki u dii jeet ki paŋ jen. Biaŋinba ki Sɔɔl mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii Li yoo na ki Sɔɔl din lakin be nan Yisaweira kaman nan daa ŋannlee na Damaskus,

ref: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
text: ki din saan yiama Yenjiantu diit ni, ki mɔɔntir Yisa po, a Yisa kpan set tee Yennu Bijae.

ref: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
text: Binba kur din gbat u mɔɔntii maŋ, li din teemm yaarlituko, ki bi boi a “Jɔɔ na kaa daan kpi binba yi Yisa sann Jerusalem na-aa? U yaan ki baar a wun soor ki lor Yisaweira, ki ŋmat namm ki saa turib Yennu jiantu yudamm kaa na-aa?”

ref: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
text: Ki daa bonchiann tan gar, ki Juu teeb na lakin leeb ki lor lor a bin kpi Sɔɔl,

ref: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
text: ki yennu nan nyiɔk ki bi guu doo maŋ tammɔi bonŋann a bin kpiu. Ki sɔɔ tan kumiiɔ ba lorin u paak biaŋinba;

ref: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
text: ŋaan nyayouŋ ki u weinanleeb tan jiiu ki teenɔ chibuk ni, ki nyinnɔ doo maŋ joonfɔnn ni, ki sikinɔ tiŋ ni. Sɔɔl benu Jerusalem

ref: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
text: Ki Banabas din tan baar u sommir, ki jiiu ki baar nanɔ toomiinba na boor ni, ki kat wannib biaŋinba ki Sɔɔl daan la ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sɔnu ni, nan wa pak nanɔ biaŋinba. U bia din ŋamm ki wannib Sɔɔl nba din mɔɔntir Yisa po nan parcheenn Damaskus biaŋinba.

ref: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
text: Banabas nba wannib linba na, ki Sɔɔl din lakin be namm, ki bi din kak ki lin Jerusalem kur po, ki mɔɔntir ti Yomdaanɔ sann nan parcheenn.

ref: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
text: U bia din piak nan Juu teeb nba piak Griik maan na ki jikit saa Yisa po, ŋaan bi din nɔi nanɔ, ki koor a bin kpiu.

ref: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
text: Ŋaan yoo nba ki Yisaweira bann nna maŋ, ki bi jii Sɔɔl ki sik nanɔ Siisarea, ki te ki u kun Tasus.

ref: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
text: Ŋanne ki Yisaweira nba be Judea nan Galilee nan Samaria na ji ki laat muksuku, ki ŋammit ki tumii ti Yomdaanɔ barii, ki tee yentina, ki Yennu Seyeeŋ be namm ki kɔɔntib para, ki bi pukii. Biaŋinba ki Piita tuun bakitnauŋ toona

ref: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
text: Li poorpoe ki Piita din yek lin siar kur po, ki bia din tan sik a wun gɔn Yennu niib nba be Lida na.

ref: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
text: Leŋe ki u din saa la jasɔɔ nna, ki u sann yi Eneas, ki u din poŋ tee gbanankpeenn daanɔe, ki din ki fit fi u dɔɔnu ni teee nan bina ŋanniin.

ref: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
text: Ki Piita tan yetɔ a “Eneas, a Yisa Masia te ki a paaka. Fiit ki ŋamm a dɔɔnu.” Li taakpaak ni ki u fiir.

ref: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
text: Lida nan Sharon doi niib kur nba din lau ki u paak na, ki bi lebit bi binbeŋ, ki waa ti Yomdaanɔ sɔnu.

ref: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
text: Poosɔɔ mun din be Jɔpa, ki u sann yi Tabita, ŋaan Griik maan ni, bi din yiu Dɔkas, ki li paak tee piarɔ. U din tee Yisaweirɔe, ki din tuun toonŋana bonchiann ki teen niib, ki bia din sommit talasdamm.

ref: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
text: Li yoo na dinn ki yiaru din soorɔ ki u kpo, ki bi din jiiu ki wurɔ, ŋaan jiiu doo nanɔ ditɔɔtuk ni ki birɔ.

ref: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
text: Jɔpa mun din ki fɔk nan Lida saami, ki Yisaweira nba din be Jɔpa din gbat nan Piita be Lida, ki bi tun jab bannlee u boor ni, a wun kakit ki baar yian yian na. Ki jab bannlee maŋ saan ki saa barin Piita.

ref: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
text: Ki u fiir ki bi jen nanɔ. Wa baar yoonba, ki bi jiiu ki doo nanɔ ditɔɔtuk maŋ ni. Ki pakɔi din be diiuk na ni, ki bi kur ŋaa lint Piitawa, ŋaan mɔ, ki jikit bi liata nan bi chinchena ki wantɔ, ki yeen a “Dɔkas nba tuu be u manfoor ni ki nyar linba ki teente na.”

ref: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
text: Ŋanne ki Piita nyinn bi kura diiuk na ni, ŋaan gbaan tiŋ ni, ki miar Yennu, ŋaan tan ŋmant kpeemɔ na paak, ki yiin a “Tabita, fiit!” Ki u munt u nunii ki la Piita, ki ji fiir ki kar.

ref: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
text: Ki Piita dɔnn ki soor u nuu ki fiin sennɔ, ŋaan yiin Yennu niib nan pakɔi na, ki tan jiiu ki wannib nan u jen u manfoor niwa.

ref: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
text: Ki li labaar din yat saan Jɔpa kur po; ki niib bonchiann din teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa yada ki waa u sɔnu.

ref: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
text: Ki Piita ji din biar be Jɔpa daa bonchiann; u din be jasɔɔ nba tee gbanfebirtɔɔ na ŋaak nie, ki bi yiu Simon.

ref: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
text: Tɔn, jasɔɔ nna din be Siisarea, ki u sann yi Kɔnelius. U din tee lanjimanba yudaansɔɔe, ki dia lanjimanba kobik u paabu ni. U paabu maŋ din yi Itali tiŋ paabu.

ref: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
text: U din tee Yenjiantitɔɔe; ki ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb kura din jiantir Yennu. U din sommit Juu teeb ni talasdamm bonchiann, ki bia din mei Yennu yoo kur.

ref: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
text: Li din tan tee kaman nan yenlekir nee, ki u la Yennu malaka yerintu ni peee, ki u kɔɔ u diiuk ni ki tan yiin a “Kɔnelius.”

ref: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: Kɔnelius nba din got la malaka maŋ na, ki jaŋmaanii soorɔ, ki u yet a “N yomdaanɔ, bee?” Ki malaka maŋ jiin a “Yennu gaar a miaruwa, ki bia gaar a ninkpabauŋ toona nba ki a tuun ki teen niib, ki bia tian a po.

ref: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
text: Mɔtana, tumin nisiab Jɔpa, ki bin saa yiin jasɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Simon, ki u sanjɔɔk tee Piita na.

ref: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
text: U tee saamɔe ki be gbanfebirtɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Simon na ŋaak ni, ki u ŋaak maŋ kpia nan mɔkgbeŋŋir na.”

ref: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
text: Malaka maŋ nba din pak nanɔ na ji din yaat, ki Kɔnelius ji yiin u ŋaatoontunna bannlee nan lanjima sɔɔ nna, wunba din tee Yenjiantitɔɔ, ki tee u kpaakyenɔ na.

ref: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
text: Wa betib linba kur teen maŋ poorpoe, ki u tumm Jɔpa.

ref: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
text: La yent sanyiɔk, ki bi saa, ki saa per Jɔpa; li yoo na ki Piita mun doo ditɔɔtuk paak yensuuk ni, a wun miar Yennu.

ref: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
text: Ki kon din tan gbatɔ, ki u ji loon a wun di jeet, ŋaan ki bi daa tee jeet siir; li yooe ki u la yerintu,

ref: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
text: ki sanpaapo loot ki bonsiar nna naan chinchenn ki nyii sanpaapo, ki bi dia li mɔgbana ŋanna ki sikint tiŋ ni,

ref: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: ki u gbat kunkɔr yiin a “Piita, fiit ki kpi ki di.”

ref: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Ŋaan ki Piita jiin a “O! O! O! N Yomdaanɔ, nna kaa. N ki mi dii ti kɔɔti.”

ref: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Ki kunkɔr na bia ŋamm yetɔ a “Linba ki Yennu kpintir a li ki kɔɔ, daa yeen a li kɔɔ.”

ref: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
text: Li din teen nna ki saa jaŋ taar munntaa, ki bi jiin bont maŋ sanpaapo. Binba ki tee Juu teeb nba teen Yisaweira biaŋinba

ref: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
text: Li din daa yaar lit Piita ki jikit saa wa la yerintu na paak. Li yoo na ki Kɔnelius toomii na boi Simon ŋaak nba be siaminba po, ki bi wannib, ki bi baar ŋaak maŋ tammɔb ni, ki set,

ref: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
text: ŋaan yiin ki boi a “Sɔɔ be nna, ki u sann yi Simon Piita-aa?”

ref: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
text: Ŋaan ki Piita lek koor a wun bann yerintu maŋ paak, ki Yennu Seyeeŋ yetɔ a “Gotir, jab banntaa be nna ki loona.

ref: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
text: Li paak, fiit ki sik, ki daa biris nan a saan nammi, kimaan mine tumm.”

ref: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
text: Ŋanne ki Piita sik, ki bet jab na a “Mine ki i loon na. Bee teen ki i baari?”

ref: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
text: Ki bi jiin a “Chanbaa Kɔneliuse tumit. U tee lanjimanba yudaanɔe, ki tee niburchimɔe biak, ki jiantir Yennu, ki Juu teeb kur teenɔ nibaakir. Tɔn, Yennu malaka won baar betɔ a wun yiina ki a baar u ŋaak ni, ki wun tan gbat linba ki a sa pak ki turɔ.”

ref: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
text: Ki Piita fan yiin jab na ki kɔɔmm, a bii be ki sanyiɔk-n yenta. Ki li tan yent, ki u teen siir, ki bi saa; ki Yisaweira nba din be Jɔpa na, ki bi siab chianɔ.

ref: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
text: Li daar din tan bɔnn yente, ki bi baar Siisarea, ŋaan sɔɔ ki Kɔnelius poŋ yiin u ninjamm nan u yɔɔsinba, ki bi kur ji kar guu Piita baaru.

ref: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.

ref: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
text: Ki Piita yetɔ a “Fiit, n mɔŋ mɔŋ tee nirɔe,” ŋaan fiinɔ sennɔ.

ref: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,

ref: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
text: Ki u betib a “I mɔŋ mɔŋ mun mi nan timm Juu teeb sennu ki chabit nan tin saan gɔn binba ki tee Juu teebi, amii ki taan nammi; ŋaan Yennu wannin a n daa yêen nisɔɔ, wa ki tee Juu nirɔ na paaki.

ref: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
text: Ki Kɔnelius yetɔ a “Li daan tee nna yooe dinna daar, ki n daan mei Yennu n ŋaak ni kut munntaa yoo yenlekir. Li yoo na ki n la ki nisɔɔ nna see ki lia liatnyinyira n tɔɔnn po,

text: Ki li paake ki n daan tun a po yiama na, ki a mun teen bonŋann ki baar na. Tɔn, ti kura be Yennu tɔɔnn, ki ji guu a ti gbat linba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu tura mɔb a a pak turit na.”

ref: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.

text: I mi nan Yennu din tur Israel teeb u maan nba want barŋanii na po, a Yisa Masia nie ki timm nan Yennu maŋ nan leeb; ŋɔɔ Yisa Masiae tee ti kur Yomdaanɔ.

ref: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.

ref: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.

ref: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and

ref: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?

text: Ki Bat Herod tur mɔb a bin lon u boor, ki bi lomɔ ki ki lau; ki u boi guura maŋ buboit, ŋaan tur mɔb a bin kpib. Li poorpoe ki Bat Herod din nyii Judea ki sik Siisarea, ki yann leŋ waama. Herod na kuun po

ref: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
text: Li taakpaak ni ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu malaka te ki yiarbiiuk baa u paak, kimaan u din ki teen Yennu dontir maŋi; ki nanwuba kɔɔ mɔɔu ki u kpo.

ref: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.

ref: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
text: Yoo nba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ din tun Banabas nan Sɔɔl a bin saan tun u toonn na, ki bi din sik Selusia ki kɔɔ ŋaruŋ, ki saa baar Salamis nba din be Saiprus tiŋ ni na,

ref: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.

ref: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
text: U din be Saiprus tiŋ ni yudaanɔ boore; ki yudaanɔ maŋ sann din yi Sejius Pɔɔlus; ki u din tee yandaanɔe, ki din yiin Banabas nan Sɔɔl a bin tan pak Yennu maan ki wun gbat.

ref: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
text: Ŋaan ki nyɔkluurɔ maŋ, wunba sann din yi Elimas, Griik maan ni na, ki u ki sakibi, ŋaan koor a wun tia yudaanɔ maŋ ki u daa tee Yisa yada.

ref: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu Seyeeŋ fiir Sɔɔl ni, wunba sann mun din yi Pɔɔl na, ki u got nyɔkluurɔ na sirrr,

ref: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
text: ki tan yetɔ a “Fin, nibiiuk daanɔ nba gbee nan kpinkpannii na! Fine tee Sintaanii bik, ki bia tee toonŋana kur dataak. Fin ŋarin, yoo kur a jikit Yennu barmɔnii ki kpintir faake.

ref: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
text: Tɔn, Yennu nuu sa paa-a ki a jɔɔn ki kan la yentu ki lin yanna.” Li taakpaak ni, ki wunn nba bɔn baa u ninbina ni, ki u ji somm ki turin, ki loon sɔɔ-n datɔ.

ref: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
text: Ki yudaanɔ na nba la linba teen na, ki u teen Yisaweirɔ, kimaan u yan din kɔɔ nan linba ki u ji bann ki jikit saa ti Yomdaanɔ po na. Antiok nba be Pisidia tiŋ ni labaar

ref: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
text: Li poorpoe jik ki Pɔɔl nan u weinanleeb kɔɔ ŋaruŋ, ki nyii Pafos ki saan Pega nba din be Pamfilia tiŋ ni na. Leŋe ki Jɔɔnn Mak din nyikib ŋaan ŋmat saan Jerusalem.

ref: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
text: Ki bi saa tɔɔt ki nyii Pega, ki baar Antiok nba din be Pisidia tiŋ ni na. Ki foon daar ki bi saan kɔɔ Yenjiantu diiuk ni ki kar,

ref: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
text: ki la ki bi karin Moses sennu ni nan Yennu sɔkiniinba sɔbu ni, ki Yenjiantu diiuk maŋ ni saakab yetib a bin yet Pɔɔl nan Banabas a “Ti naa waas, li-i tee ki i mɔk labaŋann nbaie ki sa pak ki turit, yin pak.”

ref: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
text: Ki Pɔɔl fiir ki set ki donn nuu ŋaan piin paku, ki yaa a “Yimm Israel teeb nan yimm nba kur be ki jiantir Yennu na, gbiintir man,

ref: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
text: timm Israel teeb, Yennu din gann ti yejamm ki te ki bi yabit bonchiann, yoo nba ki bi daa din tee saamm ki be Ijipt tiŋ nna; ki ŋɔɔ Yennu din jii u paŋ ki nyinnib leŋ.

ref: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
text: Ŋaan li poorpo ki u biir nibooru munnlore nba din be Kenann tiŋ nna, ŋaan te ki u mɔŋ niib Israel teeb yent tiŋ maŋ.
