ref: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
text: Ki Yennu din tan liatɔ u naan ni, ŋaan dinn David a wuu tee bi bat jik. Nnee ki Yennu din pak David po a ‘N bann nan David, wunba tee Jese bik na, mantik gboot n numm, kimaan u sa tun linba kur ki n loon na.’

ref: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
text: “Jɔɔ na yaaboona nie, ki Yennu din nyinn ti Tinntɔɔ Yisa, ki teenɔ Israel niib ni, kaman nan wa din senn mɔsonn biaŋinba na.

ref: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
text: Ŋaan Jɔɔnne din dɔŋ baaru ki din mɔɔntir teen Israel teeb kura a bin nyik bi biit, ki wun wurib Yennu nyunwuru.

ref: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: U toonn nba ji din tan gbent na, ki u boib a ‘I dukii mi a n tee ŋmee? N ki tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia na kaa. Gotir man, ŋɔɔe yaa wun tan baar n poorpo, ki n kii ŋan nan ki n pit u taasaati.’ ”

ref: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
text: Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl bia yaa a “N naa waas, a yimm nba tee Abraham yaaboona, nan i kur nba be nna ki jiantir Yennu na, maan nba ki Yennu turit ki li want tinnu maŋ po na, li tee ti yare.

ref: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
text: Niib na nba din be Jerusalem, nan bi yudamm na, bi din ki mi nan nirɔ nee tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ, ki bia ki bann Yennu sɔkiniinba maan nba ki bi tuu karin foon daar kur na paaki; ŋaan ba din pak biirɔ na, bi din gbeen Yennu sɔkiniinba mɔi maŋa.

ref: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
text: Ba din teen Yisa linba kur gbenn kaman nan Yennu gbouŋ nba din want biaŋinba na, li yoo ki bi kpaatɔ dapunpunn na paak, ki sikinɔ ki piiu kaauk ni;

ref: But God raised him from death!
text: ŋaan ki Yennu fiinɔ kuun ni.

ref: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
text: Ki binba din tuu tɔkii nanɔ ki nyi Galilee ki lin teee ki tan gbenn ki baar Jerusalem na, ji din laatɔ daa bonchiann, wa din fiir na. Li paak ki bi ji tee u siara damm mɔtana ki teen niib.

ref: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
text: Ki bia jenna, Yennu din yet a u sa fiin Yisa kuun ni, a u ji daa mi ki kpenni, ŋaan yetɔ a ‘Mɔsona nba ki n mɔŋ mɔŋ din senn, ki yaa a n sa tur David na, see ki n set gbenn tura, fin nba tee u yaaboonn na.’

ref: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
text: Ki bia jenna, gbouŋ na siarpo, David din yet Yennu a ‘A kan sak ki a Popeendaanɔ gbanant-n kpakin kaauk ni ki bati.’

ref: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
text: Tɔn, David din tun linba ki Yennu wannɔ ki gbenn; li poorpoe ki u din tan kpo, ki bi piiu u yejamm kakura ni, ki u gbanant bat.

ref: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
text: Ŋaan Yennu nba din fiin Yisa kuun ni na, ŋɔɔ yaa gbanant ki bat kaauk ni.

ref: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
text: ‘Gotir man, yimm garuk damm na, ŋaant ki lin bakiti; toona nba ki n tuun i yoo na gbaa, sɔɔ-i lekii baar ki beti li po, li tee linba ki i kan mi ki teen yadaie. Li paak, see ki i kpo.’ ”

ref: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
text: Ba gbenn lakinu yoonba, ki Pɔɔl nan Banabas din nyii Yenjiantu diiuk na ni, ki niib na barimm a bi-i ŋmat kun, ŋaan bin teen paaru ki daan jen foon daar nba baat na, ki tan wannib labaar maŋ biak.

ref: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
text: Ki Juu teeb bonchiann, nan boorganu bonchiann, binba din lebit bi jiantu ki waa Juu teeb jiantu na, ki bi kura ji din waa Pɔɔl nan Banabas. Ki bi piak namm ki kpaamm a “I kpaŋŋii dia nna man, kii waa Yennu ki yabir la Yennu ninbatinu.”

ref: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
text: Ki foon daleer nba din tan baar, li din biar waaminnee nan doo na ni sɔɔ kur-n lakin ki gbat Yennu maan.

ref: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
text: Juu teeb nba din la nigbouŋ tikii na, ki bi fiin punpona, ki piak biir Pɔɔl wannu, ki bia sukiiɔ.

ref: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
text: Ŋaan ki Pɔɔl nan Banabas lek bia yeen nan parcheenn a “Li kpaa talase nan ki tin sint pak Yennu maan ki tur yimm Juu teeb. Ŋaan ya yêt maan maŋ, ki bia dukin nan i ki ŋan nan i la manfoor nba kaa gbennu na, li paake te ki ti nyikiti, ŋaan saa binba ki tee Juu teeb ni na,

ref: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
text: kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu turit mɔb a nna, kimaan li sɔb u gbouŋ ni a u din yet Yisa Masia a ‘Mine ganna a fii tee yentu, kii yentir kii teen binba ki tee Juu teebi, kii bia baat nan tinnu kii teen tingbouŋ na teeb kur.’ ”

ref: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
text: Tɔn, binba ki tee Juu teeb maŋ nba din gbat nna maŋ, ki bi ji mɔk parpeenn, ki piak Yennu maan. Ki binba ki Yennu gannib a bin la manfoor nba kaa gbennu na teen Yisa Masia yada.

ref: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
text: Ki ti Yomdaanɔ maan maŋ yat tiŋ na lokir kur po.

ref: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
text: Li yoo na ki Juu teeb na din fiin Antiok na yudamm, nan poojaana nba din tee yentina, wutoa, ki bi jak Pɔɔl nan Banabas muksuk, ki berib nyinnib bi yent ni.

ref: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
text: Ki toomiinba na din yet a “Ti nii nan ti taai na,” ŋaan yaat saan Ikonium.

ref: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
text: Ki Yisaweira nba din biar Antiok na mantik din gbee nan Yennu Seyeeŋ nan parpeenn.

ref: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
text: Pɔɔl nan Banabas nba din baar Ikonium, ki bi saan Juu teeb Yenjiantu diiuk ni, ki mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii, li paak ki niburchiauŋ teen Yisa yada; bi siab din tee Juu teeb, ki siab mun din tee Griik teeb.

ref: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
text: Ŋaan ki Juu teeb nba din tee nikangbata din biir Yisaweira sann, ki fiin boorganu na wutoa.

ref: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
text: Ki toomiinba na ji din jii yoo bonchiann ki be leŋ, ki ŋammit piak nan parcheenn ki jikit saa ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa po, ki u turib yaak a bii tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana, ki li din want nan ba piak u ninbatinu po na, li maan maŋ set nyii u boore.

ref: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
text: Ki doo na niib din bɔkit, ki siab din see Juu teeb poor, ki leeb na mun see toomiinba na poor.

ref: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
text: Ŋanne ki Juu teeb nan boorganu na lakin leeb nan bi doo maŋ yudamm, ki dukii a bin teen toomiinba biasin ki jaatib tana;

ref: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
text: Ki jasɔɔ nna din be Listra ki tee wɔbik. Bi din marɔ ki u tee wɔbike, ki ki mi sommi.

ref: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
text: Ki u din tan gbat Pɔɔl mɔɔntii na. Ki Pɔɔl gotɔ sirrr, ki tan bann nan u mɔk yada nba sa la laafiawa.

ref: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
text: Ki u yetɔ sanpaapowa a “Fiit ki set a taa paak.” Ki u tab naat ki fiir ki ji somm.

ref: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
text: Niib na nba din la Pɔɔl nba tun linba maŋ na, ki bi ji piin yikin bi mɔŋ mɔŋ maan ni a “Ti yennii sik ti boor ni ki kpint ki naan nan nisaarii na.”

ref: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
text: Ki bi taan yi Banabas sann a Seus, ki Pɔɔl mun, ki bi yiu a Hemes, kimaan ŋɔɔ din tee barpakirɔ.

ref: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
text: Ki bi yennu Seus tingbandaanɔ nyii pochianfeek ni, ki u tan baar nan naajai nan tipuut doo na tammɔb ni, ki ŋɔɔ nan nibur na tikir a bin mann marouŋ a ki tur toomiinba maŋ.

ref: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
text: Banabas nan Pɔɔl nba din gbat ba yaa bin tun biaŋinba na, ki bi para biir, ki bi pat pat bi liata, ki chiar kɔɔ nibur na ni, ŋaan yikin a

ref: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: “Bee ki i tee nna? Ti mun tee nisaariie kaman nan yimm na, ki mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii, a tin ŋmanti ki yi nyik bon-yana maŋ, ŋaan kii waa manfodaanɔ Yennu, wunba din nan sanpaak nan tiŋ nan mɔkgbeŋŋa nan linba kur be li ni.

ref: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
text: Yaayoo niewa ki u din ŋaan ki nibooru kur din tuun bi yama ni mɔkmɔk nan mɔtana,

ref: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
text: ŋaan yoo kur u tuun bonŋana, ki li set want nan u be; ki u te ki saak nyi sanpaapo ki baa ki teeni, ki bia teeni dii dijaan-yoo, ki gboonti jeet, ki i laat parpeenn.”

ref: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
text: Toomiinba maŋ nba din pak nna maŋ, ŋaan li lek din paar nan bin tia nigbouŋ maŋ ki bi daa mann marouŋ ki teemmi.

ref: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
text: Li poorpoe ki Juu tesiab din nyii Antiok nba be Pisidia tiŋ ni, nan Ikonium, ki baar ki pak tunn nigbouŋ na yan a bin biir Banabas nan Pɔɔl. Ŋanne ki nibur na jaat Pɔɔl tana, ki ji fuurɔ nyinnɔ doo na ni, kimaan bi mi a u kpoe.

ref: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
text: Ki Yisaweira baar ki set lintɔ; ki u tan fiir, ki ŋamm nan ŋɔɔ ŋmat doo na ni biak. Biaŋinba ki bi ŋmat jen Antiok Ki li yent sanyiɔk, ki Pɔɔl nan Banabas saan Debe ki saa mɔɔnt Yisa barŋanii na leŋ,

ref: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
text: ki dak niib bonchiann, ki bi teen Yisaweira. Ŋaan ki bi ŋmat saa Listra nan Ikonium nan Antiok nba be Pisidia tiŋ ni na,

ref: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
text: ki ŋammit want Yisaweira u maan, ki cheen bi para doi maŋ ni, ki beerib a “I kpaŋŋii dia i yada maŋ nna man,” ki wantib a “Nan ki tin tan kɔɔ Yennu naan ni na, see ki ti fabir ki dii muksuk bonchiann.”

ref: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
text: Ki doi kur ni ki bi tuu gann Yisaweira saakab, ki din mei Yennu ki lorin mɔi, ki jikit Yisaweira nan bi saakab na ki kubintib bi Yomdaanɔ Yisa nuu ni, wunba ki bi poŋ teenɔ yada na.

ref: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
text: Ba din gar Pisidia digbanbis na poorpoe, ki bi baar Pega nba be Pamfilia tiŋ ni na,

ref: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
text: ki din pak Yisa maan leŋ ki fit lian yen sik Atalia.

ref: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
text: Leŋe ki bi din kɔɔ ŋaruŋ ni, ki ŋmat saan Antiok; ŋaan sɔɔ ki leŋ ki Yisaweira poŋ din teemm Yennu nuu ni a wun sommib bi toonn ni. Ki mɔtana bi tun toonn maŋ ki gbenna.

ref: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
text: Bi baaru Antiok maŋ nie, ki bi tikir Yisaweira na, ki tan betib linba kur ki Yennu tun namm, nan wa loot sɔnu biaŋinba ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb a bin teen Yisa yada ki teen u weira.

ref: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
text: Ŋaan ki bi ji din biar leŋ ki yukir bonchiann nan Yisaweira maŋ.

ref: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
text: Li yoo na ki nisiab nna din nyii Judea ki sik Antiok, ki tan want Yisaweira na a “Li kpaa talase ki i pot i puna, kaman nan Moses sennu nba senn biaŋinba na. Ŋann-i kaa, i kan la tinnu.”

ref: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
text: Ki Pɔɔl nan Banabas nan ŋamm nɔi nan leeb bonchiann nan nunmɔnn, ki Yisaweira na tan dukin ki nyinn Pɔɔl nan Banabas nan bi ni siab, a bin saan Jerusalem, ki saa la toomiinba na nan Yisaweira saakab, maan maŋ po.

ref: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
text: Tɔn, ki Yisaweira na turib sɔnu, ki bi saa ki saa gaar Fonisia nan Samaria yaka po, ŋaan bet Yisaweira nba be leŋ na a binba ki tee Juu teeb na siab lebit bi binbeŋ ki teen Yisaweira. Ki labaar maŋ din turib parpeenn ki saa yakir.

ref: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
text: Ba baar Jerusalem yoonba, ki Yisaweira nan bi saakab nan toomiinba na din gaarib saantuk. Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl nan Banabas betib linba kur ki Yennu tun namm.

ref: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
text: Ŋaan ki Yisaweira na siab nba tee Farisiinba na fiir set, ki yet a “Li kpaa talase ki boorganu na-n pot bi puna, ŋaan bia dia Moses sennu.”

ref: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
text: Li paak ki toomiinba nan saakab na lakin leeb, a bin dukin maan maŋ po.

ref: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
text: Ŋanne ki bi din pak yukir nan leeb, ki Piita tan fiir ki yet a “N naa waas, i mi nan yoo nba din gar na, Yennu din gannin a n wann Yisa barŋanii na ki tur boorganu, ki bin fit lian gbat ki teen Yisa yada ki teen u weira.

ref: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
text: Ki Yennu nba mi nisaarik par ni na, ŋɔɔe din tur boorganu u Seyeeŋ kaman nan wa kpan din turit na, ki li wann nan boorganu nba teen yada nee, ki u gaarib.

ref: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
text: U ki mɔk lukitimi, kimaan ba teen Yisa yada na paak, ki u nyik bi biit ki chabib kaman nan wa din nyik chab timm na.

ref: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
text: Li paak, bee ki i loon ki i bikin Yennu? Bee ki i loon ki i te ki boorganu nba tee Yisaweira na-n dia sennu nba ki timm nan ti yejamm din ki fit dia gbaa?

ref: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
text: Ti daa tee nna. Timm nan ŋamm jame; ti kur teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa yada ki la tinnu u ninbatinu paake.”

ref: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
text: Ŋanne ki Yisaweira na kura din ŋmin, ŋaan ji gbiintir Banabas nan Pɔɔl. Ki bi beerib bakitnauŋ toonjaana nba ki Yennu din tun namm ki tur boorganu na.

ref: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
text: Ba betib gbenn yoonba, ki Jems yetib a “N naa waas, gbiintirin man,

ref: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
text: Simon betit biaŋinba ki Yennu sint tin boorganu ninbatinu, ki nyinn niib bi ni a bii tee u mɔŋ niib.

ref: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
text: ‘Li poorpo, min, i Yomdaanɔ Yennu sa jen, ŋaan David yaaboona nba lek ki dia u naan na, see ki n gbenn dinn u yaaboonyenɔ, ki ŋamm naan maŋ,

ref: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
text: a ki li paak, nisiab kur nba be tingbouŋ na ni ki ki tee Israel teebi, ŋaan ki n gannib na a bin nakin min nba tee bi Yomdaanɔ.

ref: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
text: Ki Jems bia yet ki pukin a “Li teen n dudukit ni nan ti daa jikit linba paar ki teen boorganu na nba lebit ki waa Yennu sɔnu na paaki.

ref: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Ki la sii tee biaŋinba, li ŋan ki tin sɔb gbouŋo, ki betib ki bi daa di linba ki bi poŋ mann pata, ŋaan betib ki bii so bi mɔŋ nan bonchonchonn toona, ki bia daa ŋman bonkobuk nba ki bi ŋmakir kpii, ki bia daa di sɔmi.

ref: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
text: Moses sennu ŋarin, binba mɔɔntir sennu maŋe be, kimaan yaayoo niewa ki tan tuu dinna, bi poŋ tuu karin u sennu gbouŋ ni, Yenjiantu diit ni, foon daa kura.” Ba sɔb gbouŋ ki tur boorganu na biaŋinba

ref: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
text: Ŋanne ki toomiinba maŋ nan saakab na nan Yisaweira na kura dukin ki nyinn nisiab bi ni, a bin tun ŋamm nan Pɔɔl nan Banabas a bin saan Antiok. Ŋamme din tee Judas, wunba sann din yi Basabas na, nan Sailas. Bi jab bannlee maŋ din tee Yisaweira yudamm na yemme.

ref: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
text: Ki bi din yaa bin tumm nan gbouŋ, ki sɔb a “Timm Yisa toomiinba nan Yisaweira saakab sɔbin gbouŋ na ki foont yimm ti naa waas binba ki tee Juu teeb ki be Antiok nan Siria nan Silisia.

ref: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
text: Ti gbat ki ti nisiab baar i peŋ, a ki piaki barsai ki li daamii-i ki ŋmat i yan, ŋaan ti mun ki wannib siar, a bin tan teen nna,

ref: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
text: ki li paake te ki ti taan leeb ki soor ki gann Judas nan Sailas, ki tuumm i boor nna, ki ŋamm nan ti naa walonkai Pɔɔl nan Banabase saa,

ref: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: binba jii bi saaŋmanii ki likin ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia toona ni na.

ref: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
text: Judas nan Sailas bia sa saa beti li biaŋ biak, linba ki ti sɔb maŋ.

ref: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ nan timm kur dukin nan li ŋan ki ti te ki i dia sennu nba kpaa talase, ŋaan daa pukii linba paari.

ref: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
text: Ya sii tuun linba maŋe na: I daa di jeet nba ki bi mann pata. I bia daa di sɔmi. I biakii mi ki i ŋman bonkobuk nba ki bi ŋmakir kpii, ŋaan kii so i mɔŋ nan bonchonchonn toona. Li-i tee ki i dia i mɔŋ nna, li sii ŋan i po. Li kurii na.”

ref: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
text: Ŋanne ki bi tumm, ki bi sik Antiok, ki saa lakin taan Yisaweira na kura, ki turib gbouŋ na.

ref: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
text: Niib na nba karin gbouŋ maŋ yoonba, ki bi la parpeenn nan bi kpaanii maŋ paak.

ref: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
text: Ki Judas nan Sailas, binba din tee sɔkiniinba na, din yukir namm ki betib Yennu maan bonchiann ki cheen Yisaweira maŋ para ki totib.

ref: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
text: Bi yukiru maŋ poorpoe, ki Yisaweira na chabib, ŋaan yetib a “Yennu-n turi taaŋann.” Ki bi ŋmat kun binba tumm na boor ni.

ref: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
text: Ki Pɔɔl nan Banabas mun bia din yukir Antiok. Ki ŋamm nan leeb bonchiann din mɔɔntir ti Yomdaanɔ maan ki teen niib ki bia wantib li paak. Pɔɔl nan Banabas bɔkitu labaar

ref: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
text: Li din yann waan ki Pɔɔl yet Banabas a “Ŋaant ki tin ŋmat ki saan gɔn ti naa waas nba be digbana kur nba ki ti din saan ki saa mɔɔnt ti Yomdaanɔ maan na, ki laan ba be biaŋinba.”

ref: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Ki Banabas loon bin jii Jɔɔnn Mak,

ref: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
text: ŋaan Pɔɔl din ki sak a bin jiiu, kimaan u din tan ki joont namm bi toona na ni, ŋaan din nyikib Pamfilia, ŋaan ŋmat kun.

ref: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
text: Ki mɔniɔk din fiir Pɔɔl nan Banabas sinsuuk ni, ki bi bɔkit leeb. Ki Banabas din jii Mak, ki bi kɔɔ ŋaruŋ ki saan Saiprus.

ref: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
text: Ki Pɔɔl mun din gann Sailas, Yisaweira na ni. Ki Yisaweira na din jiib ki kubimm Yennu nuu ni, a wun sommib.

ref: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
text: Li poorpoe ki Pɔɔl nan Sailas din tan baar Debe nan Listra, ki la Yisaweitɔsɔɔ, ki u sann yi Timoti, ki u be leŋ. U naa din tee Juu nirɔ nba tee Yisaweirɔe, ŋaan ki u baa din tee Griik nirɔ.

ref: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
text: Yisaweira kur nba din be Listra nan Ikonium na din pak Timoti po bonŋann.

ref: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
text: Ki Pɔɔl bia din loon wun jiiu ki bii saa sɔnii. Ŋaan Juu teeb nba din be digbann kur yakir na ni kur din mi nan Timoti baa tee Griik nirɔe. Li paak ki Pɔɔl din pot Timoti punn.

ref: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
text: Ki bi din saa doi ni, ki teen Yisaweira na sennu nba ki Yisa toomiinba na nan saakab din senn Jerusalem na.

ref: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
text: Ki linba na chee Yisaweira maŋ para u sɔnu ni. Ki niib mun bia tee yada ki pukii Yisaweira po daar kur. Pɔɔl yerintu lanu labaar

ref: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
text: Ki bi din gar saan Frijia nan Galasia yaka po, ki loon bin saan Asia tiŋ ni, ŋaan Yennu Seyeeŋ din ki sak ki bin pak Yisa maan Asia tiŋ ni.

ref: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
text: Ba din tan baar Misia yakir terik paak, ki bi kot a bin saan Bitinia yakir po, ŋaan ki Yisa Seyeeŋ din ki sak a bin saani.

ref: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
text: Ŋanne ki bi din gar Misia yakir po, ki sik Troas.

ref: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
text: Leŋe ki Pɔɔl din la yerintu nyiɔk, ki u la ki Masedonia nirɔ, ki u baar see ki barimɔ a “Pootir mɔkgbeŋŋir na ki baar Masedonia yakir po na, ki tan sommit.”

ref: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
text: Pɔɔl nba la yerintu maŋ na, li taakpaak ni ki ti teen siir a tin saan Masedonia, kimaan ti ji din la nan Yennu betit a tin saan kii sii mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii kii teen niib na leŋ. Biaŋinba ki Lidia teen Yisaweirɔ

ref: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
text: Ŋanne ki ti bia kɔɔ ŋaruŋ, ki nyii Troas, ki tokin baar Samotras, ki li yent ki ti baar Neapolis. Leŋe ki ti nyik ŋaruŋ na,

ref: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
text: ŋaan tɔɔt ki gar baar Filipi, doo nba tee digbansaakar Masedonia yent ni na. Doo maŋ din tee Rom teeb yente. Ti din be doo maŋ ni ki li mann nan daa ŋaŋŋa nawa.

ref: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
text: Ki foon daar ki ti nyii sik doo maŋ kpenu ni. Ti din mi a Juu teeb Yenmiaru boor tee leŋe, ki ti baar kar ki pak nan poob nba tikir leŋ na.

ref: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
text: Poob nba din gbat ti maan maŋ, bi yenɔ sann din tee Lidia. U din nyii Tiatairae, ki din tee chinchenmuŋ nba ki li daauk paar na kɔɔtitɔɔ, ki bia din tee wunba jiantir Yennu. Ki Yennu din loot u par ni a wun sak Pɔɔl maan nba ki u piak na.

ref: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
text: Ki ti din wur ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb Yennu nyunwuru. Ki u din gɔɔrit, ki yet a “Li-i tee ki i mi a n set tee ti Yomdaanɔ yada nan barmɔniie, yin baar kii be n ŋaak ni.” Ŋanne ki ti baar ki kɔɔ be. Ba kɔɔn Pɔɔl nan Sailas dansarik biaŋinba

ref: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
text: Dasiar ki ti tan ŋmat saa Yenmiarboor na, ki sapaandaabir nna din mɔk u yomdamm, ki tan chetit. Ki narinbiiuk din be u ni ki yentɔ, ki narinbiiuk maŋ din teenɔ ninnyɔɔntɔɔ. U ninnyɔɔnsin maŋ ni, u din laat likirii bonchiann ki teen u damm na.

ref: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
text: Ki u din waa timm nan Pɔɔl ki tian sanpaapo a “Niib na tee Yabint Yennu daabae. Bi mɔɔntir ki teen yimm doo na teebe biaŋinba ki i sa la tinnu.”

ref: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
text: U din tian ki yikin nna ki li mann nan daa ŋaŋŋa nawa. Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl ji din tan miat nan u yikinii maŋ, ki jiant ki bet narinbiiuk nba be sapaamɔ maŋ ni na a “Yisa Masia sann ni, n tura mɔb, nyimin u ni!” Li taakpaak ni ki narinbiiuk na nyii u ni.

ref: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
text: U yomdamm nba bann nan bi liknamu sɔnu na gbennii na, ki bi soor Pɔɔl nan Sailas, ki datib baar namm bi yudamm linlakin boor ni.

ref: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
text: ki wantit bi kaasia nba ki ti sennu ki chabit a tin gaar, kimaan ti tee Rom teebe.”

ref: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
text: Ŋanne ki nibur nba be leŋ na mun jakib ki kpirib. Ki barbuura na soor Pɔɔl nan Sailas liata ki fɔb ki liat liat, ŋaan te ki bi boob nan lanbann.

ref: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
text: Ba boob bonchiann ki gbenn yoonba, ki bi ji jiib saan kɔɔmm dansarik, ŋaan bet dansarguurɔ na a wun kparib kenkenkema.

ref: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
text: Wa gbat bi mɔb maŋ, ki u mun jiib ki kɔɔmm dansarik diiuk maŋ ni pib piba, ŋaan baamm daat baana.

ref: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
text: Ki li din tan tee kaman nan tansuunii na, ki Pɔɔl nan Sailas din tan mei Yennu ki yiin yanii ki piakɔ, ki dansarkɔɔleeb na ji gbiintirib.

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
text: Yiama ki tiŋ jek bonchiann ki ŋak dansarik na fiaka. Li taakpaak ni ki tammɔi loot, ki jarit nan baana yabit yommii na kur nii nan bi taa ni, ki baa baa.

ref: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
text: Li yoo na ki dansarguurɔ na fiir gɔɔn ni, ki la yommii na tammɔi ki li loot yaa yaa, ki u dukin mi a yommii maŋ kur nyiie, ki u nɔɔt u jukbanjiak ki loon wun kpi u mɔŋ.

ref: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
text: Ki Pɔɔl tian sanpaapowa a “Daa tee a mɔŋ daŋi, kimaan ti kur be.”

ref: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
text: Ŋanne ki guurɔ na yaa a bin too muu, ŋaan faar kɔɔ yommii diiuk maŋ ni, ki gbaan wook ki jekir Pɔɔl nan Sailas tɔɔnn,

ref: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
text: ki tan fiir nyinnib, ŋaan boib a “Chanbaanba, n sa teen nlee ki la tinnu?”

ref: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
text: Ki bi jiin a “Teent ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa yada, ki a ŋaateeb-n mun teenɔ yada, ki i sa la tinnu,”

ref: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
text: ŋaan pak Yennu maan ki tur ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb kura.

ref: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
text: Li yoo maŋ nyiɔk na, ki u jiib ki wuur bi fiat na. Li poorpoe ki bi wur ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb na kura Yennu nyunwuru.

ref: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
text: Ki u bia tan jii Pɔɔl nan Sailas ki saan namm u ŋaak ni, ki saa turib jeet ki bi dii. Ki ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb ji mɔk parpeenn ki li saa yakir, kimaan ba teen Yennu yada ki kpint ki teen Yisaweira na paak.

ref: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
text: Li din yent sanyiɔko, ki Rom teeb barbuura na din tun lanjimanba dansarguurɔ na boor ni a bin yetɔ a “Ŋaan jab maŋ ki bin saan.”

ref: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
text: Ki dansarguurɔ na bet Pɔɔl a “Barbuura na tun lanjimanba a n te ki fin nan Sailas-n nyi. Li paak na, i nyi man kii saa nan laafia.”

ref: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
text: Ki Pɔɔl jiant bet lanjimanba na, “Bi daa ki bu ti buuti, ŋaan won boot nan lanbann niwor ni, ŋaan ti lek tee binba ki Rom teeb chabit bi yaak na, ki i barbuura bia won taan jiit ki lut dansarik ni, ki mɔtana ki bi ji loon bin bɔr nyinnitii? Waa! Ŋann kan teeni. Ŋaant ki barbuura maŋ-n baar bi mɔŋ ki tan nyinnit.”

ref: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
text: Li paak, ki bi baar Pɔɔl nan Sailas peŋ, ki tan teemm gafara, ŋaan nyinnib dansarik na ni, ki barimm a bin lek nyi bi doo ni.

ref: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
text: Ki Pɔɔl nan Sailas seet leŋ, ki saan kɔɔ Lidia ŋaak ni. Ki Yisaweira tan baar; ki bi pak Yisa maan ki cheen bi para, ŋaan yaat.

ref: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
text: Tɔn, ki bi ji din saa ki saa gar Amfipolis nan Apolonia, ki baar Tesalonika. Leŋe ki Juu teeb jiantu diiuk din be.

ref: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
text: Ki Pɔɔl din tan saan kɔɔ leŋ kaman nan wa din tuu kɔɔ ki tuun biaŋinba na. U din jii foon daa ŋanntaae ki kɔɔ leŋ, ki fiitir Yennu gbouŋ,

ref: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
text: ki want binba be leŋ na, ki karin Yennu gbouŋ ni, a lin wann a see ki Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia na-n di fara ki kpo; ki u kuun poorpo, ki u bia tan sa fiir jen u manfoor ni; a “Yisa nba nee ki n piaki u po, ŋɔɔe tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia maŋ.”

ref: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
text: Ki u din tan pak tunn bi siab yan, ki bi ji baar taan ŋɔɔ nan Sailas po ki waab. Ki Griik teeb bonchiann nba jiantir Yennu na, nan poojaana bonchiann, ki bi mun pukin bi po ki waa Pɔɔl nan Sailas.

ref: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
text: Juu teeb nba din la nna maŋ yoonba, ki bi donn punponn, ki lin ki tikir niyinyeka nba bi na, ki taamm, ki te ki bi muk doo na, ki tan baar kpir Jason ŋaak, ki loon Pɔɔl nan Sailas boor, a bin nyinnib niib ni.

ref: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
text: Ba ki lab yoonba, ki bi soor Jason nan Yisaweilesiab, ki datib baar namm doo na yudamm boor, ŋaan tian yeen a “Niib na lin labii tingbouŋ na ki te ki mukir muk niib, ki mɔtana ki bi tan baar ti doo na,

ref: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
text: ki Jason gaarib u ŋaak ni, ki bi lin ki want niib barsaana ki biir ti kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa sennu, ki yaa a kpanbarlɔɔe mun be, a ki bi yiu Yisa.”

ref: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
text: Ba yet nna yoonba, ki li daan nibur na nan yudamm na para ni bonchiann.

ref: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
text: Ki yudamm na te ki Jason-nba pa likirii ŋaan ki bi ŋaab. Berea doo labaar

ref: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
text: Nyiɔk nba tan bɔnn yoonba, ki Yisaweira chab Pɔɔl nan Sailas, ki bi saan Berea, ki saa baar kɔɔ Juu teeb jiantu diiuk ni.

ref: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
text: Leŋ niib yan din kɔɔ Yisa maan maŋ ni ki gar Tesalonika teeb, ki bi set din sak maan na nan bi para kura, ki daa kur ki bi karin Yennu gbouŋ, ki gorii, a bin laan Pɔɔl nba piak linba maŋ, a li set tee mɔnii amii nlee.

ref: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
text: Ŋanne ki siab bonchiann teen yada ki kpint ki teen Yisaweira. Ki Griik poojaana bonchiann nan Griik jab bonchiann mun din teen yada ki kpint ki teen Yisaweira.

ref: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
text: Ŋaan Juu teeb maŋ nba din be Tesalonika na din gbat a Pɔɔl bia mɔɔntir Yennu maan Berea, ki bi baar leŋ, ki pak ki biir Pɔɔl, ki fiin nibur na wutoa, a bin kpirɔ.

ref: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
text: Niib nba din chiant Pɔɔl na din saan nanɔ kaman nan Atens nawa, ki bi tan yaa bin ŋmat kun, ki Pɔɔl betib a bin saa bet Sailas nan Timoti a ki bin yabir ki baar yian yian na. Ki niib na ji din ŋmat kun Berea.

ref: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
text: Ki u tuu kɔɔ Yenjiantu diiuk ni ki faa labaar nan Juu teeb nan boorganu nba jiantir Yennu na. Ki u bia din tuu saan bi linlakin boor ni daa kur, ki saa faa nan binba baat leŋ na labaar, Yisa barŋanii po.

ref: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
text: Ki saakayandaansiab nna din be, ki bi yi bi yemm Epikurus teeb, ki leeb mun Stoik teeb, ki bi mun tan baar ki faa labaar nan Pɔɔl. Ki bi siab tan pak nan bi leeb a “Barifoouk daanɔ nba want u mɔŋ na mun yeen bee na?” Ki bi yemm jiin a “Li taan tee nan u piak digbangana yennii poe na.” Bi din yet nna maŋ, kimaan Pɔɔl mɔɔntir Yisa po nan u kuunfiiru po.

ref: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
text: Ŋanne ki bi jii Pɔɔl ki baar nanɔ bi saakab tintaankaanu boor nba ki bi din yi Areos Kunkonn na, ŋaan din yet Pɔɔl a “Ti sii loon ki tin bann barsaann nba ki a want na,

ref: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
text: kimaan a piak baryena, ki li tee barsaanniie ti peŋ, ki ti loon ki tin bann li paak.”

ref: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
text: Tɔn, ki Pɔɔl fiir bi tintaankaanu maŋ ni, ki set bi tɔɔnn, ŋaan yetib a “Atens teeb, n la i benu kur ni ki i loon i pata bonchiann;

ref: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
text: ki ma kak ki lin i doo na, n got i pata, ki bia la binbintir ki li sɔb li paak a ‘Yennu nba ki ti ki miu.’ N loon ki n wanni wunba ki i pukii ki teenɔ ŋaan ki miu na.

ref: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
text: Yennu maŋ, ŋɔɔe din nan dunia nan bont kur nba be li ni. Ŋɔɔe tee sanpaak nan tiŋ Yomdaanɔ, ki ki tee daanɔ nba be pochianfei nba ki niib maa ni kaa.

ref: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
text: Ki nisaarik kan fit tun siar nan u nuu ki turɔ ki lin pukinɔ siari, kimaan ŋɔɔe tee daanɔ nba teen nisaarii kur manfoa nan bona kur.

ref: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
text: Ŋɔɔe mun te ki nibooru kur nyii yeja yenɔkɔɔ ni, ki bia te ki bi be tingbouŋ na kur ni, ki u mɔŋ mɔŋ din senn yoo nba ki nibooru kur sii be, ki bia teen bi binbeboa terii.

ref: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
text: U din teen nna maŋ a bin lomɔ, a ki li pasiari bin turin ki tan nakin bannɔ. Ŋaan ŋɔɔ Yennu set ki fɔk nan ti sɔɔ.

ref: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
text: Nisɔɔ din yet a ‘Li tee Yennu paake ki ti be ki tuun.’ I yandamm pinpakit mun bia lek want nna maŋe a ‘Ti bia tee Yennu waase.’

ref: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
text: Tɔn, timm nba tee Yennu waas na, li ki ŋan ki tin dukin nan Yennu tee kaman nan siara bonninauŋ nba tee sarima amii tann nba ki nirɔ ŋammir nan u yan amii u dudukit ni.

ref: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
text: Yoo nba din gar, ki niib ki mi Yennu na, u din ki jii bi biiti. Ŋaan mɔtana u beer a sɔɔ kur-n nyik u biit,

ref: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
text: kimaan ŋɔɔ Yennu poŋ senn daar nba ki u sa bu tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb kur buut chib chib. U sa te ki sɔɔ nba ki u gannɔ na-n bu buut maŋ, ki u set fiinɔ kuun ni, ki li tee nyinn ki tur sɔɔ kur a ŋɔɔe sii tee barbuurɔ.”

ref: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
text: Ba gbat ki Pɔɔl pak kuunfiiru po na, ki siab laa ki sarikitɔ, ŋaan ki siab mun yaa a “Ti bia loon ki tin ŋamm gbiint linba ki a sa pak nee.”

ref: When Paul left the council meeting,
text: Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl din nyii ŋaan nyikib.

ref: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
text: Ki bi siab mun din teen yada ki kpint ki teen Yisaweira. Ki bi ni maŋ, Dionisius din be. Ŋɔɔe din tee daanɔ nba din tuu be Areos Kunkonn tintaankaanu ni. Ki poosɔɔ mun din be bi ni, ki u sann yi Damaris. Ki leeb mun din be ki pukimm.

ref: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
text: Leŋe ki u din saa la Juu nisɔɔ, ki u sann yi Akuila, ŋaan bi din marɔ Pontuse, ki ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaapoo Prisila nyii Itali ki baar Korint, kimaan kpanbara yudaanɔ Klɔdius din tur mɔb a Juu teeb kur-n nyi Rom. Ba be Korint na, li daa ki yukiri. Ki Pɔɔl din tan baar bi ŋaak ni,

ref: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
text: a wuu be namm, kimaan bi kur din tee gbanjabirae ki din tuun nan leeb.

ref: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
text: Ki foon daar kur u tuu kɔɔ Yenjiantu diiuk ni, ki saa faa nan Juu teeb labaar Yisa po, ki yabii u mɔŋ a wun tunn Juu teeb nan Griik teeb yan ki jiin li po.

ref: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
text: Li poorpoe ki Sailas nan Timoti din tan nyii Masedonia ki baar. Ki Pɔɔl ji din jii u yoo kur ki mɔɔntir Yisa maan ki teen Juu teeb na, ki din want nan nunmɔnn a Yisae tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia na.

ref: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
text: Ŋaan ki bi lek din ki sak maan maŋ, ki sukiiɔ. Ki u din pit pit u nii nan u taa, ŋaan betib a “Li-i tee ki i tan kɔŋ i manfoorie, li jii i mɔŋ mɔŋ yura paake. N nuu kaa jikii. La jii dinna ki saa, see ki n nyiki, ŋaan wann boorganu Yisa maan maŋ.”

ref: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
text: Ŋanne ki u nyikib ŋaan saan saa be sɔɔ ŋaak ni, ki bi yiu Titius Justus. Ki u din tee boorganu nirɔ ki din jiantir Yennu. U ŋaak maŋ din kpia nan Juu teeb jiantu diiuko.

ref: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
text: Ki jiantu diiuk maŋ yudaanɔ Krispus nan u ŋaateeb kur teen Yisa yada ki kpint ki teen u weira. Ki Korint nileeb mun bonchiann din gbat Yisa maan ki teenɔ yada ki ji tee u weira. Ki bi wurib Yennu nyunwuru.

ref: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
text: Nyayouŋo dinn ki Pɔɔl din tan la yerintu. Ki u yerintu maŋ ni ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa betɔ a “Daa tiin jaŋmaanii, ŋaan kii mɔɔntir n maan maŋ ki daa nyikiti,

ref: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
text: kimaan n be nana, ki sɔɔ kaa ki sa kpira ki teena daŋi, kimaan niib bonchiann be doo na ni ki sii tee n yab.”

ref: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
text: Li paak Pɔɔl din be leŋ binn nan ŋmaarii ŋannloob, ki din want niib Yennu maan.

ref: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
text: Tɔn, yoo nba ki Galio din tee Akaya tiŋ na yudaanɔ, ki Juu teeb din tan taan leeb ki lek Pɔɔl ki soorɔ, ki saan nanɔ buut boor,

ref: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
text: ki saa yet a “Jɔɔ na paant niib ba jiantir biaŋinba na, ki li mun biir ti sennu.”

ref: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
text: Pɔɔl nba din yaa wun pak yoonba, ki Galio yet Juu teeb maŋ a “Yimm Juu teeb, li-i tee ki maan na tee mabiiuk amii mɔyêtukue, li bo sii ŋan ki n di sukuru ki gbiinti,

ref: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
text: ŋaan la tee maan nba jii ki jiin i mɔŋ mɔmaan, nan i sana, nan i mɔŋ sennu naie, i mɔŋ mɔŋ-n got li ni, kimaan n ki sak a n sa bu li mabooru,”

ref: Then he sent them out of the court.
text: ŋaan ber nyinnib buut boor maŋ.

ref: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
text: Ŋanne ki bi soor Sosenes wunba tee Juu teeb jiantu diiuk yudaanɔ, ki bia tunn jen nanɔ buut maŋ boor ni na, ki tan boou niib na ni, ŋaan ki Galio baka taan kaa bi po. Ba jen Antiok biaŋinba

ref: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
text: Ki Pɔɔl din daa be nan Yisaweira nba be Korint ŋmaarii bonchiann. Ŋanne ki u din tan chabib, ŋaan yaa wun saan Antiok nba be Siria tiŋ ni na. Ki ŋɔɔ nan Prisila nan Akuila saan Senkrea ki kɔɔ ŋaruŋ. Ŋaan ba yaa bin kɔɔ ŋaruŋ ki saan na, ki Pɔɔl por por nan Yennu ŋaan koor u yut.

ref: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
text: Ŋanne ki bi din saa ki saa baar Efesus, siaminba ki Prisila nan Akuila yaa bin biar na; ki Pɔɔl saan saa kɔɔ Juu teeb Yenjiantu diiuk ni, ki faa labaar nan Juu teeb maŋ, Yisa Masia po.

ref: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
text: Ki bi tan yaa a wun biar namm ki yukir, ŋaan ki u ki saki.

ref: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
text: Wa tan baar Siisarea yoonba ki u tɔɔt gar Jerusalem ki saa foont Yisaweira ŋaan gar sik Antiok biak,

ref: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
text: ki saa yukir leŋ, ki bia tan nyii leŋ ki saa Galasia nan Frijia yaka po, ki saa cheen Yisaweira kur para. Apolos Po

ref: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
text: Ŋanne ki Juu jasɔɔ nna din be, ki u sann yi Apolos, ŋaan bi din marɔ Aleksandria, ki u din tan baar Efesus. U din tee mamin daanɔe, ki din mi Yennu gbouŋ bonchiann.

ref: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
text: Ki bi din poŋ wannɔ ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sɔnuewa. Ki u tan baar ki din piak want Yisa po bonŋann nan nunmɔnn, ŋaan u daa din ki bann Yisa maan maŋ kuri, kimaan u kpan din mi Jɔɔnn nba din wuur niib Yennu nyunwuru biaŋinba kuukɔɔe.

ref: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
text: Ki u din piin piak Yennu maan nan parcheenn, Juu teeb jiantu diiuk ni. Ki Prisila nan Akuila gbat u maan maŋ, ki tan jiiu ki fiit Yennu sɔnu bonchiann fanu ki wannɔ.

ref: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Li poorpoe ki Apolos dukin a wun saan Akaya. Ki Yisaweira nba din be Efesus na sommɔ, ki sɔb gbouŋ ki tur Yisaweira nba be Akaya na, a u-i taŋŋi baar, bin gaarɔ saantuk. Wa tan baar leŋ yoonba, ki u set tot binba teen yada Yennu ninbatinu po,

ref: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: kimaan ŋɔɔ nan Juu teeb nba din tan nɔi nan leeb, ki u din jikit Yennu gbouŋ, ki wantib a Yisae set tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia, ki din pak nan paŋ ki nyannib niwor ni.

ref: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
text: Apolos nba din daa be Korint na, ki Pɔɔl ŋarin din gar Galasia nan Frijia yaka po ki saan Efesus. Leŋe ki u din saa la Yisaweisiab,

ref: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
text: ki boib a “Ya teen Yisa yada ki waau na, i mi ki la Yennu Seyeeŋii?” Ki bi jiin a “Aaii. Ti poŋ taan ki mi ki gbat a Yennu Seyeeŋ be kaawa gbaa.”

ref: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
text: Ki Pɔɔl boib a “Ki ŋann, bi wuri Yennu nyunwuru nan ŋmee sann ni?” Ki bi yetɔ a “Bi wurit kaman nan Jɔɔnn nba din wuur niib biaŋinbae.”

ref: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
text: Ki Pɔɔl betib a “Jɔɔnn din wuur niib Yennu nyunwuru, ki li want a bi nyik bi biite, ŋaan beerib a bin teen wunba tan sa baar na yada, ki ŋɔɔe tee Yisa.”

ref: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Ba gbat nna yoonba, ki bi ji wurib Yennu nyunwuru nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann ni.

ref: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
text: Ki Pɔɔl jii u nii ŋaan yii bi paak, ki Yennu Seyeeŋ sik kɔɔ bi ni, ki bi ji piak barganii, ki bia piak sɔkint Yennu mɔmaan.

ref: There were about twelve men in this group.
text: Jab maŋ din tee kaman nan piik nan bannlee nee.

ref: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
text: Ŋmaarii ŋanntaa nie ki Pɔɔl ji din saan Juu teeb jiantu diiuk ni, ki want niib na nan parcheenn, ŋaan faa labaar namm, ki yabii u mɔŋ a wun kpint bi yan ki jikit saa Yennu naan po.

ref: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
text: Ŋaan niib na siab din tee tubkangbata damme, ki din ki sak maan na, ŋaan piak barbiit nibur na ni, ki biir ti Yomdaanɔ weiu sɔnu. Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl nyikib, ŋaan jii Yisaweira na ki saan namm Tiranus wannu diiuk ni; ki daar kur jik ki u faa labaar nan niib leŋ.

ref: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
text: Li din jii nna ki baar bina ŋannlee. Ki niib kura nba din be Asia tiŋ ni ki tee Juu teeb, nan nileeb kura, din gbat ti Yomdaanɔ maan maŋa. Skefa waas labaar

ref: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
text: Li yoo na ki Yennu din te ki Pɔɔl tuun toonjaana nba be li kɔɔ.

ref: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
text: Ki u din tuu jii u nunfianii ki liat tiat u mɔŋ paak gbaa, ki niib baat ki tan gaan ki paa baatib paak, ki bi yiarii na nyikitib, ki narinbiit nyi baatib ni biak.

ref: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
text: Li yoo na ki Juu nisiab din be, ki din tuu lin ki nyint narinbiit niib ni, ŋaan ji din tan yi ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann a bii nyint narinbiit, ki bi din beer narinbiit na a “Timme turi mɔb nan Yisa sann ni, wunba ki Pɔɔl mɔɔntir u maan na, nyimin man.”

ref: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
text: Binba din tuun nna maŋ din tee jab bannlore nba din tee Juu teeb jiantu yudaansɔɔ Skefa waase.

ref: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
text: Ba tan ya bin nyinn narinbiiuk jasɔɔ nna ni, ki narinbiiuk na yetib a “N mi Yisa po, ki bia mi Pɔɔl po, ŋaan ki yimm munii, i tee ŋmee?”

ref: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
text: Ki jɔɔ nba ki narinbiiuk na din be u ni na yukir ki tan chibib, bii! nan paŋ, ki nyannib, ki turib danii, ki bia pat pat bi tiat, ki bi kura chiar gbananbira, ki nyii u ŋaak ni.

ref: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Yoo nba ki Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb kura nba be Efesus na gbat nna, ki jaŋmaanii ji soor bi kura, ki bi donn ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann bonchiann.

ref: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
text: Ŋanne ki Yisaweira na bonchiann pak kat bi nɔi ni biit, niib ni, ki wann linba ki bi tuu tuun.

ref: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
text: Ki nyɔkluura bonchiann ji tikir bi gbant nan bi saba ki baar nann, ki tan joo muu niib kur nunii ni; ki bi kan gbant na yabint likirii, ki li tee pɔŋ tusa piiŋŋmu.

ref: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
text: Ŋanne din te ki ti Yomdaanɔ maan ji din ŋammit yat ki kɔɔ niib ni, ki pukii nan paŋ. Efesus mukir labaar

ref: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
text: Li poorpo ki Yennu Seyeeŋ din te ki Pɔɔl dukin a wun saan Masedonia nan Akaya, ŋaan saa paat ki gar saan Jerusalem, ki yet a li poorpo, see ki u saan bann Rom.

ref: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
text: Li paak ki u tun Timoti nan Erastus, u sommteeb ni bannlee, a bin gar u tɔɔnn po ki saan Masedonia, ŋaan ki u din biar Asia tiŋ ni.

ref: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
text: Li yoo maŋ daamuk bonbiir din fiir, kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa weiu paak.

ref: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
text: Ki jasɔɔ nna mun din be ki u sann yi Demetrius, ki u tuu jikit sarima ki din maa bi yennu nba ki bi yiu Diana na pochianfei li bonninana, ki li tee pata, ki u kɔi; ki din jii niib ki bi tuun u toona, ki bi laat likirii bonchiann.

ref: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
text: Ŋanne ki u tan yiimm ki taan bi kura, ki bia taan binba kur kur tuun li toonbooru na, ki tan betib a “N ninjamm, i mi nan ti nyɔɔt nyi ti toona na nie.

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
text: I mɔŋ mɔŋ mun poŋ gbat ki bia la nan Pɔɔl nba yeen linba na. U yaa a nirɔ nba maa yennu nan u nii, a ŋann ki tee yennu kaa, ki u maan na tunn niib yan bonchiann Efesus nna, ki maan na bia jii nna ki saan per Asia tiŋ kura.

ref: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
text: Li pasiar ki ti toona na sann tan sa kpet mann biir, ki bia jenna, ki ti yabint yennu Diana pochianfeek tan sa kpet mann ki teen yann, ki bia jenna, ki ti yennu Diana nibaakir tan sa mann ki waab, daanɔ nba ki sɔɔ kur nba be Asia tiŋ ni nan tingbouŋ na kura jiantirɔ na.”

ref: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Nibur na nba gbat nna yoonba, ki bi wutoa fiir, ki bi tian tintianii, ki yaa a “Timm Efesus teeb yennu Diana tee yabint yennue!”

ref: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
text: Ki doo na kur muk. Ki bi soor Gaius nan Aristakus ki chiar saan kɔɔ namm nitituk ni. Jab bannlee maŋ din tee Masedonia niib, binba ki Pɔɔl tuu tɔkii namm na.

ref: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
text: Ki Pɔɔl loon ki wun kɔɔ niib na ni, ŋaan ki Yisaweira ki sak a wun kɔɔ.

ref: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
text: Ki doo na yudamm ni siab nba din tee Pɔɔl yɔɔsinba din tun toomii a Pɔɔl-n di sukuru, ki daa kɔɔ nitituk maŋ ni.

ref: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
text: Li yoo na, nitituk maŋ ni, siab din tian tintianii, ki yakii kpayakit jiak jiak, ki niib nba tikir na yan ŋmat saa gar, kimaan bi bonchiann din ki bann bi lakinu maŋ paaki.

ref: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
text: Tɔn, ki Juu teeb nyinn jasɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Aleksander, ki sennɔ bi tɔɔnn, a wun pak bi paak ki turib. Ki Aleksander donn nuu sanpaapo, ŋaan ji loon wun pak tur nibur na ki nyinn Juu teeb mɔniɔk maŋ ni.

ref: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Ŋaan ba tan bann nan u tee Juu nirɔ na, ki bi ji ki sak a wun paki, ŋaan tian tintianii, ki yeen a “Timm Efesus teeb yennu Diana tee yabint yennue.” Ki bi kur tian nna ki yukir bonchiann.

ref: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
text: Li joontu ki doo maŋ saakɔɔ te ki nibur na ŋmin, ŋaan ki u betib a “Yimm Efesus teeb, sɔɔ kaa tingbouŋ na ni ki ki mi nan Efesus teebe guu ki gorii yabint Diana pochianfeeki, ŋaan mun guu ki gorii tanŋann nba nyii sanpaapo ki tan baa na.

ref: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
text: Sɔɔ bia kaa ki sa fit nɔi linba na. Li paak, li ŋan ki yii di sukuru, ki daa tee jatii,

ref: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
text: kimaan i baar nan niib na nna na, ŋaan ki bi ki jan ti pata bonti. Bi bia ki piak ki sukii biir ti yennu.

ref: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
text: Li-i tee ki Demetrius nan u toontunleeb mɔk maan nan sɔɔie, ŋann buut boor poŋ be ki mɔk li daa, ki buut damm mun bia be. Ŋaant bin saan kii sii pootir bi leeb leŋ.

ref: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki i mɔk barsai nba ki tee ki i saan nann buut boor maŋi, li ŋan ki yi saan i mɔŋ yudamm boor.

ref: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
text: “Li pasiar Rom teeb tan sa mann la ti biit, ki yet a ti tee doyeerdamme, ki jikit saa linba teen dinna na po, ki li ki mɔk paaki, ki ti mun bia kan fit wann li paak ki jikit saa daamuk na po.”

ref: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
text: U yet nnee, ŋaan ji yat niib na.

ref: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
text: Fuut maŋ nba din ŋmin na poorpoe, ki Pɔɔl ji din yiin Yisaweira na, ki tan chee bi para, ŋaan ji chabib, ki yaat ki saan Masedonia tiŋ ni,

ref: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
text: ki din lin Masedonia tiŋ yaka po, ki bia pak Yisaweira nba be leŋ na Yennu maan bonchiann ki kɔɔntib para, ŋaan gar saan Akaya,

ref: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
text: ki ji be leŋ ki saa jaŋ ŋmaarii ŋanntaa. Ki li poorpo, ki u ji tee siir a wun saan Siria, ŋaan bann nan Juu teeb lorin lora ki guu-u, ki u ji loon wun ŋmat ki saan gar Masedonia tiŋ ni.

ref: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
text: Ki Pirus bik Sopata, wunba nyii Berea na, nan Aristakus nan Sekondus, binba nyii Tesalonika na, nan Gaius wunba nyii Debe na, nan Tikikus nan Trofimus, binba nyii Asia tiŋ ni na, nan Timoti, ŋamme din saan nan Pɔɔl.

ref: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
text: Ki bi gar tɔɔnn ki ji saa guutit Troas.

ref: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
text: Jaamm nba ki bi din tuu di boroboro nba kaa datiŋ na dinu poorpoe, ki ti ji din kɔɔ ŋaruŋ, ki nyii Filipi, ki saa Troas. Ki daa ŋaŋŋmu daar ki ti baar bi boor Troas, ki biar leŋ ki jaŋ daa ŋannlore. Troas doo labaar

ref: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
text: Tɔn, ki foon daar dajouk ki ti tikir leeb, ki taan nan Yisaweira nba be leŋ na, a ti di ti Yomdaanɔ jeet. Ki Pɔɔl mɔɔnt Yennu maan dik dik dik nan tansuunii, kimaan la sa yent, ki wun gar na paak.

ref: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
text: Ti din tikir ditɔɔtuk nna nie, ki bi din joo fita bonchiann leŋ.

ref: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
text: Ki naasinsɔɔ nna mun din be ki u sann yi Yutikus, ki u din kar ditɔɔtuk na takorbonn paak. Ki Pɔɔl mɔɔnt Yennu maan mɔkmɔk, ki u kar tiŋŋit gɔɔn, ki ji tan gɔɔn ki libit nyii ditɔɔtuk munntaa na ni, ki tan baa tiŋ ni. Ki bi sik jiiu, ki sɔɔ ki u kpoa.

ref: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
text: Ki Pɔɔl nyii ditɔɔtuk na ni, ki somm sik tan fabin u paak, ki diau, ŋaan yet a “I daa te ki i para biiri. U fo,”

ref: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
text: ŋaan ŋmat ki doo ditɔɔtuk na ni, ki saa dii jeet. Li poorpo biak ki u mɔɔnt Yennu maan mɔkmɔk nan la tan yent, ki u ji nyikib, ŋaan yaan yaat gar.

ref: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
text: Ŋaan ki bi ji jii naasimɔ na ŋarin, ki u mɔk manfoor, ki bi kun nanɔ nan parpeenn, ki li ki tee waaminna.

ref: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
text: Li poorpo ki ti bia kɔɔ ŋaruŋ, ki dɔŋ gar saan Asos. Leŋe ki ti din yaa ti jii Pɔɔl, kimaan ŋɔɔ din betit a tin chetɔ. U din loon wun somm taatiŋe ki baar leŋ. Pɔɔl nba baat Miletus biaŋinba

ref: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
text: Wa din baar Asos yoonba, ki u lat. Ki ti ji jiiu ki kɔɔnɔ ŋaruŋ na ni, ki saan Mitilene.

ref: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
text: Ki ti bia tan tɔɔt leŋ ki baar Kios li sanyafannuk. Ki li bia yent ki ti baar Samos, ki li bia tan yent ki ti baar Miletus.

ref: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
text: Pɔɔl din ki loon a wun saan Efesusi, kimaan u din ki loon ki wun yukir Asia tiŋ ni, ŋaan u din loon yian yian, kimaan u din loon a li-i sa fit, wun baar Jerusalem dajaann nba ki bi yi Pentekost na. Biaŋinba ki Pɔɔl chabit Yisaweira saakab

ref: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
text: Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl din tun toomii a bin nyi Miletus doo ni, a ki saan Efesus ki saa yiin Yisaweira saakab na.

ref: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
text: Ba baar yoonba, ki u betib a “I mi n binbeŋ nba tuu be biaŋinba, ma din sint baar Asia tiŋ nna ki jikit saa.

ref: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
text: I bia mi ma din tuun Yennu toona nan sikininmɔŋ bonchiann, nan ninnyuut, ki bia la fara Juu teeb lora paak.

ref: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
text: Ki i bia mi nan ma din mɔɔntir Yennu maan, ki bia wanti niib ni nan i ŋei ni na, n din paki linba kur sa sommi, ki ki nyiki, ki bia ki tenn siari,

ref: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
text: ki bia kpaan Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb kur a bin nyik bi biit kii waa Yennu sɔnu, ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa yada.

ref: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
text: “Tɔn, ki mɔtana Yennu Seyeeŋe mabin ki n yaa n saan Jerusalem, ŋaan n ki mi linba sa saa teenin leŋi.

ref: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
text: Ŋaan ma lin doo kur na ni, Yennu Seyeeŋe tuu wantin nan nunmɔnn a dansarik nan fara tan sii tee n yare Jerusalem.

ref: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Ŋaan n ki yi n manfoor ki li tee siari, kimaan min ŋarin loon ki n tun toona nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa turin nee ki gbenn, ki li toona tee ki mii mɔɔntir barŋanii nba want Yennu ninbatinu poe.

ref: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
text: “Nnara, i mi nan n din lin i kura ŋei ni, ki mɔɔntir Yennu naan labaar. Tɔn, ki mɔtana, n mi nan i ji kan mi ki lami.

ref: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
text: Li paake te ki n beeri dinna daar na, ahoo, wunba kɔŋ manfoor nba kaa gbennu na, n nuu kaa jiki,

ref: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
text: kimaan n ki mi ki fɔrint ki ŋmat poorpo nan ki n mɔɔnt Yennu maan maŋ kur ki wanniini.

ref: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
text: Li paak, i dia i mɔŋ man bonŋann, nan Yisaweira nba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ kubin i nuu ni a yii tee bi yudamm kii goriib. Ŋamme ki Yennu jiib ki teemm u niib, kimaan u Bija Yisa nba din kpo na paak.

ref: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
text: N mi nan mi-i kaa, nibiit tan sa baar i sinsuuk ni, ki biir Yisaweira siab kaman nan kinkata nba tuu so pei biaŋinba na.

ref: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
text: Li paak, i guu man, ki daa tammit ma din kpaan i kur nan ninnyuut, ki ki nyik, yennu nan nyiɔk, ki tan jaŋ bina ŋanntaa na ni.

ref: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
text: “Mɔtana, n jikiti ki teei Yennu nuu ni, ki bia mi nan ya bann u maan nba want u ninbatinu po, li maan maŋ sa fit te ki i waau fanu, ki bia sa fit turi yiar sanpaapo, binba kur ki u teemm u niib na sinsuuk ni.

ref: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
text: I mɔŋ bia mi nan n ki nian sɔɔ likirii amii tiati.

ref: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
text: Ma din be i sinsuuk ni na, i mɔŋ mɔŋ bia mi nan n din dia n nii nee ki tuun, ki min nan n weinanleeb di ki bia gbab.

ref: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
text: Ma din tuun nna maŋ paake want nan see ki tii tuun bonchiann ki fit ki somm talasdamm. Li ŋan ki ti tiar ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa maan, nan wa yet yaa a ‘Parpeenn sii tee daanɔ nba piin piinii yare, ki gar wunba gaan.’ ”

ref: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
text: Pɔɔl nba gbenn maan maŋ yoonba, ki bi kura lakin gbaan ki miar Yennu.

ref: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
text: kimaan bi para mantik din biir nan wa yaa a bi ji kan lau na paak. Ki bi din chianɔ ki saan nanɔ mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni, a bin saa chabɔ ki wun kɔɔ ŋaruŋ.

ref: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
text: Ŋanne ki ti din tat nyik Yisaweira saakab maŋ, ŋaan tokin baar Kos, ki li yent ki ti baar Rodes, ki bia nyii leŋ ki saan Patara.

ref: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
text: Leŋe ki ti din la ŋaruŋ nba saa Fonisia tiŋ ni. Ŋanne ki ti kɔɔ ŋaruŋ maŋ ki saa.

ref: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
text: Ta tan laat Saiprus yoonba na, ki ti gar ti gaŋ po, ki tan baar Siria tiŋ ni, ki nyii ŋaruŋ na ni, doo nba ki bi yi Taya na ni, ki ŋaruŋ maŋ damm din yaa bin nyinn tiat leŋ.

ref: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
text: Ki ti saanu yoo tan baar nan tin saan, ki ŋamm nan bi poob nan bi waas din chianit, ki saa nyii doo na ni, ki dianit. Ki ti kura gbaan mɔk-kpiŋ ki miar Yennu.

ref: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
text: Ki li poorpo ki ti chab leeb, ŋaan yaan kɔɔ ŋaruŋ ki saa, ki bi mun ŋmat ki kun.

ref: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
text: Ŋanne ki ti nyii Taya ki baar Tolemais. Leŋe ki ti saan foont Yisaweira, ŋaan biar bi boor ni dayenn kuukɔɔ.

ref: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
text: Ki li yent sanyiɔk ki ti nyii Tolemais ki baar Siisarea ki saan kɔɔ Filip ŋaak ni, wunba mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii na, ki be. U din tee jab bannlore nba ki bi din gannib Jerusalem a bii tee jetorteeb yudamm na yenɔe.

ref: and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
text: Ki u din mɔk sapaamm banna, ki bi mun din tee binba sɔkint Yennu maan.

ref: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
text: Ti din be leŋ ki teen kaman nan daa ŋaŋŋa nawa; ki Yennu sɔkinii, wunba ki bi yiu Agabus na din nyii Judea ki sik,

ref: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
text: ki baar ti boor ni, ki tan jii Pɔɔl gbann, ki lor u mɔŋ mɔŋ taa nan u nii, ŋaan yet a “Yennu Seyeeŋ nba yaa biaŋinbae na, a gbann na daanɔ, a Juu teeb nba be Jerusalem na tan sa lorɔ nnee, ŋaan jiiu ki kubinɔ binba ki tee Juu teeb na nuu ni.”

ref: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
text: Ta ji din gbat nna maŋ, ki ti kura barin Pɔɔl a u ji daa saa Jerusalemi.

ref: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
text: Ŋaan ki u ji din jiin a “Bee teen ki i mɔ ki loon ki i kɔnn n kpinkpenti? Ba tan sa lorin Jerusalem na paak kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan min ŋarin poŋ teen siir nan ki n kpo ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa paaka.”

ref: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
text: Wa yet nna na, ti mi nan ti ji gbarɔii na, ki ti nyikɔ, ŋaan biar ŋmin, ŋaan yet a “Li fan nan ti Yomdaanɔ.”

ref: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
text: Ti yukiru maŋ poorpoe, ki ti ji din teen siir ki saa Jerusalem.

ref: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
text: Ki Yisaweira nba be Siisarea na, ki bi siab mun din weit ki saan. Ŋanne ki bi din jiit, ki ti saan Minason ŋaak ni. Minason maŋ poŋ din tee Yisaweikpere ki din tee Saiprus nirɔ. Pɔɔl nba gɔn Jems biaŋinba
