ref: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
text: Ta din baar Jerusalem yoonba, ki leŋ Yisaweira gaarit nan parpeenn bonchiann.

ref: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
text: Ki li yent sanyiɔk, ki timm nan Pɔɔl saan a ti saa foont Jems. Yisaweira saakab kura mun din be leŋ li yoo.

ref: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
text: Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl foontib, ŋaan kat wannib linba kur ki Yennu tun ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na, ŋɔɔ Pɔɔl mɔɔntii na po.

ref: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
text: Ba ji din gbat u maan maŋ na, ki bi kura pak Yennu, ŋaan bet Pɔɔl a “Ti naa bik, a mi Juu teeb nba tee tusa biaŋinba ki teen Yisa yada ki waa u sɔnu na, ki bi kura bia dia Moses sennu ken ken ken,

ref: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
text: ki bia gbat a po, a a tuu want Juu teeb kura nba be boorganu tinii ni a bin yêt Moses sennu, ki bia beerib a bi daa porii bi waas puna, a ki bia daa waa Juu teeb na sennii.

ref: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
text: Ki a sa teen nlee na? kimaan Juu teeb nba tee Yisaweira na lek sa gbat nan a baar na.

ref: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
text: Li paak, ti-i beta biaŋinba, fan tun li biaŋ. Jab bannae be nna ki por por nan Yennu.

ref: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
text: Tɔn, fin nan ŋamm-ii saa ki saa ŋamm i mɔŋ, ŋaan ki a pa likirii bi paak, ki bin koor bi yura, ki sɔɔ kur-n fit lian bann nan maan nba ki bi tuu piak a po na, li set ki tee mɔnii kaa, ŋaan a mɔŋ poŋ waa Moses sennu maŋ nan li sɔnu.

ref: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Ŋaan binba ki tee Juu teeb ŋaan teen Yisa yada ki waau na ŋarin, ti poŋ sɔb gbouŋ ki turib, ki betib a bi ji daa mi ki di jeet nba ki bi mann patiri, amii ki ŋman sɔmi, amii bonkobuk nba ki bi ŋmakir ki kpii, ŋaan bia soor bi mɔŋ nan bonchonchonn toona.”

ref: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
text: Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl din jii jab maŋ, ki li tan yent sanyiɔk, ki ŋɔɔ nan ŋamm din ŋamm bi mɔŋ, ki fit lian kɔɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni, a bin wann nan daa nba ki bi sii be nan ba tan sa baar nan piinii ki tur Yennu, ki gbenn bi mɔpora maŋ. Pɔɔl sooru labaar

ref: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
text: Daa ŋannlore daar nba tan per yoonba, ki Juu teeb nba nyii Asia ki baar Jerusalem ki be Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni na, ki bi tan la Pɔɔl, ki tian u paak, ŋaan te ki niib na kur fiir jir jir jir, ki soorɔ,

ref: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
text: ŋaan bia tian tintianii a “Juu teeb, sommit man! Ŋɔɔe tee nirɔ nba lin lokir kur po, ki want niib ki biirib nan Juu teeb nan Moses sennu nan Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak na, ki bia taan baat nan binba ki tee Juu teeb gbaa ti jiantu ŋasaakak na ni, ki bi tan biir ti ŋaak nba yeen na.”

ref: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
text: Ba din dɔŋ la Pɔɔl nan Trofimus, wunba din tee Efesus nirɔ na, ki bi be doo na ni, ki bi dukin ki mi a Pɔɔl kɔɔ nanɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak na nie.

ref: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
text: Li paak ki doo na niib kur din fiir, ki niib kura chiar, ki baar kpir Pɔɔl, ki soorɔ ki piit nyinnɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak na ni. Li taakpaak ni na, ki siab loon ŋaak na tammɔi.

ref: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
text: Ki bi ji yaa bin kpi Pɔɔl. Li yoo na ki Rom teeb lanjimanba yudaanɔ ji gbat a Jerusalem teeb kura fiir jir jir jir.

ref: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text: Ki u kakit yiama, ki jii lanjimanba nan bi saakab, ki bi faar sik nibur na ni. Niib na nba lau nan lanjimanba na yoonba, ki bi ji nyik Pɔɔl boot.

ref: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
text: Ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na saan soor Pɔɔl, ŋaan tur mɔb a bin baanɔ jarit ŋannlee, ŋaan boib a “Nirɔ na tee ŋmee? A u biir bia?”

ref: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
text: Ki nibur na ni, siab tian Pɔɔl paak jiak jiak. Bi barŋmatuk maŋ paak ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na ki fit bann li paak bonŋanni. Li paak, ki u tur mɔb a bin jiiu ki saan nanɔ tabdiiuk ni.

ref: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
text: Ba baar tabdiiuk na tammɔb taaŋmaata paak, ki lanjimanba na jiiu sanpaapo, kimaan nigbouŋ nba din loon bin kpiu na paak.

ref: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
text: Ki bi ji din waa bi poor ki tian tintianii a “Kpimɔ man! Kpimɔ man!” Linba ki Pɔɔl pak tur nibur na

ref: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
text: Lanjimanba maŋ nba ji din yaa bin kɔɔ nanɔ na, ki u boi lanjimanba yudaanɔ na a “A sa sak ki n pak siar ki turanii?” Ki u jiin boiɔ a “A gbia Griik maamii?

ref: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
text: Ki Pɔɔl jiin a “N tee Juu nirɔe, ŋaan bi marin Tasus nba be Silisia yakir nie. N poŋ tee digbangbeŋŋir ni nirɔe. N barimae, ŋaant ki n pak ki tur niib na.”

ref: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
text: Ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na chabɔ mɔb. Ki Pɔɔl ŋmant ki set tabdiiuk tammɔb taaŋmaata na paak, ŋaan ŋak u nuu ki tur niib na, a bin ŋmin. Ba ŋmin yoonba, ki u pak namm Juu maan ni, ki yet a

ref: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
text: “Chanbaanba nan n naa waas, gbiintir man maan nba ki n yaa n pak ki turi na.”

ref: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
text: Ba gbat ki u piak bi mɔŋ mɔŋ maan ni na, ki bi ji ŋmin soonna. Ki u piak ki saa a

ref: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
text: “N tee Juu nirɔe, ŋaan ki bi marin Tasus nba be Silisia yakir ni, ŋaan ki n tan ŋmant nirɔ Jerusalem nna. Ki n wanntɔɔ din tee Gamaliele. Ki u din wantin ti yejamm sennu chib chib. Ki n lek din waa Yennu nan nunmɔnn, kaman nan i kur nba waau dinna biaŋinba na.

ref: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
text: Ma bia din tuu la niib nba waa Yisa sɔnu na, n din tuu mukisirib, ki bia poŋ kpib gbaa, ki bia din so jasiab nan poosiab ki kɔɔntib dansarii.

ref: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
text: “Tɔn, ki dasiar ki n tan saa ki saa per Damaskus yensuuk ni, min taan taantir sɔɔ ki yentsaakar nyikint sanpagbann ni, ki sik yent ki lintima.

ref: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
text: Ki n baa tiŋ ni, ki gbat kunkɔr yin a ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bee ki a mukisirimi?’

ref: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
text: Ki n jiin ki boi ma ‘N Yomdaanɔ, ŋmee?’ Ki u betin a ‘Min, Nasaret nirɔ Yisae, ki a mukisirin maŋ.’

ref: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
text: Niib nba din chiantin na ŋarin din la yentu nawa, ŋaan ŋamm din ki gbat kunkɔr nba piak nanin na ŋarimi.

ref: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
text: “Ŋanne ki n boiɔ ‘N Yomdaanɔ, n sa teen nlee?’ Ki ti Yomdaanɔ betin a ‘Fiit kii saa Damaskus, ki bi sa saa wanna linba kur ki Yennu nyinn a a tun.’

ref: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
text: Tɔn, n din jɔɔna, kimaan yentu nba din yent ki nyɔkit n ninbina ni na paak. Ki n tɔknanleeb na ji din dat n nuu ki saan nanin Damaskus.

ref: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
text: “Ki jasɔɔ nna mun din be Damaskus, ki u sann yi Ananias, ki u din tee yentinɔ, ki bia din dia Moses sennu fanu, ki Juu teeb kura nba be Damaskus na din teenɔ nibaakir bonchiann.

ref: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
text: Ki u din baar n peŋ ki set, ŋaan yetin a ‘N naa bik Sɔɔl, nyɔɔntir!’ Li taakpaak ni na ki n nyɔɔnt ki lau.

ref: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
text: Ki u betin a ‘Yennu nba tee ti yejamm Yennu na poŋ ganna a a bann u yanbɔɔtoona, a ki bia la popeendaanɔ Masia, a ki bia gbat u mɔŋ mɔŋ kunkɔr,

ref: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
text: Li paak ki a ji kar ki guu bia? Fiit, ki n wura Yennu nyunwuru, ki Yennu sa nyik chab a toonbiit, fa sii yi Yisa sann na paak.’

ref: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
text: “Tɔn, ki n ji din ŋmat Jerusalem, ki saa kɔɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni, ki mei Yennu, ki tan la yerintu.

ref: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
text: Ki n yerintu maŋ ni, ki n la ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa, ki u betin a ‘Kakitir ki nyi Jerusalem yian yian, kimaan li-i tee ki a beer Jerusalem teeb n maan, bi kan gaar maan maŋi.’

ref: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
text: Ki n jiin a ‘N Yomdaanɔ, bi poŋ tuu mi nan mine tuu lin kɔɔ Yenjiantu diit ni, ki so niib nba waa a sɔnu na, ki boob kɔɔntib dansarii.

ref: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
text: Ki bia jenna, ba din yaa bin kpi a siara daanɔ Stiifen na, n mɔŋ mɔŋ mun din be leŋ, ki din loon ki bin kpiu. Mine bia din dia niib nba din kpiu na tiat.’

ref: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
text: Ki ti Yomdaanɔ betin a ‘Finii saawa, a mine yaa n tuma niib nba be banfɔka ki ki tee Juu teeb na tinii ni.’ ”

ref: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
text: Ki bi yikin wuyikinii ki tian, ŋaan liatir bi tiat ki pat ŋakin, ŋaan wo tant ki yarin sanpaapo, bi wutoa nba doo na paak.

ref: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
text: Ŋanne ki Rom lanjimanba yudaanɔ na tur mɔb a bin jii Pɔɔl ki kɔɔ nanɔ tabdiiuk na ni, ŋaan bia betib a bin boou, ki wun bann biit nba ki u tun ki Juu teeb tian tintianii maŋ.

ref: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
text: Ki bi lorɔ, ŋaan ji yaa bin boou. Ŋanne ki u boi lanjimanba saakabik nba see leŋ na a “Min nba ki Rom teeb chabin yaak, ya daa ki bu n buuti, sennu na chabi a yin boomii?”

ref: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
text: Lanjimanba saakabik maŋ nba gbat nna yoonba, ki u saan bi yudaanɔ na peŋ, ki saa yetɔ a “A karee? Rom teeb tur jɔɔ na bi yaaka.”

ref: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
text: Ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na mun fiir, ki tan baar boi Pɔɔl a “Betirin, Rom teeb set tura bi yaakee?” Ki Pɔɔl jiin a “Nn.”

ref: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
text: Ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na yetɔ a “Min ŋarin din pa likirii saamewa ki bi chabin yaak maŋ.” Ki Pɔɔl yetɔ a “Ŋaan min ŋarin, ba marin, bi poŋ chabin yaak maŋa.”

ref: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
text: Li taakpaak ni na, ki lanjimanba nba bo loon ki bin boi Pɔɔl boot buboit na ji furin, ki ŋmat potuntuna jik. Ki jaŋmaanii ji soor lanjimanba yudaanɔ na, kimaan wa ji bann nan Rom teeb chab Pɔɔl bi yaak na, nan wa bia te ki bi lorɔ na paak. Ba bu Pɔɔl buut biaŋinba

ref: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
text: Ŋanne ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ maŋ lek yabii a wun bann maan nba ki Juu teeb paan Pɔɔl na paak chib chib. Ki li ji tan yent sanyiɔk, ki u te ki bi lotɔ, ŋaan bia tur mɔb a Juu teeb jiantu yudamm na nan bi tituk kaanu damm kura-n tikir na, ŋaan te ki Pɔɔl baar set bi tɔɔnn po.

ref: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
text: Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl got tituk kaanu damm maŋ sirrr, ki tan yet a “Chanbaanba nan n naa waas, ma din be ki tan tuu mɔtana, n mi nan li fan nan Yennu.”

ref: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
text: Wa yet nna, ki jiantu yudaanɔ Ananias bet binba see ki kpia Pɔɔl na a bin faa Pɔɔl mɔb paak.

ref: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
text: Ki Pɔɔl betɔ a “Yennu sa faa fin, kpinkpannii daanɔ na! A kar leŋ ki bu n buut kaman nan ti sennu nba want biaŋinba na, ki bia yaa a bin faan, ŋaan ki sennu na ki chab nna.”

ref: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
text: Ki binba see ki kpia Pɔɔl na betɔ a “A tan sukii Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ-ɔɔ? ”

ref: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
text: Ki u jiin a “N naa waas, n ki mi nan u tee jiantu yudaanɔe. Yennu gbouŋ poŋ yet a ‘Li ki ŋan ki a pak barbiiuk ki jiin a yudaanɔ po.’ ”

ref: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
text: Tɔn, Pɔɔl nba ji bann nan tituk kaanu maŋ siab tee Sajusiinba, ki leeb mun tee Farisiinba na, ki u yet sanpaapowa niib na ni a “N naa waas, n tee Farisii nirɔe, Farisii bike teen. Ki n mɔk dindann nan kpeemm tan sa fiira. Li paake te ki bi bu n buut na.”

ref: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
text: Wa pak nna maŋ yoonba, ki mɔniɔk ji fiir Farisiinba nan Sajusiinba maŋ sinsuuk ni, ki bi bɔkit leeb.

ref: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
text: Ŋanne ki bi tintianii na ŋammit ki do ki pukii. Ki sennu wannteeb na siab, binba tee Farisiinba na, fiir pak nan nunmɔnn a “Ti ki la jɔɔ na biiti. Li pasiar Yennu set tun seek amii malakae, ki u pak turɔ a wun pak.”

ref: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
text: Ki mɔniɔk na din ŋamm ki fiir bonchiann, ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na mi a bi tan sa mann kpet ki dat Pɔɔl ki pat patɔ. Li paak ki u tur mɔb a lanjimanba-n saan ki saa nyinn Pɔɔl tituk kaanu damm maŋ ni, ki saan nanɔ tabdiiuk na ni.

ref: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
text: Li poorpo ki nyiɔk tan bɔnn, ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa baar Pɔɔl peŋ, ki tan betɔ a “I mɔk parcheenn. A poŋ turin siara Jerusalema, ki li bia tee talase ki a bia tun nna Rom.” Biaŋinba ki bi lorin lor a bin kpi Pɔɔl

ref: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
text: Ŋanne ki bi fiir saan jiantu yudamm nan saakab boor ki yetib a “Ti por por nba paare a ti kan di siari amii ki nyu siari, see ki tin tan kpi Pɔɔla.

ref: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
text: Li paak, yimm nan tituk kaanu damm na-n tun toomii Rom lanjimanba yudaanɔ na peŋ, ki wun nyinn Pɔɔl ki baar nanɔ i boor nna, ki lii tee kaman nan i loon ki i boiɔ buboite, ki bann u maan maŋ po chib chib na. U tan sii baat yoonba na, ŋaan sɔɔ ki ti poŋ teen siira a ti kpiu.”

ref: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
text: Ŋaan ki Pɔɔl niipoo bija mun gbat bi lora maŋ. Ki u saan tabdiiuk na ni, ki saa bet Pɔɔl li bannu.

ref: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
text: Pɔɔl nba gbat nna maŋ, ki u yiin lanjimanba saakɔyenɔ, ki tan betɔ a “Jiin naasimɔ na, ki saan nanɔ a yudaanɔ peŋ, kimaan u mɔk siare ki yaa wun betɔ.”

ref: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
text: Ki lanjimanba saakɔyenɔ na jiiu ki saan nanɔ bi yudaanɔ na peŋ, ki yetɔ a “Pɔɔl wunba ki ti diau yommik na yet a n baar nan naasimɔ na a peŋ, a ki u mɔk siare ki loon ki wun beta.”

ref: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
text: Ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na soor u nuu soonna, ki saan nanɔ kpiŋ, ki saa boiɔ, “A mɔk barlaniie ki loon ki a betimi?”

ref: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
text: Ki u yetɔ a “Juu teeb taan mɔb ki loon ki bin won boia wonn ki a nyinn Pɔɔl ki sik nanɔ bi tituk ni, ki lii tee kaman nan bi loon ki bin bo boiɔ buboite, ki bann u po chib chib na.

ref: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
text: Ŋanne ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na bet naasimɔ na a “I mi ki a yet sɔɔ nan a kumiin li labaar,” ŋaan te ki u ŋmat. Biaŋinba ki bi jii Pɔɔl ki saan nanɔ Siisarea

ref: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
text: Ŋaan jii taamm ki tur Pɔɔl ki wun jak, ŋaan tenn taanlei kii dia, kii saa nanɔ soonna, ki saa baar yudaanɔ Fiiliks peŋ, ki u daa laat daŋi,”

ref: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
text: “Min Klɔdius Lisiase sɔbin gbouŋ na, ki teen Chanbaa yudaanɔ Fiiliks, n foonta.

ref: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
text: “Juu teebe daan soor jɔɔ na, ki daan bo sa kpiuwa, ŋaan ki n gbat a Rom teeb turɔ bi yaaka. Li paak, ki n jii lanjimanba, ki saan ki saa fatɔ,

ref: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
text: ki loon ki n bann linba paake ki bi paau mapipaat maŋ. Li paak ki n jiiu ki baar nanɔ bi tituk kaanu damm boor,

ref: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
text: ki bann nan li tee bi mɔŋ sennu paake ki bi paau maan maŋ. Ŋaan li ki tee bisiat kaa ki li bo jaŋ nan u kuumi, amii dansarkɔɔnu.

ref: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
text: “Ŋanne ki bi bo kumiin a Juu teeb lorin lora a bin kpiu. Li paak ki n yaa a lanjimanba-n jiiu ki baar a peŋ yian yian, ŋaan ki n bia bet binba paau mapaauk maŋ, a bin baar a boor, ki tan wanna u biit nba tee biaŋinba.”

ref: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
text: Ŋanne ki lanjimanba na jii Pɔɔl kaman nan bi yudaanɔ nba turib mɔb biaŋinba na, ki saa nanɔ, ki saa baar doo nba yi Antipatris na, li nyiɔk maŋ.

ref: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
text: Ki li yent sanyiɔk, ki lanjimanba nba somm taatiŋ na ji ŋmat kun Jerusalem, tabdiiuk na ni, ŋaan chab tanjakira na a bin saan nanɔ.

ref: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
text: Ki bi jiiu ki saan nanɔ Siisarea, ki saa jii gbouŋ na ki tur yudaanɔ Fiiliks na, ŋaan bia jii Pɔɔl ki kubinɔ.

ref: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
text: Ki yudaanɔ maŋ gaar gbouŋ na ki karin, ŋaan boi Pɔɔl a “A nyii tinlaŋe?” Ki u jiinɔ a “Silisia.” Wa bann nna maŋ

ref: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
text: ki u yetɔ a “Binba paa-a mapaauk maŋ-i baar yoonba, n sa bu a buut.” Ŋanne ki u tur mɔb a guuteeb-ii diau Bat Herod ŋaak ni.

ref: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
text: Tɔn, ki daa ŋaŋŋmu daar tan gar, ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ Ananias, nan saakab, nan lɔya wunba ki bi yiu Tetulus na, tan baar Siisarea ki saan yudaanɔ Fiiliks boor ni, ki bia yaa a bin paan Pɔɔl maan na. Ki Fiiliks te ki bi yiinɔ.

ref: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
text: Li yoo na ki Tetulus jak ki piak paa Pɔɔl maan, ki yeen a “Chanbaa Fiiliks, a paake te ki ti tiŋ na mei, nan a minu paak ki a yabii ki teen ti tiŋ na.

ref: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
text: Siar kur po ti mɔk parpeenn nan linba na yoo kura, ki ti teena niipoouk bonchiann.

ref: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
text: “N ki mantik loon ki n biir a yoo, ŋaan n barimae ki a di sukuru ki gbiint ti maan na waan.

ref: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
text: “Ti la jɔɔ na ki u tee daamii nirɔe, ki bia jenna, ki u tuk Juu teeb, tingbouŋ na kur ni. Ŋɔɔe bia tee Nasareniinba yudaanɔ gbaa.

ref: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
text: Li poorpoe ki Chanbaa Lisias maŋ bia tur mɔb a binba paau mapaauk maŋ-n baar a boor nna, ki a mɔŋ mɔŋ mun-n tan boi ki fiitɔ, ki bann ta paau linba paakiie.”

ref: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
text: Ŋanne ki Juu teeb mun bia yaa a “Li kpan set tee mɔniie.” Pɔɔl nba piak nyint u mɔŋ biaŋinba

ref: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
text: Li yoo na ki yudaanɔ na wann Pɔɔl nuu a wun fiir pak. Ki Pɔɔl yet a “N mi nan fa teen timm Juu teeb na barbuurɔ, li dii bina bonchiann. Li paak, li teenin parpeenn nan ma yaa n pak n mɔŋ mɔŋ paak a boor nna.

ref: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
text: “Li-i tee ki a loon, a sa boi ki bann nan li ki gar kaman nan dapiik nan ŋannlee nna ki n daan doo Jerusalem, ma n saa jiant Yennu.

ref: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
text: Ki Juu teeb daan ki la ki n nɔi mɔnɔsiɔk nan sɔɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni, amii ki tikii niib ki fiint bi wutoa Yenjiantu diit ni, amii doo maŋ ni biaki.

ref: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
text: Bi bia kan fit ki wann linba ki birsuk kaa, ki bi paan mapaauk maŋi.

ref: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
text: “Min beerae nan ma waa sɔnu nba ki Juu teeb yeen a li sɔnu ki ŋan na, ŋaan min lek jiantir ti yejamm Yennue. Linba sɔb Moses sennu gbant nan Yennu sɔkiniinba gbant ni na, n teen li kura yada,

ref: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
text: ki mɔk dindann kaman nan binba paanin maan na mɔŋ mɔŋ mun bia mɔk li dindann booru nnee, nan Yennu tan sa fiin binba tee niŋamm nan binba tee nibiit kur kuun ni.

ref: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
text: Ki li paak, n yabii n mɔŋ yoo kur ma n tun linba took sɔnu, ki n par-ii be fanu Yennu nan nisaarik boor ni.

ref: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
text: “Tɔn, ma din nyii Jerusalem, li din yukir bonchiann na, ki n ji tan ŋmat leŋ ma n tur n niib likirii, ŋaan bia pi Yennu piinii.

ref: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
text: Tɔn, sinsinn, ma daan ŋamm n mɔŋ gbenna, ki n daa be Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni, ki daa pi piinii maŋ, ki bi kɔɔ ki lan leŋ. Li yoo na niib daan ki yab ŋaak maŋ ni, ki fuut mun bia kaa.

ref: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
text: Ŋaan ki Juu tesiab nba daan nyii Asia tiŋ ni na ŋarin daan be Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak na ni. Li-i bo-i tee ki bi la n biit, li bo kpaa talas ki bin bo baar ki paanin li maan.

ref: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
text: Ŋann-i kaa, fan ŋaan ki niib nba be nna na-n wann biit nba ki bi daan la ki n tun ma daan see bi tituk kaanu ni yoonba na.

ref: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
text: Bi daan ki la n biiti, see bonyenn kuukɔɔ nba ki n daan yet bi sinsuuk ni, linba ki bi ki loon, ki n yaa a ‘Buut nba ki i bu nanin na tee ma yet yaa a kpeemm fiiru be na paake.’ ”

ref: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
text: Fiiliks nba ŋarin mi Yisa weiu po bonŋann na, ki u nyik maan na, ŋaan yaa a “Lanjimanba yudaanɔ Lisias-i taŋŋi baar yoonba, n sa dukin i maan maŋ po,”

ref: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
text: ŋaan bet u lanjimanba saakɔyenɔ a wuu guu Pɔɔl, ki wuu be u yama ni, ŋaan bia chab Pɔɔl yɔɔsinba a bii teenɔ linba ki u loon. Biaŋinba ki Pɔɔl pak ki teen yudaanɔ Fiiliks nan u ŋaapoo

ref: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Ŋanne ki daa ŋaŋŋa nna tan gar, ki Fiiliks tan baar u buut boor nan u ŋaapoo Drusila, wunba din tee Juu nirɔ na, ŋaan te ki bi yiin Pɔɔl ki baar nanɔ. Ki ŋɔɔ Fiiliks gbiintir, ki Pɔɔl piak biaŋinba ki nirɔ sa teen Yisa Masia yada.

ref: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
text: Tɔn, Pɔɔl nba piak ki want yentinu nan mɔŋdianu nan buut daar baaru po yoonba, ki jaŋmaanii ji soor Fiiliks, ki u yet a “Mɔtana, i daa kii saa, ŋaan li-i tee ki n la yaak, n bia sa ŋamm yiina.”

ref: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
text: Ŋaan li yoo maŋ na ki Fiiliks bia lek loon Pɔɔl-n sa turɔ likirii. Li paake te ki u din tuu yɔɔ ki yi Pɔɔl ki piak nanɔ.

ref: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
text: Tɔn, bina ŋannlee poorpo ki Posius Festus mun gaar Fiiliks setu ki gaar yudant maŋ. Fiiliks nba din yaa wun nyik toonn na, ki u din loon ki wun penn Juu teeb para, ki ŋaan ki Pɔɔl be dansarik ni.

ref: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
text: Yudaanɔ Festus nba baar Siisarea, li daa ŋanntaa daar ki u nyii leŋ ki doo Jerusalem.

ref: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
text: Leŋe ki Yennu jiantu yudamm nan Juu teeb yudamm baar Festus peŋ ki pak paan Pɔɔl maan na, ŋaan miar Festus

ref: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
text: a wun te ki bi para-ii mei, ki te ki Pɔɔl-n baar Jerusalem, ŋaan din lor lor a bin bɔr ki laa ki tan kpi Pɔɔl sɔnu ni.

ref: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
text: Ki Festus jiin a “Pɔɔl be dansarik ni Siisarea, ŋaan n mɔŋ mɔŋ lek sa ŋmat ki saan leŋ daa ŋannlelee nawa.

ref: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
text: Li paak, ŋaant man ki i yudamm-n wein, ki tin sik Siisarea. Li-i tee ki jɔɔ na tun bonbiir ki kpetie, bin pak paanɔ mapaauk maŋ.”

ref: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
text: Festus din dii kaman nan daa ŋanniin amii dapiik na ki be namm, ki tan fit ki ŋmat Siisarea. Ki li yent sanyiɔk ni, ki u ji kar u buut paak, ŋaan te ki bi nyinn Pɔɔl ki baar nanɔ.

ref: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
text: Pɔɔl nba baar yoonba, ki u ji jak buut bunu. Ki Juu teeb nba nyii Jerusalem na, ki bi fiir ki ŋaa lint Pɔɔl, ŋaan pak bonbiir bonchiann ki biirɔ, ŋaan bia lek ki fit wann u bonbisiar maŋ paaki.

ref: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
text: Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl pak u mɔŋ mɔŋ paak, ki yet a “N ki tun ki biir Juu teeb sennu. N bia ki tun ki biir Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak siari, amii Rom kpanbara yudaanɔ.”

ref: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
text: Ŋaan ki Festus loon ki wun penn Juu teeb para. Li paak, ki u boi Pɔɔl a “A loon ki a saan Jerusalem, ki n sa bu a buut leŋii?”

ref: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
text: Ki Pɔɔl yetɔ a “N kpan daa see kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa buut bunbuboore na. Ŋannii ŋan nan ki a bu n buut. N ki tun Juu teeb bisiati, ki a mɔŋ mɔŋ mun bia mi nna.

ref: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
text: Mi-i bo-i tee mɔyêtdaanɔie, ki tun bonbiir nba ki li jaŋ nan kuun, n kan yet a n fit nyi kuun ni. Ŋaan li-i tee ki bi pak paanin mapaauk yannie, sɔɔ kan fit jiin ki kubinin bi nuu ni. Ŋaan see ki n gaan n maan maŋ kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa boor.”

ref: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
text: Ŋanne ki Festus pak nan u saakab maan maŋ po, ŋaan bet Pɔɔl a “A gaan a maan Siisa boora. Li paak, a set sa saana.” Bat Agripa nan u ŋaapoo nba gbiintir Pɔɔl maan biaŋinba

ref: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
text: Daa ŋannlee nna nba gar, ki Bat Agripa nan u ŋaapoo Beniis tan baar Siisarea a bin foont yudaanɔ Festus.

ref: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
text: Ŋaan bi din teen leŋ daa ŋaŋŋawa, ki Festus tan wann bat na Pɔɔl maan nba tee biaŋinba, ki yet a “Fiiliks daan yaat, ŋaan ŋaan sɔɔ dansarik ni,

ref: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
text: ŋaan ma daan saan Jerusalem yoonba na, ki Juu teeb saakab nan jiantu yudamm daan pak biirɔ, ki yaa a n bu u maan maŋ ki biirɔ.

ref: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
text: Ki n mun jiin ki betib a ‘Timm Rom teeb yaa boor ni ŋarin, li ki tee ti kaar kaa ki tin chab nirɔ nba ki bi yaa a u tee biirtɔɔ ki bin kpiu, ŋaan u madamm daa ki baar buut boori, ki ŋɔɔ nan u biirteeb daa ki took leeb ki u pak ki yaa wun nyinn u mɔŋi.’

ref: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
text: “Li paak, bi kura nba daan tan baar nna yoonba, n ki taantiri, ki li yent sanyiɔk ni ki n kar n buut paak, ŋaan tur mɔb ki bi kɔɔ nan Pɔɔl.

ref: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
text: “Ŋanne ki binba paau maan maŋ fiir ki set a bin pak ki biirɔ, ŋaan ki fit la u biit nba tee biaŋinba kaman nan ma daan bo dukii mi a la sii bi ki tee biaŋinba,

ref: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
text: ŋaan bi kpan nɔi mɔniɔk ki jikit saa bi mɔŋ mɔŋ Yenjiantu po, nan jasɔɔ nba din kpo, ki u sann yi Yisa na, wunba ki Pɔɔl lek yeen yaa a u be u manfoor ni na.

ref: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
text: “Ki li maan jarin. Li paak, ki n bet Pɔɔl a li-i tee ki u loon ki wun saan Jerusalem ki n sa bu u maan leŋ, wun yetin.

ref: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
text: Ki u yet a bii diau dansarik ni mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki bi tan sa saan nanɔ kpanbara yudaanɔ boor, ki wun saa jiar u maan maŋ. Ŋanne ki n tur mɔb ma bii guu-u, ki n tan te ki bin saan nanɔ kpanbara yudaanɔ na peŋ.”

ref: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
text: Ki Bat Agripa yet Festus a “N mɔŋ mɔŋ loon ki n gbat nirɔ maŋ mɔb ni.” Ki Festus jiinɔ a “Wonnewa ki a won sa gbat u mɔb ni.”

ref: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
text: Ki li yent sanyiɔk ni, ki Bat Agripa nan u ŋaapoo Beniis muk mukit bonchiann, ki lanjimanba yudamm nan doo na yudamm wei bi poor ki baar kɔɔ buut diiuk na ni ki kar. Ki yudaanɔ Festus ji tun toomii a bin jii Pɔɔl ki baar nanɔ na.

ref: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
text: Ŋanne ki Festus yet a “Bat Agripa nan yimm nba kur be nna na, i la jɔɔ na nba nee, ki Juu teeb nba be nna na nan binba kur be Jerusalem baar n boor nna nan u maan, ki paanɔ, ki yakii kpayakit nanin, ki yaa a li ki ŋan ki wuu be.

ref: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
text: Ŋaan min ŋarin la ki u ki tun bisiat nba ki li jaŋ nan kuumi. Ki u mɔŋ mɔŋ bia yaa a u loon ki wun gaan u maan maŋ kpanbara yudaanɔ peŋe. “Tɔn, li paak ki n dukin ma n te ki wun saan.

ref: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
text: Ŋaan n ki mi ma sa sɔb gbouŋ ki tur kpanbara yudaanɔ na ki pakɔ linba ki saan gaan u po. Ŋanne te ki n baar nanɔ i kur numm nna, ŋaan li mantik kpaa fin Bat Agripae. Li paak, u buboit fiitu poorpo, n sii mɔk maan nba ki n sa fit ki sɔb ki tur kpanbara yudaanɔ maŋ,

ref: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
text: kimaan li kaa paak nan ki n te ki dansarkɔɔrɔ-n saan kpanbara yudaanɔ peŋ, ŋaan ki n kan fit wann wa biir linba.”

ref: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
text: Li yoo na ki Bat Agripa yet Pɔɔl a “N chaba mɔb. Pakin nyinn a mɔŋ.” Ki Pɔɔl donn u nuu sanpaapo a wun pak ki nyinn u mɔŋ, ki yet a

ref: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
text: “Bat Agripa, li maŋin bonchiann nan ma see a tɔɔnn ni dinna, ki yaa n pak ki nyinn n mɔŋ, ki jikit saa linba kur ki Juu teeb paanin na po.

ref: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
text: Ki bia jenna, n mɔk parpeenn bonchiann nan ma see a tɔɔnn ni na, nan fa tee banntɔɔ, ki mi Juu teeb sennu nan bi mɔniɔk nba tee biaŋinba kur na. Li paak, chanbaa, n barimae, din sukuru ki gbiint ma yaa n pak linba na.

ref: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
text: Bi poŋ min ki li fɔk. Li-i tee ki bi loon, bi sa fit wann nan n tuu waa Farisiinba Yenjiantu sɔnu nba mantik paar nee.

ref: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
text: Ki mɔtana ma ji see ki bi bu n buut na, kimaan n mɔk dindann nan mɔsonn nba ki Yennu din senn ki tur ti yejamm na.

ref: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
text: Li mɔsonn maŋ paake ki timm Juu teeb booru piik nan munnlee tuu mɔk dindann ki dukii nan ti tan sa la. Li paak te ki bi jiantir Yennu nan nunmɔnn yennu nan nyiɔk na. Chanbaa Agripa, li dindann maŋ paakera, ki Juu teeb paanin maan na.

ref: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
text: Bee ki li paar i paak nan ki i teen yada a Yennu fiint kpeemmi?

ref: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
text: “Sinsinn, n mɔŋ mɔŋ din dukin nan li kpaa talase nan ki n tun bonsiar kur ki biir Nasaret nirɔ Yisa sann.

ref: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
text: Nnee ki n din tuun Jerusalem. Yennu jiantu yudamm din tuu chabin yikoo ki n so Yennu niib ki kɔɔntib dansarii. Ba din tuu bu bi maan ki tan gbenn ki chab a bin kpib na, n mɔŋ mun din tuu be binba din tuu chab a bin kpib na kann ni.

ref: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
text: “Yoo kur n din tuu yɔɔ ki mukisirib Yenjiantu diit kur ni, ki din tuu loon ki bii nɔi a bi ki waa Yisa. N din mantik donn wutoor bonchiann bi paak, ki li saa yakira. Li paak, n bia lek din tuu saa doi nba be tinganii ni, ki saa mukisirib biak. Pɔɔl nba betib u lebitu po biaŋinba

ref: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
text: “Muksuk maŋ paake ki n yaan tan fiir ma n saan ki saa mukisib Damaskus, Yennu jiantu yudamm nba chabin yikoo, ki bia turin mɔb a n tun nna.

ref: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
text: “Tɔn, Chanbaa Agripa, li din tee yensuuk nie ki n be sɔnu paak ki saa, ki tan la ki yentu nyii sanpaapo ki yeen gar yennu, ki yent nyɔkit ki lint min nan n tɔknanleeb na.

ref: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
text: Ki ti kura baa tiŋ ni. Ki n gbat ki kunkɔr pak timm Juu teeb maan ni, ki yin a ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bee ki a mukisirimi? Fa tuu yêenin na, li paake te ki li paar a paak kaman nan bonkobuk nba tuu yêen u daanɔ mɔb ki u boou lanbann na.’

ref: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: “Ki n jiin ki boi ma ‘N Yomdaanɔ, ŋmapoe?’ Ki ti Yomdaanɔ jiin a ‘Min, Yisae, ki a mukisirin maŋ.

ref: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
text: Ŋaan fiit ki set. N nyinn fine a a teen n toontunnɔ. Linba nee te ki n dɔkit a paak. Teent niib siara ki wannib fa lan biaŋinba dinna, nan linba ki n sii fiitir wanta yoo nba tan sa baar.

ref: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
text: Ma tan sa tuma siaminba na, n bia tan sa nyinna daar ni Juu teeb nan nileeb nii ni.

ref: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
text: Fine tee wunba sa yent bi numm po, ki bin nyi bunbɔnn ni ki kɔɔ yentu ni, ki bia nyinnib Sintaanii nuu ni ki bin kɔɔ Yennu nuu ni, a wun nyik bi biit ki chabib, ki bin la yiar, binba ki Yennu teemm u niib na sinsuuk ni, kimaan ba teenin yada paake.’ Pɔɔl nba want u toona po biaŋinba

ref: King Agrippa, I obeyed this vision from heaven.
text: “Li paak, Chanbaa Agripa, n din ki yêt Yendɔuŋ ni yerintu maŋi.

ref: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
text: Ŋaan sinsinn n din dɔŋ ki mɔɔnt Damaskus teebe, ki pukin Jerusalem nan Judea tiŋ ni doi nba be lokir kur po. Li poorpoewa, ki n tan mɔɔnt ki tur binba ki tee Juu teebi. N wannu ki tur niib na kur tee a bin nyik bi biit kii waa Yennu sɔnu, kii tuun bonŋann, ki lin wann nan bi set nyik bi biit nan mɔnii.

ref: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
text: “Li din tee linba na paake, ki Juu teeb din soorin, ma daa din be Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni na, ki bi din loon ki bin kpin,

ref: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
text: ŋaan la jii ki tan tuu dinna, ki Yennu guun, ki n ji be ki see ki mɔɔntir u maan ki teen tatimm nan paarib kura na. Ma piak linba na, ŋaan ŋann nan Moses nan Yennu sɔkiniinba nba din tuu piak linba kur na, li ki mɔk bɔkitu. Ŋamm din piak linba tan sii tee,

ref: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
text: a Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia tan sa fabir di fara ki kpo, a ŋɔɔe bia tan sii tee sinsinn daanɔ nba tan sa fiir kuun ni, a ki li paak ki u bia tan sa mɔɔnt ki tur Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb mun biak, ki beerib barii nba tan sii yentir bi numm po.”

ref: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
text: Pɔɔl nba din piak a wun nyinn u mɔŋ na, ŋanne ki Festus tian u paak ŋaan yet a “Pɔɔl, a waata! A bannchiauŋ paake te ki a waat.”

ref: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
text: Ki Pɔɔl jiin a “Chanbaa Festus, n ki waat kaa. Maan nba ki n piak na, li tee barmɔniie, ki bia mɔk paak.

ref: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
text: Chanbaa Agripa, n mɔk parcheenn ki sa pak ki tura, kimaan a mɔŋ mi linba na po, ki n bia teen yada nan barii maŋ ki jikit saa Yisa po ki bɔr a boori, kimaan linba din teen maŋ, li din ki woon kaa.

ref: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
text: Chanbaa Agripa, a teen Yennu sɔkiniinba maan yadawa-aa? Ŋaan n lek mi nan a teen yadawa.”

ref: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
text: Ki Bat Agripa jiin Pɔɔl a “Fin dukii mi a a pinpauk mintik na sa fit dakin ki n teen Yisaweirɔwa.”

ref: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
text: Ki Pɔɔl jiin a “N pinpauk-i fɔk amii la ki fɔki, min yaa Yenmiaru tee ki fin nan niib kura nba gbiintir n maan na dinna na, see ki fin nan ŋamm-n teen Yisaweira kaman nan ma tee biaŋinba na, ŋaan jarit nba na kuukɔɔe ki i kii mɔki.”

ref: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
text: Ŋanne ki Bat na nan yudaanɔ na nan Beniis, nan binba kur kar leŋ na kur fiir ki nyii.

ref: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
text: Ba yaa bin jii yat yoonba, ki bi ji beer bi leeb a “Jɔɔ na taan ki tun biit ki li ŋan nan bin kpiu, amii ki kɔɔnɔ dansariki.”

ref: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
text: Ki Bat Agripa yet Festus a “Nirɔ na-i daan bo-i kii miar a u sa gaan u maan na kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa peŋi, ti bo sa fit ŋaauwa.”

ref: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
text: Ba ji din tan dukin a bin ji Pɔɔl ki saan nanɔ Rom, Itali tiŋ ni, ki bi jii ŋɔɔ nan dansarkɔɔleeb, ki turib Rom lanjimanba saakɔɔ nna, ki u sann yi Julius, ki u be lanjimanba paabu nba ki bi yi Kpanbara Yudaanɔ Paabu na, ki u jii lanjimanba na,

ref: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
text: ki bi ji kɔɔ ŋaruŋ nba nyii Adramitium, kimaan ŋaruŋ maŋ poŋ teen siir nan wun nyi Siisarea ki saan Asia tiŋ ni doi nba kpia mɔkgbeŋŋir na kpiŋ; ki Masedonia nirɔ Aristakus, wunba din nyii Tesalonika na, nan min Luk mun bia din chian Pɔɔla.

ref: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
text: Ki li tan yent sanyiɔk ni, ki ti baar Saidon. Ki lanjimanba saakɔɔ Julius din dia Pɔɔl fanu, ki din chabɔ a wun saan Yisaweira nba be leŋ na peŋ, ki bii teeu saantuk.

ref: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
text: Ki ti tan nyii leŋ ki yaat saa, ki wouŋ fukit ki da tookit, ki ti pek kpian Saiprus tiŋ po ki saa, ki tiŋ maŋ tia wouŋ na ki turit.

ref: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
text: Ta poot mɔkgbeŋŋir na yoonba na, ki ti tan per Silisia nan Pamfilia tinii po, ki ji baar Maira, Lisia tiŋ ni na.

ref: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
text: Leŋe ki lanjimanba saakɔɔ na la ŋaruŋ nba nyii Aleksandria ki saa Itali tiŋ ni. Ki u te ki ti lɔɔr ki kɔɔ ŋaruŋ maŋ;

ref: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
text: ki saa waan waaminna, ki li tan gbatit. Ti din jii daa saamewa, ki tan baar Kinidus, Kreet tiŋ ni. Ki wouŋ na bia lek ki nyik ki tin saan ta saa siaminba po na. Li paak ti din bɔkii, ki gaar Kreet tiŋ maŋ poe. Li po maŋe ki wouŋ din ki paari, ki ti tan kpian nan Salmone, Kreet tiŋ ni, ki gar.

ref: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
text: Ta din saa na, li din paar bonchiann nan ta tan baar Kreet doo nba yi Setŋanu na. Leŋ din ki fɔk nan Lasea doo, Kreet tiŋ ni.

ref: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
text: Ta baar yukir bonchiann na, Juu teeb mɔloru dagbeŋŋir ji din poŋ gara, ki yoo nba ki ninbɔŋ tuu tan baar mɔkgbeŋŋir paak na tan baar; li paak ki Pɔɔl kpaanib ki yetib a

ref: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
text: “N yɔɔs, n mi nan ti sɔnu na ni, ninbɔŋ sa daamit bonchiann, ki li ki tee tiat nba be ŋaruŋ na ni, amii ŋaruŋ na kuukɔɔ paak kaa, ŋaan nan ti mɔŋ mɔŋ manfoa gbaa sii be ninbɔŋ maŋ ni.”

ref: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
text: Ŋaan lanjimanba saakɔɔ na baka din taan kaa nan linba ki Pɔɔl pak na po, ŋaan fiin ŋaruŋ na saakɔɔ nan li daanɔ na poe.

ref: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
text: Ŋaan ŋaruŋ maŋ nba din tuu see doo nba na din ki fan nan tin set leŋ fufuk yoo, li paak, bi bonchiann ji din yaa a bin ŋaan ki bin fiir leŋ ki saan, a li pasiar bi tan sa baar Fiiniks. Leŋ din tee Kreet tiŋ dooe, ki ŋaruŋ sinsee boor be leŋ ki took yenbaa po, ki bi loon bii be leŋ fufuk yoo. Ninbɔŋ nba din be mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni labaar

ref: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
text: Li yoo na ki bi la niigaŋ po na wouŋ ji da waan waaminna, ki bi ji dukin a li pasiar li sii tee wounŋaŋo, linba sa te ki bin fit nyann ba dukii biaŋinba, ki li yoo na, ki bi ji fiir ki dat kutkpiatir nba so ŋaruŋ ki sent na, ki kɔɔn ŋaruŋ maŋ ni, ki yaan jak bi sɔnu ki saa, ki bɔɔk Kreet mɔk-kpiŋ.

ref: days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing.
text: Li ki yukiri ki wonpaaruk nba nyi yonput po na fikit tiŋ na ni, ki da siik mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni,

ref: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
text: ki li jiit, ki ti saa baar ki gar tinbik nba ki bi yi Kɔda na. Ta gaar kpiant yoonba ki tinbik maŋ gɔɔr wouŋ na ki u barik, ki ti ji gbaa gbaa saa gɔɔr burgbetir nba tuuk ŋaruŋ na paak na,

ref: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
text: ki bi ji dat burgbetir na ki kɔɔnn ŋaruŋ na ni, ŋaan bia lor ŋaruŋ na nan ŋmiit kenkema ki lint. Bi din tiin jaŋmaanii, ki mi a bi tan sa mann kpet ki kpian Libia tiŋ ni, siaminba ki nyun ki sumii na. Leŋ din tee ninbɔŋ boore. Ŋanne ki bi sikin ŋaruŋ na chinchenn ki ji ŋaan ki wouŋ na yaan jiib ki saa namm.

ref: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
text: Wonpaaruk nba mantik din da na, ki li yent sanyiɔk ni, ki bi piin ki nyinn bonkɔɔtkara ŋaruŋ maŋ ni,

ref: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
text: ki li sanyafannuk jik, ki bi jii bi mɔŋ nii ki nyinn nyinn ŋaruŋ tiat nba be ŋaruŋ maŋ ni na, ki lu lu mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni.

ref: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
text: Li din jii daa bonchiann ki sanpagbana dɔkin sanpaapo, ki ti ki fit la yennu amii ŋmaabira gbaa, ki wouŋ na lek bia da nan paŋ. Ta din mɔk dindann ki dukii mi a ti sa tinn na, ki li kur nyii peb.

ref: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
text: Li din yukir bonchiann ki bi ki dii, ki Pɔɔl tan fiir set ki yet a “N yɔɔs, i daaŋŋi bo-i sak n mɔb, li daan bo sii ŋan. I bo tan kii laat ninbɔŋ ki di fara na booru.

ref: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
text: Ŋaan mɔtana, yii saak n mɔb kii mɔk parcheenn man. I sɔɔ nan yenɔ kuukɔɔwa kan kpo gbaa, nɔkira ŋaruŋ maŋe sa biir.

ref: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
text: Kimaan Yennu nba ki n tee u yɔɔ ki jiantirɔ na, u malaka won sik n paak nyiɔk na,

ref: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
text: ki tan betin a ‘Pɔɔl, daa tiin jaŋmaanii. See ki bi tan jiia ki saan nana kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa na peŋ. A paak, ki Yennu yaa wun tinn niib kur nba be nana ŋaruŋ na ni.’

ref: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
text: “Li paak, n yɔɔs, i mɔk parcheenn man, kimaan n teen Yennu yada nan li kpan sii tee kaman nan wa betin nae.

ref: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
text: Ŋaan wouŋ lek sa tutit nan paŋ, ki daamit, ki te ki tin baar tinbisiak paak.”

ref: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
text: Ŋanne ki bi ji din pot ŋmiit na, ki kutkpiata maŋ baa nyun ni, ŋaan bia lot ŋmiit nba lor daat nba ki bi dia ki nuun ŋaruŋ na, ki ji fit donn chinchenn ŋaruŋ numm po, a wouŋ-ii da kii saa namm. Ŋanne ki bi tan per gbingbann,

ref: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
text: Ŋanne ki ŋaruŋ kanna na ji loon bin bot ŋaan nyik ŋaruŋ na. Ki bi sikint burgbetir na nyun paak, ŋaan ji tee ser ki li tee kaman nan bi yaa bin sikin kutkpiata ŋaruŋ maŋ tɔɔnn poe na.

ref: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
text: Ki jaŋmaanii kɔɔb, kimaan bi dukii mi a bi ŋaruŋ na tan sa mann kpet ki do tana paak. Li paak, ki bi ji jii kutkpiata ŋanna nba so ŋaruŋ ki sent na, ki lu nyun ni, ki gaan gaan ŋaruŋ maŋ poorpo, ŋaan lek ji miar Yennu a lin yent sanyiɔk.

ref: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
text: Ŋaan ki Pɔɔl yet lanjimanba saakɔɔ na nan lanjimanba maŋ a “Ŋaruŋ kanna na-i kaa ŋaruŋ na ni, i sɔɔ kan tinni.”

ref: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
text: Ŋanne ki lanjimanba na pot pot ŋmiit nba ki bi lor lor burgbetir maŋ, ŋaan te ki li baa nyun ni.

ref: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
text: Li paak, n barimiie ki yi lek di jeet, kimaan li tee linba sa te ki i ŋamm la paŋe. I sɔɔ kan kpo fabaa.”

ref: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
text: Pɔɔl nba pak nna maŋ, ŋaan jii boroboro, ki tur Yennu niipoouk bi nunii ni, ŋaan ji ŋmit dii.

ref: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
text: Ŋanne ki bi para ji chee, ki bi kura jak jeet na ki di.

ref: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
text: Sɔɔ kur nba din dii jeet na ki jaŋ yoonba, ki bi jii boroboro na dii nba din tenn ŋaruŋ maŋ ni na ki lu mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni. Ŋaruŋ na kɔnnu labaar

ref: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
text: La tan yent sanyiɔk, ki ŋaruŋ kanna na la tiŋ na, ki ji ki banni, ŋaan la lɔtuk nna mɔkgbeŋŋir na kpiŋ, ki li mɔk gbingbanŋann, ki bi bikin a li-i sa fit, bin te ki ŋaruŋ na-n saan kɔɔ leŋ ki do gbingbann na.

ref: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
text: ki ŋaruŋ na tɔɔnn po fer tiŋ ni. Li tɔɔnn po na nba fer tiŋ ni biaŋinba na, ki ŋaruŋ na gbar saanu, ki nyunpana ji lia li poorpo nan paŋ ki jintir.

ref: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
text: Tɔn, ki lanjimanba na ji loon bin kpi dansarkɔɔra na kur, a nnee kaa, li pasiar bi tan sa mann ki duk poot ki chiar.

ref: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
text: Ŋaan lanjimanba maŋ saakɔɔ na ŋarin loon ki wun tinn Pɔɔl. Li paak, ki u tiab a bi daa kpibi, ŋaan tur mɔb a sɔɔ kur nba mi nyunduku, bin yukir baa nyun na ni sinsinn ki duk poot.

ref: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
text: Ŋaan binba biar ki ki mi duku na-n mun soor soor daat amii ŋarunyaka ki wɔb ki duk poot. Ti kura ji din teen nnee ki tinn ti mɔŋ.

ref: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
text: Ta din la tinnu na poorpoe, ki ti bann nan tiŋ maŋ sann yi Malta.

ref: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
text: Leŋ niib din diat bonŋann, kimaan saak din baa, ki waat be bonchiann, ki bi joo mugann ki turit a ti nanii, ŋaan teen ti kur sommir sommir.

ref: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
text: Ki Pɔɔl tan tikir daginganii bonchiann ki kpab nan waauk, ki teen muu na ni, ki muu na tonu tan gbat waauk na, ki u nyii ki jokir Pɔɔl nuu.

ref: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
text: Ki leŋ niib nba la ki waauk na jɔɔn Pɔɔl nuu paak, ki bi ji beer bi leeb a “Jɔɔ na kpan tee nikpiiruko. Wa lek tinn u mɔŋ mɔkgbeŋŋir nna, ŋaan kpeemm ŋarin kan nyikɔ ki wuu be u manfoor ni.”

ref: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
text: Ki Pɔɔl ŋak u nuu, ki waauk na baa muu na ni, ŋaan ki Pɔɔl ki la daŋ nan waama.

ref: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
text: Ki bi ji daan a bin laan u siar po sa mɔr, amii u tan sa kpir baa ki kpo. Ba ji daan daan ki tan goo ki ki la siar teenɔ na, ki bi nyik bi dindann maŋ, ŋaan yaa a u tee yensɔɔe.

ref: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
text: Leŋ bia din ki fɔk nan Publius kpaant nba be na. Ŋɔɔe bia din tee tiŋ maŋ ni bat. Ki u din gaarit saantuk ki saa jaŋ damunjaa ŋanntaa.

ref: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
text: Ki Publius baa mun din dɔɔ yiar gbanantouŋ nan nɔniikak ni. Ki Pɔɔl tan kɔɔ u diiuk ni, ki jii u nii ki yii u paak, ŋaan miar Yennu ki te ki u paak.

ref: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
text: Wa din te ki u paak na, ki baatleeb nba be leŋ na kura din baar, ki u tan te ki bi mun paak.

ref: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
text: Ki bi din pit piinii bonchiann. Ta bia din tan fi a tin kɔɔ ŋaruŋ yoonba, ki bi baar nan linba ki ti tan sii loon sɔnu ni, ki kɔɔn ŋaruŋ ni ki turit. Ta din saa Rom biaŋinba

ref: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
text: Ti benu leŋ ŋmaarii ŋanntaa poorpoewa, ki ti din kɔɔ ŋaruŋ nba din nyii Aleksandria ki din be Malta fufuk yoo na, ki bi yi ŋaruŋ maŋ Yenjaasinba. Ki li fiir saa,

ref: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
text: ki yaan tan baar doo nba ki bi yi Sairakus na, ki biar leŋ daa ŋanntaa,

ref: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
text: ki tan tɔɔt gar baar Regium doo ni. Ki li tan yent ki wouŋ yaan jak danu, ki nyii niigaŋ po ki tutit. Ki ti jii daa ŋannlee ki yaan baar Puteoli doo ni.

ref: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
text: Leŋe ki ti la Yisaweisiab. Ki bi gɔɔrit a tii be namm ki jaŋ daa ŋannlore. Ki ti be namm, ki tan nyii leŋ ki saa Rom doo ni.

ref: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
text: Yisaweira nba be Rom doo ni na nba gbat ti po na, ki bi fiir baar chetit Apius daak ni, ki siab mun chetit Saamŋei Ŋanntaa nba kpia Rom na. Pɔɔl nba lab yoonba, ki li turɔ parcheenn, ki u tur Yennu niipoouk.

ref: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
text: Ta din baar Rom na, ki bi sak chab Pɔɔl ki u be u yur po ŋaak, ki bi te ki lanjima guu-u.

ref: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
text: Tɔn, li daa ŋanntaa daare ki Pɔɔl yiin Juu teeb yudamm lakinu. Ba tikir leeb yoonba, ki u yetib a “N naa waas, n lek ki tun bonbisiar ki biir ti niib amii ti yejamm sennu, ŋaan bi din soorin ki kɔɔnin dansarik Jerusaleme, ŋaan jiin ki kubinin Rom teeb nuu ni.

ref: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
text: Ki bi boin buboit, ki tan loon ki bin nyikin, kimaan bi ki la ma tun bonbisiar nba jaŋ nan bin kpimi.

ref: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
text: Ŋaan Juu teeb nba din ki sak a bin nyikin na, li paak ji din te ki n yaa n sa gaan n maan kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa peŋ, ŋaan li din ki tee a n loon ki n pak biir ti niib siari.

ref: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
text: “Li paake te ki n yiini ma n tan lai ŋaan beti labaar na po. Ma mɔɔntir Yennu Niganntɔɔ po na, wunba ki Juu teeb daan dindann na poe, ŋanne te ki bi baanin jarit na.”

ref: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
text: Ki bi betɔ yaa “Ti ki la gbant ki li nyii Judea ki baar ki li want a po, amii ti sɔɔ bia ki nyii leŋ ki baar nan labasiari, amii sɔɔ mun bia ki pak bonbisiar ki saan gaan a po biaki.

ref: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
text: Ŋaan ti sii loon ki tin bann a dudukit ni. Ti poŋ mi nan siar po kura, niib bonchiann piak ki biir jiantu nba ki a jiantir na.”

ref: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
text: Li paak ki bi senn daar. Ki niib bonchiann tan baar Pɔɔl peŋ daar maŋ ni. Ki u kat wannib nan nunmɔnn Yennu naan po, sanyiɔk ni mɔkmɔk nan dajouk, ki wantib Yisa barii nba be Moses sennu ni nan Yennu sɔkiniinba sɔbu ni biaŋinba, ki yabii a wun tunn niib na yan ki jiin Yisa po.

ref: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
text: Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl din yet mɔbonyennkɔɔ a “Yoo nba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ nba din te ki u sɔkinii Aisaya nba din pak tur ti yejamm na, u din pak mɔniie,

ref: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
text: ki din yet yaa nna: ‘I saa ki saa bet niib na a i tan sa gbiint ki gbiint, ŋaan lek kan bann li paaki. a ki bia tan sa got ki got, ŋaan bia lek kan la,

ref: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
text: Pɔɔl nba din pak nna yoonba, ki Juu teeb ji din yat saa, ŋaan jak leeb nan mɔniɔk bonchiann.

ref: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
text: Pɔɔl nba din be ŋaak nba na ni, u din pa likirii ŋaak maŋ paak, ŋaan din be leŋ bina ŋannlee. Nirɔ nba kur tan baar u boor, u tuu gaarɔ bonŋann.

ref: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
text: Ki u bia din tuu mɔɔntir Yennu naan po, ki bia want niib ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia po, ki din tuu piak u yama ni nan parcheenn.

ref: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
text: Min Pɔɔl, wunba tee Yisa Masia toontunnɔ, ki u yiinin a n teen u toomii, ki Yennu gannin a n mɔɔnt u barŋanii.

ref: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
text: Yennu poŋ din dɔŋ senn u mɔsonn barŋanii maŋ poewa, ki u sɔkiniinba din mɔɔntir u mɔsonn maŋ, ki li sɔb u gbounyeeŋ ni.

ref: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Ŋɔɔ paake ki Yennu ninbatinu be n paak ki te ki n tee u toomii, ki n jii u sann ki saan nibooru kur boor ni, ki te ki bi teenɔ yada ki saak u mɔb;

ref: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
text: ki i mun be daansiab nba ki Yisa Masia gannib na kann ni.

ref: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: Li paak, ki n sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen yimm nba be Rom doo ni, ki Yennu looni ki ganni a i teen u mɔŋ niib na. Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir. Pɔɔl nba teen Yennu niipoouk biaŋinba

ref: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
text: Sinsinn, n teen Yennu niipoouk Yisa Masia sann ni, i kura paak, kimaan tingbouŋ na teeb kur gbia i yada po.

ref: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
text: Yennu tee n siara daanɔ nan n ki mi nyik Yenmiaru i kura paaki. Ŋɔɔ maŋe ki n waau nan n par kura ki mɔɔntir u Bija barŋanii na, ŋɔɔe tee n siara daanɔ.

ref: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
text: N mei Yennu a li-i tee u loommie li kan yukir ki n sa baar i boor ki tan gɔni,

ref: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
text: kimaan n mantik loon ki n sommi ki yin gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ piinsiar, ki ŋanne sa te ki yin set fanu kii mɔk paŋ u sɔnu ni.

ref: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
text: Ma want linba tee a min nan yimm nba teen yada biaŋinba na, ti sii kɔɔnt ti leeb para, ti yada teenu na paake.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
text: N naa waas, n loon yin bann nan n yabir nan taar muŋŋa nawa a n baar ki tan gɔni, ŋaan ki siar gɔɔrin mɔkmɔk nan mɔtana ki n ki fit gɔniini. N loon ki n te ki niib-n wei ti Yomdaanɔ i boor ni kaman nan ma daan teen biaŋinba na nileeb sinsuuk ni na.

ref: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
text: Li paak, ki n ninbinn mɔn bonchiann a n mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋ ki tur yimm nba be Rom doo ni na. Yisa barŋanii nba mɔk paŋ biaŋinba

ref: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Fei ki mɔkin nan ki n mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋ, kimaan li tee Yennu paŋ nba tint wunba kur teen Yisa yada nee; sinsinn li tee Juu teeb yare, ki mun bia tee nibooru kur yar.

ref: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
text: Barŋanii maŋ want nan Yennu yet a nirɔ tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn, yada teenu paake; nee nyii paanu ki tur binba teen Yisa yada, kaman nan la sɔb u gbouŋ ni biaŋinba na a “Wunba teen niŋanɔ Yennu boor u yada na paak, li daanɔ sii mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu.” Nifosaarii kur nba mɔk biit biaŋinba

ref: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
text: Yennu wann nifosaarii a u donn wutoor bi paak, kimaan bi tuun toonbiit ki ki waa u sɔnu, ki bi biit sɔnii maŋ te ki niib ki bant u barmɔnii.

ref: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
text: Yennu donn wutoor, kimaan nifosaarik nba sii fit mi u po biaŋinba, ŋanne fiit ki be paanu ni, kimaan ŋɔɔ Yennu mɔŋ mɔŋe fiitir ki wannib.

ref: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
text: Yoo nba ki Yennu din nan dunia mɔkmɔk nan mɔtana, u naamii maŋ ni ki bi bann u benu nba ki nifosaarik ninbinn ki fit laat na, ki bia mi nan u mɔk paŋ nba kaa gbennu, ki set tee Yennu. Li paak, ki bi ji ki mɔk leku Yennu tɔɔnn ki sa yet a bi ki mi bi biiti.

ref: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
text: Bi mi Yennu, ŋaan bi ki teenɔ nibaakir nba ki u yen na. Bi bia ki teenɔ niipoouk; ki bi mɔk jatdudukit, ki bi yan ki yenti, ki ki mɔk nyɔɔti.

ref: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
text: Ki bi yaa a bi mɔk yame, ŋaan tee jatit;

ref: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
text: ba bo sii jiantir Yennu nba kaa gbennu na, bi ki saki, ŋaan jiantir pata nba ki niib nammir ki li naan nisaarii, amii nɔɔnii, amii bonkobit, amii bonfurinkaa nba furin tiŋ ni.

ref: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
text: Li paak, ki Yennu nyikib, ki bi tuun toonbiit nba ki bi para loon, ki bi dint bi mɔŋ fei bi gbanant toona ni nan bi leeb.

ref: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
text: Bi jii linba tee Yennu barmɔnii ki lu, ŋaan sak faak, ki lebit ki jiantir linba ki Yennu nann, ŋaan nyik Yennu mɔŋ mɔŋ, wunba yen baakir kur mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
text: Li paak, ki Yennu nyikib ki bi tuun bi mɔŋ loomm nba mɔk fei, ki bi poob nyik bii dɔɔ nan jab ŋaan ŋmat ki dɔɔ nan bi leeb;

ref: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
text: ki jab mun nyik bii dɔɔ nan poob ŋaan ŋmat ki dɔɔ nan bi leeb, ki bi niauŋ be bi leeb ni bonchiann, ki jab tee bonsia linba mɔk fei nan bi leeb, li paak, ki bi la tubdatu nba jaŋ nan bin gaar bi gbanant ni, bi biit maŋ paak.

ref: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
text: Nifosaarii nba din ki loon ki bin bann Yennu, li paake ki Yennu nyikib nan bi dudukbiit, ki bi tuun toona nba bi bonchiann.

ref: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
text: Ki biit kur din be bi ni, ki bi gbee nan biit nan nianchiauŋ nan yanbɔmm nan funfunbiit nan nikpinu nan kunkɔna nan fai nan dudukbiit, ki bia tee sanjanjanna;

ref: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
text: ki piak biir bi leeb, ki ki loon Yennu, ki sukii bi leeb ki mɔk garuk ki dont bi mɔŋ, ki bia ŋammii dukii dudukbiit sɔnii, ki ki tee bi naanba nan bi baanba nibaakir.

ref: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
text: Bi bia ki mɔk yansian bi ni; bi ki waa bi mɔsona biaki; bi ki mɔk loomm nan ninbatinu bi leeb po.

ref: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
text: Bi mi nan Yennu sennu wann nan binba tuun linba na booru, bi nyɔɔt tee kuume, ŋaan bi lek kpan tuun ŋanne, ki bia mɔk parpeenn nan binba tuun li toonbooru mun.

ref: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
text: Li paak, nifosaarik nba want u lɔɔ biit, u ki mɔk sɔnu, kimaan fa want a lɔɔ biit na, li want nan a mɔŋ mɔŋ mɔk biit, kimaan fa want a lɔɔ biit na, a mɔŋ mɔŋ mun tuun nnee.

ref: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
text: Ti mi nan Yennu tan sa bu binba tuun nna na buuta; ki u buut maŋ tan sii tee barmɔniie.

ref: Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse?
text: Fa want leeb biit ba tuun biaŋinba, ŋaan mun tuun nna na, a dukii nan Yennu kan dat a tubirii?

ref: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
text: amii a yisin loonchiauŋ nan sukuru bonchiann nba ki Yennu mɔk nant na amii nlee? a ki mi nan Yennu mɔk burchint nana a wun te ki a nyik a biit na ŋaan kii be fanuee?

ref: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
text: Ŋaan a par wakii ki a lek ki sak nan fan nyik toonbiit tumu na, li paak, a pukii a mɔŋ fara nba ki a tan sa la daar nba ki Yennu tan sa bu niib buut nan barmɔnii, ki u wutoor na tan sa nyi paanu;

ref: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
text: kimaan sɔɔ kur sa la u toona nyɔɔt nba tee biaŋinba.

ref: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
