text: Nisiab mɔk mintir ki tuun toonŋana ki loon ki Yennu-n pakib ki turib nyɔɔt nan manfoor, ki ŋamme set sa la manfoor nba ki mɔk gbennu.

ref: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
text: Ŋaan binba mɔk kunkɔna, ki ki sak linba tee barmɔnii na, ŋaan tuun toonbiit, Yennu wutoor sa do bi paak.

ref: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: U sa jii fara nan muksuk ki tur nisaarii nba kur tuun toonbiit na; Juu teeb nee sa la li tubdatu sinsinn, ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na-n ji joont;

ref: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: ŋaan Yennu sa jii paku nan nyɔɔt nan parmaasir ki tur binba kur tuun toonŋana na; Juu teebe sa gaar sinsinn, ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na-n joont;

ref: God doesn't have any favorites!
text: kimaan Yennu ki mɔk ningannii.

ref: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
text: Binba kur ki mi Yennu sennu ki tuun toonbiit, bi lek sa kpo bi toonbiit tumu na paaka, li ki tee ba ki dia sennu maŋ paak na kaa. Binba kur mɔk Yennu sennu na, ŋaan tuun toonbiit, ki Yennu sa jii sennu maŋe ki bu namm.

ref: God accepts those who obey his Law, but not those who simply hear it.
text: Li ki tee a binba gbia sennu nee tee niŋamm Yennu boori, ŋaan li tee binba tuun biaŋinba ki sennu na wann na, ŋamme tee niŋamm u boor.

ref: when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says. The Jews and the Law
text: Tɔn, li tan sii tee nnee daar maŋ daar nba ki Yennu tan sa bu niib kur para ni buut Yisa Masia paak, kaman nan ma mɔɔntir barŋanii maŋ biaŋinba na. Juu teeb nan sennu labaar

ref: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
text: Gotir, a yi a mɔŋ Juu nirɔe, ki mɔk parbifaanu a sennu na tee a yare, a li paak a a tee Yennu yɔɔe,

ref: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
text: ki mi linba ki Yennu loon fii tuun, ki a bia tumii sennu na ni ki mi linba ŋan nan tumu,

ref: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
text: ki teen yada nan a fit want jɔɔnii sɔnu, ki bia tee yentu ki tur binba be bunbɔnn ni na;

ref: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
text: ki fit kpaan jatit na kpaanii, ki bia want binba ki mi, kimaan a mi barmɔnii na nba sɔb biaŋinba sennu gbouŋ na ni.

ref: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
text: A want leeb barmɔnii na, ŋaan bee ki a ki want a mɔŋi? A want leeb a nanyuk ki ŋani, ŋaan fin jaamii?

ref: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
text: Fin nba want niib a bi daa tuun bonchonchonn toona na, ŋaan fin chommii? Fin nba ki loon pata maan na, ŋaan a jaan jiantu diit ni bona-aa?

ref: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
text: Fin nba nyu bara sennu maŋ paak na, a yêen sennu maŋ ki dint Yennu fei,

ref: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
text: kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yimm Juu teeb paake ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na piak ki sukii Yennu.”

ref: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
text: Fi-i sak Yennu sennu, a punpotu mɔk nyɔɔt, ŋaan fi-i kii sak sennu na, li ŋman a ki poti.

ref: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
text: Ŋaan binba ki tee Juu teeb ki ki pot, ŋaan sak sennu maŋ mɔbona ki waar, Yennu sa gaarib kaman nan wunba pot nee.

ref: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
text: Yimm Juu teeb mɔk sennu maŋ ki li sɔb gbouŋ ni; ki i bia porii puna, ŋaan yimme bia biir sennu na; ki binba ki pot bi puna ŋaan waa sennu na, ŋamme sa bu i buut ki biiri.

ref: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
text: Nirɔ nba tee Juu nirɔ paanu ni kuukɔɔ, u ki tee Juu nirɔ nan mɔnii. Potu nan mɔnii mun ki tee gbanant potu, linba be paanu ni.

ref: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
text: Juu nirɔ nan barmɔnii tee wunba tee Juu nirɔ u par nie. Potu nan mɔnii mun tee par nie, ki li potu tee Yennu Seyeeŋ tumu; li ki tee gbanant paanu ni kaa. Li daanɔ laat paku Yennu boore, li ki tee nisaarik boori.

ref: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
text: Tɔn, nyɔlante ki Juu teeb la ki gar binba ki tee Juu teebi? Bi punpotu mɔk nyɔɔtii?

ref: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
text: Li mɔk nyɔɔt bonchiann. Sinsinn tee Yennu din jii u sennu mɔbona na ki guun Juu teebe;

ref: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
text: ki bi siab-i diŋŋi kii tee yadateenteeb, li want nan Yennu ki tee barmɔnii daanɔ amii?

ref: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
text: Aaii, li ki want nna kaa. Niib kur-i bo-i tee fai damm, ŋaan Yennu ŋanin tee barmɔnpakirɔɔe. Li waa nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinba nee a “Niib-i bu fin Yennu buut, bi sa la ki a mɔk mɔnii, ki bia di mɔniie.”

ref: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
text: Ŋaan ti toonbiit-i fiit wann nan Yennu mɔk mɔnii, bee ki ti sa yeti? Wa dat ti tuba na paak, ki ti ji yet a u mɔk biitii? Aaii, n kan yet nna kaa.

ref: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
text: Yennu-i kii be fanu, u bo kan fit kii bu tingbouŋ na niib buuti.

ref: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
text: Tɔn, sɔɔ-i boi a “N faak na-i want nan Yennu set tee barmɔnii daanɔ, ki bia tee ki niib-n turɔ baakir, bee teen ki u sa dat n tubir a n tee biitdaanɔ?”

ref: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
text: Siab bia dukii a “Ŋaant ki tin tun toonbiit a bonŋann-n tan baar.” Nisiab sukiin ki yeen a mine yet nna. Yennu sa dat bi tuba kaman nan la jaŋ namm biaŋinba. Nirɔ kaa ki tee niŋanɔ

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
text: Ki li tee nlee? Timm nba tee Juu teeb na, ti binbeŋ chee binba ki tee Juu teeb binbeŋ amii? Aaii, ti ki chee bi. N poŋ dɔŋ yet a biit yikoo be Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb ni

ref: The Scriptures tell us, “No one is acceptable to God!
text: kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Sɔɔ kaa ki tee niŋanɔ, aaii, nan yenɔkɔɔwa gbaa.

ref: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
text: Sɔɔ kaa ki mɔk minu, sɔɔ kaa ki loon Yennu.

ref: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
text: Sɔɔ kur kpet sɔnuwa, sɔɔ kur tuun biite; sɔɔ kaa ki tuun toonŋana, sɔɔ kaa nan yenɔkɔɔwa gbaa;

ref: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
text: a bi mɔi yaa nan kaayaant nee, ki fai nyi bi jerima ni, ki linba nyi bi mɔi ni tee kaman nan waauk lɔbii nee;

ref: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
text: mɔpora nan mɔtont kuukɔɔe nyi bi mɔi ni.

ref: These people quickly become violent.
text: Bi sa kpi nirɔ yiama;

ref: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
text: a ki siaminba kur ki bi gar, parbiir tuu tan baar leŋe.

ref: They don't know how to live in peace.
text: Bi ki mi sɔnu nba ki bi sa tɔkin ki lin baar nan parmaasiri.

ref: They don't even fear God.”
text: Bi binbeŋ ni, bi ki tiin Yennu.”

ref: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
text: Tɔn, ti mi nan Yennu sennu-i yet linba, li yeen ki teen binba be li yikoo ni, li paake ki niib kur sii muu bi mɔi, ki tingbouŋ na teeb kur be Yennu buut ni, ki u tɔɔnn sɔɔ kaa ki mɔk mɔnii.

ref: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
text: Sɔɔ kaa ki sa fit wei sennu na nba senn biaŋinba ki teen niŋanɔ Yennu tɔɔnn; sennu na paake te ki nifosaarik bann nan u tuun toonbiit. Biaŋinba ki Yennu te ki nifosaarik tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn

ref: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
text: Ŋaan mɔtana, Yennu mɔŋ nba lon sɔnsau nba sa te ki nifosaarik-n teen niŋanɔ na ji be yeenin nie, ki u sɔkiniinba maan nan sennu nee wantit li po, ŋaan li ki nyi sennu na saku ni kaa.

ref: God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ.
text: Yennu te ki nisaarii kur nba teen Yisa Masia yada nee tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn, li nyii ba teen Yisa Masia yada nie, ki lukitin kaa,

ref: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
text: kimaan nifosaarii kur tun toonbiit ki ki be fanu kaman nan Yennu nba loon biaŋinba na.

ref: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
text: Yennu ninbatinu paake ki u yet a bi tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn; li tee piiniie, kimaan Yisa Masia pa bi paak ki nyinnib daabsin ni.

ref: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
text: ŋaan mɔtana u nyik chabit ki loon wun wann nan u set be fanu, ki bia yet a wunba teen Yisa yada na mun tee niŋanɔ ŋɔɔ Yennu tɔɔnn.

ref: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
text: Li paak, siar be ki ti mɔk parbifaant nannii? Aaii, li ki tee a nifosaarik nba waa sennu na paak kaa ki ti sa la tinnu, ŋaan li tee ta teen Yisa Masia yada na paake.

ref: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
text: Ti dukii ki yeen a nisaarik yada teenu paake ki Yennu yet a u tee niŋanɔ; ki li ki tee a wa waa sennu na paak kaa.

ref: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
text: Yennu tee Juu teeb kuukɔɔ Yennuee? Aaii, u tee Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb Yennue.

ref: There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith.
text: Yennu tee Yen-yenɔkɔɔe ki sa yet a Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb na, ki bi kur-n teen niŋamm u tɔɔnn bi yada teenu paak.

ref: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
text: Tɔn, ta yet a nifosaarik laat tinnu u yada teenu nie na, ŋanne te ki sennu na tee yannii? Aaii, li ki tee nna kaa. Ti lek see sennu na poe.

ref: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
text: Tɔn, ti sa yet a bee ki jikit saa ti yeja Abraham po, nan u binbeŋ po?

ref: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
text: Wa din waa sennu na-i diŋŋi te ki Yennu yet a u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn, u bo sii mɔk linba bo sa te ki u mɔk parbifaant, ŋaan u kii mɔk parbifaant Yennu tɔɔnni.

ref: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
text: Li-i tee ki nirɔ tuun toona, u nyɔɔt kii tee piinii kaa, ŋaan li sii tee paauko.

ref: But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him.
text: Ŋaan nirɔ nba ki teen sennu weiu na yada, ŋaan teen Yennu wunba nyikit biitdamm bi biit ki chabitib na yada, Yennu sa jii li daanɔ ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn; li daanɔ yada paake ki Yennu sa jiiu.

ref: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
text: Nnee ki David din yet a daanɔ nba ki Yennu jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn ki li ki tee a u toonŋana paak kaa, li daanɔ mɔk parpeenn bonchiann.

ref: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
text: Damm nba ki Yennu ki tian bi toonbiit po, bi mɔk parpeenn.”

ref: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
text: Li parpeenn maŋ tee binba pot na kuukɔɔe yar amii? Aaii, nan binba ki pot na mun yaa yare. Ti poŋ bo yet a li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Abraham din teen Yennu yadae, ki u yada maŋ paake ki Yennu jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn.”

ref: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
text: Nlee yooe ki Yennu din jiiu nna? Yennu poŋ din yiu niŋanɔewa ki u tan pot amii u din potewa ki Yennu yiu niŋanɔ? Aaii, Yennu poŋ din yiu niŋanɔewa ki u tan pot.

ref: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
text: U din pota, ŋaan u potu maŋ tee siara nba want nan u yada maŋ paake ki Yennu poŋ din jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ, li poorpoewa ki u ji tan pot. Li paak, binba kur tee Yennu yada ŋaan ki pot na, Abraham tee bi baae; ba tee Yennu yada paake, ki Yennu yib niŋamm.

ref: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
text: Abraham tee binba pot na mun yaa baae, li ki tee a ba pot na paak kaa, ŋaan ba bia waa biaŋinba ki ti baa Abraham din teen Yennu yada na, li poorpoewa ki u din tan pot; bi yada paake ki Abraham tee bi baa. Ta gaar Yennu mɔsonn yada paak biaŋinba na

ref: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
text: Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki tur Abraham nan u yaaboona a tingbouŋ na tan sii tee bi yare, li din ki tee nan Abraham nba waa sennu na paak kaa ki Yennu senn li mɔsonni, ŋaan li din tee u yada paake nan Yennu nba din jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn na.

ref: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
text: Li-i bo-i tee ki Yennu mɔsonn na tee binba dia sennu maŋ yarie, ŋann ŋanin, nirɔ yada bo sii tee yanne, ki Yennu mɔsonn bo kii mɔk nyɔɔti.

ref: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
text: Sennu nee baat nan Yennu wutoor li mɔyêtuk paak, ŋaan bi-i kii senn sennu, mɔyêtuk sii kaa.

ref: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
text: Yennu mɔsonn na tee binba teenɔ yada na yare; u ninbatinu paak, ki u senn mɔsonn maŋ ki li set tee Abraham yaaboona kur yar, li ki tee a binba waa sennu na kuukɔɔ yar kaa, ŋaan binba kur teen Yennu yada kaman nan Abraham nba din teen biaŋinba, kimaan ŋɔɔ Abrahame tee ti kura baa

ref: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
text: kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni ki u yet Abraham a “N teena nibooru bonchiann baae.” Li paak, Yennu boor Abraham tee ti baae, Yennu wunba ki Abraham teenɔ yada na, ŋɔɔe te ki kpeemm laat manfoa, ki bia piak linba daa ki be po kaman nan li set be.

ref: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
text: Siar din ki want dindann ŋaan Abraham lek ji din mɔk dindann nan Yennu sa turɔ bika, ki bia teen yada, ki u yada teenu paak ki u din teen nibooru bonchiann baa kaman nan Yennu nba yetɔ a “A yaaboona tan sa pɔt bonchiann.”

ref: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
text: U mantik din teen Yennu yada, u din ki mɔk birsuk nan Yennu mɔsonn na, u yadae din te ki u mɔk paŋ bonchiann, ki u din piak Yennu.

ref: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
text: U mantik din teen Yennu yada ki ki birisi, ki mi nan Yennu nba senn biaŋinba, u set sa fit tuma.

ref: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
text: Ŋaan Abraham kuukɔɔ paak kaa ki li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yennu jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ,”

ref: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
text: ŋaan li sɔb ki teen timm nba ki Yennu sa gaarit mun ki ti tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn, kimaan ta teenɔ yada, ŋɔɔ Yennu wunba fiin ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa kuun ni na.

ref: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
text: Ŋɔɔe din jii Yisa ki chab ki u kpo ti toonbiit paak, ŋaan bia fiinɔ kuun ni, a wun te ki tii tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn.

ref: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
text: Tɔn, ta teen Yisa yada na paake, ki Yennu yet a ti tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn, ki parmaasir ji be timm nan Yennu sinsuuk ni ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak.

ref: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
text: U paake ki Yennu tint ninbaauk ti yada maŋ paak; ki ti la u ninbatinu maŋ ki mɔk parpeenn, kimaan ti mɔk dindann nan ti tan sii mɔk binbenŋaŋ nba ki Yennu sa turit na.

ref: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
text: Ti bia mɔk parpeenn ti fardinu ni, kimaan ti mi nan fardinu baat nan sukurue,

ref: And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope
text: ki sukuru mun baat nan binbenŋaŋ, ki binbenŋaŋ maŋ mun baat nan dindann,

ref: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
text: ki dindann maŋ ni ti tan kan di fei, kimaan Yennu jii loomm nba ki u mɔk na ki teen ti para ni, u Seyeeŋ nba ki u turit na paak.

ref: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
text: Li paar nan nirɔ-n gaar nipeemɔ paak ki kpo, ŋaan li pasiar a sa la sɔɔ wun gaar niŋanɔ paak ki kpo;

ref: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
text: ŋaan Yennu wann nan u set loonit bonchiann kimaan ta din tee biitdamm na Yisa Masia din gaar ti paak ki kpo.

ref: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
text: U sɔn paak, ki Yennu yet a ti tee niŋamme; li paak mun u kpan sa nyinnit tubdatu nba baat ti paak na ni.

ref: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
text: Ti din tuu tee Yennu dataie, ŋaan mɔtana jik u teent u yɔɔsinbae kimaan u Bija nba din kpo na sɔn paak, ki ta tee u yɔɔs na, birsuk ji kaa nan u sa tinnit u manfoor paaki.

ref: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
text: Ŋaan li kur kaa na; ti bia mɔk parpeenn Yennu nba sommit bonchiann biaŋinba ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak, kimaan u paake ki Yennu teent u yɔɔsinba mɔtana. Adam nan Masia labaar

ref: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
text: Tɔn, niyenɔkɔɔe din te ki biit baar tingbouŋ na ni, ki u biit na paake te ki kuun be. Li paake te ki kuun kpi niib kur, kimaan nisaarik kur tun toonbiita.

ref: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
text: Biit din be tingbouŋ na niewa ki Yennu din tan tur Moses sennu na, ŋaan sennu-i kaa, sɔɔ kan fikis nirɔ ki yet a u biir sennu maŋi.

ref: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
text: Adam yoo neewa ki tan tuu Moses yoo, ki kuun lek mɔk yikoo sɔɔ kur paak, ki li kpi binba mɔk biit nba ki tee kaman nan Adam biit nba din tee biaŋinba na. Tɔn, Adam din tee wunba din yaa wun wei poor na ninnauŋo.

ref: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
text: Ŋaan bi bannlee na ki jan, kimaan Yennu piinii nba ki u pit yann na ki tee kaman nan Adam toonbiit na kaa. Li tee mɔniie nan niib bonchiann kpo niyenɔkɔɔ toonbiit paake; Yennu ninkpabauŋ yab, ki u piinii nba ki u teen niib bonchiann ki li nyii yenɔkɔɔ Yisa Masia ninkpabauŋ na mun bia yab.

ref: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
text: Bɔkitu be Yennu piinii nan niyenɔkɔɔ Adam toonbiiuk sinsuuk ni; kimaan niyenɔkɔɔ Adam toonbiiuk maŋ poorpoe ki Yennu din bu u buut ki yet a u toonbiiuk maŋ ŋan nan tubdatu; ŋaan ŋɔɔ Yisa Masia piinii nba ki u pit yann na tee nyikinchab, toonbichiauŋ poorpoe.

ref: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
text: Niyenɔkɔɔ maŋ toonbiiuk paake ki kuun kpi sɔɔ kur, ki li nyii niyenɔ maŋ boore; ŋaan linba wei niyenɔkɔɔ Yisa Masia toonn na gar nnawa; niib kur nba la Yennu ninbatinu nba yab, nan u piinii nba tee nyikinchab piinii na, bi sa di naan kii mɔk manfoa Yisa Masia paak.

ref: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
text: Niyenɔkɔɔ maŋ toonbiiuk paake te ki sɔɔ kur ŋan nan tubdatu, nnee mun, niyenɔkɔɔ toonŋann paake te ki sɔɔ kur laat tinnu nan manfoor.

ref: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
text: Niyenɔkɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Adam na mɔyêtuk paake te ki niib kur teen biitdamm; li mun tee nnee nan niyenɔkɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Masia na mɔsaku paake te ki niib bonchiann teen niŋamm Yennu boor.

ref: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
text: Sennu na baar a lin ŋamm fiit wann toonbiit nba yab biaŋinbae; ki toonbiit nba yab na, Yennu ninbatinu mun yab ki gar nna.

ref: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
text: Li paak, biit nba din kpi nifosaarii ki te ki bi kpenn biaŋinba na, nnee mun ki Yennu ninbatinu paak te ki u yet a bi tee niŋamm u boor, ki turib manfoa nba kaa gbennu, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak.

ref: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
text: Li paak, ti sa yet nlee? Ti bia sii be ti toonbiit tumu nie ki Yennu ninbatinu na-n pukin amii-ii?

ref: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
text: Aaii, ŋann kaa. Li tee kaman nan ti kpo nee, ki li paak biit ki mɔk ti yaaki. Ŋannimi ti kan biar toonbiit tumu ni.

ref: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
text: Timm nba ki bi wurit Yennu nyunwuru nan Yisa Masia sann ni na, li tee kaman nan ti lakin kpo nanɔ nee.

ref: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
text: Li paak, ti din kpoe nan Yisa Masia ki bi lakin piit nanɔ Yennu nyunwuru ni; ki u Baa Yennu din jii u paŋ ki fiinɔ kuun ni, nnee ki ti mun fiir a tii be manfopaann ni.

ref: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
text: Li-i tee ki timm nan Masia lakin kpo, nnee biak ki ti bia sa lakin ki fiir kuun ni nan wa din fiir biaŋinba na.

ref: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
text: Ti mi nan ti binbenkper na lakin ki kpo nan Yisa u dapunpunn paak, a ti binbenkper na ji daa mɔk yaak, ki ti ji kii tee daaba biit paŋ ni.

ref: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
text: Nirɔ-i kpo, u nyii biit paŋ niwa.

ref: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
text: Ta kpo nan Masia na, ti mɔk yada nan ti sii be nanɔ.

ref: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
text: Ti mi nan Yennu fiin Masia kuun niwa ki u ji kan mi kpo; kuun ki mɔk u yaaki.

ref: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
text: U din kpo ti biit paake taar yomm, ŋaan wa ji be u manfoor ni na, u be ki teen Yennue.

ref: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
text: Li paak, i mun dukin nan li tee kaman nan i kpoe ki biit ji ki mɔk i yaaki, ŋaan Yisa Masia paak i ji be i manfoor ni Yennu boor.

ref: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
text: Li paak, i daa te ki biit-n yent i gbanant ki te ki i tuun biit loommu.

ref: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
text: Daa teen i gbanant siar po yaak ki li tuun toonbiit, ŋaan jii i mɔŋ ki tur Yennu, ŋaan kii tee kaman nan i nyii kuun nie ki kɔɔ manfoor ni na; jiin i mɔŋ kur ki tur Yennu ki wun jii-i ki tun yentinu toona.

ref: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
text: Biit daa mɔk i yaak, kimaan sennu na ki yeniini, ŋaan Yennu ninbatinu nee yeni. Yentinu daaba labaar

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
text: Tɔn, sennu na nba ki yenti, ŋaan ki Yennu ninbatinu nee yent na, ti sa biar toonbiit tumu ni amii? Aaii, ti kan teen nna kaa.

ref: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
text: Fi-i jii a mɔŋ ki pet ki tur nirɔ ki tee u daabir ki saak u mɔb, li tee mɔniie nan a set tee u daabire. Fi-i sak ki tuun toonbiit, a tee biit daabire, ki li joontu tee kuume; ŋaan fi-i sak Yennu mɔb, a tee mɔsakdaanɔe, ki ŋanne sa te ki a teen niŋanɔ Yennu boor.

ref: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
text: Sianyoo, biit din mɔk i yaak, ŋaan mɔtana i mantik saak barmɔnii wannu nba ki bi din wanti na; li paak, ki n teen Yennu niipoouk.

ref: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
text: Mɔtana i ji ki tee biit daaba, ŋaan yentinue ji mɔk i yaak.

ref: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
text: Ya din tuu tee biit daaba na, i din tuu ki tee yentinu yabi.

ref: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
text: Toona nba ki i din tuun ki li mɔki fei mɔtana na, li mɔk nyɔlante ki turiini? Li toonbooru baat nan kuume.

ref: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
text: Ŋaan mɔtana i ji nyii biit daabsin niwa ki ji tee Yennu daabae; li paak, ki i teen popeendamm, ki mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu; ŋanne tee i nyɔɔt.

ref: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
text: Kimaan biit nyɔɔt tee kuume, ŋaan Yennu piinii tee manfoor nba kaa gbennue, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak.

ref: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
text: N naa waas, i kur mi sennu na po, li paak, i sa fit soor ma yaa n yet linba na paaka. Sennu na so wunba be u manfoor nie.

ref: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
text: Li paak, sennu na nɔɔk ni, li kpaa talas nan poo-n taan nan u sɔrɔ yoo nba ki u sɔrɔ daa be u manfoor ni, ŋaan u sɔrɔ na-i taŋŋi kpo yoonba, sennu na ji ki so poo na.

ref: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
text: N naa waas, li mun tee nnee ki turi; i tee Masia gbanant bɔkire, ki din kpoe ki sennu na ji ki soini; ŋaan mɔtana i gaa nan Masia wunba din fiir kuun ni nee, a tii tuun linba ŋan ki tur Yennu.

ref: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
text: Yoo nba ki ti din tuun ti gbanant loomm nee, ki sennu na te ki ti mɔk nianbiiuk ki li tuun ti gbanant ni, ki li baat nan linba tee kuun ti paak.

ref: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
text: Mɔtana ŋanin, sennu na ki diati, kimaan ti din kpoe ki sennu na ji ki mɔk ti yaaki. Li paak, ti ji fit tuun ki teen Yennu nan manfopaann nba ki u Seyeeŋ turit; ti ji ki tuun nan sennkper nba ki bi din sɔb gbouŋ ni na kaa. Sennu nan biit labaar

ref: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
text: Li paak, ti sa yet a nlee? Ti sa yet a sennu na ki ŋan amii? Ti kan yet nna kaa. Sennu na-i bo-i kaa, n bo kan bann biit nba tee linba. Sennu na-i bo-i kii wann a n daa mɔk niauŋ n lɔɔ bont paaki, n bo kan bann niauŋ nba tee linba,

ref: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
text: ŋaan ki biit la yaak sennu na ni, ki te ki nianbooru booru bonchiann fiir n par ni. Sennu na-i bo-i kaa, biit bo sii tee bonkpeenne.

ref: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
text: Yoo nba ki n din ki mi sennu maŋ na, n din mɔk manfoore; ŋaan ma tan bann sennu maŋ, ki biit nba be n par ni na fiir, ki n kpo.

ref: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
text: Sennu nba bo yaa li baat nan manfoor na, ki li baar nan kuun ki turin.

ref: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
text: Biit la u sɔnu sennu na nie ki kpannin, ki leŋe ki li kpiin.

ref: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
text: Sennu na tiauŋ ŋanin yeene, ki bia tee barmɔnii nan linba fan.

ref: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
text: Tɔn, li want nan linba ŋane baar nan kuun n paak amii? Aaii, li ki want nna kaa. Biit nee jii linba ŋan na ki baar nan kuun turin, a lin wann biit nba tee linba. Ŋanne ki ti ji bann nan biit tee bonbilankante. Bona ŋannlee nba ki nisaarik loon

ref: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
text: Ti mi nan sennu na tee Yennu Seyeeŋ yare, ŋaan min ŋanin tee nisaarike, ki tee biit daabir,

ref: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
text: kimaan linba ki n tuun na, n ki mi li paaki, kimaan n ki tuun toonŋana nba ki n looni; ŋaan toonbiit nba ki n nammir na, ŋanne ki n tuun.

ref: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
text: Mi-i tun toonbiit nba ki n bo ki loon ki n tun na, li want nan n sak nan sennu maŋ ŋane.

ref: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Li paak, li ki tee n mɔŋ kaa tuumiri, ŋaan biit nba be n ni nee te ki n tuun.

ref: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
text: N mi nan bonŋansiar kaa n par ni; n loon ki n tun linba ŋan, ŋaan n kan fit ki tuumiri;

ref: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Li paak, mi-i tuun linba ki n bo ki loon mii tuumi, li ji ki tee min kaa tuumi, ŋaan li tee biit nba be n ni nee tuun.

ref: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
text: N la ki bonsiar be n ni yoo kura, mi-i yaa n tun linba ŋan, ŋaan biit bia lek tuu be nanin.

ref: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
text: Yennu sennu nee te ki n par peen,

ref: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
text: ŋaan ki n laat sennsiar nba tuun n gbanant ni, ki li ki sak nan sennu nba ki n par loon na, ki lorin nan dansarkɔɔrɔ na, a n sak biit nba be n ni na.

ref: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
text: Aba, n tee parbiir daanɔe. Ŋmee sa fatin ki nyinnin gbanant nba saa nanin kuun ni na?

ref: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
text: N piak Yennu wunba fatin ki lun Yisa Masia ni na paak. Li paak, n mɔŋ waa Yennu sennu na n par ni, ŋaan ki n gbanant waa biit sɔnu na.

ref: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
text: Tɔn, mɔtana Yennu kan bu ki biir binba be Yisa Masia ni,

ref: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
text: kimaan ma tee Yisa Masia yɔɔ na paake, ki Seyeeŋ nba teent manfoor na fatin biit nba baat nan kuun na nuu ni ki lun.

ref: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
text: Yennue tun u mɔŋ Bija tingbouŋ na ni, a wun gaar gbanant nba naan nan timm biitdamm gbanant na, ki tan gaar ti paak ki kpo ti biit paak. Ki u kuun maŋ paak, Yennu biir biit nba be timm nisaarii para ni na paŋ. Sennu na ki fit tun nna, kimaan timm nisaarii ki fit diar fanu.

ref: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
text: Yennue tun nna, a timm nba ki ti binbeŋ waa Yennu Seyeeŋ na ki li ki tee ti nisaarii loomm na, sa sak ki wei barmɔnii sɔnu, sennu nba wann biaŋinba.

ref: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
text: Binba binbeŋ waa nisaarik loomm na jiit bi dudukit ki tee nisaarik loomm nie, ŋaan binba binbeŋ waa Yennu Seyeeŋ na mun jiit bi dudukite ki tee Seyeeŋ na loomm ni.

ref: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
text: Fi-i jii a yan ki gaan nan nisaarik loomm, li tee kuume, ŋaan fi-i jii a yan ki gaan nan Yennu Seyeeŋ loomm na, li tee manfoore nan parmaasir.

ref: Our desires fight against God, because they do not and cannot obey God's laws.
text: Fi-i jii a yan ki gaan nisaarik loomm ni, a tee Yennu dataake, ki ki saak Yennu sennu, ki set kan fit ki sakiri.

ref: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
text: Wunba waa nisaarik loomm kan fit penn Yennu pari.

ref: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ-i set be i ni, i benu ki waa nisaarik loommu, ŋaan li waa Yennu Seyeeŋ na loomme. Wunba ki mɔk Masia Seyeeŋ maŋ u ni, li daanɔ ki tee Masia yɔɔ.

ref: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
text: I gbanant nba lek kpo biit paak na, ŋaan Masia-i be i ni, i sei mɔk manfoor, kimaan Yennu yet a i tee niŋamm u boor.

ref: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
text: Ki ŋɔɔ Yennu nba fiin Yisa kuun ni na, u Seyeeŋ na-i be i ni, ŋɔɔ nba fiin Masia kuun ni na, ŋɔɔe bia sa tur i gbanant manfopaann u Seyeeŋ nba be i ni na paak.

ref: My dear friends, we must not live to satisfy our desires.
text: Li paak, n naa waas, i mi man tin wei gbanant loomm.

ref: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
text: Li-i tee ki i waa gbanant loomm, i sa kpowa, ŋaan li-i tee ki i kpi gbanant loomm Yennu Seyeeŋ paŋ na paak, i sa la manfoor.

ref: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
text: Binba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ ŋmakitirib sɔnu na, ŋamme tee Yennu waas.

ref: God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father.
text: U Seyeeŋ nba ki u turi na, u ki te ki i tee daaba nan jaŋmaandamm kaa, ŋaan u te ki i tee Yennu waase ki yi Yennu a “Ti Baa.”

ref: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ maŋe te ki ti mɔŋ sei mi bonŋann nan ti tee Yennu waas.

ref: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
text: Ta set tee u waas na paak, ti sa la u bonŋann nba ki u bir ki guun u niib nawa; ti bia sa lakin nan Masia ki gaarira. Kimaan ti-i di fara Masia paak, u tan sa donnit u peŋa. Parpeenn nba ki ti sa la na labaar

ref: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
text: N mi nan fara nba ki ti di mɔtana na, li kan fit yekin nan parpeenn nba ki ti tan sa la Yendɔuŋ ni na.

ref: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
text: Dunia bona kur nba ki Yennu nann na guu nan ninmɔnne nan yoo nba ki Yennu tan sa nyinn u waas yeenin ni,

ref: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
text: kimaan dunia bona kur din biira ki ji ki mɔk nyɔɔti, li din ki tee li loomm kaa, li din tee Yennu loomme ki li teen nna; ŋaan ki u bia lek ŋaan ki li mɔk dindann.

ref: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
text: Dasiar be ki dunia bona maŋ tan sa nyi kuun daabsin ni, ki kuun ji kan diar, ki li mun tan sii mɔk parpeenn nba ki Yennu waas tan sa gaar na.

ref: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
text: Ti mi nan li yiar dunia bona maŋ ki tan tuu mɔtana kaman nan poo nba tuu mɔ matyiaru ki kurii biaŋinba na.

ref: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
text: Li ki tee dunia bona na kuukɔɔ kaa ki li yiari, ŋaan timm nba mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋ nba tee sinsinn piinii na mun kuriie ti para ni, ki guu yoo nba ki Yennu tan sa nyinn ki wann yeenin ni a ti tee u waase, ki te ki ti gbanant-n nyi daabsin ni.

ref: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
text: Ta la tinnu nee ki ti mɔk dindann na. Tɔn, nirɔ-i la linba ki u bo mɔk dindann li paak na, ŋann ŋanin, li ji ki tee dindann. Ki ŋmee jin sii mɔk dindann nan linba ki u poŋ lar paaki?

ref: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
text: Ŋaan ti-i mɔk dindann nan linba ki ti ki laat paak, ŋann ŋanin, ti ji tuu guu nan sukurue.

ref: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
text: Nnee mun ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na baar a wun sommit ti bakint ni, kimaan ti ki mi biaŋinba ki ti sii mei Yennu. Ŋɔɔ Yennu Seyeeŋ mɔŋe mei Yennu ki teent ki te ki ti kurii ki mei ti para ni ki ti mɔbona kan fit pak li paaki.

ref: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
text: Ki Yennu nba laat nisaarik par ni mi Seyeeŋ na dudukit nba tee, kimaan Seyeeŋ na mei Yennu u niib paak, ki li waa nan ŋɔɔ Yennu nba loon biaŋinba na.

ref: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
text: Binba ki Yennu yiimm na, ki u mɔk linba paak ki u yimme, ki bi mɔk loomm nanɔ, ki ti mi nan linba kur tuun, u te ki li teen bonŋann ki teen ŋamme.

ref: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
text: U poŋ din senn nan binba ki u yiimm na a bi binbeŋ-ii tee kaman nan u Bija binbeŋ nee, ki Bija na-n teen Bikperik, waas bonchiann sinsuuk ni.

ref: God then accepted the people he had already decided to choose, and he has shared his glory with them. God's Love
text: Ki Yennu yiin binba ki u senn na, ki binba ki u yiimm na ki u yet a bi tee niŋamm u boor, ki binba ki u yet a bi tee niŋamm u boor na, ki u donnib. Yennu loomm labaar

ref: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
text: Ti-i kpaa ti ninbina ki got linba na kur paak, ti sa yet a nlee? Li-i tee ki Yennu be ti po, ŋmee ji be ki sa biiriti?

ref: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
text: Yennu ki minnit u mɔŋ Bija na, ŋaan jiiu ki turit; ŋɔɔ Yennu turit u Bija maŋ, ki nlee ki u ji kan turit linba kur biari?

ref: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
text: Ŋmee sa fikis Yennu nigannkabi? Yennue nyinnib ki bi ki tee biitdamm u boori.

ref: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
text: Ŋmee be ki sa bu bi buut ki biiribi? Sɔɔ kaa. Yisa Masia wunba din kpo ki fiir kuun ni na amii? Yisa Masia wunba be Yennu niidiitu po ki tee ti sindantinnɔ na amii?

ref: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
text: Ŋmee sa fit nyinnit Yisa Masia loomm ni? Ti fara amii maan amii sukit amii kon amii namu amii parbiir amii kuume? Aaii.

ref: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
text: Li teen ki turit kaman nan la sɔb biaŋinba Yennu gbouŋ ni na a “A paake ki ti be kuun fara ni sanyiɔk niwa ki tan tuu dajouk, ki ti tee kaman nan peeuk nba ki bi tuu tee siir a bin kpiu nee.”

ref: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
text: Ŋaan nan linba na kur li tee yanne, kimaan ti la nyannu kura Yisa Masia wunba loont na paak.

ref: I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future,
text: N mi fanu nan siar kaa ki sa fit bɔkit timm nan Masia loomm ni; kuun amii manfoor amii malakanba amii kpanbara amii yentdamm amii linba tee mɔtana amii linba tan sa teen amii

ref: and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
text: linba be sanpaapo amii tinpɔɔr ni amii; siar kaa naauŋ kur ni ki sa fit bɔkit timm nan Yennu loomm ni, loomm nba nyii ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia ni na.

ref: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
text: N tee Masia nirɔe ki ki fa faaki. Yennu Seyeeŋ nee yen n dudukit ki teen siara a n ki fa faak, linba ki n yaa n pak na tee mɔniie;

ref: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
text: a n mɔk parbiir bonchiann nba kaa gbennu,

ref: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
text: n mɔŋ booru paak. Li-i bo-i sa fit ki n set n niib paak ki Masia-n biirin, n bo sa sak nna.

ref: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
text: Ŋamme tee Israel teeb, ki Yennu din gannib ki din te ki bi tee u waas, ki din taan namm nan yentsaakar, ki turib mɔlora, ki bia turib u sennu, ki wannib barmɔnii jiantu, ki senn mɔsona ki turib.

ref: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
text: Bi yejamm din tee nijaanae, ki Masia nba din kpant nisaarik na, u din tee bi booru ni yenɔe. Yennu nba yen bont kur na, u sa la paku ki li kii mɔk gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
text: N ki yeen a Yennu mɔsonn na ji kan tun kaa, kimaan Israel yaaboona kur kaa tee Israel teeb nan barmɔnii.

ref: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
text: Li paak, binba ki Abraham marib nifosaarik maaru na ki tee Yennu waasi. Binba tee Yennu mɔsonn maaru waas na, ŋamme set tee Yennu waas.

ref: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
text: Yennu mɔsonn na din tee a “Yoo nba ki n tan sa jen na, li yooe ki Sara sa mar bonjabik.”

ref: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Ŋaan li kur kaa na. Ti yaa Rebeka din mar waas bannlee ki bi taa baa, wunba tee ti yeja Aisak.

ref: Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done.
text: Yoo nba ki bi daa din ki marib ki bi ki tun linba ŋan amii linba bi na, ki Yennu din yet a bikperik na tan sii tee waarɔ po na jiamɔ. Yennu din yet nna a u loomm na-n saan tɔɔnn. Li paak, u loomm na tee wun gann niibe, li ki tee bi toona paak kaa ki bi tee u niibi; ŋaan li tee wa loon ki gann nee.

ref: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
text: Li tee kaman nan Yennu nba yet u gbouŋ ni biaŋinba nee a “N loon Jakɔb ki gar Esɔ.”

ref: The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to.
text: Yennu din yet Moses a “Binba ki n loon ki n tinib ninbaauk na, ŋamme ki n sa tinib ninbaauk.”

ref: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
text: Li ki tee nisaarik loomm amii binbeŋ kaa, ŋaan li tee Yennu ninbatinue.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni ki tur Faro a “Min Yennu dinna naan linba na paake a n jiia ki wanna n paŋ, ki te ki n sann-n mɔɔnt lin tingbouŋ na kur.”

ref: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
text: Li paak, binba ki Yennu loon ki wun tinib ninbaauk na, ŋamme ki u tiinib ninbaauk, ki bia teen siab nikangbata kaman nan wa loon biaŋinba. Yennu wutoor nan ninbatinu labaar

ref: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
text: Tɔn, li pasiar ki i yenɔ-n boin a ki li-i tee nnaie, nlee ki Yennu laat nisaarik biiti? Ŋmee sa fit ki yêt linba ki Yennu senni?

ref: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
text: Ŋaan n yɔɔk, a tee ŋmee ki sa pak jiin Yennu? Bobir kan fit boi bobmaarɔ a “Bee ki a maan ki n tee nna?”

ref: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
text: Bobmaarɔ mɔk yaak ki sa jii yɔuk bur ki maa bobŋann nan bob-biiuk.

ref: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
text: Nnee ki Yennu tun ki loon wun wann u wutoor ki niib kura-n bann u paŋ. Li paak ki u mɔk mintir nan binba ki u wutoor sa baar bi paak ki li bo jaŋ nan wun biirib na;

ref: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
text: ki bia poŋ dɔŋ gann niib nba ki u tinib ninbaauk na a wun donnib ki wann wa mɔk burchint bonchiann biaŋinba.

ref: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
text: Li tee kaman nan Hosea nba din sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinba, a Yennu yet ki yaa a “Niib nba din ki tee n niib na, ŋamme ki n sa jiib ki bii tee n niib; a ki booru nba ki n din ki loon na, ŋamme ki n sa yiimm booru nba ki n loomm.

ref: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
text: a ki siaminba ki bi din yetib a bi ki tee n niib na, a leŋe biak ki bi sa yiimm ‘Manfodaanɔ Yennu na waas.’ ”

ref: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
text: Yennu sɔkinii Aisaya din fiit yeenin ni ki wann Israel teeb po ki yet a “Israel teeb-i lekii yab kaman nan mɔkgbeŋŋir tanbiinii na, ŋaan bi waaminnae sa la tinnu;

ref: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
text: kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ sa dat tingbouŋ na teeb tuba yian yian ki gbenn kaman nan wa yet biaŋinba na.”

ref: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
text: Li tee kaman nan Aisaya nba din yet nee a “Ti yabint Yomdaanɔ-i bo-i kii tennit waas, li bo tan sa tun ki turit kaman nan la din tun ki tur Sodom nan Gomora doi ni niib biaŋinba nae.” Israel teeb nan barŋanii labaar

ref: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
text: Tɔn, ti sa yet nlee? Binba ki tee Juu teeb na din ki kor a bin teen niŋamm Yennu boori, ŋaan ki Yennu yet a bi tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn, bi yada na paak.

ref: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
text: Bee ki bi din kɔŋi? Bi din ki kor a wun yet a bi tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn bi yada paaki; bi din kor a Yennu-n yet a bi tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn bi toona paake; bi gbiat tanne ki baa,

ref: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
text: tann nba ki bi sɔb li po Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Gotir, min Yennu bir tann Sion doo ni, linba sa te ki niib-n gbiat ki baa; a li tee tangbeŋŋire, ki bi sa birint li paak ki baa. Ŋaan wunba teenɔ yada, u kan di fei.”

ref: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
text: N naa waas, n loon nan n par kur ki mei Yennu a wun tinn n mɔŋ booru.

ref: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
text: Bi ki mi sɔnu nba te ki Yennu yet a niib tee niŋamm u boor, ŋaan loon bi mɔŋ sɔnu, ki li paak, bi ki jii bi mɔŋ ki lor nan Yennu sɔnu na.

ref: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
text: Kimaan Moses sennu weiu ji kan te ki Yennu-n yet a sɔɔ tee niŋanɔ u boori; ŋaan wunba kur teen Masia yada, Yennu sa yet a li daanɔ tee niŋanɔe u boor. Tinnu nba tee booru kur yar biaŋinba

ref: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
text: Moses din sɔb a “Wunba tee popeendaanɔ kaman nan sennu nba senn biaŋinba, u sa la manfoor li paaka.”

ref: But people whose faith makes them acceptable to God will never ask, “Who will go up to heaven to bring Christ down?”
text: Ŋaan binbenŋaŋ nba waa yada teenu na paake, Yennu gbouŋ yet nnee a “A daa dukii a par ni a Ŋmee tan sa do Yendɔuŋ ni?”

ref: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
text: “A bia daa boi a Ŋmee tan sa sik kpeenn tiŋ ni?”

ref: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
text: Yennu gbouŋ nba want biaŋinba na tee a “Yennu barŋanii na perawa; li be a mɔb ni nan a par ni.” Barŋanii maŋ tee yada labaar nba ki ti mɔɔntir na.

ref: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
text: Li-i tee ki a set pak ki kat nyinn a mɔb ni ki yet a Yisa tee a Yomdaanɔ, ki bia teen yada a par ni nan Yennu set fiinɔ kuun ni, Yennu sa tinnawa.

ref: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Daanɔ nba teenɔ yada, u kan mi di fei.”

ref: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
text: Bɔkitu kaa Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb sinsuuk ni, Yennu kuukɔɔ nee tee ti kura Yomdaanɔ, ki binba kur meiɔ, u sommitib nan burchintuk.

ref: All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba kur mei ti Yomdaanɔ sommir po, u sa la tinnu.”

ref: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
text: Tɔn, niib-i kii teenɔ yada, bi sa teen nlee ki miarɔ sommir po? Ki bi-i kii gbat barŋanii maŋi, bi sa teen nlee ki teenɔ yada? ki bi-i kii mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋi, nlee ki niib sa gbat li po?

ref: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
text: Ki Yennu-i kii tun toomiinba, nlee ki barŋanii maŋ sa mɔɔnti? Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Binba lin ki mɔɔntir barŋanii maŋ, bi baaru ŋan bonchiann.”

ref: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
text: Ŋaan niib kur kaa sak barŋanii maŋi. Aisaya din sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “N Yomdaanɔ, ŋmee sak barŋanii na?”

ref: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
text: N loon ki n boi ki laan niib ki gbat barŋanii maŋ amii? Mɔmɔmm, bi gbata. Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Niib gbat bi kunkɔra nan bi mɔmaan tingbouŋ lokir kur po.”

ref: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
text: N yaa n ŋamm ki boie ki laan Israel teeb din ki bann li paak amii? Moses tiauŋo din dɔŋ jiin sinsinn, a Yennu din yet Israel teeb, a u sa te ki bii mɔk funfunn nan niib nba ki mɔk booru na paak, ki te ki bi wutoa-n do booru nba tee jatit na paak.

ref: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
text: Ki Aisaya mɔk par, ki yet a Yennu yaa a niib nba ki fiin u po na, bi tan lauwa; a ki niib nba ki boi u po na, ki u jii u mɔŋ ki fiit wannib.

ref: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
text: Ŋaan ki u yet Israel teeb po a u tant u nuu yennu ni nan nyiɔk a wun gaarib, ŋaan ki bi tee tubkangbata ki ki saak u mɔbi.

ref: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
text: N loon ki n boi ki laan i dukii a Yennu nyik u mɔŋ niib Israel teeb amii? Aaii, u ki nyikibi kaa. N mɔŋ tee Israel nirɔe, ki tee Abraham yaaboonn, ki nyii Benjamin naakuuk ni.

ref: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
text: Yennu ki ŋaan u niib nba ki u dɔŋ gannib na. I mi la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni ki want Elaija nba din beer Yennu, Israel teeb biit po biaŋinba a

ref: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
text: “N Yomdaanɔ, bi kpii a sɔkiniinba kura, ki biir a binbinta na, a ki n kuukɔɔe biar, ki bi kor a bin kpin.”

ref: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
text: Tɔn, li want nan Israel teeb ki la ba loon linba. Bi waaminnae ki Yennu gannib ki bi lar; ŋaan binba biar na teen tubkangbata damme.

ref: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
text: Li tee nnee mɔtana. Niib waaminnae daa be ki Yennu gannib u ninbatinu paak.

ref: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Yennu-i gann niib, u ninbatinu paake ki u gannib, ki li ki tee bi tumu paak kaa. Yennu-i bo-i gann niib bi tumu paakie, u ninbatinu na bo kii tee barmɔnii ninbatinu kaa.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
text: Li tee kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Bina nba gar ki tan tuu dinna, Yennu te ki bi yan lin, ki bi bia ki laati, ki bi tuba ki gbia.”

ref: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
text: Ki li ji sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a David yet a “Yennu-n te ki linba ki nibooru na jiantir na, lii tee tanbare ki soorib, ki bin di fara;

ref: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
text: ki te ki bi ninbina-n jɔɔn, ki bi daa laati, ki bi sii bɔɔ ki bi sai ni kan naati.”

ref: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
text: N loon ki n boi ki laan i dukin nan Israel teeb din gbiat ki baa a bin biir amii? Aaii, li ki tee nna kaa. Ba tun toonbiit na paak, ki Yennu tinn binba ki tee Juu teeb na. Yennu tun nna a ŋamm Juu teeb na-ii mɔk funfunn binba ki tee Juu teeb na paak.

ref: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
text: N piak ki teen yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb nee: Yennu nba te ki n tee Masia toomii i boor ni na, n sii mɔk yuugontir n toonn maŋ paak.

ref: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
text: Mi-i bo-i sa fit te ki n mɔŋ booru niib Juu teeb-ii mɔk funfunn binba ki tee Juu teeb na paak, li pasiar ŋanne sa te ki Juu teeb na siab-n tinn.

ref: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
text: Yennu nba din yêt Juu teeb na, ki u ji te ki tingbouŋ na teeb soorɔ yɔɔs; Yennu-i ŋmant ki gaarib ki bi tee u niib, li ji sii tee nlee? Li sii tee manfoore ki tur kpeemm.

ref: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
text: Li-i tee ki sinsinn boroboro na tee Yennu yarie, linba biar na mun bia tee u yare. Li-i tee ki tiik jiina tee Yennu yarie, li yiinii na mun bia tee u yare.

ref: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
text: Juu teeb na tee kaman nan ŋaak ni tiik nba ŋan nee, ki Yennu kɔnn tiik na yiinsia, ki jii muuk ni tiik yiinii na ki tukin nan ŋaak ni yar na, ki li taan yomm. Yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na tee kaman nan muuk ni tiik yiinu nee, ki gaan Yennu barŋanii nba nyii Juu teeb boor ni na, ki tee kaman nan muuk ni tiik yiinu nba gaan bonŋann nba nyii ŋaak ni tiik ni na.

ref: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
text: Li paak, a daa mɔk parbifaant nan yiinii nba ki Yennu kɔnnib ki lu ki bi tee kaman nan ŋaak ni tiik yiinii na. Daa mɔk parbifaant, kimaan a tee yiinu kuukɔɔe; nyakir nee teena sommir, fin kaa teen nyakir na sommiri.

ref: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
text: Ŋaan a sa jiin ki yet a “Mɔniie, a Yennu kɔnn yiinii na ki tur min nba tee muuk ni yiinu na yiare.”

ref: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
text: Li set tee mɔniie; ba ki teen yada na paake ki u kɔnnib, ki fin nba teen yada na paake ki u jiin tukina li paak. Ŋannimi a ji daa mɔk parbifaant dudukit li paaki, ŋaan kii mɔk jaŋmaanii.

ref: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
text: Gotir Yennu nba mɔk ninbatinu ŋaan bia mɔk ninkoonii biaŋinba. U mɔk ninkoonii ki tur binba ki teen yada, ŋaan u tiina ninbaauk; fi-i kpaŋŋi teen yada, u mun sii tiina ninbaauk, ŋaan li-i kii tee nna, u mun bia sa kɔnnae ki lua.

ref: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
text: Juu teeb-i lebit bi dudukit ki ŋmat teen Yisa yada, Yennu sa te ki bin tukin nan ŋaak ni tiik na, kimaan u sa fit ki tun nna.

ref: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
text: Yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na tee kaman nan muuk ni tiik yiinu nee, ki Yennu kɔnnir ki tukinir nan ŋaak ni tiik na. Li ki paar ki wun jii yiinii nba ki u kɔnn ŋaak ni tiik na ni, ki bia tukin nan li mɔŋ tiik na biaki. Yennu nba tiin sɔɔ kur ninbaauk biaŋinba

ref: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
text: N naa waas, n loon ki yin bann woonin ni bonsiar, a i daa dukii nan i tee yan damme; ki li tee a Juu tesiab yura paar, ki li sii be nnee ki tan baar yoo nba ki binba ki tee Juu teeb kur nba ki Yennu gannib na, bin tan teen Yisa yab.

ref: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
text: Nnee ki Juu teeb kur sa la tinnu. Li tee nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a Yennu yet a “Tinntɔɔ sa nyi Sion doo nie, ki baar ki tan nyinn Jakɔb yaaboona biit kur ni.

ref: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
text: Ki ma din senn mɔsonn ki turib na tee nnee; n tan sa nyinnib bi biit ni.”

ref: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
text: Juu teeb nba din ki sak Yisa barŋanii na paake, ki bi teen Yennu datai, ki li te ki yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na la tinnu; Yennu nba din gann Juu teeb booru ki loomm na, bi yejamm paake te ki u loomm;

ref: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
text: kimaan Yennu-i gann niib ki turib piinii, u ji ki lebitir u pari.

ref: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
text: Yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na, i din ki sak Yennu mɔb; ki mɔtana ki u ji tini ninbaauk, kimaan Juu teeb nba ki sakɔ na.

ref: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
text: Mɔtana, Juu teeb ki sak Yennu kaman nan ya din ki sakɔ biaŋinba na; Yennu nba din tini ninbaauk biaŋinba na, u sa tinib mun ninbaauk.

ref: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
text: Yennu loon ki niib kur-n bann nan bi tee tubkangbata dammewa, ki wun fit tinib ninbaauk. Yenpaku labaar

ref: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
text: Yennu mɔkint ki mɔk gbennu; ki u yan nan bannu sumii bonchiann. Ŋmee mi ŋɔɔ Yennu dudukit ki sa fit bann u sɔnii?

ref: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Ŋmee mi Yennu dudukit ki sa kpaanɔɔ?

ref: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
text: Ŋmee mi ki tur Yennu siar ŋaan ji tan loon wun pa ki jiinii?”

ref: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
text: Yennue din nan bonsiar kur, ki dia li kura, ki u paake ki li kur be. Ŋaant ki wun la paku yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
text: Li paak, n naa waas, Yennu ninbatinu paake te ki n barimi ma yi jii i gbanant ki turɔ, ki lii tee mannu nba fo, kii waa ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔ, ki li sa maŋɔ; ŋanne tee Yenjiantu nan barmɔnii.

ref: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
text: I daa te ki i binbeŋ tee kaman nan tingbouŋ na niib binbeŋ na, ŋaan ŋaant ki Yennu-n lebit i dudukit nan barmɔnii, kii ŋamm i binbeŋ fanu; ŋanne ki i sa bann Yennu yanbɔɔt nba ŋan ki bia tee fanu ki gbenn.

ref: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
text: Yennu nba tinin ninbaauk na paake ki n yeen sɔɔ kur a sɔɔ daa dukii u par ni a u tee nijaann ki gar wa tee biaŋinba; ŋaan ŋaant ki sɔɔ kur-ii dukii ki bann yada nba ki Yennu turɔ na bikinu.

ref: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
text: Tɔn, nirɔ gbanant bɔkite, ki mɔk bont kur nan li toonn, ŋaan taan ki tee gbananyennkɔɔ.

ref: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
text: Li mun bia tee nnee nan ta yab bonchiann, ŋaan timm nan Masia lakin tee gbananyennkɔɔ, ki ti kura na gbanant taan ki tukin ki ji teen gbananyenn.

ref: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
text: Li paak, sɔɔ kur-n jii u piinii nba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ turɔ na ki tun biaŋinba ki Yennu turɔ yaak na. A piinii na-i tee fii sɔkint Yennu maan naie, li ŋan fan tun a yada biaŋ;

ref: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
text: ki a piinii na-i tee a somm leeb, li ŋan fan somm a leeb; li-i tee a teen wanntɔɔ, li ŋan fan wann leeb;

ref: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
text: li-i tee ki a kpaan a leeb kpaanii, fan tun nna; fin nba mun pi piinii, i tuun nna kaman nan burchimɔ na; wunba mun mɔk yaak niib paak, li ŋan wun tun yaak toonn maŋ bonŋann; ki wunba mun mɔk ninbatinu nan niib, li ŋan wuu mɔk ninbatinu nan parpeenn.

ref: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
text: Ŋaant ki loomm-ii tee barmɔnii loomm, ŋaan kii soor linba ŋan na fanu, ŋaan kii nân biit.

ref: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
text: I loon leeb man bonŋann kaman nan nirɔ nan u naa bik nba tuu loon leeb biaŋinba na, ki kot kii baakit leeb;

ref: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
text: kii tee nikakitii, ki daa tiin gbannyakiri, ŋaan kii tuun ti Yomdaanɔ toona nan i para kura,

ref: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
text: ŋaan kii mɔk parpeenn i dindann paak, kii mɔk mintir i fardinu sinsuuk ni, ŋaan kii mei Yennu yoo kur;

ref: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
text: ŋaan paat linba ki i mɔk na ki tur Yennu niib nba ki mɔki, ŋaan kii gaan saamm kii chantirib i ŋei ni.

ref: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
text: Binba mukisi, yin jiimm a “Yennu mɔsonŋann-ii be i paak.” Mɔmɔmm, yii jiimm nna, i daa teemm mɔpor gbaa.

ref: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
text: Wunba mɔk parpeenn, yii mun-ii mɔk parpeenn mun; ki wunba mun mɔk parbiir, yii mun-ii mɔk parbiir mun.

ref: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
text: Yii mɔk man dudukyennkɔɔ ki daa mɔk dontinmɔŋ; ŋaan kii saak toona nba ki mɔk dontinmɔŋi, ki daa dukii nan i mɔk ninyentiri.

ref: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
text: I daa tuun biit ki jiint biit paaki; kotir i mɔŋ kii tuun linba ki niib kur sa la ki li ŋan.

ref: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
text: Yii yabir i mɔŋ man kii mɔk parmaasir nan niib kur.

ref: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
text: N naa walonkai, i kpan daa tuun biit ki jiint biit paaki, ŋaan ŋaant ki Yennu-n pa pann maŋ ki dat bi tuba ki jiin. Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a ti Yomdaanɔ yet a “Min kuukɔɔe mɔk yaak nba ki sa dat niib tuba; mine sa jiin ki tun biit maŋ.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
text: Yii tee kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “A dataak-i mɔ kon, fan dinnɔ, nyunyukiru-i soorɔ, fan turɔ nyun wun nyu; a ki fi-i tee nna, a dintɔ feie.”

ref: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
text: Daa ŋaant ki biit-n nyannani, ŋaan jii toonŋana ki nyann biit.

ref: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
text: Nirɔ kur-ii saak yent damm mɔi; kimaan sɔɔ ki mɔk u mɔŋ yikoo ki li ki nyii Yennu boori. Yennue te ki binba mɔk yikoo na mɔk yikoo.

ref: People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished.
text: Wunba ki saak yent damm sennu na, li want nan u bia ki saak Yennu mɔbi; ki wunba kur tun nna, u baat nan tubdatue u mɔŋ paak.

ref: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
text: Binba tuun toonbiit nee tiin yent damm jaŋmaanii, ŋaan binba ki tuun toonbiiti, bi ki tiin yent damm jaŋmaanii. I-i kii loon yii tiin yent daanɔ, li ŋan yii tuun linba ŋan, ki wun paki;

ref: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
text: u tee Yennu toontunnɔe ki tuun linba ŋan ki teeni; ŋaan fi-i tuun toonbiit, fii tiinɔ, kimaan u mɔk yaak nba ki sa dat a tubir. U tee Yennu toontunnɔe ki see Yennu paak ki daar binba tuun toonbiit na tuba.

ref: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
text: Li paak, li kpaa talas ki a gaar yent damm mɔi, ki li ki tee a mantik tiin tubdatu na kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan a mi nan li tee linba ŋane.

ref: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
text: Li paake te ki ti pa lampo, kimaan Yennue senn yent damm na a yoo kur bin jiir ki dia tingbouŋ na bonŋann.

ref: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
text: Daa mɔk sɔɔ panni. Ya sii mɔk pann nba tee, yii loon man i leeb. Wunba loon u lɔɔ, li daanɔ gbeen Yennu sennu na kura.

ref: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Yennu sennu na yet a “Daa chommi; daa kpi a lɔɔ; daa jaami; daa mɔk niauŋ a lɔɔ bont paaki;” ki senn-yena biar ki pukin linba na paak ki li kur taan ki yet a “I loon a lɔɔ kaman nan fa loon a mɔŋ biaŋinba na.”

ref: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
text: Ki fi-i loon a lɔɔ, a kan tun bonbiir ki turɔ; kimaan niib loommu tee Yennu sennu gbeenu.

ref: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
text: Yii tee nna man kimaan ya mi yoo na nba tee biaŋinba na. Yoo na baara ki i sa fiir i gɔɔn ni. Ti tinnu daar pera, ki ki fɔk kaman nan la din fɔk biaŋinba yoo nba ki ti din piin ki teen Yisa Masia yada na.

ref: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
text: Nyiɔk na bɔnn ki gaare, ki li ji loon lin yent. Li paak, ŋaant man ki tin nyik bunbɔnn ni toona nba tee toonbiit na, ŋaan jii tɔbtiat nba ki Yennu teent na.

ref: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
text: Ŋaant ki ti toona-ii yeen kaman nan niib nba tuu be yentu ni na, ki daa nyu daan ki yibi, ki daa tuun yuukpeensimi. Ti daa baa joona, ki daa tuun toonbiit booru booru, ki daa kɔn kunkɔna, ki daa mɔk funfuna nan leebi.

ref: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
text: Yii tee man kaman nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nba tee biaŋinba na, ŋaan nyik i gbanant loonbiit, ki daa dukii li po jiki.

ref: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
text: Gaat man yadakanpɔɔna damm na, ŋaan daa nɔi namm mɔniɔk bi yada ni.

ref: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
text: Sɔɔ yadae te ki u di jeet kur, ki wunba yada ki paar na mun kɔɔ nant ŋmamu.

ref: But you should not criticize others for eating or for not eating. After all, God welcomes everyone.
text: Ki wunba di jeet kura na daa gorii wunba kɔɔ jesiat na yanni, ki wunba mun kɔɔ jesiat na daa biir wunba di jeet kur na sanni, kimaan Yennu gaarɔwa.

ref: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
text: Ki a tee ŋmee ki sa biir sɔɔ toontunnɔ? Li tee u mɔŋ Yudaanɔ nee sa yet wa dii nan wa baa, ki u set sa diwa kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ mɔk paŋ nba sa te ki wun di.

ref: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
text: Tɔn, nisiab mun dukii a dayena ŋan ki gar dalia, ki siab mun jii daa kur ki li jame. Sɔɔ kur-n bann u mɔŋ dudukit ni wa teen linba yada.

ref: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
