text: Wunba dukii a dayena ŋan ki gar dalia na, u tee nna ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ baakire; ki wunba mun di jeet kur na, u tee nnee ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ baakir mun, kimaan u teen Yennu niipoouk jeet na paak; ki wunba mun kɔɔ jesiat na mun tee nnee ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ baakir, ki mun bia teen Yennu niipoouk.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
text: Sɔɔ kaa ki be ki fo u yur paak, amii ki kpenn u mɔŋ loomm kuumi.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
text: Ti-i fo, ti Yomdaanɔ paake te ki ti fo; ki ti-i kpo, u loomm paake te ki ti kpo. Li paak, ta fo amii ta kpo, ti tee ti Yomdaanɔ yabe.

ref: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
text: Masia din kpo ki bia fiir kuun ni a wun teen footib nan kpeemm Yomdaanɔ.

ref: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
text: Ki fin, bee teen ki a biir a naa biki? Bee teen ki a yisin a naa biki? Ti kur tan sa set Yennu tɔɔnn ki wun bu ti buut.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “I swear by my very life that everyone will kneel down and praise my name!”
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu yet a “Ma set be na, booru kur sa gbaan n tɔɔnn, ki sɔɔ kur sa yet a n set tee Yennu.”

ref: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
text: Li paak, sɔɔ kur tan sa fiit u mɔŋ ki tur Yennu. Daa te ki a naa bik-n baa

ref: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
text: Li paak, ti daa biir leeb sana, ŋaan kii mi i mɔŋ par ni nan i kan te ki i naa bik-n tun toonbiit ki baa.

ref: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
text: Min nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa nba taan leeb ki ji tee niyenɔ na, ŋanne te ki n bann nan jeet kaa ki kɔɔ. Ŋaan sɔɔ-i dukin nan jesiat kɔɔ, ŋann ŋanin li kɔɔ u boor.

ref: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
text: Fi-i te ki a naa bik par biir jeet nba ki a di na paak, ŋann ŋanin a ji ki tuun loomm toonn kaa. Nirɔ nba ki Masia din gaar u paak ki kpo na, a daa te ki wun nyik Yennu sɔnu a jeetdinu paaki.

ref: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
text: Linba ki a dukii a li ŋan, a daa te ki niib-n pak biit li paaki.

ref: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
text: Kimaan Yennu naan labaar ki tee jeetdinu amii nyunu labaar kaa, ŋaan li tee popeenn nan parmaasir nan parpeenn yar nba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ turite.

ref: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
text: Wunba waa Masia nna, Yennu sii mɔk parpeenn nan li daanɔ, ki niib bia sii teeu nibaakir.

ref: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
text: Li paak, ŋaant man ki tin yabir ki tun linba baat nan parmaasir, nan linba sa somm ti leeb.

ref: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
text: I mi ki a biir Yennu toona, jeet paak. Jeet kur ŋane, ŋaan li ki ŋan ki a di jeet nba sa te ki a naa bik-n baa.

ref: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
text: Linba ŋan tee ki a nyik linba sa te ki a naa bik-n baa, li-i lekii tee nant ŋmamu amii daan nyunu.

ref: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
text: Fa teen yada ki jikit saa jeetdinu po biaŋinba na, ŋaant ki lii be fin nan Yennu sinsuuk ni. Wunba tun linba ki u dukin nan li ŋan, ki ki mɔk birsuk li paaki, parpeenn tee u yare.

ref: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
text: Ŋaan wunba mɔk birsuk linba ki u di na paak, ŋaan dii, u mɔk biit, kimaan u ki dir nan yada. Nirɔ-i kii teen yada nan toonsiar ŋani, ŋaan tan tuun toonn maŋ, li tee biite.

ref: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
text: Timm nba ki ti yada chee na, li ŋan ki tii mɔk sukuru ki somm ti leeb nba yada daa ki chee na, ki daa tuun ti mɔŋ loomm kuukɔɔ kaa.

ref: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
text: Ŋaant man ki ti kura baka-ii be ti naa waas paak ki sommib ki bin wei Yisa Masia sɔnu fanu.

ref: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
text: Ŋɔɔ Masia din ki loon wun tun u mɔŋ loommi. Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Binba sukii Yennu na, bi sukii mine.”

ref: And the Scriptures were written to teach and encourage us by giving us hope.
text: Linba kur sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni na, li sɔb ki turit a tii tumiie. U gbouŋ maŋ teent sukuru, ki bia kɔɔntit para, a tii mɔk dindann.

ref: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
text: Yennu nba teent sukuru ki sommit na, wun turi dudukyennkɔɔ kaman nan Yisa Masia nba loon biaŋinba;

ref: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
text: ki i kura sii mɔk dudukyennkɔɔ ki dont Yennu wunba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Baa na. Binba ki tee Juu teeb na Yenjiantu labaar

ref: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
text: Yii gaan leeb man kaman nan Masia nba gaari na, ki Yennu sann-n la paku.

ref: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
text: Ma yeen linba na paak tee a Masia din kpint u mɔŋ ki tee Juu teeb na toontunnɔe, ki wann nan Yennu set tee mɔmɔmm daanɔe, ki gbeen Yennu mɔsona nba ki u din senn ki tur bi yejamm nba din tee nijaana na,

ref: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
text: ki te ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na-ii dont Yennu sann u ninbatinu paak. Li sɔb nnee Yennu gbouŋ ni a David yet a “N sa tura niipoouk binba ki tee Juu teeb na boor, ki yin yanii ki paka.”

ref: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
text: Li bia sɔb ki yet a “Yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na, yii mɔk man parpeenn, yimm nan Yennu niib.”

ref: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
text: Li bia sɔb a “Yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na, pakin man ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu; nibooru kura-n pakɔ bonŋann.”

ref: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
text: Aisaya mun din sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Jese yaaboonn tan sa baar ki Yennu sa chabɔ yaak, ki u tan sii tee binba ki tee Juu teeb na yudaanɔ, ki bi tan sii teeu yada.”

ref: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
text: Yennu nba turit dindann na, ya teenɔ yada na paak, ki u sa turi parmaasir nan parpeenn bonchiann, ki ŋɔɔ Yennu Seyeeŋ paŋ sa te ki i dindann na-n ŋamm yabit. Linba paak te ki Pɔɔl sɔbir nan parcheenn

ref: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
text: N naa waas, n mi n par ni nan i binbeŋ ŋan, ki i mi Yennu maan bonŋann ki fit ki kpaan i leeb;

ref: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
text: ŋaan ma sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teeni na, n mɔk parcheenn ki yaa n tiari baryena, kimaan Yennu mɔk ninbatinu n paak

ref: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
text: ki te ki n tee Yisa Masia toontunnɔ, a n mɔɔnt u barŋanii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teebi, a ki bi sii tee piinii ki teenɔ, ki u Seyeeŋ na sa te ki bin teen nifoŋamm ki penn u par.

ref: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
text: Yisa Masia paake ki n mɔk yuugontir Yennu toonn ni;

ref: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ paŋ nee te ki n tuun bakitnauŋ toona na, ki ŋanne te ki bi bann paŋ nba ki u mɔk. Ki n nyii Jerusalem ki bia lin doi ni ki tan baar Ilirikum tiŋ ni ki mɔɔntir Masia barŋanii kura.

ref: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
text: Ŋanne ki n lin nan ninmɔnn ki mɔɔntir Masia barŋanii na ki teen niib nba ki mi gbati; n ki loon mii lin kii lei siaminba ki niib poŋ dɔŋ mɔɔnt leŋi,

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
text: kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Niib nba ki bi daa ki mɔɔnt u maan ki turib na, bi sa gbat ki bia bann li paak, kii mi u po.” Pɔɔl nba din lor a wun saan gɔn Rom teeb biaŋinba

ref: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
text: Li paak, bona bonchiann tuu gɔɔr n sɔnu ki n ki fit baat ki gɔntiini.

ref: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
text: Ŋaan mɔtanae ki n ji daa gbenn n toona nba be yaka na ni, ki ma poŋ sat ki tuu loon bina bonchiann ki n gɔni na,

ref: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
text: mɔtana n sa gɔniiwa; ki n gɔnu maŋ poorpo waaminna, n sii loon ki yin sommin ki n saan Speenn tiŋ ni.

ref: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
text: kimaan Yisaweira nba be Masedonia nan Akaya tinii ni na, ki bi dukin dudukit ki yent bi para a bin lakin likirii tur Yennu niib nba tee tatimm ki be Jerusalem na.

ref: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
text: Li tee mɔniie nan bi mɔŋ loomme ki bi turib piinii na. Li ŋan bin turib, kimaan Yennu barŋanii nba nyii Jerusalem niib boor ki baarib na paak. Ŋanne te ki li ŋan nan bin turib piinii maŋ.

ref: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
text: Mi-i baar Jerusalem ki turib piinii maŋ, n sa nyi leŋ ki tɔɔt gar i boor ki saan Speenn tiŋ niwa.

ref: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
text: N mi nan mi-i baar i boor, Masia sommir sii be ti ni bonchiann.

ref: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
text: Tɔn, n naa waas, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann paak nan Yennu Seyeeŋ loomm paak, n mei-i, yii mei Yennu nan ninmɔnn n paak ki sommin,

ref: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
text: ki wuu guun ki Judea tiŋ ni niib nba ki teen Yisa Masia yada na, ki bi daa kpimi; kii biakii meiɔ nan piinii nba ki n sa tur Yennu niib na, bin gaar nan parpeenn.

ref: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
text: Li-i tee ki i miar Yennu nna ki turin, ki u-i sak, n sa baar nan parpeenn, ki tan foi waan i boor.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
text: Yennu nba teent parmaasir na sii be nani. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
text: Tɔn, n loon ki n wanni ti niipoo Fiibe po; u be Kenkrea doo ni ki sommit Yisaweira leŋ; li-i tee ki u baar i boor,

ref: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
text: yin gaarɔ ti Yomdaanɔ Masia sann paak kaman nan Yennu niib nba bo sii gaan bi leeb biaŋinba na, ki sommɔ wa loon linba i boor, kimaan u mun tuu sommit niib bonchiann, nan n mɔŋ gbaa.

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
text: N foont Akuila nan u ŋaapoo Prisila binba tee n toontunleeb Yisa Masia toona ni na.

ref: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
text: Bi din jii bi manfoa ki tinn n manfoor, li paak ki n teemm niipoouk; li ki tee n kuukɔɔ kaa teemm niipoouku, ŋaan min nan binba ki tee Juu teeb ŋaan tee Yisaweira na, bi mun bia teemm niipoouk bonchiann.

ref: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
text: Ŋaan bia foont Yisaweira nba lakii ki jiantir Yennu bi ŋaak ni na. Ki foont n yɔɔŋaŋ Epanetus wunba din tee sinsinn nirɔ nba be Asia tiŋ ni ki din teen Masiaweirɔ na.

ref: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
text: Ki foont Mari wunba din tuu tuun bonchiann ki teeni na;

ref: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
text: ki foont Andronikus nan Junias binba tee n booru ni niib, ki min nan ŋamm din lakin kɔɔ dansarik; ki Masia toomiinba mib bonŋann na, ki bi din sint teen Masiaweira ki n tan wei na.

ref: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
text: Ki foont n yɔɔŋaŋ Ampliatus wunba ki n loonɔ ti Yomdaanɔ paak na.

ref: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
text: Ki foont Urbanus wunba tee ti toontunlɔɔ Masia toona ni na; ki foont n yɔɔŋaŋ Stakis mun biak.

ref: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
text: Ki bia foont Apeles wunba din la bikinu ŋaan lek bia daa waa Masia nan barmɔnii na; ki foont Aristobulus ŋaateeb;

ref: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
text: ki foont n booru ni nirɔ nba ki bi yiu Herodion na; ki foont Nasisus ŋaateeb nba teen ti Yomdaanɔ yada na.

ref: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
text: Ki foont Traifena nan Traifosa binba tuun ti Yomdaanɔ toona na; ki foont n yɔɔŋaŋ Pesis wunba tuun ti Yomdaanɔ toona bonchiann na.

ref: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
text: Ki foont Rufus wunba mantik waa ti Yomdaanɔ sɔnu bonŋann na; ki foont u naa wunba tuu loonin kaman nan wa loon u mɔŋ bik biaŋinba na.

ref: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
text: Ki foont Asinkritus nan Flegon nan Hemes nan Patrobas nan Hemas nan Masia niib kur nba be namm na.

ref: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
text: Ki foont Filologus nan Julia nan Nereus nan u niipoo, nan Olimpas nan Yennu niib kur nba be namm na.

ref: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
text: Yii foont leeb kii kpammii man; Masiaweira nba be siar kur po foonti. Kpaanii

ref: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
text: N naa waas, n kpaaniie, i mi man binba bɔkitir Yisa niib ŋaan dɔkib u sɔnu ni na ŋaan bia ki saak wannu nba ki i gaar na; lɔkitirib man;

ref: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
text: li nibooru ki waa ti Yomdaanɔ Masia, ŋaan waa bi mɔŋ niauŋo. Bi jikit mɔmante ki kpann binba yaa dudukit ki chee na.

ref: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
text: Tɔn, sɔɔ kur gbat ya gaar Masia barŋanii biaŋinba, linba na paake ki n mɔk parpeenn nani. N loon ki yii mɔk yan ki saan jiin linba ŋan paak, ŋaan daa saak bonbiiri.

ref: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Li kan yukir ki Yennu nba teent parmaasir na sa te ki yin fiir ŋmaa Sintaanii yur paak. Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi. Foontii biak

ref: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
text: Tɔn, n toontunlɔɔ Timoti foonti; ki n ninjamm Lusius nan Jason nan Sosipata mun bia foonti.

ref: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
text: Min Tesius nba sɔbin linba ki Pɔɔl pak gbouŋ na ni na foonti ti Yomdaanɔ sann paak.

ref: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
text: Li ŋan ki tin baak Yennu, kimaan u fit te ki i set waau fanu kaman nan ma mɔɔnt barŋanii ki jiin Yisa Masia po na, ki fiit woonin ni maan nba din bɔr bina bina na.

ref: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Ŋɔɔ Yennu sɔkiniinba gaar ki sɔbir gbouŋ ni ki li fiit woonin ni; ki Yennu nba kaa gbennu na tur mɔb a booru kur-n bannir, a niib kur-n la yaak ki teen Masia yada ki sak u mɔb;

ref: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
text: ŋɔɔ Yennu kuukɔɔe tee yandaanɔ na; niib kur-n pakɔ mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu, Yisa Masia sann paak. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
text: Min Pɔɔl nba ki Yisa Masia yiinin a n teen u toomii Yennu loomm ni, min nan ti naa bik Sosenes sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen Yennu mɔtaauk niib nba be Korint doo ni.

ref: To God's church in Corinth. Christ Jesus chose you to be his very own people, and you worship in his name, as we and all others do who call him Lord.
text: Yisa Masia paake ki i tee Yennu niib, ki u yiini kii teen u niib nan binba kur yi ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann siaminba kur; ŋɔɔe tee bi Yomdaanɔ ki mun bia tee ti Yomdaanɔ.

ref: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi ki bia turi parmaasir. Pɔɔl nba teen Yennu niipoouk biaŋinba

ref: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
text: N teen Yennu niipoouk yoo kur i paak nan wa turi sommir Yisa Masia paak na,

ref: who helps you speak and understand so well.
text: kimaan i la bonsiar kur i pinpakit ni nan i minu ni u boore.

ref: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
text: Masia labaar nba ki ti din wanni na, i gaarir fanuwa.

ref: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
text: Li paak, i ki pɔt Yennu Seyeeŋ piinii na yenn gbaa nan ya guu daar nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia tan sa jen na.

ref: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
text: U sii guui nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia jenu daar maŋ ki i tan daa mɔk biiti.

ref: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
text: Yennu-i yaa u sa tun linba, u tuun ŋanne. U yiinie a yimm nan u bik ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-ii mɔk mɔtaauk nan leeb. Masia niib bɔkitu labaar

ref: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
text: N naa waas, n barimiie ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann ni a yin taan mɔb, ki mɔniɔk daa be i sinsuuk ni, ki yii taa mɔyemmkɔɔ nan dudukyennkɔɔ.

ref: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
text: N naa waas, Kloe ŋaatesiab betin a i mɔk mɔniɔk nan leeb i sinsuuk ni.

ref: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
text: Masia maŋ bɔkit bɔkitee? Bi din kpaa min Pɔɔl dapunpunn paak i paakii? amii bi wuri Yennu nyunwuru min Pɔɔl sann paakii?

ref: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
text: N teen Yennu niipoouk nan ma ki wur i sɔɔ Yennu nyunwuru na, see Krispus nan Gaius.

ref: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
text: Li paak, sɔɔ kan fit yet a bi wurɔ Yennu nyunwuru n sann ni.

ref: I did baptize the family of Stephanas, but I don't remember if I baptized anyone else.
text: N bia wur Stefanas ŋaateeb Yennu nyunwurue, ŋaan n ji ki tian ma wur wunba ki pukini.

ref: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
text: Kimaan Masia ki tumin a n tan wur niib; u tumin a n tan mɔɔnt u barŋanii nee ki tur niib. Li ki tee a n jikit nisaarik mir kaa ki beeribi; mi-i bo-i kii tuun nna, li bo sa te ki Masia nba kpo dapunpunn paak na, u kuun maŋ-n teen yanne. Masia kuun nba wantit Yennu yan po biaŋinba

ref: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
text: Nisiab nba bot Yennu sɔnu ni na jii Masia kuun dapunpunn paak labaar na ki li tee yanne, ŋaan timm nba tinn na mi nan li tee Yennu paŋe.

ref: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a u yet a “N sa biir yandamm minu na ki te ki bannteeb bannu na-n biar yann.”

ref: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
text: Li paak, tingbouŋ na yandamm nan mirteeb nan mɔnɔdamm na be lee? Yennu te ki tingbouŋ na yandamm teen yanne.

ref: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
text: Yennu nba mɔk yan na te ki tingbouŋ na teeb yan kan fit te ki niib-n bannɔ. Li paak, ki u te ki binba teen Yisa yada na tinn; jatii labaar nba ki ti mɔɔntir ki teen niib na paak ki bi tinn.

ref: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
text: Juu teeb loon bin la bakitnauŋ toonaewa ki fit lian teen yada; ki Griik teeb mun loon yan;

ref: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
text: timm ŋanin mɔɔntir ki wantib biaŋinba ki Masia kpo dapunpunn paak nee, ki maan maŋ dont Juu teeb wutoa, ki bia tee jatmaan ki teen binba ki tee Juu teebi.

ref: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
text: Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb ki Yennu gannib na ŋanin mi nan Masia kuun labaar na tee Yennu paŋ nan u yame.

ref: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
text: Linba ki Yennu tuun ki nisaarik jiir ki li tee jatii na, li mɔk yan ki gar nisaarik yan; ki linba ki Yennu teen ki nisaarik dukii a li ki mɔk paŋ na, ŋaan li mɔk paŋ ki gar nisaarik yar.

ref: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
text: N naa waas, dukint man ya din tee biaŋinba yoo nba ki i din sak Yisa barŋanii na. Nisaarik minu ni, i waaminne mɔk yan amii paŋ amii ki tee nijaana.

ref: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
text: Ŋaan Yennue gann binba tee jatit tingbouŋ na teeb boor ni, a wun te ki yandamm-n di fei; ki bia gann tatimm tingbouŋ na teeb boor ni, a wun te ki paŋ damm-n di fei.

ref: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
text: Yennue gann tarii nan binba ki mɔk nyɔɔt, nan binba ki tee siar, a wun teen linba ki niib dukii a li mɔk nyɔɔt na yann.

ref: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
text: U teen nnee a sɔɔ daa nyu bara u boor.

ref: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
text: Ŋɔɔ Yennue taan timm nan Yisa Masia ki u tee ti yan. U paake ki Yennu nyinnit daabsin ni, ki yet a ti tee niŋamm u boor, ki ji tee u niib.

ref: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
text: Li paak, yii tuun linba sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba nyu bara, wun nyu bara maŋ ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu paak.”

ref: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
text: N naa waas, ma din baar i boor ni na, n din ki mɔɔntir Yennu labaar na ki teeni nan mɔmant amii nifosaarik minu ni kaa.

ref: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
text: Ŋaan ma din be nani na, n din ki mɔk duduksiat ki pukin Yisa Masia po, nan u dapunpunn paak kuun na po.

ref: At first, I was weak and trembling with fear.
text: Ma baar i boor ni yoonba, n baar nan chɔkinte, ki jaŋmaanii nan jijekit soorin.

ref: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
text: N ki pak labaar maŋ nan minu nba sa maŋ niib kaawa ki bin sak li, ŋaan n pake ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na wann u paŋ,

ref: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
text: a ya teen Yisa Masia yada na, li ki tee nisaarik yan paak kaa, ŋaan li tee Yennu paŋ paake. Yennu yan po

ref: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
text: Mɔmɔmm, ti piak nan yame ki teen binba ki bi para chee nan Yisaweiu ni na. Li ki tee tingbouŋ na niib yan amii tingbouŋ na paŋ damm nba ki bi panii sa teen yann na.

ref: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
text: Yan nba ki ti piak na tee Yennu woonin ni yan nba woon ki nirɔ ki mi, linba ki Yennu poŋ dɔŋ gann a wun donnit, ki sɔɔ ki dunia daa din ki nan na.

ref: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
text: Tingbouŋ na ŋaayudamm sɔɔ kaa ki mi yan na. Bi-i bo-i mir, bi din bo kan kpaa ti Yomdaanɔ wunba yen paku kura na dapunpunn paaki.

ref: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Linba ki nirɔ ki la, ki bia ki gbati, ki bia ki dukin a li sa fit teen na, ŋanne ki Yennu teen siir ki guun binba loonɔ na.”

ref: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na te ki ti bannir, kimaan Seyeeŋ na mi bont kur nan Yennu woonin ni maan gbaa.

ref: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
text: Nifosaarik seek nba be u ni na kuukɔɔe mi nisaarik dudukit. Nnee mun, sɔɔ ki mi Yennu dudukit see Yennu Seyeeŋ na kuukɔɔ.

ref: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
text: Seek nba ki ti gaar na, u ki tee tingbouŋ na seek kaa, ŋaan u tee Yennu Seyeeŋ nba sa te ki tin bann piinii nba ki u turite.

ref: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
text: Li paak, ki ti ki piak maan nba tee nisaarik wannu yami; ŋaan ti piak linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ wantit, ki bia want Yennu barmɔnii na ki teen niib nba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ be bi ni na;

ref: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
text: ŋaan wunba ki mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋ maŋ, li yɔɔ ki gaan Yennu Seyeeŋ na maami, kimaan li sii tee jatiie ki turɔ, ki u kan bann li paaki, kimaan li bannu nyi Seyeeŋ na boor nie.

ref: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
text: Ki wunba mɔk Seyeeŋ na bant bona kur paake, ŋaan nirɔ kan fit ki bann u binbeŋ ni,

ref: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
text: kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Ŋmee sa fit bann Yennu dudukit ki ŋmakitɔɔ?” Ŋaan timm ŋanin mɔk Masia dudukit.

ref: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
text: N naa waas, li tee mɔniie, n din ki fit pak ki turi kaman nan ma din bo sa pak ki tur niib nba mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋ na; n din pak ki turi kaman nan ma bo sa pak ki tur tingbouŋ na teeb nee, ki i binbeŋ bia tee kaman nan waas binbeŋ nee Masia sɔnu ni.

ref: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
text: N mɔɔnti biime, ki li ki tee jekpabaa kaa, kimaan i ki jaŋ nanni. Mɔtana gbaa i bia lek daa ki jaŋ nanni,

ref: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
text: kimaan i binbeŋ bia daa be kaman nan tingbouŋ na niib binbeŋ nee; kimaan funfunn nan mɔniɔk-i be i sinsuuk ni, i ki waa tingbouŋ na teeb binbeŋ kaa-aa?

ref: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
text: Ki sɔɔ-i yet a u tee Pɔɔl yɔɔe, ki lɔɔ mun yet a u tee Apolos yɔɔe, ŋann i ki tuun kaman nan tingbouŋ na teeb binbeŋ na kaa-aa?

ref: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
text: Ŋmee tee Pɔɔli, ki ŋmee mun tee Apolosi? Ti kpan tee Yennu toontunna nba din te ki i sak Yisa mɔb nee. Ti kur na tuun toona nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ turit a tii tuume.

ref: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
text: Min din bur bonbooriie ki Apolos mun maant nyun, ŋaan Yennue te ki li pia ki kpaat.

ref: What matters isn't those who planted or watered, but God who made the plants grow.
text: Wunba bur nan wunba maant nyun ŋanin tee yanne; Yennu ki tee yanni, kimaan ŋɔɔe te ki li kpaat.

ref: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
text: Bɔkitu kaa wunba buur nan wunba maant nyun na sinsuuk ni. Yennue sa turib nyɔɔt ba tun linba na paak.

ref: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
text: Ti lakin tuun ki teen Yennue. I tee kaman nan Yennu kpaab nee, ki bia tee kaman nan Yennu diiuk na.

ref: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
text: Yennu nba sommin biaŋinba na paak, n tee kaman nan tanmaaŋanɔ nee, ki piin fiakir, ki sɔɔ mun maa ki tɔɔnt li paak. Ŋaan wunba kur maa ki tɔɔnt li paak, wun soor u mɔŋ.

ref: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
text: Masia baaru daar maŋ nie, ki sɔɔ kur toona nba tee biaŋinba tan sa nyi paanu. Muue tan sa fiit wannir, ki bikin sɔɔ kur toona ki wann la tan sii tee biaŋinba.

ref: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
text: Li-i kii dii linba ki nirɔ maa tɔɔn fiakir maŋ paak na, ŋann ŋanin u sa la nyɔɔta.

ref: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
text: Ŋaan wunba yar dii muu, u sa kɔŋ li nyɔɔta, ŋaan u mɔŋ ŋanin sa tinna ki li sii tee nan u chiare ki nyii muu ni na.

ref: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
text: I tee Yennu diiuko, ki Yennu Seyeeŋ be i ni; i ki mi nna-aa?

ref: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
text: Li paak, wunba biir Yennu diiuk, Yennu mun sa biir li yɔɔwa. Kimaan Yennu diiuk tee diyeeŋe; ki yimme tee u diiuk maŋ.

ref: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
text: Sɔɔ daa kpann u mɔŋi. Sɔɔ-i be i sinsuuk ni ki dukii nan u tee yan daanɔe tingbouŋ na binbeŋ ni, li ŋan wun kpant u mɔŋ jatuk ki set teen yan daanɔ.

ref: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
text: Linba ki tingbouŋ na teeb dukin ki li tee yan na, li tee jatiie Yennu boor, nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yennu biir yan damm bannu.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
text: ki bia sɔb leer boor a “Ti Yomdaanɔ mi nan yan damm dudukit tee yanne.”

ref: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
text: min Pɔɔl nan Apolos nan Piita nan tingbouŋ na, nan manfoor nan kuun, nan mɔtana nan yoo nba baat na, yimme yen linba na kura.

ref: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
text: Ki Masia mun yeni, ki Yennu mun yen Masia.

ref: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
text: Ŋann amii, i jiit ki tii tee Masia toontunna nba ki Yennu jiit ki guunit u woonin ni maan na.

ref: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
text: Li paak, linba tee majaann tee nnee, li ŋan ki toontunnɔ-n tuun nan barmɔnii.

ref: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki i bu n buut kaman nan nifosaarii nba tuu bu bi leeb buut na, n baka kaa; n mɔŋ gbaa ki bu n mɔŋ buute.

ref: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
text: N nɔɔk ni yeene, ŋaan li ki want a n ki tuun biiti, ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔe tee wunba bu n buut.

ref: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
text: Li paak, i daa bu sɔɔ buut mɔtana, kii guu yoo nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ tan sa baar na, wunba tan sa jii linba be bunbɔnn ni ki fiit wannir paanu ni, ki tan sa fiit para ni maan ki wann paanu ni; ki li yoo na ki sɔɔ kur tan sa la u nyɔɔt nba tee biaŋinba Yennu boor.

ref: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
text: N naa waas, i paake ki n bikin linba na kur ki tur n mɔŋ nan Apolos, a i tumii ti boor ki wei Yennu gbouŋ nba wann biaŋinba na bonŋann, ki sɔɔ daa dont u mɔŋ ti yenɔkɔɔ paak ki gar u lɔɔ.

ref: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
text: Ŋmee tura saakatuk a leeb ni? Yennu kaa tura linba kur ki a mɔk na-aa? ki bee teen ki a dont a mɔŋ, ki li tee nan linba ki a mɔk maŋ ki tee piinii kaa na?

ref: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
text: I poŋ mɔk linba kur ki i loonii? i poŋ teen gordammaa? ta lek kaa i boor nna, ŋaan i tee kpanbara-aa? N poŋ loon yin set tee kpanbara nan barmɔnii, ki timm nan yimm-n taan teen kpanbara.

ref: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
text: Min yaa boor n dukin nan Yennu gann timm nba tee toomiinba na ki ti tee tariie, ki tee kaman nan niib nba ki bi sa kpib paanu ni, ki teemm yaarlituk niib, ki tingbouŋ na teeb kur nan malakanba goriit.

ref: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
text: Ti tee jatite Masia paak, ŋaan yimm ŋanin tee yandamme Masia sɔnu ni; ti ki mɔk paŋ, ŋaan yimm ŋanin mɔk paŋ; ki niib teeni nibaakir ŋaan yisin timm.

ref: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
text: La nyii sianyoowa mɔkmɔk nan mɔtana, ti lin nan kome nan nyunyukiru, ki lia tachiat, ki bi boot, ki ti lin yirib ki kaa binbeboori.

ref: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
text: Ti niŋinniie ki ti tuun ki di; bi-i sukirit, ti tuu jiimm mɔŋamme; bi-i dintit fara, ti tuu mɔk sukurue;

ref: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
text: bi-i pak biir ti sana, ti tuu pak jiimm nan loomme. Ti kpant tariie, ki tee kaman nan tingbouŋ na tanpenn paak mɔɔt na, ki bia tee kaman nan tingbouŋ na jakint na ki tan tuu mɔtana.

ref: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
text: N ki sɔbin na ki teeni a n dinni fei kaa, ŋaan n kpaaniie ya tee n walonkai na paak.

ref: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki i lek mɔk wannteeb tusa piik Masia ni, i ki mɔk baanba bonchianni; kimaan Yisa Masia nie ki n mari barŋanii nba ki n wanni na paak.

ref: and I want you to be like me.
text: N barimiie, yii waa n binbeŋ man.

ref: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
text: Linba na paake ki n tuun Timoti i boor, wunba tee n bilonkak ki tee barmɔnii daanɔ ti Yomdaanɔ ni; u sa tiari ma be biaŋinba Masia ni, nan ma lin want Masiaweira biaŋinba siaminba kur.

ref: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
text: Siab mɔk dontinmɔŋe, ki dukii a n kan baar ki gɔniini.

ref: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
text: Li-i tee ki ti Yomdaanɔ loon, n sa gɔni mɔtana, ki la dontinmɔŋ damm na, li ki tee bi pinpakit paak kaa, ŋaan n yaa n laan bi set mɔk paŋ amii bi ki mɔki.

ref: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
text: Yennu naan ki tee mɔb ni, li tee paŋe.

ref: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
text: I loon lanne? I loon ki n baar i boor nan ninmɔnn amii n baar soonnee?

ref: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
text: Li set tee mɔniie ki bi yeen a bonchonchonsin be i sinsuuk ni, linba ki binba ki waa Yisa Masia na mɔŋ kan tumi. N gbat a sɔɔ be i sinsuuk ni ki dɔɔ nan u baa poo;

ref: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
text: ŋaan ki i mɔk dontinmɔŋ ki ki tiin fei li paaki. Li ki jaŋ nan i para-n biirii? Li ŋan ki yin nyinn li nibooru i sinsuuk ni.

ref: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
text: jiin nirɔ nba tun toonbiit maŋ ki teenɔ Sintaanii nuu ni ki wun biir u gbanant, ki ti Yomdaanɔ baaru daar maŋ u seek tan sa la tinnu.

ref: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
text: I dontinmɔŋ maŋ ki ŋani. I ki mi a datiŋ waaminnae te ki boroboro yon kur fuutir piirii?

ref: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
text: Li paak, yi nyinn i binbenkper biit, kii tee nipaamm, kaman nan ya set tee niib nba nyik biit ni biaŋinba na, kimaan Masia nba tee ti marouŋ na gaar ti paak ki kpo.

ref: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
text: Li paak, ta waa u sɔnu biaŋinba na, ŋaant ki tin nyik binbenkper na, ki nyinn dudukbiit nan biit kur na, kii ji kii tee u weira nan barmɔnii, ki biit-ii kaa ti ni.

ref: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
text: N poŋ sɔb gbouŋ ki turi ki yet a i daa waa bonchonchona na.

ref: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
text: Li ki tee a n yaa a i nyinn i mɔŋ nan tingbouŋ na bonchonchona, amii nianchiauŋ damm, amii nanyukii, amii patmann teeb kaa; li-i tee nna ŋanimi, see ki i nyii tingbouŋ na ni.

ref: I was talking about your own people who are immoral or greedy or worship idols or curse others or get drunk or cheat. Don't even eat with them!
text: N sɔb ki yeeni a i daa tɔk nan daanɔ nba yaa a ŋɔɔ waa Masiae ŋaan tuun bonchonchonn toona, amii ki mɔk nianchiauŋ, amii ki mann pata, amii ki mɔk sanjaauŋ, amii ki tee danyuyuukpeen, amii ki tee nanyukɔɔ na; a poŋ daa kaar nan li nirɔ ki di jeeti.

ref: God judges everyone else. The Scriptures say, “Chase away any of your own people who are evil.”
text: Li poŋ sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “I ber biitdaanɔ na ki nyinnɔ i sinsuuk ni.”

ref: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
text: I yenɔ-i mɔk maan nan u lɔɔ nba waa Masia, ŋaan ki ki jiir ki saan nann Masiaweira boor ki bi saa ŋammir, ŋaan jiir ki saan nann binba ki waa Masia na buut bunu boor, li sii mɔk fei hei.

ref: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
text: I ki mi a Masiaweira tan sa bu tingbouŋ na teeb buutii? ki li-i tee ki i tan sa bu tingbouŋ na teeb buut, ŋann i kan fit ki bu barbisbis mɔtana?

ref: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
text: I ki mi a ti tan sa bu malakanba buutii? Nlee ki i kan fit bu mɔtana binbeŋ na barii?

ref: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
text: Li-i tee ki i mɔk maan, bee ki i jikitir ki saa nann binba ki tee Masiaweira na boori?

ref: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
text: Na tee feie ki turi. Mɔniɔk-i baar yimm Masia niib sinsuuk ni, sɔɔ kaa i sinsuuk ni ki mɔk yan nba sa fit ŋamm maan maŋii?

ref: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
text: Ŋaan ki i naawayemm saa nan bi leeb barbuurɔ wunba ki teen Masia yada na boor, ki u bu bi buut.

ref: When one of you takes another to court, all of you lose. It would be better to let yourselves be cheated and robbed.
text: Ya lek mɔk barii nan i naa waas na gbaa, ŋaan li tee biite. A naa bik-i tun biira amii ki u ŋmaba, ki a kurint sikin, li ki sɔ-ɔɔ?

ref: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
text: I ki mi nan biitdamm tan kan kɔɔ Yennu naan ni-ii? I daa kpann i mɔŋ; binba tee joonbaarit amii bonchonchona amii patmannteeb amii jab nba mɔk niauŋ ki dɔɔ nan bi jaleeb

ref: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
text: amii nanyukii amii nianchiauŋ damm amii danyuyuukpeena amii sanjanjanna amii fatiteeb, binba na kur nan yenɔkɔɔwa kan kɔɔ Yennu naan ni.

ref: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
text: I siab din tuu tee nnee; ŋaan ki Yennu wuur i biit na ki i ji tee u niib ki bia tee popeendamm u peŋ, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nan Yennu Seyeeŋ na paak. Jiim i gbanant kii tuun linba sii dont Yennu sann

ref: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
text: I siab yeen a “Bont kura ki kɔɔ.” Li tee mɔniie; ŋaan bont kur kaa ŋan ki turani. I yaa a bont kur ki kɔɔ, ki li set tee mɔniie, ŋaan min ŋanin kan sak ki bonsiar-n yentimi.

ref: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
text: I siab yeen a “Jeet tee poor yare, ki poor mun tee jeet yar.” Mɔniie; ŋaan Yennu sa gɔɔr li ŋannlee na kur. Yennu ki nan ti gbanant a ti jiir kii chommi, ŋaan a ti jiir ki lii tee ti Yomdaanɔ yar. Ti Yomdaanɔe yen ti gbanant na.

ref: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
text: Yennue din fiin ti Yomdaanɔ kuun ni, ki mun bia tan sa jii u paŋ ki fiint.

ref: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
text: I ki mi nan i gbanant na gaa nan Masia gbanantee? Li paak, daa jikit gbanant nba gaa nan Masia gbanant na ki gaant nan poochonchonna.

ref: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
text: Wunba jii u mɔŋ ki taan nan poochonchonn, ŋɔɔ nan li daanɔ ŋanin taan gbananyennkɔɔwa. Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Jɔɔ nan u poo sa taan ki tee gbananyennkɔɔe.”

ref: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
text: Wunba taan u mɔŋ nan ti Yomdaanɔ, ti Yomdaanɔ nan li daanɔ taan ki tee seyennkɔɔe.

ref: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
text: Yi nyinn i mɔŋ man bonchonchonsin ni. Toonbiit kur nba biar, li ki tuun gbanant ni; ŋaan wunba chomm ŋanin biir u mɔŋ gbanante.

ref: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
text: I gbanant na tee Seyeeŋ nba ki i gaar Yennu boor na diiuko, ki u be i ni. I ki mi nnee-ee? I ki yen i mɔŋi, Yennue yeni;

ref: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
text: u daaie; li paak, yii jii i gbanant kii tuun linba sii dont u sann.

ref: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
text: N yaa n jiin i buboit nba ki i daan sɔb turin nee. Li ki ŋan ki jɔɔ-n kpian poo;

ref: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
text: ŋaan bonchonchonsin paak, li ŋan ki jɔɔ kur-ii mɔk u mɔŋ poo, ki poo kur-ii munii mɔk u mɔŋ sɔrɔ.

ref: Husbands and wives should be fair with each other about having sex.
text: Li ŋan ki jɔɔ-n jii u mɔŋ ki chab u poo, ki poo mun-n jii u mɔŋ ki chab u sɔrɔ.

ref: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
text: Poo ki yen u mɔŋ gbananti, u sɔrɔe yen. Jɔɔ mun ki yen u mɔŋ gbananti, u pooe yen.

ref: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
text: I daa yêen leeb i gbanant loomm nakira yoo nba ki i tan taan mɔyemmkɔɔ a yin miar Yennu; ki li poorpo ki yii bia taan nan leeb, ki Sintaanii daa fit ki bikinti ki nyanti ya kan fit soor i mɔŋ na paak.

ref: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
text: Li tee maame ki n yet, ŋaan li ki tee sennu kaa.

ref: I wish all of you were like me, but God has given different gifts to each of us.
text: N bo loon ki sɔɔ kur-ii be kaman nan ma be biaŋinba nee, ŋaan sɔɔ kur nan u piiniie ki li nyii Yennu boor jiak jiak.

ref: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
text: N yeen na ki teen jakpaka nan pookarankaana nan pakɔi, a bi-i sa sak be bi kɔɔ kaman nan ma be biaŋinba na, li bo sii ŋan ki turib.

ref: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
text: Ŋaan bi-i kii fit so bi mɔŋi, ŋann nna bin kɔɔn poob, ki poob mun-n kun jab; li sɔ nan ya sii mɔk li niauŋ ŋaan kan fit maakiri.

ref: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
text: Binba mɔk jab, n sent sennue, li ki tee min kaa senti, ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔe sent, a poo daa mi nyikit u sɔrɔ.

ref: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
text: Ŋaan fi-i nyik a sɔrɔ, a ji daa kun jaganɔ, ŋaan kii tee pookarankaan; amii fan di sindant ki ŋmat a sɔrkperɔ na boor. Jɔɔ-ii munii mi wun yêt u ŋaapoo.

ref: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
text: Binba biar nee ki min yeen, ti Yomdaanɔ kaa yeti. Masiaweirɔ-i mɔk poo, ki poo na ki tee Masiaweirɔ, ŋaan ki li tee poo maŋ loommue a wuu be u boor ni, jɔɔ na daa lubinɔ.

ref: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
text: Poo nba tee Masiaweirɔ mun-i mɔk sɔrɔ nba ki tee Masiaweirɔ, ŋaan ki li tee jɔɔ maŋ loommue a wuu diau, poo maŋ daa yaatir ŋaan nyikit u sɔrɔ na.

ref: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
text: Ŋaan wunba ki tee Masiaweirɔ-i loon wun yêt u lɔɔ, fan ŋaanɔ wuu saa. Ŋann ŋanin sennu ji ki so bi sɔɔ. Yennue yiinit a tii be nan parmaasir.

ref: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
text: Fin poo, a teen nlee bann nan a kan tinn a sɔrɔ? ki fin poodaanɔ, a teen nlee bann nan a kan tinn a poo? I be binbeŋ nba ni ki Yennu senna

ref: If you are already circumcised, don't try to change it. If you are not circumcised, don't get circumcised.
text: Wunba dɔŋ pot u punn ŋaan tan teen Masiaweirɔ, ŋaant ki wun dian nna; wunba mun ki pot ŋaan teen Masiaweirɔ, wuu kii loon u ji daa porii u punni.

ref: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
text: Wunba pot nan wunba ki pot, li kur tee yanne. Linba mantik tee barkpaann tee i dia Yennu sennu bonŋann.

ref: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
text: Sɔɔ kur-ii be binbeŋ nba ni ki u tuu beewa ŋaan tan teen Masiaweirɔ.

ref: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
text: Li-i tee ki a din tee daabir yoo nba ki a teen Masiaweirɔ na, a daa te ki li daamiiani, ŋaan fi-i sa la yaak ki nyinn a mɔŋ daabsin ni, fan teen nna.

ref: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
text: Wunba poŋ tuu tee daabir yoo nba ki u teen Masiaweirɔ na, u ji tee ti Yomdaanɔ yɔɔe; nnee mun, li-i tee ki a yen a mɔŋ yoo nba ki a teen Masiaweirɔ na, a ji tee Masia daabire.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
text: Yennu daaie; li paak, i daa tee niib daaba.

ref: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
text: Mɔtana, n ji piak ki teen binba daa ki kɔɔn poob amii ki daa ki kun jab nee, ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ ki chabin a n betib nna; ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ ninbatinu paake ki n teen barmɔnii daanɔ.

ref: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
text: Mɔtana ninmɔnn na paak, n dukin nan li ŋan ki nirɔ-ii be biaŋinba ki u tuu be na.

ref: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
text: Li-i tee ki a mɔk poo, ŋann ŋanin a daa beerɔ. Li-i tee ki a ki kɔɔn poo, ŋann ŋanin a ji daa kɔɔnt poo.

ref: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
text: Fi-i dak poo jii a ki tun biiti; poo mun-i kun jɔɔ u ki tun biiti; ŋaan n loon ki n nyinni muksuk nba ki binba mɔk poob nan binba mɔk sɔrib sa la tingbouŋ na nie.

ref: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
text: N naa waas, n loon ki n wanni nan yoo na ki fɔk, li paak mɔtana li ŋan ki binba mɔk poob-ii be kaman nan bi ki kɔɔn poob na;

ref: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
text: ki binba mɔ, bii be kaman nan bi ki mɔ na; ki binba mɔk parpeenn, bii be kaman nan bi ki mɔk parpeenn na; ki binba kpentir, bii be kaman nan bi ki mɔk na;

ref: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
text: ki binba di tingbouŋ na manu, li ŋan ki bii be kaman nan bi baka kaa leŋ na. Kimaan tingbouŋ na nba tee biaŋinba mɔtana, li sa gara.

ref: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
text: N ki loon ki siar-n daamiini. Jakpakir ŋanin gorii wa sa teen biaŋinba ki tun ti Yomdaanɔ toonae ki te ki lin penn ti Yomdaanɔ par;

ref: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
text: ŋaan pookɔɔntɔɔ baka be tingbouŋ na bona nie, wa sa teen biaŋinba ki penn u ŋaapoo par.

ref: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
text: Li paak, u niauŋ bɔkit munnlee-e. Pookarankaan amii sapaamɔ baka mun be ti Yomdaanɔ toona nie nan wa sa teen biaŋinba ki u gbanant nan u seek-ii taŋŋii tee Yennu yar fanu; ŋaan wunba mɔk jɔɔ ŋanin gorii tingbouŋ na bona nan wa sa teen biaŋinba ki penn u sɔrɔ pare.

ref: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
text: N piak linba na i mɔŋ sommir paake, li ki tee a n yaa n bari baruk kaa, ŋaan n loon yii be fanu, ki jii i mɔŋ kur ki pet tur ti Yomdaanɔ.

ref: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
text: Jɔɔ-i mɔk sarkpeŋ ki u tan beer poo, ki jɔɔ na mɔk dudukit u po ki kan fit soor u mɔŋi, ŋaant ki wun tun biaŋinba ki u loon; li ŋan ki bin kɔɔn leeb. Li ki mɔk biit bi sinsuuk ni.

ref: But it is better to have self-control and to make up your mind not to marry.
text: Ŋaan wunba par chee, ki u mi nan siar kan daamɔ, ki u sa fit soor u mɔŋ niauŋ, ki dukin u par ni a u kan kɔɔnɔ, ŋann ŋan.

ref: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
text: Ŋaan wunba kɔɔn u sarkpeŋ, li ŋan; wunba mun ki kɔɔn u sarkpeŋ, u tun linba ŋan ki gar wunba kɔɔn nawa.

ref: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
text: Poo nba mɔk u sɔrɔ ki u daa be u manfoor ni, u ki yen u mɔŋi; ŋaan u sɔrɔ-i kpo, u ji mɔk yaak nba ki sa kun wunba ki u loon, ŋaan li ŋan ki u jɔɔ maŋ-ii tee Masiaweirɔ.

ref: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
text: Tɔn, ki jikit saa patmannu nant po, ti mi nan ti kur mɔk bannu, ki li set tee mɔnii. Bannu te ki nirɔ dont u mɔŋ, ŋaan loomm ŋanin sommit nirɔe Masia waanu ni.

ref: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
text: Wunba dukii nan u mi siar, li daanɔ daa ki mi nan wa bo sii mi na.

ref: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
text: Daanɔ nba loon Yennu, ŋɔɔe ki Yennu miu.

ref: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
text: Tɔn, ki jikit saa nant nba ki bi jii mann pata na; ti mi nan patir tee bont nba ki fo, ki bia mi nan Yen-yenɔkɔɔe be.

ref: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
text: Pata-i lekii ji be gbaa, ki be sanpaapo amii tingbouŋ nna, kimaan pata bonchiann nan yomdamm nba ki niib jiantir na bonchiann be,

ref: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
text: ŋaan timm yaa boor ni ŋanin, Yen-yenɔkɔɔe be; ŋɔɔe tee ti Baa Yennu nba din nan bonsiar kur, ki u nie ki ti fo; ki Yomdaan-yenɔkɔɔe be, wunba tee Yisa Masia. U paake ki bonsiar kur nan; u paake biak ki ti fo.

ref: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
text: Sɔɔ kur kaa mɔk li yami. Siab mantik maan patmannu nant dinu ki yukir, li paak, bi-i dii li jeboorik, bi bia tuu daa dukiie nan nant maŋ set tee patir nante. Bi dudukit ki paari, ki bi dukii a li mɔk biit.

ref: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
text: Ŋaan nant kaa sa te ki ti ki fɔk nan Yennu; kimaan ti-i dii, li ki pukint siari; ti-i biakii kii dii, li bia ki jiintit poori.

ref: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
text: I mi man, li pasiar yin tan jii i yaak na ki lin kpant baruk ki tur niib nba yada waar na ni.

ref: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
text: Ki wunba yada waar-i la yimm nba mɔk bannu na ki i be pochianfeek ni ki di, li sa te ki ŋɔɔ mun-n di nant nba tee pata yare.

ref: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
text: Fi-i tee nna ki tuun biit ki teen a naa waas, ki biir bi dudukit nba ki chee na, li want nan a tuun biit ki teen Masiae.

ref: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
text: Li-i tee jeet nba ki n di na paakiie te ki n baant n naa biki, ŋann n ji kan ŋman nant nba sa te ki wun baa.

ref: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
text: N ki yen n mɔŋii? N ki tee Masia toomii kaa-aa? N ki la ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa-aa? N toona nba ki n tuun ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ na paak kaa ki i mun tee mɔsakdammii?

ref: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
text: Siab-i kii sak a n tee Masia toomii, ŋaan yimm ŋanin, li kpaa talas yin sak, kimaan ya tee mɔsakdamm na, ŋanne sa wann nan n set tee toomiie.

ref: When people question me, I tell them
text: Niib-i piak ki biir n sann, n sa jii nnee ki nyi leŋ.

ref: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
text: Ti ki mɔk yaak nba ki ti sii di ki nyu-uu?

ref: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
text: Ti bia ki mɔk yaak nba ki sa jii poo kii lin nanɔ kaman nan toomiinba siab nan ti Yomdaanɔ naa waas nan Piita nba tee biaŋinba na-aa?

ref: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
text: I dukii a min nan Banabas kuukɔɔe ki mɔk yaak nba sa nyik ti toona tumu ŋaan ki yii dintitii?

ref: Do soldiers pay their own salaries? Don't people who raise grapes eat some of what they grow? Don't shepherds get milk from their own goats?
text: Lanjima lannɔe tuun toonn ŋaan pa u mɔŋi? ki ŋmee sii wuu tiinii ŋaan kan di li lɔɔna? amii ŋmee sii kpaar bonkobit ŋaan kan nyu li biimi?

ref: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
text: Li sɔb Moses sennu na ni a “Naab-i ŋmaan dii ki pinii dibin, fa daa bobint u mɔbi.” Yennu baka be nei kuukɔɔ niee?

ref: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
text: amii u ki mantik yeen timm kaa-aa? Mɔmɔmm, li sɔb ki teen timme. Wunba ko nan wunba jaan tun nnee ki mɔk dindann ki bi tan sa chent dii maŋ.

ref: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
text: Ti din turi Yennu Seyeeŋ yare; ki li bo ki jaŋ nan i turit gbanant yarii?

ref: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
text: Siab-i mɔk yaak ki sa gaar bona i boor, ki timm ŋanin ki mɔk li yaakii? Ŋaan ti ki jii yaak na ki tun nan toonni, ŋaan ti di fara bonsiar kur ni, kimaan ti ki loon ki tin te ki Masia barŋanii na-n jir poori.

ref: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
text: I poŋ mi nan binba tuun Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni na, bi jeet nyi bi toonn maŋ nie; ki binba mann mannu ki teen Yennu ki ŋammii binbintir na mun gaan bi yar leŋe.

ref: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
text: Nnee mun ki Yennu tur mɔb a binba mɔɔntir u barŋanii, bi jeet mun sii nyi leŋe.

ref: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
text: Ŋaan n ki jii yaak na ki tun nan toonni, ki bia ki sɔbin na a i turin siari. Mi-i kpo, li sɔ nan sɔɔ-n te ki n parbifaant-ii tee yann.

ref: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
text: Li ki tee a n mɔk parbifaanu barŋanii nba ki n mɔɔntir na paak kaa, kimaan li kpaa talas ki n mɔɔntir. Mi-i kii mɔn barŋanii na, parbiir sii tee n yare.

ref: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
text: Mi-i bo-i gann toonn maŋ n tiauŋue, n bo sa la nyɔɔt, ŋaan min-i kaa gann n mɔŋi, li tee Yennue guunin toonn maŋ.

ref: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
text: Lanne tee n nyɔɔti? N nyɔɔt tee ma mɔɔntir barŋanii na yann, ki ki jii yaak nba ki n mɔkir barŋanii toona ni na ki gaan siari.

ref: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
text: N ki tee sɔɔ daabir, ŋaan n jii n mɔŋ ki kpant daabir ki tur sɔɔ kur a n te ki niib bonchiann-n baar Yisa Masia boor.

ref: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
text: Juu teeb boor ni, n jii n mɔŋ ki teen kaman nan ŋamm nee a n te bin tinn. Binba mun waa Moses sennu na, n jii n mɔŋ ki teen kaman nan bi yenɔ na, ŋaan n mɔŋ ki be sennu na ni, ki loon ki binba waa sennu na-n tinn.

ref: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
text: Binba ki waa sennu na, ki n jii n mɔŋ ki teen kaman nan bi yenɔ na, a bin tinn. Na ki wann nan n ki waa Yennu sennu na; n waa Masia sennu na.

ref: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
text: Binba yada waar na ki n kpint ki teen kaman nan bi yenɔ na a bin tinn. N lebit n mɔŋ bonsiar kur ni ki tur niib kur ki loon bi siab-n tinn.

ref: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
text: Barŋanii na paake ki n tee linba na kur a n tan la li nyɔɔt.

ref: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
text: Li-i tee nantaar, nantatinna kur tuu tiine, ŋaan yenɔkɔɔe tuu tan di ki gaar piinii. Li paak, yabit ki tin di ki gaar piinii maŋ.

ref: Athletes work hard to win a crown that cannot last, but we do it for a crown that will last forever.
text: Nantatinna kur so bi mɔŋe bont kur ni a bin gaar piinii nba ki wa nan lin gbenni; ŋaan timm so ti mɔŋ a ti tan gaar piinii nba kaa gbennu.

ref: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
text: Ŋannii teen ki n tiin a n sa baar li paak; ŋannii teen ki n ki gbaa yanni.

ref: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
text: N ki loon min nba mɔɔntir barŋanii na ki teen niib na-n tan kɔŋ piinii na. Ŋannii teen ki n mukis n gbanant ki u saakin.

ref: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
text: N naa waas, n loon ki yin bann nan sanpagbouŋ din tuu baak ti yejamm kur paake, ki bi tan gar poot mɔkgbeŋŋir.

ref: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
text: Ki bi kur lakin dii jeet nba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ turib na;

ref: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
text: ki bia lakin nyuu nyun nba ki Seyeeŋ maŋ turib na; kimaan bi lakin nyuu nyun nba nyii tanpiiuk ni; kimaan Masiae din chiamm.

ref: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
text: Ŋaan linba na kura, Yennu din ki mɔk parpeenn nan bi siabi, ki te ki bi kpo kpo, ki bi gbanant yat dɔɔ dɔɔ fɔɔr ni.

ref: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
text: Li paak, li tee wannue ki kpaanit a ti daa loon biit kaman nan ba loon biaŋinba na;

ref: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
text: ti bia daa jiantir pata kaman nan bi siab nba din tuun; ki li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Ki niib na kar ki di ki nyu, ŋaan ji fiir leŋ ki chomm.”

ref: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
text: Ti daa tuun bonchonchonsin kaman nan bi siab nba din tuu tuun, ki dayennkɔɔ ki niib tusa piinnlee nan ŋanntaa kpo.

ref: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
text: Siab yeen a “Sɔnu be ki tin tun linba kur.” Mɔmɔmm; ŋaan bont kur kaa ŋani, bont kur kaa bia mɔk nyɔɔti.

ref: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
text: Ti daa bikii ti Yomdaanɔ kaman nan bi siab nba din tun, ki wai kpiib na.

ref: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
text: Ti daa mɔk bunburimii kaman nan bi siab nba din tuu mɔk, ki kuun malaka kpiib na.

ref: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
text: Linba na kur tun ki turib ki li tee kpaanii ki tur leeb, ki li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni ki tee kpaanii ki tur timm nba be yoo nba ki dunia loon gbennu na.

ref: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
text: Li paak, wunba mi nan u be fanu, wun soor u mɔŋ ki daa baa.

ref: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
text: Bikinu nba kur baar i boor ni, li tee linba yɔɔ baat niib boore; ŋaan Yennu tee barmɔnii daanɔ ki kan te ki bikinu-n baar i boor ki gar i paŋ teriki; ŋaan yoo nba ki bikinu baar i boor, u sa turi paŋ nba ki i sa nyannir ki nyi leŋ.

ref: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
text: N naa waas, li paak, yi nyinn i mɔŋ patmannu ni.

ref: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
text: N piak ki teen yimm yan damme, i mun dukin linba ki n pak ki turi na.

ref: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
text: Niipoouk turu ŋmanbanyuutik nba ki ti tuu tur Yennu niipoouk ŋaan nyu leŋ na paak, ŋanne see Masia sɔn ni; ki boroboro nba ki ti tuu ŋmit ŋmit ki ŋman na, ŋanne mun bia see Masia gbanant ni.

ref: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
text: La tee boroboro kunn na, timm ŋanin yaba ŋaan taan tee gbananyenn, kimaan ti kur lakin ŋman boroboro kunn nee.

ref: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
text: Gotir Israel teeb nba mann mannu ki teen Yennu na; ŋamm nan Yennu taan yomme na.

ref: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
text: Ma yeen linba na, li wann nan patir nan li nant nba ki bi mann na, li mɔk nyɔɔtii? Aaii, li ki mɔk nyɔɔti.

ref: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
text: N yeen a binba mann mannu ki teen pata na, bi set mann ki teen Sintaaniie, li ki tee Yennu kaa. Ki n ki loon ki sɔɔ-n taan nan Sintaanii.

ref: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
text: N kan sak ki i lakin kar ki nyu ti Yomdaanɔ Masia ŋmanbanyuutik ni ki bia lakin kar ki nyu Sintaanii ŋmanbaŋ ni.

ref: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
text: Ti bikii ti Yomdaanɔe a wun donn wutoor ti paakii? Ti paarɔee?

ref: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
text: Daa dukii a mɔŋ kuukɔɔ po, ŋaan kii biakii dukii leeb po.

ref: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
text: Yi ŋman nant nba ki bi kɔi daak ni, ŋaan daa boi ki laan bee nantee, ki daa ŋaant ki li daama a dudukit ni,

ref: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
text: kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Tingbouŋ na nan li bont kur tee Yennu yare.”

ref: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
text: Wunba ki tee Masiaweirɔ ŋaan tan baanta jeet, fii diwa, ki daa boi buboit jeet maŋ po, ki daa ŋaant ki li daama a dudukit ni.

ref: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
text: Ŋaan sɔɔ-i wanna a nant na bi mann patire, fa daa di ki a tan sa ŋmat u yan. Ŋaan sɔɔ sa boi a “Li sa teen nlee ki n yanbɔɔt nba loon biaŋinba sa biir lɔɔ dudukiti?

ref: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
text: Li-i tee ki n tur Yennu niipoouk n jeet na paak, nlee ki sɔɔ sa pak ki biir n sann jeet nba ki n tur Yennu niipoouk li paak na?”

ref: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
text: Tɔn, li-i tee ki i di, amii ki i nyu bonnyunyuut, amii ki i tuun linba kur, yii tee man linba sa tur Yennu baakir.

ref: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
text: Daa tee linba ki ŋan Juu nirɔ numm ni, amii Griik nirɔ numm ni, amii Yennu niib numm ni.

ref: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
text: Li paak, i waa n binbeŋ kaman nan ma waa Masia binbeŋ biaŋinba na. Yur yitu Yenjiantu yoo labaar

ref: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
text: N naa waas, n mɔk parpeenn nan ya tian linba ki n wanni na kur, ki bia tee nnee.

ref: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
text: Ŋaan bonyennkɔɔe be ki n loon ki n tiari, nan Masia tee jɔɔ kur yudaanɔ, ki jɔɔ kur mun tee u poo yudaanɔ, ki Yennu mun tee Masia yudaanɔ.

ref: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
text: Li paak, jɔɔ nba yaa wun miar Yennu amii ki mɔɔnt barŋanii na, ŋaan yir siar ki dɔkin u yur, u ki tur Masia baakiri.

ref: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
text: Poo nba yaa wun miar Yennu, amii ki mɔɔnt barŋanii na ŋaan ki ki yir siar, u dinn u sɔrɔ feie; ki li tee kaman nan u koor u yur nee.

ref: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki poo ki yir siar ki dɔkin u yur, ŋaan li ŋan wun koor u yut; ŋaan la tee fei ki poo-n koor u yut, amii ki pɔɔ u yut na, li ŋan wun yir siar ki dɔkin u yur.

ref: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
text: Li ki kpaa talas nan jɔɔ-n yir u yur siari, kimaan u naan nan Yennue ki bia tee Yennu paku; ŋaan poo ŋanin tee jɔɔe paku.

ref: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
text: Kimaan jɔɔ naauŋ ki nyii poo ni kaa, ŋaan poo naauŋo nyii jɔɔ ni.

ref: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
text: Yennu ki nan jɔɔ ki tur poo kaa, ŋaan u nan pooe ki tur jɔɔ.

ref: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
text: Ŋaan malakanba paak, li ŋan ki poo-n yir siar ki dɔkin u yur ki lin wann nan jɔɔ na mɔk poo na yaak.

ref: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
text: Ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ boor ni ŋanin, jɔɔ-i bo-i kaa, poo mun bo kaa, ki poo-i munii kaa, jɔɔ sii kaa;

ref: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
text: kimaan sintansimm dinn Yennu din jii jɔɔ kapirue ki nan poo, ki pooe mun maar jɔɔ, ŋaan bont kur nyi Yennu boore.

ref: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
text: Yi boi i mɔŋ ki laan li ŋan ki poo-ii mei Yennu ki kan dɔkin u yur siari.

ref: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
text: ŋaan poo yut-i tee yuumunt, li teenɔ fante, kimaan poo yuumunt tee u yuubobike.

ref: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
text: Ŋaan sɔɔ-i loon wun nɔi mɔniɔk ki jiin li po, n sa yet a timm amii Yenweiraleeb nba kur ki mɔk sennleer ki pukin na po. Ti Yomdaanɔ jeet dinu labaar

ref: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
text: Ma yaa n beti linba na ŋanin, n ki piaki li paak kaa, kimaan ya tuu lakin leeb na, ya tuun biaŋinba, li ki mɔk nyɔɔt ki turiini, ŋaan li mɔk biite.

ref: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
text: Sinsinn, n gbia ki bi yeen a ya tuu lakin leeb na, a lukitin tuu be i sinsuuk ni, ki n set gaar yenninba ki li tee mɔmɔmme.

ref: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
text: Li set tee mɔniie nan lukitin kpan be i sinsuuk ni, kimaan ŋanne sa wann binba set waa barmɔnii sɔnu na yeenin ni.

ref: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
text: Kimaan ya lakin leeb yoonba na, sɔɔ kur di u dinue, ki siab mɔ kon, ki yemm gboot.

ref: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
text: Linba na wantit nan yoo nba kur ki i tan ŋman ti Yomdaanɔ boroboro ki nyuu tilɔɔna nyun maŋ, li want nan u din kpo ti paake. I sii tee nnee mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki u tan sa jen.

ref: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
text: Wunba ki bann ti Yomdaanɔ gbanant na paak ŋaan ŋman ki nyuu, u sa la tubdatu nba bi u paak, wa ŋman ŋaan nyuu na paak.

ref: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
