text: Jeet na dinu poorpo, ki u bia jii nyunyuŋmaŋ nan tilɔɔna nyun, ŋaan yetib a “Tilɔɔna nyun na tee n sɔn nyinn, ki wann nan Yennu senn mɔsonpaann ki tur niib; a ki yoo nba kur ki i yaa i nyu, nyun man kii tian n po.”

ref: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
text: N gaar wannu na ti Yomdaanɔ boore ki mun tɔɔt ki turi, a nyiɔk nba ki bi fikit ti Yomdaanɔ na, ki u jii boroboro

ref: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
text: ŋaan tur Yennu niipoouk, ki ŋmit ŋmit ki tur u poorpoweiteeb a bin ŋman, ŋaan yetib a “Nee tee n gbanant nba ki n yaa n tur ki bin kpir i paak na, a li paak, yii yɔɔ kii tee nna kii tian n po.”

ref: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
text: Ŋannimi, wunba kur ŋman ti Yomdaanɔ boroboro na ki nyuu u bonnyunyuut na, ŋaan bo ki jaŋ nan wun tun nna, li daanɔ tun biit ki tur ti Yomdaanɔ gbanant nan u sɔn nawa.

ref: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
text: Sɔɔ kur-n ŋamm u mɔŋ fanuwa, ŋaan lian fit ŋman boroboro na ŋaan nyu bonnyunyuut na.

ref: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
text: Ŋannii teen ki i bonchiann chɔk ki yiar, ki siab mun kpo.

ref: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki i ŋamm i mɔŋ fanu ki ŋman, i kan la tubdatu.

ref: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
text: Ŋaan ki ti Yomdaanɔ-i bu ti buut ki ti la tubdatu, u ji tan kan biir timm yoo nba ki u tan sa biir tingbouŋ na nileeb.

ref: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
text: Li paak, n naa waas, li-i tee ki i lakin leeb a i ŋman ti Yomdaanɔ jeet na, yin guur leeb.

ref: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
text: Ŋaan wunba ki kon mɔkɔ, ki u kan fit guur ti Yomdaanɔ dinu na, ŋann wun di u ŋaak niwa, ki fit lian baar, ŋanne kan baar nan tubdatu ki turɔ yoo nba ki i lakin nan leeb. Maan nba biar na, mi-i taŋŋi baar, n sa pak ki turi.

ref: My friends, you asked me about spiritual gifts.
text: N naa waas, n loon ki yin bann Yennu Seyeeŋ piinii nba tee biaŋinbae nan barmɔnii.

ref: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
text: I mi nan yoo nba ki i daa din ki teen Masia yada na, i din lin waa patae, linba ki piaki, ki kɔɔnti bonbiir ni.

ref: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
text: N loon ki yin bann nan Yennu Seyeeŋ na-i be wunba ni, li daanɔ kan fit yet mɔb ki biir Yisa; sɔɔ mun kan fit yet a Yisae tee ti Yomdaanɔ, see ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na be u ni ki te ki u yet nna.

ref: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
text: Seyeeŋ piinbooru booru be, ŋaan Seyeeŋ yenɔkɔɔe be.

ref: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
text: Yenjiantbooru booru mun be; ŋaan Yomdaan-yenɔe be.

ref: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
text: Toonbooru booru mun be, ŋaan Yen-yenɔkɔɔe te ki sɔɔ kur tuun u toonn.

ref: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
text: Seyeeŋ na teen Masiaweira kur linba want Seyeeŋ na benu a tin somm Masiaweira na kur.

ref: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
text: Seyeeŋ na sa tur sɔɔ ki u mɔk yan paku; ki bia tur sɔɔ bannu ki u piak bannu maan; ŋaan ki li kur nyi Seyeen-yenɔ nie.

ref: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
text: Seyeeŋ maŋe teen sɔɔ ki u mɔk yada, ki bia teen sɔɔ paŋ nba te ki baatib paak na;

ref: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
text: ki bia teen sɔɔ yaak ki u tuun bakitnauŋ toona; ki bia teen sɔɔ ki u sɔkint Yennu maan; ki bia teen sɔɔ ki u bant piinii nba nyi Yennu Seyeeŋ ni nan linba ki nyi Seyeeŋ na ni; ki bia teen sɔɔ ki u piak mabooru booru; ki bia teen sɔɔ ki u fit kpintir mabooru maŋ.

ref: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
text: Linba na kur tee Seyeeŋ maŋ toonae, ki u pi sɔɔ kur piinii kaman nan ŋɔɔ Seyeeŋ nba loon biaŋinba. Gbanant paak barjouk labaar

ref: The body of Christ has many different parts, just as any other body does.
text: Masia tee kaman nan gbanant nee; gbanant tee gbananyennkɔɔ nee, ŋaan ki li ji bɔkit bɔkit, ŋaan lek tee gbananyenn, nan la lek bɔkit bɔkit na gbaa.

ref: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
text: Ti kura nba tee Juu teeb, nan binba ki tee Juu teebi, nan daaba, nan yentinmɔŋ damm, bi wur ti kur Yennu nyunwuru u Seyeeŋ yenɔkɔɔ sann ni a ti taan teen gbananyennkɔɔ; ki li Seyeeŋ yenɔkɔɔ maŋ bia gbeen ti kura.

ref: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
text: Gbanant na ki mɔk linba gaa li paak na yennkɔɔ kaa, ŋaan li yaba.

ref: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
text: Li-i tee ki taar yet a u ki tee gbanant bɔkirii ŋaan yaa kimaan u ki tee nuu, ŋann kaa sa te ki u ki tee gbananti.

ref: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
text: Ki tubir mun-i yet a wa ki tee ninbinn na, li paak amii u ki tee gbanant bɔkirii, ŋann kaa sa te ki u ki tee gbananti.

ref: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
text: Ki gbanant na kur-i taan tee ninbinnie, li sa teen nlee kii gbia? ki li-i taan tee tubir, nlee ki li sa gbat nubiru?

ref: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
text: Ŋaan Yennu taan gbanant na kur ki teen gbananyennkɔɔ ni nan wa loon biaŋinba.

ref: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
text: Li kur-i bo-i taan tee bonyennkɔɔ, li bo sii tee gbanantaa?

ref: It takes many parts to make a single body.
text: La tee biaŋinbae na, linba gaa gaa gbanant paak yaba, ŋaan li tee gbananyennkɔɔe.

ref: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
text: Ninbinn kan fit yet nuu a u ki loonɔ; ki yur mun kan fit bet taabira a u ki loomii.

ref: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
text: Ki ti gbananbɔka nba gaa nan gbanant paak ki tee kaman nan bi ki mɔk paŋ na-i kaa, gbanant na kan tun.

ref: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
text: Gbananbɔka yenn yenn nba gaa ki ŋan na, li ki loon sommiri. Yennu maŋe ŋamm li gbanant na ki tur gbananlia nba gaa gaa na ki ti gorii ki bi ki mɔk nyɔɔt na, ki ji teen gbananlia fant ki gar.

ref: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
text: Li paak, ti gbanant ki mɔk lukitimi. Gbanant kur nba gaa gaa na, bi fiin bi leeb po.

ref: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
text: Gbananbɔka yennkɔɔ-i di fara, gbananbɔka lia na mun tuu di li farae; gbananbɔka yennkɔɔ-i la baakir, lia na mun tuu mɔk parpeenne.

ref: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
text: Yimme lakin tee Masia gbanant, ki sɔɔ kur tee u gbanant nba gaa na yenninba.

ref: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu gann toomiinba u niib sinsuuk ni ki bi tee saakab; ki wei gann sɔkiniinba; ki gann wannteeb; ki wei paan binba tuun bakitnauŋ toona; ki gann binba te ki baatib paak, ki gann sommteeb; ki wei paan saakab nba ŋan ki gorii toona paak; ki wei paan binba piak mabooru booru.

ref: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
text: Ti kur kaa tee toomiinba, amii sɔkiniinba, amii wannteebi. Ti kur kaa sii mɔk yikoo ki sa tun bakitnauŋ toona.

ref: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
text: Ti kur kaa bia mɔk yikoo ki sa te ki baatib paaki, amii ki pak barganii, amii ki kpint barganii.

ref: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
text: Yii loon man piinii nba tee piinjaana nan ninmɔnn. Ki n sa wanni sɔnu nba gar li kur.

ref: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
text: Tɔn, mi-i lekii fit piak barganii kur, amii ki fit piak malakanba maan, ŋaan ki ki mɔk loommi, n tee kaman nan gingann amii gbekuru fuut nee.

ref: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
text: Mi-i fit sɔkint Yennu mɔmaan, ki bant woonin ni maan kur, ki mɔk bannu, ki mɔk yada nba sa fit te ki jɔɔr-n fikit ki saan boleer po, ŋaan ki ki mɔk loommi, n tee yanne.

ref: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
text: Mi-i jii n mɔkint kur ki tur tatimm, ki lek jii n gbanant chab ki bi joo muu gbaa Masia paak, ŋaan ki ki mɔk loommi, n ki mɔk nyɔɔti.

ref: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
text: U ki mɔk nyatuku; u mantik ki loon u mɔŋ nyɔɔt kuukɔɔ kaa; u ki dont wutoor yann yanni; u bia ki dukii bonbiir ki jiint biit biit paaki.

ref: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
text: Wunba mɔk loomm, u ki mɔk parpeenn nan biit po, ŋaan mɔk parpeenn nan barmɔnii po.

ref: Love is always supportive, loyal, hopeful, and trusting.
text: Wunba mɔk loomm ki bakit; u yada nan u dindann nan u sukuru ki mɔk baanu.

ref: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
text: Loomm ki mɔk gbennu. Barsɔkinu nan barganii paku nan bannu tan sa gar.

ref: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
text: Ti bannu nan barsɔkinu na waar;

ref: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
text: ŋaan linba na kur-i taŋŋi baar, li yoo na ki ti sa nyik linba waar na kur.

ref: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
text: Ma din tuu tee bik, n pinpakit nan n bannu nan n dudukit din tuu tee bisuko; ŋaan ma ji tan beer nirɔ, n ji nyik bisin ni bonae.

ref: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
text: Ta mi linba mɔtana waar, ki li tee kaman nan ta sa got nyun ni ki li bɔn biaŋinba na; ŋaan ti tan sa lau yeenin ni. Ma mi linba na, li daa waar; ŋaan n tan sa mir fanu kaman nan Yennu nba min biaŋinba na.

ref: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
text: Tɔn, yada nan dindann nan loomm, li bona ŋanntaa daa be mɔtana; ŋaan loomme gar li kur.

ref: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
text: Yii loon loomm ki gar bont kur; ŋaan kii biakii loon Yennu Seyeeŋ piinii nan ninmɔnjar; ŋaan kii mantik-ii loon piinii nba tee barsɔkinu.

ref: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
text: Wunba piak barganii nba ki Yennu seyeeŋ wannɔ na, u piak teen Yennue, u ki piak teen niib kaa, kimaan sɔɔ ki bant li paak, wa piak woonin ni maan Seyeeŋ paŋ po na;

ref: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
text: ŋaan wunba sɔkint Yennu maan ki teen niib, u teemm paŋe, ki kɔɔntib para, ki bia teemm parpeenn.

ref: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
text: Wunba piak barganii, u sommit u mɔŋ kuukɔɔe; ŋaan wunba sɔkint Yennu maan, u sommit Masiaweirae.

ref: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
text: N loon ki sɔɔ kur-ii piak barganii, ŋaan n bia mantik loon ki i kura-ii sɔkint Yennu maan. Wunba sɔkint Yennu maan, li mɔk nyɔɔt ki gar wunba piak barganii, see ki sɔɔ be ki fit kpint maan na ki wann Masiaweira ki lin sommib.

ref: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text: N naa waas, mi-i baar i boor ki tan piak barganii, somm lanne ki n sommitiini? Sommir kaa; see ki n baar ki fiit bona nba nyii Yennu boor ki wanni, amii yan, amii ki sɔkin Yennu maan ki turi, amii wannu.

ref: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
text: Wunba faa tinpanii ŋaan ki faa nan ninmɔnn faanu, ŋmee sa teen siir nan tabu?

ref: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
text: Nnee mun, li-i tee ki i piak barganii, nlee ki bi sa bann ya piak linba; li paku sii tee yanne; see ki i piak linba ki bi sa fit bann li paak.

ref: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
text: Mabooru bonchiann be tingbouŋ na ni, ŋaan li kur mɔk paake. Ŋann ki mi-i kii mi maan paak, n tee saamɔe ki tur wunba piak nanin na; ki wunba mun piak teenin na mun tee saamɔe n boor.

ref: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
text: Ya set loon Seyeeŋ piinii na, yii loon piinii nba sa somm Masiaweira.

ref: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
text: Wunba piak barganii, wuu mei Yennu wun te ki wun fit sɔkin linba ki u piak maŋ.

ref: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
text: Mi-i mei Yennu nan barganii ni, n seek nee mei, ŋaan n yan kaa leŋi.

ref: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
text: Ki n sa teen nlee? N sa miar Yennu nan n seeke, ki bia miarɔ nan n yan ni; ki yin yanii ki tur Yennu nan n seek, ki bia yin yanii nan n yan ni.

ref: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
text: Fi-i jii barganii ki mei Yennu nan u Seyeeŋ paŋ ni, ki nirɔ nba ki a be nanɔ na ki mi fa yeen linba, u sa teen nleewa ki sak a Yenmiaru na a li tee mɔmɔmme?

ref: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
text: A Yenmiaru na lek ŋan, ŋaan li ki mɔk sommir ki tur sɔɔ.

ref: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
text: N tur Yennu niipoouk nan ma piak barganii ki gar i kur na;

ref: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
text: ŋaan mi-i be Masiaweira sinsuuk ni, n sii loon ma n pak mɔbona ŋaŋŋmu nba mɔk paak ki lin somm leeb na, ki gar ma sa pak mɔbona tusir barganii ni.

ref: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
text: N naa waas, i daa dia bisin ni dudukiti, ŋaan biit ni, yii tee kaman nan sanpantii nee; ŋaant man ki i dudukit-ii tee subintɔɔ dudukit.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a u yet a “N tan sa tun nisiab bin nyi siarpo ki tan pak barganii ki tur n niib, ŋaan bi lek kan gbat li paaki.”

ref: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
text: Li paak, barganii tee nyinne ki tur binba ki teen Masia yada na, li ki tee binba teenɔ yada na kaa. Ŋaan sɔkinii maan tee wannu ki tur yada damm, ki li ki tee binba ki teen Masia yada na kaa.

ref: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
text: Masiaweira kur-i lakin leeb, ki sɔɔ kur piin piak barganii, ki binba ki tee Masiaweira amii binba ki bann li po tan kɔɔ, bi kan yet a i waatee?

ref: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
text: ŋaan Masiaweira-i lakin leeb, ki bi gaan Yennu maan ki sɔkint teen niib, ki wunba ki tee Masiaweirɔ amii wunba ki bann li po tan kɔɔ ki gbat ba sɔkint linba na, ŋanne sa te ki wun bann u biit ki bu u mɔŋ buut,

ref: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
text: ki u sa bann u woonin ni dudukit ki li dɔɔ yeenin ni, ki u sa gbaan ki pukii Yennu ki bet a “Mɔmɔmm, Yennu be nani.” Masiaweira nba sii jiantir Yennu biaŋinba

ref: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
text: N naa waas, n ji sa yet a bia? Li-i tee ki i lakin leeb a yi jiant Yennu, ki sɔɔ mɔk yaŋ, ki sɔɔ mɔk wannu, ki sɔɔ mɔk mafiit kar ki sa wann, ki sɔɔ mɔk barganii paku, ki sɔɔ mun mɔk barganii maŋ kpintu; yi teen linba na kur ki lin somm Masiaweira.

ref: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
text: Bi-i loon bin pak barganii, bi daa gar niib bannlee amii banntaa, bii piak yenɔ yenɔ ŋaan ki sɔɔ mun-ii kpintir li paak yenn yenn.

ref: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
text: Wunba sa fit kpint li paak na-i kaa, wunba piak barganii na-n ŋmin Masiaweira na sinsuuk ni, ŋaan pak ki tur u mɔŋ nan Yennu.

ref: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
text: Ŋaant man ki sɔkiniinba bannlee amii banntaa-n pak, ki binba biar na-n got maan na ni.

ref: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki sɔɔ be i sinsuuk ni ki tan loon wun sɔkin Yennu maan, wunba bo piak na-n ŋmin.

ref: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
text: I kur sa fit ki sɔkin Yennu maan yenɔ yenɔ, a i kur-n tumii ki lii kɔɔnti para.

ref: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
text: Yennu sɔkinii sa fit soor u mɔŋ yoo nba ki li baarɔ;

ref: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
text: kimaan Yennu ki yiinit a tii tee kpir kpir; ŋaan kii tee soonna.

ref: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
text: Siar-i be ki bi loon bin boi bann, bin boi bi sɔrib ŋei niwa. Li tee feie ki poo-n pak Masiaweira tintaankaanu sinsuuk ni.

ref: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
text: Labaarii? Yennu mɔmaan na nyii i boorii? li baar i kuukɔɔ booree?

ref: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
text: Wunba mi nan u tee Yennu sɔkinii amii ki mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋ piinii, wun bann nan linba ki n sɔbin ki teeni na tee ti Yomdaanɔ sennue.

ref: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
text: Ŋaan wunba ki gbat, i mun daa gbia u yari.

ref: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
text: Li paak, n naa waas, yii loon ki i sɔkin Yennu maan, ki daa yêen barganii paku.

ref: But do everything properly and in order.
text: Ŋaant ki bont kura-n tun bonŋann ki wei leer paak.

ref: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
text: N naa waas, n loon ki n tiari barŋanii nba ki n din mɔɔnt ki turi, ki i gaar ki diar fanu na poe.

ref: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
text: Li-i tee ki i diar fanu, i sa tinn, ŋaan nnee kaa, i yada na sii tee yanne.

ref: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
text: Kimaan n din beti labaar nba ki n mun din gaar na, ki li tee barjaann ki gar barii kura; a Masia din gaar timm biit damm paak ki kpo, kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinba na;

ref: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
text: ki bi din piiu; ki u fiir kuun ni daa ŋanntaa daar kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinba na;

ref: Christ appeared to Peter, then to the twelve.
text: ki u dɔkit Piita paak, ki bia dɔkit toomiinba piik nan bannlee na ni.

ref: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
text: Li poorpo biak ki u dɔkit yomm ki wann u poorpoweiteeb nba yab gar niib kobiiŋŋmu na. Bi siab bonchiann daa be bi manfoa ni, ki bi siab mun kpowa.

ref: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
text: Li poorpoe ki u bia tan dɔkit Jems paak, ki ji tan dɔkit toomiinba kura paak.

ref: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
text: Linba na kur poorpo, ki u bia dɔkit n paak, min nba tee kaman nan poonyiir na.

ref: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
text: Toomiinba na kur ŋaan mine tan joont, ki n bo ki jaŋ nan bii yin toomii, kimaan ma din dinn Yisaweira fara na paak.

ref: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
text: Ŋaan Yennu sommir paake ki n tee ma tee wunba, ki sommir nba ki u jii ki turin na, li ki tee yanni; n tun ki gar toomiinba na kura, ŋaan li ki tee a mine tumi, li tee Yennu sommir nba be n ni nee tun.

ref: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
text: Li-i tee minie, li-i bia tee ŋammie, ŋanne ki ti kur want niib barŋanii na, ŋanne ki i mun teen yada. Kuun fiiru labaar

ref: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
text: Li paak, ta mɔɔntir barii na ki yaa a Masia din fiir kuun ni, ki nlee ki i siab yeen a binba kpo na tan kan fiiri?

ref: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
text: Li-i tee ki kuun fiiru kaaie, li want nan Masia din ki fiir kuun ni;

ref: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
text: ki Masia-i diŋŋi kii fiir kuun nie, ŋann ŋanin ti ki mɔk labaar nba ki ti ji sa mɔɔnti; ki i mun ki mɔk linba ki i sa teen yada.

ref: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
text: Ki li-i tee nneeie, ŋann ŋanin ti teen fai siara damme na ki tur Yennu, kimaan ti poŋ teen Yennu siara damm a u din fiin Masia kuun ni; ŋaan kuun fiiru-i kaa, u set din ki fiinɔ kuun ni.

ref: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
text: Kpeemm fiiru-i kaa, ŋann ŋanin Yennu din ki fiin Masia kuun ni.

ref: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
text: Masia-i diŋŋi bo-i kii fiir kuun ni, i yada tee yanne, ki i bia daa be i toonbiit ni.

ref: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
text: Li bo bia want nan binba teen Yisa yada ŋaan kpo na, bi bote.

ref: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
text: Ta teen Masia yada na, li-i tee ki li mɔk nyɔɔt tingbouŋ na benu ni kuukɔɔie, ŋann ŋanin ti bo sii mɔk ninbaauk ki gar sɔɔ kura.

ref: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
text: Ŋaan li tee barmɔniie, Yennu set din fiin Masia kuun niwa. Ŋɔɔ Masia tee sinsinn daanɔ nba fiir kuun ni; ki li want nan binba kur kpo na mun tan sa fiir kuun ni.

ref: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
text: Nirɔe baar nan kuun tingbouŋ na ni; nnee mun ki nirɔ bia baar nan kuun fiiru.

ref: Adam brought death to all of us, and Christ will bring life to all of us.
text: Kimaan Adam paake ki nifosaarii kur kpenn; nnee mun Masia paak nifosaarii sa la manfoa.

ref: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
text: Sɔɔ kur sa fiir u kuun fiiru yoo; Masiae fiir sinsinn, ki binba tee u niib mun tan sa fiir u baaru daar maŋ.

ref: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
text: Li poorpo ki li joontu tan sa baar, ki u tan sa nyann naandinu kur, nan yent nan paŋ kur; ŋaan jii naan na ki tur u Baa Yennu.

ref: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
text: Masia tan sii yen yent kur mɔkmɔk ki tan baar yoo nba ki u sa nyann u datai kur ki teemm u nuu ni.

ref: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
text: Dataak nba ki u tan sa joont ki biir tee kuume.

ref: When the Scriptures say he will put everything under his power, they don't include God. It was God who put everything under the power of Christ.
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “U jii bont kur ki teen u nuu ni;” ŋaan li ki wann a Yennu mɔŋ pukin bont kur maŋ ni.

ref: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
text: Ki bont kur-i taŋŋi be Masia nuu ni, ŋann ŋanin u mɔŋ tan sii be Yennu nuu ni, wunba te ki bont kur be u nuu ni na; Yennu sii tee bont kur saaka yɔɔ.

ref: If the dead are not going to be raised to life, what will people do who are being baptized for them? Why are they being baptized for those dead people?
text: Nirɔ-i kii fi kuun ni, bee ki bi wur siab Yennu nyunwuru ki ki wurib bi mɔŋ paak, ŋaan wurib ki teen kpeemmi? Kpeemm-i kii fi, bee ki bi tee nna?

ref: And why do we always risk our lives
text: Ki bee ki ti kɔɔ maan ni yoo kuri?

ref: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
text: N naa waas, n mɔk parbifaant i paak nan ya be biaŋinba nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia na, linba na paake ki n yeen a n tookit kuun yoo kur na.

ref: What do you think I gained by fighting wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead are not raised to life, “Let's eat and drink. Tomorrow we die.”
text: Ki min nan Efesus doo ni bonkobibiit kɔn, ki mi-i tee nifosaarik nba kan fiir kuun nie, li nyɔɔt tee lanne ki turimi? Kpeemm-i kii fi kuun ni, ŋaant man ki tii di kii nyu ŋaan kii mi nan ti won sa kpo.

ref: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
text: Ŋaant ki i yan-n jen, ki ji daa tuun toonbiiti. I siab ki mi Yennu; ŋanne ki n sɔbin na ki yaa n dinni fei. Gbanant fiiru labaar

ref: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
text: Sɔɔ sa boi a “Nlee ki binba kpo na tan sa fiir kuun ni? a bi gbanant tan sii tee nlee?”

ref: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
text: Jatuk na, linba ki a bur, li kan fit pia li yoo; see ki li kpo daanna ki tan fit lian pia.

ref: Wheat seeds and all other seeds look different from the sprouts that come up.
text: Fi-i yaa a bur bonboorik, li kii tee kaman nan linba sa pia na kaa; a buur bonboorik nee, li pasiar fan bur dii amii linba kur;

ref: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
text: ŋaan Yennue sa tur bonboorik maŋ lin kpaat nan wa loon lii tee biaŋinba na; ki bonboorik kur mɔk li mɔŋ naauŋo.

ref: People, animals, birds, and fish are each made of flesh, but none of them are alike.
text: Gbanant bont kur ki taa naauŋo, nifosaarii gbanant tee yomme, ki bonkobit yar mun tee yomm, ki nɔɔnii yar mun tee yomm, ki janii yar tee yomm.

ref: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
text: Sanpaapo bona mɔk bi gbanant bi kɔɔe, ki tingbouŋ na ni bona mun mɔk li gbanant li kɔɔ. Sanpaapo bona gbanant fant be li kɔɔe, ki tingbouŋ na bona gbanant fant mun be li kɔɔ;

ref: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
text: yennu mɔk yentu li kɔɔe, ki ŋmaarik mun mɔk yentu li kɔɔ, ki ŋmaabira mɔk yentu li kɔɔ, ki ŋmaabira sinsuuk ni mɔk yentu kɔɔt kɔɔt.

ref: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
text: Li tee nnee kuun fiiru ni; bi-i pii gbanant tiŋ ni li tuu tan pɔɔke, ŋaan li-i fiir li kan mi pɔɔki.

ref: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
text: Bi piit nan tingbouŋ na gbanante, ŋaan ti-i fiir ti sii tee gbanant nba sii tee Seyeeŋ yare. Gbanant nba tee tingbouŋ na yar be; ki gbanant nba tee Seyeeŋ yar mun be.

ref: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
text: Kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Sinsinn nirɔ Adam na din tee nifosaarike;” ŋaan nirɔ nba joont ki baar na tee Seyeeŋ nba teent manfoore.

ref: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
text: Gbanant nba tee Seyeeŋ yar na kaa baar sinsinni, ŋaan tingbouŋ na gbanant nee baar sinsinn, li poorpo ki Seyeeŋ yar na wei.

ref: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
text: Sinsinn nirɔ na din nyii tant nie, ki nirɔ nba wei na nyii Yendɔuŋ ni.

ref: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
text: Tingbouŋ na teeb tee kaman nan jɔɔ nba ki Yennu jii tant ki namɔ nee; ki binba tee Yendɔuŋ ni yab tee kaman nan jɔɔ nba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni na.

ref: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
text: Ti kura gbanant tee kaman nan Adam wunba nyii tant ni yar nee; dasiar ti bia tan sii tee kaman nan jɔɔ nba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni na yar nie.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
text: N naa waas, n beerie nan gbanant nba tee nant nan sɔn na kan fit kɔɔ Yendɔuŋ ni; ki gbanant nba pɔɔk kan fit la manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

ref: I will explain a mystery to you. Not every one of us will die, but we will all be changed.
text: N yaa n beti woonin ni maame. Ti siab tan sa kpo; ki siab mun tan sii be mɔkmɔk nan Yisa baaru daar; ŋaan ti kur sa lebita.

ref: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
text: Li daar maŋ, kikaak-i taŋŋi bui, ti kur sa lebit yomme yian yiama kaman nan ninbinn kabintu na; kikaak sa bui, ki kpeemm-n fiir nan gbanant nba kan mi kpo, ki timm nba tan daa sii be manfoor ni daar maŋ, ti gbanant sa lebite.

ref: Our dead and decaying bodies will be changed into bodies that won't die or decay.
text: Li kpaa talas nan gbanant nba pɔɔkit na tan sa kpant gbanant nba ki pɔɔkite, ki gbanant nba kpenn na tan sa kpant gbanant nba ki kpennie.

ref: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
text: Kuun tonu tee biite, ki biit kpit sennu paŋ ni.

ref: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
text: Ti teen Yennu nipoouk nan wa teent nyannu ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak na.

ref: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
text: Li paak, n naa waas, i be man fanu ki daa baa Masiaweiu ni. Yii jii i mɔŋ kii tuun ti Yomdaanɔ toona yoo kur, kimaan i mi nan toona nba ki i tuun ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ na, li tan kan baa yanni.

ref: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
text: Tɔn, n loon ki n wanni likirii nba ki i lakin ki yaa a i somm Yennu niib nba be Judea tiŋ ni na; yi tun biaŋinba ki n wann Masiaweira nba be Galasia tiŋ ni na.

ref: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
text: Lasir daar kur, sɔɔ kur-n tuu nyinn likirii, wa tun ki la biaŋinba, ki bir, ki mi-i taŋŋi baar, ki i ji daa lakii likirii.

ref: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
text: Mi-i taŋŋi baar, n tan sa sɔb gbouŋ ki tun binba ki i gann na ki bin saan nan i piinii na Jerusalem.

ref: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
text: Li-i taŋŋi jaŋ nan man saan n mɔŋ, tin tɔk ki saan. Pɔɔl nba soor wun tun biaŋinba labaar

ref: After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you
text: N lor soor ki loon ki n tɔkin nyi Masedonia tiŋ ni; li poorpo ki n baar ki gɔni.

ref: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
text: Dasiar n tan sii be i boor yoo waaminna amii piar yoo kur; ki i tan sa sommin ki n gar ki saan siaminba kur ki n saa.

ref: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
text: N ki loon ki n baar i boor ki gɔni waaminna ŋaan gar kaa, ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ-i sak, n loon ki n jii yoo bonchiann i boor.

ref: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
text: Sɔnu loot ki yaa bonchiann ki turin ki n sa tun Yisa toona, ŋaan datai bonchiann mun be.

ref: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
text: Timoti-i taŋŋi baar i boor, i mi man ki yin kɔɔnɔ jaŋmaanii, kimaan u tuun ti Yomdaanɔ toonae kaman nan ma mun tuun biaŋinba na.

ref: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
text: I mi man ki sɔɔ-n siannɔ, ŋaan te ki wun nyi i boor nan parmaasir ki baar n boor, kimaan n gorii ŋɔɔ nan ti naa waas sɔnu.

ref: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
text: Ti naa bik Apolos ŋanin, n barimɔe ki loon ŋɔɔ nan ti naa waas na-n tɔk ki baar i boor, ŋaan u ki loon wun baar mɔtana; li-i tee ki u tan sa la yoo, u sa baara. Kpaanii nan foontii labaar

ref: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
text: Yii guu man kii be fanu i yada ni, kii mɔk par nan paŋ.

ref: Show love in everything you do.
text: Yii tuun man bont kur nan loomm.

ref: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
text: I poŋ mi Stefanas nan u ŋaateeb po, ŋamme din tee sinsinn niib nba be Akaya tiŋ ni ki din teen Masiaweira, ki bi jii bi mɔŋ ki tuun ki teen Yennu niib.

ref: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
text: N naa waas, n barimiie, yii tokii nibooru na binbeŋ nan bi toontunleeb kur binbeŋ, nan binba tuun bonchiann ki teen Yennu niib na.

ref: I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you.
text: N mɔk parpeenn Stefanas nan Fotunatus nan Akaikus baaru na paak, kimaan ya daan kaa n boor nna, n daan mɔk parbiir, ŋaan ba baar na, n ji ki mɔk parbiiri,

ref: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
text: kimaan bi chee min nan yimm para; li paak, yi tur li nibooru nibaakir.

ref: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
text: Masiaweira nba be Asia tiŋ ni na foonti. Akuila nan Prisika nan Masiaweira nba lakii ki jiantir Yennu bi ŋaak ni na foonti fanu ti Yomdaanɔ sann ni.

ref: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
text: Ti naa waas na kur foonti. I foont leeb kpammii man.

ref: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
text: Min Pɔɔle jii n mɔŋ nuu ki sɔbin foonti.

ref: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
text: Wunba kur ki loon ti Yomdaanɔ, mɔpor-ii be u paak. Maranata; li paak tee “Ti Yomdaanɔ, baat.”

ref: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
text: N loon i kur nan barmɔnii Yisa Masia sann paak. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
text: Min Pɔɔl wunba ki Yennu loomm paak ki u teenin Yisa Masia toomii na, min nan ti naa bik Timotie sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen Yennu niib nba be Korint, nan u niib kur nba be Akaya tingbouŋ ni nna kur.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi ki bia turi parmaasir. Pɔɔl nba teen Yennu niipoouk biaŋinba

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
text: Ŋaant ki ti tur Yennu nba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Baa na niipoouk; ŋɔɔe tinit ninbaauk; u boor nie ki ti parmaann kur nyi.

ref: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
text: Ŋɔɔe maan ti para ti fara kur ni, a tin mun fit maan binba be fara booru booru ni na para, kaman nan Yennu nba maan ti para biaŋinba na.

ref: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
text: Ti fara dinu Masia sann paak nba yab biaŋinba, nnee ki ti parmaann mun bia yab kimaan ŋɔɔ Masia sann paak.

ref: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
text: Ta loon ki tin maan i para ki te ki yin la tinnu na paake te ki ti di fara. Ti-i la parmaann, li tee i paake, a tin mun maan i para, ki yin mun la sukuru ki di fara kaman nan ta mun dir biaŋinba na.

ref: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
text: Li paak ti mɔk dindann ki jii jiin i po a i sa saan tɔɔnn, kimaan ti mi nan ya di fara kaman nan ta di biaŋinba na, nnee ki i mun bia sii laat parmaann kaman nan ta laat biaŋinba na.

ref: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
text: N naa waas, n loon ki n tiari fara nba ki ti din dii Asia yent ni nee. Fara maŋ din mantik gar ti paŋ terik hali ki ti tan ŋaa dindann ti mɔŋ mɔŋ manfoa po,

ref: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
text: kimaan ti ji din tan dukii nan kuun ki fɔk nanti, ŋaan linba na din tun a lin wannit nan li ki ŋan nan tii daan ti mɔŋ paaki, ŋaan kii daan Yennu nba fiint kpeemm na paake;

ref: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
text: ŋɔɔe din nyinnit kuun dangbeŋŋir na ni. U sa tinnita, ki ti mɔk dindann nan u bia tan sa tinnita.

ref: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
text: Yi mun bia sommit nan Yenmiaru man, ki Yennu sa gat niib bonchiann miaru nba ki bi mei teent na, ki sa sommit, ki niib bonchiann sa turɔ niipoouk ti paak. Pɔɔl dudukit lebitu po

ref: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
text: Linba paak te ki ti mɔk yuugontir na tee nnee: ti dudukite wantit nan ti mɔk barmɔnpaku nan yentinu tingbouŋ na niib boor, ki mantik daan be nna ki jiin timm nan yimm weinanleeb po na; ki Yennu sommir paake te ki ti be nna maŋ, li ki tee nisaarii yan paak kaa.

ref: I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely,
text: Ti sɔbin linba ki i sa fit karin ki bann li paake ki teeni na, ŋaan mɔk dindann nan i tan sa bann fanu

ref: just as you already partly understand us. Then when our Lord Jesus returns, you can be as proud of us as we are of you.
text: kaman nan ya poŋ bann waaminna biaŋinba na, nan i tan sa fit kii mɔk yuugontir ti paak, kaman nan ta mun sii mɔk yuugontir i paak, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa jenu daar na.

ref: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
text: Ma bann linba na paake te ki n daan lor ma n poŋ jen i boor ni, ki yin la sommir taar munnlee,

ref: once on my way to Macedonia and again on my return from there. Then you could send me on to Judea.
text: kimaan n daan lor nan mi-i taŋŋi gaar saa Masedonia man bot gɔniie, ki mi-i taŋŋi jent man bia bot gɔni, ki la sommir i boor ni linba sa sommin ki n ŋmat saan Judea.

ref: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
text: Ki n lor maŋ daan tee laat loree? amii ma daan lor ma n sa tun linba maŋ, li daan tee kaman nan tingbouŋ na yab nba tuu yet a, “Nn, nn,” ŋaan bia tan yet a “Aaii, aaii” nee-ee?

ref: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
text: Ŋaan Yennu nba tee barmɔnii daanɔ na paak, n mɔsonn nba ki n daan turi na daan ki tee “Nn” nan “Aaii” kaa.

ref: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
text: Kimaan Yisa Masia nba tee Yennu Bija na, wunba ki min nan Sailas nan Timoti din mɔɔnt u po ki turi na, u ki tee “Nn” nan “Aaii” kaa, ŋaan u mɔlora kur ni tee “Mme.”

ref: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
text: Kimaan ŋɔɔe tee “Nn” Yennu mɔsona kur ni. Linba na paake te ki ti yet a “Mɔmɔmm” Yisa Masia ni, ki li dont Yennu sann.

ref: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
text: Yennue din gannit ki ti be ti kuukɔɔ, ki u senn timm nan yimm fanu Masia nie.

ref: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
text: Yennue tee n siaradaanɔ nan linba paak daan te ki n ki baar Korint i boor ni na tee n tan daa te ki i laat tubdatu.

ref: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
text: Ti kan fit mukisi ki wanni ya sa teen yada biaŋinba, kimaan ti mi nan i mɔk yada ki see fanu, ŋaan ti tuun nani a tii wanti ya sa teen biaŋinbae kii mɔk parpeenn.

ref: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
text: Li paak te ki n ji daan lor n mɔŋ par ni nan n kan baar i boor ni ki tan turi parbiiri.

ref: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
text: Li-i tee ki n baar ki tan biir i para ni, ŋmee ji sa penn n pari, see yimm nba ki n biir i para ni na.

ref: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
text: Li paake te ki n din sɔbi gbouŋ, ma n ki loon ki n baar ki tan la parbiir yimm nba bo sa penn n par ni na paak. Kimaan n teen yada nan n parpeenn tee i parpeenn nee.

ref: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
text: Ma din sɔb gbouŋ na ki turi, n din sɔbir nan parbiir bonchiann nan ninnyuut; li din ki tee a n loon ki n biir i para ni kaa, ŋaan n din loon ki yin bann loomm nba ki n mɔk ki jii jiin i poe. Nyikinchab labaar

ref: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
text: Li-i tee ki sɔɔ tun i yenɔ biit, u ki tun min kuukɔɔ kaa biiti, ŋaan u tun i kuraewa biit maŋ, kimaan ma ki loon ki n mantik jii nan ninmɔnn na paake te ki n yeen linba na.

ref: Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
text: I kure daan lakin ki dat u tubir, li paak, li ji jaŋa,

ref: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
text: ŋaan li ŋan yin nyik ki chabɔ, ŋaan kii maant u par, ki daa te ki wun baa tarrri.

ref: You should make them sure of your love for them.
text: N barimiie, ŋaant ki wun bann nan i bia daa loonɔ bonchiann.

ref: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
text: Linba na paake te ki n din sɔbin ma n laan i set saak n mɔb bonsiar kur ni.

ref: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
text: Li-i tee ki i nyik linba kur ki chab wunba, n mun nyik chabɔe na, kimaan mi-i nyik chabɔ linba na paak, i paak te ki n nyik chabɔ Masia sann ni;

ref: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
text: i daa teen Sintaanii yaaki, kimaan ti mi u sɔnii nba tee biaŋinba. Troas tingbouŋ po

ref: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
text: Ma din tan baar Troas tingbouŋ ni ma n mɔɔnt Yisa barŋanii mɔɔntii na, ki n bann nan Yennu set loot u toona sanjouk leŋa.

ref: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
text: Ŋaan ma din ki la n naa bik Taitus leŋ na paak, ki n ki la pardɔɔnn; li paak ki n chab leŋ teeb, ŋaan gar saan Masedonia. Nyannu nba be Masia ni labaar

ref: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
text: Ŋaan ti teen Yennu niipoouk, kimaan ta gaa nan Masia na paak te ki ti nyant bonsiar kur. Yennu jiit a ti fiit Masia ki wann sɔɔ kur mun; ki u barŋanii bannu tee kaman nan tulaarii nba nubir man biaŋinba nee.

ref: In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death. No one really has what it takes to do this work.
text: Binba ki la tinnu na boor ni, ti tee kaman nan nubiru nba baat nan kuun nee, ŋaan binba la tinnu boor ni ŋanimiie, ti tee kaman nan nubiru nba baat nan manfoor nee.

ref: A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
text: Ŋmee jaŋ nan toona maŋi? Tɔn, ti ki tee kaman nan binba dia Yennu mɔmaan maŋ ki li tee kpinkpouŋ turib na kaa, ŋaan ti mɔk barmɔnpaku, kimaan ŋɔɔ Yennu tunt; li paak ki ti be u tɔɔnn, ki mɔɔntir u mɔmaan Yisa sann ni.

ref: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
text: Ki linba na want nan ti bia want ti mɔŋee? Li daa tee talase nan sɔɔ-n sɔb ti siara gbant ki turii? amii ti gaar ti siara gbant i boor ni-ii? kaman nan nisiab nba tuun biaŋinba na. Aaii.

ref: But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand.
text: Yimme tee ti siara gbouŋ ki taab ti para ni a sɔɔ kur-n karin ki bann.

ref: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
text: Li dɔɔ paanu ni nan yimme tee Masia gbouŋ maŋ ki u din sɔb ki tunt nann; li tee gbouŋ nba ki nisaarik kaa jii makmɔɔnu ki sɔb tanpatlai paaki, ŋaan manfodaanɔ Yennu Seyeeŋe sɔbir nisaarii para ni.

ref: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
text: Linba paak te ki ti piak nna na tee ta teen Yennu yada Masia sann paak nee.

ref: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
text: Ti ki want ti mɔŋ nan ti sa fit tun siar nan ti mɔŋ mɔŋ paŋ kaa, ŋaan Yennu sann paak ti sa fit tun siara.

ref: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
text: Ŋɔɔe din te ki ti jaŋ nan u lorpaanu toona; ki lorpaanu maŋ ki tee Moses sennu nba ki bi din sɔb na kaa, li tee Seyeeŋ yare; kimaan sennu maŋ din baat nan kuume, ŋaan ki Yennu Seyeeŋ teent manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

ref: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
text: Yoo nba ki bi din sɔb sennu maŋ tann paak na, li yooe ki Yennu yentsaakar din sik ki nyirii Moses numm po; ki li nyiriku paak, ki Israel teeb lek din ki fit goriiɔ. Ki li-i tee ki sennu maŋ nba din baat nan kuun ki bia gaar na din baar nan yentsaakar,

ref: So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful?
text: i mi a nlee ki Seyeeŋ sɔnu kii be nan yentsaakar ki gariri?

ref: If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious?
text: Li-i tee ki sennu nba wann niib a bi mɔk biite din baar nan yentsaakar, ki Seyeeŋ sɔnu nba te ki niib tee niŋamm na be nan yentsaakar ki garir.

ref: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
text: Sennu nba din tuu be nan yentsaakar na din tan kɔŋ li yentsaakar na, kimaan yentsaakar nba yab garɔ paak.

ref: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
text: Li-i tee ki sennu nba din gar na din baar nan yentsaakar, ki Seyeeŋ sɔnu nba sii be nan yoo nba kaa gbennu, li yentsaakar sii yab bonchiann ki gar nna.

ref: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
text: Ta mɔk dindann nba na paake te ki ti mɔk parcheenn.

ref: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
text: Ŋaan ti ki tee kaman nan Moses nba din jii chinchenn ki pin u numm po a Israel teeb daa laat yentu nba be sennu nba yaa lin gar na paak kaa.

ref: The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
text: Ŋaan ki bi dudukit din ki yenti; bi-i karin Yennu gbounkper na yoonba, li tee kaman nan chinchenn maŋ bia daa dɔɔk bi dudukit nan dinna gbaa, kimaan see wunba jii u mɔŋ ki tabin nan Masiae, ki chinchenn maŋ tuu dɔkit u dudukit ni.

ref: When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over
text: Hali nan dinna, bi-i taŋŋi karin Moses sennu na, chinchenn na bia daa dɔɔk bi para ni.

ref: with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki nirɔ ŋmat ki waa ti Yomdaanɔ, chinchenn maŋ sa dɔkite.

ref: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
text: Ki ti Yomdaanɔ tee Seyeeŋ nae; ki siaminba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Seyeeŋ na be na, yentinmɔŋ be leŋe.

ref: So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
text: Li paak, chinchenn maŋ ji ki dɔɔk ti kura nunii po, ki ti laat ti Yomdaanɔ nba mɔk ŋant bonchiann biaŋinba, ki li kpintit ki ti naan nanɔ, ki ti ŋant bia pukii yoo kura, ki ŋant maŋ nyi ti Yomdaanɔ nba tee Seyeeŋ na boor nie.

ref: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
text: Yennu ninbatinu paake ki u jii toona na ki teen ti nii ni, li paake te ki ti ki mɔk kpinkpangbatuku.

ref: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
text: Li paake te ki ti yêt bunbɔnn ni toona, nan bontiteet nba baat nan fei, ti ki tuun ki dɔk niib na, ti ki lebit Yennu maan ki kpann niibi, ŋaan ti mɔŋ be Yennu tɔɔnn ki mɔɔntir u maan paanu ni nan barmɔnii, a sɔɔ daa mɔk dudukbiit ki jii jiin ti bontiteet po.

ref: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
text: Li paak, li-i tee ki Yennu barŋanii paak bɔrie, li bɔr binba kpet Yennu sɔnu ni na boor nie.

ref: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
text: Bi ki teen Masia yada, kimaan Sintaanii nba ki tingbouŋ na niib jiantirɔ na dɔkint bi dudukite. Ŋɔɔe dɔkint bi nunii po a bi daa laat yentu nba dɔkit bi paak na. Li yentu nyi barŋanii nba want Masia paŋ na boor nie; ŋɔɔe tee Yennu naauŋ.

ref: We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants.
text: Ti ki mɔɔntir ti mɔŋ mɔŋ po kaa, ŋaan ti mɔɔntir Yisa Masia poe, a ŋɔɔe tee ti Yomdaanɔ na, ŋaan want nan timme tee u toontunna ki teeni, u sann paak.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
text: Yennu nba din yet a “Yentu-n yent bunbɔnn ni” ŋɔɔe te ki u yentu na yentir ti para ni, a ti bann u yentsaakar nba tee biaŋinba; ki li yentu yeen Yisa Masia numm po.

ref: We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us.
text: Ŋaan timm nba mɔk Seyeeŋ mɔkint na tee kaman nan yɔkmaanu nee, ki li want nan paŋ nba gar paŋ kur tee Yennu yare, ŋaan li ki tee ti yari.

ref: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
text: Ti laat fara bonchiann yoo kur, ŋaan li kan fit ki nyannit; ti tuu kɔŋ sommir sianyoo, ŋaan ti ki kɔŋ dindann;

ref: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
text: bi dintit fara, ŋaan Yennu ki nyikititi; bi lut tiŋ ni, ŋaan kan fit kpiti;

ref: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
text: Yoo nba ki ti fo na ti be kuun nuu nie Yisa paak, a u manfoor nba be ti gbanant nba yaa lin tan kpo na-n nyi paanu ni.

ref: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
text: Ki kuun dia ti nii, li paak ki yimme mɔk manfopaann.

ref: In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Ma teen yada na paak te ki n pak nna,” ki ti mun mɔk Seyeeŋ nba din te ki nirɔ maŋ teen yada na, li paak, ti mun teen yada ki piak.

ref: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
text: Kimaan ti mi nan Yennu nba din fiin ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa kuun ni na, ŋɔɔe tan sa fiin timm nan yimm nba waa Yisa mun kuun ni, ki sa baar nant u mɔŋ boor ni.

ref: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
text: Kimaan linba na nba kur be a lin somm yimme, ki niib bonchiann nba tan sa la Yennu ninbatinu na, bi tan sa turɔ niipoouk bonchiann, ki dont u sann. Yada sɔnu po

ref: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
text: Li paake te ki ti ki mɔk kpinkpangbatuku; hali nan ti gbananchɔkint maŋ nɔɔk ni gbaa, ŋaan Yennu pantir ti seek daar-n tuu daar.

ref: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
text: Li paak, farmintik nba ki ti di ki li kan yukiri, li tan sa baar nan parpeenn nba yab ki kaa gbennu ki turit, ki parpeenn maŋ tan sii yab ki gar ti fardinu na bonchiann,

ref: Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
text: kimaan ti baka kaa linba ki ti laat na po kaa, ŋaan ti baka be linba ki ti ki laat na poe; kimaan linba ki ti laat na, li kan yukiri ki li tan sa gar, ŋaan linba ki ti ki laat na ŋanin tan sii be nan yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
text: Kimaan ti mi nan gbanant nba ki ti mɔk mɔtana na-i taŋŋi kpo, ti mɔk ŋei nba ki Yennu sa turit sanpaapo, ki li ki tee nifosaarik kaa maa ŋei maŋi, ki li bia tan sii be nan yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
text: Li paak, ti ji kar kpiinee ki guu daar nba ki Yennu tan sa jii ti sei ki kɔɔn ŋaak maŋ ni.

ref: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
text: Kimaan ti-i taŋŋi kɔɔ leŋ maŋi, ti sei ji kan mi ki kɔŋ binbeboori.

ref: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
text: Ŋaan ti sei nba daa woo gbanant na ni na, ti kuriie. Li ki tee a ti ji ki loon gbanant na benu kaa, ŋaan ti loon sanpaapo gbanant na, a li daar ki manfoor na-n tan nak kuun ki lin gbenne.

ref: God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it.
text: Yennue ŋammit ki ti teen siir lebitu na po, ki turit u Seyeeŋ, ki li wantit linba ki u tan sa turit na po.

ref: So always be cheerful! As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord.
text: Li paak, ti mɔk parcheenn yoo kura, ki bann nan ti sei-i be tingbouŋ na gbanant na ni, ti kaa sanpaapo ti Yomdaanɔ boor ni;

ref: But we live by faith, not by what we see.
text: kimaan ti waa yada sɔnue, ti ki waa linba ki ti ninbinn lar na kaa.

ref: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
text: Ti mɔk parcheenn ki summan tin nyi gbanant na nie ki kun ti Yomdaanɔ boor ni.

ref: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
text: Li paak, ti-i be tingbouŋ na ni, amii ti-i taŋŋi be u boor ni, ti ninbinn mɔn a ti penn u par nie.

ref: After all, Christ will judge each of us for the good or the bad that we do while living in these bodies. Bringing People to God
text: Kimaan ti kur binbeŋ tan sa nyi paanu Masia tɔɔnn po barbuut daar, ki sɔɔ kur tan sa gaar piinii kaman nan u toona nba din tee biaŋinba tingbouŋ na ni na, la tan ŋanii, la bii, li fan nana. Ta kpant Yennu yɔɔsinba Masia paak biaŋinba

ref: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
text: Ta tiin ti Yomdaanɔ biaŋinba na, li paak ki ti daak niib. Yennu mi ti binbeŋ nba be biaŋinba; ki n mɔk dindann nan i mun bia mi i para ni nan n binbeŋ nba be biaŋinba.

ref: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
text: Ti ki dont ti mɔŋ kaa i boor ni, ŋaan ti loon ki tin turi yaak ki yin fit fa parbia ti paak, ki fit pak tur binba gorii nifosaarik nanyer po ki mɔk yuugontir u paak, ŋaan ki gorii u binbeŋ ni na.

ref: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
text: Ti-i tee ninkamaŋit dammie, Yennu paake te ki ti tee nna; ŋaan ti-i munii kii tee ninkamaŋit dammi, i paake te ki ti tee nna.

ref: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
text: Masia loommu diat; ta ji bann nan ŋɔɔ niyenɔkɔɔe din gaar sɔɔ kur paak ki kpo na, li want nan sɔɔ kur din kpo nanɔe.

ref: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
text: Ŋɔɔe din gaar sɔɔ kur paak ki kpo, a binba fo na, bi kii be ki tur bi mɔŋ kaa, ŋaan bi sii be ki tur wunba din gaar bi paak ki kpo ki bia fiir kuun ni nee.

ref: We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way.
text: Li paak, ti ji ki gorii nisɔɔ nanyer po kaa; ta din got Masia nanyer po biaŋinba na, ŋaan ti ji ki goriiɔ nna kaa.

ref: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
text: Li paak, nirɔ nba be Masia ni, li yɔɔ ji tee nipaamɔɔe; u binbenkper kur ji gara; gotir man, binbenpaaŋ ji dɔkit li yɔɔ niwa.

ref: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
text: Linba na kur nyi Yennu boore. Ŋɔɔe ŋammitit Masia paak, a ti ji daa tee u datai kaa, a ŋaan ti ji sii tee u yɔɔsinbae, ki bia turit u yiini maŋ toona a ti te ki niib-n mun kpant u yɔɔsinba.

ref: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
text: Li want nan Masia nba din kpo na, ŋanne tur Yennu sɔnu a wun teen tingbouŋ na niib u yɔɔsinba, ki nyik chabib bi toonbiit, ŋaan jii u barŋanii nba wantib biaŋinba ki u teeb u yɔɔsinba na, ki teen ti nuu ni.

ref: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
text: Li paak ki ti ji tee Masia taatara ki Yennu te ki ti yi niib. Masia paak ki ti barimi a yin tunn ki teen Yennu yɔɔsinba.

ref: Christ never sinned! But God treated him as a sinner, so Christ could make us acceptable to God.
text: Kimaan ti paake ki Yennu din te ki Masia wunba ki mi tun biit na din set timm biitdamm seenu ni, a tin la yentinu binbeŋ ŋɔɔ Masia ni.

ref: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Li paak, timm nba tuun Yennu toona u sommir paak na, ti barimiie, i daa te ki u ninbatinu na tee yanni.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “When the time came, I listened to you, and when you needed help, I came to save you.” That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
text: Kimaan Yennu yet a “Yoo nba din tan jaŋ na, n din gbiint i miaru na. Ki yoo nba din tan jaŋ nan niib-n la tinnu na, n din sommiiwa.” Gbiintir man, dinnae tee yoo nba jaŋ. Dinnae ki yaak be ki niib sa la tinnu maŋ.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
text: Timm ŋanin ki tuun bonsiar linba sa gɔɔr sɔɔ Yisa Masia sɔnu ni, ki sɔɔ-n tan mann yet a ti toona na mɔk biiti.

ref: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
text: Ti binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba nae want niib nan ti set tee Yennu toontunnae, kimaan ti laat fara, nan muksuk, nan maan, ŋaan lek di sukuru li kur na ni.

ref: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
text: Niib dia kpaasa ki boot, ki loontit dansarii ni; ki ti paak ki niib fu fuut; ki ti lek tuun toona ki ki gɔɔnt nyiɔku, ki ki di sianyoo gbaa.

ref: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
text: Ti tee popeendamme, ki ti mi Yennu mɔmaan, ki mɔk sukuru nan burchint, ki gbee nan Yennu Seyeeŋ, ki loon niib,

ref: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
text: ki beerib Yennu mɔmaan nba tee barmɔnii na, ki mɔk ŋɔɔ Yennu paŋ; ki bia mɔk yentinu binbeŋ nba tee jatiat nba be ti nuudiitu ni nan nuugaŋ ni na.

ref: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
text: Nisiab teent nibaakir, ki nisiab mun sukiit, nisiab biir ti sana, ki nisiab mun piakit; nisiab yeen a ti lin kpann niibe, ŋaan ki ti piak barmɔniie.

ref: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
text: Ti ki mɔk sann niib boor ni, ŋaan ti mɔk sann Yennu boor ni. Kuun tiin waat, ŋaan ki ti lek mɔk manfoor; Yennue te ki fara baatit, ŋaan u ki te ki li kpiti.

ref: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
text: Ti mɔk parbiir ŋanin, ŋaan Yisa paak ti mɔk parpeenne yoo kura. Ti tee tariie, ŋaan timme te ki niib bonchiann tee mɔkita Yennu boor ni. Ti ki mɔk tingbouŋ na mɔkint siari, ŋaan ti mɔk linba gar mɔkita kura. Nanna kurae want nan ti set tee Yennu toontunnae.

ref: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
text: Yimm Korint doo teeb, ti poŋ beti ti nɔɔk ni barii kura. Ti looni bonchiann;

ref: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
text: li ki tee timm kaa ki looniini; yimme ki loon timm.

ref: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
text: N piak teeni kaman nan nirɔ nba bo sa pak u tiauŋ waas biaŋinba nee; yii loonin man kaman nan ma looni biaŋinba na. I mi binba ki teen Masia yada na sɔnii man

ref: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
text: I daa mɔk weinanleeb nan niib nba ki teen Yisa yada na. Linba fan nan linba ki fan sa fit lakin ki be nan leebaa? Yentu nan bunbɔnn sa fit lakin taan mɔb ki be nan leebaa?

ref: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
text: Masia nan Sintaanii sa fit taan mɔbaa? Nisɔɔ nba waa Yisa sɔnu nan wunba ki waa-r, bi sa fit taan binbenyeŋaa?

ref: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
text: Yennu diiuk nan pochianfeek tee yemmee? Aaii. Timme tee diiuk ki tur manfodaanɔ Yennu, kimaan ŋɔɔ Yennu din yet a “N sii be n niib para nie, kii somm bi sinsuuk ni. N sii tee bi Yennue, ki bi mun sii tee n niib.”

ref: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
text: Li paak ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu bia yet a “Bɔkitir i mɔŋ man, ki nyi tingbouŋ na niib sinsuuk ni man, ki nyik biit kur, ki n sa gaari.

ref: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
text: N sii tee i Baae, ki yii tee n bonjabis nan n sapaanbis. Min, i Yomdaanɔ Yennu nba mɔk paŋ kur na beeri nna.”

ref: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
text: N naa walonkai, Yennu nba senn mɔsona ki turit na paak, yi ŋaan ki ti nyik linba kur tee toonbiit, nan linba kur tee dudukbiit, ŋaan tiin Yennu, kii tee popeendamm nan barmɔnii. Pɔɔl parpeenn labaar

ref: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
text: Yii loonit man. Ti ki mi tun sɔɔ bonbiiri, amii ki tun siar ki biir sɔɔ, amii ki ŋmab sɔɔ.

ref: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
text: N ki sɔb nna a n biiri kaa. Ti lek looni, kaman nan ma poŋ beti biaŋinba na. Mi-i lekii kpo amii ma be n manfoor ni, ŋaan ti lek sii looni.

ref: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
text: N ki mɔk birsuk i paaki; n mɔk yuugontir nani, ki n par mei i paak bonchiann. Ti fardinu ni kur ŋaan n mɔk parpeenn nani.

ref: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
text: Ta din baar Masedonia ti din ki fit foi. Ti din la muksuk lokir kur po; niib din kɔn nant Yennu mɔmaan na paak; jaŋmaanii din be ti para ni,

ref: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
text: ŋaan Yennu nba maant binba mɔk parbiir na para daan te ki Taitus baar ti boor ni ki tan maan ti parawa. U baaru ti boor ni daan te ki ti para maak.

ref: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
text: Ŋann kuukɔɔ kaa daan maak ti para, ŋaan u bia daan betit i binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba; ŋanne mun daan te ki ti para maak. U daan betit a i nunbinn mɔn nan ki i bia lan, a i mɔk parbiir i toonbiit paak, a ki i nunbinn mɔn nan ki i saak n mɔb. Linba na te ki n mantik mɔk parpeenn.

ref: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
text: Ma din sɔb gbouŋ nba ki turi na, li gbouŋ din turi parbiir waaminnawa, ki li din te ki n mɔ kɔɔbia i parbiir maŋ paaka, ŋaan mɔtana n ji ki mɔ kɔɔbia li paaki,

ref: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
text: ŋaan n ji mɔk parpeenne. I parbiir maŋ paak kaa te ki n mɔk parpeenni. I parbiir nba te ki i nyik i toonbiit na paake te ki n mɔk parpeenn. Yennue din te ki i para biir i toonbiit paak, li paake te ki li gbouŋ mɔk nyɔɔt turi.

ref: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
text: Kimaan Yennu-i te ki nisɔɔ la parbiir u biit paak, ŋanne sa te ki wun nyik u biit na ki la tinnu, ki li kan mi ki biir u par jikii; ŋaan tingbouŋ na parbiir ŋanin baat nan kuume.

ref: Just look what God has done by making you feel sorry! You sincerely want to prove you are innocent. You are angry. You are shocked. You are eager to see that justice is done. You have proved that you were completely right in this matter.
text: Yennu nba te ki i la parbiir na, i mɔŋ la biaŋinba ki li turi nyɔɔta; i baka be Yennu labaar ni, ki i bia loon ki i wann nan i ki mɔk biiti; i bia tiin Yennu; i loon ki i bia lan; i bia loon ki i tun Yennu loomm; i poŋ dat biitdaanɔ na tubira. Ya tun nna maŋ na, li set want nan i ki mɔk bisiat labaar maŋ ni.

ref: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
text: Ma din sɔb gbouŋ maŋ ki turi na, li ki tee nisɔɔ nba tun toonbiit na paak kaa ki n din sɔbiri, li bia din ki tee nisɔɔ nba ki u biirɔ na paak kaa din te ki n sɔbiri. N din loon ki yin bann i baka nba be ti boor ni na biaŋinba Yennu boor, ŋanne din te ki n sɔbir.

ref: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
text: Ki ti para maak i powa. Ti bia mɔk parpeenn bonchiann, kimaan Taitus daan la pardɔɔnn i kur po, ki li daan teenɔ parpeenn, li paake te ki ti mun mɔk parpeenn.

ref: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
text: N poŋ din paki u boor niwa, ki i ki te ki n dii fei. Ta poŋ tun beeri labaar nba kur na, ti beeri barmɔniie. Nnee mun, ta din piaki Taitus boor ni na, ti din piak mɔniie.

ref: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
text: U tiar ya daan gaarɔ saauŋ biaŋinba ki baakɔ, ki bia baak wa daan beti linba na, ki sakir. Ŋanne te ki u mantik looni.

ref: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
text: N mi nan i lek bia sii ŋammit tuun linba kur fane. Li paake te ki n mɔk parpeenn bonchiann.

ref: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Ti naa waas, ti loon ki i bann Masiaweira nba be Masedonia na nba tur Yennu piinii biaŋinba.

ref: Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously.
text: Bi din la fara nan bikinn nan namu bonchiann, ŋaan bi lek mɔk parpeenne. Bi namu ni ŋaan bi din wann burchint ki tur Yennu piinii bonchiann.

ref: They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to.
text: N sa fit teen bi siara nan ba din mɔk linba, bi din turɔ nan bi paŋ terike, ki poŋ din saa gar ba bo sa turɔ biaŋinba gbaa;

ref: They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people.
text: ki din barint a tin gaar bi piinii maŋ ki tur Yennu niib nba tee tarii na. Li din tee bi yanbɔɔte nan bin turib.

ref: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
text: Bi din tur ki li gar ta poŋ din dukii a bi sa tur biaŋinba. Sinsinn bi jii bi mɔŋe ki tur ti Yomdaanɔ, ki lian fit jii bi mɔŋ ki tur timm, Yennu loomm ni.

ref: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
text: Ŋaan yimm ŋanin, Taituse daan te ki i piin ki gaan likirii ki teen Yennu. Ti din betɔ a wun yabir ki sommi ki yin tun likirii toona maŋ kur ki gbenn nan loomme.

ref: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
text: Tɔn, i teen Yisa yada, ki mi u labaar fanu, ki fit piak leeb u mɔmaan, ki waau nan ninmɔnn, ki bia loonit; ya tuun linba na kur bonchiann na, ti loon ki yii tuun toonŋana maŋ mun nnee bonchiann.

ref: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
text: Li ki tee ma yeeni linba na a n mukisi kaa a yin tur; n poŋ beti biaŋinba ki Yennu nisiab nunbinn mɔn nan bin turɔ piinii, a lin te ki n bann biaŋinba ki i mun loonɔ.

ref: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
text: I mi ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nuu nba yaa biaŋinba. U tee mɔkitɔɔe, ŋaan ki i paak ki u din kpant tarik, ki u tarik kpantu maŋ paak te ki yimm ji teen mɔkita.

ref: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
text: Yi ŋaan ki i nunbinn-ii bia mɔn piinii turu paak, kaman nan ya sa fit tur biaŋinba, ki gaar linba tenna tont.

ref: It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
text: Nisɔɔ nba loon ki wun tur Yennu piinii, ŋaan turɔ kaman nan wa mɔk biaŋinba, Yennu sa gaara. Yennu ki loon ki nirɔ-n turɔ ki gar wa mɔk linba.

ref: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
text: N ki loon ki yin tur leeb ŋaan di fara li paaki. N loon ki i kur-ii jamme.

ref: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
