text: Linba te ki n yeen na tee nnee: ya mɔk ki li yab mɔtana na, li ŋan ki yi somm binba ki mɔk na, ki li-i tee ki i mun tan ki mɔk yoonba, ŋaan ki bi mun mɔk ki li yab, bi mun sa sommiiwa. Ŋanne sa te ki i kur-ii jan nan leeb,

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
text: kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinba na a “Wunba din la bonchiann na, u din ki dii ki tenni; ki wunba la waaminna na, li din ki pɔtɔ.” Taitus nan u toontunleeb labaar

ref: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
text: Ti piak Yennu nan wa te ki Taitus baka be i ni kaman nan ti baka nba mun be i ni biaŋinba na;

ref: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
text: kimaan ta barimɔ a wun saan i boor ni na, u saka; ŋaan u mɔŋ mɔŋ numm mɔn nan wun saan i boor ni; ki u yaa wun saan nan u mɔŋ loomm.

ref: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
text: Ti tan sa te ki ti naa bisɔɔ-n weiɔ ki saan i boor ni. Ti naa bik maŋ nba mɔɔntir Masia barŋanii biaŋinba na paak te ki Masiaweira kur piakɔ bonchiann.

ref: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
text: Bi bia gannɔ a wuu waa-t ki ti somm Yennu niib nba tee tarii na. Linba nee sa dont ti Yomdaanɔ sann, ki bia wannib nan ti set loon ti leeb sommir.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift.
text: Ti tan sa jii likirii bonchiann ki saa sommib, ŋaan ti ki loon ki sɔɔ-n pak biir ti sann a ti loon li likirii na ni siari.

ref: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ mi nan ti tuun nnee nan li sɔnu, ki ti loon ki niib-n mun bann nan ti set tuun nan li sɔnue.

ref: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
text: Li paak, ti sa tun ti naa bilɔɔ mun ki wun chian Taitus nan u weinanlɔɔ ki bin tɔk baar i boor ni. Yoo kur, ti laat ti naa bik maŋ binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba, ki bann nan u numm set mɔn Yennu toona paak, kimaan u ki mɔk birsuk i paaki. Ŋanne te ki u mantik loon ki wun saan i boor ni.

ref: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
text: Nisɔɔ-i boi-i Taitus labaar po, yin betib a u tee n toontunlɔɔe wunba sommitin ki n beeri Masia barŋanii. Nisɔɔ-i boi-i ti naa waas nba waa Taitus na labaar po, yin betib a bi tee Masiaweira toontunnae, a bi binbenŋaŋ paake te ki niib dont Masia sann.

ref: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
text: Yi wann ti naa waas maŋ nan Masiaweira kura ya loomm biaŋinba, a bin bann nan ta daan piaki biaŋinba bi boor ni na, li tee mɔniie.

ref: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
text: Li ji ki kpaa talas ki n bia sɔb gbouŋ ki beti biaŋinba ki i sa somm Yennu niibi.

ref: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
text: N mi nan i loon ki yin sommib. N daan paki Masedonia teeb boor ni, ki betib biaŋinba ki yimm nba be Akaya tingbouŋ ni na din loon ki yin somm Yennu niib binnpo na. I numm nba din mɔn Yennu niib sommir paak na, li din te ki bi mun loon ki bin sommibe.

ref: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
text: N loon ki n te ki ti naa waas na-n saan i boor nie ki la nan ta piaki biaŋinba na, li set tee mɔniie; i sii be kaman nan ma yetib biaŋinba na, ki teen siir ki gaar likirii na.

ref: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
text: Nna-i kaa, ki Masedonia tiŋ ni niib maŋ tan wein ki baar i boor ni, ki sɔɔ ki i daa ki teen siiri, li sa dinnin feiwa; ki li kii tee min kuukɔɔ kaa sa di fei, ti kurae sa di fei, kimaan ti daan paki bi boor niwa.

ref: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
text: Li paak, n dukin a li ŋan ki n barin ti naa waas na ki bin dɔŋ gar n tɔɔnn po ki saan i boor ni ki saa sommi ki yin gaar likirii maŋ kaman nan ya daan senn i mɔsonn biaŋinba na, ki mi-i taŋŋi baar yoonba man la ki i poŋ gaar tonta. Ŋanne sa wann nan i gaarir nan i mɔŋ loomm nee, ŋaan ki sɔɔ kaa mia-i ki i gaari.

ref: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
text: N beerie, wunba bur dii waaminna tan sa jaan waaminnae; ki wunba mun bur dii saama mun tan sa jaan saamae.

ref: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
text: Wunba kur dukin u par ni a wun pi piinii biaŋinba, li yɔɔ-n pi nna, ki daa piin nan parbiiri, amii ki pi nan muksuku, kimaan Yennu loon nisɔɔ nba piin piinii nan parpeenne.

ref: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
text: Yennu sa fit turi bonsiar kur ki i kan kɔŋ bonsiari. I sa la ki lin gar ya loon biaŋinba, ki fit somm binba kur ki mɔk na,

ref: The Scriptures say, “God freely gives his gifts to the poor, and always does right.”
text: kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba nuu ŋanin tian ki teen tarii, u burchintuk maŋ kan mi ki gbenni.”

ref: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
text: Yennu nba teen niib diboorii ki bi buur, ŋaan bia teemm jeet ki bi di na, ŋɔɔe sa turi bont kur ki lin jaŋi ki gar, ki yin ji mantik somm niib.

ref: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
text: Li-i tee ki i sommit niib bonchiann, Yennu sa sommi bonchiann. Ya turit piinii a ti somm leeb na, li paak te ki niib bonchiann piak Yennu i piinii maŋ paak.

ref: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
text: Li-i tee ki i sommit Yennu niib ki teemm linba ki bi ki mɔk, li sa te ki niib saama-n tur Yennu niipoouk bonchiann.

ref: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
text: Li-i tee ki i sommitib, ŋanne sa wannib nan i set gaar Masia barŋanii ki sakira, ki i nuu tian ki i chentir namm nan niib kur ya mɔk linba. Ŋanne sa te ki bin dont Yennu sann,

ref: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
text: ki miarɔ ki turi, kii looni, kimaan Yennue te ki i nuu tian bonchiann.

ref: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
text: Ŋaant man ki tin tur Yennu niipoouk, kimaan ŋɔɔe turit u Bija nba gar piinii kur saaka.

ref: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
text: Tɔn, i siab yeen a ma din be i boor ni na, a n din ki mɔk par i boor ni; a ŋaan mi-i saan ki be siarpo, a n tuu mɔk pare, ki ji sɔbin gbant ki kpaani. Min Pɔɔl tiauŋ, n mɔk nibaakir ki be soonna kaman nan Masia nba be biaŋinba na, ki barimi a yin ŋamm i binbeŋ man,

ref: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
text: ki mi-i taŋŋi baar i boor ni yoonba, n kan dat i siab tuba. N mi nan mi-i baar i boor ni yoonba see ki n dat nisiab nba ya a ti waa tingbouŋ na niib nba waa linbae na tuba.

ref: We live in this world, but we don't act like its people
text: Li tee barmɔniie nan ti mun lek tee nisaariie, ŋaan ti ki kɔn kunkɔnn siar nba tee nisaarii yar kaa,

ref: or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments
text: li paak, ti ki jikit nisaarik paŋ kaa a ti kɔn li kunkɔnni, ti jikit Yennu paŋ nba sa biir Sintaanii paŋ kur nee ki kɔn. Ti piak ki nyant niib nba ki sak Yennu mɔmaan nee,

ref: and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ.
text: ki nyant niib nba mɔk karinbaanii ki dukii a bi mɔk yan ki gar Yennue, a ti te ki sɔɔ kur dudukit-n sak gaar Masia.

ref: And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
text: Ti-i bo-i mi binba be i sinsuuk ni ki sak Yennu mɔb na, ti bo sa dat binba ki sak na tubae.

ref: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
text: Yimm gorii nisaarik numm po kuukɔɔe. Nisɔɔ-i be i sinsuuk ni ki yaa a u tee Masia yɔɔe, yin te ki li yɔɔ-n tiar nan ti mun tee Masia niibe kaman nan wa tee biaŋinba na.

ref: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ din turin yudantuk na a n sommie ki yin fit weiɔ fanu, u din ki turin a n biiri kaa. Mi-i lekii nyu bara ki gar ma bo sa nyu biaŋinba, ŋaan n kan di fei.

ref: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
text: I daa dukii nan n loon ki n sɔb gbouŋ nba sa jeeniini.

ref: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
text: Nisiab yeen a n sɔbin gbant ki li naan nan pandaansɔɔ yar nee, a ŋaan ki mi-i taŋŋi baar i boor ni yoonba a n ji tuu ki mɔk paŋ, a ki n mɔbona na ji tuu tee yanne.

ref: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
text: Li ŋan ki li niib-n bann nan ta sɔbin ti gbant ni a ti sa tun biaŋinba na, ti-i taŋŋi baar yoonba na, li biaŋe ki ti set sa tun.

ref: We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves.
text: Mɔmɔmm, ti ki loon ki ti jii ti mɔŋ ki bikin nan nisiab nba dont bi mɔŋ, ki bia ki loon ki tii tee nan bi yemm na. Bi ki mɔk yami, bi gorii leeb binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinbae ki bikii nan bi mɔŋ.

ref: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
text: Ŋaan timm ŋanin, ti ki dont ti mɔŋ ki gaar ti toona nba tee biaŋinba. Ti mɔk yuugontir nan toona nba ki Yennu nyinnit ki turit na paake. Ta beeri Yennu mɔmaan nba nae tee toona maŋ yenninba.

ref: We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
text: Ti din dɔŋ siab ki baar i boor ni ki din beti Masia labaar. Ta tuun nna maŋe tee Masia nba sennit linba a tii tuun.

ref: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
text: Ti ki loon pinpakit nisiab toona paaki. Ti mɔk dindann nan i sa ŋamm teen Yisa yada bonchiann, ki sommit ki ti ŋamm tun Yennu toona bonchiann i boor kaman nan wa jiir ki turit a ti tunn biaŋinba na.

ref: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
text: Ki ti sa fit ki saan dosai nba boor fɔk ki gar i doo na, ki mɔɔnt li barŋanii. Ti kan saan siaminba ki siab poŋ din tun na, ti ki loon pinpakit nisiab toona paaki.

ref: The Scriptures say, “If you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.”
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Nirɔ nba loon parbifaant, li ŋan ki wun faa parbiir linba ki ti Yomdaanɔ tun na paake.”

ref: You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
text: Kimaan nisɔɔ nba piak u mɔŋ, li ki want nan Yennu numm gboou kaa; ti Yomdaanɔ-i piak wunba, ŋanne want nan Yennu numm gboou.

ref: Please put up with a little of my foolishness.
text: Mi-i piak jatiie, ŋaan yimm-n lek yabir i mɔŋ ki di sukuru n paak.

ref: I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ.
text: N looni bonchiann kaman nan Yennu nba looni biaŋinba na. I tee kaman nan sapaamɔ nba ki mi jɔɔ nae, ki n loon ki n jiie ki tur Masia ki yii waa ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔ.

ref: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
text: N mɔk jijeet i paak ma n mi niib tan sa mann kpanni a i ji daa waa Masia fanu, kaman nan Sintaanii nba din kpann Adam ŋaapoo Iif biaŋinba na.

ref: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
text: Nisɔɔ-i baar i boor ni ki beeri labalia nba ki tee Yisa labaar nba ki ti din beti na, bee te ki i tuu di sukuru nan li yɔɔ? Bee te ki i gaan labaar nba ki tee Yisa barŋanii; bee te ki i bia gaan seek nba ki tee Yennu Seyeeŋi?

ref: I think I am as good as any of those super apostles.
text: Niib nba kpanni a bi tee Yisa toomiinba na, n ki dukin nan bi garimi.

ref: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
text: Li-i lekii tee ki n ki mi barii paku fanu, ŋaan n mi Yennu mɔmaan na nan li sɔnu, ki yoo kur ti bia din wanni li kura paanu ni.

ref: Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge?
text: N lek sikin n mɔŋe ma n donni, kimaan ma din beti Yennu barŋanii maŋ na, n ki gaar i bonsiar li paaki. Ki ma din tun nna maŋ, n mɔk biite li paakii?

ref: I robbed other churches by taking money from them to serve you.
text: Ma din beeri Yennu mɔmaan na, Masia nisiab din tuu pan li paak, ŋaan yimme bo sa turin li sommir.

ref: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
text: Ma din be i boor ni yoonba ki ki mɔk siar na, ŋaan n lek din ki mukisi ma yin sommin nan siari. Ti naa waas nba din nyii Masedonia din baar nan bonsiar nba ki n din loon na. Ma lek din be biaŋinba li yoo na, n bia lek sii be nnee; n kan mukisi a yin sommin nan bonsiari.

ref: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
text: Mɔmɔmme ki n piak Masia paak, n kan mi mukis i sɔɔ la nyii Akaya tingbouŋ ni loksiar kura po a li yɔɔ-n sommin nan bonsiari.

ref: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
text: Bia paake? Linba na want a n ki looniee? Yennu ŋanin mi nan n looni.

ref: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
text: N lek bia sii beeri Yennu mɔmaan na, ŋaan n kan mi ki boi-i li paatii po. Li paak, kpinkpannii damm maŋ nba gaan paatii na kan fit donn bi mɔŋ ki yet a bi mun tee kaman nan timm na.

ref: Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ.
text: Li nibooru kpanniie a bi tee Masia toomiinba, ŋaan bi ki tee u toomiinba kaa. Ba tuun biaŋinba na, bi kpanniie.

ref: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
text: Bi-i tuun nna, ya daa te ki li teei yaarlituku; nnee ki Sintaanii tiauŋ nant u mɔŋ kaman nan yentu malaka na.

ref: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
text: Li paak, Sintaanii toontunna-i kpann a bi tee Yennu yabe, ya daa te ki li teei yaarlituk mani. Yennue tan sa dat bi tuba kaman nan bi toona nba jaŋib biaŋinba. Pɔɔl fardinu Masia paak labaar

ref: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
text: N bia sa ŋamm betiiwa, i daa dukii nan n tee jatuko. Li-i tee ki i lekii dukii nnaie, ŋaan yin di sukuru nanin ki n pak n mɔŋ waaminna.

ref: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
text: Ma yaa n beti linba na ki tee ti Yomdaanɔ mɔmaan kaa. Mi-i piak n mɔŋ mɔmaan, n set piak kaman nan n tee jatuk nee.

ref: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
text: Niib bonchiann want bi mɔŋe; li paak, n mun sa wann n mɔŋa.

ref: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
text: I dukii a i mɔk yame, ŋaan ki bee te ki i di jatit sukuru ki mɔk parpeenn nammi?

ref: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
text: Ma yeen linba na tee nnee: nisɔɔ-i soori ki ji-i teeni u daaba, ki ŋmab i bont, ki kpanni, ki mɔk karinbaanii, ki pebiti tanpakii, ŋaan i tuu di sukuru nan li yɔɔe.

ref: I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way. If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do.
text: Ta din ki teeni nna na paake te ki i biir ti sana a ti ki mɔk paŋ amii nlee? Nisɔɔ-i mɔk par ki want u mɔŋ, n mun sa tee nnee. N set piak kaman nan jatuk nee!

ref: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I.
text: Kpinkpannii damm maŋ-i yi bi mɔŋ Juu teebie, n mun tee Juu nirɔe; bi-i yi bi mɔŋ Israel teebie, n mun tee Israel nirɔe; bi-i tee Abraham yaaboonaie, n mun bia tee yenɔe;

ref: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
text: bi-i yi bi mɔŋ Masia toontunnaie, mine set tuun mantik; mine set tun Masia toona nan barmɔnii bonchiann, ki kɔɔ dansarii bonchiann. Niib boon kpaasa bonchiann Masia paak. N din la nunbɔŋ ki din per kuuma.

ref: Five times my own people gave me
text: Taar muŋŋmu ki Juu teeb din boon kpaasa piinntaa nan ŋannyiia.

ref: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
text: Rom teeb din boon lanbana taar munntaa. Niib din tan jaatin sianyoo tana. Ŋarint din libit chaan nanin nyun ni taar munntaa; ki yentumunn nan nyamunn ki n din be dajingbir paak mɔkgbeŋŋir nyun paak.

ref: During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
text: N din tuu somm yoo kur ki saa n sɔnii, ki laat nyun ni nunbɔŋ, ki bia tuu laat nunbɔŋ fatiteeb boor ni, nan n tiauŋ niib boor ni, nan niboorganu boor ni, ki bia laat nunbɔŋ digbangbeŋŋa ni, nan muuk ni, nan mɔkgbeŋŋa ni, nan nisiab nba kpann a bi tee Masiaweira na boor ni.

ref: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
text: N din set tuun Yisa Masia toona nan nunmɔnn, ki laat fara, ki ki gɔɔnt nyiɔku; kon nan nyunyukiru din tuu soorin; ki n kɔŋŋit jeet; ki waat son; ki n bongbabkar ki jaŋŋitimi.

ref: Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches.
text: Linba na kuukɔɔ nunbɔŋ kaa ki n din laati; n din tuu mɔk parbiire daar kura Masiaweira kur paak.

ref: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
text: Nisɔɔ-i tee yadapɔɔrin daanɔ, li mun tuu baŋ n kpinkpante; nisɔɔ-i te ki u lɔɔ tun toonbiit, li bont dont n wutoore.

ref: If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am.
text: Mi-i faa parbiir, n sii faa parbiir n kpinkpant baŋŋu paake.

ref: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Baa Yennu, wunba yen paku nan yoo nba kaa gbennu na, ŋɔɔe mi nan n piak barmɔniie;

ref: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
text: ma din be Damaskus doo ni yoonba, Bat Aretas yoo na, doo maŋ ni saakɔɔ din loon wun soorime, ki din te ki lanjimanba see doo maŋ tammɔb ni ki gorii a bin soorin.

ref: But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
text: Ŋanne ki n yɔɔsinba din jiin ki kpeenin kpanchubigbeŋŋir ni, ki din dɔɔnt sikinin takoru nba be doo maŋ joonn ni na, ki n din nyii jɔɔ maŋ nuu ni.

ref: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
text: Li kpaa talas ki mii lekii mɔk parbifaant, ŋaan li ki mɔk nyɔɔti. Mɔtana n sa beti ti Yomdaanɔ nba wannin bakitnant nba, ki kat labaar nba ki wannin.

ref: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
text: Li taar munntaae na ki n tuu tee siir ma n baar i boor ni. Ŋaan n ki yaa n baar ki loon siar kaa i boor ni, ŋaan n loon yimme, li ki tee n loon i likirii kaa. Waas ki loon bont teen bi dammi. Wadamme loon bont ki teen waas. Yimm tee n waase;

ref: I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
text: Li bakitnauŋ bont kuukɔɔ paak ki n sii mɔk parbifaant; n kan pak n mɔŋi; n sa pak n mɔŋ n nuu baŋŋu paake.

ref: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
text: Mi-i loon ki n faa parbiir, ŋaan n kii tee kaman nan jatuk na kaa, kimaan mɔmɔmm kuukɔɔe ki n kpan sii piak; ŋaan min poŋ ki loon ki n faa parbiiri. Min ki loon ki nisɔɔ-n pakin ki gar pinpakit nba fan nanin. N loon ki bin got n binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinbae, ki gbat ma piak biaŋinba, ki li poorpo ki bin bann ma tee nibooru nba.

ref: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
text: Yennu nba din wannin bakitnant nba bonchiann na, ŋaan u ki loon ki n donn n mɔŋ li paaki; li paak te ki u din te ki muksuk nyii Sintaanii boor ni ki baarin. Li muksuk yiarin kaman nan kunkonn nba tuu ŋmuu nirɔ gbanant ki li yiarɔ biaŋinba nee.

ref: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
text: N miar ti Yomdaanɔ taar munntaa ma wun nyinn li muksuk n ni;

ref: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
text: ŋaan u lek ki saki, ŋaan betin a “N sommir na sa te ki a nyann muksuk maŋa, kimaan wunba ki mɔk u mɔŋ paŋ na set sa la n paŋe.” Li paak, n sii faa parbiir n butuŋ na paak nan parpeenn, a Masia paŋ-ii be n ni.

ref: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
text: Li-i tee Masia paak ki n di butuŋ, ki laat sukit, ki di fara nan muksuk, ki laat barii, n mɔk parpeenn, kimaan mi-i taŋŋi kii mɔk n tiauŋ paŋ yoonba na, li yooe ki n laat Masia paŋ. Pɔɔl baka nba be Korint teeb ni biaŋinba

ref: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
text: Li-i bo-i tee ki yimme piakin, ŋanne bo sii ŋan; ŋaan ya ki piakin paak te ki n piak n mɔŋ kaman nan n tee jatuko na. Mi-i kii tee siar, ŋaan binba kpann a bi tee Masia toomiinba na ki garimi.

ref: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
text: Ma din be i boor ni ki tuun bakitnauŋ nyina nan toonjaana, ki di sukuru yoo kur na, ŋanne want nan n set tee Masia toomiie.

ref: You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
text: Nlee ki Masiaweileeb na cheeini? Ma ki boi-i bonsiar sommir po na, ŋanne te ki bi cheeinii? Li-i tee ŋanniie te ki n mɔk biit, yin teenin gafara man!

ref: So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much?
text: li paake te ki li maŋin nan man jii n mɔŋ ki turi, ki tun nan nunmɔnn ki sommi. Ki mi-i looni bonchiann, yimm sii loonin waaminnee?

ref: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
text: Li tee barmɔniie, n din ki mukisi a yin turin bonsiari, ŋaan ki i siab yeen a n din kpanniie ki fati. Nlee ki n din fatiini?

ref: I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
text: N din barin Taitus ma wun saan i boor ni, ki bia din te ki ti naa bisɔɔ mun weiɔ. Taitus din mi ki ŋmab i bontii? Aaii. Ti ki mɔk li dudukiti. Ma tuun biaŋinba, u mun tuun nnee. Ma waa sɔnu nba u mun waa ŋanne.

ref: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
text: I dukii a ta piak na, ti loon ki ti nyinn ti mɔŋ barii niee? Li ki tee nna kaa. Ti be Yennu tɔɔnn poe, ki piak Masia nba loon biaŋinba. Ti naa walonkai, ti loon ki ti sommie ki i wei Yennu sɔnu fanu. Ŋanne te ki ti beeri.

ref: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
text: N yan put i paake nan ma tan sa baar i boor ni ki kan lai ki i be kaman nan ma loon yii be biaŋinba na; ki i mun kan la min ki n be kaman nan ya loon mii be biaŋinba na: n mɔk jijeet nan n sa lai ki i kɔn nan i leeb, ki mɔk funfunn nan i leeb, ki dont wutoor i leeb paak, ki lukitir i leeb, ki sukii i leeb, ki biir i leeb, ki mɔk karinbaanii, ki tee kpirkpir;

ref: I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
text: n bia mɔk jijeet nan Yennu sa te ki n di fei i paak; ki n par sa biir i siab bonchiann paak, kimaan bi poŋ tun toonbiit, nan jakint toona, nan bonchonchonn toona, ki ji ki tiin fei, ki bia ki nyik bi biit maŋi.

ref: I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.”
text: N sa baar ki tan foonti ki pukin taar munntaa. Li-i tee ki i jii sɔɔ taari, n kan saki, see ki siaradamm bannlee amii banntaa pak biir li yɔɔ.

ref: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
text: Ma din baar i boor taar munnlee ni na, n din kpaan i kur nba poŋ tun toonbiit na, nan i leeb kura. Ma ji ki be i boor ni na, ŋaan n lek kpaani kaman nan ma din poŋ kpaani biaŋinba sinsinn na; mi-i taŋŋi baar, n sa dat bi tubawa.

ref: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
text: Ŋanne sa wann nan Masiae te ki n piak. U ki tee butimɔɔ kaa i sinsuuk ni, u mɔk paŋ i boor ni.

ref: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
text: U din jii u paŋ maŋe ki bakin bir ŋaan din sak ki bi kpaau dapunpunn paak. Mɔtana, Yennu paŋ paak ki u be u manfoor ni. Mɔtana, timm nba lakin be u ni na, ti ki mɔk paŋi, ŋaan ta lakin taan nanɔ na paak, ti mun mɔk manfoor Yennu paŋ paake, ki fit jii li paŋ ki wanni.

ref: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
text: Gotir i mɔŋ mɔŋ binbeŋ ni ki laan i set teen Yisa Masia yada amii i ki teeni; li kpaa talas ki yii gorii i mɔŋ binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba. I ki mi nan Yisa Masia be i niee? U set be i ni, kimaan li-i tee ki u ki be i ni, ŋann i ki tee u niib nan barmɔnii.

ref: I hope you will discover we have not failed.
text: N mɔk dindann nan i sa bann nan timm ŋanin tee u niibe nan barmɔnii.

ref: We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
text: Ti mei Yennu teeni a i daa tuun toonbiiti. Li ki tee a ti want ti mɔŋ a ti tee barmɔnii damm kaa; li-i lekii tee ki i dukii a timm ki tee Masiaweira, ŋaan ti loon ki yii tuun toonŋanae,

ref: All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it.
text: kimaan ti ki loon ki tin jiin Yennu mɔmaan poori; ti loon ki mɔmaan maŋe-n dɔŋ tɔɔnn po.

ref: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
text: Li paak, ma ki be i boor ni na, ki n sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teeni, a mi-i taŋŋi baar i boor ni yoonba, li kii kpaa talas ki n dat i tuba ki wanni n paŋ; ŋanne te ki n sɔbir. Ti Yomdaanɔe din turin yudantuk na a n sommi ki yin wei u sɔnu fanu. U din ki turin yudantuk maŋ a n biiri kaa.

ref: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
text: N naa waas, linba ji biar tee min chabie. Ŋammit i sɔnu man, kii saak n kpaanii man, kii mɔk mɔtaauk, nan parmaasir; ki Yennu nba loonit ki teent parmaasir na sii be nani.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
text: I foont i leeb kpammii man.

ref: From the apostle Paul and from all the Lord's followers with me. I was chosen to be an apostle by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised him from death. No mere human chose or appointed me to this work. To the churches in Galatia:
text: Min Pɔɔl nba tee Masia toomii, nan ti naa waas nba be nna na kur foont Masiaweira nba be Galasia tiŋ ni. Nisaarik kaa teenin Masia toomii, nisaarii kaa bia tumin, ŋaan ti Baa Yennu nan Yisa Masia nba ki u din fiinɔ kuun ni nee din gannin.

ref: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi ki bia turi parmaasir.

ref: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
text: Yisa Masiae din sak ti Baa Yennu loomm ki din kpo ti biit paak a wun fatit tingbouŋ na biit daabsin ni.

ref: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
text: Ŋɔɔ Yennu kuukɔɔe yen dontir nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm. Barŋanii yennkɔɔe be

ref: I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message,
text: Li bakitime nan ya gaan labagann na yiama, ŋaan nyik Yennu wunba din yiini Masia ninbatinu paak na.

ref: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
text: Nisiab be ki yabii bi mɔŋ a bin lebit Masia barŋanii na ki ŋmat i dudukit; ŋaan barmɔniie, barŋansiar ji kaa.

ref: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
text: Li-i lekii tee Yendɔuŋ ni malaka amii timmi mɔɔnt barŋansiar ki li be li kuukɔɔ ki ki tee ta poŋ din mɔɔnt linba turi na, mɔpor-ii be bi paak.

ref: I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
text: Ti poŋ din betiewa, ŋaan n bia ŋammit beerie mɔtana: nisɔɔ-i mɔɔnt labaar ki turi ki li ki tee linba ki i din gaar na, mɔpor-ii be u paak.

ref: I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ. How Paul Became an Apostle
text: N ki yiab ma n gaar nisaarik pinpakit kaa, ŋaan Yennu yare ki n loon, kimaan mi-i loon nisaarik pinpakitie, n bo kan teen Masia daabiri. Biaŋinba ki Pɔɔl tan teen Masia toomii

ref: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
text: N naa waas, n yaa n tiari nan barŋanii nba ki n mɔɔnt teeni na ki nyii nisaarik nba din pak linba na ni kaa.

ref: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
text: Nisaarik kaa din wannin ki n gaar, ŋaan Yisa Masia mɔŋ mɔŋe din fiit wannin.

ref: You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it.
text: I poŋ gbat ma din tuun biaŋinba yoo nba ki n din waa Juu teeb jiantu na, a n din tuu mukisir Yennu mɔtaauk niib bonchiann, ki yabii n mɔŋ ma n biirib ki gbenn.

ref: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
text: Timm Juu teeb jiantu sɔnu ni, mine din gar tɔɔnn ti waakir ni, kimaan n din jii n par kura ki waa ti yejamm kaar nba want linba.

ref: to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone.
text: Ki wa din dukin ki nyinn u Bik maŋ paanu ni ki wannin a n mɔɔnt u barŋanii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb yoonba, n din ki saan nisaarik boor ni ki saa faa sɔɔ baani.

ref: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
text: N bia din ki saan Jerusalem ki saa la binba din dɔŋ teen toomiinba nanin na, ŋaan n din fiire li taakpaak ni ki saan Arabia tiŋ ni; li poorpoe ki n ŋmat saan Damaskus doo ni.

ref: Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for
text: Ki bina ŋanntaa poorpoe ki n din tan saan Jerusalem ki foont Piita, ki din biar u boor ni daa piik nan ŋaŋŋmu.

ref: The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother.
text: Li-i kii tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa naa bik Jemsi kaa, ŋaan n din ki la toomiinba na sɔɔ.

ref: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
text: Yennu mɔŋ mi nan ma sɔb linba na tee barmɔniie ki faak kaa leŋi.

ref: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
text: Li poorpoe ki n din saan Siria nan Silisia tinii ni,

ref: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
text: ŋaan li yoo na ki Masiaweira nba be Judea tiŋ ni na din ki mimi.

ref: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
text: Ba din mi linba din tee bi leeb nba din yeen a “Jɔɔ nba din tuu mukisirit na mun ji dia barŋanii nba ki u din yaa wun biir nee ki mɔɔntir teen niib.”

ref: And because of me, they praised God.
text: Ki bi din piak Yennu n paak linba na po.

ref: Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus.
text: Tɔn, li bina piik nan ŋanna poorpoe, ki min nan Banabas din ŋmat saan Jerusalem, ki n jii Taitus mun;

ref: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
text: Yennue din fiit li sɔnu saanu paanu ni ki wannin, li paake te ki n saan. Leŋe ki n yiin saakab na, ti kuukɔɔ, ki wannib ma mɔɔntir barŋanii mɔɔntii maŋ biaŋinba ki teen binba ki tee Juu teeb na, kimaan n ki loon ma poŋ tun linba, nan ma sa tun linba na-n tan biar yann ki nyɔɔt kaa.

ref: Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised.
text: Tɔn, n tɔknanlɔɔ Taitus tee Griik nirɔe, ŋaan sɔɔ din ki miau a wun kokit u punni.

ref: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
text: Binba be ki fa faak bi mɔŋ paak a bi mun tee Masiaweira na ŋanin din loon ki Taitus-n tun nnawa. Li niib din kɔɔ ti sinsuuk ni woonin ni, a bin bann ta mɔk yentinmɔŋ nba Yisa Masia ni na. Bi loon tii tee Moses sennu daabae,

ref: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
text: ŋaan ta mun loon tin dia barŋanii nan li barmɔnii sɔnu ki turi na paak, ti ki sak turib nan waaminnawa.

ref: Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message.
text: Ŋaan binba ki bi yib saakab na, bi-i tee nijaana amii bi-i kii teeie, li ki kpaa talas min yaa boor, kimaan Yennu ki bikii nan ningannii; ŋamm nba ki bi yib saakab na din ki turin kpaanpaanii.

ref: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
text: Ŋaan ba set la linba n boor ni tee Yennu nba gannin a n mɔɔnt u barŋanii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na, kaman nan biaŋinba ki u din gann Piita a wun mɔɔnt li barŋanii ki tur Juu teeb na.

ref: God, who had sent Peter on a mission to the Jews, was now using me to preach to the Gentiles.
text: Kimaan Yennu paŋ paak, Piita nba din tee toomii ki tur Juu teeb biaŋinba na, nnee ki n mun tee toomii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na.

ref: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
text: Jems nan Piita nan Jɔɔnn mɔŋ nba ki bi yib saakab na, ŋaan bi mɔŋ din bann nan Yennue turin toongannkar na; li paak min nan Banabas nan ŋamm din foont leeb ki yokin ti leeb nii ni, ŋanne din want a ti kura mɔk mɔtaauk nan ti leeb. Timm nan ŋamm din sake a min nan Banabas-ii mɔɔntir barŋanii kii teen binba ki tee Juu teeb na, ki bi mun-ii mɔɔntir kii teen Juu teeb.

ref: They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do. Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
text: Linba yaa kuukɔɔ ki bi din betit li po din tee nnee: a ti daa tammit talasdamm nba be bi sinsuuk ni na po. Li mune ki n set poŋ din loon tumu. Min Pɔɔl nba din yakii nan Piita biaŋinba

ref: When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong.
text: Ŋanne ki n din tan yakii nan Piita wa din be Antiok yoo nba na, kimaan u din mɔk biit.

ref: He used to eat with Gentile followers of the Lord, until James sent some Jewish followers. Peter was afraid of the Jews and soon stopped eating with Gentiles.
text: Jems din tun Juu tesiab Antiok doo ni. Ba din daa ki baar yoonba na, Piita nan binba ki tee Juu teeb din lakin di jeet, ŋaan ba tan baar yoonba ki u ji ki sak a u sa di nammi, kimaan u din tiin niib nba teen punkokitu yada nee.

ref: He and the others hid their true feelings so well that even Barnabas was fooled.
text: Li paak, ki Juu leeb na mun piin tuun jaŋmaantoona, ki waa Piita nba din tun linba maŋ, ki Banabas mɔŋ mun ŋmat ki waa bi jaŋmaantoona maŋ.

ref: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
text: Ma din la ki bi ki tɔk barŋanii sɔnu maŋ fanu yoonba, ki n bet Piita bi kur numm ni ma “A tee Juu nirɔe, ŋaan a binbeŋ tee kaman nan wunba ki tee Juu nirɔ nee, ŋannii kaa, bee ki a mukisib a bii be kaman nan Juu teeb na?” Niib sa tinn yada paak

ref: We are Jews by birth and are not sinners like Gentiles.
text: Tɔn, ti tee Juu teeb matiie, ti ki tee niboorgamm nba ki bi yib biitdamm na kaa,

ref: When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't!
text: Li-i bo-i kii tee nnaie, ŋann ŋanin ti-i yabii a Yennu-n yet a ti tee niŋamm u boor Masia paak, ŋaan ti lek bia tee biitdamme kaman nan binba ki tee Juu teeb na. Linba na want nan Masia ki kɔn toonbiit paak amii? Aaii, li ki want nna kaa.

ref: But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first.
text: Kimaan mi-i jiŋŋi piin ki sent sennu sɔnu nba ki n din biira naie, ŋann want nan n mɔŋ tee sennu maŋ biirtɔɔe na.

ref: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
text: Ŋaan sennu na paak ki n kpo sennu maŋ boor, a mii mɔk manfoor Yennu boor. Li tee kaman nan bi din taan kpaa min nan Masia dapunpunn paak nee.

ref: I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me.
text: Li paak, li ji ki tee min nba fo na kaa, ŋaan li tee Masia nba fo ki be n ni nee. Mɔtana, ma mɔk manfoor ki be n gbanant ni na, n be ki teen Yennu Bija Masia yada; ŋɔɔe loonin ki din gaar n paak ki kpo.

ref: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
text: N ki te ki Yennu ninbatinu teen yanni; li-i tee ki Yennu bo sa yet a nirɔ nba sak sennu nae tee niŋanɔ u boor, li bo want nan Masia kuun maŋ tee yanne.

ref: You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you?
text: Yimm Galasia teeb, bonjatana! Yimm nba poŋ mi Yisa Masia dapunpunn paak kuun po nyik nyik na, ŋmee jin tan kpanniini?

ref: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
text: I betin bonyennkɔɔ man, ya din tuun sennu na toona paake din te ki i gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ amii ya din gbat barŋanii na ki teen yada na paake din te ki i gaarɔ?

ref: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
text: Bee te ki i ki mɔk yami? I din piin Masiaweiu nan Yennu Seyeeŋ paŋe; bee ki i ji tan loon yin gbenn nan i mɔŋ mɔŋ paŋi?

ref: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
text: Ya din la Yennu yaarlituk toona nba na kura kaa nyɔɔt nan waama i boorii? N dukin nyɔɔt be li paak.

ref: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
text: Ya din tuun sennu toona na paake te ki Yennu turi Seyeeŋ ki tuun yaarlituk toona i sinsuuk ni amii ya gbat barŋanii ki teen yada na paake?

ref: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
text: Ŋanne want nan niib nba teen Yisa yada na, ŋamme tee Abraham yaaboona.

ref: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
text: Ki li bia sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a ŋɔɔ Yennu din poŋ mi nan u tan sa yet a binba ki tee Juu teeb mun tee niŋamm u boor bi yada paak. Ŋann paake ki u poŋ din yet Abraham barŋanii maŋ a “Fin paake ki n tan sa teen mɔsonŋann nisaarik booru kura paak.”

ref: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
text: Li paak, Yennu tee mɔsonŋann binba teenɔ yada na paak, kaman nan wa din teen mɔsonŋann Abraham nba din teenɔ yada na paak.

ref: Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.”
text: Binba waa sennu ki dukii a sennu na sa tinnib na, Yennu mɔtomm be bi paak, kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba ki waa sennu kur nba sɔb gbouŋ maŋ ni na, Yennu mɔtomm be u paak.”

ref: The Law isn't based on faith. It promises life only to people who obey its commands.
text: Ŋaan sennu dianu nan yada teenu bɔkita; kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Daanɔ nba waa sennu kur fanu sii mɔk manfoor.”

ref: But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse.
text: Ŋaan ti din be Yennu mɔtomm nie, kimaan ti kan fit wei sennu fanu, ŋaan Masia tiauŋo din gaar ti paak ki din be mɔtomm maŋ ni ki nyinnit li mɔtomm ni, kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba ki bi kpaau dapunpunn paak na, Yennu mɔtomm be u paak.”

ref: And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit. The Law and the Promise
text: Yisa Masia din tun linba na a ŋɔɔ yaa paak ki binba ki tee Juu teeb-n mun la mɔsonŋann nba ki Yennu din senn Abraham paak; a ti-i teen yada tin gaar Seyeeŋ nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn a wun turit na. Sennu nan mɔsonn labaar

ref: My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement.
text: N naa waas, ŋaant ki n jii nisaarik nan u lɔɔ binbeŋ ki bikin ki yin bann linba ki n beeri na paak: niib bannlee-i sak lor a bin tun siar ki pot mɔɔnu nan leeb, bi yenɔkɔɔ ji kan fit nyi u lɔɔ na poorpo nan waama.

ref: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
text: Yennu nba din tur Abraham nan u yaaboonn u mɔsona maŋ yoonba, u din ki yet a “A yaaboona” kaa, ŋaan u din yet a “A yaaboonn yenɔkɔɔ,” wunba tee Masia.

ref: What I am saying is that the Law cannot change or cancel God's promise made
text: Ma want linba tee nnee: Yennu din senn mɔsonjaann; ki sennu nba din tan baar bina kobii ŋanna nan piinntaa poorpo na, li din kan fit kpeen Yennu mɔsonn maŋi.

ref: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
text: Kimaan li-i tee sennu dianu paake ki Yennu teen niib u piinii, ŋann li wann nan u bo kan sak tur sɔɔ piinii u mɔsonn paak; ŋaan li din tee Yennu mɔsonn paake ki u tur Abraham u piinii maŋ.

ref: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
text: Li-i bo-i tee nnaie, sennu na nyɔɔt tee bee, ŋann amii? toonbiit paake, ki Yennu din tur nifosaarii sennu na ki pukin u mɔsonn na paak, ki din loon a sennu maŋ-ii diab mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Abraham yaaboonn nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki turɔ na tan baar. Ki malakanba din jii sennu maŋ ki teen nisaarik nuu ni a u tee sɔkintɔɔ,

ref: There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him. Slaves and Children
text: ŋaan Yennu tiauŋo din tur Abraham u mɔsonn maŋ, li din ki tee sɔkintɔɔ kaa turɔ. Sennu nyɔɔt labaar

ref: Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law.
text: Tɔn, ŋanne want nan sennu na kɔn nan Yennu mɔsona-aa? Aaii, li ki want nna kaa; ŋaan Yennu-i bo-i senn sennu nba sa fit teen nirɔ manfoor, u bo sa yet a niib tee niŋamm u boor sennu maŋ weiu paaka.

ref: But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
text: Ŋaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a sɔɔ kur saaka mɔk biite, a Yennu-n tur binba teen Yisa Masia yada na piinii nba ki u din senn mɔsonn a u sa turib na; bi yada maŋ paake ki u sa turib.

ref: The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith.
text: Sennu na din tuu diat kaman nan dansarii nee ki tan baar yoo nba ki Yennu fiit yada teenu labaar ki wannit na.

ref: In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God.
text: Li paak, sennu na din ŋman ti yomdaanɔ nba diat mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Masia tan baar a tin teenɔ yada ki Yennu-n yet a ti tee niŋamm u boor nee.

ref: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
text: Ŋaan mɔtanae ti teen Masia yada; li paak, sennu ji ki tee ti yomdaanɔ.

ref: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Ya teen Yisa Masia yada ki be u ni na, ŋann paake ki i tee Yennu waas.

ref: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
text: Yimm nba ki bi din wuri Yennu nyunwuru Masia sann ni na, yimme gaar u mɔŋ mɔŋ binbeŋ.

ref: Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman.
text: Li paak, bɔkitu ji ki be binba tee Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb sinsuuk ni, li bia ki be binba tee daaba nan yentinmɔŋ damm sinsuuk ni, li ki be poob nan jab sinsuuk ni; ya be Yisa Masia ni na paake te ki i kura tee yenɔkɔɔ.

ref: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
text: Ki i-i tee Masia yabie, ŋann i tee Abraham yaaboonae, ki tan sa gaar piinii nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn a u sa turib na.

ref: Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs.
text: Tɔn, n bia ŋamm yeen a ŋaak bik nba lek tuu ya wun tan di u baa faar na, ŋaan yoo nba ki u daa tee bonbunn na, bi daa tuu diau kaman nan daabir nee.

ref: This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set.
text: Yoo nba ki u tee bonbunn na, nisiabe tuu diau ki wubiɔ mɔkmɔk nan wa tan sa beer jɔɔ, ki jaŋ yoo nba ki u baa din senn na.

ref: This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
text: Ti mun tee nnee; yoo nba ki ti din tee kaman nan bonbunna Yennu sɔnu ni, dunia nna seek paŋe din tuu diat.

ref: But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law,
text: Ŋaan yoo nba din tan jaŋ na, ki Yennu tun u mɔŋ mɔŋ Bija, ki poo din marɔ, ki u din be Juu teeb sennu ni,

ref: so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children.
text: a wun tinn binba din tuu be sennu maŋ ni na, a tin kpant Yennu waas.

ref: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
text: Ta tee u waas na paak, ki u tun u Bija maŋ Seyeeŋ na ki u be ti para ni, ki ti yi ŋɔɔ Yennu a “N Baa, n Baa.”

ref: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
text: Li paak, a ji ki tee daabir kaa, ŋaan a ji tee Yennu bike; ki fa tee u bik na paak, u mun sa tura linba kur ki u mɔk ki teen u waas. Pɔɔl baka nba be Galasia teeb ni biaŋinba

ref: Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real.
text: Sianyoo i din tuu ki mi Yennu, li yoo i din tuu tee daaba ki tur linba set ki tee Yennue,

ref: But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers?
text: ŋaan mɔtana i bann Yennuwa, ki Yennu mun mi-i, li jin teen nlee ki i loon ki i ŋmat tunn ki wei bonsiar nba ki mɔk yaak nba sa sommi na boor ni? Bee teen ki i jin loon ki i bia kpant bi daaba?

ref: I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
text: Ŋanne te ki n mɔk jijeet i paak nan ma tun linba kur turi na biar yanne na.

ref: My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me
text: N naa waas, n barimiie, yii naan kaman nan min na man, kimaan n mɔŋ mun ji naan kaman nan yimm nee. I ki tun siar biirimi.

ref: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
text: Ma din yiar na paake din te ki n mɔɔnt barŋanii sinsinn ki turi.

ref: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
text: Li yoo maŋ ki n gbanant din ki maŋi, ŋaan i lek din ki yêtimi; i lek din gaarin kaman nan ya din bo sa gaar Yennu malaka biaŋinba nee, ki bia gaarin kaman nan ya din bo sa gaar Yisa Masia biaŋinba na.

ref: Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me.
text: I din mɔk parpeenn; ki bee te ki i ji ki mɔki? N mɔŋ sa yet nan li-i diŋŋi bo-i sa fit, i din bo sa sak ki nyinn i mɔŋ ninbina ki turima;

ref: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
text: ki mɔtana, ma beeri barmɔnii na, n ji kpant i dataakee?

ref: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
text: Nisiab bikii a bi mɔk loomm bonchiann i paak, ŋaan bi dudukit ki ŋani. Bi lorin a bin bɔkit min nan yimme, ki yin tunn kii waa bi kuukɔɔ.

ref: It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you.
text: Nirɔ baka-i be sɔnŋanu ni yoo saaka, li ŋan, mi-i lekii be i boor ni amii mi-i kaa.

ref: My children, I am in terrible pain until Christ may be seen living in you.
text: N walonkai, li yiarin bonchiann i paak mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Masia binbeŋ na tan sa gbee i ni.

ref: I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled. Hagar and Sarah
text: N yan ni ŋmat i paake. N bo summan ki n be i boor ni mɔtana ki lek ŋamm jii n binbeŋ nba sɔ ki turi. Bibiiuk nba jiin Sara nan Hagar po

ref: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
text: Yimm nba loon ki i be sennu ni na, i ki gbat sennu maŋ nba yet biaŋinba-aa?

ref: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
text: a Abraham din mɔk bonjai bannlee; ki bi yenɔ naa din tee daabire, ki lɔɔ naa mun din tee pookuur poo.

ref: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
text: Ki daabir na din mar u bonjabik kaman nan niib nba maar biaŋinba nee, ŋaan ki pookuur poo na mar u bonjabik kaman nan biaŋinba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn turɔ na.

ref: All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave.
text: Bi poob bannlee na see Yennu mɔlora ŋannlee na setu nee. Daabir maŋ sann din tee Hagar; ki u see mɔlor nba ki Yennu senn Sainai jɔɔr paak na setu ni, ki din mar u bik daabsin ni,

ref: Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
text: kimaan Hagar see Sainai jɔɔr nba be Arabia tiŋ ni setu nee, ki ŋman nan tingbouŋ na ni Jerusalem na, ki ŋɔɔ nan u waas tee daaba,

ref: But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave.
text: ŋaan sanpaapo Jerusalem na tee yentinmɔŋ doo, ki bia tee ti naa;

ref: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
text: kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yimm pookanmata, yii mɔk parpeenn! Yimm nba ki mi matyiaru tonu na yii yiin parpeenn yanii man! Kimaan poo nba be u kuukɔɔ tan sii mɔk waas bonchiann ki gar poo nba sɔrɔ daa ki mi gɔrin nyikɔ.”

ref: My friends, you were born because of this promise, just as Isaac was.
text: Tɔn, n naa waas, timme mun tee Yennu mɔsonn waas kaman nan Aisak nba din tee biaŋinba na.

ref: But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today.
text: Ba din mar bonjabik nba kaman nan niib nba maar biaŋinba na din tuu mukis wunba ki bi din marɔ nan Seyeeŋ yikoo ni, ki li mun bia lek tee nnee nan mɔtanawa.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.”
text: Ŋaan li sɔb nlee Yennu gbouŋ ni? Li sɔb a “Berin poo nba tee daabir na nan u bonjabik na, kimaan daabir bonjabik na daa mi lakin di bi baa maŋ faar nan pookuur poo bonjabik na.”

ref: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
text: N naa waas, ma yeen linba tee a ti ki tee poo nba tee daabir na waas kaa, ŋaan ti tee pookuur poo na waase.

ref: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
text: Yisa Masia poŋ fat timma, ki ti be ti yama nie. Li paak, i daa te ki siar ji dia-i ki i tee li daabir.

ref: I, Paul, promise you that Christ won't do you any good if you get circumcised.
text: Min Pɔɔl mɔŋ mɔŋ beeri a li-i tee ki i sak ki dia punkokitu sennu na, li want nan Masia ki mɔk nyɔsiat turiini.

ref: If you do, you must obey the whole Law.
text: N ŋammit beer nifosɔɔ nba waa punkokitu sɔnu nee, nan li kpaa talase ki wun wei sennu kura.

ref: And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Yimm nba yabii a Yennu-n yet a i tee niŋamm u boor sennu weiu paak na, i poŋ bɔkit i mɔŋ nan Masia, ki bia nyinn i mɔŋ Yennu ninbatinu ni.

ref: But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ.
text: Ŋaan timm teen Masia yada, ki u Seyeeŋ mun be ti ni; li paak, ti mɔk dindann ki guu yoo nba ki Yennu sa yet a ti tee niŋamm u boor.

ref: If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
text: Kimaan ti-i be Yisa Masia ni, ti-i lekii pot ti puna amii ti-i kii poti, li ki kpaa talasi. Ti-i teen Yisa Masia yada ki loon leeb, ŋanne tee linba kpaa talas.

ref: You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth.
text: Ŋaan i din tuun fanu na, ki ŋmee tan te ki i nyik barmɔnii saku?

ref: And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you.
text: Yennu nba ganni na kaa tun linba na.

ref: A little yeast can change a whole batch of dough,
text: Bi tuu yeen a “Datiŋ waaminnee te ki boroborkunn fikitir piir.”

ref: but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
text: Ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ paak, n bia mɔk yada a i sii mɔk dudukyenn nanin; ki li-i tee nirɔ nbae ŋmat i dudukit, Yennu sa dat daanɔ maŋ tubira.

ref: My friends, if I still preach that people need to be circumcised, why am I in so much trouble? The message about the cross would no longer be a problem, if I told people to be circumcised.
text: N naa waas, mi-i bia lekii mɔɔntir a niib-ii porii bi puna gbaa, nlee ki bi bia sii mukisimi? Li-i bo-i tee barmɔniie ki n mɔɔntir punpotu po nan ma mɔɔntir dapunpunn po na, ŋann li bo kii mɔk muksuku.

ref: I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
text: Min bo summan ki binba ŋmat i dudukit na-n nyik punpotue, ŋaan tɔɔ bi mɔŋ lana.

ref: My friends, you were chosen to be free. So don't use your freedom as an excuse to do anything you want. Use it as an opportunity to serve each other with love.
text: N naa waas, Yennu yiini yentinmɔŋ nie, ŋaan i daa te ki i yentinmɔŋ maŋ tan te ki i niauŋ-n tan mann nyanniini. Ŋaan yimm-n jii loomm ki somm i leeb man.

ref: All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself.
text: Kimaan sennu maŋ yur yet a “I loon a lɔɔ kaman nan fa loon a mɔŋ biaŋinba na.”

ref: But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves. God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki i tee i leeb danii ki biir i leeb kaman nan muuk ni bonkobit na, i set sa baan i leeb yann na. Yennu Seyeeŋ nan nisaarik binbeŋ labaar

ref: If you are guided by the Spirit, you won't obey your selfish desires.
text: Ŋaan n beerie, ŋaant ki Seyeeŋ-ii dia i binbeŋ man, ki daa te ki i niauŋ tuun nisaarik loommu.

ref: The Spirit and your desires are enemies of each other. They are always fighting each other and keeping you from doing what you feel you should.
text: Kimaan nisaarik loomm nan Seyeeŋ maŋ loomm ki waa leebi. Loomm-nba taar munnlee na tee dataie nan leeb i ni; li paak, i kan fit tun ya loon linba fanu.

ref: But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
text: Li-i tee ki i saak ki Yennu Seyeeŋ ŋmakitiri sɔnu, i ji ki be sennu maŋ ni.

ref: People's desires make them give in to immoral ways, filthy thoughts, and shameful deeds.
text: Nisaarik binbeŋ nba want li mɔŋ linba ni tee bonchonchonn toonae, nan jakint toona, nan lanmiok toona,

ref: They worship idols, practice witchcraft, hate others, and are hard to get along with. People become jealous, angry, and selfish. They not only argue and cause trouble, but they are
text: nan patmannu, nan nyɔkdinu, nan dataar, nan kunkɔna, nan funfunbiiuk, nan wutoor, nan bɔkitu, nan lukitin,

ref: envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
text: nan punponn, nan dayibu, nan bandakir, ki bia jii bonlia nba naan nna ki pukin; n ji beeri kaman nan ma poŋ beti biaŋinba na, binba tuun linba na kan mi di Yennu naami.

ref: God's Spirit makes us loving, happy, peaceful, patient, kind, good, faithful,
text: Ŋaan Yennu Seyeeŋe te ki nirɔ mɔk loomm, nan parpeenn, nan parmaasir, nan sukuru, nan burchint, nan ŋamm, nan yada,

ref: gentle, and self-controlled. There is no law against behaving in any of these ways.
text: nan sikininmɔŋ, nan mɔŋdianu. Sennsau ji kaa ki sa biir wunba diari.

ref: And because we belong to Christ Jesus, we have killed our selfish feelings and desires.
text: Ki Yisa Masia nba yen binba na ŋanin poŋ din kpii bi nisaarik binbeŋ nan li loonbiit kura.

ref: God's Spirit has given us life, and so we should follow the Spirit.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ nba turit manfoor na, ŋɔɔe ki li ŋan tii waau.

ref: But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
text: Li ki ŋan ki tii mɔk karinbaanii, amii tii fiint ti leeb wutoa, amii tii mɔk funfunbiit nan ti leebi.

ref: My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself.
text: N naa waas, li-i tee ki i la ki sɔɔ tun toonbiit, yimm nba be nan Yennu Seyeeŋ na, li ŋan ki yin sommɔ nan loomm sɔnii kur ni, ki wun ŋamm set fanu, kii mi ki bikinu sa te ki i mun bot.

ref: You obey the law of Christ when you offer each other a helping hand.
text: Yi jii i leeb jikpiata man, ki sak Masia sennu.

ref: If you think you are better than others, when you really aren't, you are wrong.
text: Sɔɔ-i kii tee siar, ŋaan bikii a u mun tee sɔɔe, u kpann u mɔŋe.

ref: Do your own work well, and then you will have something to be proud of. But don't compare yourself with others.
text: Li ŋan ki sɔɔ kur-n bikin u mɔŋ binbeŋa, ki fit kii mɔk yuugontir u mɔŋ mɔŋ toonŋana paak, ki daa bikii wa tun linba nan u lɔɔ toona,

ref: We each must carry our own load.
text: kimaan li kpaa talase nan sɔɔ kur-n tun u mɔŋ toona.

ref: Share every good thing you have with anyone who teaches you what God has said.
text: Nisɔɔ nba ki bi wantɔ Yisa Masia barŋanii na, li ŋan ki li yɔɔ-n mun jii linba ki u mɔk kur ki chent nan u wanntɔɔ maŋ.

ref: You cannot fool God, so don't make a fool of yourself! You will harvest what you plant.
text: I daa kpann i mɔŋ mani; sɔɔ kan fit kpann Yennu; nisaarik nba bur linba, ŋanne ki u tan sa jaan kpaŋ.

ref: If you follow your selfish desires, you will harvest destruction, but if you follow the Spirit, you will harvest eternal life.
text: Wunba bur u mɔŋ gbanant loomm, u tan sa jaan kuume u gbanant loonbiit maŋ paak; ŋaan wunba bur Seyeeŋ nba loon biaŋinba na, u tan sa jaan manfoor nba kaa gbennu, Seyeeŋ maŋ paak.

ref: Don't get tired of helping others. You will be rewarded when the time is right, if you don't give up.
text: Li paak, ti daa bakit nan toonŋana tumu mani, kimaan ti-i kii nyik toonŋana, yoo nba jaŋ na ki ti tan sa gaar li nyɔɔt.

ref: We should help people whenever we can, especially if they are followers of the Lord. Final Warnings
text: Li paak, ti-i la yaak, ŋaant ki tin tun linba ŋan ki tur sɔɔ kura; ŋaan mantik kii tuun kii teen binba teen Masia yada ki tee u weira na. Kpaanjoontik nan foontii labaar

ref: You can see what big letters I make when I write with my own hand.
text: Tɔn, i la ma sɔb sɔbin gbeŋŋa na nan n mɔŋ mɔŋ nuu.

ref: Those people who are telling you to get circumcised are only trying to show how important they are. And they don't want to get into trouble for preaching about the cross of Christ.
text: Niib nba loon bin penn Juu teeb para na, ŋamme mukisi a yin pot i puna, a Juu teeb maŋ daa dintib fara Masia dapunpunn na paaki.

ref: They are circumcised, but they don't obey the Law of Moses. All they want is to brag about having you circumcised.
text: Binba porii bi puna na mɔŋ ki waa Yennu sennu, ŋaan bi loon ki yin pote ki bii faa japaat i punpotu maŋ paak;

ref: But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of his cross, the world is dead as far as I am concerned, and I am dead as far as the world is concerned.
text: ŋaan min ŋanin kan mi faa japaat see ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia dapunpunn na kuukɔɔ paak; kimaan u dapunpunn maŋ paake te ki n kpii tingbouŋ na niauŋ n gbanant ni, ki tingbouŋ na niauŋ mun nyii n gbanant ni.

ref: It doesn't matter if you are circumcised or not. All that matters is that you are a new person.
text: Bɔkitu kaa wunba pot u punn nan wunba ki pot u punn sinsuuk ni. Linba ji kpaa talas tee binbenpaaŋ nba ki Yennu turit nee.

ref: If you follow this rule, you will belong to God's true people. God will treat you with undeserved kindness and will bless you with peace.
text: Parmaasir nan ninbatinu-ii be binba kur waa wannu nba na paak; bi tee Yennu niibe.

ref: On my own body are scars that prove I belong to Christ Jesus. So I don't want anyone to bother me anymore.
text: I mi man ki sɔɔ-n ji turin farsiar, kimaan n poŋ mɔk fara dɔkit n gbanant paak, ki li want nan n tee Yisa daabire.

ref: My friends, I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you! Amen.
text: N naa waas, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, i para ni. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus. To God's people who live in Ephesus and are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
text: Min Pɔɔl wunba ki Yennu gannin a n teen Yisa Masia toomii na foont Yisa Masiaweira nba teenɔ yada ki be Efesus doo ni.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Christ Brings Spiritual Blessings
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir. Yennu Seyeeŋ nba sommitit Masia paak biaŋinba

ref: Praise the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ for the spiritual blessings that Christ has brought us from heaven!
text: Ŋaant ki ti tur Yennu wunba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Baa na niipoouk man, kimaan ta be Yisa Masia ni na paak ki ŋɔɔ Yennu turit Seyeeŋ piinii kur nba be sanpaapo.

ref: Before the world was created, God had Christ choose us to live with him and to be his holy and innocent and loving people.
text: Yennu nba poŋ daa din ki nan tingbouŋ yoonba nawa ki u din gannit a tii tee u niib Yisa Masia paak, kii tee popeendamm ki daa mɔk biit u boor ni. Kimaan Yennu loonit,

ref: God was kind and decided that Christ would choose us to be God's own adopted children.
text: li paak, u loomm nan u lora ni u poŋ din dukinewa a Yisa Masia nie ki u sa teenit u waas.

ref: God treated us with undeserved grace because of the Son he dearly loves, and so we should praise God.
text: Ŋaant ki tin pak Yennu kimaan u ninbatinu nba yab bonchiann biaŋinba na, nan wa tint ninbaauk u Bilonkak na paak.

ref: Christ sacrificed his life's blood to set us free, which means our sins are now forgiven. Christ did this because of God's gift of undeserved grace to us. God has great wisdom and understanding,
text: Gotir, Yennu ninbatinu nba be ti paak ki ki mɔk bikinu na. Yennu nba mɔk yanŋann nan bannu bonchiann biaŋinba na ki u din tun wa poŋ dukin biaŋinba

ref: and by what Christ has done, God has shown us his own mysterious ways.
text: ki fiit wannit woonin ni lora nba ki u din senn a wun tan tun Masia ni na.

ref: Then when the time is right, God will do all he has planned, and Christ will bring together everything in heaven and on earth.
text: Yoo maŋ-i taŋŋi jaŋ chib, ki u ji sa tun gbenn u lora maŋ, ki lakin u naamii kur sanpaapo nan tiŋ ni ki Masia-ii tee yudaanɔ.

ref: God always does what he plans, and this is why he appointed Christ to choose us.
text: Yennu tun siar kur u dudukit nan u lora nee, ki gannit a tii tee u niib, kaman nan u bibikit nba din tee biaŋinba pinpiik niwa na.

ref: He did this so we Jews would bring honor to him and be the first ones to have hope because of him.
text: Timm nba dɔŋ mɔk dindann Masia ni na, tii piak Yennu kii dontir u sann man.

ref: Christ also brought you the truth, which is the good news about how you can be saved. You put your faith in Christ and were given the promised Holy Spirit to show you belong to God.
text: Ŋaan yimme mun din gbat tinnu barŋanii nba tee barmɔnii maŋ ki teen Yisa Masia niib. Ya teen Masia yada na paak ki Yennu jii u Seyeeŋ nba tee u yent dɔk na turi nan wa din lor biaŋinba na.

ref: The Spirit also makes us sure we will be given what God has stored up for his people. Then we will be set free, and God will be honored and praised. Paul's Prayer
text: Ŋanne tee siara nan ti tan sa la ti yiar Yennu boor, ki bia want nan u sa tur u niib yentinmɔŋ nan barmɔnii. Tii pakɔ kii dontir u sann man. Pɔɔl Yenmiaru labaar

ref: I have heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people.
text: Li paak, yoo nba ki n din gbat ya teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa yada na nan ya loon Yennu niib biaŋinba na po mɔkmɔk nan dinna,

ref: So I never stop being grateful for you, as I mention you in my prayers.
text: n teen Yennu niipoouk i paak ki ki nyiki. N tuu tian i poe n miaru ni,

ref: I ask the glorious Father and God of our Lord Jesus Christ to give you his Spirit. The Spirit will make you wise and let you understand what it means to know God.
text: ki lek mei ti Baa Yennu wunba tee paŋ daanɔ ki bia tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Yennu, a wun turi Seyeeŋ nba sa turi yan ki fiit Yennu wanni kii ŋamm bannɔ.

ref: My prayer is that light will flood your hearts and you will understand the hope given to you when God chose you. Then you will discover the glorious blessings that will be yours together with all God's people.
text: N bia meiɔ ma wun yent i nummpo ki yin bann dindann nba paak ki u yiini na, ki bia bann nan bona nba ki u tan sa tur u niib nae ŋan bonchiann,

ref: I want you to know about the great and mighty power that God has for us followers. It is the same wonderful power he used
text: ki bia bann wa tuun toonjaana nba teen timm nba teenɔ yada na.

ref: when he raised Christ from death and let him sit at his right side in heaven.
text: U panchiauŋ toonbooru nba ki u din jii ki fiin Masia kuun ni ki kaanɔ Yendɔuŋ ni u niidiitu po nee, ki u bia tuun ti ni na.

ref: There Christ rules over all forces, authorities, powers, and rulers. He rules over all beings in this world and will rule in the future world as well.
text: Masiae yen sanpaapo yent damm, nan yikoo damm, nan paŋ damm, nan yomdamm kur; ŋɔɔe mɔk sann nba gar yikoo kur sana nba be dunia na ni nan dunia nba baat na ni.

ref: God has put all things under the power of Christ, and for the good of the church he has made him the head of everything.
text: Yennu jii bonsiar kura yaak ki teen Masia nuu nee. U jii ŋɔɔe a wuu tee Yennu nigannkab kura Yomdaanɔ.

ref: The church is the body of Christ and is filled with Christ who completely fills everything.
text: U nigannkab maŋe tee u gbanant. Ŋamme gbee u gbanant maŋ ni, ki u mun jii piinii kur ki gbeen bi ni.

ref: In the past you were dead because you sinned and fought against God.
text: Yoo nba din gar na, i mɔyêtuk nan biit paak, li want nan i kpoe.

ref: You followed the ways of this world and obeyed the devil. He rules the world, and his spirit has power over everyone who doesn't obey God.
text: I din waa tingbouŋ na narinbiit sɔnue ki sak tingbouŋ na bat Sintaanii nba dia niib nba ki saak Yennu mɔb na.

ref: Once we were also ruled by the selfish desires of our bodies and minds. We had made God angry, and we were going to be punished like everyone else.
text: Barmɔnii, ti kura mun din naan kaman nan li nibooru nee, ki waa ti gbanant niauŋ, ki tuun ti gbanant nan ti dudukit loomm. Timm nan ŋamm toona din baat nan Yennu wutoore ti kura paak.

ref: But God was merciful! We were dead because of our sins, but God loved us so much he made us alive with Christ, and God's gift of undeserved grace is what saves you.
text: li paak, ta din yêt Yennu mɔb ki li want nan ti kpoe na yoonba, li yoo na, ki u din nyinnit kuun ni, ki kɔɔnit manfoor ni Masia paak; Yennu ninbatinu paak ki i tinn na.

ref: God raised us from death to life with Christ Jesus, and he has given us a place beside Christ in heaven.
text: Ta be Yisa Masia ni na paak ki Yennu din lakin fiin timm nan ŋɔɔ, ki ti lakin dii naan nanɔ Yendɔuŋ ni.

ref: God did this so in the future world he could show how truly good and kind he is to us because of what Christ Jesus has done.
text: U din tun linba na a wun wann sɔɔ kura u ninbatinu nba yab biaŋinbae, ki tun burchint turit Yisa Masia paak.

ref: It isn't something you have earned, so there is nothing you can brag about.
text: Sɔɔ kan fit nyu bara li paaki, kimaan li ki tee u tiauŋ toona nyɔɔt kaa; li tee Yennu piiniie.

ref: God planned for us to do good things and to live as he has always wanted us to live. This is why he sent Christ to make us what we are. United by Christ
text: Yisa Masia nie ki Yennu teent ta tee binba, ki turit toonŋana binbeŋ a tii tuun linba ki u din poŋ lor a ti tun na. Masia niib kur tee yomme u ni

ref: Don't forget that you are Gentiles. In fact, you used to be called “uncircumcised” by those who take pride in being circumcised.
text: Yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na, Juu teeb yi yimm ŋanin punjoote, ŋaan yi bi mɔŋ sankpanna; yi tian man ya din tee nibooru nba sianyoo nba gar na.

ref: At that time you did not know about Christ. You were foreigners to the people of Israel, and you had no part in the promises God had made to them. You were living in this world without hope and without God,
text: I din ki mi Masia li yoo. I din tee saamme ki bi ki kaani ki pukinti Yennu nigannkab ni. I din ki mi Yennu tingbouŋ na ni, ki bia din ki mɔk dindann, kimaan wa din tur u niib mɔsona nba i din ki pukin li ni;

ref: and you were far from God. But Christ offered his life's blood as a sacrifice and brought you near God.
text: ŋaan mɔtana i ji be Yisa Masia nie, li paak yimm nba din tuu be banfɔkira maŋ ji nyat nakinɔɔe u kuun paak,

ref: Christ has made peace between Jews and Gentiles, and he has united us by breaking down the wall of hatred that separated us. Christ gave his own body
text: kimaan ŋɔɔe taan Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb ki teemm niyenɔkɔɔ, ki parmaasir baar; u gbanant paak ki u biir dataar nba din tuu puun bi sinsuuk ni na.

ref: to destroy the Law of Moses with all its rules and commands. He even brought Jews and Gentiles together as though we were only one person, when he united us in peace.
text: U din kpeen Juu teeb sennu nan li kɔɔt na kura a wun te parmaasir-n baar nibooru munnlee maŋ sinsuuk ni, ki bii be u ni ki taan kii tee nipaamm yenɔkɔɔ.

ref: On the cross Christ did away with our hatred for each other. He also made peace between us and God by uniting Jews and Gentiles in one body.
text: U dapunpunn paak kuun maŋ te ki u biir dataar maŋ bi sinsuuk ni, ki jiib ki taamm booru yenɔkɔɔ ki jen namm Yennu boor ni.

ref: Christ came and preached peace to you Gentiles, who were far from God, and peace to us Jews, who were near God.
text: Li paak, Masia din baar mɔɔnt parmaasir barŋanii mɔɔntii ki tur yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb ki din tuu fɔk nan Yennu na, ki mun bia baar nann ki tur Juu teeb nba din tuu kpia nanɔ na.

ref: And because of Christ, all of us can come to the Father by the same Spirit.
text: Masia paake ki Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb na kura fit baar ti Baa Yennu maŋ boor ni, u Seyeeŋ yenɔkɔɔ ni.

ref: You Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens with everyone else who belongs to the family of God.
text: Mɔtana, yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb nan Yennu niib tee tiŋ yeŋkɔɔ waase, ki ji tee Yennu ŋaateeb; i ji ki tee saamm kaa.

ref: You are like a building with the apostles and prophets as the foundation and with Christ as the most important stone.
text: Fiakir yennkɔɔ nba ki sɔkiniinba nan toomiinba din fɔkin na paake ki i mun tɔɔnn ki Yisa Masia mɔŋ tee li tanjaann.

ref: Christ is the one who holds the building together and makes it grow into a holy temple for the Lord.
text: Ŋɔɔe dia Yennu niib maŋ ki bi be fanu ki kpaatir ti Yomdaanɔ boor ni.

ref: And you are part of that building Christ has built as a place for God's own Spirit to live.
text: Yimm nan ŋɔɔ nba taan nan leeb na paak, ki yimm nan ŋamm mun bia taan teen diiuk nba tur Yennu ki u Seyeeŋ be i ni.
