ref: You have surely heard about God's gift of undeserved grace in choosing me to help you.
text: N ki biris nan i poŋ gbat nan Yennu turin toona na i sommir paaki.

ref: In fact, this letter tells you a little about how God has shown me his mysterious ways.
text: U din fiit u woonin ni lor na ki wannima.

ref: No one knew about this mystery until God's Spirit told it to his holy apostles and prophets.
text: Mɔtana Yennu ji ŋaan ki u Seyeeŋ fiit u woonin ni lor na ki wann u toomiinbaŋamm nan u sɔkiniinba, ŋaan li din ki tee nna sianyoo ni.

ref: And the mystery is this: Because of Christ Jesus, the good news has given the Gentiles a share in the promises God gave to the Jews. God has also let the Gentiles be part of the same body.
text: U woonin ni lor maŋ tee nee: barŋanii maŋ paak ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na ji fit taan la yiar Yendɔuŋ ni nan Juu teeb; ŋaan bia taan namm ki tee gbananyennkɔɔ ki fit mɔk namm wa din senn mɔsonn nba Yisa Masia ni na.

ref: God treated me with kindness. His power worked in me, and it became my job to spread the good news.
text: Yennu ninbatinu paak ki u din turin yaak a n teen u toomii kii mɔɔntir u barŋanii wa turin paŋ biaŋinba na.

ref: I am the least important of all God's people. But God was kind and chose me to tell the Gentiles that because of Christ there are blessings that cannot be measured.
text: Yennu niib sinsuuk ni, mine tan joont wunba ki be kann ni, ŋaan Yennu tur mine yaak a man jii barŋanii mɔɔntii nba wantit Masia nba sii teent binba ki tee Juu teeb na bonŋana bonchiann biaŋinba,

ref: God, who created everything, wanted me to help everyone understand the mysterious plan that had always been hidden in his mind.
text: ki bia te ki niib kur nunii-n yent ki bin bann biaŋinba ki Yennu woonin ni lor maŋ tuun gbeent Masia ni. Yaayoo niewa ki Yennu, wunba din nan bonsiar kur, din diar woonin ni

ref: Then God would use the church to show the powers and authorities in the spiritual world that he has many different kinds of wisdom.
text: a Masia niib paak malakanba yent damm nan Yendɔuŋ ni yikoo damm-n tan tumii mɔtana u subinbooru booru.

ref: God did this according to his eternal plan. And he was able to do what he had planned because of all Christ Jesus our Lord had done.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nie ki u din tun linba na kaman nan wa din lor biaŋinba na.

ref: Christ now gives us courage and confidence, so we can come to God by faith.
text: Ta teen Masia yada ki be u ni na, li paak, ki ti mɔk yuugontir ki sa baar Yennu boor nan parcheenn.

ref: This is why you should not be discouraged when I suffer for you. After all, it will bring honor to you. Christ's Love for Us
text: Li paak, n barimiie a i daa te ki i para-n biir nan ma di fara i paak na, kimaan li kur tee i nyɔɔt paake. Masia loomm labaar

ref: I kneel in prayer to the Father.
text: Linba na paak, ki n gbaa n duna paak ti Baa Yennu tɔɔnn po,

ref: All beings in heaven and on earth receive their life from him.
text: wunba yaa boor ni ki Yendɔuŋ ni nan tingbouŋ na ni nibooru kur gaan bi yejamm maaru ni sana.

ref: God is wonderful and glorious. I pray that his Spirit will make you become strong followers
text: Yennu paŋ nba yab bonchiann biaŋinba na, n meiɔ a wun turi paŋ maŋ u Seyeeŋ paak, ki paŋ maŋ-ii be i ni,

ref: and that Christ will live in your hearts because of your faith. Stand firm and be deeply rooted in his love.
text: n bia meiɔ a Masia-n jii i para ki teen u binbeboor i yada paak. N meiɔ a wun te ki i fit set chib, ki baa jiina ki fɔkin fiakir, ki loomm na-ii paar,

ref: I pray that you and all God's people will understand what is called wide or long or high or deep.
text: ki yimm nan Yennu niib kur-n fit bann Masia loomm yarimu, nan li fɔkint, nan li sumiiu nba jan biaŋinba,

ref: I want you to know all about Christ's love, although it is too wonderful to be measured. Then your lives will be filled with all that God is.
text: ki fit bann Masia loomm nba gar siar kur, ki i benu-ii tee kaman nan Yennu benu na.

ref: I pray that Christ Jesus and the church will forever bring praise to God. His power at work in us can do far more than we dare ask or imagine. Amen.
text: ŋɔɔ Yennu tan sa la nibaakir Yisa Masia nan u weira paak, yoo kur nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: As a prisoner of the Lord, I beg you to live in a way that is worthy of the people God has chosen to be his own.
text: Min nba kɔɔ dansarik ti Yomdaanɔ toona paak na, n kpaaniie a yii ŋaan ki i binbeŋ-ii tee fanu kaman nan Yennu nba loon ki lii tee biaŋinba na.

ref: Always be humble and gentle. Patiently put up with each other and love each other.
text: Yii sikint i mɔŋ, kii be soonna nan sukuru, kii mɔk yandiŋir nan i leeb, nan loomm.

ref: Try your best to let God's Spirit keep your hearts united. Do this by living at peace.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ nba din turi mɔtaauk nba na, yi yabir i mɔŋ kii be nan parmaasir.

ref: There is one God who is the Father of all people. Not only is God above all others, but he works by using all of us, and he lives in all of us.
text: Yennu tee yenɔkɔɔe ki tee nisaarii kura Baa, ki bia tee sɔɔ kura Yomdaanɔ nba tuun siar kura ni, ŋaan bia be siar kura ni.

ref: Christ has generously divided out his gifts to us.
text: Masia nba turit linba na paak, ti kura gaar u piingaŋire,

ref: As the Scriptures say, “When he went up to the highest place, he led away many prisoners and gave gifts to people.”
text: kaman nan la poŋ sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinba ki yet a “Wa din doo sanpaapo yoonba ki u nyinn yommii ki ŋaab yann, ŋaan turib piinii.”

ref: When it says, “he went up,” it means that Christ had been deep in the earth.
text: Tɔn, “U din doo sanpaapo” paak tee bee? Li want nan sinsinn u din sik tingbouŋ na nie.

ref: so his people would learn to serve and his body would grow strong.
text: U tun linba na a binba tee Yennu niib kura-n fit jaŋ u toona maŋe, ki chee Masiaweira.

ref: This will continue until we are united by our faith and by our understanding of the Son of God. Then we will be mature, just as Christ is, and we will be completely like him.
text: Li paak, ti sa taan teen Yennu Bija yada ki bannɔ, kii tee niib nba yaa binbeŋ ŋan ki jaŋ nan ŋɔɔ Masia yar na.

ref: We must stop acting like children. We must not let deceitful people trick us by their false teachings, which are like winds that toss us around from place to place.
text: Ti ji kii tee waas kaa; nifobiit nan kpinkpannii damm ji kii diat laŋ lin kaman nan ba poŋ teen siab biaŋinba na.

ref: Love should always make us tell the truth. Then we will grow in every way and be more like Christ, the head
text: Ŋaan ti sii piak barmɔniie nan loomm kii nɔb kii pukii, ki Masiae tee ti yudaanɔ.

ref: of the body. Christ holds it together and makes all of its parts work perfectly, as it grows and becomes strong because of love. The Old Life and the New Life
text: Ŋɔɔe te ki gbanant bɔka kur dia bi leeb, ki gbanant maŋ tukita dia nan leeb; li kura-i bo-i tuun nan leeb chib chib, gbanant maŋ bo sii nɔbee nan leeb. Ti binbenpaaŋ labaar

ref: As a follower of the Lord, I order you to stop living like stupid, godless people.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ sann paak, n kpaaniie, i ji daa be kaman nan binba ki mi Yennu, ki bi dudukit be bunbɔnn ni, ki li ki mɔk nyɔɔt biaŋinba na.

ref: Their minds are in the dark, and they are stubborn and ignorant and have missed out on the life that comes from God. They no longer have any feelings about what is right,
text: Bi tee tubkangbatae ki ki bant siari, li paak, bi ki pukin Yennu nba tur manfopaann nba na ni.

ref: and they are so greedy they do all kinds of indecent things.
text: Bi ji ki tiin fei, ki lin tuun jakint toona, ki ki dia bi mɔŋ nan waaminnawa.

ref: But this isn't what you were taught about Jesus Christ. He is the truth, and you heard about him and learned about him.
text: I din gbat u po ki tee u weirae, ki bi bia wanni barmɔnii nba be u niwa.

ref: You were told that your foolish desires will destroy you and that you must give up your old way of life with all its bad habits.
text: Li paak, yabit i mɔŋ man ki nyann niauŋ nba be i binbekperik nba na ni.

ref: Let the Spirit change your way of thinking
text: Ŋaant ki i dudukit nan i para-n ŋamm ki pant,

ref: and make you into a new person. You were created to be like God, and so you must please him and be truly holy. Rules for the New Life
text: kii mɔk binbenpaaŋ nba ki Yennu turi na, kii naan Yennu, kii be fanu, kii tee popeendamm nan barmɔnii.

ref: We are part of the same body. Stop lying and start telling each other the truth.
text: Li paak, ŋaant ki ti nyik faak, ŋaan sɔɔ kur-ii piak barmɔnii kii teen u lɔɔ, kimaan ti tee niyenɔkɔɔe Masia ni.

ref: Don't get so angry that you sin. Don't go to bed angry
text: Li-i tee ki a donn wutoor ŋaan fa daa tuun toonbiiti, ki bia daa diar a par ni ki yennu baa,

ref: and don't give the devil a chance.
text: ki daa teen Sintaanii yaaki.

ref: If you are a thief, quit stealing. Be honest and work hard, so you will have something to give to people in need.
text: Wunba tuu jaan u leeb bont na wun ji nyik jamu, ŋaan yabir tun toona ki fit tur tatimm linba ki bi loon.

ref: Stop all your dirty talk. Say the right thing at the right time and help others by what you say.
text: I daa piak mɔbonbiit ki teen i leebi, ŋaan te ki i mɔbona-ii tee linba kpaa talas ki mɔk nyɔɔt, ki i leeb-i gbat, lin pukimm binbeŋ nba fan.

ref: Don't make God's Spirit sad. The Spirit makes you sure that someday you will be free from your sins.
text: I daa te ki Seyeeŋ nba tee Yennu yent dɔk i paak na-n la parbiiri. Ŋɔɔe tee dɔk nba want nan Daar baat na ki Yennu tan sa turi yentinmɔŋ nan barmɔnii.

ref: Stop being bitter and angry and mad at others. Don't yell at one another or curse each other or ever be rude.
text: Yi nyik tonu binbeŋ kur, nan wutoor. Sɔɔ ji daa mɔk kunkɔnchiauŋ nan u lɔɔ, sɔɔ daa sukii u lɔɔ, ki bia nyik naŋ dudukit kura i ni.

ref: Instead, be kind and merciful, and forgive others, just as God forgave you because of Christ.
text: Yii tee burchimm nan ninkpabauŋ damm man i leeb boor. Yii nyikit leeb biit kii chabitib kaman nan Yennu nba nyikit i yara ki chabiti biaŋinba Masia paak na.

ref: Do as God does. After all, you are his dear children.
text: Ya tee Yennu walonkai na, yii yabir i mɔŋ kii naan nan ŋɔɔ na,

ref: Let love be your guide. Christ loved us and offered his life for us as a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: ki ŋaan ki loomm-ii dia-i kaman nan Masia nba din loont bonchiann ki gaar ti paak ki kpo ki teen mannu nba ki Yennu loon na.

ref: You are God's people, so don't let it be said that any of you are immoral or indecent or greedy.
text: Ya tee Yennu waas na paak, li poŋ ki ŋan niib-ii gbia bonchonchonn toona barii, amii jakint barii, amii nianchiauŋ barii i sinsuuk ni.

ref: Don't use dirty or foolish or filthy words. Instead, say how thankful you are.
text: Yii teen Yennu niipoouk yoo kura, ŋaan daa te ki bonchonchonn toona barii nan jakint barii nan baryana nyi i mɔi ni.

ref: Being greedy, indecent, or immoral is just another way of worshiping idols. You can be sure people who behave in this way will never be part of the kingdom that belongs to Christ and to God. Living as People of Light
text: Li ŋan yii mi nan wunba tee bonchonchonn amii jakint daanɔ amii nianchiauŋ daanɔ, ŋamme kan la yiar Masia nan Yennu naan ni; ki nianchiauŋ tee kaman nan patmannu nee.

ref: Don't let anyone trick you with foolish talk. God punishes everyone who disobeys him and says foolish things.
text: Kimaan li toonbiit tumu paake te ki Yennu wutoor baat mɔyêtdamm paak. I daa te ki sɔɔ-n jii u jat wannu ki kpanniini.

ref: So don't have anything to do with anyone like that.
text: I bɔkit i mɔŋ i kɔɔ, ŋaan nyik li damm.

ref: You used to be like people living in the dark, but now you are people of the light because you belong to the Lord. So act like people of the light
text: I ji tee Yennu waase, ki be yentu nie; i ki be bunbɔnn ni kaa kaman nan ya din tuu be na; li ŋan ki yi tee yentu na waas,

ref: and make your light shine. Be good and honest and truthful,
text: kimaan linba tee barmɔnii nan yentinu nan toonŋana kura tee yentu yare.

ref: Don't take part in doing those worthless things that are done in the dark. Instead, show how wrong they are.
text: ki daa tokii binba tuun bunbɔnn ni toona ki nyɔɔt kaa li ni, ŋaan te ki bi bunbɔnn ni toona maŋ-n yent.

ref: It is disgusting even to talk about what is done in the dark.
text: Linba ki bi tuun bunbɔnn ni na mɔk fei bonchiann gbaa nan tii piak li po.

ref: But the light will show what these things are really like.
text: Ŋaan ba tan sa nyinn bonsiar kur yeenin ni yoonba, bi bunbɔnn ni binbeŋ maŋ kura sa fiite perrr.

ref: Light shows up everything, just as the Scriptures say, “Wake up from your sleep and rise from death. Then Christ will shine on you.”
text: Kimaan linba kur ki bi fiit li gbina, li ji tuu dɔɔ yeenin nie. Li paake ki li din sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Fin nba gɔɔnt, fan fiir gɔɔn ni, ki li want nan a fiir kuun nie na, ki Masia sa tura u yentu.”

ref: Act like people with good sense and not like fools.
text: Li paak, i daa be jatbenu, ŋaan kii tee subindamm.

ref: These are evil times, so make every minute count.
text: Yii jii i yoo kii tuun fanu, kimaan yoo na ni tee yoo biiuko.

ref: Don't be stupid. Instead, find out what the Lord wants you to do.
text: Li paak, yii bant linba tee ti Yomdaanɔ loomm ki daa tee i mɔŋ jatiti.

ref: Don't destroy yourself by getting drunk, but let the Spirit fill your life.
text: I daa nyu daan ki yibi; ŋann sa biirie, ŋaan yii yabir i mɔŋ paak kii gbee nan Yennu Seyeeŋ.

ref: When you meet together, sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, as you praise the Lord with all your heart.
text: Yii yiin yanii kii piak ti Yomdaanɔ i para ni, kii yiin nan i leeb Yennu gbouŋ ni yanii nan Seyeeŋ yanii.

ref: Always use the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to thank God the Father for everything. Wives and Husbands
text: Yoo kur yii teen ti Baa Yennu niipoouk bonsiar kur paak, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann ni. Ŋaapoob nan bi sɔrib labaar

ref: Honor Christ and put others first.
text: Masia paak, yi sikin i mɔŋ ki tur i leeb man.

ref: A wife should put her husband first, as she does the Lord.
text: Poob, ya baakit Masia biaŋinba na, yii saak i sɔrib mɔi,

ref: A husband is the head of his wife, as Christ is the head and the Savior of the church, which is his own body.
text: kimaan Masia nba mɔk yaak u niib paak biaŋinba na, nnee ki jɔɔ mun mɔk yaak u poo paak. Masia niib tee u gbanante ŋaan ki u tee bi Tinntɔɔ.

ref: Wives should always put their husbands first, as the church puts Christ first.
text: Poob mun-ii saak bi sɔrib mɔi bont kura ni kaman nan Masia niib nba saakɔ biaŋinba na.

ref: A husband should love his wife as much as Christ loved the church and gave his life for it.
text: Jab, yii loon i poob kaman nan Masia nba loon u niib ki gaar bi paak ki kpo biaŋinba na.

ref: He made the church holy by the power of his word, and he made it pure by washing it with water.
text: U din tun linba na, ki bia jii Yennu barii nan nyun ki wurib, a wun ŋammib fanu

ref: Christ did this, so he would have a glorious and holy church, without faults or spots or wrinkles or any other flaws.
text: ki jiin teemm u mɔŋ nuu ni nan fant, a bi fant na daa mɔk dansiari, ki bia tee popeendamm ki daa mɔk biit nan waaminnawa.

ref: In the same way, a husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself. A husband who loves his wife shows he loves himself.
text: Jɔɔ nba loon u poo, u loon u mɔŋe, li paak, li ŋan ki jab-ii loon bi poob kaman nan ba loon bi mɔŋ gbanant biaŋinba na.

ref: None of us hate our own bodies. We provide for them and take good care of them, just as Christ does for the church,
text: (Sɔɔ ki mi yêt u mɔŋ gbananti; sɔɔ kura gorii u gbanante fanu ki dint u mɔŋ kaman nan Masia nba tuun biaŋinba teen u niib na.

ref: because we are each part of his body.
text: Timme tee u gbanant maŋ,)

ref: As the Scriptures say, “A man leaves his father and mother to get married, and he becomes like one person with his wife.”
text: kaman nan Yennu gbouŋ nba yet biaŋinba na a “Linba na paak ki li ŋan ki jɔɔ-n nyik u naa nan u baa, ŋaan jii u mɔŋ ki taan nan u ŋaapoo ki bin ji teen yomm.”

ref: This is a great mystery, but I understand it to mean Christ and his church.
text: Yennu mɔmaan na sumii bonchiann, ŋaan min mi nan li want Masia nan u niib poe.

ref: So each husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself, and each wife should respect her husband.
text: Ŋaan li bia want yimme a li ŋan ki jɔɔ kura-ii loon u poo kaman nan wa loon u mɔŋ biaŋinba na, ki poo kura-ii munii baakit u sɔrɔ.

ref: Children, you belong to the Lord, and you do the right thing when you obey your parents. The first commandment with a promise says,
text: Waas, yii saak i damm mɔi ti Yomdaanɔ Masia paak, kimaan ŋanne took nan yin tun.

ref: and you will have a long and happy life.”
text: ki a bont kur-n somm fanu, ki a manfoor-ii biakii fɔkit tingbouŋ na ni.”

ref: Parents, don't be hard on your children. Raise them properly. Teach them and instruct them about the Lord. Slaves and Masters
text: Baanba, i daa dia i waas diabiiuk ki dont bi wutoa, ŋaan kii wantib kii kpaamm ti Yomdaanɔ nba loon biaŋinba. Daaba nan bi chanbaanba labaar

ref: Slaves, you must obey your earthly masters. Show them great respect and be as loyal to them as you are to Christ.
text: Tɔn, binba tee daaba mun-ii saak bi chanbaanba mɔi, kii tiimm jaŋmaanii, kii tuun toonn kii teemm nan i para kura kaman nan ya bo sii tuun ki teen Masia biaŋinba na.

ref: Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. You are slaves of Christ, so with your whole heart you must do what God wants you to do.
text: I daa tiimm yoo nba ki bi tuu gorii-i ki i tuun na kuukɔɔ kaa, a bin pakiini, ŋaan yi jii i mɔŋ teen Masia daaba kii tuun linba tee Yennu loomm nan i para kura.

ref: Gladly serve your masters, as though they were the Lord himself, and not simply people.
text: Yii tuun i toona nan parpeenn ki lii want nan i tuun ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ Masiae, ŋaan daa dukii nan i tuun teen nisaariki;

ref: You know that you will be rewarded for any good things you do, whether you are slaves or free.
text: kii tian nan ti Yomdaanɔ tan sa tur wunba yen u mɔŋ nan daabir kura bi toonŋana paauko.

ref: Slave owners, you must treat your slaves with this same respect. Don't threaten them. They have the same Master in heaven that you do, and he doesn't have favorites. The Fight against Evil
text: Chanbaanba, yii mun-ii dia i daaba bonŋann ki daa diab nan ninmɔnni. Yii mun-ii tian nan yimm nan i daaba maŋ kura taan mɔk Yomdaanɔ yenɔkɔɔe Yendɔuŋ ni, ki u ki mɔk lukitimi. Yennu naagbankɔŋŋir labaar

ref: Finally, let the mighty strength of the Lord make you strong.
text: Linba joont tee nnee, yii chee i para ti Yomdaanɔ ni, kii mɔk yabint paŋ nba ki u turi na.

ref: Put on all the armor that God gives, so you can defend yourself against the devil's tricks.
text: Yi jii Yennu naagbankɔŋŋir na ki lia, ki lin te ki yin fit nyann Sintaanii muksubiiuk.

ref: We are not fighting against humans. We are fighting against forces and authorities and against rulers of darkness and powers in the spiritual world.
text: Kimaan ti ki kɔn nan wunba mɔk gbanant nan sɔn kaa, ŋaan ti kɔn nan narinbiite nan bi yikodamm, nan mɔtana dunia bunbɔnn ni yudamm, nan sanpagbann ni narinbiit.

ref: So put on all the armor that God gives. Then when that evil day comes, you will be able to defend yourself. And when the battle is over, you will still be standing firm.
text: Li paak, yi jii Yennu naagbankɔŋŋir na ki lia, ki fit set dabiir nba tan sa baar maŋ daar, ki daa nyikiti.

ref: Be ready! Let the truth be like a belt around your waist, and let God's justice protect you like armor.
text: I set man, ki jii barmɔnii ki bobin i sai ni, kii mɔk yentinu nba tee kaman nan naagbankɔŋŋir na,

ref: Your desire to tell the good news about peace should be like shoes on your feet.
text: ki teen siir kii mɔɔntir barŋanii nba teen niib parmaasir na, linba tee kaman nan taasaat na.

ref: Let your faith be like a shield, and you will be able to stop all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
text: Linba pukin na paak, yi jii yada nba tee kaman nan naagbankɔŋŋir nba ki i sa fit kpeen Sintaanii peenii nba mɔk muu na,

ref: Let God's saving power be like a helmet, and for a sword use God's message that comes from the Spirit.
text: ki dia i tinnu nba tee kaman nan kut fokirik, ki bia dia Yennu barii nba tee kaman nan jukbanjiak nba ki u Seyeeŋ tuun nann na.

ref: Never stop praying, especially for others. Always pray by the power of the Spirit. Stay alert and keep praying for God's people.
text: Yii mei Yennu yoo kur nan ninmɔnn, u Seyeeŋ nba sommi biaŋinba na; ki yin chekin i mɔŋ nan gbankur ki pukin Yenmiaru kii teen Masiaweira kura,

ref: Pray that I will be given the message to speak and that I may fearlessly explain the mystery about the good news.
text: ki bia miar Yennu tur n mun ki man fit la sɔnu ki pak nan parcheenn ki mɔn woonin ni barŋanii na,

ref: I was sent to do this work, and this is the reason I am in jail. So pray that I will be brave and will speak as I should. Final Greetings
text: kimaan li paake ki n tee toomii ki kɔɔ dansarik ni. Yii mei Yennu ki turin ki man mɔn barŋanii maŋ nan parcheenn kaman nan la kpaa talas nan man pak biaŋinba na. Foont joontii labaar

ref: I pray that God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will give peace, love, and faith to every follower!
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n tur yimm Masiaweira kur parmaasir nan loomm nan yada.

ref: May God be kind to everyone who keeps on loving our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Yennu-n somm binba kur loon ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nan loomm nba kaa gbennu na.

ref: From Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus. To all God's people who belong to Christ Jesus at Philippi and to all your bishops and deacons.
text: Min Pɔɔl nan Timoti, timm nba tee Yisa Masia toontunna nee sɔb gbouŋ na ki teen Yennu niŋamm kur nba tee Yisa Masiaweira nba be Filipi na, nan bi yudamm nan bi sommteeb na.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul's Prayer for the Church in Philippi
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir. Pɔɔl mei Yennu bi paak

ref: Every time I think of you, I thank my God.
text: N teen Yennu niipoouk yoo nba kura ki n tiar i po.

ref: And whenever I mention you in my prayers, it makes me happy.
text: Yoo kura, mi-i mei Yennu teeni, n tuu mei nan parpeenne,

ref: This is because you have taken part with me in spreading the good news from the first day you heard about it.
text: kimaan ya sommitin biaŋinba barŋanii toona maŋ pinpiik niwa nan mɔtana na paak.

ref: God is the one who began this good work in you, and I am certain that he won't stop before it is complete on the day that Christ Jesus returns.
text: Li paak, n ki mɔk birsuk nan Yennu nba din piin toonŋann i ni na, u sii diar nna mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki u tan sa gbenn toonŋann maŋ Yisa Masia daar maŋ.

ref: You have a special place in my heart. So it is only natural for me to feel the way I do. All of you have helped in the work God has given me, as I defend the good news and tell about it here in jail.
text: N yan be i ni. Ki mi-i dukii i po nna, li set ŋan, kimaan la nyii yoo nba ki n din be n yama ni ki gbaa barŋanii maŋ paak ma n sennir nawa, ki tan baar yoo nba ki n ji kɔɔ dansarik na, i kura mun lek mɔk li yaak booru nba ki Yennu turin na.

ref: God himself knows how much I want to see you. He knows I care for you in the same way Christ Jesus does.
text: Yennue tee n siara daanɔ nan n yan set be i ni nan Yisa Masia mɔŋ mɔŋ loomm.

ref: I pray that your love will keep on growing and you will fully know and understand
text: N mei Yennu ma i loomm-ii yɔɔ kii pukii yoo nan yoo, ki i barmɔnii bannu nan i subinii kura bia sii pukii,

ref: how to make the right choices. Then you will still be pure and innocent when Christ returns. And until that day,
text: ki yin fit gann linba mantik ŋan, ki li poorpo ki i be ki tan daa mɔk jakint nan biit Masia daar maŋ ni,

ref: Jesus Christ will keep you busy doing good deeds that bring glory and praise to God. What Life Means to Paul
text: ki i binbeŋ-ii mɔk yentinu ki yii tuun toonŋana bonchiann, Yisa Masia paŋ ni, ki Yennu sann-n la dontir nan paku. Manfoor tee Masia

ref: My dear friends, I want you to know that what has happened to me has helped to spread the good news.
text: N naa waas, n loon ki yin bann nan linba kura tumin na somm barŋanii maŋ a lin saan tɔɔnne nan barmɔnii.

ref: The Roman guards and all the others know I am here in jail because I serve Christ.
text: Li paak, kpanbar gbanjot nan nileeb nba be nna na kur bann nan ma tee Masia toontunnɔ na paake ki n kɔɔ dansarik na.

ref: Now most of the Lord's followers have become brave and are fearlessly telling the message.
text: N sarik ni benu na tur ti naa waas bonchiann yuugontir ti Yomdaanɔ sɔnu ni, ki pukii yoo kur, ki bi mɔɔntir Yennu maan nan parcheenn, ki jaŋmaanii ji kaa.

ref: Some are preaching about Christ because they are jealous and envious of us. Others are preaching because they want to help.
text: Ŋaan siab mɔɔntir Masia maan maŋ kimaan bi tee punpondamm nan kunkɔnkɔnnae, ŋaan ki siab mun mɔɔntir nan bi para ni nan barmɔnii loomm.

ref: They love Christ and know I am here to defend the good news about him.
text: Binba tuun nan bi loomm na, bi mi nan Yennue poŋ din turin barŋanii toona na.

ref: But the ones who are jealous of us are not sincere. They just want to cause trouble for me while I am in jail.
text: Nisiab ki mɔɔntir Masia po nan bi para kuri, ŋaan bi tuun nan funfunne; bi dukii nan bi sa pukin mine fara yoo nba ki n daa be dansarik nna.

ref: But that doesn't matter. All that matters is that people are telling about Christ, whether they are sincere or not. This is what makes me glad. I will keep on being glad,
text: Ŋaan ŋann ki biiri. N mɔk parpeenn li paak, ŋamme sa fit ki mɔɔnt Masia maan maŋ sɔnbooru nba kur ni; bi-i mɔɔntir bi dudukŋant ni amii bi dudukbiit ni, min lek bia sii mɔk parpeenn li paak,

ref: because I know that your prayers and the help that comes from the Spirit of Christ Jesus will keep me safe.
text: kimaan n mi nan i Yenmiaru nan i sommir nba nyi Yisa Masia Seyeeŋ boor ni na paak, n sa nyi dansarik na niwa.

ref: I honestly expect and hope I will never do anything to be ashamed of. Whether I live or die, I always want to be as brave as I am now and bring honor to Christ.
text: N par ni loomm nan n dindann tee ki n daa dint n mɔŋ feie, ŋaan n sii mɔk parcheenn mɔtana nan yoo kura, ki mi-i lekii kpo amii ma be, ŋaan li kura sa dont Masia sann n po,

ref: If I live, it will be for Christ, and if I die, I will gain even more.
text: kimaan mi-i be n manfoor ni, li tee Masia paake; ŋaan li-i tee kuun ki n kpo, ŋanne mantik sa baar nan nyɔɔt ki turin.

ref: It is a hard choice to make. I want to die and be with Christ, because this would be much better.
text: Li bona ŋannlee nee daarin; n mantik bo loon man nyik manfosiar nba nee, ki saan kii sai be Masia boor ni, ŋanne bo sii tee linba chee ki gar.

ref: But I know that all of you still need me. This is why I am sure I will stay on to help you grow and be happy in your faith.
text: N mi nan ŋanne sɔ ki birsuk kaa, li paak te ki n mi nan n sii beewa. N sii be i kura boor ni, ki i sii mɔk parpeenn ki i yada-n chee,

ref: Then, when I visit you again, you will have good reason to take great pride in Christ Jesus because of me.
text: ki mi-i taŋŋi jen i boor ni yoonba i binbeŋ sa turin yuugontir bonchiann nan li sɔnu, ki yin pak Yisa Masia n manfoor paak.

ref: Above all else, you must live in a way that brings honor to the good news about Christ. Then, whether I visit you or not, I will hear that all of you think alike. I will know that you are working together and are struggling side by side to get others to believe the good news.
text: Mɔtana, linba kpaa talas tee a yi ŋaan ki i binbeŋ sɔnu-ii tee nan Masia barŋanii nba loon lii tee biaŋinba na, ki mi-i lekii fit saan lai amii mi-i kii fit laini, ŋaan n sa gbat a i see fanu nan dudukyennkɔɔ, ki taan mɔtaauk ki yabii i mɔŋ ki barŋanii maŋ-ii saa tɔɔnn.

ref: Be brave when you face your enemies. Your courage will show them that they are going to be destroyed, and it will show you that you will be saved. God will make all of this happen,
text: I daa tiin i datai mani; yii mɔk parcheenn ki lin wannib nan ŋamme tan sa baa ŋaan ki yin do, kimaan Yennue teeni li nyannu.

ref: and he has blessed you. Not only do you have faith in Christ, but you suffer for him.
text: Ya poŋ mɔk yaak nan yin tun ki tur Masia na paak, ki li ki tee ya teenɔ yada na paak kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan ya bia di fara u paak na,

ref: You saw me suffer, and you still hear about my troubles. Now you must suffer in the same way.
text: ki gbaa kaman nan ya poŋ din la ma lek gbaa biaŋinba na, ki mɔtana i bia gbat nan n daa gbaa nna.

ref: Christ encourages you, and his love comforts you. God's Spirit unites you, and you are concerned for others.
text: Ya be Masia ni biaŋinbae teeni paŋ, ki u loomm maŋŋit i para, ki i mɔk burchint nan ninbatinu i leeb paak, ki mɔk Seyeeŋ weinanleeb,

ref: Now make me completely happy! Live in harmony by showing love for each other. Be united in what you think, as if you were only one person.
text: ŋanne te ki n beeri ma yii mɔk dudukit yennkɔɔ ki pukin n parpeenn na, kii mɔk loomm yennkɔɔ ki i dudukit kura-ii tee yennkɔɔ.

ref: and think the same way that Christ Jesus thought:
text: Yi ŋaan ki binbeŋ booru nba ki Yisa Masia din mɔk na mun-ii be i ni:

ref: Christ was truly God. But he did not try to remain equal with God.
text: U din tuu yɔɔ be kaman nan Yennu nee, ŋaan u din ki mi dukin a u sa jii u mɔŋ ki yekin nan Yennu,

ref: Christ was humble. He obeyed God and even died on a cross.
text: U din sikin u mɔŋ, ki sak ki kpo dapunpunn paak gbaa.

ref: Then God gave Christ the highest place and honored his name above all others.
text: Linba na paake ki Yennu din donnɔ gar sɔɔ kura, ŋaan turɔ sansiar nba tee sanjaann ki gar sana kura.

ref: So at the name of Jesus everyone will bow down, those in heaven, on earth, and under the earth.
text: Ŋanne te ki binba be sanpaapo nan tingbouŋ na nan kpeentiŋ, bi kur tan sa gbaan bi duna paak ki tur ŋɔɔ Yisa sann maŋ nibaakir,

ref: And to the glory of God the Father everyone will openly agree, “Jesus Christ is Lord!” Lights in the World
text: ki sɔɔ kura tan sa yet a ŋɔɔ Yisa Masia tee Yomdaanɔ kii dont ti Baa Yennu sann. Yii nyirii kaman nan ŋmaajara na man

ref: My dear friends, you always obeyed when I was with you. Now that I am away, you should obey even more. So work with fear and trembling to discover what it really means to be saved.
text: N naa walonkai, ma din be i boor ni ki i sak n mɔb biaŋinba na, ki mi-i lekii kaa i boor ni, li kpaa talas ki yin sak n mɔb. Yii biakii tuun i toona nan yentinu nan gbananjekint man, ki tont i tinnu,

ref: God is working in you to make you willing and able to obey him.
text: kimaan Yennu yɔɔ tuun nanie, a yii loon yii tuun u loomm kii biakii tuun u loomm maŋ.

ref: Do everything without grumbling or arguing.
text: Yii tuun siar kura ki daa burimi, amii ki nɔi mɔniɔku;

ref: Then you will be the pure and innocent children of God. You live among people who are crooked and evil, but you must not do anything they can say is wrong. Try to shine as lights among the people of this world,
text: ŋanne sa te ki i daa mɔk biit, ŋaan kii mɔk popeenn, kii tee Yennu waas nba ki mɔk daŋ, kimaan i be nijakina nan biitdamm sinsuuk nie na. Li kpaa talas ki yii nyirii bi sinsuuk ni kaman nan ŋmaajara nba nyirii sanpaapo biaŋinba na,

ref: as you hold firmly to the message that gives life. Then on the day when Christ returns, I can take pride in you. I can also know that my work and efforts were not useless.
text: ŋaan yetib manfoor labaar. Li-i tee ki i tuun nna, n tan sii mɔk yaak nan mii mɔk yuugontir i paak Masia daar maŋ, kimaan li tan sa wann nan n fara nan n toona ki tee yanni.

ref: Your faith in the Lord and your service are like a sacrifice offered to him. And my own blood may have to be poured out with the sacrifice. If this happens, I will be glad and rejoice with you.
text: Li-i tee ki n lu n miar na ki pukin fara nba ki n poŋ dii ma n te ki yin teen yada na, li bo sii manin, ki n bo sii mɔk parpeenn nan i kura.

ref: In the same way, you should be glad and rejoice with me. Timothy and Epaphroditus
text: Li kpaa talas ki i mun-ii mɔk parpeenn nanin man. Timoti nan Epafrodaitus labaar

ref: I want to be encouraged by news about you. So I hope the Lord Jesus will soon let me send Timothy to you.
text: N mɔk dindann ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa ni nan man tun Timoti i boor ni mɔtana, ki gbat i labaar ki n par ni-n dɔɔr.

ref: I don't have anyone else who cares about you as much as he does.
text: Ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔe fiin i po nan barmɔnii kaman nan ma fiin i po biaŋinba na.

ref: The others think only about what interests them and not about what concerns Christ Jesus.
text: Sɔɔ kur nba ji biar gbaa nan bi mɔŋ mɔŋ yarae jiak jiak, bi ki fiin linba tee Yisa Masia yar na po.

ref: But you know what kind of person Timothy is. He has worked with me like a son in spreading the good news.
text: I mɔŋ mi wa tun biaŋinba ki wann u kotir biaŋinba po, nan wa tuun nanin barŋanii toona ni kaman nan bik nba tuun nan u baa biaŋinba na.

ref: I hope to send him to you, as soon as I find out what is going to happen to me.
text: Mi-i la bont nba yaa lin ŋmakit n po biaŋinba, n mɔk dindann ma n tumɔe yiama.

ref: And I feel sure the Lord will also let me come soon.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ paak, n mɔk yada nan n mɔŋ sa fit baar i boor ni mɔtana.

ref: I think I ought to send my dear friend Epaphroditus back to you. He is a follower and a worker and a soldier of the Lord, just as I am. You sent him to look after me,
text: N dukin ki li kpaa talase nan man tun ti naa bik Epafrodaitus, wunba tuun nan nunmɔnn ki gbaa a wun sommin nan Masia toonn, ŋɔɔe bia tee wunba ki i daan teenɔ i toomii ki u daan sommin na.

ref: but now he is eager to see you. He is worried, because you heard he was sick.
text: U set loon wun la i kura. U par daan biir bonchiann, kimaan i daan poŋ gbat nan u yiare.

ref: In fact, he was very sick and almost died. But God was kind to him, and also to me, and he kept me from being burdened down with sorrow.
text: Li set tee barmɔniie, u daan yiare ki per kuuma, ŋaan Yennu daan tinɔ ninbaauk, ki li ki tee u kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan bia tin n mɔŋ ninbaauk, ki nyinnin parbijaann ni.

ref: Now I am more eager than ever to send Epaphroditus back again. You will be glad to see him, and I won't have to worry any longer.
text: Li paak, n jekiree nan man tumɔ i boor ni, ki i-i lau yoonba ki yin yaan la parpeenn, ki n mɔŋ parbiir-n bot.

ref: Be sure to give him a cheerful welcome, just as people who serve the Lord deserve.
text: Yii gaarɔ nan parpeenn man kaman nan i naa bik na ti Yomdaanɔ ni. Yi tur nibooru nba tee nan ŋɔɔ na nibaakir man,

ref: He almost died working for Christ, and he risked his own life to do for me what you could not.
text: kimaan u daan ki kɔn u manfoor paak ki tan yaa wun kpo Masia toonn na paak, a wun turin sommir nba ki i daan ki fit turin na.

ref: Finally, my dear friends, be glad that you belong to the Lord. It doesn't bother me to write the same things to you that I have written before. In fact, it is for your own good.
text: Li gbennu nira, n naa waas, yii mɔk parpeenn yimm nan ti Yomdaanɔ benu ni. Min yaa baka kaa nan ma ŋamm sɔb linba ki n poŋ daan sɔb turi sinsinn na; mi-i tuun nna, li sii tee i yumanne.

ref: Watch out for those people who behave like dogs! They are evil and want to do more than just circumcise you.
text: I mi man biitdamm na, nisiab nba naan nan bɔi na, ki pot bi gbanant na.

ref: But we are the ones who are truly circumcised, because we worship by the power of God's Spirit and take pride in Christ Jesus. We don't brag about what we have done,
text: Timm jiantir Yennu u Seyeeŋ nie, ki mɔk yuugontir Yisa Masia paak; linba na gar punpotuwa. Timm ki teen nifosaarik yar yada kaa.

ref: although I could. Others may brag about themselves, but I have more reason to brag than anyone else.
text: Wunba-i dukii a ŋɔɔ sa fit teen nifosaarik yar yada, min mɔŋ sa fit teen li bonbooru yadawa. N mɔk linba te ki n sa tun nna,

ref: I was circumcised when I was eight days old, and I am from the nation of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin. I am a true Hebrew. As a Pharisee, I strictly obeyed the Law of Moses.
text: kimaan n mun din tee daa ŋanniin bike ki bi pot n punn. Bi marin ki n tee Juu nirɔe, Benjamin booru ni, ki tee Juu nirɔ nan barmɔnii. Li-i tee Juu teeb sennu dianu nba tee biaŋinba po naie, n din waa sennu maŋ kaman nan Farisiinba nba waa biaŋinba nee nan nunmɔnn,

ref: And I was so eager I even made trouble for the church. I did everything the Law demands in order to please God.
text: ki din tan mukis Masia niib gbaa. Nirɔ-i sa sak sennue ki fit kii tee yentinɔ, min din be ki kaa bisiat jiin li po.

ref: But Christ has shown me that what I once thought was valuable is worthless.
text: Ki n din bo sa fit kan linba na kur kaman nan li mɔk nyɔɔte, ŋaan min yiin linba kaa nyɔɔte Masia paak.

ref: and to know that I belong to him. I could not make myself acceptable to God by obeying the Law of Moses. God accepted me simply because of my faith in Christ.
text: kii set kii be nanɔ nan barmɔnii. N ji ki mɔk n tiauŋ yentinu booru nba tee sennu weiu na kaa. Mɔtana, ma teen Yisa Masia yada nee ki n gaar yentinu booru nba na, li yentinu tee yada paake, ki nyi Yennu boor ni na.

ref: All I want is to know Christ and the power that raised him to life. I want to suffer and die as he did,
text: Linba kur ki n ji loon tee ki n bann Masia, ki bann u kuun fiiru paŋ, ki di fara nanɔ, ki n binbenkperik-n gbenn kaman nan wa din kpo biaŋinba na,

ref: so that somehow I also may be raised to life. Running toward the Goal
text: ki n mɔŋ-ii mɔk dindann nan n tan sa fiir kuun ni nan manfoora. Poot nantatinu labaar

ref: I have not yet reached my goal, and I am not perfect. But Christ has taken hold of me. So I keep on running and struggling to take hold of the prize.
text: N ki yaa a n poŋ nyann ki yent binbeŋ maŋ amii n poŋ ji be fanu kaa; n gbaae ma n nyann ki yent binbeŋ maŋ; kimaan Yisa Masia din fatima, ki n ji tee u yɔɔe.

ref: My friends, I don't feel I have already arrived. But I forget what is behind, and I struggle for what is ahead.
text: N naa waas, n ki dukii a n poŋ nyannir ki li tee n yare, ŋaan bonyennkɔɔe ki n tuun, n lek ji tammit linba be n poorpoe, ŋaan gbaa ma n baar linba be n tɔɔnn po na.

ref: I run toward the goal, so I can win the prize of being called to heaven. This is the prize God offers because of what Christ Jesus has done.
text: Li paake te ki n tɔkin poot sɔnu maŋ ki tiin sunsonn saa nyannu bɔɔnu maŋ peŋ ma n sa gaar paauk nba tee sanpaapo manfoor ki Yennu din yiinin li paak Yisa Masia ni na.

ref: All of us who are mature should think in this same way. And if any of you think differently, God will make it clear to you.
text: Li kpaa talase ki timm nba kur tee subirteeb Seyeeŋ ni na-ii mɔk dudukit maŋ booru, ŋaan i siab-i mɔk dudukgantie, Yennu sa te lin yent li damm nunii powa.

ref: But we must keep going in the direction that we are now headed.
text: Ŋaant man ki tii dia barmɔnii kuukɔɔ kaman nan ta din dia biaŋinba nan mɔtana na.

ref: My friends, I want you to follow my example and learn from others who closely follow the example we set for you.
text: N naa waas, yii biakii tokii min man. Yi jii i dudukit teen damm nba waa siarŋana nba ki ti din senn turi na ni.

ref: I often warned you that many people are living as enemies of the cross of Christ. And now with tears in my eyes, I warn you again
text: N poŋ din beti linba na po yoo nba gar nawa, ŋaan n bia ŋammit beerie mɔtana nan ninnyuut, nisiab be ki bi binbeŋ jiib teemm Masia dapunpunn paak kuun na dataie.

ref: that they are headed for hell! They worship their stomachs and brag about the disgusting things they do. All they can think about are the things of this world.
text: Bi tan sa joont nan tubdatu nba kaa gbennue, kimaan bi poore tee bi yennu. Bi mɔk yuugontir nan linba dintib fei nee. Bi dukii linba tee tingbouŋ na bona kuukɔɔ poe.

ref: But we are citizens of heaven and are eagerly waiting for our Savior to come from there. Our Lord Jesus Christ
text: Ŋaan timm ŋanin tee Yendɔuŋ ni waase, ki jekir ki guu ti Tinntɔɔ, wunba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia, a wun nyi Yendɔuŋ ni baar.

ref: has power over everything, and he will make these poor bodies of ours like his own glorious body.
text: U tan sa lebit ti gbananchɔkint nba sa kpo na ki lii naan kaman nan u Yendɔuŋ ni gbananpaann nee. U tan sa jii paŋ nba te ki u yen bonsiar kur nee ki tun nna.

ref: Dear friends, I love you and long to see you. Please keep on being faithful to the Lord. You are my pride and joy. Paul Encourages the Lord's Followers
text: Li paak, n naa waas, yimm nba ki n looni, ki loon ki n lai na, yimme te ki n mɔk parpeenn nan yuugontir. N naa walonkai, setir fanu man Yennu sɔnu ni, ti Yomdaanɔ tɔɔnn.

ref: Euodia and Syntyche, you belong to the Lord, so I beg you to stop arguing with each other.
text: N niipoob Yuodia nan Sintike, n barimiie, yii yabir kii be nan parmaasir nan i leeb ti Yomdaanɔ paak.

ref: And, my true partner, I ask you to help them. These women have worked together with me and with Clement and with the others in spreading the good news. Their names are now written in the book of life.
text: Ki fin nba mun tee n weinanlɔɔ nan barmɔnii, n loon ki fin somm poob bannlee maŋ, kimaan ŋamm nan Klement nan binba kur tee n toontunleeb ki bi sana be Yennu manfoor gbouŋ ni na din lakin tun nanin bonchiann barŋanii maŋ mɔnu ni.

ref: Always be glad because of the Lord! I will say it again: Be glad.
text: Yii mɔk parpeenn yoo kur, yimm nan ti Yomdaanɔ benu ni. N bia ŋammit beeri ma yii mɔk parpeenn man.

ref: Always be gentle with others. The Lord will soon be here.
text: Yi ŋaan ki sɔɔ kur-n bann i niŋantin po. Ti Yomdaanɔ baate mɔtana.

ref: Don't worry about anything, but pray about everything. With thankful hearts offer up your prayers and requests to God.
text: I daa biir i para bonsiar paaki. Yimm-ii mei Yennu linba kpaa talas turi paak. Yii mei Yennu, kii teenɔ niipoouk i para ni yoo kura ki betɔ i loomm nba tee linba.

ref: Then, because you belong to Christ Jesus, God will bless you with peace that no one can completely understand. And this peace will control the way you think and feel.
text: Ki Yennu parmaasir nba gar nirɔ bannu terik na sa dia i para nan i dudukit Yisa Masia ni.

ref: Finally, my friends, keep your minds on whatever is true, pure, right, holy, friendly, and proper. Don't ever stop thinking about what is truly worthwhile and worthy of praise.
text: N naa waas, linba joont tee nnee, yi ŋaan ki i dudukit-n gbee nan linba tee barmɔnii, nan linba ŋan nan baakir, nan linba ŋan, nan linba mɔk burchint, nan linba took, nan linba yeen, nan linba mɔk fant, nan linba ŋan nan paku.

ref: You know the teachings I gave you, and you know what you heard me say and saw me do. So follow my example. And God, who gives peace, will be with you. Paul Gives Thanks for the Gifts He Was Given
text: Jiin linba ki i tumii ki gaar n boor ni nan n mɔbona nan n toona na kii tuun nann toona. Ki ti Yennu nba teent parmaasir na sii be nani. Piinii niipoouk turu labaar

ref: The Lord has made me very grateful that at last you have thought about me once again. Actually, you were thinking about me all along, but you didn't have any chance to show it.
text: N mɔk parpeenn bonchiann ti Yomdaanɔ ni nan la yukira ŋaan ki i fiin n po na paak; setik nan barmɔnii, i daan loonima, ŋaan i daan ki mɔk yaake.

ref: I am not complaining about having too little. I have learned to be satisfied with whatever I have.
text: N ki dukii nan i tamm n po kaa ki n yeen linba na, kimaan min poŋ tumii biaŋinba ki n numm sa gboo linba ki n mɔka.

ref: I know what it is to be poor or to have plenty, and I have lived under all kinds of conditions. I know what it means to be full or to be hungry, to have too much or too little.
text: N mi kɔŋŋir ni binbeŋ nan jaŋŋir ni binbeŋ. N poŋ tumii li kura; li paak, mi-i be siaminba kur ki li tee kon, amii gboor, li-i lekii yab amii li-i kii yabi, ŋaan n numm gboot linba ki n mɔke yoo kura.

ref: Christ gives me the strength to face anything.
text: Masia nba teenin paŋ na paak, n sa fit set binbeŋ kur ni.

ref: It was good of you to help me when I was having such a hard time.
text: Ŋaan i daan tun fanu bonchiann nan ya daan sommin n fara dinu ni na.

ref: My friends at Philippi, you remember what it was like when I started preaching the good news in Macedonia. After I left there, you were the only church that became my partner by giving blessings and by receiving them in return.
text: Yimm Filipi teeb mi nan ma din mɔɔntir Masia barŋanii pinpiik niwa, yoo nba ki n din nyii Masedonia tiŋ ni na, yimm kuukɔɔe din tee Masia niib nba sommitin; ki i gaan piinii nba tee Yennu Seyeeŋ yar na n boor ni, ŋaan mun teenin i piinii nba tee tingbouŋ na yar.

ref: Even when I was in Thessalonica, you helped me more than once.
text: Ma din tan be fara ni Tesalonika doo ni na, yimme din tun ki teenin sommir.

ref: I am not trying to get something from you, but I want you to receive the blessings that come from giving.
text: Li ki tee a n loon mii gaan i piinii kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan n loon ki yin gaar Seyeeŋ nyɔɔt nba sa pukin i po.

ref: I have been paid back everything, and with interest. I am completely satisfied with the gifts you sent with Epaphroditus. They are like a sweet-smelling offering or like the right kind of sacrifice that pleases God.
text: N gaar i piinii na munna ki li jaŋŋima. Piinii nba ki i daan tun Epafrodaitus nann na te ki n gboowa. Li piinii naan nan mannu nba nubir mane ki teen Yennu, ki penn u par ni, ki u gaarir.

ref: I pray that God will take care of all your needs with the wonderful blessings that come from Christ Jesus!
text: Li paak, Yennu sa turi linba tee i loomm kur kaman nan u mɔkint nba yab biaŋinba sanpaapo, Yisa Masia paake.

ref: May God our Father be praised forever and ever. Amen. Final Greetings
text: Niib-ii dontir ti Baa Yennu sann mɔtana nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm. Foont joontii labaar

ref: Give my greetings to all who are God's people because of Christ Jesus. The Lord's followers here with me send you their greetings.
text: Yii foont Yisa Masia popeendamm kura. Yisaweira nba be nanin nna na foonti.

ref: All of God's people send their greetings, especially those in the service of the Emperor.
text: U popeendamm na kur foonti, ki mantik tee binba tee Kpanbar Siisa ŋaak toontunna na.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is also a follower.
text: Min Pɔɔl, wunba tee Yisa Masia toomii Yennu loomm paak na, nan ti naa bik Timoti, sɔbin gbouŋ na

ref: To God's people who live in Colossae and are faithful followers of Christ. I pray that God our Father will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: ki teen Yennu niib nba be Kolosa ni, ki tee ti naa waas nba teen Masia yada na. Ti Baa Yennu-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir. Pɔɔl nba teen Yennu niipoouk Yenmiaru ni biaŋinba

ref: Each time we pray for you, we thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Ti-i mei Yennu i paak yoonba, ti teen ŋɔɔ Yennu wunba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Baa na niipoouk yoo kura,

ref: We have heard of your faith in Christ and of your love for all God's people,
text: kimaan ti gbat ya teen Yisa Masia yada biaŋinba na, ki bia loon Yennu niib kura,

ref: because what you hope for is kept safe for you in heaven. You first heard about this hope when you believed the true message, which is the good news.
text: kimaan pinpiik ni ya din gbat barmɔnii barŋanii yoonba, i din gbat bonŋana nba guui Yendɔuŋ ni na poe.

ref: The good news is spreading all over the world with great success. It has spread in this same way among you, ever since the first day you learned the truth about God's wonderful kindness
text: Ki barŋanii maŋe baat nani kaman nan la yat ki kpaatir tingbouŋ na kur ni ki ŋammit niib binbeŋ, ki bia tuun nna i mɔŋ boor ni, la nyii daar nba ki i din gbat ki bann Yennu ninbatinu nan barmɔnii maŋ nba tee biaŋinba na.

ref: from our good friend Epaphras. He works together with us for Christ and is a faithful worker for you.
text: I din tumii Yennu ninbatinu maŋ po ti toontunlɔɔ Epafras wunba ki ti loonɔ na boor ni; ŋɔɔe tee Masia toontunnɔ nba tuun nan barmɔnii i boor ni ki turit,

ref: We have not stopped praying for you since the first day we heard about you. In fact, we always pray that God will show you everything he wants you to do and that you may have all the wisdom and understanding his Spirit gives.
text: Linba na paak, ta daan gbat i po, ti ki nyik Yenmiaru i paaki a ŋɔɔ Yennu-n te ki i bann u loomm nba tee biaŋinba, ŋaan turi Seyeeŋ yan nan li subinii kur,

ref: Then you will live a life that honors the Lord, and you will always please him by doing good deeds. You will come to know God even better.
text: ki yin fit kii be kaman nan ti Yomdaanɔ nba loon biaŋinba, ki bia fit kii tuun linba sii pent u par, ki yin fit kii tuun toonŋana kur, kii ŋammit bant Yennu.

ref: His glorious power will make you patient and strong enough to endure anything, and you will be truly happy.
text: Ŋɔɔ Yennu-n turi paŋ bonchiann kaman nan wa mɔk paŋ kur biaŋinba na, linba sa te ki i mɔk gbankur bonsiar kur ni, nan sukuru nan parpeenn.

ref: I pray that you will be grateful to God for letting you have part in what he has promised his people in the kingdom of light.
text: Yii teen ti Baa Yennu niipoouk man. Ŋɔɔe te ki ti jaŋ nan tin fit la yiar u boor, yentu ni, u niib sinsuuk ni,

ref: God rescued us from the dark power of Satan and brought us into the kingdom of his dear Son,
text: ki bia nyinnit Sintaanii bunbɔnn naan ni, ŋaan jiit nan laafia ki kɔɔnt u Bilonkak maŋ naan ni.

ref: Christ is exactly like God, who cannot be seen. He is the first-born Son, superior to all creation.
text: Sɔɔ kan fit la Yennu, ŋaan u Bija maŋ Masia tee u naauŋo. Ŋɔɔe gar bont kura, ki tee Yennu bonnankaa kura yur,

ref: Everything was created by him, everything in heaven and on earth, everything seen and unseen, including all forces and powers, and all rulers and authorities. All things were created by God's Son, and everything was made for him.
text: kimaan u nie ki Yennu din nan bonsiar kur nba be sanpaapo nan tingbouŋ na ni. Ŋɔɔe din nan ta laat bona nba nan ta ki laat bona nba, yentdamm, nan yomdamm, nan yudamm, nan yikodamm. Yennu din nan bonsiar kura ŋɔɔ Masia nie, ŋaan jii bonsiar kur turɔ.

ref: God's Son was before all else, and by him everything is held together.
text: Ŋɔɔ Masia din be ki bonsiar kura din kaa, ki u paake ki bont kur daa be.

ref: He is the head of his body, which is the church. He is the very beginning, the first to be raised from death, so that he would be above all others.
text: Ŋɔɔe tee u niib yur, ŋaan ki bi tee u gbanant. U niib maŋ manfoor nyi u boor nie. Ŋɔɔe tee Yennu Bija wunba din kpo ki fiir kuun ni sinsinn, a u kuukɔɔ-ii tee bonsiar kur yudaanɔ,

ref: God himself was pleased to live fully in his Son.
text: kimaan Yennu tiauŋ din dukin a li ŋan a Masia-ii mɔk ŋɔɔ Yennu ninnauŋ.

ref: And God was pleased for him to make peace by sacrificing his blood on the cross, so that all beings in heaven and on earth would be brought back to God.
text: Li paak, u Bija maŋ paake ki Yennu te ki bont kur nba be sanpaapo nan tiŋ ni ŋamm teemm u yɔɔsinba nan parmaasir; ki sɔn nba nyii u Bija maŋ ni dapunpunn paak na paake ki u te ki parmaasir be timm nan ŋɔɔ Yennu sinsuuk ni.

ref: You used to be far from God. Your thoughts made you his enemies, and you did evil things.
text: Sianyoo, i dudukbiit nan i toonbiit din te ki i tee Yennu dataie, ki fɔk nanɔ,

ref: But his Son became a human and died. So God made peace with you, and now he lets you stand in his presence as people who are holy and faultless and innocent.
text: ŋaan mɔtana, u Bija gbanant kuun maŋ paak, u ji teeni u yɔɔsinbae a wun jii-i ki yii tee popeendamm, ki daa mɔk jakint nan biit, ki baar u boor ni,

ref: But you must stay deeply rooted and firm in your faith. You must not give up the hope you received when you heard the good news. It was preached to everyone on earth, and I myself have become a servant of this message. Paul's Service to the Church
text: li paak, yii yabii man setik nan mɔnii, ki teen Yisa yada kii see chimm, ki daa fɔrii nyi dindann nba ki i la yoo nba ki i din gbat u barŋanii; ki bi din mɔɔntir barŋanii maŋ ki teen niib kur nba be tingbouŋ na ni; ki min Pɔɔl teen li toontunnɔ na. Pɔɔl nba teen Masia niib toontunnɔ biaŋinba

ref: I am glad I can suffer for you. I am pleased also that in my own body I can continue the suffering of Christ for his body, the church.
text: Ki mɔtana n mɔk parpeenn n fara dinu ni i paak, kimaan ma di fara maŋ biaŋinba na, n tontir fara dinu nba ki Masia din senn ma n di u gbanant nba tee u weira na paak na.

ref: God's plan was to make me a servant of his church and to send me to preach his complete message to you.
text: Ki Yennu nba turin toonn maŋ a n tun na, ŋɔɔe teenin Masia niib maŋ daabir i sommir paak, a niib-n bann Yennu barii maŋ mun.

ref: For ages and ages this message was kept secret from everyone, but now it has been explained to God's people.
text: Barii maŋe tee woonin ni maan nba ki Yennu din woon sianyoo, ki nibooru kur ki bannir, ŋaan ki u tan fiit wann u niib mɔtana.

ref: God did this because he wanted you Gentiles to understand his wonderful and glorious mystery. And the mystery is that Christ lives in you, and he is your hope of sharing in God's glory.
text: Yennu lor a wun fiit woonin ni maan maŋ ki wann u niib maŋe. U woonin ni maan maŋ ŋan bonchiann, ki tee niib kur yare. Li want nan Masia be i ni; li paak, i mɔk dindann nan i tan sii be fanu ki gbenn.

ref: We announce the message about Christ, and we use all our wisdom to warn and teach everyone, so all of Christ's followers will grow and become mature.
text: Li paak, Masia maŋ poe ki ti mɔɔntir, ki kpaan sɔɔ kura, ki want sɔɔ kur nan yan booru kur, a tin te sɔɔ kur-n chee nan Masia weiu, ki jiib wann Yennu.

ref: This is why I work so hard and use the mighty power he gives me.
text: Linba na paak ki n lek yabii nan n paŋ kur nba ki Masia turin ki li yab bonchiann.

ref: I want you to know what a struggle I am going through for you, for God's people at Laodicea, and for all of those followers who have never met me.
text: Kimaan n loon ki yin bann n baka nba be i ni biaŋinbae, nan Masia niib nba be Laodisea, nan binba daa ki mi lan nan bi ninbina na.

ref: I do it to encourage them. Then as their hearts are joined together in love, they will be wonderfully blessed with complete understanding. And they will truly know Christ. Not only is he the key to God's mystery,
text: N yaa bi para-n chee, ki bin lakin taan leeb nan loomm, ki bann Yisa barii paak, ki teenɔ yada nan barmɔnii, ki daa mɔk birsuku, ki bann Yennu woonin ni labaar maŋ, linba tee Masia po;

ref: but all wisdom and knowledge are hidden away in him.
text: ki ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔe mɔk barmɔnii yan nan bannu kura.

ref: I tell you these things to keep you from being fooled by fancy talk.
text: Li paak te ki n beeri, i daa te ki sɔɔ-n jii mɔmant ki kpanniini,

ref: Even though I am not with you, I keep thinking about you. I am glad to know that you are living as you should and your faith in Christ is strong. Christ Brings Real Life
text: kimaan ma lek ki be i peŋ nan n gbanant na, ŋaan n seek be nani, ki n par peen i paak ma la ya be fanu biaŋinba na, nan ya teen Masia yada nan barmɔnii biaŋinba na. Barmɔnii manfoor be Masia nie

ref: You have accepted Christ Jesus as your Lord. Now keep on following him.
text: Biaŋinba ki i din poŋ gaar Yisa Masia a wuu tee i Yomdaanɔ na, yii be u ni nna man,

ref: Plant your roots in Christ and let him be the foundation for your life. Be strong in your faith, just as you were taught. And be grateful.
text: ki sikin i yada jiina u ni, ki i yada-ii be u ni, ki i yada-ii mɔk paŋ kaman nan ba din wanni biaŋinba na, kii teenɔ niipoouk yoo kura.

ref: Don't let anyone fool you by using senseless arguments. These arguments may sound wise, but they are only human teachings. They come from the powers of this world and not from Christ.
text: Li paak, i daa te ki sɔɔ dia nisaarik yan kpinkpann-yana ki kpanni; linba ki gaa nan Masia po, ŋaan tee tingbouŋ na yan nan nisaarik wannu,

ref: God lives fully in Christ.
text: kimaan Masia gbanant nie ki Yennu ninnauŋ be,

ref: And you are fully grown because you belong to Christ, who is over every power and authority.
text: ki i gaar manfomunn yimm nan u benu ni. Ŋɔɔe tee yudamm nan paŋ damm kura paarɔ.

ref: Christ has also taken away your selfish desires, just as circumcision removes flesh from the body.
text: Yimm nan u benu nie ki i gaar punpotu; ŋaan li ki tee nisaarik nuu pot i gbananti; Masia punpotu na nee nyinnit biit ni,

ref: And when you were baptized, it was the same as being buried with Christ. Then you were raised to life because you had faith in the power of God, who raised Christ from death.
text: kimaan ba din wuri Yennu nyunwuru yoonba na i din pukin Masia poe ki bi pii-i, ki i bia fiir nanɔ nyunwuru maŋ ni, i yada nba be Yennu paŋ toona nba din fiin Masia kuun ni na paak.

ref: You were dead, because you were sinful and were not God's people. But God let Christ make you alive, when he forgave all our sins.
text: I din tee kpeemme sianyoo, kimaan i toonbiit paak nan ya din ki tee Juu teeb, ki bia din ki mɔk sennu na paak. Ŋaan mɔtana Yennu nyik chab i toonbiite, ki turi manfoor Masia ni.

ref: God wiped out the charges that were against us for disobeying the Law of Moses. He took them away and nailed them to the cross.
text: Ba din kpaau dapunpunn paak yoonba na, u din kpeen ti biit pann nba din sɔb dɔɔ na nan li sennu munn munn na.

ref: There Christ defeated all powers and forces. He let the whole world see them being led away as prisoners when he celebrated his victory.
text: Dapunpunn maŋ paake, ki Masia din biir Sintaanii nan narinbiit paŋ kura, ki din nyinn wa din nyannib biaŋinba na siara paanu ni.

ref: Don't let anyone tell you what you must eat or drink. Don't let them say you must celebrate the New Moon festival, the Sabbath, or any other festival.
text: Li paak, i daa te ki sɔɔ senti sennu ya sii di linba, amii ya sii nyu linba; i daa te ki sɔɔ gani jaamm dagaŋŋa, amii ŋmaapaaŋ daa, amii foon daa ki teeniini.

ref: These things are only a shadow of what was to come. But Christ is real!
text: Li sennu booru din tee yunyuŋo ki want nan sɔɔe baat; li yɔɔe tee Masia.

ref: Don't be cheated by people who make a show of acting humble and who worship angels. They brag about seeing visions. But it is all nonsense, because their minds are filled with selfish desires.
text: Sɔɔ-i yaa ŋɔɔe gar leeb ki laat yerintii ki loon a yi sikin i mɔŋ kii jiantir malakanba, i daa saak man; nna sa biiriwa. Li daanɔ dont u mɔŋ u dudukbiit ni, ŋaan ki li kaa paaki;

ref: They are no longer part of Christ, who is the head of the whole body. Christ gives the body its strength, and he uses its joints and muscles to hold it together, as it grows by the power of God. Christ Brings New Life
text: u ji ki dia Masia; ŋɔɔ Masia tee u niib yur, ŋaan taamm, ki bi gaa nan leeb kaman nan gbanant tukita nan li jiina na, ŋɔɔ Masiae dintib ki bi nɔbii kaman nan Yennu nba loon biaŋinba na. Barmɔnii manfoor tee Masia yare

ref: You died with Christ. Now the forces of the universe don't have any power over you. Why do you live as if you had to obey such rules as,
text: I kpo nan Masia, ki u fati dunia ni bona paŋ yent ni, ki bee ki i jin be kaman nan i bia daa tee tingbouŋ na yabi? Bee te ki i jɔbi i mɔŋ tee sennii nba yaa a

ref: “Don't handle this. Don't taste that. Don't touch this.”?
text: “A daa so nanna,” amii “A daa lemi linba na,” amii “A daa siit bonsia,”

ref: After these things are used, they are no longer good for anything. So why be bothered with the rules that humans have made up?
text: linba ki nirɔ sa di ki lin biir ki kan yukiri. Li sennii tee nisaarii wannue.

ref: Obeying these rules may seem to be the smart thing to do. They appear to make you love God more and to be very humble and to have control over your body. But they don't really have any power over our desires.
text: Setik nan mɔniie, li sennboorii naan linba mɔk yame, ŋaan li ki mɔki; bi jiantir ba loon biaŋinbae, ki sikint bi mɔŋ, ki mukis bi gbanant; ŋaan nirɔ-i wei linba na kur, li kan fit sommɔ ki wun dia u gbanant loommu.

ref: You have been raised to life with Christ. Now set your heart on what is in heaven, where Christ rules at God's right side.
text: I din fiir nan Masia kuun ni, li paak yi jii i para gaan nan sanpaapo bona, siaminba ki Masia kar Yennu niidiitu po na.

ref: Think about what is up there, not about what is here on earth.
text: Yii lor i dudukit nan leŋ bona na, ŋaan daa lorinn nan tingbouŋ na bona kaa,

ref: You died, which means that your life is hidden with Christ, who sits beside God.
text: kimaan i poŋ din kpo nan Masia, ki bɔr i manfoor nanɔ Yennu ni.

ref: Christ gives meaning to your life, and when he appears, you will also appear with him in glory.
text: Masia nba teent manfoor na nba tan sa dɔkit yoonba, i mun tan sa dɔkit nanɔe nan yentchiauŋ. Manfopaann nan manfokper labaar

ref: Don't be controlled by your body. Kill every desire for the wrong kind of sex. Don't be immoral or indecent or have evil thoughts. Don't be greedy, which is the same as worshiping idols.
text: Li paak, yi nyik tingbouŋ na ni niauŋ nba tuun toona i ni na; li yenninbae tee bonchonchonsin, nan linba tee jakint toona, nan kunkɔmaasu, nan nianbiiuk, nan nianchiauŋ, linba tee patmannu booru.

ref: God is angry with people who disobey him by doing these things.
text: Nanna paake te ki Yennu wutoor tan sa do binba ki sak u mɔb na paak.

ref: And this is exactly what you did, when you lived among people who behaved in this way.
text: Sianyoo li niauŋo din tuu yeni, ki i din be li ni.

ref: But now you must stop doing such things. You must quit being angry, hateful, and evil. You must no longer say insulting or cruel things about others.
text: Ŋaan mɔtana yi fian linba na nba munna ki lu; ki linba tee naŋ, nan wutoor, nan nifobiisin, nan niib sanbiiruk, nan barbiit, i daa te ki li nyi i mɔi ni.

ref: And stop lying to each other. You have given up your old way of life with its habits.
text: I daa fa faak ki teen leebi, kimaan i poŋ nyik linba tuu tee i binbenkper na nan li toonawa;

ref: Each of you is now a new person. You are becoming more and more like your Creator, and you will understand him better.
text: ŋaan ji mɔk linba tee binbenpaaŋe. Binbenpaaŋ nee ki Yennu nba tee li nantɔɔ na pantir yoo kura, ki li naan u mɔŋ binbeŋ, a wun te ki yin bannɔ setik nan mɔnii.

ref: It doesn't matter if you are a Greek or a Jew, or if you are circumcised or not. You may even be a barbarian or a Scythian, and you may be a slave or a free person. Yet Christ is all that matters, and he lives in all of us.
text: Li paak, bɔkitu ji kaa binba tee Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb sinsuuk ni; binba pot puna nan binba ki pot kura ji ki mɔk bɔkitu nan leebi; bɔkitu ji kaa muuk ni niib nan doo ni yab sinsuuk ni; bɔkitu bia kaa daaba nan yentinmɔŋ damm sinsuuk ni, ŋaan i kur tee Masia yab, ki u be i kur ni.

ref: God loves you and has chosen you as his own special people. So be gentle, kind, humble, meek, and patient.
text: Li paak, ya tee Yennu nigannkab ki tee popeendamm ki bia tee u walonkai na, yii mɔk ninkpabauŋ, nan burchint, nan sikininmɔŋ, nan soonna binbeŋ, nan sukuru,

ref: Put up with each other, and forgive anyone who does you wrong, just as Christ has forgiven you.
text: kii di leeb sukuru; ki sɔɔ-i mɔk bunburimii u lɔɔ paak, wun nyik chabɔ. Li kpaa talas ki yin mun nyik chab leeb kaman nan ti Yomdaanɔ nba poŋ nyik chabi biaŋinba na.

ref: Love is more important than anything else. It is what ties everything completely together.
text: Yi pukin loomm; ŋanne fan ki gar binbeŋ kur, ki taan dia li kura nan barmɔnii mɔtaauk.

ref: Each one of you is part of the body of Christ, and you were chosen to live together in peace. So let the peace that comes from Christ control your thoughts. And be grateful.
text: Yi ŋaan ki Masia parmaasir nba na-ii dia ya dukii linba; Yennu lakin yiini gbananyennkɔɔ ni a yii mɔk parmaasir maŋe. Li paak, yii teen Yennu niipoouk man.

ref: Let the message about Christ completely fill your lives, while you use all your wisdom to teach and instruct each other. With thankful hearts, sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs to God.
text: Li kpaa talas ki Masia labaar na-ii be i para ni chib chib, kii want i leeb, kii bia kpaan i leeb nan i yan kur; kii yiin Yennu gbouŋ ni yanii, nan yenpaku yanii, nan Seyeeŋ yanii; kii yiin yanii kii teen Yennu nan niipoouk i para ni.

ref: Whatever you say or do should be done in the name of the Lord Jesus, as you give thanks to God the Father because of him. Some Rules for Christian Living
text: Li paak, ya tuun linba, amii ya yeen linba, yii tuun nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann ni, kii teen ti Baa Yennu niipoouk u sann ni. Masia niib binbeŋ labaar

ref: A wife must put her husband first. This is her duty as a follower of the Lord.
text: Poob, yi jii i mɔŋ tur i sɔrib, kaman nan la kpaa talas nan Masia niib-ii tuun biaŋinba.

ref: A husband must love his wife and not abuse her.
text: Poosɔrib, yii loon i poob man, ki daa dia nibisin benu ki be nammi.

ref: Children must always obey their parents. This pleases the Lord.
text: Waas, yii saak i damm mɔi yoo kura, kimaan ŋanne pent Yennu par ni.

ref: Parents, don't be hard on your children. If you are, they might give up.
text: Baanba, i daa dont i waas wutoa, i-i tee nna bi sii mɔk parbiire.

ref: Slaves, you must always obey your earthly masters. Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. Honor the Lord and serve your masters with your whole heart.
text: Daaba, yii saak i yomdamm mɔi bonsiar kur ni, ki li daa tee yoo nba ki bi gorii-i na kuukɔɔ kaa, a yin penn bi para ki gaar pinpakiti, ŋaan yii tuun nan nunmɔnn yoo kura, kimaan ya baak ti Yomdaanɔ biaŋinba na.

ref: Do your work willingly, as though you were serving the Lord himself, and not just your earthly master.
text: I-i tuun linba kur, yin tun nan nunmɔnn kaman nan i tuun teen ti Yomdaanɔe, ŋaan ki li ki tee nisaarik kaa.

ref: In fact, the Lord Christ is the one you are really serving, and you know he will reward you.
text: Yi tian man nan ti Yomdaanɔ maŋ tan sa turi nyɔɔt nba tee u niib yar sanpaapo, kimaan Masia tee barmɔnii Yomdaanɔ ki i tuun teenɔ.

ref: But Christ has no favorites! He will punish evil people, just as they deserve.
text: Ki biitdaanɔ mun tan sa gaar u toonbiit tubdatu, wa tun linba paak, kimaan Yennu tan sa dat u tuba, ki lukitin kaa.

ref: Slave owners, be fair and honest with your slaves. Don't forget that you have a Master in heaven.
text: Yomdamm, yii tuun fanu tur i daaba man, kii mi nan i mɔŋ mun mɔk Yomdaanɔ Yendɔuŋ ni. Kpaanii labaar

ref: Never give up praying. And when you pray, keep alert and be thankful.
text: Yii yabii nan Yenmiaru man, kii mei ŋɔɔ Yennu nan nunmɔnn, ki turɔ niipoouk,

ref: Be sure to pray that God will make a way for us to spread his message and explain the mystery about Christ, even though I am in jail for doing this.
text: kii biakii meiɔ kii teent ki tin fit la yaak ki mɔɔnt barŋanii ki fiit Masia woonin ni barii, kimaan barii maŋ paake te ki n kɔɔ dansarik na.

ref: Please pray that I will make the message as clear as possible.
text: Li paak, yii mei Yennu kii teenin ki n fit pak kaman nan la kpaa talas nan min pakir fanu biaŋinba na.

ref: When you are with unbelievers, always make good use of the time.
text: I mi man i binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba ki tur binba ki tee Masia niib na, kii jii i yoo kii tuun fanu.

ref: Be pleasant and hold their interest when you speak the message. Choose your words carefully and be ready to give answers to anyone who asks questions. Final Greetings
text: Li kpaa talas ki i mɔbona-ii man, kii mɔk baakir, ki yii mi biaŋinba ki i sa gat buboit ki lii took ki tur sɔɔ kura. Foontjoontii labaar

ref: Tychicus is the dear friend, who faithfully works and serves the Lord with us, and he will give you the news about me.
text: Ti naa bilonkak Tikikus, wunba tee yada daanɔ, ki bia tee ti toontunanlɔɔ ti Yomdaanɔ ni na sa beti n labaar.

ref: I am sending him to cheer you up by telling you how we are getting along.
text: Li paake te ki n tumɔ i boor ni, ma wun beti ti kur nba be biaŋinba, ki i para-n dɔɔr.

ref: Onesimus, the dear and faithful follower from your own group, is coming with him. The two of them will tell you everything that has happened here.
text: Ŋɔɔ nan Onesimus, wunba tee ti naa bilonkak nba mɔk yada ki tee i yɔɔ nee sa saan. Bi sa beti bonsiar kura nba tuun nna na.

ref: Aristarchus is in jail with me. He sends greetings to you, and so does Mark, the cousin of Barnabas. You have already been told to welcome Mark, if he visits you.
text: Aristakus, min nan wunba kɔɔ dansarik na foonti, ki Banabas niipoo bija Mak mun bia foonti.

ref: Jesus, who is known as Justus, sends his greetings. These three men are the only Jewish followers who have worked with me for the kingdom of God. They have given me much comfort.
text: Joshua, wunba sann yi Justus na mun teeni u foontii. Bi banntaa nba na kuukɔɔe tee Juu teeb nba waa Masia ki tuun nanin Yennu naan toona maŋ ni. Ki bi maak n par.

ref: Your own Epaphras, who serves Christ Jesus, sends his greetings. He always prays hard that you may fully know what the Lord wants you to do and that you may do it completely.
text: Epafras wunba mun bia tee i yenɔ nan Yisa Masia toontunnɔ na foonti. U mei Yennu nan nunmɔnn yoo kura ki teeni a ŋɔɔ Yennu-n te ki yin set kenken, kii tee nisubirteeb nba tuun Yennu loomm na,

ref: I have seen how much trouble he has gone through for you and for the followers in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
text: kimaan n dii u siara nan u set tun bonchiann ki sommiti nan Laodisea teeb nan Hierapoli teeb.

ref: Our dear doctor Luke sends you his greetings, and so does Demas.
text: Ti naa bilonkak Luk, wunba tee baat-tebirɔ na, ŋɔɔ nan Demas foonti.

ref: Give my greetings to the followers at Laodicea, especially to Nympha and the church that meets in her home.
text: Yii foont ti naa walonkai nba be Laodisea na, ki bia foont ti niipoo Nimfa nan Masiaweira nba lakin leeb u ŋaak ni a bin jiantir Yennu na.

ref: After this letter has been read to your people, be sure to have it read in the church at Laodicea. And you should read the letter that I have sent to them.
text: I-i karin gbouŋ na gbenn yoonba, yin yabir ki bia karin ki tur Masiaweira nba mun be Laodisea na, ki bia karin Laodisea teeb gbouŋ nba ki n daan sɔb turib na.

ref: Remind Archippus to do the work that the Lord has given him to do.
text: Yi bet Akipus a wun yabir u mɔŋ kii tuun toona nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ din turɔ na fanu.

ref: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL. Don't forget that I am in jail. I pray that God will be kind to you.
text: N sɔb linba na nan n tiauŋ nuue: Min Pɔɔl foonti. I daa tammit ma baa nan bant na po. Yennu-n sommi.

ref: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God the Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ. I pray that God will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Min Pɔɔl nan Sailas nan Timotie sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen niib nba be Tesalonika ki tee ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nigannkab na; ŋɔɔ Yennu-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir. Tesalonika teeb yada nan bi binbeŋ labaar

ref: We thank God for you and always mention you in our prayers. Each time we pray,
text: Ti teen Yennu niipoouk i kura paak, ŋaan tian i po yoo kur ti Yenmiaru ni,

ref: we tell God our Father about your faith and loving work and about your firm hope in our Lord Jesus Christ. The Thessalonians' Faith and Example
text: kimaan Yennu nba tee ti Baa na tɔɔnn po, ti tian biaŋinba ki i teenɔ yada ki tuun toonŋana, nan i loomm nba te ki i tuun nan nunmɔnn biaŋinba, ki i dindann be ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia ni chib chib biaŋinba.

ref: My dear friends, God loves you, and we know he has chosen you to be his people.
text: Ti naa waas, ti mi nan Yennue looni ki poŋ ganni a yii tee u yab,

ref: When we told you the good news, it was with the power and assurance that come from the Holy Spirit, and not simply with words. You knew what kind of people we were and how we helped you.
text: kimaan barŋanii na din ki tee ti mɔb ni mɔmaan kuukɔɔ kaa ki ti din baar nann turiini, ŋaan li bia din mɔk Seyeeŋ yikooe ki ti piakir ki birsuk kaa. Yoo nba ki ti din be i peŋ na, i mi ta din be biaŋinba; i sommir paake ki ti din be nna maŋ.

ref: So, when you accepted the message, you followed our example and the example of the Lord. You suffered, but the Holy Spirit made you glad.
text: I din tokii biaŋinba ki ti din waa ti Yomdaanɔ, ki din gaar barŋanii maŋ i farchiauŋ ni nan parpeenn nba nyi Seyeeŋ boor ni na.

ref: You became an example for all the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia.
text: Ki ya din tuun biaŋinba na din wann ti naa waas nba be Masedonia nan Akaya tinii ni na mun nba sii tuun biaŋinba na.

ref: And because of you, the Lord's message has spread everywhere in those regions. Now the news of your faith in God is known all over the world, and we don't have to say a thing about it.
text: Kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ barŋanii maŋ din nyii i boore, ki lin Masedonia nan Akaya tinii ni, ki linba bia pukin, ya teen Yennu yada biaŋinba poŋ lin lokir kur powa. Li paak, li ji ki kpaa talas nan ki ti pak siar bi boori.

ref: Everyone is talking about how you welcomed us and how you turned away from idols to serve the true and living God.
text: Niib na kur piak jiint ya din gaarit biaŋinba ta din foonti yoonba na, bi piak biaŋinba ki i tunn nyik pata ŋaan baar Yennu boore, a yii jiantir ŋɔɔ Yennu nba tee manfodaanɔ na,

ref: They also tell how you are waiting for his Son Jesus to come from heaven. God raised him from death, and on the day of judgment Jesus will save us from God's anger.
text: ki guur u Bija Yisa, wunba tan sa nyi Yendɔuŋ ni ki baar na; wunba ki u din fiinɔ kuun ni, ki u fatit Yennu wutoor nba baat na ni na.

ref: My friends, you know our time with you wasn't wasted.
text: Ti naa waas, i mɔŋ mi nan ta din baar foonti na, li tan ki teen yanni.

ref: As you remember, we had been mistreated and insulted at Philippi. But God gave us the courage to tell you the good news about him, even though many people caused us trouble.
text: I mi nan ta lek daa din be Filipi doo ni, ki bi dinnit fara nan fei biaŋinba, ki see ti paak bonchiann na, ŋaan ta din baar i boor ni, ti din mɔk parcheenn nba nyi Yennu boor ni nan tin mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋ turi.

ref: We didn't have any hidden motives when we won you over, and we didn't try to fool or trick anyone.
text: Kimaan ta din beeri linba na din ki tee linba din nyii faak nan dudukbiit ni, amii ti din ki kpanni kaa,

ref: You also know we didn't try to flatter anyone. God himself knows what we did wasn't a cover-up for greed.
text: Kimaan i mɔŋ mi fanu nan Yennu tee ti siara daanɔ nan ti din ki baar i boor ni nan mɔmant kaa, amii maan nba dɔkin ti nianchiauŋ.

ref: We were not trying to get you or anyone else to praise us.
text: Ti din ki loon i sɔɔ-n pakiti, amii nisɔɔ.

ref: But as apostles, we could have demanded help from you. After all, Christ is the one who sent us. We chose to be like children or like a mother nursing her baby.
text: Ta tee Masia toomiinba na, ti din mɔk paŋ nba ki ti din bo sa fit boi li powa, ŋaan ti din be i sinsuuk ni soonnae, ki din tee kaman nan biyɔɔrɔɔ nba yɔɔr waas biaŋinba na.

ref: We cared so much for you, and you became so dear to us, that we were willing to give our lives for you when we gave you God's message.
text: Ta din looni bonchiann na paak, ti din mɔɔntir Yennu barŋanii ki teeni; li kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan ti bia din jii ti mɔŋ mɔŋ manfoa ki likin saaŋmanii i paak, kimaan i mantik teen ti niloonkab ki li yabita.

ref: My dear friends, you surely haven't forgotten our hard work and hardships. You remember how night and day we struggled to make a living, so we could tell you God's message without being a burden to anyone.
text: Ti naa waas, setik nan barmɔniie, i mi ta din tuun di fara biaŋinba. Ta din be i boor ni ki mɔɔntir Yennu barŋanii maŋ ki teeni na, ti din tuun yennu ni nan nyiɔko, a ti daa teeni farsiari.

ref: Both you and God are witnesses that we were pure and honest and innocent in our dealings with you followers of the Lord.
text: Yimm nan Yennue tee ti siaradamm ki jiin ta din be nani nan binbenyeeŋ nan yentinu, ki kaa biit biaŋinba na po.

ref: You also know we did everything for you that parents would do for their own children.
text: I mi biaŋinba ki ti din kpaani kaman nan waas baa nba tuu kpaamm biaŋinba na,

ref: We begged, encouraged, and urged each of you to live in a way that would honor God. He is the one who chose you to share in his own kingdom and glory.
text: ki maant i para, ki lek tun kpaani a yii be binbensiak nba sa gboo Yennu numm. Ŋɔɔe yiini a wun kɔɔni u tiauŋ naan ni nan yentchiauŋ.

ref: We always thank God that you believed the message we preached. It came from him, and it isn't something made up by humans. You accepted it as God's message, and now he is working in you.
text: Linba yaa paak bia te ki ti piak Yennu yoo kura na tee nnee: ta din baar nan Yennu maan i boor ni, i din gbatir ki gaarir; i din ki gaarir kaman nan nisaarik yar na kaa, ŋaan i din gaarir kaman nan li tee Yennu yare. Ki li set tee ŋanne, kimaan yimm nba teenɔ yada na, maan maŋ tuun i para ni.

ref: My friends, you did just like God's churches in Judea and like the other followers of Christ Jesus there. And so, you were mistreated by your own people, in the same way they were mistreated by their people.
text: Ti naa waas, li muksubooru nba baari na, li muksubooru mun din baar binba tee Yennu niib Yisa Masia paak na Judea tiŋ ni; yimm nan ŋamm dii muksuboor-yeŋkɔɔe. I mɔŋ mɔŋ niib nba mukisi biaŋinba na, li muksuboorue ki bi niib, Juu teeb, mun din mukisib.

ref: Those evil people killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and they even chased us away. God doesn't like what they do and neither does anyone else.
text: Ŋamm Juu teebe din kpii ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nan Yennu sɔkiniinba, ki bia mukis timm na. Linba na paak, bi ki penn Yennu par ni, ki bia ki penn nirɔ kur par ni.

ref: They keep us from speaking his message to the Gentiles and from leading them to be saved. They have always gone too far with their sins. Now God has finally become angry and will punish them. Paul Wants To Visit the Church Again
text: Bi yabii bi mɔŋ paak gbaa a bin gɔɔrit ki ti daa mɔɔntir Yennu barŋanii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na a bin la tinnu. Linba nee want nan bi poŋ pukin bi toonbiit nba ki bi tuu tuune mɔkmɔk nan Yennu wutoor nba ji daan tan baar nan tubdatu bi paak. Pɔɔl nba loon wun bia gɔn Tesalonika teeb biaŋinba

ref: My friends, we were kept from coming to you for a while, but we never stopped thinking about you. We were eager to see you and tried our best to visit you in person.
text: Ti naa waas, la jiit yoo waaminna ki ti ki be i boor ni na, ti tee nan kpeebii nee, ŋaan ti dudukit ŋanin be nani, ki ti lek bia yabii a ti baar lai nan ti ninbina,

ref: We really wanted to come. I myself tried several times, but Satan always stopped us.
