text: kimaan yoo kur, min Pɔɔl lek loon ki n baar i boor nee, ŋaan ki Sintaanii gɔin sɔnu.

ref: After all, when the Lord Jesus appears, who else but you will give us hope and joy and be like a glorious crown for us?
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa-i jen yoonba, yimme sii tee ti dindann nan ti nyɔɔt, ki i paake ki ti tan sa set u boor nan parpeenn nan yuugontir.

ref: You alone are our glory and joy!
text: Setik nan mɔnii, i paake ki ti mɔk sann nan parpeenn.

ref: Finally, we couldn't stand it any longer. We decided to stay in Athens by ourselves
text: Li paak, la ji daan tan paak ti nii yoonba, ti daan sak biar Atens doo nie ti kuukɔɔ,

ref: and send our friend Timothy to you. He works with us as God's servant and preaches the good news about Christ. We wanted him to make you strong in your faith and to encourage you.
text: ŋaan daan tun ti naa bik Timoti, wunba ki timm nan ŋɔɔ taan tuun ki teen Yennu ki mɔɔntir Masia barŋanii, a wun chee i yada ki kɔɔnni para,

ref: We didn't want any of you to be discouraged by all these troubles. You knew we would have to suffer,
text: ki lin te ki i sɔɔ daa ŋmat poor muksuk na paaki. I mɔŋ mi nan li muksubooru tee Yennu loomme ki turit,

ref: because when we were with you, we told you this would happen. And we did suffer, as you well know.
text: kimaan ta din daa be i boor ni yoonba na, ti din beti a bi tan sa mukisit; ki mɔtana i mɔŋ mi fanu nan li set tan baar tee nnee chib chib.

ref: At last, when I could not wait any longer, I sent Timothy to find out about your faith. I hoped Satan had not tempted you and made all our work useless.
text: Li paak, la daan tan paak n nii yoonba, ki n daan tun Timoti, a wun saa bann a i nyik Masia sɔnu amii, ki laan Sintaanii bikini, ki ti toona fara kur teen yannee?

ref: Timothy has now come back from his visit with you and has told us about your faith and love. He also said that you always have happy memories of us and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you.
text: Mɔtana Timoti poŋ jenna, ki turit labaar nba penn ti para ni ki jikit saa i yada nan i loomm po. U betit a i kpan tun tian ti poe fanu, ki loon yin lat kaman nan ta loon tin lai biaŋinba na.

ref: My friends, even though we have a lot of trouble and suffering, your faith makes us feel better about you.
text: Ti naa waas, ti be fara nan parbiir kur ni, ŋaan ta gbat i yada po, li te ki ti para dɔɔre.

ref: Your strong faith in the Lord is like a breath of new life.
text: Mɔtana, li set maŋŋit nan ya set chib ti Yomdaanɔ sɔnu ni na paak.

ref: How can we possibly thank God enough for all the happiness you have brought us?
text: Ti teen Yennu niipoouk bonchiann i paak, kimaan ta mɔk parpeenn biaŋinba u boor ni i paak na.

ref: Day and night we sincerely pray that we will see you again and help you to have an even stronger faith.
text: Nyiɔk nan yennu kur ti meiɔ nan ti para kura a wun te ki timm nan yimm-n la leeb ki tin ŋamm i yada.

ref: We pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus will let us visit you.
text: Ti Baa Yennu tiauŋ nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa-n ŋamm sɔnu turit ki ti baar i boor ni.

ref: May the Lord make your love for each other and for everyone else grow by leaps and bounds. This is how our love for you has grown.
text: Ya loon i leeb nan sɔɔ kur biaŋinba na, wun te ki i loomm maŋ-n pukin bonchiann kaman nan ta looni biaŋinba na.

ref: And when our Lord comes with all his people, I pray he will make your hearts pure and innocent in the sight of God the Father.
text: Nnee ki u sa chee i para, ki i sii tee popeendamm nba ki mɔk biit ti Baa Yennu boor ni, nan yoo nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa tan sa jen nan u yab kura na.

ref: Finally, my dear friends, since you belong to the Lord Jesus, we beg and urge you to live as we taught you. Then you will please God. You are already living that way, but try even harder.
text: Ti naa waas, ya din tumii ti peŋ biaŋinba ki i sii tuun ki pent Yennu par ni na, nnee ki i poŋ din tuun. Mɔtana, ti barimiie ki kpaani ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann paak, yii ŋammit kii tuun nna man,

ref: Remember the instructions we gave you as followers of the Lord Jesus.
text: kimaan i mi kpaanii nba ki ti din turi ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann ni na.

ref: God wants you to be holy, so don't be immoral in matters of sex.
text: Kimaan Yennu loon ki yii tee popeendamm ki daa tuun bonchonchonn toona.

ref: Don't be a slave of your desires or live like people who don't know God.
text: ki daa tuun nna nan nianbiiuk binbeŋ kaman nan binba ki mi Yennu nba be biaŋinba na.

ref: You must not cheat any of the Lord's followers in matters of sex. Remember, we warned you that he punishes everyone who does such things.
text: Sɔɔ daa tuun biir u lɔɔ nba waa Masia nan li biitbooru ni, amii kii ŋmab u lɔɔ. Ti poŋ din beti li powa, ŋaan bia kpaani nan nunmɔnn a Yennu tan sa dat binba tuun nna tuba.

ref: God didn't choose you to be filthy, but to be pure.
text: Yennu ki yiint a tii tee biitdamm kaa, ŋaan u yaa tii tee popeendamme.

ref: So if you don't obey these rules, you are not really disobeying us. Instead, you are disobeying God, who gives you his Holy Spirit.
text: Li paak, wunba ki sak wannu nba na, li ki tee nisaarik yar kaa ki u yêeni, ŋaan u yêen Yennu nba teeni u Seyeeŋ na mɔŋ mɔŋe.

ref: We don't have to write you about the need to love each other. God has taught you to do this,
text: Li ki kpaa talas ki tin sɔb gbouŋ ki tiari ya sii loon Masiaweira biaŋinba. Yennu mɔŋe poŋ wanni biaŋinba ki i sii loon i leeb.

ref: and you already have shown your love for all his people in Macedonia. But, my dear friends, we ask you to do even more.
text: Ki i poŋ set tuun li biaŋe na, ki loon ti naa waas kur nba be Masedonia tiŋ ni na. Ti naa waas, ti barimiie a yii ŋammit kii tuun nna man,

ref: Try your best to live quietly, to mind your own business, and to work hard, just as we taught you to do.
text: ki ŋaan ki lii tee i dudukit ki yii be soonna, ki i baka-ii be i mɔŋ toona ni, kii tuun i tiauŋ toona kii di, kaman nan ta poŋ din beti biaŋinba sinsinn na.

ref: Then you will be respected by people who are not followers of the Lord, and you won't have to depend on anyone. The Lord's Coming
text: Ŋanne sa te ki binba ki tee Masiaweira na-n turi nibaakir, ki li ji kii kpaa talas ki yin jɔɔb i mɔŋ ki denn sɔɔ i bonlonkara paaki. Ti Yomdaanɔ jenu labaar

ref: My friends, we want you to understand how it will be for those followers who have already died. Then you won't grieve over them and be like people who don't have any hope.
text: Ti naa waas, ti loon ki yin bann barmɔniie ki jikit saa Masiaweira nba kpo na po, ki daa mɔk parbiir kaman nan binba ki mɔk dindann na.

ref: We believe Jesus died and was raised to life. We also believe that when God brings Jesus back again, he will bring with him all who had faith in Jesus before they died.
text: Ti teen yada nan Yisa din kpo ŋaan bia fiir kuun niwa; li paak, ki ti bia teen yada nan Yennu tan sa te ki Yisa-n jen nan u weira nba din kpo na.

ref: Our Lord Jesus told us that when he comes, we won't go up to meet him ahead of his followers who have already died.
text: Ta wanti linba na tee ti Yomdaanɔ mɔmaame: ti Yomdaanɔ maŋ jenu daar, damm nba tan sii be bi manfoa ni na kan dɔŋ binba din kpo na ki chet Masia sanpaapo,

ref: With a loud command and with the shout of the chief angel and a blast of God's trumpet, the Lord will return from heaven. Then those who had faith in Christ before they died will be raised to life.
text: kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ tan sa nyi Yendɔuŋ ni ki sik nan tintian paari, nan malaka saakɔɔ yiiniie, nan Yennu kikaak mɔnii, ki Masiaweira nba din kpo na tan sa fiir sinsinn;

ref: Next, all of us who are still alive will be taken up into the clouds together with them to meet the Lord in the sky. From that time on we will all be with the Lord forever.
text: li yoo, damm nba tan sii be bi manfoa ni na nan ŋamm tan sa chet ti Yomdaanɔ sanpaapo; ki timm nan ti Yomdaanɔ ji tan sa taan leeb ki be nan yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: Encourage each other with these words.
text: Li paak, yii dia mɔmaan na kii maant i leeb para man.

ref: I don't need to write you about the time or date when all this will happen.
text: Ti naa waas, li ki kpaa talas ki tin sɔb wanni yoo nba ki linba na tan sa tume,

ref: You surely know that the Lord's return will be as a thief coming at night.
text: kimaan i mɔŋ poŋ mi fanu nan ti Yomdaanɔ jenu daar maŋ tan sii tee kaman nan nanyukɔɔ nba tuu baat nyiɔk biaŋinba nee.

ref: People will think they are safe and secure. But destruction will suddenly strike them like the pains of a woman about to give birth. And they won't escape.
text: Yoo nba ki niib tan sii yeen a “Parmaasir nan laafia be na,” li yoo maŋe ki biiru tan sa baarib yiama kaman nan matyiaru nba baat poo biaŋinba na, ŋaan bi tan kan fit fat bi mɔŋ li barbiiuk ni.

ref: My dear friends, you don't live in darkness, and so that day won't surprise you like a thief.
text: Ti naa waas, yimm ki be bunbɔnn ni kaa; li paak, li daar tan kii tee kaman nan nanyukɔɔ baaru na kaa i boor ni,

ref: You belong to the light and live in the day. We don't live in the night or belong to the dark.
text: kimaan i tee yentu waase, ti ki tee nyiɔk yab kaa; ti ki be bunbɔnn ni kaa.

ref: Others may sleep, but we should stay awake and be alert.
text: Li paak, ti daa dɔɔ gɔɔnt kaman nan nisiab nba tuun biaŋinba na, ŋaan tii nyɔi kii sub man,

ref: People sleep during the night, and some even get drunk.
text: kimaan binba gɔɔnt na gɔɔnt nyiɔko, ki binba nyu daan yib na mun yib nyiɔk.

ref: But we belong to the day. So we must stay sober and let our faith and love be like a suit of armor. Our firm hope that we will be saved is our helmet.
text: Ŋaan timm nba ki tee nyiɔk yab na, tii sub man, ki jii yada nan loomm lia kaman nan naagbankɔŋŋir na, ki bia jii tinnu dindann ki yir kaman nan naagbankɔŋŋir na.

ref: God doesn't intend to punish us, but wants us to be saved by our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Kimaan Yennu din ki gannit a wun tan dat ti tuba kaa, ŋaan u gannit a tin la tinnu ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak;

ref: Christ died for us, so we could live with him, whether we are alive or dead when he comes.
text: ŋɔɔe din gaar ti paak ki kpo, a ti-i lekii kpo amii ti-i be u jenu yoo maŋ, timm nan ŋɔɔ-n tan taan kii be.

ref: This is why you must encourage and help each other, just as you are already doing. Final Instructions and Greetings
text: Li paak, yii kɔɔnt i leeb para ki somm i leeb kaman nan ya tuun biaŋinba mɔtana na. Kpaanjoontii nan foontjoontii labaar

ref: My friends, we ask you to be thoughtful of your leaders who work hard and tell you how to live for the Lord.
text: Ti naa waas, ti barimiie a i baka-ii be binba tuun ti Yomdaanɔ toonn nani, ki tee i yudamm u sɔnu ni, ki teeni kpaanii.

ref: Show them great respect and love because of their work. Try to get along with each other.
text: Yii teemm nibaakir nan loomm nba yab, kimaan bi toona nba ki bi tuun na paak. I mɔŋ-ii be nan i leeb nan parmaasir man.

ref: My friends, we beg you to warn anyone who isn't living right. Encourage anyone who feels left out, help all who are weak, and be patient with everyone.
text: Ti naa waas, ti barimiie a yi kpaan gbannyakdamm, ki maŋ jaŋmaandamm para, ki somm tarii, ki di sukuru nan sɔɔ kura.

ref: Don't be hateful to people, just because they are hateful to you. Rather, be good to each other and to everyone else.
text: I kpan daa tuun biit ki jiint biit paaki, ŋaan yii te ki lii tee i lor yoo kur nan yin tun linba ŋan ki tur i leeb nan sɔɔ kur man.

ref: Always be joyful
text: Yii mɔk parpeenn yoo kur man,

ref: and never stop praying.
text: kii mei Yennu yoo kur man,

ref: Whatever happens, keep thanking God because of Jesus Christ. This is what God wants you to do.
text: kii teen Yennu niipoouk bonsiar kur ni, kimaan ŋanne tee Yennu nba loon biaŋinba, ya tee Yisa Masiaweira na paak.

ref: Don't turn away God's Spirit
text: I daa gɔi Yennu Seyeeŋ nba loon biaŋinba;

ref: or ignore prophecies.
text: i daa fiakit Yennu sɔkiniinba maami,

ref: Put everything to the test. Accept what is good
text: ŋaan yi tuu mintaa ki bikin bonsiar kur, ki gaar linba ŋan,

ref: and don't have anything to do with evil.
text: ki nyi biitbooru booru kura ni.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will make you completely holy. And may your spirit, soul, and body be kept healthy and faultless until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
text: Yennu nba teent parmaasir na tiauŋ-n teeni popeendamm ki gbenn, ki dia i sei nan i manfoa nan i gbanant mun, ki li daa mɔk biit ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia jenu maŋ po.

ref: The one who chose you can be trusted, and he will do this.
text: Yennu nba yiini na sa sak tun nna, kimaan u tee barmɔnii daanɔe.

ref: Friends, please pray for us.
text: Ti naa waas, yii mei Yennu ki turit man.

ref: Give the Lord's followers a warm greeting.
text: Yii foont Masiaweira na kura ki kpammii.

ref: In the name of the Lord I beg you to read this letter to all his followers.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ sann ni ki n kpaani, yi karin gbouŋ na tur Masiaweira kur.

ref: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God our Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Min Pɔɔl nan Sailas nan Timoti sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masiaweira nba be Tesalonika ki bia tee Yennu yab na.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! When Christ Returns
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir. Masia baaru labaar

ref: My dear friends, we always have good reason to thank God for you, because your faith in God and your love for each other keep growing all the time.
text: Ti naa waas, li kpaa talase nan tii teen Yennu niipoouk yoo kur i paak. Ti-i tun nna, li ŋan, kimaan i yada mantik pukii bonchiann, ki ya mɔk loomm nba ki teen i leeb na pukii.

ref: This is why we brag about you to all of God's churches. We tell them how patient you are and how you keep on having faith, even though you are going through a lot of trouble and suffering.
text: Li paak te ki ti mɔk yuugontir i paak Yennu niib boor ni, i sukuru nan yada nba ki i mɔk i muksuk nan i fara ni, kii lek be nan gbankur na.

ref: All this shows that God judges fairly and is making you fit to share in his kingdom for which you are suffering.
text: Linba nee want nan Yennu barbunu tee mɔnii; li paak ki u tan sa yet a i jaŋ nan i be u naan ni; u naan maŋ paake ki i di fara maŋ.

ref: It is only right for God to punish everyone who is causing you trouble,
text: Yennue tuun linba took; u sa te ki binba dinti fara maŋ na-n mun di fara nnee,

ref: but he will give you relief from your troubles. God will do the same for us, when the Lord Jesus comes from heaven with his powerful angels
text: ki fat yimm nba di fara na, ki mun bia fatit. Yennu tan sa tun linba na yoo nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa nan u yabint malakanba tan sa nyi Yendɔuŋ ni ki baar nan mupeenu,

ref: and with a flaming fire. Our Lord Jesus will punish anyone who doesn't know God and won't obey his message.
text: ki tan dat binba ki sak u barŋanii ki bia ki mi Yennu na tuba;

ref: Their punishment will be eternal destruction, and they will be kept far from the presence of our Lord and his glorious strength.
text: ki ti yabint Yomdaanɔ nba be yentchiauŋ ni tan sa nyinnib u boor ni, ki bi sa la biiru nba kaa gbennu;

ref: This will happen on the day when the Lord returns to be praised and honored by all who have faith in him and belong to him. This includes you, because you believed what we said.
text: u tan sa tun nnee daar nba ki u tan sa baar ki gaar dontir nan paku u weira kur nba teenɔ yada na boor ni. I mun tan sii tee bi yemme, kimaan i poŋ teen barŋanii nba ki ti din beti na yadawa.

ref: God chose you, and we keep praying that God will make you worthy of being his people. We pray for God's power to help you do all the good things you hope to do and your faith makes you want to do.
text: Li paake ki ti mei Yennu yoo kur ki teeni. Ti meiɔ a wun te ki yin jaŋ nan manfoor nba ki u din ganni a i taŋŋi be leŋ na nie. Ti meiɔ a wun jii u paŋ ki te ki yii tuun toonŋana kur nba ki i loon a i tuun na, ki gbenn toona kur nba ki i tuun i yada paak na.

ref: Then, because of the undeserved grace of God and our Lord Jesus Christ, you will bring honor to the name of our Lord Jesus, and he will bring honor to you.
text: Nnee sa te ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann na-n la dontir i paak, ki yin mun la dontir u paak, ŋɔɔ Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia ninbatinu paak.

ref: When our Lord Jesus returns, we will be gathered up to meet him. So I ask you, my friends,
text: N naa waas, ki jikit saa ti Yomdaanɔ jenu maŋ nan timm nan ŋɔɔ nba tan sa chet leeb ki taan be na po,

ref: not to be easily upset or disturbed by people who claim the Lord has already come. They may say they heard this directly from the Holy Spirit, or from someone else, or even that they read it in one of our letters.
text: n loon ki n beti ki i daa mɔk jijeet i para ni amii jekiruk nan nisiab nba yaa a daar maŋ poŋ baara na po. Li pasiar bi beeri a timm din yet nna sɔkinii mɔmaan wannu ni, amii barii ni, amii gbouŋ ni.

ref: But don't be fooled! People will rebel against God. Then before the Lord returns, the wicked one who is doomed to be destroyed will appear.
text: I daa te ki sɔɔ kpanni gbaa, kimaan li daar kan baar see ki mɔyêtuk yoo baar sinsinn, yoo nba ki niib bonchiann tan sa yêt Yennu mɔb na; ki nifobiiuk bia tan sa nyi paanuwa; ki Yennu poŋ sennɔ a u sa biirɔ.

ref: He will brag and oppose everything holy or sacred. He will even sit in God's temple and claim to be God.
text: U tan kan sak Yenjiantu nan jiantii kura, ŋaan jɔɔb u mɔŋ teen yudaanɔ nba gar linba ki niib jiantir kura. U tan sa kɔɔ Yenjiantu diiuk ni gbaa ki pur u mɔŋ Yennu.

ref: Don't you remember I told you this while I was still with you?
text: Ma din be i boor ni yoonba na n din beti linba na kura; i ki tianii?

ref: You already know what is holding this wicked one back until it is time for him to come.
text: Siare lek daa diau; i mi la tee linba, ŋaan ki u tan sa dɔkit Yennu tiauŋ yoo maŋ nie.

ref: His mysterious power is already at work, but someone is holding him back. And the wicked one won't appear until this someone is out of the way.
text: Ki nifobiit ŋanin poŋ be ki tuun bi toona woonin ni, ki wunba diab nee lek daa sii diab mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Yennu tan sa berɔ.

ref: Then he will appear, but the Lord Jesus will kill him simply by breathing on him. He will be completely destroyed by the Lord's glorious return.
text: Ki yoo maŋ ki nifobiiuk maŋ tan sa nyi paanu ni, ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa tan sa kpiu nan ŋɔɔ Yisa tiauŋ mɔmaan, ki ŋɔɔ Yisa baaru nan u yentchiauŋ tan sa biirɔ.

ref: When the wicked one appears, Satan will pretend to work all kinds of miracles, wonders, and signs.
text: Nifobiiuk maŋ tan sa baar nan Sintaanii yaake ki tuun yaarlituk nan bakitnauŋ toonboori kur a wun kpann niib.

ref: Lost people will be fooled by his evil deeds. They could be saved, but they will refuse to love the truth and accept it.
text: U tan sa jɔɔb faak boori kur ki kpann binba ki Yennu tan sa biirib na. Bi tan sa biira, kimaan ba ki loon barmɔnii nba bo sa tinnib maŋ ki yêtir na paak.

ref: So God will make sure they are fooled into believing a lie.
text: Li paake te ki Yennu te ki faak paŋ tuun bi ni, ki bi tee linba ki tee barmɔnii na yada.

ref: All of them will be punished, because they would rather do evil than believe the truth. Be Faithful
text: Yennu tan sa biir binba ki teen barmɔnii maŋ yada ŋaan loon toonbiit tumu na. Yennu nba ganni ki tinni biaŋinba

ref: My friends, the Lord loves you, and it is only natural for us to thank God for you. God chose you to be the first ones to be saved. His Spirit made you holy, and you put your faith in the truth.
text: Ti naa waas, yimm nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ looni na, li kpaa talas ki ti pak Yennu yoo kur i paak, kimaan Yennu din ganni pinpiik neewa a yin la tinnu. I din teen barmɔnii barŋanii yada, ki u Seyeeŋ bia ŋammi ki teeni popeendamm, li paak te ki i la tinnu na.

ref: God used our preaching as his way of inviting you to share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Ta din mɔɔnt barŋanii nba ki turi na, ki Yennu yiini ki tinni a yii be fanu kaman nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nba be biaŋinba na.

ref: My friends, this is why you must remain faithful and follow closely what we taught you in person and by our letters.
text: Ti naa waas, li paak, setir man chib, ki dia wannu nba ki ti din wanni ti mɔmaan ni, nan ti gbouŋ ni na.

ref: God our Father loves us. He treats us with undeserved grace and has given us eternal comfort and a wonderful hope. We pray that our Lord Jesus Christ and God our Father
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia binba loonit ki turit pardɔɔnn nba kaa gbennu nan dindann nba yab u ninbatinu paak na,

ref: will encourage you and help you always to do and say the right thing.
text: bin kɔɔnni para ki turi paŋ ki yin fit tun linba ŋan kii bia piak barii nba ŋan yoo kur.

ref: Finally, our friends, please pray for us. This will help the message about the Lord to spread quickly, and others will respect it, just as you do.
text: Ti naa waas, linba joont tee nnee, yii mei Yennu kii teent man ki ti Yomdaanɔ barŋanii maŋ-n lin yian yian kii mɔk yikoo kaman nan la din tee i boor ni biaŋinba na.

ref: Pray that we may be kept safe from worthless and evil people. After all, not everyone has faith.
text: I bia kii mei Yennu ki wun nyinnit nifobiit nan biitdamm nuu ni, kimaan sɔɔ kur kaa teen barŋanii maŋ yada.

ref: But the Lord can be trusted to make you strong and protect you from harm.
text: Ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ tee barmɔnii daanɔe, ki sa turi paŋ kii guui ki biitdaanɔ Sintaanii kan fit teeni siari.

ref: He has made us sure that you are obeying what we taught you and that you will keep on obeying.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ paak ki ti teen yada nan i tuun ta din beti linba na, kii bia sii tuun nna yoo kura.

ref: I pray that the Lord will guide you to be as loving as God and as patient as Christ. Warnings against Laziness
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ maŋ-n sommi ki yin bann Yennu nba loont biaŋinba, ki bia mɔk gbankur nba ki Masia teent na. Li kpaa talas ki sɔɔ kur-ii tuun toona

ref: My dear friends, in the name of the Lord Jesus, I beg you not to have anything to do with any of your people who loaf around and refuse to obey the instructions we gave you.
text: Ti naa waas, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann ni ki ti beeri, yi nyinn i mɔŋ nan ti naa wasiat nba tee gbannyakdamm kura na sinsuuk ni, nan binba ki waa ta din turi kpaanii nba na sinsuuk ni.

ref: and we didn't accept food from anyone without paying for it. We didn't want to be a burden to any of you, so night and day we worked as hard as we could.
text: Ti din ki mi gaar sɔɔ sommir yann ki ki pa li paaki. Ŋaan ti kpan din tuune ki nyi naauk, ki lek tuun yennu nan nyiɔk, a ti daa te ki sɔɔ biir i siar ti paaki.

ref: We also gave you this rule: If you don't work, you don't eat.
text: Ta din be i boor ni na ti din tuu yɔɔ beeri a “Wunba kur ki saak toona, li yɔɔ ki ŋan nan bin turɔ jeeti.”

ref: Now we learn that some of you just loaf around and won't do any work, except the work of a busybody.
text: Ti gbia a nisiab be i sinsuuk ni ki ki loon toona, ŋaan lin kɔɔ bi leeb barii nie, li paak te ki ti beeri linba na.

ref: So, for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ, we ask and beg these people to settle down and start working for a living.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann ni ki ti beer niib maŋ ki kpaamm a bi baka-ii be bi tiauŋ toona ni, kii be soonna, kii di bi naauk nyɔɔt.

ref: Dear friends, you must never become tired of doing right.
text: Ti naa waas, yii mi ki i bak nan toonŋana tumu man.

ref: Be on your guard against any followers who refuse to obey what we have written in this letter. Put them to shame by not having anything to do with them.
text: Li pasiar ta teeni labaar nba ti gbouŋ ni na, nisɔɔ kan sakiri. Li-i tee nnaie, yin bann i yan ni nan li yɔɔ, ki i baka daa be u po, ki wun di fei.

ref: Don't consider them your enemies. Instead, speak kindly to them as you would to any other follower. Final Prayer
text: Ŋaan i daa teeu kaman nan u tee i dataake na, yi kpaanɔ kaman nan i naa bike na. Barjoontii

ref: I pray that the Lord, who gives peace, will always bless you with peace. May the Lord be with all of you.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ wunba teent parmaasir na-n turi parmaasir yoo kur, siar kura ni. Ti Yomdaanɔ-ii be nan i kur.

ref: I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to all of you.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n somm i kura.

ref: From Paul. God our Savior and Christ Jesus commanded me to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, who gives us hope.
text: Min Pɔɔl tee Yisa Masia toomii, kimaan ti Tinntɔɔ Yennu nan Yisa Masia binba tee ti dindann na turin yaak;

ref: Timothy, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind and merciful to you. May they bless you with peace! Warning against False Teaching
text: mine sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen fin Timoti, wunba tee n bimɔŋ, yada teenu nie. Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n somma, ki bia tina ninbaauk, ki bia tura parmaasir. Faak damm kpinkpannii labaar

ref: When I was leaving for Macedonia, I asked you to stay on in Ephesus and warn certain people there to stop spreading their false teachings.
text: I be Efesus kaman nan ma poŋ meia biaŋinba yoo nba ki n din be sɔnu ni ki saa Masedonia na. A kpaan nisiab nba want wannboorganu na ki bin nyik

ref: You needed to warn them to stop wasting their time on senseless stories and endless lists of ancestors. Such things only cause arguments. They don't help anyone to do God's work that can only be done by faith.
text: ki daa fiin bi yejamm ni barkpera nan yaadindɔnfɔkit nba baat nan mɔniɔk. Ŋann ki mɔk sommir ki teen niib ki bi sa tumii Yennu sɔnu; yada paake te ki ti bannir.

ref: You must teach people to have genuine love, as well as a good conscience and true faith.
text: Ŋaan n kpaanii maŋ paak tee a lin fiin loomm nan popeenn nan dudukŋant nan yada nan mɔnii.

ref: There are some who have given up these for nothing but empty talk.
text: Ki nisiab nyik linba nawa, ŋaan bot kɔɔ jat labafaanu ni.

ref: They want to be teachers of the Law of Moses. But they don't know what they are talking about, even though they think they do.
text: Bi loon bii tee Yennu sennu wannteebe, ŋaan ki mi bi mɔŋ mɔŋ mɔbona paak, ki bia ki mi ba piak linba po maŋ paaki.

ref: We know the Law is good, if it is used in the right way.
text: Ti mi nan sennu maŋ ŋan, li-i tee ki sɔɔ diar ki tuun kaman nan la want a tii tuun nann biaŋinba na.

ref: We also understand it wasn't given to control people who please God, but to control lawbreakers, criminals, godless people, and sinners. It is for wicked and evil people, and for murderers, who would even kill their own parents.
text: I tian nan sennu ki see nifoŋamm paak kaa, ŋaan li see binba tee mɔyêtdamm na paake, nan binba ki tiin Yennu, nan biitdamm paak, nan jakint damm, nan binba ki teen Yennu nibaakir paak, nan binba kpi bi baanba nan bi naanba, nan nifokpiira,

ref: The Law was written for people who are sexual perverts or who live as homosexuals or are kidnappers or liars or won't tell the truth in court. It is for anything else that opposes the correct teaching
text: nan bonchonchona paak, nan binba mɔk niauŋ ki dɔɔ nan bi jaleeb, nan binba jaan niib, nan binba tee faak damm, nan binba tee faak siara na, nan linba kur ki waa barmɔnii wannu paake;

ref: of the good news the glorious and wonderful God has given me. Being Thankful for God's Kindness
text: ki Masia barŋanii nba wann Yennu nba ŋan bonchiann biaŋinba na mun wann nnee; ki ŋɔɔ Yennu din teen barŋanii maŋ n nuu ni ki n mɔɔntirir. Yennu ninbatinu labaar

ref: I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me the strength for my work because he knew he could trust me.
text: N teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia wunba turin paŋ a man tun u toona niipoouk, kimaan ŋɔɔe yet a n jaŋ nan toona maŋ, ki te ki n tuun teenɔ,

ref: I used to say terrible and insulting things about him, and I was cruel. But Christ had mercy on me because I didn't know what I was doing, and I had not yet put my faith in him.
text: ma lek din biir u sann ki mukis u niib, ki sukiiɔ na, ŋaan u bia gaarin u ninbatinu niwa, kimaan n din ki mie, ki bia ki teenɔ yada, ki tuun nna.

ref: Christ Jesus our Lord treated me with undeserved grace and has greatly blessed my life with faith and love just like his own.
text: Ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia din tan tinin ninbaauk nba yab ki kaa bikinu, ki te ki n teenɔ yada ki loonɔ.

ref: “Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners.” This saying is true, and it can be trusted. I was the worst sinner of all!
text: Ki ŋɔɔ Yisa Masia din baar tingbouŋ na ni, a wun tan tinn biitdamme; labaar maŋ tee barmɔniie ki ŋan ki ti gaarir ki teenn yada; ki mine din bi ki gar biitdamm kura,

ref: But since I was worse than anyone else, God had mercy on me and let me be an example of the endless patience of Christ Jesus. He did this so that others would put their faith in Christ and have eternal life.
text: ŋaan ki u tin min nba din tee biitdamm yudaanɔ ninbaauk ki dii sukuru n paak, a lii tee siara ki wann binba tan sa teenɔ yada ki gaar manfoor nba kaa gbennu na.

ref: I pray that honor and glory will always be given to the only God, who lives forever and is the invisible and eternal King! Amen.
text: Dont Yennu ki turɔ nibaakir yoo nba kaa gbennu. Ŋɔɔe tee kpanbar nba kan mi kpo ki sii be yoo nba kaa gbennu; ki sɔɔ ki laatɔ; ki u tee Yen-yemmir na. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: Timothy, my son, the instructions I am giving you are based on what some prophets once said about you. If you follow these instructions, you will fight like a good soldier.
text: N bik Timoti, n jikit kpaanii maŋ ki tee a nuu ni. Kpaanii maŋ tee kaman nan mɔbona nba ki sɔkiniinba din poŋ pak a po sianyoo nba gar na. Jiin barii maŋ ki teenn jatiat ki kɔn kunkɔnŋann,

ref: You will be faithful and have a clear conscience. Some people have made a mess of their faith because they didn't listen to their consciences.
text: ki teen barmɔnii wannu yada, kii tuun linba ki a mi nan li tee toonŋana. Nisiab ki sak nnee; li paak te ki bi yada biir gbenn.

ref: Two of them are Hymenaeus and Alexander. I have given these men over to the power of Satan, so they will learn not to oppose God.
text: Bi siabe tee Haimeneus nan Aleksander, binba ki n daan jiib teemm Sintaanii nuu ni a wun mukisib. Linba nee sa wannib yan ki bin nyik Yennu sann biiru.

ref: First of all, I ask you to pray for everyone. Ask God to help and bless them all, and tell God how thankful you are for each of them.
text: Li paak, linba gar kur tee yii puk Yennu, kii meiɔ, kii boiɔ, kii teenɔ niipoouk niib kur paak.

ref: Pray for kings and others in power, so we may live quiet and peaceful lives as we worship and honor God.
text: Yii mei Yennu batnba nan binba kur tee yikoo damm na paak, a tii be nan laafia nan parmaasir, kii tee yentina, kii be soonna.

ref: This kind of prayer is good, and it pleases God our Savior.
text: Ŋanne ŋan ki pent ti Tinntɔɔ Yennu par ni;

ref: God wants everyone to be saved and to know the whole truth, which is,
text: ŋɔɔe loon sɔɔ kur-n bann u maan nba tee barmɔnii ki wun tinnib;

ref: There is only one God, and Christ Jesus is the only one who can bring us to God. Jesus was truly human, and he gave himself to rescue all of us.
text: kimaan Yen-yenɔkɔɔe be, ki sindantinnɔ tee yenɔkɔɔ ki be Yennu nan nisaarik sinsuuk ni; li yɔɔe tee Yisa Masia

ref: This is why God chose me to be a preacher and an apostle of the good news. I am telling the truth. I am not lying. God sent me to teach the Gentiles about faith and truth.
text: Li paak ki Yennu gannin ki n teen Masia toomii ki mɔɔntir barŋanii maŋ ki teen binba ki tee Juu teeb na, ki wannib ŋɔɔ Masia maan nba tee barmɔnii na, a bin teenɔ yada. Ma beeri linba na, n ki fa faak kaa, li tee barmɔniie.

ref: I want everyone everywhere to lift innocent hands toward heaven and pray, without being angry or arguing with each other.
text: N loon ki jab-ii mei Yennu siaminba kur. Jab nba set tee popeendamm ki ki mɔk wutoor amii mɔniɔk na sa fit donn bi nii sanpaapo kii mei Yennu.

ref: I would like for women to wear modest and sensible clothes. They should not have fancy hairdos, or wear expensive clothes, or put on jewelry made of gold or pearls.
text: N bia loon ki poob na mun-ii be kaman nan Yisaweira nba bo sii be biaŋinba na, ki bann chinchenbabu nan taliannu nba jaŋ nan Yisaweira na. Bi-i lorin bi yut nan kut nyinyirit, amii ki pir sarinbana amii laan nyinyirit nan tiat nba ki li daauk paar, linba na kan teemm fanti;

ref: Women who claim to love God should do helpful things for others,
text: linba sa turib fant tee binbenŋaŋ kaman nan pooyentina nba bo sii be biaŋinba na.

ref: and they should learn by being quiet and paying attention.
text: Poob-ii be soonna kii tumii nan mɔsaku kura.

ref: They should be silent and not be allowed to teach or to tell men what to do.
text: Min ki sak ki poosɔɔ-n wann jab, amii wuu mɔk yaak ki gar jabi; poo-ii be soonna;

ref: and the man Adam wasn't the one who was fooled. It was the woman Eve who was completely fooled and sinned.
text: Ki nyik nna, Sintaanii din fit kpann Iife ki u biir Yennu sennu, u din ki kpann Adami.

ref: But women will be saved by having children, if they stay faithful, loving, holy, and modest.
text: Ŋaan poo-i teen Yisa yada, kii loon niib, kii tee popeendaanɔ, kii be soonna, Yennu sa tinnɔ u maaru niwa.

ref: It is true that anyone who desires to be a church official wants to be something worthwhile.
text: Barii nba na tee barmɔniie, ki jasɔɔ nba loon ki wun teen Masiaweira yudaanɔ, li tee toonŋanne ki u loon.

ref: That's why officials must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. They must be self-controlled, sensible, well-behaved, friendly to strangers, and able to teach.
text: Li ki ŋan ki Masiaweira yudaanɔ-ii mɔk bisiati. Li kpaa talase ki wuu mɔk poo yenɔkɔɔ, kii tee subindaanɔ, kii dia u mɔŋ fanu, kii gaan saamm u ŋaak ni kii chantirib, kii tee wunba sa fit wann niib Yennu maan,

ref: They must not be heavy drinkers or troublemakers. Instead, they must be kind and gentle and not love money.
text: ki daa nyu daan ki yibi, ki daa loon fuut nan kunkɔna, ŋaan kii be soonna nan parmaasir, ki daa loon likirii bonchianni.

ref: Church officials must be in control of their own families, and they must see that their children are obedient and always respectful.
text: Li kpaa talase ki u fit kii dia u mɔŋ ŋaak niib fanu, ki te ki u waas-n sak u mɔb kii mɔk nibaakir.

ref: If they don't know how to control their own families, how can they look after God's people?
text: Ŋaadaanɔ-i kii mi wa sa dia u ŋaak niib biaŋinba, nlee ki u sa fit dia Yennu niibi?

ref: They must not be new followers of the Lord. If they are, they might become proud and be doomed along with the devil.
text: Li kpaa talas ki wuu tee daanɔ nba poŋ waa Masia ki li yukira; nnee-i kaa, ki u tan sa mann kpet donn u mɔŋ nan karinbaanii ki la tubdatu kaman nan Sintaanii nba din tun biaŋinba ki la tubdatu na.

ref: Finally, they must be well-respected by people who are not followers. Then they won't be trapped and disgraced by the devil. Church Officers
text: Li kpaa talas ki wuu tee daanɔ nba ki binba ki waa Masia na gbaa-n yet a u binbeŋ ŋan, ki u daa mi di fei ki Sintaanii baruk tan sa mann ki soorɔ. Masiaweira sommteeb labaar

ref: And they must have a clear conscience and hold firmly to what God has shown us about our faith.
text: Li kpaa talas ki bii dia yada barii nba ki Yennu fiit wannit na fanu, ki bi daa mɔk biiti.

ref: They must first prove themselves. Then if no one has anything against them, they can serve as officers.
text: Li kpaa talas ki bin bikimm sinsinn; bi-i kii mɔk biit bi bikinu maŋ ni, tɔn, bi sii fit tuun sommteeb toona.

ref: Women must also be serious. They must not gossip or be heavy drinkers, and they must be faithful in everything they do.
text: Li kpaa talas ki bi poob-ii munii mɔk binbenŋaŋ, ŋaan daa tee sanjanjanna. Li kpaa talas ki bii tee subindamm kii tuun bi toona kur fanu.

ref: Church officers must be faithful in marriage. They must be in full control of their children and everyone else in their home.
text: Li kpaa talas ki Masiaweira sommtɔɔ-ii mɔk poo yenɔkɔɔ, ki bia fit dia u waas nan u ŋaak niib fanu.

ref: Those who serve well as officers will earn a good reputation and will be highly respected for their faith in Christ Jesus. The Mystery of Our Religion
text: Masiaweira sommteeb nba tuun bi toona fanu na sa la nibaakir Yisa Masiaweira boor ni, ki bia mɔk parcheenn ki bet niib ba teenɔ yada biaŋinba na po. Barŋanii nba tee barjaann biaŋinba

ref: I hope to visit you soon. But I am writing these instructions,
text: Ma sɔbin gbouŋ nba teena na, ŋaan n mɔk dindann nan man baar mɔtana ki la-a,

ref: so if I am delayed, you will know how everyone who belongs to God's family ought to behave. After all, the church of the living God is the strong foundation of truth.
text: ŋaan li-i yukir ki n ki baar, gbouŋ na sa te ki a bann ta sii be biaŋinba Yennu ŋaaniib sinsuuk ni, binba ki ŋɔɔ Manfodaanɔ Yennu gannib na. Ŋamme dia barŋanii nba tee barmɔnii na, ki bia set li paak.

ref: Here is the great mystery of our religion: Christ came as a human. The Spirit proved that he pleased God, and he was seen by angels. Christ was preached to the nations. People in this world put their faith in him, and he was taken up to glory.
text: Li tee barmɔniie nan Masia labaar nba ki Yennu fiit wannit na sumii bonchiann: U din dɔkit nan gbanante, Yennu Seyeeŋ wann nan u be fanu; ki malakanba din lau. Ki niib din mɔɔnt u labaar ki tur nibooru, ki bi teenɔ yada tingbouŋ na ni, ki Yennu din tan donnɔ sanpaapo ki u la dontir.

ref: God's Spirit clearly says that in the last days many people will turn from their faith. They will be fooled by evil spirits and by teachings that come from demons.
text: Tɔn, Yennu Seyeeŋ set wantit yeenin ni nan yoo nba waa poorpo na ki nisiab tan sa nyik Masia labaar nawa, ŋaan sak narinbiit nba sa kpannib na, ki bia wei Sintaanii wannu na.

ref: They will also be fooled by the false claims of liars whose consciences have lost all feeling. These liars
text: Binba want li kpinkpannii wannu na fa faak, ki bi dudukit ji ki mukisib li paaki.

ref: will forbid people to marry or to eat certain foods. But God created these foods to be eaten with thankful hearts by his followers who know the truth.
text: Li nibooru want a jakunt nan pookɔɔnt ki ŋani, a ki jesiat bia ki ŋan nan dinu; ŋaan timm nba bann Masia barŋanii ki teenɔ yada na, ti mi nan Yennu din nan jebooru kura a niib-ii die, ki turɔ niipoouk li paak.

ref: Everything God created is good. And if you give thanks, you may eat anything.
text: Bonsiar kur nba ki Yennu din nan na ŋan ki tee jeete; ti-i turɔ niipoouk li paak ki di, li ŋan nan dinu; ti daa yêen li siari,

ref: What God has said and your prayer will make it fit to eat. Paul's Advice to Timothy
text: kimaan Yennu yet a li kura ŋan; ki ta meiɔ ki teenɔ niipoouk li paak na, ŋanne bia te ki jeet maŋ yeen. Yisa Masia toontunŋanɔ labaar

ref: If you teach these things to other followers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus. You will show you have grown up on the teachings about our faith and on the good instructions you have obeyed.
text: Li-i tee ki a jii kpaanii nba na tur Yisa Masiaweira na, a sii tee u toontunŋanɔe, ki sii dint a mɔŋ nan maan nba ki ti teen yada na nan wannŋanu nba ki a waa maŋ na.

ref: Don't have anything to do with worthless, senseless stories. Work hard to be truly religious.
text: Daa saak faak labaar kimaan li ki tee Yennu yari; li ki mɔk nyɔɔti; ŋaan tumii yentinu binbeŋ nan nunmɔnn.

ref: We have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of everyone, but especially of those who have faith. This is why we work and struggle so hard.
text: Ti gbaae ki tuun nan nunmɔnn kimaan ti mɔk dindann Manfodaanɔ Yennu paak, kimaan u be ki tee sɔɔ kur Tinntɔɔ, ki sa tinn sɔɔ kur nba teenɔ yada.

ref: Teach these things and tell everyone to do what you say.
text: Wantib ki kpaamm linba na kura.

ref: Don't let anyone make fun of you, just because you are young. Set an example for other followers by what you say and do, as well as by your love, faith, and purity.
text: Daa te ki sɔɔ-n sianna kimaan fa pɔ na paaki. A te ki a pinpauk nan a binbenŋaŋ, nan a loomm, nan a yada, nan a binbenyeeŋ-n wann Masiaweira nba bo sii be biaŋinba.

ref: Until I arrive, be sure to keep on reading the Scriptures in worship, and don't stop preaching and teaching.
text: Jiin a yoo nan a yaak ki karin Yennu gbouŋ ki wannib li paak, kii mɔɔntir Masia barii mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki n tan sa baar.

ref: Use the gift you were given when the prophets spoke and the group of church leaders blessed you by placing their hands on you.
text: Daa tammit Yennu Seyeeŋ piinii nba be a ni na po; ki Yennu din tura piinii maŋ kimaan sɔkiniinba din pak a po yoo nba ki yudamm din yii bi nii a paak.

ref: Remember these things and think about them, so everyone can see how well you are doing.
text: I tuun linba na nan ninmɔnn, ki sɔɔ kur-n la a pukinu.

ref: Be careful about the way you live and about what you teach. Keep on doing this, and you will save not only yourself, but the people who hear you.
text: I mi a mɔŋ nan a wannu po. I dia wannu nba na, kimaan li-i tee ki a diar, a sa tinn a mɔŋ nan binba ki a wantib na.

ref: Don't correct an older man. Encourage him, as you would your own father. Treat younger men as you would your own brother,
text: A daa yakii nan jakpersɔɔ, ŋaan pak turɔ kaman nan a mɔŋ mɔŋ baa na, kii dia naasimm kaman nan a mɔŋ mɔŋ ninjamm na,

ref: and treat older women as you would your own mother. Show the same respect to younger women that you would to your sister.
text: ki jii pookpera na ki bii tee kaman nan a naanba na, ki poopaannii na-ii naan nan a mɔŋ mɔŋ niipoob na nan binbenyeeŋ kura ni.

ref: Take care of any widow who is really in need.
text: I teen pakɔi nba be bi kuukɔɔ na nibaakir,

ref: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should learn to serve God by taking care of her, as she once took care of them. This is what God wants them to do.
text: ŋaan pakɔsɔɔ nba mɔk waas amii yaaboona, u waas amii yaaboona maŋ-n piin ki tumii bi yentinu toona nba tee linba ki tur bi mɔŋ ŋei ni, nnee ki bi damm mun sa di bi niwubit nyɔɔt, kimaan linba ki Yennu loone na.

ref: A widow who is really in need is one who doesn't have any relatives. She has faith in God, and she keeps praying to him night and day, asking for his help.
text: Pakɔɔk nba be u kuukɔɔ ki kaa sɔɔ, ŋaan ki u dindann be Yennu ni, ki u mei Yennu yoo kur, ŋɔɔe tee barmɔnii pakɔɔk.

ref: A widow who thinks only about having a good time is already dead, even though she is still alive.
text: Ŋaan pakɔɔk nba tuun bonchonchonsin, u poŋ kpoewa ŋaan daa be maŋ.

ref: Tell all this to everyone, so they will do the right thing.
text: Sent sennu na ki bii waa ki daa mɔk biiti.

ref: People who don't take care of their relatives, and especially their own families, have given up their faith. They are worse than someone who doesn't have faith in the Lord.
text: Wunba ki gorii u nikpiimm, amii linba mantik gar tee u kii fiin u mɔŋ mɔŋ ŋaak niib po, li daanɔ kɔŋ yadawa; ki wunba ki tee Masiaweirɔ gbaa sɔu.

ref: For a widow to be put on the list of widows, she must be at least
text: A daa kaan pakɔi nba daa ki kpet gar bina piinnloob na ki kɔɔnt pakɔi kann ni. Linba pukin, li kpaa talas ki wuu tee wunba din kun jɔɔ yenɔkɔɔe,

ref: She must also be well-known for doing all sorts of good things, such as raising children, giving food to strangers, welcoming God's people into her home, helping people in need, and always making herself useful.
text: ki poŋ tuu mɔk sanŋann u toona paak, ki tee wunba poŋ tuu mi waas wubiru fanu, ki gaan saamm u ŋaak ni, ki tuun sikininmɔŋ toona ki teen u leeb nba waa Masia na, ki sommit niib fara ni, ki poŋ jii u mɔŋ kura teen toonŋana tumu ni.

ref: Don't put young widows on the list. They may later have a strong desire to get married. Then they will turn away from Christ
text: Ŋaan a daa pukii poopaannii na pakɔi kann ni, kimaan bi-i taŋŋi loon jakunt yoonba bi tuni nyikit Masia li yooe,

ref: and become guilty of breaking their promise to him.
text: ki ji pot bi mɔsonn nba ki bi din senn turɔ sinsinn na, ki ji kɔɔ biit ni.

ref: Besides, they will become lazy and get into the habit of going from house to house. Next, they will start gossiping and become busybodies, talking about things that are none of their business.
text: Linba pukin, bi bia biir bi yoo ki ki loon toona, ŋaan lin lɔɔn ŋei ki tumii sanjaauŋ, nan niib barkpeena ni kɔɔnu, ki lin piak linba ki ŋan nan bii piaki.

ref: I would prefer that young widows get married, have children, and look after their families. Then the enemy won't have any reason to say insulting things about us.
text: Li paak, min bo sii loon ki pakɔi nba tee poobis na-n kun jabe ki mar waas, ki got bi ŋei fanu, ki ti datai daa laat yaak ki piak biit jiint ti po,

ref: If a woman who is a follower has any widows in her family, she should help them. This will keep the church from having that burden, and then the church can help widows who are really in need. Church Leaders
text: Ŋaan poosɔɔ nba tee Masiaweirɔ ki mɔk pakɔi u ŋaak ni, li kpaa talas ki wun gotib fanu, ki daa jikit bi jikpiatir jint Masia niibi, ki Masia niib mun-n got pakɔi nba be bi kuukɔɔ na.

ref: Church leaders who do their job well deserve to be paid twice as much, especially if they work hard at preaching and teaching.
text: Masia niib yudamm nba diab fanu na ŋan nan nibaakir bonchiann; ki binba mɔɔntir Masia labaar ki wantib li paak, ŋamme mantik ŋan nan nibaakir;

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “Don't muzzle an ox when you are using it to grind grain.” You also know the saying, “Workers are worth their pay.”
text: kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Naab-i ŋmaan dii ki pinii dibin, fa daa bobint u mɔbi,” ki bia sɔb a “Toontunnɔ ŋan ki wun gaar u paatii.”

ref: Don't listen to any charge against a church leader, unless at least two or three people bring the same charges.
text: Tɔn, bi-i paan saakasɔɔ barii, a daa te ki a baka be li ni, nɔkira siara damm teen niib bannlee amii bin gar nna.

ref: But if any of the leaders should keep on sinning, they must be corrected in front of the whole group, as a warning to everyone else.
text: Binba tuun toonbiit na, i yakii namm, niib ni, ki binba biar na-n tin jaŋmaanii.

ref: In the presence of God and Christ Jesus and their chosen angels, I order you to follow my instructions! Be fair with everyone, and don't have any favorites.
text: Yennu tɔɔnn nan Yisa Masia tɔɔnn nan malakagannteeb tɔɔnn po ki n teena kpaanii nba na, a fan sak wannu na ki daa mɔk ningannsai amii lukitin ki teen nisɔɔ bonsiar kur ni.

ref: Don't be too quick to accept people into the service of the Lord by placing your hands on them. Don't sin because others do, but stay close to God.
text: A daa mantik jekir ki yii a nii sɔɔ paak a wun teen Masiaweira yudaanɔ. A daa kɔɔnt a nuu siab toonbiit tumu ni; i dia a mɔŋ fanu.

ref: Stop drinking only water. Take a little wine to help your stomach trouble and the other illnesses you often have.
text: A laafia nba ki jaŋŋita na paak, a daa nyu nyun kuukɔɔ, ŋaan a tuu nyu daan waan ki lin somm a nɔɔk ni.

ref: Some people get caught in their sins almost at once, even before the time of judgment. But other people's sins don't show up until later.
text: Nisiab toonbiit poŋ dɔɔ paanu nie yoo nba ki bi daa ki bu bi buuti; ŋaan ki nisiab toonbiit mun sa nyi paanu bi buut bunu poorpoe.

ref: It is the same with good deeds. Some are easily seen, but none of them can be hidden.
text: Toonŋana mun bia tee nnee, li yenninba nyii paanuwa, linba lek ki nyii paanu ni gbaa, ŋaan li kan fit bɔri.

ref: If you are a slave, you should respect and honor your owner. This will keep people from saying bad things about God and about our teaching.
text: Li kpaa talase ki binba tee daaba na-n tur bi yomdamm nibaakir, ki nisɔɔ daa piak biir Yennu sann nan u wannu.

ref: If any of you slaves have owners who are followers, you should show them respect. After all, they are also followers of Christ, and he loves them. So you should serve and help them the best you can. False Teaching and True Wealth These are the things you must teach and tell the people to do.
text: Binba ki bi yomdamm tee Masiaweira na daa fiakit bi yomdamm maŋ, ba yib bi naa waas na paak, ŋaan li kpaa talas ki ŋamme-n mantik tun fanu, kimaan bi yomdamm nba gaan bi toona nyɔɔt na teen Masia yada, ki Yennu loonib. Wannu nba ki tee barmɔnii labaar I wantib kii kpaamm toona na po.

ref: Anyone who teaches something different disagrees with the correct and godly teaching of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Sɔɔ-i want wannganu ki ki wei ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia mɔbona nan Yennu sɔnu wannu na,

ref: Those people who disagree are proud of themselves, but they don't really know a thing. Their minds are sick, and they like to argue over words. They cause jealousy, disagreements, unkind words, evil suspicions,
text: u gbee nan karinbaaniie ki ki bann siari, ki bia mɔk mɔniɔk nan kunkɔna niauŋ, ki li baat nan funfunn, nan kunkɔna, nan nisukit, nan pinpakbiit,

ref: and nasty quarrels. They have wicked minds and have missed out on the truth. These people think religion is supposed to make you rich.
text: nan mɔniɔk yoo kura nibiit boor ni; ŋamm yaa dudukit ki somm fanu, ki ji ki mɔk barmɔnii; bi dukii nan Yennu sɔnu tee sɔnu nba sa te nirɔ-n teen mɔkitɔɔe.

ref: And religion does make your life rich, by making you content with what you have.
text: Li-i tee ki nirɔ numm gboot wa mɔk linba, Yennu sɔnu set mɔk nyɔɔte,

ref: We didn't bring anything into this world, and we won't take anything with us when we leave.
text: kimaan ti din ki baar nan bonsiar tingbouŋ nna ni, ki ti bia tan kan nyi nan bonsiar tingbouŋ na ni,

ref: So we should be satisfied just to have food and clothes.
text: ŋaan ti-i mɔk jeet nan tiat, li ŋan ki ti numm-n gboor;

ref: People who want to be rich fall into all sorts of temptations and traps. They are caught by foolish and harmful desires that drag them down and destroy them.
text: ŋaan binba loon bii tee mɔkita, ŋamme baa jinjammii ni, nan barit ni, nan jatii nan pobiir nba biir niib ki baantib yann na ni,

ref: The love of money causes all kinds of trouble. Some people want money so much they have given up their faith and caused themselves a lot of pain. Fighting a Good Fight for the Faith
text: kimaan likirii loomme tee bonbiir kur jiinn. Nisiab poŋ jii bi mɔŋ ki teen likiloonbiiuk na ni; li paak, ki bi ji ki teen Masia yada. Ŋanne te ki bi laat parbibooru booru. Pɔɔl nba kpaan Timoti kpaanii biaŋinba

ref: Timothy, you belong to God, so keep away from all these evil things. Try your best to please God and to be like him. Be faithful, loving, dependable, and gentle.
text: Ŋaan fin Timoti nba tee Yennu nirɔ na, li ŋan ki a dɔŋ nyi linba na nba kura ni, ŋaan kii loon fii be fanu, kii mɔk yentinu, ki teen Yennu yada, kii loon niib, kii mɔk sukuru, kii be soonna.

ref: Fight a good fight for the faith and claim eternal life. God offered it to you when you clearly told about your faith, while so many people listened.
text: Yabit a mɔŋ kii waa yada sɔnu nan nunmɔnn, kii mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu; ki Yennu din yiina ki a gaar manfoor maŋ yoo nba ki a din nyinn a mɔŋ siarŋann niib bonchiann numm ni na.

ref: The glorious God is the only Ruler, the King of kings and Lord of lords. At the time that God has already decided, he will send Jesus Christ back again.
text: Yennu nba ki ti piakɔ na, ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔe tee Yudaanɔ, ki bia tee kpanbara kur Kpanbar, ki bia tee yomdamm kur Yomdaanɔ; ŋɔɔe tan sa te ki wun dɔkit yoo nba jaŋ;

ref: Only God lives forever! And he lives in light that no one can come near. No human has ever seen God or ever can see him. God will be honored, and his power will last forever. Amen.
text: ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔe kan kpo; u be yentchiauŋ nba ki nirɔ kan fit nakin ni; ŋɔɔe ki nisɔɔ daa ki mi lau amii ki sa fit lau; ŋɔɔe yen paku nan paŋ yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: Warn the rich people of this world not to be proud or to trust in wealth that is easily lost. Tell them to have faith in God, who is rich and blesses us with everything we need to enjoy life.
text: A kpaan mɔkita na a bi daa mɔk dontinmɔŋ amii bin teen bi dindann mɔkint nba tee wouŋ na ni; ŋaan bin teen bi dindann kur Yennu ni; ŋɔɔe turit bonsiar kur a ti gaar kii mɔk parpeenn.

ref: Instruct them to do as many good deeds as they can and to help everyone. Remind the rich to be generous and share what they have.
text: Bii tuun toonŋana, ki bi toonŋana maŋ-ii yab; ki bi nii-ii yaa nan piinii turu.

ref: This will lay a solid foundation for the future, so they will know what true life is like.
text: Nnee sa te ki bii mɔk mɔkint sanpaapo kii guu bi mɔŋ yoo nba tan sa baar na; ki bi sii fit mɔk manfoor nba tee barmɔnii manfoor.

ref: Timothy, guard what God has placed in your care! Don't pay any attention to godless and stupid talk that sounds smart but really isn't.
text: Timoti, i guu barŋanii nba ki Yennu jii ki teen a nuu ni na. A nyik jat mɔniɔk nan labafaanu nba ki mɔk nyɔɔt ki bia ki tee Yennu yari, ki nisiab yir “bannu” na;

ref: Some people have even lost their faith by believing this talk. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
text: kimaan nisiab poŋ yaa a ŋamm mɔkir, ŋaan ki li paak ki bi nyik Masia sɔnu. Yennu sommir-ii be i ni.

ref: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus. God himself chose me to be an apostle, and he gave me the promised life that Jesus Christ makes possible.
text: Min Pɔɔl wunba tee Yisa Masia toomii Yennu loomm paak na, a n mɔɔnt manfoor nba ki Yennu din lor turit Yisa Masia paak na,

ref: Timothy, you are like a dear child to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Christ Jesus will be kind and merciful to you and will bless you with peace! Do Not Be Ashamed of the Lord
text: n sɔb gbouŋ na ki teen n bilonkak Timoti. Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n somma, ki bia tina ninbaauk, ki bia tura parmaasir. Niipoouk turu nan kpaanii labaar

ref: Night and day I mention you in my prayers. I am always grateful for you, as I pray to the God my ancestors and I have served with a clear conscience.
text: Yennu nba ki n jiantirɔ nan dudukŋant kaman nan n yejamm nba din tuun biaŋinba na, mi-i meiɔ, n tian a po yoo kura n Yenmiaru ni, nyiɔk nan yennu ni, ki teenɔ niipoouk a paak.

ref: I remember how you cried, and I want to see you, because this would make me truly happy.
text: N tian a ninnyuut po, ki lek loon ki n la-a kii mɔk parpeenn bonchiann.

ref: I also remember the genuine faith of your mother Eunice. Your grandmother Lois had the same sort of faith, and I am sure you have it as well.
text: N tian fa teen Yisa Masia yada nan barmɔnii biaŋinba; ki a yaa Lois nan a naa Yunis mun poŋ din teenɔ yadawa. N ki mɔk birsuk nan a mun bia teenɔ yada.

ref: So I ask you to make full use of the gift God gave you when I placed my hands on you. Use it well.
text: Li paak, ki n tiana ma fan fiin piinii nba ki Yennu tura yoo nba ki n din jii n nii yii a paak na,

ref: God's Spirit doesn't make cowards out of us. The Spirit gives us power, love, and self-control.
text: kimaan Seyeeŋ nba ki Yennu turit na ki teenit jaŋmaandamm kaa, ŋaan Seyeeŋ maŋ teent paŋ nan loomm, nan dianmɔŋ binbeŋ.

ref: Don't be ashamed to speak for our Lord. And don't be ashamed of me, just because I am in jail for serving him. Use the power that comes from God and join with me in suffering for telling the good news.
text: Li paak, mɔɔntir ti Yomdaanɔ labaar ki daa tiin fei. A bia daa tiin fei nan ma kɔɔ dansarik u paak na, ŋaan sak nan fardinu u barŋanii maŋ paak, kaman nan Yennu nba tura paŋ biaŋinba na.

ref: God saved us and chose us to be his holy people. We did nothing to deserve this, but God planned it because he is so kind. Even before time began God planned for Christ Jesus to show kindness to us.
text: Ŋɔɔ Yennue tinnit ki yiinit a tii tee popeendamm; wa yiinit biaŋinba, li ki tee ti toonŋana paak kaa, ŋaan u mɔŋ din senn a u sa tint ninbaauk Yisa Masia paak; u din senn nnee yoo nba ki u daa ki nan dunia na.

ref: Now Christ Jesus has come to offer us God's gift of undeserved grace. Christ our Savior defeated death and brought us the good news. It shines like a light and offers life that never ends.
text: Ŋaan ti Tinntɔɔ Yisa Masia baaru paake ki u fiit wannit li po; ki ŋɔɔ Yisa Masia din biir kuun paŋ, ŋaan bia wannit a ti-i sak u barŋanii maŋ ti sa la manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

ref: My work is to be a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher.
text: Yennu poŋ din yiinin a n teen toomii nan wanntɔɔ ki mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋ.

ref: This is why I am suffering now. But I am not ashamed! I know the one I have faith in, and I am sure he can guard until the last day what he has trusted me with.
text: Li paake te ki n di fara dansarik ni; ŋaan ki n ki tiin fei, kimaan n mi ma jii n mɔŋ ki teen wunba nuu ni, n mi ki birsuk kaa nan u sa fit dia linba ki n jii ki teen u nuu ni na fanu mɔkmɔk nan Daar maŋ daar.

ref: Now follow the example of the correct teaching I gave you, and let the faith and love of Christ Jesus be your model.
text: I dia ma din wanna barŋanii sɔnu na fanu, kii waa-r nan yada nan loomm nba ki Yisa Masia teent na.

ref: You have been trusted with a wonderful treasure. Guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit, who lives within us.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ paŋ nba be ti ni na paak, i dia barŋanii nba ki u jii ki teen a nuu ni na.

ref: You know that everyone in Asia has turned against me, especially Phygelus and Hermogenes.
text: A poŋ mi nan sɔɔ kur nba be Asia na nyikima; bi siabe tee Fijelus nan Hemojenes.

ref: I pray that the Lord will be kind to the family of Onesiphorus. He often cheered me up and wasn't ashamed of me when I was put in jail.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ-n tin Onesiforus ŋaateeb ninbaauk, kimaan u daan yoo cheen n par. U daan ki tin fei nan ma kɔɔ dansarik na paak;

ref: Then after he arrived in Rome, he searched everywhere until he found me.
text: ŋaan wa daan tan baar Rom yoonba, u daan piin loon ma be siame nan wa daan tan la n boor.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus will ask God to show mercy to Onesiphorus on the day of judgment. You know how much he helped me in Ephesus.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ-n tinɔ ninbaauk Daar maŋ daar. A mɔŋ mi fanu wa din sommin bonchiann biaŋinba Efesus ni.

ref: Timothy, my child, you must let Christ Jesus make you strong by his gift of undeserved grace.
text: N bik, fin ŋanimi chee a par Yisa Masia nba somma biaŋinba u ninbatinu na paak.

ref: You have often heard me teach. Now I want you to tell these same things to followers who can be trusted to tell others.
text: Fa poŋ din gbat wannu nba n peŋ niib bonchiann boor ni na, a jiir ki wann nisiab nba tee yadadamm, binba mun sa fit wann nileeb mun.

ref: As a good soldier of Christ Jesus you must endure your share of suffering.
text: A sak nan fardinu Yisa Masia paak kaman nan lanjiŋanɔ na.

ref: Soldiers on duty don't work at outside jobs. They try only to please their commanding officer.
text: Lanjima wunba loon u saakɔɔ numm-n gboou, u ji ki ŋmat u binbeŋ nan linba ki tee lanjimanba toona ni.

ref: No one wins an athletic contest without obeying the rules.
text: Wunba tiin poot nantaar kan fit nyann ki gaar li nyɔɔt, see ki u sak wei poot sennu kur.

ref: And farmers who work hard are the first to eat what grows in their field.
text: Li kpaa talas ki kpaarɔ nba tun nan ninmɔnn-n gaar jeet sinsinn tor.

ref: If you keep in mind what I have told you, the Lord will help you understand completely.
text: I dukii linba ki n beera na po, kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ sa te ki fan fit bann li kura paak.

ref: Keep your mind on Jesus Christ! He was from the family of David and was raised from death, just as my good news says.
text: I tian Yisa Masia po; u din fiir kuun ni, ki din tee David yaaboonn; kaman nan ma mɔn barŋanii ki wann niib biaŋinba na;

ref: And because of this message, I am locked up in jail and treated like a criminal. But God's good news isn't locked in jail,
text: barŋanii maŋ paak, ki n be nan jarit ki di fara kaman nan biitdaanɔ na; Yennu barii maŋ ŋanin ki be jarit ni.

ref: and so I am willing to put up with anything. Then God's special people will be saved and given eternal glory because they belong to Christ Jesus.
text: Ŋaan Yennu nigannkab na paak ki n lek di sukuru fara kura ni, a bin mun la tinnu Yisa Masia paak, kii mɔk parpeenn nba kaa gbennu na.

ref: Here is a true message: “If we died with Christ, we will live with him.
text: Barii nee tee mɔmɔmm: “Ti-i kpo nanɔ, ti mun tan sii mɔk manfoor nanɔe.

ref: If we don't give up, we will rule with him. If we deny that we know him, he will deny that he knows us.
text: Ti-i ŋammit yabii, ti mun tan sa di naan nanɔe; ŋaan ti-i yaa a ti ki miu, u mun tan sii yaa a u ki miti;

ref: If we are not faithful, he will still be faithful. Christ cannot deny who he is.” An Approved Worker
text: ti-i ji kii mɔk yada, ŋaan ŋɔɔe mantik tee barmɔnii daanɔ, kimaan u kan mi nɔi u mɔŋ binbeŋi.” Toontunnɔ nba sa penn Yennu par na labaar

ref: Don't let anyone forget these things. And with God as your witness, you must warn them not to argue about words. These arguments don't help anyone. In fact, they ruin everyone who listens to them.
text: A tiar a niib linba na, ki turib kpaanii Yennu boor a bi daa kɔn mɔbona paaki; ŋann ki mɔk nyɔɔt, ŋaan li biir binba gbiin nee.

ref: Do your best to win God's approval as a worker who doesn't need to be ashamed and who teaches only the true message.
text: Yabit a mɔŋ paak ki Yennu-n paka, kii tee toontunnɔ nba dia barmɔnii mɔmaan ki want niib fanu ki tan kan di fei u toona paak.

ref: Keep away from worthless and useless talk. It only leads people farther away from God.
text: A nyinn a mɔŋ labafaanu nba ki tee Yennu yari ki bia ki mɔk nyɔɔti, ki sa te ki niib-n fɔkit nan Yennu.

ref: That sort of talk is like a sore that won't heal. And Hymenaeus and Philetus have been talking this way
text: Li wannbooru naan nan nar fiauk nba tuu di gbanant nee. Haimeneus nan Filetus daan want li wannu boorue.

ref: by teaching that the dead have already been raised to life. This is far from the truth, and it is destroying the faith of some people.
text: Bi ki want niib barmɔnii labaar, ŋaan tuni nisiab yada ki yaa a ti kuunfiiru na yoo poŋ taan baar gara;

ref: But the foundation God has laid is solid. On it is written, “The Lord knows who his people are. So everyone who worships the Lord must turn away from evil.”
text: ŋaan Yennu nigannkab ŋanin see, ki kan mi ki dammi; ki mɔbona nba sɔb pukin bi paak tee nnee a: “Ti Yomdaanɔ mi binba tee u yab,” ki bia sɔb a, “Wunba kur yet a u tee ti Yomdaanɔ yɔɔe, li kpaa talase ki wun nyik toonbiit tumu.”

ref: In a large house some dishes are made of gold or silver, while others are made of wood or clay. Some of these are special, and others are not.
text: Ŋmanbooru bonchiann be ŋasaakak ni; kut sɔnt kuukɔɔe kaa li ni, ŋaan ŋmanmɔni nan senii mun bia be. Ki li siab nyɔɔt yab bonchiann, ŋaan ki siab nyɔɔt ki yabi.

ref: This is also how it is with people. The ones who stop doing evil and make themselves pure will become special. Their lives will be holy and pleasing to their Master, and they will be able to do all kinds of good deeds.
text: Wunba-i nyik biit ki tee popeendaanɔ, u sii tee kaman nan ŋmaŋ nba mɔk nyɔɔt ti Yomdaanɔ boor, ki jaŋ nanɔ, ki teen siir a wun tun toonŋana kura.

ref: Run from temptations that capture young people. Always do the right thing. Be faithful, loving, and easy to get along with. Worship with people whose hearts are pure.
text: Nyint a mɔŋ nan naasimm nianbiiuk ni, ŋaan kot ki fin nan binba para yeen ki bi yi ti Yomdaanɔ ki loon sommir na-n wei yentinu sɔnu, nan yada sɔnu, nan loomm sɔnu, nan parmaasir sɔnu.

ref: Stay away from stupid and senseless arguments. These only lead to trouble,
text: A tin ki nyik jatii nan li mɔniat. A poŋ mi nan li joontir nan kunkɔnae.

ref: and God's servants must not be troublemakers. They must be kind to everyone, and they must be good teachers and very patient.
text: Li ki ŋan ki ti Yomdaanɔ toontunnɔ-ii kɔn nan niibi. Li ŋan ki wuu tee burchimɔe sɔɔ kur boor ni, kii tee wanntɔŋanɔ nba mɔk sukuru,

ref: Be humble when you correct people who oppose you. Maybe God will lead them to turn to him and learn the truth.
text: kii tee wunba dia soonna binbeŋ kii kpaan binba nɔi nanɔ na, kimaan li pasiar Yennu-n tan turib yaak ki bin nyik bi dudukbiit ki bann barmɔnii wannu,

ref: They have been trapped by the devil, and he makes them obey him, but God may help them escape.
text: ki la yan ki lɔkit Sintaanii barit, a bin ji sak ŋɔɔ Yennu loomm.

ref: You can be certain that in the last days there will be some very hard times.
text: A mi nan tingbouŋ na dajoontii yoo tan sii paar.

ref: People will love only themselves and money. They will be proud, stuck-up, rude, and disobedient to their parents. They will also be ungrateful, godless,
text: Niib tan sii tee jawudamme, nan likilonna, nan karinbaanii damm, nan garukdamm, nan binba sukii niib, nan binba ki saak bi damm mɔi, nan binba ki tian ŋamm po, nan binba ki tiin Yennu;

ref: heartless, and hateful. Their words will be cruel, and they will have no self-control or pity. These people will hate everything good.
text: bi tan kii mɔk loomm nan niibi, bi tan sii tee mɔlanfikdamme, ki kan soor bi mɔŋi, ki sii tee kpikpiruk damm; ki sii naan bonsosoot; bi tan sii nan toonŋanae,

ref: They will be sneaky, reckless, and puffed up with pride. Instead of loving God, they will love pleasure.
text: bi tan sii nyi leeb poae, ki tee nikaatkaatdamm, ki dont bi mɔŋ; bi tan sii loon bi mɔŋ mɔŋ loomme ki gar Yennu,

ref: Even though they will make a show of being religious, their religion won't be real. Don't have anything to do with such people.
text: bi tan sii dia Yenjiantu sanne, ŋaan ki saak Yennu paŋ. Nyint a mɔŋ li nibooru ni.

ref: Some men fool whole families, just to get power over those women who are slaves of sin and are controlled by all sorts of desires.
text: Bisiabe lin ki doi ŋei ki kpann pooliinii nba tuu tuun toonbiit bonchiann ki waa bi para nianbiit booru booru, ki nyint bi yan.

ref: These women always want to learn something new, but they never can discover the truth.
text: Yoo kura, li poob tumiie ŋaan kan mi bann barmɔnii sɔnu.

ref: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, these people are enemies of the truth. Their minds are sick, and their faith isn't real.
text: Janes nan Jamberes nba din ki sak nan Moses biaŋinba na, li jabooru mun tee nnee, bi kɔn nan barmɔnii, bi dudukit bia ki ŋani, bi bia ki waa yada na sɔnu;

ref: But they won't get very far with their foolishness. Soon everyone will know the truth about them, just as Jannes and Jambres were found out. Paul's Last Instructions to Timothy
text: ŋaan bi kan fit kpann niib ki yukiri, kimaan sɔɔ kur tan sa la bi jatii nba tee biaŋinba kaman nan ba din la Janes nan Jamberes jatii nba din tee biaŋinba na. Kpaanjoontik labaar

ref: Timothy, you know what I teach and how I live. You know what I want to do and what I believe. You have seen how patient and loving I am, and how in the past I put up with
text: Ŋaan fin ŋanin poŋ wei n wannu nan n binbeŋ, nan n dudukita. A poŋ mi ma teen Masia yada biaŋinba, nan n sukuru, nan n loomm nan niib, nan n gbankur,

ref: trouble and suffering in the cities of Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. Yet the Lord rescued me from all those terrible troubles.
text: nan niib nba mukisin biaŋinba, nan n fardinu. A mi linba kura tumin Antiok, nan Ikonium nan Listra. A mi ma din la muksubiit biaŋinba; ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔe din nyinnin li kur ni.

ref: In fact, anyone who belongs to Christ Jesus and wants to live right will have trouble from others.
text: Barmɔniie, niib sa mukis wunba kur loon wuu tee yentinɔ Yisa Masia paak na;

ref: But evil people who pretend to be what they are not will become worse than ever, as they fool others and are fooled themselves.
text: ki nibiit nan kpinkpannii damm lek tan sii ŋammit pukii bi bitime, ki kpann leeb, ki bi leeb mun sa kpannib.

ref: Keep on being faithful to what you were taught and to what you believed. After all, you know who taught you these things.
text: Ŋaan fin ŋanin, a dia barii nba ki a tumii ki teenir yada na, kimaan a poŋ mi nan binba din tee a wannteeb na tee barmɔnii damme;

ref: Since childhood, you have known the Holy Scriptures that are able to make you wise enough to have faith in Christ Jesus and be saved.
text: ki a bisin niwa a poŋ mi Yennu gbouŋ nba fit teena yan ki wanta fa sa teen Yisa Masia yada ki tinn biaŋinba na.

ref: Everything in the Scriptures is God's Word. All of it is useful for teaching and helping people and for correcting them and showing them how to live.
text: Maan kur nba be Yennu gbouŋ ni na nyii u mɔb nie, ki li kura mɔk nyɔɔte, ki wantit u po, ki kpaanit, ki ŋammit ti benu, ki wantit yentinu sɔnu,

ref: The Scriptures train God's servants to do all kinds of good deeds.
text: a nisɔɔ nba jiantir Yennu binbeŋ-ii ŋan, ki wun teen siir kii tuun toonŋana booru kura.

ref: When Christ Jesus comes as king, he will be the judge of everyone, whether they are living or dead. So with God and Christ as witnesses, I command you
text: Yisa Masia nba tan sa bu footib nan kpeemm buut, wunba tan sa jen ki di naan na, ŋɔɔ nan Yennu tɔɔnn po ki n beera,

ref: to preach God's message. Do it willingly, even if it isn't the popular thing to do. You must correct people and point out their sins. But also cheer them up, and when you instruct them, always be patient.
text: a fii mɔɔntir u barii; ki yoo-i lekii jaŋ amii li-i kii jaŋi, fii mɔɔntir, kii piak niib bi toonbiit po, kii kpaanib, kii kɔɔntib para, kii wantib barii maŋ nan sukuru yoo kura,

ref: The time is coming when people won't listen to good teaching. Instead, they will look for teachers who will please them by telling them only what they are itching to hear.
text: kimaan yoo baat na ki niib ji tan kii gbiin wannŋanu, ŋaan tan sii waa bi mɔŋ mɔŋ nianbiite, ki sii gbiin wannteeb bonchiann binba sii wantib ba loon bii gbia linba.

ref: They will turn from the truth and eagerly listen to senseless stories.
text: Bi kan gbiint barmɔnii barii maŋi, ŋaan loon a bii gbiin labagana.

ref: But you must stay calm and be willing to suffer. You must work hard, telling the good news and to do your job well.
text: Ŋaan fii tee subindaanɔ yoo kur, kii saak fardinu, kii mɔɔntir Yisa Masia barŋanii, kii tuun toona kur nba ki Yennu tura na.

ref: Now the time has come for me to die. My life is like a drink offering being poured out on the altar.
text: Kimaan min ŋanin, yoo baar na nan man lu n miar na; yoo baar nan man nyi tingbouŋ na niwa.

ref: I have fought well. I have finished the race, and I have been faithful.
text: N gbaa gbigbaaŋana; n tun n toonn gbenna; n lek dia yada labaar.

ref: So a crown will be given to me for pleasing the Lord. He judges fairly, and on the day of judgment he will give a crown to me and to everyone else who wants him to appear with power. Personal Instructions
text: Li paak, ki nyɔɔt nba tee yentinu nyɔɔt dɔɔ guun sanpaapo; ki ti Yomdaanɔ wunba bu niib buut nan barmɔnii na tan sa turin nyɔɔt maŋ Daar maŋ daar. U tan kan tur min kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan u tan sa tur binba kur mun loon ki wun jen na. Pɔɔl nan Timoti bannlee mɔbona

ref: Come to see me as soon as you can.
text: Yabit ki baar n peŋ yian yian na. Kreskens saan Galasiawa, ki Taitus mun saan Dalmasiawa,

ref: Demas loves the things of this world so much that he left me and went to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
text: Demas mantik loon tingbouŋ na bona bonchiann, li paak u nyikin ki mun saan Tesalonikawa.

ref: Only Luke has stayed with me. Mark can be very helpful to me, so please find him and bring him with you.
text: Luk kuukɔɔe ji be nanin. Jiin Mak baar nanɔ na, kimaan u toona mɔk nyɔɔt turin bonchiann.

ref: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
text: N tun Tikikus a wun saan Efesus.

ref: When you come, bring the coat I left at Troas with Carpus. Don't forget to bring the scrolls, especially the ones made of leather.
text: Fi-i baat yoonba, fan jii n liatir nba ki n daan nyik Troas, ki li be Kapus boor ni; a bia jii n gbounpinkara nan n kadagbant na.

ref: Alexander, the metalworker, has hurt me in many ways. But the Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
text: Aleksander wunba kur saauk na din tumin linba bi bonchiann; Yennu sa dat u tuba wa tun linba na paak.

ref: Alexander opposes what we preach, so you had better watch out for him.
text: I mi a mɔŋ nanɔ, kimaan u kɔn nan barŋanii nba ki ti mɔɔntir na bonchiann.

ref: When I was first put on trial, no one helped me. In fact, everyone deserted me. I hope it won't be held against them.
text: Ma daan kɔn n mɔŋ paak sinsinn na, sɔɔ ki set n poorpo; bi kur nyikima; Yennu daa teemm biit li paaki.

ref: But the Lord stood beside me. He gave me the strength to tell his full message, so that all Gentiles would hear it. And I was kept safe from hungry lions.
text: Ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ daan be nanin ki turin paŋ ki n daan fit mɔn barŋanii maŋ kur ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na kura a bin gbat. Ŋɔɔe daan nyinnin yambɔr mɔb ni.

ref: The Lord will always keep me from being harmed by evil, and he will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. Praise him forever and ever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: Ŋɔɔe bia sa nyinnin bonbiit kura ni, ki kunnin ki kɔɔnin u naan nba be sanpaapo nan laafia. Ŋɔɔe yen dontir nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm. Foontii nba joont labaar

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and to the family of Onesiphorus.
text: N jikit n foontii ki teen Prisila nan Akuila, nan Onesiforus ŋaateeb.

ref: Erastus stayed at Corinth. Trophimus was sick when I left him at Miletus.
text: Erastus daan biar Korinta. Trofimus daan kaa laafia ki n daan nyikɔ Miletus.

ref: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, and Claudia send you their greetings, and so do the rest of the Lord's followers.
text: Yabit ki baar nnawa ki tinkook-n do. Yubulus nan Pudens nan Linus nan Klɔdia teena bi foontii; ki Masiaweira kura bia foonta.

ref: I pray that the Lord will bless your life and will be kind to you.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ-ii be nana. Yennu sommir-ii be i ni.

ref: From Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. I encourage God's own people to have more faith and to understand the truth about religion.
text: Min Pɔɔl, wunba tee Yennu daabir nan Yisa Masia toomii na, mine ki Yennu gannin ki tumin u nigannkab boor a n chee bi yada, ki te bin bann u barmɔnii labaar nba wantit yentinu sɔnu.

ref: Then they will have the hope of eternal life God promised long ago. And God never tells a lie!
text: N mɔk dindann nan n sii mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu. Yennu nba ki mi fa faak na din senn mɔsonn yaayoo niwa a u sa turit manfoor maŋ,

ref: So, at the proper time, God our Savior gave this message and told me to announce what he had said.
text: ki yoo nba din tan jaŋ na ki u fiit wann li labaar u maan ni; ki u din jii labaar maŋ ki teen n nuu ni, ki n mɔɔntir kaman nan ti Tinntɔɔ Yennu nba turin mɔb na.

ref: Titus, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and Christ Jesus our Savior will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! What Titus Was To Do in Crete
text: N sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen fin Taitus wunba tee n bimɔŋ ki ti taa yada yennkɔɔ na. Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Tinntɔɔ Yisa Masia-n somma, ki bia tura parmaasir. Taitus toona po

ref: I left you in Crete to do what had been left undone and to appoint leaders for the churches in each town. As I told you,
text: N din te ki a biar Kreet tiŋ ni ma a ŋamm bonsiar kur nba kpaa talase, ki bia gann Masiaweira saakab doo kur ni kaman nan ma din wanna biaŋinba na;

ref: they must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. Their children must be followers of the Lord and not have a reputation for being wild and disobedient.
text: li kpaa talas ki Masiaweira saakɔɔ daa mɔk biiti; li kpaa talas ki u mɔk poo yenɔkɔɔ; li kpaa talas ki u waas-n teen Masia yada, bi daa tee waabiit nan tubkangbata;

ref: Church officials are in charge of God's work, and so they must also have a good reputation. They must not be bossy, quick-tempered, heavy drinkers, bullies, or dishonest in business.
text: kimaan Masiaweira yudaanɔ yen Yennu toona; li paak, li kpaa talas ki u daa mɔk biiti; u daa tee bongann, amii wunba dont wutoor yann yanni, amii wunba nyu daan ki yibi, amii wunba loon kunkɔna, amii wunba mantik mɔk likilonchiauŋi.

ref: Instead, they must be friendly to strangers and enjoy doing good things. They must also be sensible, fair, pure, and self-controlled.
text: Li kpaa talas ki wuu mi saamm gaaru, kii loon toonŋana tumu, kii sub, kii be fanu, kii tee popeendaanɔ, kii mi mɔŋdianu.

ref: They must stick to the true message they were taught, so their good teaching can help others and correct everyone who opposes it.
text: Li kpaa talas ki u dia barmɔnii labaar nba ki ti din wanni na. Ŋanne sa te wun fit wann niib wannu nba took, ki fit nyann binba ki saak barmɔnii wannu maŋ.

ref: There are many who don't respect authority, and they fool others by talking nonsense. This is especially true of some Jewish followers.
text: N beera linba na, kimaan mɔyêtdamm be bonchiann, ki Juu tesiab mantik teen nnee, ki dia bi mɔmaan nba kaa nyɔɔt ki kpann bi leeb na.

ref: But you must make them be quiet. They are after money, and they upset whole families by teaching what they should not.
text: Li kpaa talas ki fin gɔɔr bi barii, kimaan bi likilonbiiuk paak ki bi lin want niib linba ki ŋan nan bii want, ki ŋmat ŋasai niib kur dudukit.

ref: Don't pay any attention to any of those senseless Jewish stories and human commands. These are made up by people who won't obey the truth.
text: ki ji daa gbiintir Juu teeb faak labaar, ki bia daa gbiintir niib nba ki saak barmɔnii labaar na sennii.

ref: Everything is pure for someone whose heart is pure. But nothing is pure for an unbeliever with a dirty mind. That person's mind and conscience are destroyed.
text: Popeendamm boor ni, bonsiar kur yeene; ŋaan biitdamm nba ki teen Masia yada na, bonsiar ki yeen bi boori, kimaan bi dudukit nan bi yan ni ki yeeni.

ref: Such people claim to know God, but their actions prove they really don't. They are disgusting. They won't obey God, and they are too worthless to do anything good.
text: Bi yi bi mɔŋ Yenmiirae, ŋaan ki bi toona want nan bi ki sak bann Yennu. Bi tee mɔyêtdamme, ki ki pent Yennu par ni, ki kan fit tun toonŋansiari.

ref: Titus, you must teach only what is correct.
text: Ŋaan fin-ii want linba tee wannmɔnu.

ref: Tell the older men to have self-control and to be serious and sensible. Their faith, love, and patience must never fail.
text: A kpaan jasaakab ki bii be soonna, kii sub, kii dia bi mɔŋ, kii dia Masia labaar fanu, kii loon niib, kii mɔk gbankur.

ref: Tell the older women to behave as those who love the Lord should. They must not gossip about others or be slaves of wine. They must teach what is proper,
text: A bia kpaan poosaakab ki bii tee popeendamm. Bi daa tee sanjanjanna, ki daa nyu daan ki yibi. Li kpaa talas ki bii want linba ŋan,

ref: so the younger women will be loving wives and mothers.
text: kii want poobis na ki bii loon bi sɔrib nan bi waas,

ref: Each of the younger women must be sensible and kind, as well as a good homemaker, who puts her own husband first. Then no one can say insulting things about God's message.
text: kii dia bi mɔŋ, kii tee popeendamm, kii tee ŋaapooŋamm, kii be fanu, kii saak bi sɔrib mɔi. Ŋanne sa te ki sɔɔ kan fit pak bonbiir ki jikit saa Yennu labaar po.
