ref: Tell the young men to have self-control in everything.
text: A bia kpaan naasimm ki bin dia bi mɔŋ.

ref: Always set a good example for others. Be sincere and serious when you teach.
text: A mɔŋ-ii be fanu bonsiar kur ni, kii want leeb ba sii be fanu biaŋinba. A wann niib Masia labaar nan barmɔnii, kii sub.

ref: Use clean language that no one can criticize. Do this, and your enemies will be too ashamed to say anything against you.
text: I piak mɔmɔmm nba ki sɔɔ kan mi yiinir yanni, ki binba loon ki bii kɔn nant daa laat bonbiir nba ki bi sa pak ti po; ŋanne ki bi sa di fei li paak.

ref: Tell slaves always to please their owners by obeying them in everything. Slaves must not talk back to their owners
text: Li ŋan ki daaba-ii saak bi yomdamm mɔi, kii pent bi para bonsiar kur ni. Bi daa nɔi nan bi yomdamm mɔniati,

ref: or steal from them. They must be completely honest and trustworthy. Then everyone will show great respect for what is taught about God our Savior. God's Kindness and the New Life
text: ki daa jaan bi siari. Ŋaan li kpaa talas ki bin wann bi mɔŋ nan bi be fanu nan barmɔnii yoo kura; ŋanne ki bi binbeŋ kur sa wann nan ti Tinntɔɔ Yennu wannue ŋan bonchiann.

ref: God has shown us undeserved grace by coming to save all people.
text: Kimaan Yennu tur nisaarik kur sɔnu a bin la tinnu; ŋanne fiit wann u ninbatinu nba yab biaŋinba.

ref: He taught us to give up our wicked ways and our worldly desires and to live decent and honest lives in this world.
text: Li ninbatinue wannit a tin nyik linba kur ki tee yentinu na, nan tingbouŋ na loonbiit, ŋaan dia ti mɔŋ, kii be fanu, kii tiin Yennu tingbouŋ na ni,

ref: We are filled with hope, as we wait for the glorious return of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ.
text: kimaan ti guu ki mɔk dindann nba turit parpeenn nan ti yabint Yennu nan ti Tinntɔɔ Yisa Masia sa dɔkit nan yentsaakar ni;

ref: He gave himself to rescue us from everything evil and to make our hearts pure. He wanted us to be his own people and to be eager to do right.
text: ŋɔɔe din gaar ti paak ki kpo a wun nyinnit biit kur daabsin ni, ki te ki ti tee popeendamm nba tee u kuukɔɔ yab, kii teen niib nba numm mɔn nan toonŋana tumu.

ref: Teach these things, as you use your full authority to encourage and correct people. Make sure you earn everyone's respect.
text: Wantib linba na kura, kii cheen bi para, kii kpaanib Yennu nba tura yudant biaŋinba na; sɔɔ daa goriia yanni.

ref: Remind your people to obey the rulers and authorities and not to be rebellious. They must always be ready to do something helpful
text: A tiarib ki bin tur bi tiŋ yudamm nan yentdamm nibaakir, ki sak bi mɔi, ki teen siir ki tun toonŋana kura.

ref: and not say cruel things or argue. They should be gentle and kind to everyone.
text: Betirib ki bi daa piak barbiit sɔɔ paaki, ki daa kɔn nan leebi, kii be soonna, ki tur sɔɔ kur nibaakir;

ref: We used to be stupid, disobedient, and foolish, as well as slaves of all sorts of desires and pleasures. We were evil and jealous. Everyone hated us, and we hated everyone.
text: kimaan sianyoo ti mɔŋ din ki mɔk yami, ti din tee mɔyêtdamme, ki waa sɔnu nba ki tooki, ki waa nianbiit sɔnbooru booru. Ti din tuu ki mi dudukit nba ŋan; ti din tee funfunn damme, ki din nan ti leebe ki bi mun namit.

ref: God our Savior showed us how good and kind he is.
text: Ŋaan ti Tinntɔɔ Yennu loomm nan u burchint nba din tan fiit nyii paanu yoonba,

ref: He saved us because of his mercy, and not because of any good things we have done. God washed us by the power of the Holy Spirit. He gave us new birth and a fresh beginning.
text: ki u din tinnit. Li din ki tee ti tiauŋ toonŋana paak kaa, ŋaan u mɔŋ ninbatinu paake ki u din tinnit, ki din wurit ki nyinn ti biit, ki te ki u Seyeeŋ ŋamm nant, ki turit manfopaann,

ref: God sent Jesus Christ our Savior to give us his Spirit.
text: ki din jii u Seyeeŋ maŋ ki turit bonchiann, ti Tinntɔɔ Yisa Masia paak.

ref: Jesus treated us much better than we deserve. He made us acceptable to God and gave us the hope of eternal life.
text: Yennu ninbatinu paak ki u din tun nna a wun teent niŋamm u boor; ki bia teent u waas nba mɔk dindann ki ti sa la manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

ref: This message is certainly true. These teachings are useful and helpful for everyone. I want you to insist that the people follow them, so that all who have faith in God will be sure to do good deeds.
text: Barii na tee barmɔniie. N loon ki a jii a dudukit kur kii wantib linba na, ki binba teen Yennu yada na-n jii bi yoo ki tun linba ŋan; ŋanne ŋan ki mɔk nyɔɔt ki tur sɔɔ kura.

ref: But don't have anything to do with stupid arguments about ancestors. And stay away from disagreements and quarrels about the Law of Moses. Such arguments are useless and senseless.
text: Ŋaan nyik mɔnɔyanna, nan yejamm ni yaadindɔuŋ kannu, ki daa kɔn nan leeb, ki bia daa nɔi mɔniɔk sennu paak, kimaan linba na kura ki ŋan, ki ki mɔk nyɔsiati.

ref: Warn troublemakers once or twice. Then don't have anything else to do with them.
text: Nirɔ-i tee tukinadaanɔ, a kpaanɔ munnlee amii munntaa; ki u baka-i kaa li kpaanii ni, fan nyikɔ ki daa fiin u po,

ref: You know their minds are twisted, and their own sins show how guilty they are. Personal Instructions and Greetings
text: kimaan a mi nan li nibooru nyik Masia weiuwa, ki u toonbiit nee want u mɔŋ biit. Kpaanii nba joont

ref: I plan to send Artemas or Tychicus to you. After he arrives, please try your best to meet me at Nicopolis. I have decided to spend the winter there.
text: Mi-i tun Atemas amii Tikikus a boor ni yoonba, fan yabir ki chetin Nikopolis doo ni na, kimaan n sii be leŋe gbintankaaŋ yoo.

ref: When Zenas the lawyer and Apollos get ready to leave, help them as much as you can, so they won't have need of anything.
text: Yabit a mɔŋ ki somm Senas, wunba tee barbuurɔ na, nan Apolos, ki bin la ba loon linba kur ki piin bi somma na.

ref: Our people should learn to spend their time doing something useful and worthwhile.
text: Li kpaa talas ki Masiaweira-ii tuun toonŋana kii sommit niib bi fara ni, ki daa be binbeŋ nba kaa nyɔɔti.

ref: Greetings to you from everyone here. Greet all of our friends who share in our faith. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
text: Binba be n boor ni na kura foonta. A foont binba teen Masia yada ki loonit na. Yennu-n somm i kura.

ref: From Paul, who is in jail for serving Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is like a brother because of our faith. Philemon, you work with us and are very dear to us. This letter is to you
text: Min Pɔɔl wunba kɔɔ dansarik Yisa Masia sann paak na, nan ti naa bik Timoti sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen ti toontunlɔɔ Filemon wunba ki ti loonɔ na,

ref: and to the church that meets in your home. It is also to our dear friend Apphia and to Archippus, who serves the Lord as we do.
text: nan Masiaweira nba lakii jiantir Yennu a ŋaak ni na; ki bia teen ti niipoo Afia nan ti toontunlɔɔ Akipus.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Philemon's Love and Faith
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi ki bia turi parmaasir. Filemon loomm nan yada labaar

ref: Philemon, each time I mention you in my prayers, I thank God.
text: N naa bik, yoo kur, mi-i tian a po n Yenmiaru ni, n teen Yennu niipoouk,

ref: I hear about your faith in our Lord Jesus and about your love for all God's people.
text: kimaan n poŋ gbat fa teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa yada ki loon u yab kur biaŋinba.

ref: As you share your faith with others, I pray they may come to know all the blessings Christ has given us.
text: N mei Yennu a timm nan fin nba taan ki teen Masia yada na paak, li sa te ki tin bann fanu binbenŋaŋ nba tee ti yar u ni na.

ref: My friend, your love has made me happy and has greatly encouraged me. It has also cheered the hearts of God's people. Paul Speaks to Philemon about Onesimus
text: N naa bik, a loomm maŋ turin parpeenn nan pardɔɔnn bonchiann, kimaan a cheen Yisa yab kur para. Pɔɔl mei sɔnu ki teen u yɔɔk Onesimus

ref: Christ gives me the courage to tell you what to do.
text: Li paak, Masia paak n bo kii mɔk jaŋmaanii nan man chaba ki fan tun linba tooki,

ref: But I would rather ask you to do it simply because of love. Yes, as someone in jail for Christ,
text: ŋaan loomm paak ki n meia, min Pɔɔl nba tee saakɔɔ ki bia kɔɔ dansarik mɔtana Yisa Masia paak na,

ref: I beg you to help Onesimus! He is like a son to me because I led him to Christ here in jail.
text: n meia ki teen Onesimus, wunba tee n bik, kimaan ma kɔɔ dansarik na n kpant u baae Masia ni.

ref: Before this, he was useless to you, but now he is useful both to you and to me.
text: Sinsinn na, u daan tuu ki mɔk nyɔɔt ki turani, ŋaan mɔtana u ji mɔk nyɔɔt bonchiann ki teen min nan fin kura.

ref: Sending Onesimus back to you makes me very sad.
text: Mɔtana, n tuumɔ a wun ŋmat a boor ni; ki n par loomm kura be u ni.

ref: I would like to keep him here with me, where he could take your place in helping me while I am here in prison for preaching the good news.
text: N bo loon ki mii diau n boor ni nna na ki wun gaar a paak ki sommin yoo nba ki n kɔɔ dansarik Masia barŋanii paak na.

ref: But I won't do anything unless you agree to it first. I want your act of kindness to come from your heart, and not be something you feel forced to do.
text: N ki loon ki n mukisa kaa ki a sommimi, ŋaan n loon ki a sommin nan a mɔŋ par ni loomme. N ki loon ki n tun siar ki li ki tee a mɔŋ par loommi.

ref: Perhaps Onesimus was taken from you for a little while so you could have him back for good,
text: Li pasiar, u yaatu na paak sa te ki a diau yoo kur.

ref: but not as a slave. Onesimus is much more than a slave. To me he is a dear friend, but to you he is even more, both as a person and as a follower of the Lord.
text: Mɔtana u ji ki tee daabir kaa, ŋaan u gar daabira, u tee ti naa bilonkake; ma loonɔ bonchiann biaŋinba na, ŋaan a sii loonɔ ki gar nna, kimaan u lek tee a daabire ki bia tee a naa bik ti Yomdaanɔ ni.

ref: If you consider me a friend because of Christ, then welcome Onesimus as you would welcome me.
text: Li paak, li-i tee ki a dukii a n tee a yɔɔkie, fan gaarɔ kaman nan fa bo sa gaar min biaŋinba na.

ref: If he has cheated you or owes you anything, charge it to my account.
text: Li-i tee ki u biir a siar amii u dia a pansiarie, fan te ki man pa li pann.

ref: With my own hand I write: I, PAUL, WILL PAY YOU BACK. But don't forget you owe me your life.
text: Na nna, n sɔbin nan n mɔŋ mɔŋ nuue; min Pɔɔl sa pa-a; ŋaan min kaa sa beta, a mɔŋ mi nan a dia n pann a manfoor paak.

ref: My dear friend and follower of Christ our Lord, please cheer me up by doing this for me.
text: Li paak, n naa bik, sommitin ti Yomdaanɔ paak, ki dɔɔn n par, kimaan ti set tee weinanleebe Masia ni.

ref: I am sure you will do all I have asked, and even more.
text: N sɔbin gbouŋ maŋ ki teena, ki ki mɔk birsuk nan a sa sak tun ki lin gar ma beta linba na kura.

ref: Please get a room ready for me. I hope your prayers will be answered, and I can visit you.
text: A bia lon diiuk ki guunin, kimaan i Yenmiaru paak, n mɔk dindann nan Yennu sa te ki n baar foonti. Foontii labaar

ref: Epaphras is also here in jail for being a follower of Christ Jesus. He sends his greetings,
text: Epafras wunba ki min nan ŋɔɔ kɔɔ dansarik Yisa Masia paak na teena u foontii;

ref: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, who work together with me.
text: ki n toontunleeb, Mak, nan Aristakus, nan Demas, nan Luk, mun bia teena bi foontii.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi i para ni.

ref: Long ago in many ways and at many times God's prophets spoke his message to our ancestors.
text: Yaayoo ni, ki Yennu din jikit yoo bonchiann nan sɔnii bonchiann ki te ki sɔkiniinba piak teen timm Juu teeb yejamm;

ref: But now at last, God sent his Son to bring his message to us. God created the universe by his Son, and everything will someday belong to the Son.
text: ŋaan mɔtana u te ki u Bija nba ki u gannɔ a wun yent bonsiar kur na poŋ pak nan timm; ki u din te ki u Bija maŋ nan dunia kur.

ref: God's Son has all the brightness of God's own glory and is like him in every way. By his own mighty word, he holds the universe together. After the Son had washed away our sins, he sat down at the right side of the glorious God in heaven.
text: Ŋɔɔe tee Yennu mɔŋ mɔŋ ninnauŋ, ki yentir Yennu yentu na, ki dia dunia yaka kur nan u mɔmaan nba mɔk paŋ. Wa din tun linba sa te ki niib-n la nyikinchabu bi biit paak yoonba na, ki u ji kar ti yabint Yennu nuudiitu po Yendɔuŋ ni. Yennu Bija maŋ paŋ labaar

ref: He had become much greater than the angels, and the name he was given is far greater than any of theirs. God's Son Is Greater than Angels
text: Yennu nba te ki u Bija maŋ sann gbeŋ gar malakanba sana biaŋinba na, nnee ki u Bija maŋ set garib.

ref: God has never said to any of the angels, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!” Neither has God said to any of them, “I will be his Father, and he will be my Son!”
text: Kimaan Yennu daa ki mi yet malakasɔɔ a “Fine tee n Bija, dinna ki n wann nan n tee a Baa.” Yennu bia ki mi yet malakasɔɔ po a “N tan sii tee a Baa, ki a tee n Bija.”

ref: When God brings his first-born Son into the world, he commands all of his angels to worship him.
text: Ki Yennu nba din tan yaa wun baar nan u Bija wunba tee bonnankaa yur na tingbouŋ na ni yoonba, u gbouŋ yet u po a “Li kpaa talas ki Yennu malakanba kura-n jiantɔ.”

ref: And when God speaks about the angels, he says, “I change my angels into wind and my servants into flaming fire.”
text: Ŋaan malakanba na po ŋanin u din yet a “U te ki u malakanba tee sei, ki bia teemm ki bi tee mupeenu.”

ref: But God says about his Son, “You are God, and you will rule as King forever! Your royal power brings about justice.
text: Ŋaan li yet u Bija na po ŋanin a “Yennu, a naan na sii be nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Ki yentinu dammir na tee a naan dammir.

ref: You loved justice and hated evil, and so I, your God, have chosen you. I appointed you and made you happier than any of your friends.”
text: Fa loon yentinu ŋaan nân biit na paak te ki a Yennu na ganna ki tura parpeenn bonchiann ki gar wa tur a yɔɔsinba biaŋinba.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “In the beginning, Lord, you were the one who laid the foundation of the earth and created the heavens.
text: Li bia din yet a “N Yomdaanɔ, fine din nan tingbouŋ na pinpiik niwa, ki nan sanpaak nan a mɔŋ nii.

ref: They will all disappear and wear out like clothes, but you will last forever.
text: Bi tan sa kpowa, ŋaan fin lek sii yɔɔ be; bi kur tan sa kpete ki chat chat kaman nan chinchenn na,

ref: You will roll them up like a robe and change them like a garment. But you are always the same, and you will live forever.”
text: ki fan pɔbimm kaman nan baauŋ na, ki bin lebit kaman nan chinchenn na. Ŋaan fin ŋanin kpan sii yɔɔ tee yomme ki a manfoor na kaa joontu.”

ref: God never said to any of the angels, “Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you!”
text: Tɔn, Yennu daa ki mi bet malakasɔɔ a “Karin n nuudiitu po nna na mɔkmɔk ki man tan jii a datai na ki teemm a taakpakta.”

ref: Angels are merely spirits sent to serve people who are going to be saved.
text: Li paak, malakanba tee bee? Bi kur tee sei nba jiantir Yennue, ki ŋɔɔ Yennu tuumm a bii sommit niib nba la tinnu na.

ref: We must give our full attention to what we were told, so we won't drift away.
text: Li paak, li kpaa talase ki tin gbiint barmɔnii nba ki ti din gbat na fanu ki tan daa kɔŋŋitiri.

ref: The message spoken by angels proved to be true, and all who disobeyed or rejected it were punished as they deserved.
text: Labaar nba ki malakanba din mɔn ki tur ti yejamm na din tee barmɔniie, ki wunba din tuu ki sakir ki weir din tuu la tubdatu nba jaŋɔe.

ref: So if we refuse this great way of being saved, how can we hope to escape? The Lord himself was the first to tell about it, and people who heard the message proved to us that it was true.
text: Li paak, ti-i kii sak ki gbiint tinnjaann maŋ labaar fanu, ti sa teen nlee ki nyi tubdatu ni? Ti Yomdaanɔ tiauŋo din dɔŋ mɔn tinnu maŋ labaar na po, ki binba din gbat na din wannit a li set tee barmɔniie;

ref: God himself showed that his message was true by working all kinds of powerful miracles and wonders. He also gave his Holy Spirit to anyone he chose to. The One Who Leads Us To Be Saved
text: ki Yennu nba din tuun bakitnauŋ nan yaarlituk toona nan nyina ki bia teemm u Seyeeŋ piinii u lomm ni biaŋinba na, u pukii u siara bi wannu maŋ paak. Wunba yen tinnu maŋ labaar

ref: We know that God did not put the future world under the power of angels.
text: Dunia nba baat ki ti pak li po na, Yennu din ki yaa a malakanbae tee li yudammi.

ref: Somewhere in the Scriptures someone says to God, “What makes you care about us humans? Why are you concerned for weaklings such as we?
text: Li sɔb siar po Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yennu, nisaarik tee bee ki a dukii u po; u mun tee bee ki a baka be u ni?

ref: You made us lower than the angels for a while. Yet you have crowned us with glory and honor.
text: A din te ki u pɔ malakanba yoo waaminna, ki bia te ki u la dontir nan paku,

ref: And you have put everything under our power!” God has put everything under our power and has not left anything out of our power. But we still don't see it all under our control.
text: ŋaan yen bonsiar kura.” Li yet a Yennu din teen nisaarike “bonsiar kur yudaanɔ.” Li want nan u yen bont kurewa, ŋaan mɔtana nisaarik daa ki yent bonsiar kuri.

ref: What we do see is Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels. Because of God's gift of undeserved grace, Jesus died for everyone. And now that Jesus has suffered and died, he is crowned with glory and honor!
text: Ŋaan ti laat Yisae. Ŋɔɔe ki Yennu din te ki u pɔ malakanba yoo waaminna, a wun gaar sɔɔ kur paak ki kpo, ŋɔɔ Yennu ninbatinu ni na. U kuun fara dinu maŋ paak ki Yennu te ki u la dontir nan paku.

ref: Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory.
text: Kimaan Yennu nba din nan bonsiar kur ki bia dia li kur na, wa jii u waas bonchiann ki kɔɔmm u naan ni na, li din tokin u boor ni a wun te ki bi tinnu daanɔ Yisa-n teen bi Tinntɔɔ nan barmɔnii u fara dinu ni.

ref: Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters.
text: Ŋɔɔ wunba te ki nisaarii teen Yennu niib, ki ŋɔɔ nan ŋamm kur ji taan ki mɔk Baa yenɔkɔɔ. Li paake ki Yisa ki tiin fei nan wun yiimm u naa waasi.

ref: He even said to God, “I will tell them your name and sing your praises when they come together to worship.”
text: U bet Yennu a “N tan sa mɔɔnt a sann n naa waas boor; n tan sa paka bi lakinn ni.”

ref: He also said, “I will trust God.” Then he said, “Here I am with the children God has given me.”
text: U bia yet a “N tan sa teen Yennu yada,” ki bia yet a “Min nan waas nba ki Yennu din turin nee be na.”

ref: We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death.
text: Tɔn, Yennu waas nba tee nisaarii na paak, Yisa mun din tee nisaarike kaman nan ŋamm na, a u kuun ni wun tan biir Sintaanii nba mɔk kuun paŋ na,

ref: But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying.
text: ki nyinn binba tiin kuun jaŋmaanii na bi jaŋmaanii maŋ ni, kimaan bi din tee jaŋmaanii yommiie bi manfoa kur ni.

ref: Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants.
text: Barmɔniie, li ki tee malakanba ki u sommit, ŋaan Abraham yaaboonae ki u sommit.

ref: He had to be one of us, so he could serve God as our merciful and faithful high priest and sacrifice himself for the forgiveness of our sins.
text: Linba na want nan li din kpaa talase a wuu tee kaman nan u naa waas na u binbeŋ kur ni, ki teen bi mantɔyudaanɔ nba tee ninbatinu daanɔ ki bia tee barmɔnii daanɔ, ki jii u mɔŋ ki teen marouŋ ki tur nisaarii Yennu boor ni, a wun nyik chab bi biit.

ref: And now that Jesus has suffered and was tempted, he can help anyone else who is tempted.
text: Ki u sa fit somm binba yaa bin kɔɔ jinjammii ni, kimaan jinjammii din jamm u tiauŋo ki dinnɔ fara.

ref: My friends, God has chosen you to be his holy people. So think about Jesus, the one we call our apostle and high priest!
text: N naa waas nba tee Yennu niib nba ki u din ganni na, yii dukii Yisa po, wunba ki Yennu din tumɔ a wun tan teen mantɔyudaanɔ ti Yenjiantu sɔnu ni.

ref: Jesus was faithful to God, who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in serving all God's people.
text: Moses nba din tee barmɔnii toontunnɔ Yennu ŋaateeb sinsuuk ni biaŋinba na, nnee ki Yisa mun din tee barmɔnii toontunnɔ Yennu nba din gannɔ na boor ni.

ref: But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house.
text: Ŋamaarɔ jaŋ nan bin teenɔ nibaakir ki gar ba teen ŋaak tiauŋ biaŋinba na; nnee ki Yisa jaŋ nan bin teenɔ nibaakir ki gar ba teen Moses biaŋinba na.

ref: Of course, every house is built by someone, and God is really the one who built everything.
text: Ŋaak-i taŋŋi see, sɔɔe sennir; li paak Yennue sent bonsiar kura.

ref: Moses was a faithful servant and told God's people what would be said in the future.
text: Moses din tee barmɔnii toontunnɔ Yennu ŋaateeb sinsuuk ni ki din wann linba po ki Yennu din yaa wun tan pak sianyoo na.

ref: But Christ is the Son in charge of God's people. And we are those people, if we keep on being brave and don't lose hope. A Rest for God's People
text: Masia ŋanin tee barmɔnii daanɔ nba yen Yennu ŋaak maŋe ki tee u Bija na. Ti-i mɔk parcheenn nan yuugontir ti dindann paak, ŋann timme tee Yennu ŋaateeb maŋ. Yennu niib foon labaar

ref: It is just as the Holy Spirit says, “If you hear God's voice today,
text: Li paak, i sak man Seyeeŋ nba yet biaŋinba na a “I-i gbat Yennu kunkɔr dinna,

ref: don't be stubborn! Don't rebel like those people who were tested in the desert. *
text: ya daa nyar i tuba kaman nan i yejamm nba din nyar bi tuba biaŋinba ki din kɔn nanɔ ki bikinɔ ba din be muuk ni yoonba na.

ref: For forty years your ancestors tested God and saw the things he did.
text: Yennu din yet a ‘Bi poŋ din la ma din tun linba kur ki saa jaŋ bina piinna na, ŋaan leŋe ki bi din bikinin.

ref: “Then God got tired of them and said, ‘You people never show good sense, and you don't understand what I want you to do.’
text: Ŋanne din te ki n donn wutoor niib maŋ paak ki din yet a Bi para ni, bi bota ki ki mi n sɔnii.

ref: God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ”
text: N din donn wutoor ki porib mɔb a Bi kan mi baar ma bo yaa n turib foon tiŋ nba na ni.’ ”

ref: My friends, watch out! Don't let evil thoughts or doubts make any of you turn from the living God.
text: Tɔn, n naasinyɔɔsinba, yi bann i yan ni ki sɔɔ daa tee biitdaanɔ ki kɔŋŋit yada ki tan mann ŋmat poor ki nyik manfodaanɔ Yennu na.

ref: You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn.
text: Ŋaan i sommit leeb man daar-n tuu daar, ta daa be ti manfoor ni yoonba na, ki biit daa kpanni ki i tee tubkangbata damm.

ref: We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end.
text: Kimaan ti-i dia ti sinsinn yada na chib ki saa baar ti manfoor joontu, timm nan Masia tee bonyenne.

ref: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
text: Ki lamme din gbat u kunkɔr ŋaan kɔn nanɔ ki yêtɔɔ? Ŋamm kaa din tee binba nyii Ijipt ki Moses din tee tɔɔndaanɔ na-aa?

ref: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
text: Yennu daa din por lamme mɔb a “Bi kan mi baar ma bo yaa n turib foon tiŋ nba na ni.” Li din tee binba din yêt u mɔb nee.

ref: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
text: Li paak, bi din ki fit kɔɔ tiŋ maŋ ni, kimaan bi yadakaarin paak.

ref: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
text: Mɔtana Yennu ji tur timme mɔlor maŋ a tin la foon nba ki u din piak li po na. Li paak, i bann i yanni man ki daa ŋaant ki sɔɔ-n tan kɔŋ foon maŋi.

ref: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
text: Kimaan ti gbat barŋanii kaman nan ba mun din gbatir biaŋinba, ŋaan li din ki turib nyɔɔti, kimaan ba din gbatir yoonba bi din ki gaarir nan yada.

ref: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
text: Li paak, timm nba teen yada na, ti la foon maŋa. Li din tun kaman nan Yennu nba din yet biaŋinba na a “N wutoore doo, ki n por mɔb a ŋamm tan kan kɔɔ foon tiŋ nba ki n din bo sa turib na ni.” Ŋaan wa din tan yet linba na na, ki sɔɔ ki u poŋ tun u naamii toona gbenn pinpiik niwa.

ref: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Kimaan Yennu gbouŋ ni siar po li poŋ yet li daaŋannlore daar maŋ po a “Ki Yennu din nyik u toona kura ki foi daaŋannlore daar.”

ref: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
text: Ŋaan ki u bia yet nna na a “Bi tan kan mi kɔɔ n foon tiŋ nba ki n din bo yaa n turib na ni.”

ref: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
text: Binba din gbat barŋanii na sinsinn na din ki la li foon, kimaan bi din yêt Yennu mɔb. Li paak, nisiab be ki tan sa la foon maŋ.

ref: Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn!”
text: Li paak, ki Yennu bia senn daleer ki yi daar maŋ “dinna.” Ki bina bonchiann din gar, ki Yennu din te ki David yet li po kaman nan la poŋ piak biaŋinba na a “Dinna, i-i gbat Yennu kunkɔr yoonba, ya daa nyar i tuba man.”

ref: If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest.
text: Kimaan Joshua-i diŋŋi turib foon, li poorpo Yennu ji bo kan pak daleer po.

ref: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
text: Li paak, foon dasiar bia daa be ki tee Yennu niib yar, kaman nan wa din foi daaŋannlore daar biaŋinba na.

ref: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
text: Kimaan wunba la Yennu foon maŋ, u nyik u toona nee na kaman nan Yennu nba din nyik u naamii toona ni biaŋinba na.

ref: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
text: Li paak, ŋaant man ki tin yabir ti mɔŋ ki la foon maŋ, ŋaan ki ti sɔɔ tan daa kɔŋŋit kaman nan ba din yêt Yennu mɔb ki tan kɔŋŋir biaŋinba na.

ref: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
text: Kimaan Yennu mɔmaan maŋ tee barii nba foe, ki mɔk paŋ, ki bia di ki gar jukbanjiak nba kur ŋma mɔi ŋannlee. Yennu mɔmaan fit ŋma ki kɔɔ baat nisaarik seek nan u manfoor boor ni, ki kɔɔ baat u tukita nan u kpaba beesin ni, ki kɔɔ bɔkitir u dudukit nan u par ni bibikit.

ref: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
text: Ki bonnankar kur dɔɔ yeenin ni, ki Yennu wunba tan sa bu bi buut na ninbina laatib; ki bonsiar kaa ki bɔri. Yisae tee ti mantɔjaann

ref: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
text: Li paak, ŋaant man ki tii dia ti yada nba ki ti fiit wann na kenken, kimaan ti mɔk mantɔyudaanjaann, ŋɔɔe tee Yennu Bija Yisa wunba poŋ gar saan Yennu tiauŋ boor ni na.

ref: Jesus understands every weakness of ours, because he was tempted in every way that we are. But he did not sin!
text: Ti mantɔyudaanɔ maŋ ki tee wunba kan fit tint ninbaauk ti butuŋ paak kaa, ŋaan u mɔŋ din la bikinu sɔnii booru kur ni kaman nan ta mun laat biaŋinba na, ŋaan u lek din ki tun biiti.

ref: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
text: Li paak, ŋaant man ki tii mɔk parcheenn ki baar Yennu nba tee burchindaanɔ na naangbouŋ boor ni, a tin la ninbatinu nan sommir ki lin sommit yoo nba ki ti loonn na.

ref: Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins.
text: Tɔn, mantɔyudaanɔ kur nba ki bi gannɔ niib sinsuuk ni na, bi sennɔ a wun gaar u leeb paake ki mann mannu ki tur Yennu bi biit paak.

ref: A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people.
text: Ŋaan u mɔŋ nba tee butundaanɔ na sɔnii bonchiann ni na paak, u sii fit be nan binba pɔɔr yan ki kpet sɔnu na soonna,

ref: This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others.
text: ki u mɔŋ nba tee butundaanɔ na paak, li kpaa talas ki wun mann mannu u mɔŋ toonbiit paak kaman nan wa mann mannu niib na toonbiit paak biaŋinba na.

ref: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
text: Sɔɔ ki fit dont u mɔŋ a wuu tee mantɔyudaanɔ, see ki Yennu gannɔ kaman nan wa din gann Aron ki teenɔ mantɔyudaanɔ biaŋinba na.

ref: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
text: Nnee mun, Masia din ki donn u mɔŋ ki gann u mɔŋ a wuu tee mantɔyudaanɔ, ŋaan Yennue din yetɔ a “A tee n Bijae; la nyii dinna ki n wann nan n tee a Baae.”

ref: In another place, God says, “You are a priest forever just like Melchizedek.”
text: Ki siar po u bia yet a “A tan sii tee mantɔɔ ki jikit saa yoo nba kaa gbennu, kii tee Melkisedek paabu ni mantɔɔ.”

ref: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
text: Tɔn, Yisa nba din be tingbouŋ na ni yoonba na u din mei Yennu ki barimɔ, ki bui nan ninnyuut Yennu nba sa fit fatɔ kuun ni na boor ni. Ki wa din baakit Yennu na paak ki Yennu din gbat u miaruwa.

ref: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
text: U tee Yennu Bijae, ŋaan u mun din dii farae Yennu mɔbsaku ni,

ref: Suffering made Jesus perfect, and now he can save forever all who obey him.
text: ki din tan gbenn fara dinu maŋ, ki te ki binba kur saak u mɔb na la tinnu nba kaa gbennu.

ref: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu yiinɔ manteeb yudaanɔ, Melkisedek paabu ni. Yada lunu kpaanii labaar

ref: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
text: Ti bo sa pak Masia nan Melkisedek po bonchiann ŋaan li paar nan ki ti wanni, kimaan i ki bant li paak yiami.

ref: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
text: Nna yoo li ji bo kpaa talase ki yii tee wannteeb, ŋaan i bia loon ki sɔɔ-n wanni Yennu mɔmaan pinpiik ni sɔnii na. I bia daa ŋan nan biime, i ki jaŋ nan jeŋmankati,

ref: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
text: kimaan wunba daa mɔɔr biin tee bibunne, ki daa ki bann yentinu wannu na fanu.

ref: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
text: Ŋaan jeŋmankat tee saakab yare, ŋamm tuu tumii ki fit bann linba tee bonbiir nan linba tee bonŋann.

ref: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
text: Li paak, ŋaant man ki tii dia saakayan kii tumii Masia barii ki saan tɔɔnn po; ŋaan ŋamm ki tumii u barii bonchiann ki pukin pinpiik ni wannu po; li ji ki kpaa talas a bin ŋamm wanni a Nyikin toonbiit nan kuun sɔnu, ki teen Yennu yada,

ref: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
text: ki bia wanni Yennu nyunwuru po, nan niiyiinu niib paak, nan kpeemm fiiru po, nan tubdatu nba kaa gbennu na po.

ref: Let's grow up, if God is willing.
text: Yennu-i sak, ti sa saan tɔɔnne.

ref: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
text: Tɔn, tiŋ nba ki saak baa li paak ki li maakit ki tee jeet teen binba ŋammitir na, Yennue senn mɔsonŋann ki teen tiŋ maŋ paak;

ref: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
text: ŋaan tiŋ maŋ-i tee kunkonii nan mɔbiit, li ki mɔk nyɔɔti, li kan yukir ki Yennu tan sa por mɔb ki lin di muu biir.

ref: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
text: Ti naa walonkai, ta lek piak linba na, ŋaan ti ki mɔk birsuk i po. Ti mi nan i yare tee linba chee ki bia tee tinnu yar.

ref: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
text: Kimaan Yennue ŋan, ki kan tamm ya tun linba ki turɔ amii ya loonɔ biaŋinba ki somm Masiaweira, ki bia daa sommitib na.

ref: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
text: Ti loon ki i sɔɔ kur-n yabir u mɔŋ paake mɔkmɔk nan li joontu, ki ya mɔk dindann biaŋinba na, li set sa teen mɔnii ki turi.

ref: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
text: Ti ki loon ki yii tee gbannyakdammi, ŋaan ti loon ki yii tee binba teen Masia yadae, kii mɔk sukuru, ki tan gaar linba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn li po na. Yennu mɔsonn nba kaa birsuk

ref: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
text: U din yetɔ a “Li tee barmɔniie, n senn mɔsonŋann a paak ki bia sa tura yaaboona bonchiann.”

ref: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
text: Ki Abraham din mɔk sukurue, ki tan gaar linba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn a wun tan turɔ na.

ref: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
text: Nirɔ-i senn mɔsonn, u tuu jii wunba garɔ sanne ki senn, ki mɔsonn maŋ-n kpi mɔniɔk kura.

ref: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
text: Tɔn, binba din yaa bin gaar Yennu nba din senn mɔsonn nba na, u din loon wun wannib a u kan mi lebit u dudukiti; li paak ki u din pukin u mɔlor u mɔsonn maŋ paak.

ref: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
text: Li paak, bona ŋannlee nba kaa lebitu na be ki wann nan Yennu set sa gbeen u mɔsonn maŋ. Li paak, timm nba chiar saan u boor ni ki tinn na mɔk parcheenn ki gaar dindann nba be ti tɔɔnn po na chib.

ref: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
text: Dindann nba ki ti mɔk na tee kaman nan manfoor jaantarik nba dia ti manfoor fanu. Li tee dindann nba gar Yendɔuŋ ni diiuk kaaruk nee ki kɔɔ diiuk maŋ linn po.

ref: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
text: Leŋe ki Yisa poŋ din gaar ti paak ki gar ki tee ti sɔnliitɔɔ, ki teen ti mantɔyudaanɔ nan yoo nba kaa gbennu, ki bia tee Melkisedek paabu ni mantɔɔ.

ref: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
text: Melkisedek maŋ din tee Salem tiŋ bate ki bia din tee yabint Yennu mantɔɔ. Abraham nba din tan nyii tɔb nba ki u din kɔn nyann batnba banna na, Melkisedek din chetɔ ki senn mɔsonŋann u paak;

ref: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
text: ki Abraham din nyinn bona piik ni yennkɔɔ wa din kɔn fat bont nba na kur ni ki turɔ.

ref: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
text: Labasiar ki be ki want Melkisedek baa po amii u naa po amii u yejamm po. Labasiar kaa ki want u maru po amii u kuun po. U naan nan Yennu Bija nee. U lek bia yɔɔ tee Yennu mantɔɔe nan yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
text: Li paak, i la wa din tee nijaann biaŋinba, kimaan ti yejasaakɔɔ Abraham lek din tan nyinn wa din kɔn fat bont nba tɔb ni na bona piik ni yennkɔɔ ki turɔ.

ref: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
text: Ki sennu din wann a Liifai teeb yaaboona nba tee manteeb na, a bin tuu gaar bi niib Juu teeb nba mɔk linba li bona piik ni yennkɔɔ, ŋaan bi niib maŋ mun bia lek din tee Abraham yaaboonae.

ref: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
text: Melkisedek din ki tee Liifai teeb yaaboonn kaa, ŋaan u din gaar bona piik ni yennkɔɔ Abraham boor, ki bia senn mɔsonŋann ŋɔɔ Abraham wunba din gaar Yennu mɔsona na paak.

ref: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
text: Mɔniɔk kaa nan nisɔɔ nba senn mɔsonŋanne tee nijaann ki gar wunba gaan mɔsonŋann maŋ.

ref: Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive.
text: Manteeb nba din tuu gaan niib nba mɔk linba li bona piik ni yennkɔɔ na din tee nisaarii ki din tuu tan kpo; ŋaan Melkisedek wunba din gaar Abraham bona piik ni yennkɔɔ na ŋanin be, kaman nan Yennu gbouŋ nba wann biaŋinba na.

ref: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
text: Ti sa fit yet a yoo nba ki Abraham din nyinn u bont ni bona piik ni yennkɔɔ na, u yaaboona Liifai teeb binba din tan gaan bona piik ni yennkɔɔ maŋ, mun din mi bi bona piik ni yennkɔɔ nyinnu,

ref: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
text: kimaan ba lek daa din ki mar Liifai na, ŋaan ti sa fit yet a yoo nba ki Melkisedek din chet Abraham na, ŋɔɔ Liifai din be u yeja Abraham maŋ ni.

ref: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
text: Yennu din tur Juu teeb sennu ki wann Liifai teeb bi mannu toona nba sii tee biaŋinbae. Ki Liifai teeb manteeb toona-i diŋŋi be fanuie, li din bo kan kpaa talas ki mantɔganɔ wunba ki set Aron setu ni ŋaan see Melkisedek setu ni na-n baari.

ref: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
text: Kimaan yoo nba ki manteeb yudantuk lebitu nba din tuu tan baar ki bin lebit na, sennu mun bia din tuu lebite,

ref: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
text: ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa wunba tee mantɔganɔ maŋ nyii boorganu nba ki bi sɔɔ din ki mi tun mannu toonni.

ref: Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
text: Sɔɔ kur poŋ mi nan ŋɔɔ Yisa nyii Juda nibooru ni, nibooru nba ki Moses din ki mi pak siar bi po ki jikit saa manteeb po. Mantɔlɔɔ nba naan nan Melkisedek na labaar

ref: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
text: Yoo nba ki mantɔganɔ nba naan nan Melkisedek na din tan baar nee ki ti mantik ŋamm bann barii maŋ paak.

ref: That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end.
text: Ŋɔɔe tee mantɔɔ, ki nisaarik sennii ni kaa ki u teen li mantɔɔ, ŋaan li tee manfoor nba kaa gbennu na paŋ nie ki u teen mantɔɔ.

ref: In this way a weak and useless command was put aside,
text: Li paak, sennkperu na ji din bakine, kimaan li din ki mɔk paŋi, ki bia ki mɔk nyɔɔti,

ref: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
text: kimaan Moses sennu maŋ din kan fit te ki siar be fanu. Ŋaan mɔtana ti ji mɔk linba turit dindann nba chee na, ki li nie ki ti chat nakin Yennu boor ni.

ref: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
text: Yennu din tuu ki teen manteleeb na li mɔsonn booru. Ŋaan Yisa din teen mantɔɔ yoo nba ki Yennu din tan tur mɔsonn ki betɔ a “Min Yennu senn mɔsonn ki ji kan lebit n dudukit, ki yet a ‘Fine sii tee mantɔɔ ki jikit saa yoo nba kaa gbennu.’ ”

ref: This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God.
text: Li paak, nnee te ki Yisa tee wunba wann nan mɔsonn nba chee na kaa lotu.

ref: There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died.
text: Tɔn, yaayoo ni manteeb maŋ din tuu tan kpoe, ki bi leeb na-n mun gaar bi toona; li paak, ki bi din yab.

ref: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
text: Li paak, binba chat nakin Yennu, ŋɔɔ Yisa sann paak na, u sa fit tinnib mɔtana nan yoo nba kaa gbennu, kimaan u lek be u manfoor ni yoo kur, ki mei Yennu ki teemm.

ref: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
text: Li paak, Yisae tee ti mantɔɔ nba jaŋ nan ti sommir. Ŋɔɔe tee popeendaanɔ, ki ki mɔk bisiati; u be u kɔɔe nan biitdamm, ki Yennu donnɔ ki gar Yendɔuŋ ni teeb kura.

ref: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
text: Manteyudaanleeb na din tuu mann marouŋ daar-n tuu daar bi tiauŋ biit paak sinsinn, ki bia mann marouŋ ki tur niib; ŋɔɔ Yisa ki tee nna kaa; u din jii u mɔŋ ki mann marouŋ yomkɔɔe, ŋaan ki li marouŋ gbenn ki tur sɔɔ kura.

ref: The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
text: Moses sennu na din tuu gann nisaarii ki teemm manteeb yudamm; ŋaan ki bi din tuu ki be fanu ki gbenni. Ŋaan sennu maŋ poorpoe ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn nan u Bija Yisa wunba sii yɔɔ be fanu ki jikit saa yoo nba kaa gbennu na, ki teenɔ mantɔɔ.

ref: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
text: Barii na barnɔɔk want nan ti mɔk mantɔyudaanɔ nba tee nna mun, ki kar Yennu nba mɔk paŋ kur na naangbouŋ nuudiitu po Yendɔuŋ ni,

ref: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
text: ki tuun mantɔɔ yudant toona Yennu diiuk ni; li diiuko tee barmɔnii jiantu diiuk ki ti Yomdaanɔ din ŋammir, linba ki tee nisaarik ŋammu.

ref: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
text: Li-i bo-i tee ki u be tingbouŋ na ni, u bo kii tee mantɔɔ fabaa, kimaan manteeb nba teen Yennu piinii, Juu teeb sennu nba senn biaŋinba na, poŋ be.

ref: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
text: Ba tuun linba na tee Yendɔuŋ ni diiuk toona siara ninnauŋo; Moses nba din tun linba na din naan nnee; kimaan Moses nba din yaa wun faa Yenjiantu lanbouŋ yoonba na, Yennu din kpaanɔ ki yet a “A tun bonsiar kur kaman nan ma wanna jɔɔr na paak biaŋinba na.”

ref: Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
text: Ŋaan Yennu tur Yisa mannu toona nba chee bi mannu toona na, kaman nan u mɔlor nba chee ki be Yennu nan nisaarii sinsuuk ni biaŋinba na, kimaan u mɔlor maŋ gaa nan mɔsona nba chee nae.

ref: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
text: Kimaan sinsinn mɔlor na-i diŋŋi fit te ki nisaarii be fanu, li ji din bo kii kpaa talas ki Yennu-n tur mɔlor pukin taar munnlee.

ref: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
text: Ŋaan Yennu din la nisaarii biite yoo nba ki u yet a “Min nba tee i Yomdaanɔ nae beeri a yoo baat na, ki n tan sa lor mɔlorpaann ki tur Israel niib nan Juda niib.

ref: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
text: Li tan kii tee kaman nan mɔlor nba ki n din senn ki tur bi yejamm daar nba ki n din kub bi nii ki nyinnib Ijipt tiŋ ni na. Bi din ki teen mɔlor nba ki n din lor ki turib na yada, li paak, ki n baka din kaa bi po.

ref: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Min nba tee i Yomdaanɔ na beeri ma yaa n senn mɔlor ki tur Israel niib daa nba baat na ni: n ji sa tabin n sennii maŋ bi dudukit nie, ki sɔbir tabin bi para ni. Mine sii tee bi Yennu ki bi tee n niib.

ref: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
text: Li ji kii kpaa talas ki bi sɔɔ-n wann u lɔɔ amii ki bet u lɔɔ a ‘Fini, bant ti Yomdaanɔ,’ kimaan bi kura tan sii mi mine, binba tee nijaana nan binba tee nitarii mun.

ref: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
text: N tan sa nyik bi biit ki chabib, ki ji kii tian bi biit na po.”

ref: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
text: Yennu nba piak mɔlorpaann maŋ po nee, ki sinsinn mɔlor na teen mɔsonkper; ki bonsiar nba kpet ki kokir, li ji kan yukir nan botu.

ref: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
text: Bi din faa bonkobgbant lanbouŋo, ki li mɔk paklooru nan linn po, ki yi paklooru na Yennu yar. Bi din senn fitire paklooru na ni, ki teebur see leŋ ki boroboro tɔɔ li paak ki bi teen Yennu.

ref: Behind the curtain was the most holy place.
text: Ki li linn po mun ki bi yi leŋ Yennu yar nan barmɔnii.

ref: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
text: Sarima binbintir din be, ki bi jokit wubit li paak ki teen Yennu, ki Yennu sennu lakir din be ki bi tabin sarima li kur po, ki sarima bobir din be lakir na ni nan jeet nba yi mana na leŋ. Ki Aron patu nba din bint fapaana na kpee lakir maŋ ni, ki tana ŋannlee nba ki bi din koor ki sennii sɔb li paak na kpee li ni.

ref: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
text: Bonsia nba ki bi yir cherubim din tɔɔ lakir maŋ paak, ki bi kpinkpant bakin siaminba din tee toonbiit nyikinchab boor ni na, ki li want nan Yennu be leŋ. Ŋaan mɔtana ji ki tee yoo nba ki ti sa pak bont maŋ po nan li barnɔɔk kuri.

ref: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
text: Ba din senn bona kura nna nee, ki manteeb din tuu kɔɔ Yenjiantu lanbouŋ maŋ paklooru ni yoo kur ki tuun Yenjiantu toona.

ref: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
text: Ŋaan manteeb yudaanɔ kuukɔɔe din tuu kɔɔ lanbouŋ na linn po yomkɔɔ binn nɔɔk ni. U din tuu dia sɔme ki mann marouŋ ki tur Yennu, u mɔŋ toonbiit paak, nan niib nba din tuu kpet tun toonbiit paak.

ref: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
text: Yennu Seyeeŋ din want Yenjiantu lanbouŋ na po fanu, a siaminba tee Yennu yar nan barmɔnii na daa ki loot yoo nba ki paklooru maŋ daa din see na.

ref: This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices.
text: Linba na tee barjokit ki tur timm nba be mɔtana, ki want nan binba din jiantir Yennu na nba din tuu teenɔ bi piinii nan bi bonkobit sɔn na kan fit wuur bi para ni biit Yennu boor ni,

ref: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
text: kimaan ŋann din tuu tee jeet nan bonnyunyuut nba kɔɔ nan linba ki kɔɔ na po, nan gbanant ŋammu po. Linba na kur din tee gbanant senniie a bii waa-r mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Yennu tan sa senn u sennpaanu.

ref: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
text: Ŋaan Masia wunba tee sɔnŋanu nba na mantɔyudaanɔ na baar na. Wa kɔɔ jiantu lanbouŋ nba ni ki tuun na tee lanbonjaann nba be fanu nan barmɔniie; li ki tee nisaarik nba faa linba kaa, li bia ki tee tingbouŋ na ni yar kaa.

ref: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
text: Masia nba din saan kɔɔ jiantu lanbouŋ maŋ ni ki saa gar boor nba tee Yennu yar nan barmɔnii na ni yomkɔɔ yoonba, ŋaan ki li gbenn nna, u din ki dia buunii nan naajai sɔn kaa ki mann marouŋ, ŋaan u din jii u mɔŋ sɔme ki pa ki nyinnit daabsin ni yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
text: Bi din tuu jii buunii nan naajai sɔme, ki bia jii naasar ki mann ki joo muu, ŋaan jii li tanpent na ki mii mii niib nba din tuu mɔk yanbɔmm Yennu tɔɔnn na paak, ki lin ŋamm bi gbanant a bii yeen.

ref: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
text: Linba na-i ŋamm gbanant, ki Masia sɔn mantik sa nyinn biit ti para ni a tii waa manfodaanɔ Yennu, kimaan Seyeeŋ nba be yoo nba kaa gbennu paŋ ni ki ŋɔɔ Masia din jii u mɔŋ ki mann marouŋ nba be fanu fanu ki tur Yennu.

ref: In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use.
text: Tɔn, nirɔ-i sɔb u fadinu gbouŋ, see ki bi tan wann nan wunba sɔb fadinu gbouŋ na kpowa.

ref: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
text: Yoo nba ki wunba sɔb fadinu gbouŋ maŋ daa be u manfoor ni na, fadinu gbouŋ maŋ ki mɔk yikoo. Li yikoo be u kuun poorpoewa.

ref: Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect.
text: Li paake te ki sinsinn mɔlor na din tuu mɔk yikoo sɔn marouŋ poorpo.

ref: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
text: Sinsinn, Moses din tuu mɔn Yennu sennu na kure kaman nan la sɔb biaŋinba na. Li poorpo wun jii dufatlai ki lor nan pekobmɔnt ki pakii naajai nan buunii sɔn nba ŋmat nan nyun na, ki miir sennu gbouŋ na paak nan niib maŋ kur paak,

ref: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
text: ki beerib a “Mɔlor nba ki Yennu senn turi a yin sakir na sɔme na.”

ref: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
text: Moses bia din tuu mii sɔn maŋ Yenjiantu lanbouŋ na paake, nan bonsiar kur nba ki bi dia jiantir Yennu na paak.

ref: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
text: Barmɔniie, Yennu sennu wannu ni, sɔme din tuu ŋamm bont kur ki li be fanu ki ki mɔk biiti, ki sɔm-i kii nyii, Yennu kan nyik chab niib biiti. Masia marouŋ nee kpeen biit

ref: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
text: Li paak, li din tuu kpaa talase ki bin mann marouŋ ki ŋamm bona nba tee Yendɔuŋ ni bona ninnauŋ na; ŋaan marouŋ nba chee sa ŋamm Yendɔuŋ ni bona tiauŋ.

ref: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
text: Nifosaarik nba din ŋammir boor nba tee Yennu yar ki nannir kaman nan Yendɔuŋ ni bont nba tee li barmɔnii na, ki Masia din ki saan kɔɔ leŋ kaa, ŋaan u din saan kɔɔ Yendɔuŋ mɔŋ mɔŋ nie, siaminba ki u be Yennu tɔɔnn ti paak na.

ref: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
text: Juu teeb manteeb yudaanɔ na din tuu kɔɔ nan bonkobit sɔn boor nba tee Yennu yar na ni yomkɔɔe binn kur nɔɔk ni; ŋaan Masia ŋanin ki yaa wun mann marouŋ taar bonchiann kaa.

ref: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
text: Li-i diŋŋi bo-i tee nnaie, ŋann la din nyii dunia namu pinpiik niwa u din bo sa di fara taar bonchiann. Ŋaan mɔtana, yoo nba ji tan per joontu na ki u din dɔkit a wun jii u mɔŋ ki mann marouŋ ki kpeen biit.

ref: We die only once, and then we are judged.
text: Yennu senn a see ki nirɔ kur-n kpo yomkɔɔ, ki li poorpo Yennu-n bu u buut.

ref: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
text: Li paak, Masia nba din jii u mɔŋ mann marouŋ yomkɔɔ ki nyinn nifosaarii bonchiann biit na tan sa jena, ki ji tan kan boi biit po kaa, ŋaan u tan sa tinn binba guu-u nan bi para kur nee.

ref: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
text: Juu teeb sennu na din tuu tee Yendɔuŋ ni bonŋana ninnauŋ nba din be sɔnu ki baat nee, ŋaan li din tuu ki tee li mɔŋ mɔŋ kaa. Li paak, binn kur marouŋ nba din ki gbent na kan fit teen binba baat nan marouŋ na fanu.

ref: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
text: Li marouŋ-i bo-i fit ŋamm bi para, bi din bo sa mann yomkɔɔe ŋaan ji nyik, ki biit ji kii waabi.

ref: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
text: Li paak, Masia nba din yaa wun sik tingbouŋ na ni yoonba na, u din bet Yennu a “A ki loon marouŋ nan piinii kaa, ŋaan a ŋamm gbanansiare ki turin.

ref: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.”
text: A ki mɔk parpeenn nan bonkobit nba ki bi mann marouŋ ki jokitir muu, biit paak na.

ref: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
text: Li paake te ki n din yet a ‘Yennu, mine na, n sa tun a loomm kaman nan la sɔb a gbouŋ ni ki yet n po biaŋinba na.’ ”

ref: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
text: Sinsinn, u din yet a “A ki mɔk parpeenn nan marouŋ nan piinii, nan bonkobit nba ki bi mann marouŋ ki jokitir muu na, nan marouŋ nba ki bi tuu mann biit paak na kaa.” Bi din tuu mann marouŋ maŋ kura kaman nan sennu nba wann biaŋinbae.

ref: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
text: Li poorpo ki u bia yet a “Yennu, mine na, n sa tun a lomma.” Li paak, Yennu faa kpeen mannkpera kura, ŋaan jii Masia marouŋ na ki senn li setu ni.

ref: So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
text: Yisa Masia din tun linba ki Yennu loon wun tun na, ki jii u gbanant ki mann marouŋ yomkɔɔ ki te ki ti teen Yennu niib.

ref: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
text: Mannbooryeŋ nba ki Juu teeb mantɔɔ kur tuun daar-n tuu daar na kan fit nyinn biiti.

ref: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
text: Ŋaan Masia ŋanin din mann marouŋ yomkɔɔe, linba mɔk yikoo yoo kur biit paak, ŋaan ŋmat doo Yendɔuŋ ni ki kar Yennu nuudiitu po.

ref: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
text: Leŋe ki u be ki guu mɔkmɔk nan Yennu nba tan sa dinn u datai tiŋ ni.

ref: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
text: Li paak, ŋɔɔ din mann marunyemmire ki te ki binba ki u nyinnib biit ni na be fanu yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
text: Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ mun bia teent u siara a

ref: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
text: “Yennu yet a ‘Ma yaa n turib mɔlor nbae na daar maŋ poorpo; n tan sa teen n sennii bi para ni, ki sɔbir bi dudukit ni. ’ ”

ref: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
text: Li poorpo ki u bia ŋamm yet a “N ji kan mi ki tiar bi yanbɔmm nan bi biit po jikii.”

ref: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
text: Yennu nba nyik chab biit na, u ji ki loon marunsiar biit paaki. Yi ŋaan ki tin chat nakin Yennu boor ni man

ref: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
text: N naa waas, Yisa kuun maŋ paak ti mɔk parcheenn nan ki tin kɔɔ boor nba tee Yennu yar nan barmɔnii na ni.

ref: by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
text: Yisa gbanant, linba tee sɔnpaanu nan sɔnu nba mɔk manfoor nae loot tammɔb maŋ.

ref: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
text: Ti mantɔjaann nba yen Yennu ŋaak maŋ na paak,

ref: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
text: yi ŋaan ki tin chat nakin Yennu boor ni man nan para nba be fanu, nan barmɔnii yada, ki daa mɔk birsuku, nan ti para nba ki Yisa sɔn nyinn dudukbiit li ni na, ki jii ti gbanant nba ki ti din wuur nan nyun nba kaa jakint na.

ref: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
text: Yi ŋaan ki tii mɔk dindann nba ki ti fiit wann na fanu man, ki daa birisi, kimaan wunba din senn mɔsonn ki turit na tee barmɔnii daanɔe.

ref: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
text: Yi ŋaan ki tin yabir ki fiin leeb loomm nan bi toonŋana,

ref: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
text: ki daa nyikit lakina nan ti leeb kaman nan nisiab nba nyikit lakinu biaŋinba na, ŋaan i sommit leeb man, ki mantik tee ta ji laat ki ti Yomdaanɔ daar maŋ nyarii naki biaŋinba na paak.

ref: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
text: Ta bann barmɔnii maŋ nba fiit wannit na, ti-i biakii ŋammit tuun toonbiit, marunsiar ji kaa ki sa wuur ti biit maŋi.

ref: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
text: Linba ji biar tee buut bunu nba mɔk jaŋmaaniie, nan muchiauŋ nba baat ki tan sa biir binba yêt Yennu na.

ref: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
text: Wunba din tuu yêt Moses sennu na weiu, ki niib bann ki teen siaradamm bannlee amii ki gar niib bannlee, bi din tuu kpiu, ki ninbatinu kaa.

ref: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
text: Li paak, nirɔ nba fiak Yennu Bija maŋ ki ki baakit sɔn nba ŋamm Yennu mɔlor ki nyinnɔ u biit ni na, ki sukii burchinseyeeŋ na, bikint li yɔɔ nba tan sa jaŋ nan tubdatbiiuk nba.

ref: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
text: Kimaan ti mi wunba din yet a “Mine sa pa pann ki jiin biit.” Li bia sɔb a “Ti Yomdaanɔe tan sa bu u niib buut.”

ref: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
text: Li tee bonbilankante ki sɔɔ-n tan kɔɔ manfodaanɔ Yennu wutoor ni.

ref: Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light.
text: I tian man yoo nba gar na labaar; Yennu yentu nba din yent i paak yoonba, i din di fara bonchiann, ŋaan li farsiar din ki nyanniini.

ref: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
text: Sianyoo ni bi din tuu sukii-i paanu ni, ki daamii bonbiir, ki sianyoo i din tuu tan teen siir nan yin pukin binba ki bi din tun nna maŋ paak ki di namm nan li farawa.

ref: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
text: I din mɔk ninbatinu nan binba be dansarii ni; ki sianyoo bin din tan fat i yent, ŋaan ki i lek mɔk parpeenn, kimaan i mi nan ya bia mɔk yent nba chee ki ki mɔk gbennu yaayoo.

ref: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
text: Li paak, i daa nyikit yabiru mani, kimaan i yabiru na baat nan nyɔchiauŋo.

ref: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
text: Li ŋan ki yii mɔk sukuru, ki fit tun Yennu lomm na, ki la wa din senn mɔsonn a u sa turit linba na.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Li tenn yoo waaminnae ki wunba baat na sa baar, ki kan yukir nan baaru.

ref: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
text: Ŋaan n niŋamm na ŋanin tan sa teenin yada kii waan; ŋaan bi yenɔ-i ŋmat poor, n tan kii mɔk parpeenn nanɔ.”

ref: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
text: Timm ki tee nisiab nba ŋmat poor ki baa yann kaa, ŋaan ti teen Masia yada ki u tinnit.

ref: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
text: Yada tee, ta ki mɔk birsuk nan bont nba ki ti mɔk dindann li paak na set be ŋaan daa ki lar nan ti ninbinn nee.

ref: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
text: Yennu din pak yaayoo ni niib na bi yada paake.

ref: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
text: Ti yada paake ki ti bann nan Yennu mɔmaame din nan dunia na, ki ta laat bona nba na u din ki jii bonsiar kaa ki nanni.

ref: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
text: Abel yada paake ki u din mann mannu tur Yennu ki li mannu ŋan ki gar Keenn yar. U yada paake ki Yennu din pakɔ ki purɔ yentinɔ, kimaan ŋɔɔ Yennu tiauŋ din gaar u piinii nawa. Abel din kpowa, ŋaan u yada paake ki u bia lek daa piak.

ref: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
text: Enok yada paake ki u din tan ki kpo, ŋaan ki Yennu din jiiu ki doo nanɔ Yendɔuŋ ni, ki sɔɔ ji ki lau; kimaan Yennue poŋ din jiiu. Ki li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a yoo nba ki Yennu daa din ki jiiu na, ŋɔɔ Yennu numm din gboouwa.

ref: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
text: Nirɔ-i kii teen Yennu yada, li yɔɔ kan fit penn ŋɔɔ Yennu par ni, kimaan wunba kur baat Yennu boor ni, li kpaa talas ki li yɔɔ-n teen yada nan Yennu set be ki teen binba loonɔ na nyɔɔt.

ref: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
text: Nowa yada paake din te ki u gbat Yennu kpaanii, barii nba ki u daa din kan fit lar nan u nunbinn na po. U din sak Yennu mɔb ki kpaa ŋarungbeŋŋir ki ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb kɔɔ leŋ ki tinna. Ŋanne din wann nan tingbouŋ na niib mɔk biit; ŋaan ki u yada maŋ paak ki Yennu din purɔ yentinɔ.

ref: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
text: Yada paake din te ki Abraham sak Yennu yiinii ki saan tiŋ nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki yaa a u sa turɔ na ni. U din nyik u mɔŋ dooe ŋaan ki ki mi wa saa siami.

ref: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
text: U yada paake ki u din daa tee saamɔ tiŋ nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki yaa a u sa turɔ na ni. U din be bonkobgbant lanbont nie; u waas Aisak nan Jakɔb nba din tan gaar Yennu mɔsonn na mun din be li biaŋe.

ref: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
text: Kimaan Abraham din guu a wun tan kɔɔ Yennu nba din bɔɔn doo nba ki maa na, ki li doo tee linba sii yɔɔ ki be.

ref: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
text: U ŋaapoo Sara mun din kpeta ki ji kan fit mari, ŋaan ŋɔɔ Sara yada paake ki u din tan soor poor ki mar, kimaan u mi nan Yennu nba senn mɔsonn na tee barmɔnii daanɔe.

ref: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Li paak, Abraham nba din tee nirɔ nba ji tan ki fɔk nan kuun na, ŋaan u kuukɔɔ yaaboonae tan yab kaman nan ŋmaabira nba yab biaŋinba na, ki bia yab kaman nan mɔkgbeŋŋir tanbiinii nba yab ki kaa kann biaŋinba na.

ref: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
text: Niib nba na kur din tuu tan kpo nan bi yada ŋaan daa ki gaar Yennu nba din senn mɔsonn linba a u sa turib na, ŋaan bi yada maŋ paak, bi din dɔŋ lar banfɔkirewa, kimaan bi din bann ki sak nan bi tee saamme ki tee nitintaaka tingbouŋ na ni.

ref: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
text: Niib-i yeen linba na, li set fiit ki want nan bi lek daa gorii bi tiauŋ digbann siar poe.

ref: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
text: Ki niib maŋ baka din kaa ba poŋ din nyii digbansiar nba na po kaa. Bi-i diŋŋi biar dukii li po, bi din bo sa fit ŋmata.

ref: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
text: Ŋaan bi baka din be Yendɔuŋ ni digbann nba chee na poe. Ki li paak Yennu mɔk yuugontir nan bin yiinɔ bi Yennu, kimaan u poŋ din ŋamm dosaue ki guumm.

ref: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
text: Ki Yennu din poŋ yetɔ a, “Aisak nie ki a tan sa la yaaboona nba ki n din senn mɔsonn a n sa tura na.”

ref: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
text: Ki Abraham ji din bann nan Yennu fit fiint niib kuun ni; ki li set din tee kaman nan Abraham din gaar Aisak kuun nie.

ref: Isaac had faith, and he promised blessings to Jacob and Esau.
text: Aisak yada paake din te ki u senn mɔsonŋann nba yaa lin tan baar Jakɔb nan Esɔ paak na.

ref: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
text: Yoo nba ki Jakɔb din yaa wun kpo na, u yada paake din te ki u senn mɔsonŋann Josef bonjai na paak, ki din tan diin u patu ki jiant Yennu.

ref: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
text: Josef manfoor nba din tan yaa lin gbenn yoonba na, u yada paake ki u din pak Israel teeb nyinu po Ijipt tiŋ ni, ŋaan din wannib ba tan sa teen u kpaba biaŋinba.

ref: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
text: Moses baa nan u naa nba din marɔ yoonba ki la ki u fan bonchiann na, bi yada paak ki bi din bɔrɔ ki tan jaŋ ŋmaarii ŋanntaa, ki din ki jee bat sennu na.

ref: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
text: Moses nba din tan kpaat yoonba, u yada paake ki u din ki sak bii yiu Bat Faro na bipoo bija.

ref: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
text: U din sak nan wun di fara nan Yennu niibe ŋaan ki ki sak toonbiit parpeenn nba tee waaminna na.

ref: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
text: U din dukin a bi-i sukiiɔ Masia paak, li mɔk nyɔɔt bonchiann ki chee Ijipt tiŋ ni mɔkint kura, kimaan u ninbinn din kpaa nyɔɔt nba baat na paake.

ref: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
text: Ŋɔɔ Moses yada paake din te ki u nyii Ijipt ki ki jee bat na wutoor jaŋmaanii, ŋaan lek saa tɔɔnn kaman nan u la Yennu nba ki sɔɔ ki laatɔ nan ninbinn na.

ref: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
text: U yada paake din te ki u din piin yukitgar jaamm, ki din senn sennu a bin mii sɔn bi ŋei tammɔi paak, ki kuun malaka na tan daa kpi Israel teeb bijakpera.

ref: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
text: Israel teeb yada paake ki mɔkgbenmuŋ na din bɔkit ki teen tinkooŋ ki turib ki bi poot, ŋaan ki Ijipt teeb mun din yabir bi mɔŋ a bin poot, ki mɔkir maŋ nyun na jiin mubin ki diib.

ref: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
text: Israel teeb nba din tan baar Jeriko yoonba, bi yada paake ki bi din somm lint li joona daa ŋannlore, ki li baa.

ref: Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
text: Poochonchonn nba ki bi yiu Rahab na yada paake din te ki Israel teeb din ki kpiiu, ŋaan kpii binba din ki gbat Yennu mɔb na, kimaan Israel teeb niib nba din baar a bin diis doo maŋ ni na, u din gaarib saauŋ.

ref: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
text: N bia beti siab labaar pukinii? Yoo-i bo-i yab, n bo sa beti Gideon labaar, nan Barak labaar, nan Samson labaar, nan Jefta labaar, nan David labaar, nan Samuel labaar, nan Yennu sɔkiniinba labaawa.

ref: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
text: Bi yada paak ki bi siab din kɔn ki nyann tinii, ki siab mun tun linba tee fanu, ki siab mun gaar Yennu mɔsona, ki siab soor yambɔra mɔi,

ref: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
text: ki siab mun kpeen mupeuŋ, ki siab tin nyii jukbanjai kuun ni. Ki siab mun din tee tariie ŋaan din tan kpant paarib, ki siab mun din took tɔb nan paŋ ki te ki lanjimanbasaamm tin chiar.

ref: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
text: Ki poosiab din la bi nikpiimm nba din kpo na fiir kuun ni. Ki siab nba din teen Yennu yada paak, ki niib boob ki kpiib, ki bi ki yêt Yennu weiu a bin nyi kuun maŋ ni, kimaan bi loon bin fiir kuun ni ki la manfoor nba chee.

ref: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
text: Ki niib din daan siab nyatuk, ki bia boob kpaasir; ki bi baan bi siab nan jarit, ki jiib saan kɔɔmm dansarii ni.

ref: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
text: Bi din jaat bi siab tanae, ki ŋma bi siab ki poriib; ki bi jikit jukbanjai ki kpi bi siab; ki leeb din lia pegbant, ki leeb bia lia bugbant ki lin nan namu, ki niib mukisib ki dintib fara biak.

ref: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
text: Tingbouŋ na teeb din ki jaŋ nan bii be nammi. Ki bi din lin yeek kunkoot paak nan jɔɔt ni, ki lin dɔɔ tanfiat nan fɔrii ni.

ref: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
text: Binba na kur yada paake ki Yennu din pakib, ŋaan ki bi din ki gaar linba ki u din senn mɔsonn a u sa turib na,

ref: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
text: kimaan ti paake ki u din mantik dukii bonsiar nba chee na po, ŋanne tee a u sa te ki timm nan ŋamm-n tan lakin tee binba be fanu nan barmɔnii.

ref: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
text: Binba na kur tee siaradamme ki lintit. Li paak, yi ŋaan ki tin yabir ki fok lu linba kur sa gɔɔrit Masia sɔnu ni, nan biit nba sa soorit; yi ŋaan man ki tii di sukuru ki gbaa gbaar nba yaa lin baarit na.

ref: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
text: Ŋaant ki tin kpan kpaa ti ninbina Yisa paak; ŋɔɔe ki ti yada gaa u paak li pinpiik niwa nan li joontu ni na. Dapunpunn na din ki te ki u nyik u yabiru, kimaan parpeenn nba din baat u paak na paak, u din ki dukin ki li mɔkɔ fei nan wun kpo li dapunpunn paaki, ŋanne te ki u ji kar Yennu naangbouŋ paak u nuudiitu po mɔtana.

ref: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
text: Yin lek bikin wa din gaar naŋ biaŋinba biitdamm boor ni ki jii u tiauŋ. Li paak, i daa te ki i gbanu baŋŋit ki i nyikit yabiru.

ref: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
text: Kimaan ya yabii ki kɔn nan biit na ŋaan i sɔɔ daa ki mi yabir teee ki li saa baar kuumi.

ref: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
text: I ki tian Yennu nba kpaani kaman nan baa nba tuu kpaan u waas biaŋinba na-aa? Li sɔb a “N bik, ti Yomdaanɔ-i kpaana kpaanii yoonba, fan gbiint, li-i tee ki u dat a tubir, fa daa nyikit a yabiru,

ref: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
text: kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ kpaan wunba ki u loonɔe, ki wa gaar wunba ki teenɔ u bik na, li yɔɔe ki u daar u tubir.”

ref: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
text: Yabit i fardinu ni kaman nan bik nba tuu di u baa tubdatu fara biaŋinba na. Yennue teeni tubdatu ya tee u waas na paak, kimaan bik kaa ki ki laat tubdatu u baa boor ni.

ref: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
