text: Li-i tee ki u ki dat i tuba kaman nan wa tee u waas kur biaŋinba na, ŋann li want nan i tee kpenkiŋmiae, i ki tee u waas kaa.

ref: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
text: Ti baanba nba be tingbouŋ na ni ki tuu daar ti tuba, ki ti teemm nibaakir. Li paak, li ŋan ki ti mantik sikin ti mɔŋ ki tur Yennu wunba tee sei Baa nae, a tin la manfoor.

ref: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
text: Ti baanba nba be tingbouŋ na ni jikit yoo waaminna ki daar ti tuba ba loon biaŋinbae, ŋaan Yennu ŋanin daar ti tuba ti mɔŋ sommir paake, a ti binbeŋ-n ŋamm kii yeen kaman nan ŋɔɔ Yennu binbeŋ na.

ref: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
text: Tubdatu yoo tone, li ki mɔk parpeenni, ŋaan li poorpoewa ki binba dii tubdatu fara na ji tuu la nyɔɔt nba tee parmaasir nan yentinu binbeŋ. Kpaanii

ref: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
text: Li paak, i kakit man i dunkpeena ni, ki chekin i nii nba baŋ na man,

ref: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
text: kii tɔɔk sɔnu nba took, ki wunba ki u taa wɔb-n la laafia ŋaan daa kpeti.

ref: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
text: Yabit kii mɔk parmaasir i leeb sinsuuk ni, kii mɔk binbenyeeŋ, kimaan sɔɔ-i kii mɔk ŋann, li yɔɔ tan kan la ti Yomdaanɔ.

ref: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
text: Guut i mɔŋ man ki tan daa kɔŋŋit Yennu ninbatinu na. I sɔɔ daa mɔk tonu u par ni, ki u tonu na-n baar nan parbiir leeb paak, ki biir niburchiauŋi.

ref: Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal.
text: I sɔɔ daa tuun bonchonchonn toona amii ki gorii Yennu barii yann kaman nan Esɔ wunba din jii u saakatuk ki daa jeet ki dii taar yomm na.

ref: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
text: I mi nan li poorpo u din tan loon wun gaar u baa mɔsonŋann ki mɔ nan ninnyuut ŋaan la yêtu, kimaan u ji din ki la sɔnu nba ki u sa teen ki lebit wa poŋ din biir linba na.

ref: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
text: Tɔn, yimm ki baar bonsiar nba ki i sa fit siir kaman nan Israel teeb nba din baar Sainai jɔɔr boor na kaa, siaminba ki mupeuŋ din di ki bunbɔnn bɔnn, ki wounpaaruk daa,

ref: or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop,
text: ki kikaak bui, ki kunkɔr pak labaar. Ba din gbat li kunkɔr yoonba bi din jak baruŋo ki ji ki loon li kunkɔr-n pak pukini.

ref: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
text: Kimaan sennu nba ki bi gbat na, paak bi niiwa. Li din yet a, “Bonkobsiɔk gbaa-i lekii sii jɔɔr maŋ, bin jaatɔ tana ki kpiu.”

ref: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
text: Li boor ni din mɔk jaŋmaansooruk bonchiann, ki Moses din yet a “Jaŋmaanii mɔkin, ki n gbanu jekir.”

ref: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
text: Ŋaan yimm ŋanin baar jɔɔr nba yaa sann yi Sion, linba tee manfodaanɔ Yennu doo nba tee Yendɔuŋ ni Jerusalem, siaminba ki malakanba tusa tusa lakin nan parpeenn.

ref: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
text: Yimm tan baar niib nba ki Yennu teemm u waas na boore, ki bi sana sɔb Yendɔuŋ nie. I baar Yennu nba tee nisaarik kur barbuurɔ na boor ni, nan popeendamm nba ki Yennu te ki bi sei be fanu na boor nie.

ref: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
text: I baar Yisa, wunba tee sindantinnɔ ki baar nan Yennu mɔlorpaann na boore. I baar Yisa boor, ki u sɔn nba din nyii u ni na want barii nba ŋan ki gar Abel sɔn.

ref: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
text: Li paak, i mi man ki i yêt wunba kpaani na mɔb, kimaan Israel teeb nba din yêt wunba din kpaamm tingbouŋ na ni mɔb na, u din tan ki nyik bi tubdatu; ki ti-i yêt u kpaanii nba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni na, ti tan sa teen nlee ki nyi tubdatu ni?

ref: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
text: Li yoo na, u kunkɔr din nik tingbouŋ nae, ŋaan mɔtana u senn mɔsonn ki yet a “N ji kan nik tingbouŋ na kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan n ji tan sa nik tingbouŋ na nan sanpaapoewa mun.”

ref: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
text: Mɔbona nba yet a, “N ji tan” na, li fiit want nan bonnankaa kur tan sa nik ki yaat bot li binbeboore, ŋaan ki linba tan kan fit nik na ŋanin-n tan sa biar li boae.

ref: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
text: Li paak, ta gaar naan nba kan mi ki nik na paak, yi ŋaan ki tii teen Yennu niipoouk man, ki jiantɔ nan nibaakir nan jaŋmaanii ki u numm-n gboo,

ref: Our God is like a destructive fire!
text: kimaan Yennu maŋ tee kaman nan mudikau nee.

ref: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
text: Ya tee Masia niib na paak, i loon i leeb man yoo kur.

ref: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
text: I daa tammit saamm gaaru po, kimaan nisiab din tan gaar malakanba saauŋ, ŋaan ki mi a bi tee malakanbae.

ref: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
text: Yii tian nisiab nba be dansarii ni na po kaman nan yimm nan ŋamme lakin be li ni; kii tian nisiab nba laat muksuk na po, kimaan i mun daa be tingbouŋ na ni nan i gbanante.

ref: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
text: Yii mɔk nibaakir nan i leeb nan i pookɔɔnt nan i jakunt ni ki daa chommi, kimaan Yennu tan sa dat bonchonchona nan joonbaarit nan sapaanwɔbira nan naasinwɔbira tuba.

ref: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
text: Yi nyinn i niauŋ nan liklommu, ŋaan te ki i numm-n gboo nan ya mɔk linba; kimaan Yennu yet a “N kan mi ki nyikani, n bia kan mi ki ŋaanani.”

ref: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
text: Li paak, ŋaant ki tii mɔk parcheenn ki yet a “Ti Yomdaanɔ tee n sommtɔɔ, n kan tin jaŋmaanii, nisaarik sa teenin bee?”

ref: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
text: I tian man i yudamm nba din tuu piak Yennu maan ki teeni na po; ki yii dukii bi binbeŋ nyɔɔt nba tee biaŋinba ki yii tokii bi yada.

ref: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
text: I daa ŋaant ki wannsaanbooru booru-n te ki i boti, ki i tan sa mann tut sɔnŋanu maŋ. Li ŋan ki Yennu sommir-n tur nirɔ paŋ u par ni; jeet sennii weiu kan turɔ paŋi. Nisiab nba dia jeet sennii poŋ ki laat sommsiar li ni.

ref: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
text: Tɔn, binba mann mannu Juu teeb Yenjiantu boor ni na ki mɔk yaak a bii di linba be ti mannu binbintir na ni.

ref: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
text: Ki Juu teeb manteeb yudaanɔ tuu kɔɔ nan bonkobit sɔn Yennu diiuk ni, boor nba tee Yennu yar nan barmɔnii na ni, ki mann marouŋ biit paak, ŋaan joo bonkobit maŋ nant muu bi doo na kpiŋ po.

ref: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
text: Li paak, ki Yisa mun din kpo doo na kpiŋ po, a wun nyinn niib biit ni, u mɔŋ sɔn paak.

ref: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
text: Li paak, ŋaant ki tin nyi ki saan u boor ni man, ki sak feidinu u paak.

ref: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
text: Kimaan doo nba sa biar kaa tingbouŋ na ni; dosau nba baat nee ki ti guur.

ref: Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus.
text: Li paak yi ŋaan ki tii dontir Yennu sann yoo kur Yisa sann ni; paku nba nyi ti mɔi ni nae tee mannu ki turɔ.

ref: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: I daa nyikit toonŋana tumu, kii chentir ya mɔk linba nan i leeb. Li mannboorue pent Yennu par ni.

ref: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
text: Yii saak i yudamm mɔi ki sikin i mɔŋ ki turib, kimaan bi baka be i benu ni, kimaan bi tan sa wann Yennu bi toona nba ki bi din tuun. Ŋaant ki bin tan wannɔ nan parpeenn, ŋaan tan daa wantɔ nan parbiiri. Nna tan kii mɔk nyɔɔt ki turiini.

ref: Pray for us. Our consciences are clear, and we always try to live right.
text: I mei Yennu man ti paak. Ti ki mɔk dudukbisiat ti toona ni, kimaan ti loon tin tun linba tooke yoo kur.

ref: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
text: Ŋaan n mantik barimiie bonchiann, i mei Yennu man ki turin ki wun te ki n jen i boor ni mɔtana. Yenmiarjoontik labaar

ref: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
text: Yennu nba teent parmaasir nae din fiin ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa kuun ni. Ŋɔɔ Yisae tee ti guutɔjaann na, kimaan u din kpo, ki u sɔn ŋamm Yennu mɔlor nba sii see yoo nba kaa gbennu;

ref: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
text: li Yennu-n ŋamm i binbeŋ fanu a yii tuun u loomm, kii tuun toonŋana kur; Yisa Masia paak wun te ki tii tee binba sii pent u par. Ŋɔɔe yen dontir nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm. Barjoontii labaar

ref: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
text: N naa waas, n barimiie, yi di sukuru ki gbiint kpaanii na; kimaan ma sɔb gbouŋ nba turi na ki fɔki.

ref: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
text: N loon ki i bann nan bi nyinn ti naa bik Timoti dansarik niwa. Li-i tee ki u sa baar n boor ni mɔtana, i sa la min nan ŋɔɔ.

ref: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
text: Yi foont i yudamm kura nan Yennu niib na kura ki turit. Ti naa waas nba nyii Itali tiŋ ni na foonti.

ref: I pray that God will be kind to all of you!
text: Yennu sommir-ii be i kura ni.

ref: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
text: Min Jems, wunba tee Yennu toontunnɔ ki bia tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia toontunnɔ, n foont Israel nibooru piik nan bannlee nba yat ki be tingbouŋ na kur ni. Yada nan yan labaar

ref: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
text: N naa waas, yii mɔk man parpeenn yoo nba ki i di fara booru booru na,

ref: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
text: Ŋaant man kii ŋamm kii mɔk gbankur fanu nan li joontu, ki i tan kan kɔŋ binbenŋaŋi.

ref: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
text: Li-i tee ki i sɔɔ pɔt yan, wun miar Yennu wunba pi sɔɔ kur piinii nan burchint, ki ki dint sɔɔ fei na, ki u sa turɔwa.

ref: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
text: Fi-i mei Yennu, fan teen yada ki daa mɔk birsuku; ŋaan fin nba meiɔ nan birsuk, a tee kaman nan mɔkgbeŋŋir nyunpana nba ki wouŋ da ki li fi ki baa nee.

ref: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
text: Li ŋan ki ti naa waas nba tee tarii-ii mɔk yuugontir Yennu nba donnib na paak,

ref: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
text: ki ti naa waas nba mun tee mɔkita-ii munii mɔk yuugontir Yennu nba sikinib na paak, kimaan li kan yukiri ki bi sa gar kaman nan tipuuk nba tuu koor ki baa na;

ref: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
text: kimaan yennu-i nyii ki yekit tipuuk paak, puuk na tuu koore ki baa, ki li ŋant-n ji biir. Li mun bia tee nnee ki tur mɔkita; yoo nba ki bi dukii bi toona nba sa saan tɔɔnn biaŋinba na, li yooe bin kpo. Bikinu labaar

ref: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
text: Binba nyann bikinu na, parpeenn tee bi yare, kimaan Yennu sa turib nyɔɔt nba tee manfoor nba ki u din senn mɔsonn a u sa tur binba loonɔ na.

ref: Don't blame God when you are tempted! God cannot be tempted by evil, and he doesn't use evil to tempt others.
text: Sɔɔ-i la bikinu, u daa yaa a Yennue bikinɔ. Siar kaa ki sa fit bikin Yennu, Yennu mun ki bikii sɔɔ.

ref: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
text: Nirɔ-i loon wun tun u mɔŋ nianbiiuk toona, nnee tee bikinu.

ref: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
text: Nianbiiuk-i soor nirɔ, li sa baar nan toonbiite, ki toonbiit joontu tee kuume.

ref: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
text: N naa walonkai, i daa te ki siar kpanniini.

ref: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
text: Bonŋann kur nyii sanpaapoe ti Baa Yennu boor, wunba din nan sanpaapo yentu kura. Ŋɔɔ ŋanin ki lebitir kaman nan sanpaapo yentu nba tuu lebitir biaŋinba na; u bia daa tee ŋɔɔ Yennue.

ref: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
text: U mɔŋ loommo ki u teent u waas, u maan nba tee barmɔnii na paak, a tii chee u bonninaant kura.

ref: My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry.
text: N naa waas, n loon yin bann nee; yii gbiintir yian, ŋaan kii mi biaŋinba ki i piak, ki bia daa dont wutoor yian yiami,

ref: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
text: Wunba kur gbat maan maŋ, ŋaan ki ki tuun biaŋinba ki li wanni, u tee kaman nan nirɔ nba tuu got ki la u mɔŋ mianu ni nee;

ref: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
text: Ŋaan wunba dukii sennŋann nba sa fit nyint niib biit daabsin ni, ŋaan ki tammir, ŋaan mɔk gbankur ki tuun biaŋinba ki li wann na, parpeenn sii tee li daanɔ yare, kimaan u tumu na paak.

ref: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
text: Wunba mi nan u tee Yisaweirɔ ŋaan ki ki so u mɔbi, li daanɔ kpann u mɔŋe. Li want nan u waanu na tee yanne.

ref: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
text: Wunba sommit kpeebii nan pakɔi bi fara ni, ki bia nyinn u mɔŋ tingbouŋ na biit ni, ŋɔɔe set tee Yisaweirɔ nan barmɔnii, ki ki mɔk biit ti Baa Yennu boori.

ref: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
text: N naa waas, ya teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia wunba tee paŋ daanɔ na yada biaŋinba, i daa mɔk ningannii.

ref: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
text: Li-i tee ki mɔkitɔɔ wunba lia taŋant ki pir sarinbaŋ kɔɔ i Yenjiantu diiuk ni, ki nandaanɔ nba lia tachiat mun kɔɔ,

ref: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
text: ki i tur mɔkitɔɔ na nibaakir, ki betɔ a wun kar tɔɔnn po, ŋaan bet nandaanɔ na a wuu see amii wun kar tiŋ ni,

ref: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
text: n naa waas, li-i tee ki i tuun nna, i tee niib nba mɔk ningannii nan i leeb, ki bia mɔk dudukbiite.

ref: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
text: N naa walonkai, gbiintir man, Yennue gann binba tee nandamm tingbouŋ na niib boor, a bin teen mɔkita u boor, kimaan bi teenɔ yada, ki teen u waas nba sa di naan nba ki u din senn mɔsonn a u sa tur binba loonɔ na.

ref: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
text: Ŋaan yimme yisin nandamm. Mɔkita nee mukisiri ki so-i ki saa nani buut boor. Nna kaa-aa?

ref: Aren't they the ones who make fun of your Lord?
text: Ŋamme bia piak biir Yisa Masia sanŋann nba ki Yennu turi na.

ref: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “I loon a lɔɔ kaman nan fa loon a mɔŋ biaŋinba na.” Li-i tee ki i waa Yennu naan sennu maŋ, li ŋan,

ref: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
text: ŋaan li-i tee ki i mɔk ningannii nan leeb, li tee biite, ki sennu maŋ fiitir ki want nan i set tee sennu biir damme.

ref: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
text: Wunba sak ki wei sennu na kur, ŋaan tan biir sennii maŋ yenn, li want nan u biir sennii na kure.

ref: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
text: Sennii maŋ nɔɔk ni Yennu nba yet a “Daa chomm na,” ŋɔɔe bia yet a “Daa kpi nirɔ.” Li paak, li-i tee ki a ki chomm, ŋaan kpi, a biir sennii maŋ kura.

ref: Speak and act like people who will be judged by the law that sets us free.
text: Yii piak ŋaan kii tuun kii mi nan Yennu tan sa jii sennu nba nyint niib biit daabsin ni nee, ki bu i buut.

ref: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
text: Binba ki tin bi leeb ninbaauk, Yennu sa bu bi buut ki kan tinib ninbaauku; ŋaan ki ninbatinue nyant tubdatu. Yada nan toona labaar

ref: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
text: N naa waas, daanɔ nba yaa a u teen Yisa Masia yada, ŋaan ji ki tuun popeenn toona, li mɔk nyɔɔtii? U yada maŋ booru sa fit tinnɔwa-aa?

ref: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
text: Li-i tee ki a naa bik be ki ki laat gbab, ki bia ki laat di,

ref: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
text: ki a ji beerɔ a “N naa bik, a kun maanua, kii gbab, kii bia di,” ŋaan kan sommɔ nan siari, li mɔk nyɔɔtii?

ref: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
text: Yada kuukɔɔ-i be, ŋaan toonŋana kaa, yada maŋ tee yadakpeeuŋo.

ref: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
text: Li-i tee ki nirɔ yet a “Sɔɔe mɔk yada, ki lɔɔ mun tuun toonŋana,” n mun sa jiin a “Li-i tee ki a ki tuun toonŋana, lannpoe want nan a set mɔk yada? Ŋaan n toonŋana nee wanta nan min set mɔk yada.”

ref: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
text: A teen yada nan Yen-yenɔkɔɔe be-ee? Li ŋan. Narinbiit mun teen yada nan Yen-yenɔkɔɔe be, ki bia jekir nan jaŋmaanii.

ref: Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless?
text: Fin jatuk na, li-i tee ki a mɔk yada ŋaan toonŋana kaa, li tee yanne; a loon ki a bann na-aa?

ref: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
text: Na a lawa-aa? Wa teen Yennu mɔmaan yada paake ki u din tun toonn maŋ; toonn maŋe mun din wann nan u teen Yennu yada nan barmɔnii.

ref: This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
text: Ki Yennu mɔmaan nba sɔb u gbouŋ ni nee din tan teen mɔmɔmm a “Abraham din teen Yennu yada, li paak, ki Yennu yaa a u tee niŋanɔ u boor.” Li paak, ki bi din yiu Yennu yɔɔk.

ref: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
text: Na a lawa-aa? Nirɔ toonŋana paake ki Yennu sa yet a li daanɔ tee niŋanɔ u boor; ki li ki tee u yada kuukɔɔ paaki.

ref: Anyone who doesn't breathe is dead, and faith that doesn't do anything is just as dead!
text: Li paak, gbanant nba kaa seek, li tee gbanankpeeuŋo, yada-i munii be ki toonŋana kaa, li mun tee yadakpeeuŋo.

ref: My friends, we should not all try to become teachers. In fact, teachers will be judged more strictly than others.
text: N naa waas, i nisiab bonchiann daa loon bin teen wannteebi, kimaan i poŋ mi nan binba tee wannteeb na, bi buut tan sii paar ki gar binba ki tee wannteebi.

ref: All of us do many wrong things. But if you can control your tongue, you are mature and able to control your whole body.
text: Ti kur tun linba ki tokin bonchiann, ŋaan wunba ki piak mabiit, li daanɔ set tee niŋanɔe ki sa fit soor u gbanant kura.

ref: By putting a bit into the mouth of a horse, we can turn the horse in different directions.
text: Ti-i jii taangarika ki wukimm a bii tuun ta loon biaŋinba, bi gbanant paŋ kur be ti nuu nie.

ref: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
text: Bia dukint ŋarint labaar po; bi yab bonchiann, ŋaan wounpaaruku da, bi saae, ŋaan dabik nnee ki kantɔɔ na dia ki kann ki bi saa wa loon siaminba.

ref: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
text: Jerinn mun tee bonbik nnee, ŋaan bia mɔk yaak bonchiann. Gotir, mupɔɔrik nnee tuu di fɔgbeŋŋir.

ref: The tongue is like a spark. It is an evil power that dirties the rest of the body and sets a person's entire life on fire with flames that come from hell itself.
text: Jerinn tee kaman nan muu nee; li be ti gbanant ni ki mɔk biitbooru booru kura, ki sa fit biir ti gbanant kur ki bia di nifosaarik binbeŋ kaman nan muu na; ki li muu nyii Sintaanii boore.

ref: All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles, and sea creatures can be tamed and have been tamed.
text: Nisaarike mɔk bonkobit nan nɔɔnii nan wai nan nyun ni bona kur yaak,

ref: But our tongues get out of control. They are restless and evil, and always spreading deadly poison.
text: ŋaan nisaarik kan fit soor jerinn; li mɔk biite, ki bia gbee nan lɔbii nba baat nan kuun, ki bia ki foi.

ref: My dear friends, with our tongues we speak both praises and curses. We praise our Lord and Father, and we curse people who were created to be like God, and this isn't right.
text: Mɔb nba ki maŋamm nyi na ni, li mɔb maŋe ki mabiit mun nyi. N naa waas, li-i tee nnaie, li ki ŋani.

ref: Can clean water and dirty water both flow from the same spring?
text: Bunbunn nba bunii nyunmamm nee bia bunii nyuntouŋuu?

ref: Can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine produce figs? Does fresh water come from a well full of salt water? Wisdom from Above
text: Ki kinkaŋ sa fit lon saanawa-aa? Amii gaak sa fit lon kinkaŋ lɔɔnawa-aa? Nnee mun, nyuntomm kan fit kpant nyunmammi. Yan nba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni labaar

ref: Are any of you wise or sensible? Then show it by living right and by being humble and wise in everything you do.
text: Ŋmee mɔk yan nan bannu i sinsuuk ni? Sɔɔ-i be, wuu be fanu ki sikin u mɔŋ kii tuun toonŋana, ki li sa wann nan u set mɔk yan.

ref: But if your heart is full of bitter jealousy and selfishness, don't brag or lie to cover up the truth.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki i mɔk funfunn nan dontinmɔŋ i para ni, i daa nyu bara, ki daa fa faak, ki daa yêt barmɔnii.

ref: This kind of wisdom doesn't come from above. It is earthly and selfish and comes from the devil himself.
text: Li yan ki nyi Yennu boor ni kaa, li tee tingbouŋ na nan gbanant loomm nan Sintaanii yare,

ref: Whenever people are jealous or selfish, they cause trouble and do all sorts of cruel things.
text: kimaan funfunn nan dontinmɔŋ nba be sian, daamuk nan biitbooru booru kur be leŋe.

ref: When peacemakers plant seeds of peace, they will harvest justice.
text: Binba mɔk parmaasir ki te ki parmaasir-n baar niib sinsuuk ni na, ŋamme te ki yentinu be.

ref: Why do you fight and argue with each other? Isn't it because you are full of selfish desires that fight to control your body?
text: Bee te ki i kɔn ki nɔi nan leebi? Li tee i nianbiiuk nba be i para ni ki kɔne.

ref: You want something you don't have, and you will do anything to get it. You will even kill! But you still cannot get what you want, and you won't get it by fighting and arguing. You should pray for it.
text: I loon bonsia, ŋaan kan la, ki ji tee siir nan nikpinu. I mɔk funfunn i leeb bona paak, ŋaan kan la, li paak, ki kunkɔna nan mɔniat be i sinsuuk ni. I ki mɔk ya loon linba na, kimaan i ki mei Yennu li po.

ref: Yet even when you do pray, your prayers are not answered, because you pray just for selfish reasons.
text: I mei Yennu ki ki laat kimaan i meiɔ ki loon i mɔŋ nianbiiuk yare.

ref: You people aren't faithful to God! Don't you know if you love the world, you are God's enemies? And if you decide to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.
text: I nyik Yennuwa, ki waa bonliae. Li-i tee ki i tee tingbouŋ na yɔɔsinbaie, i tee Yennu dataie; I ki mi nnee-ee? Wunba loon wuu tee tingbouŋ na yɔɔk, li yɔɔ teen u mɔŋ Yennu dataake.

ref: Do you doubt the Scriptures that say, “God truly cares about the Spirit he has put in us”?
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “U loon Seek nba ki u teent na bonchiann.”

ref: In fact, God treats us with even greater kindness, just as the Scriptures say, “God opposes everyone who is proud, but he blesses all who are humble with undeserved grace.”
text: Ŋaan u bia teent sommir bonchiann. Li paak, li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yennu kɔn nan karinbaanii damme, ŋaan sommit binba sikin bi mɔŋ.”

ref: Surrender to God! Resist the devil, and he will run from you.
text: Li paak, yii saak Yennu mɔb man, ŋaan yêt Sintaanii, ki u sa chiar ŋaan nyiki.

ref: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Clean up your lives, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you people who can't make up your mind.
text: Chatir nakin Yennu boor man, ki u mun sa nakini. Yimm biitdamm, wuut i nii man; yimm dudukit ŋannlelee damm, ŋammit i para man.

ref: Be sad and sorry and weep. Stop laughing and start crying. Be gloomy instead of glad.
text: Yii mɔk parbiir ki bui nan ninnyuut i biit paak; i daa ji laa, yii mɔ man; ki ŋaan ki i parpeenn-n kpint ki teen parbiir.

ref: Be humble in the Lord's presence, and he will honor you. Saying Cruel Things about Others
text: Sikint i mɔŋ ti Yomdaanɔ tɔɔnn, ki u sa donni. Daa piak mabiit leeb paaki

ref: My friends, don't say cruel things about others! If you do, or if you condemn others, you are condemning God's Law. And if you condemn the Law, you put yourself above the Law and refuse to obey either it
text: N naa waas, i daa piak mabiit leeb paaki. Wunba piak mabiit u naa bik paak ki biirɔ, li tee Yennu sennue ki u piak mabiit li paak ki biir. Li-i tee ki a biir sennu na, a ki tee wunba waa-r kaa, ŋaan a tee li biirtɔɔe.

ref: or God who gave it. God is our judge, and he can save or destroy us. What right do you have to condemn anyone? Warning against Bragging
text: Yennu kuukɔɔe mɔk yaak ki sa fit senn sennu, ki bia sa fit bu ti buut, ki bia mɔk paŋ nba sa fit tinn niib, ki bia sa fit biirib, ki ŋmee mun be ki mɔk yaak ki sa bu u lɔɔ buuti? Yennue mɔk yaak manfoor paak

ref: You should know better than to say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to the city. We will do business there for a year and make a lot of money!”
text: Gotir man, yimm nba tuu yeen a “Dinna amii wonn, ti sa saan digbann nawa, ki sa di binn kii kpentir kpinkpouŋ ki la nyɔɔt bonchiann,”

ref: What do you know about tomorrow? How can you be so sure about your life? It is nothing more than mist that appears for only a little while before it disappears.
text: i ki mi linba sa baar wonni. I manfoor tee bee? Li tee kaman nan wunn nba tuu nyi sanyiɔk ni, ŋaan li-i yann lin ŋmat kɔɔ nee.

ref: You should say, “If the Lord lets us live, we will do these things.”
text: Li paak, li ŋan tin tuu yet a “Li-i tee Yennu loommie, ti sii mɔk manfoor, ki tun nna nan nnee.”

ref: Yet you are stupid enough to brag, and it is wrong to be so proud.
text: Ŋaan i mɔk karinbaanii ki nyu bara. Ya nyu bara biaŋinba na, li ki ŋani.

ref: If you don't do what you know is right, you have sinned.
text: Li paak, wunba mi toonŋana tumu, ŋaan ki tuumi, li daanɔ mɔk biit.

ref: You rich people should cry and weep! Terrible things are going to happen to you.
text: Yimm gordamm, gbiintir man! yii mɔ man kii fabin fara nba baat i paak na.

ref: Your treasures have already rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
text: I mɔkint na kur biir, ki wobii mɔɔ i tiata.

ref: Your money has rusted, and the rust will be evidence against you, as it burns your body like fire. Yet you keep on storing up wealth in these last days.
text: I likirii mun biira, ki li sa wann nan i mɔk biite, ki li sa biir i gbanant kaman nan muu nba di biaŋinba na. I senn i mɔkint nee ki guu i buut daar na.

ref: You refused to pay the people who worked in your fields, and now their unpaid wages are shouting out against you. The Lord All-Powerful has surely heard the cries of the workers who harvested your crops.
text: I ŋmab i toontunna binba jaan i dii i kpaant ni na, ŋaan ki pabi, ki bi mɔ; ki bi mɔnii na kɔɔ yabint Yennu tuba ni.

ref: While here on earth, you have thought only of filling your own stomachs and having a good time. But now you are like fat cattle on their way to be butchered.
text: Ya be tingbouŋ na ni, i be i yama ni, ki waa i gbanant niauŋ; i dint i mɔŋe, ŋaan i lek be kaman nan naajakpamma nba ki bi sa kpiu na.

ref: You have condemned and murdered innocent people, who couldn't even fight back. Be Patient and Kind
text: I din piak ki biir popeendamme ki kpib, ŋaan bi ki kɔn nani. Sukuru labaar

ref: My friends, be patient until the Lord returns. Think of farmers who wait patiently for the autumn and spring rains to make their valuable crops grow.
text: N naa waas, yii mɔk gbankur man kii guu ti Yomdaanɔ baaru daar maŋ. Gotir man kpaarɔ nba tuu mɔk gbankur ki guu kpafusaak mɔkmɔk nan sajoontik ki u jeet-n ji tan nan bonŋann.

ref: Be patient like those farmers and don't give up. The Lord will soon be here!
text: Li paak, i mun-ii mɔk gbankur, ki chee i para kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ baaru daar per na.

ref: Don't grumble about each other or you will be judged, and the judge is right outside the door.
text: N naa waas, i daa mɔk bunburimii i leeb paaki, ŋaan li-i tee ki i tuun nna, Yennu sa bu i buut. Gotir man, barbuurɔ na nakin ki see i tammɔi ni.

ref: My friends, follow the example of the prophets who spoke for the Lord. They were patient, even when they had to suffer.
text: N naa waas, ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu sɔkiniinba nba din pak u sann ni na, gotir man ba din laat fara ki mɔk sukuru biaŋinba na, ki lii tee wannu ki turi.

ref: In fact, we praise the ones who endured the most. You remember how patient Job was and how the Lord finally helped him. The Lord did this because he is so merciful and kind.
text: Li nibooru nba mɔk sukuru na, ti yib parpeenn damme. I poŋ gbat Job nba din tan mɔk sukuru biaŋinba na, ki li poorpo ki Yennu din tan nyinnɔ u fara ni. Li want Yennu nba mɔk ninbatinu ki tinit ninbaauk biaŋinbae.

ref: My friends, above all else, don't take an oath. You must not swear by heaven or by earth or by anything else. “Yes” or “No” is all you need to say. If you say anything more, you will be condemned.
text: N naa waas, linba kpaa talase tee nee: i daa poor mɔpora. I daa yi sanpaapo amii tiŋ ni bonsiar sann ki poor mɔpora. Ŋaant ki i nn-ii tee i nn, ki i aaii-ii tee aaii, ki Yennu tan daa dar i tuba. Yenmiaru labaar

ref: If you are having trouble, you should pray. And if you are feeling good, you should sing praises.
text: Sɔɔ-i be i ni ki mɔk parbiir, wun miar Yennu. Wunba mun mɔk parpeenn, wun yin yanii kii piak Yennu.

ref: If you are sick, ask the church leaders to come and pray for you. Ask them to put olive oil on you in the name of the Lord.
text: Sɔɔ-i be i boor ki yiar, wun yiin Masiaweira saakab ki bin tan sɔɔrɔ kpan nan ti Yomdaanɔ Masia sann ni, ŋaan miar Yennu ki turɔ.

ref: If you have faith when you pray for sick people, they will get well. The Lord will heal them, and if they have sinned, he will forgive them.
text: Ki bi-i meiɔ nan yada, ki miaru maŋ sa tinnɔ, ki ti Yomdaanɔ sa te ki u paak; ki li-i tee ki u tun toonbiitie, Yennu sa nyik u biit maŋ ki chabɔ.

ref: If you have sinned, you should tell each other what you have done. Then you can pray for one another and be healed. The prayer of an innocent person is powerful, and it can help a lot.
text: Fiitir i toonbiit ki wann leeb, ki miar Yennu ki tur leeb, ki Yennu sa te ki i paak. Popeendaanɔ-i miar Yennu nan ninmɔnn, li mɔk paŋ bonchiann.

ref: Elijah was just as human as we are, and for three and a half years his prayers kept the rain from falling.
text: Elaija din tee nifosaarike kaman nan timm na, ŋaan din miar Yennu nan nunmɔnn a saak daa baa, ki saak set din ki baa ki saa jaŋ bina ŋanntaa nan ŋmaarii ŋannloob.

ref: But when he did pray for rain, it fell from the skies and made the crops grow.
text: Li poorpo ki u bia miar Yennu, ki saak ji baa, ki tiŋ maak, ki jeet nan fanu.

ref: My friends, if any followers have wandered away from the truth, you should try to lead them back.
text: N naa waas, sɔɔ-i kpet ki nyii mɔmɔmm sɔnu ni, ki sɔɔ jennɔ mɔmɔmm sɔnu maŋ ni,

ref: If you turn sinners from the wrong way, you will save them from death, and many of their sins will be forgiven.
text: i mi nan daanɔ nba nyinn biitdaanɔ u sɔnbiiuk na ni, li daanɔ nyinn u manfoor kuun nie, ki te ki Yennu nyik chab toonbiit bonchiann.

ref: From Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ. To God's people who are scattered like foreigners in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
text: Min Piita nba tee Yisa Masia toomii nee sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen Masiaweira nba yat ki be Pontus nan Galasia nan Kapadosia nan Asia nan Bitinia tinii ni ki tee saamm na.

ref: God the Father decided to choose you as his people, and his Spirit has made you holy. You have obeyed Jesus Christ and are sprinkled with his blood. I pray that God will be kind to you and will keep on giving you peace! A Real Reason for Hope
text: Ti Baa Yennu dɔŋ ki ganni ki mi nan yimm tan sa teen u niiba, ki u Seyeeŋ te ki i tee popeendamm, ki u ganni a yii saak Yisa Masia mɔb, ki u wuur i para nan u sɔn. Yennu-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir bonchiann. Barmɔnii dindann labaar

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God is so good, and by raising Jesus from death, he has given us new life and a hope that lives on.
text: Pakin man Yennu nba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Baa na, kimaan u tint ninbaauk bonchiann ki turit manfopaann Yisa Masia kuun fiiru paak. Linba nee te ki ti mɔk dindann nan ti tan sii mɔk manfoor,

ref: God has something stored up for you in heaven, where it will never decay or be ruined or disappear.
text: ki la linba tee Yennu waas yar, ki li sii be yoo nba kaa gbennu, ki kan biir, li fant bia kan siiri; ki li be Yendɔuŋ ni

ref: You have faith in God, whose power will protect you until the last day. Then he will save you, just as he has always planned to do.
text: ki guu yimm nba ki Yennu paŋ dia-i i yada paak, a yin la i tinnu nyɔɔt nba teen siir ki sa nyi paanu dajoontii na.

ref: On that day you will be glad, even if you have to go through many hard trials for a while.
text: Li paak, i mɔk parpeenn; li-i lekii tee a i mɔk parbiir yoo waaminna mɔtana kimaan ya la farbooru booru na paak, i mɔk parpeenne.

ref: Your faith will be like gold that has been tested in a fire. And these trials will prove that your faith is worth much more than gold that can be destroyed. They will show that you will be given praise and honor and glory when Jesus Christ returns.
text: Fara maŋ bikii-i a lin laan i teen Masia yada fanu amii-ii? Li tee kaman nan ba tuu jii sarima ki teen muu ni a bin la li biaŋ nee. I yada teenu ŋan ki tur Yennu ki gar sarima gbaa. I yada teenu-i ŋan, ŋanne sa te ki yin la paku nan dontir nan nibaakir yoo nba ki Yisa Masia tan sa nyi paanu.

ref: You have never seen Jesus, and you don't see him now. But still you love him and have faith in him, and no words can tell how glad and happy
text: I ki mi lau ŋaan loonɔ; i bia ki laatɔ mɔtana, ŋaan teenɔ yada ki mɔk parpeenn bonchiann nba ki nirɔ kan fit yet li manu nba tee biaŋinba.

ref: you are to be saved. This is why you have faith.
text: Ya teenɔ yada na, li paak i la tinnuwa.

ref: Some prophets told how God would treat you with undeserved grace, and they searched hard to find out more about the way you would be saved.
text: Tinnu nee ki sɔkiniinba din loon bin bann li paak nan ninmɔnn, ki din sɔkint a li tan sii tee yimme ki Yennu sa tini ninbaauk.

ref: The Spirit of Christ was in them and was telling them how Christ would suffer and would then be given great honor. So they searched to find out exactly who Christ would be and when this would happen.
text: Masia Seyeeŋ nba din be bi ni din wannib a u tan sa la fara; ki li poorpo ki u bia tan sa la nibaakir bonchiann; ki bi koor ki loon bin bann yoo nba ki li tan sa tun, ki bia loon bin bann la tan sa tun biaŋinba.

ref: But they were told that they were serving you and not themselves. They preached to you by the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven. And their message was only for you, even though angels would like to know more about it. Chosen To Live a Holy Life
text: Ki Yennu fiit wannib a bi ki sɔkint bi mɔŋ yoo labaar kaa, ŋaan yimm poe ki bi sɔkint. Yennu Seyeeŋ nba ki u din tumɔ a wun nyi Yendɔuŋ ni nee mun te ki binba mɔɔntir barŋanii na beeri barii maŋ. Linba nee ki malakanba mun loon bin bann li paak.

ref: Be alert and think straight. Put all your hope in how God will treat you with undeserved grace when Jesus Christ appears.
text: Li paak, yi teen i dudukit siir nan yan, ki soor i mɔŋ, kii mɔk dindann nan barmɔnii a i sa di mant bonchiann yoo nba ki Yisa Masia tan sa nyi paanu na.

ref: Behave like obedient children. Don't let your lives be controlled by your desires, as they used to be.
text: I sak Yennu mɔb man, ki ji daa waa loonbiit nba ki i din waa yoo nba ki i din ki mi Yisa Masia na.

ref: Always live as God's holy people should, because God is the one who chose you, and he is holy.
text: Yennu nba yiini na tee Popeendaanɔe, li paak, i mun-ii tee popeendamm man,

ref: That's why the Scriptures say, “I am the holy God, and you must be holy too.”
text: kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yii tee popeendamm man, kimaan min Yennue tee Popeendaanɔ.”

ref: You say that God is your Father, but God doesn't have favorites! He judges all people by what they do. So you must honor God while you live as strangers here on earth.
text: Li-i tee ki i mei Yennu, i yiu Baae; ŋɔɔe bu niib kur buut kaman nan ba tun biaŋinba na, ki ningannii kaa; li paak, yoo nba kur ki i be tingbouŋ na ni, yii tiinɔ man.

ref: You were rescued from the useless way of life you learned from your ancestors. But you know you were not rescued by such things as silver or gold that don't last forever.
text: Kimaan i mi nan Yennu nba nyinni binbenbiiuk nba ki i din gaar i baanba boor na, u ki jii bont nba sa biir kaman nan likirii na kaa ki pa,

ref: You were rescued by the precious blood of Christ, that spotless and innocent lamb.
text: ŋaan u jii Yisa Masia sɔn nba ŋan gar likirii kura; ki li tee kaman nan mannu pebik nba ki mɔk biit amii dɔk u paak nee.

ref: Christ was chosen even before the world was created, but because of you, he did not come until these last days.
text: Yennu nba daa din ki nan dunia yoonba na, ŋaan ki u poŋ gann Masia a wuu tee mannu maŋ, ŋaan ki u tan nyii paanu dajoontii na ni ti paak.

ref: And when he did come, it was to lead you to have faith in God, who raised him from death and honored him in a glorious way. This is why you have put your faith and hope in God.
text: U paake ki i teen Yennu yada; ŋɔɔe din fiinɔ kuun ni ki turɔ paŋ; li paak, ki i mɔk yada nan dindann Yennu boor.

ref: You obeyed the truth, and your souls were made pure. Now you sincerely love each other. But you must keep on loving with all your heart.
text: Ya sak ki gaar Masia labaar nee te ki i nyii biit ni, ki ji loon i naa waas nan barmɔnii; li paak, yii loon i leeb man nan i para kura.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Humans wither like grass, and their glory fades like wild flowers. Grass dries up, and flowers fall to the ground.
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Nisaarik kur tee kaman nan mɔɔt nee, ki u fant tee kaman nan tipuuk na. Mɔɔt jɔɔkit ki li puut suur baa,

ref: But what the Lord has said will stand forever.” Our good news to you is what the Lord has said.
text: ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ maan na ŋanin kaa gbennu.” Maan maŋe tee barŋanii nba ki bi din mɔɔntir ki teeni na.

ref: Stop being hateful! Quit trying to fool people, and start being sincere. Don't be jealous or say cruel things about others.
text: Ŋannimi, yi nyik biit kur, nan faak kur, nan kpinkpannii, nan funfunn, nan sanjaauŋ.

ref: Come to Jesus Christ. He is the living stone people have rejected, but which God has chosen and highly honored.
text: Baat u boor man; ŋɔɔe tee manfoor tann nba ki niib yêtɔ, ŋaan ki Yennu gannɔ ki u ŋan bonchiann na.

ref: And now you are living stones being used to build a spiritual house. You are also a group of holy priests, and with the help of Jesus Christ you will offer sacrifices that please God.
text: Ŋaant man ki Yennu-n jii-i ki yi teen kaman nan manfoor tana na ki wun maa ŋaak nba ki u Seyeeŋ sii be li ni; ki i sii tee Yennu mɔŋ mɔŋ toontunna a yii tuun u toonn nan u Seyeeŋ paŋ, ki u sa gaar li toonn Yisa Masia paake;

ref: It is just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a choice and precious cornerstone. No one who has faith in this one will be disappointed.”
text: kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a u yet a “Gotir, n gann tann ki bir Sion doo ni, ki li tee lɔtik ni tann, ki bia ŋan bonchiann, ki wunba kur teenɔ yada, u kan di fei.”

ref: You are followers of the Lord, and this stone is precious to you. But it isn't precious to those who refuse to follow him. They are the builders who tossed aside the stone that turned out to be the most important one of all.
text: Yimm nba teenɔ yada na mi nan u ŋan bonchiann ki turi; ŋaan binba ki teenɔ yada, bii mi nan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Tann nba ki tanmaara yêtɔ na, ŋanne set tee lɔtik ni tanŋann.”

ref: But you are God's chosen and special people. You are a group of royal priests and a holy nation. God has brought you out of darkness into his marvelous light. Now you must tell all the wonderful things he has done. The Scriptures say,
text: Ŋaan yimm ŋanin tee Yennu nigannkabe, ki bia tee u naan ni niib binba jiantirɔ, ki bia tee nibooru nba tee popeendamm, ki bia tee u tiauŋ niib, ki yii mɔɔntir u burchintuk po, ŋɔɔ nba nyinni bunbɔnn ni, ki baar nani u bakitnauŋ yentu ni.

ref: “Once you were nobody. Now you are God's people. At one time no one had mercy on you. Now God has treated you with kindness.” Live as God's Servants Should
text: Sianyoo i din ki tee Yennu niibi, ŋaan mɔtana i set tee u niibe; sianyoo u din ki tini ninbaauku, ŋaan mɔtana u tini ninbaauka. Yennu daaba labaar

ref: Dear friends, you are foreigners and strangers on this earth. So I beg you not to surrender to those desires that fight against you.
text: N naa walonkai, ya tee saamm tingbouŋ na ni na, n kpaaniie a yi nyik gbanant loonbiit nba kɔn nan i sei na,

ref: Always let others see you behaving properly, even though they may still accuse you of doing wrong. Then on the day of judgment, they will honor God by telling the good things they saw you do.
text: ŋaan kii be fanu tingbouŋ na niib sinsuuk ni, ki li-i tee ki bi sukii-i a i tee biitdamm, bi sa la i toonŋana ki dont Yennu, daar nba ki u tan sa baar na.

ref: The Lord wants you to obey all human authorities, especially the Emperor, who rules over everyone.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ paak, i saak man yentdamm mɔi; yii saak wunba tee yent kur daanɔ mɔb;

ref: You must also obey governors, because they are sent by the Emperor to punish criminals and to praise good citizens.
text: wa senn sennudamm a bin dat binba tuun toonbiit na tuba, ŋaan kii guu binba tuun toonŋana, yii saak bi mɔi mun.

ref: God wants you to silence stupid and ignorant people by doing right.
text: Kimaan Yennu loommu tee a yii tuun toonŋana ki gɔɔr jatdamm maan nba ki mɔk nyɔɔti.

ref: You are free, but still you are God's servants, and you must not use your freedom as an excuse for doing wrong.
text: I be nan yentinmɔŋ binbeŋ; ŋaan i daa dia i yentinmɔŋ binbeŋ na ki li teeni yaak ki i tuun toonbiiti, ŋaan kii mɔk Yennu daaba binbeŋ.

ref: Respect everyone and show special love for God's people. Honor God and respect the Emperor. The Example of Christ's Suffering
text: Yii teen sɔɔ kur nibaakir, kii loon Masiaweira, ŋaan tin Yennu, kii teen yent kur daanɔ nibaakir.

ref: Servants, you must obey your masters and always show respect to them. Do this, not only to those who are kind and thoughtful, but also to those who are cruel.
text: Yimm nba tee toontunna, yii saak i yudamm mɔi ŋaan bia kii teemm nibaakir, ki li ki tee niŋamm kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan nan binba tee nibiita gbaa.

ref: God will bless you, even if others treat you unfairly for being loyal to him.
text: Li-i tee ki a la tubdatu a toonŋana paak, ki a dukii Yennu po ki di sukuru, ŋanne sa penn u par;

ref: You don't gain anything by being punished for some wrong you have done. But God will bless you, if you have to suffer for doing something good.
text: ŋaan fi-i tun biit ki bi dat a tubir, ki a gaar tubdatu na soonna, nlee ki bi sa pakani? Ŋaan fi-i tun linba ŋan ki gaar tubdatu li paak soonna, nee sa penn Yennu par.

ref: After all, God chose you to suffer as you follow in the footsteps of Christ, who set an example by suffering for you.
text: Ki Yennu din yiinit a tii di fara, kimaan Masia tiauŋ din di fara ti paak, a tii gotir u binbeŋ kii waa u taapauŋ.

ref: Christ did not sin or ever tell a lie.
text: U ki tun biit gbaa; sɔɔ bia ki mi gbat ki faak nyii u mɔb ni.

ref: Although he was abused, he never tried to get even. And when he suffered, he made no threats. Instead, he had faith in God, who judges fairly.
text: Ba din sukiiɔ, u din ki jiin sukiti; wa bia din be fara ni, u ki jeen sɔɔ, ŋaan din jii u mɔŋ ki teen Yennu wunba bu niib buut fanu na nuu ni.

ref: Christ carried the burden of our sins. He was nailed to the cross, so we would stop sinning and start living right. By his cuts and bruises you are healed.
text: Ŋɔɔ Masia mɔŋ mɔŋe din jii ti toonbiit u gbanant ni, ki bi kpaau dapunpunn paak, a tin nyi biit ni ŋaan tee niŋamm. U daŋ paake ki i la laafia.

ref: You had wandered away like sheep. Now you have returned to the one who is your shepherd and protector.
text: I din tuu tee kaman nan pebotkai nee, ŋaan mɔtana i jen i kpaarik wunba guu i sei na boora.

ref: If you are a wife, you must put your husband first. Even if he opposes our message, you will win him over by what you do. No one else will have to say anything to him,
text: Poob, sakin man i sɔrib mɔi, ki bi siab-i kii teen barŋanii na yada, ŋaan li pasiar bi poob binbenŋaŋ na paak, bi sa teen yadawa, li ki kpaa poo talas nan wun yet siar gbaa,

ref: because he will see how you honor God and live a pure life.
text: kimaan bi-i la bi poob nba teen Yisa yada na binbeŋ ki li ŋan, bi mun sa teenɔ yadawa.

ref: Don't depend on things like fancy hairdos or gold jewelry or expensive clothes to make you look beautiful.
text: Poob, daa jikit i mɔŋ ki want i mɔŋ fant nan yuloru, amii sarima tuliiat, amii chinchenŋana gbabu.

ref: Be beautiful in your heart by being gentle and quiet. This kind of beauty will last, and God considers it very special.
text: Ŋaant man ki i fant-ii tee soonna nan ŋminŋminn i para ni; ŋanne tee fant nba kan biiri, ki bia ŋan bonchiann Yennu boor ni.

ref: Long ago those women who worshiped God and put their hope in him made themselves beautiful by putting their husbands first.
text: Yaayoo ni pooŋamm nba din teen Yennu yada na, nnee ki bi din faŋŋit bi mɔŋ, ki din saak bi sɔrib mɔi.

ref: For example, Sarah obeyed Abraham and called him her master. You are her true children, if you do right and don't let anything frighten you.
text: Nnee ki Sara din sak u sɔrɔ Abraham mɔb ki din yiu u yomdaanɔ. Li-i tee ki i tuun ki li ŋan, kii ki tiin siari, ŋann i tee Sara maŋ bonpoie.

ref: If you are a husband, you should be thoughtful of your wife. Treat her with honor, because she isn't as strong as you are, and she shares with you in the gift of life. Then nothing will stand in the way of your prayers. Suffering for Doing Right
text: Poosɔrib, i mun-ii be fanu man nan i poob, kii mi nan bi tee tariie i boor, kii teemm nibaakir, kimaan yimm nan ŋamm kur tan sa gaar Yennu piinŋann nba tee manfoor; ŋanne bia sa te ki Yennu sa gaar i miaru. Masiaweira binbeŋ labaar

ref: Finally, all of you should agree and have concern and love for each other. You should also be kind and humble.
text: Linba biar na tee nnee; i kur-ii mɔk man dudukyenn, ki i baka-ii be i leeb ni, kii mɔk loomm nan Masiaweira, kii tiin i leeb ninbaauk, kii sikint i mɔŋ.

ref: Don't be hateful and insult people just because they are hateful and insult you. Instead, treat everyone with kindness. You are God's chosen ones, and he will bless you. The Scriptures say,
text: I daa tuun biit ki jiint biit paaki, i bia daa sukii ki jiint wunba sukiini, ŋaan kii jiinɔ a “Yennu-n senn mɔsonŋann a paak,” kimaan Yennu din yiini a yin gaar u mɔsonŋanne.

ref: “Do you really love life? Do you want to be happy? Then stop saying cruel things and quit telling lies.
text: Kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba loon parpeenn manfoor, ki loon wun tan la daa nba ŋan, wun nyinn u mɔŋ barbiit nan faak maan ni,

ref: Give up your evil ways and do right, as you find and follow the road to peace.
text: ki nyik bonbiir tumu, ŋaan kii tuun toonŋana, ŋaan lon parmaasir sɔnu kii waa-r,

ref: The Lord watches over everyone who obeys him, and he listens to their prayers. But he opposes everyone who does evil.”
text: kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ kpaa u ninbina niŋamm paake, ki bia gbia bi miaru yoo kura; ŋaan kɔn nan toonbiit damm.”

ref: Can anyone really harm you for being eager to do good deeds?
text: I numm-i mɔn nan toonŋana tumu, sɔɔ be ki sa teeni siarii?

ref: Even if you have to suffer for doing good things, God will bless you. So stop being afraid and don't worry about what people might do.
text: Ŋaan li-i lekii tee ki i la tubdatu i toonŋana paak, parpeenn sii tee i yare; i daa tiin nisaarik, i bia daa te ki li daamiini.

ref: Honor Christ and let him be the Lord of your life. Always be ready to give an answer when someone asks you about your hope.
text: Tinin ti Yomdaanɔ Masia i para ni; yoo kur, i teen siir ki jiin wunba boi-i ya mɔk dindann biaŋinba na po.

ref: Give a kind and respectful answer and keep your conscience clear. This way you will make people ashamed for saying bad things about your good conduct as a follower of Christ.
text: Yi gatɔ soonna kii tiin Yennu, ki ŋaan ki i benu-ii ŋan, ki bi-i sukii-i ya tuun toonŋana Masia paak na, bi sa di fei bi sukit maŋ ni.

ref: You are better off to obey God and suffer for doing right than to suffer for doing wrong.
text: Li-i tee Yennu loomm nan i di fara toonŋana paak, li chee nan i di fara toonbiit paak;

ref: Christ died once for our sins. An innocent person died for those who are guilty. Christ did this to bring you to God, when his body was put to death and his spirit was made alive.
text: kimaan Masia din gaar biitdamm kur paak ki kpo yomkɔɔe; niŋanɔ maŋe din gaar biitdamm paak ki kpo, a wun te tin saan Yennu boor. Bi din kpiu, ŋaan ki u bia din fiir kuun ni Yennu Seyeeŋ paak.

ref: Christ then preached to the spirits that were being kept in prison.
text: Li Seyeeŋ nie ki u din saan sei nba kɔɔ sarik kpeentiŋ na boor, ki saa mɔɔnt Yennu maan ki turib;

ref: They had disobeyed God while Noah was building the boat, but God had been patient with them. Eight people went into that boat and were brought safely through the flood.
text: ŋamm din tee niib nba din ki sak Yennu mɔb yoo nba ki u din dii sukuru Nowa yoo na, yoo nba ki ŋɔɔ Nowa din ŋamm ŋarungbeŋŋir; ki niib banniin kuukɔɔ din kɔɔ ŋaruŋ maŋ ni ki tinn nyun ni na.

ref: Those flood waters were like baptism that now saves you. But baptism is more than just washing your body. It means turning to God with a clear conscience, because Jesus Christ was raised from death.
text: Nyun maŋ tee Yennu nyunwuru ninnauŋ nba tinnit mɔtana nee; li ki tee ti gbanant paak wuru, ŋaan li tee ti mei Yennu a wun wuur ti para ni Yisa Masia kuun fiiru na paake.

ref: Christ is now in heaven, where he sits at the right side of God. All angels, authorities, and powers are under his control.
text: U saan Yendɔuŋ nie ki be Yennu nuudiitu po, ki malakanba nan yentdamm kur saak u mɔb.

ref: Christ suffered here on earth. Now you must be ready to suffer as he did, because suffering shows that you have stopped sinning.
text: Masia nba din dii fara ti paak na, i mun chee i para kii dukii nna, kimaan wunba dii gbanant fara, u nyii toonbiit tumu niwa.

ref: It means you have turned from your own desires and want to obey God for the rest of your life.
text: La jii yoo na ki saa, i daa jikit i gbanant ki tuun nisaarik loonbiiti, ŋaan kii waa Yennu loomm.

ref: You have already lived long enough like people who don't know God. You were immoral and followed your evil desires. You went around drinking and partying and carrying on. In fact, you even worshiped disgusting idols.
text: Yoo nba gar, i din jii i yoo ki tuun toonbiit bonchiann; i din tuun i mɔŋ loonbiite, ki nyu daan ki yib, ki di diina ki mɔk fuut, ki tikii leeb danyuboa, ki mann pata, ki tuun kaman nan binba ki tee Masiaweira na nba loon bii tuun biaŋinba.

ref: Now your former friends wonder why you have stopped running around with them, and they curse you for it.
text: Ŋaan mɔtana, ya ji ki waab ki tuun li toonbiit na, li bakitibe, ki bi ji biir i sana.

ref: But they will have to answer to God, who judges the living and the dead.
text: Ŋaan bi tan sa bu bi mɔŋ buut ki tur Yennu nba teen siir ki sa bu footib nan kpeemm buut na.

ref: The good news has even been preached to the dead, so that after they have been judged for what they have done in this life, their spirits will live with God.
text: Ŋanne teen ki bi din mɔɔnt Masia barŋanii ki tur binba kpo na gbaa; bi lek din kpo kaman nan sɔɔ kur nba kpenn biaŋinba nee; bi din mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋe ki turib a bin la yaak kii mɔk manfoa bi sei ni, Yennu paak. Yan nba ki Yennu teeni na dianu labaar

ref: Everything will soon come to an end. So be serious and be sensible enough to pray.
text: Bont kura gbennu ji ki fɔk; li paak, yi soor i mɔŋ kii sub kii mei Yennu.

ref: Most important of all, you must sincerely love each other, because love wipes away many sins.
text: Linba gar bont kur tee a yii loon i leeb man fanu, kimaan loomm dɔkint biit bonchiann.

ref: Welcome people into your home and don't grumble about it.
text: Yii gaar leeb saauŋ ki daa burimi.

ref: Each of you has been blessed with one of God's many wonderful gifts to be used in the service of others. So use your gift well.
text: Sɔɔ kur nan wa gaar yan nba nyii Yennu boore, i diar fanu ki jii yan maŋ ki somm i leeb.

ref: If you have the gift of speaking, preach God's message. If you have the gift of helping others, do it with the strength that God supplies. Everything should be done in a way that will bring honor to God because of Jesus Christ, who is glorious and powerful forever. Amen. Suffering for Being a Christian
text: Wunba ki Yennu sennɔ a wuu mɔɔntir barŋanii na, wuu mɔɔntir linba ki Yennu teenɔ nee; wunba mun ki Yennu sennɔ a wuu sommit leeb, wuu sommitib nan u paŋ nba ki Yennu teenɔ na, ki Yennu-n la paku bonsiar kur ni Yisa Masia paak; ŋɔɔ Yennu yen paku nan paŋ kur yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm. Masiaweiu paak fara dinu labaar

ref: Dear friends, don't be surprised or shocked that you are going through testing that is like walking through fire.
text: N naa walonkai, ya di fargbeŋŋa nba bikii-i na, i daa te ki li bakitiri ki li tee barsaann i boori.

ref: Be glad for the chance to suffer as Christ suffered. It will prepare you for even greater happiness when he makes his glorious return.
text: Ya di fara Masia paak na, yii mɔk parpeenn man, ki yoo nba ki u tan sa nyi paanu nan yentsaakar ni, i sa la parpeenn bonchiann.

ref: Count it a blessing when you suffer for being a Christian. This shows that God's glorious Spirit is with you.
text: Bi-i sukii-i Masia sann paak, parpeenn tee i yare, kimaan Yennu Seyeeŋ nba mɔk paŋ bonchiann be i ni.

ref: But you deserve to suffer if you are a murderer, a thief, a crook, or a busybody.
text: Ŋaan sɔɔ fardinu-i be nikpinu amii nanyuk amii toonbiit tumu amii kpikpiruk toona paak, li ki ŋani;

ref: Don't be ashamed to suffer for being a Christian. Praise God that you belong to him.
text: ŋaan fi-i di fara fa tee Masiaweirɔ na paak, a daa tiin fei, ŋaan dont Yennu, Masia sann paak.

ref: God has already begun judging his own people. And if his judgment begins with us, imagine how terrible it will be for those who refuse to obey his message. The Scriptures say,
text: Yoo baara ki Yennu sa piin ki bu niib buut; ki u sa bu u tiauŋ niib buute sinsinn; ki li-i tee ki u bu timm nba tee u niib na buut sinsinn, ki binba ki sak u barŋanii na sa joont nlee?

ref: “If good people barely escape, what will happen to sinners and to others who don't respect God?”
text: Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Li-i paar ki niŋamm-n tinn, ki biitdamm nan binba ki tiin Yennu ji sa teen nlee ki tinni?”

ref: If you suffer for obeying God, you must have complete faith in your faithful Creator and keep on doing right.
text: Li paak, binba di fara Yennu loomm paak na, bii tuun toonŋana, ki jii bi mɔŋ ki pet tur bi nantɔɔ Yennu nba tee barmɔnii daanɔ na.

ref: Church leaders, I am writing to encourage you. I too am a leader, as well as a witness to Christ's suffering, and I will share in his glory when it is shown to us.
text: Yudamm nba be i sinsuuk ni, n kpaaniie, min wunba mun tee yudaanɔ, ki n ninbinn din la Masia nba din dii fara biaŋinba, ki n bia sa lakin di mant bonchiann nba tan sa nyi paanu ni na,

ref: Just as shepherds watch over their sheep, you must watch over everyone God has placed in your care. Do it willingly in order to please God, and not simply because you think you must. Let it be something you want to do, instead of something you do merely to make money.
text: n kpaaniie, yii guu Yennu niib nba be i nuu ni na, ki li daa tee miar kaa, ŋaan ki lii tee i yanbɔɔte, Yennu nba loon biaŋinba; ki li bia daa tee likirii paaki; ŋaan ki lii tee i kpan koor a i tume.

ref: Don't be bossy to those people who are in your care, but set an example for them.
text: Daa mantik dia niib nba ki Yennu tura ki a diab na nan ninmɔnni, ŋaan te ki i binbenŋaŋ-n wannib ba sii be biaŋinba.

ref: Then when Christ the Chief Shepherd returns, you will be given a crown that will never lose its glory.
text: Ki Masia wunba tee yudamm kur yudaanɔ na-i taŋŋi jen, i sa la piinii nba kan biiri.

ref: All of you young people should obey your elders. In fact, everyone should be humble toward everyone else. The Scriptures say, “God opposes proud people, but he helps everyone who is humble.”
text: Nnee mun, yimm nba tee nipaauŋ, yii saak saakab mɔi; ki i kur-ii gaan leeb mɔi, ki i kur-n teen tarii i leeb boa, kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Ŋɔɔ Yennu yêen karinbaandamme, ŋaan sommit binba sikint bi mɔŋ na.”

ref: Be humble in the presence of God's mighty power, and he will honor you when the time comes.
text: Li paak, yi sikin i mɔŋ ki teen Yabint Yennu nuu ni, ki u sa donni yoo nba ki u loon.

ref: God cares for you, so turn all your worries over to him.
text: Jiin man i muksuk kur ki turɔ, kimaan u baka be i ni.

ref: Be on your guard and stay awake. Your enemy, the devil, is like a roaring lion, sneaking around to find someone to attack.
text: Yii dia i mɔŋ man, ŋaan kii mi i dataak nba tee Sintaanii na po; u line kaman nan yambɔr nee, ki loon wun la sɔɔ ki soor ŋman.

ref: But you must resist the devil and stay strong in your faith. You know that all over the world the Lord's followers are suffering just as you are.
text: Yêtirɔ man, ŋaan set teen Masia yada fanu, kimaan i mi nan Masiaweira leeb nba be tingbouŋ na ni mun di fara kaman nan ya di biaŋinba nee.

ref: But God shows undeserved kindness to everyone. That's why he appointed Christ Jesus to choose you to share in his eternal glory. You will suffer for a while, but God will make you complete, steady, strong, and firm.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki i di fara yoo waaminna, ki Yennu nba mɔk ninbatinu kur na sa ŋamm i binbeŋ, ki chee i para, ki turi paŋ; ŋɔɔe din yiini Masia paak a yii di u mant nba kaa gbennu na.

ref: God will be in control forever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: Ŋɔɔe sii mɔk paŋ kura mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm. Foontii nba joont

ref: Silvanus helped me write this short letter, and I consider him a faithful follower of the Lord. I wanted to encourage you and tell you how kind God really is, so you will keep on having faith in him.
text: Sailas wunba ki n mi nan u tee Masiaweirɔ nan barmɔnii na, ŋɔɔe sommin ki n sɔb gbouŋ nba ki fɔk na a n chee i para, ki kpaani, a ŋannii tee Yennu maan nan barmɔnii. Li paak, yii diar fanu man.

ref: Greetings from the Lord's followers in Babylon. They are God's chosen ones. Mark, who is like a son to me, sends his greetings too.
text: I leeb nba be Babilɔnn ki Yennu din gannib na, bi foonti. N bik Mak mun foonti.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. I pray that God will give peace to everyone who belongs to Christ.
text: Yi foont i leeb nan loomm foontii. Parmaasir-ii be yimm nba kur tee Masiaweira na paak.

ref: From Simon Peter, a servant and an apostle of Jesus Christ. To everyone who shares with us in the privilege of believing that our God and Savior Jesus Christ will do what is just and fair.
text: Min Simon Piita, wunba tee Yisa Masia daabir nan u toomii nae sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen binba teen Yennu nan ti Tinntɔɔ Yisa Masia yada u burchint paak, kaman nan ta mun teemm yada biaŋinba na.

ref: I pray that God will be kind to you and will let you live in perfect peace! May you keep learning more and more about God and our Lord Jesus. Living as the Lord's Followers
text: Ya bann Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa na paak, ninbatinu nan parmaasir-n ŋamm pukini. Yennu niib binbeŋ labaar

ref: We have everything we need to live a life that pleases God. It was all given to us by God's own power, when we learned he had invited us to share in his wonderful goodness.
text: Yennu paŋ yab bonchiann; ki u teent linba kur tee manfoor nan yentinu yar kimaan ta miu na paak; ŋɔɔe din yiinit u yentsaakar nan u binbenŋaŋ ni.

ref: God made great and marvelous promises, so his nature would become part of us. Then we could escape our evil desires and the corrupt influences of this world.
text: Li paak, ki u din senn mɔsonjaana nba ŋan bonchiann ki turit a tin nyi kuun nba be tingbouŋ na ni loonbiit na paak, ŋaan gaar u binbeŋ.

ref: If you keep growing in this way, it will show that what you know about our Lord Jesus Christ has made your lives useful and meaningful.
text: I binbeŋ-i be nnaie, li sa te ki i ŋamm bann ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia yoo kur, kii mɔk nyɔɔt bonchiann ki turɔ.

ref: But if you don't grow, you are like someone who is nearsighted or blind, and you have forgotten that your past sins are forgiven.
text: Ŋaan nisɔɔ nba ki mɔk binbenbooru maŋi, li yɔɔ jɔɔne, ki poŋ tamma nan Yisa din nyinn u toonbiit sianyoowa.

ref: My friends, you must do all you can to show God has really chosen and selected you. If you keep on doing this, you won't stumble and fall.
text: N naa waas, Yennue yiini ki ganni, li paak, yi ŋamm yabir i mɔŋ paak man, ki te ki lin set wann nan i tee u niibe. Li-i tee ki i tuun linba na i kan mi ki baa;

ref: Then our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ will give you a glorious welcome into his kingdom that will last forever.
text: ki Yennu sa te ki yin tan kɔɔ ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nba tee ti Tinntɔɔ na naan ni; u naan maŋe yɔɔ be ki ki mɔk gbennu.

ref: You are holding firmly to the truth you were given. But I am still going to remind you of these things.
text: Li paak, n sii yɔɔ ki tiani labaar na poe yoo kur; ŋaan n lek mi nan i poŋ gbatira, ki mir fanu, ki diar chimm;

ref: In fact, I think I should keep on reminding you until I leave this body.
text: ŋaan n dukin nan li ŋan ki n ŋamm tiari ki fiin i dudukit ma daa be n manfoor ni na.

ref: And our Lord Jesus Christ has already told me that I will soon leave it behind.
text: N mi nan li kan yukiri ki n sa nyi n gbanant ni kaman nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nba din betin na.

ref: This is why I am doing my best to make sure that each of you remembers all of this after I am gone. The Message about the Glory of Christ
text: Li paak te ki n yaa n yabir n mɔŋ ki sɔb gbouŋ na ki lii tiani labaar na po yoo kur nan n kuun poorpo. Yennu nba donn Masia biaŋinba

ref: When we told you about the power and the return of our Lord Jesus Christ, we were not telling clever stories someone had made up. But with our own eyes we saw his true greatness.
text: Ta poŋ din beti nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia tee paŋ daanɔe, ki bia tan sa jena, labaar maŋ ki tee faak kaa, li tee barmɔniie. Ti ninbinae din la wa mɔk paŋ biaŋinba.

ref: God, our great and wonderful Father, truly honored him by saying, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Ki ti din be nanɔ yoo nba ki ti Baa Yennu turɔ paku yentsaakar ni. Li yoo maŋe ki kunkɔr nyii ti yabint Yennu nba tee yentsaakar daanɔ boor ni ki yet a, “N Bilonkakii na, wunba ki n numm gboou nee na.”

ref: We were there with Jesus on the holy mountain and heard this voice speak from heaven.
text: Ti mɔŋ mɔŋ tubae din gbat kunkɔr maŋ ki li nyii Yendɔuŋ ni na. Li yooe ki ti din be nanɔ jɔɔr nba ki Yennu gannir na.

ref: All of this makes us even more certain that what the prophets said is true. So you should pay close attention to their message, as you would to a lamp shining in some dark place. You must keep on paying attention until daylight comes and the morning star rises in your hearts.
text: Ŋanne mantik kat wantit nan Yennu sɔkiniinba nba din pak Yisa labaar na po din tee barmɔniie. Li ŋan ki yin gbiint ba din yet linba na fanu. Bi mɔbona maŋ tee kaman nan fitir nba teen yentu bunbɔnn ni biaŋinba nee mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Yisa tan sa jen; ŋanne sa te ki i numm-n yent fanu.

ref: But you need to realize that no one alone can understand any of the prophecies in the Scriptures.
text: Ŋaan linba gar li kur tee nee; yii tian man nan Yennu sɔkiniinba nba din sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni barii nba na din ki tee bi tiauŋ mɔmaan kaa,

ref: The prophets did not think these things up on their own, but they were guided by the Spirit of God.
text: kimaan Yennu sɔkiniinbasɔɔ din ki mi dukin u mɔŋ loomm ni ki paki, ŋaan Yennu Seyeeŋe din wantib biaŋinba ki bi sa pak labaar nba nyii ŋɔɔ Yennu boor ni na.

ref: Sometimes false prophets spoke to the people of Israel. False teachers will also sneak in and speak harmful lies to you. But these teachers don't really belong to the Master who paid a great price for them, and they will quickly destroy themselves.
text: Faak sɔkiniinba nba din be Juu teeb sinsuuk ni sianyoo biaŋinba na, nnee ki faak wannteeb mun bia sii be i sinsuuk ni, ki wanti faak wannu woonin ni, linba sa biiri. Bi sa yêt ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa wunba din daab na gbaa; ki Yennu sa dat bi tuba yiama.

ref: Many people will follow their evil ways and cause others to tell lies about the true way.
text: Niib bonchiann sa weib ki mun tuun bi mɔŋ loonbiit tumu. Bi paak ki niib sa fiak barmɔnii sɔnu na.

ref: They will be greedy and cheat you with smooth talk. But long ago God decided to punish them, and God doesn't sleep.
text: Bi sii mɔk nianchiauŋ, ki sii kpanni ki ŋmabi. Yoo nba din gar na Yennu poŋ din yet a bi tee biitdamme, ŋaan li ji kan yukir ki u sa dat bi tuba.

ref: God did not have pity on the angels that sinned. He had them tied up and thrown into the dark pits of hell until the time of judgment.
text: Malakanba nba din tun toonbiit na, Yennu din ki nyik bi tubdatu. U din jii jɔrite ki lorib ŋaan jiib lu muu nba kan mi kpeen na ni; muu maŋ be bootuk nba sumii bonchiann nie, ki bia bɔn bonchiann; bi sii be nni mɔkmɔk nan daar nba ki u tan sa bu bi buut.

ref: And during Noah's time, God did not have pity on the ungodly people of the world. He destroyed them with a flood, though he did save eight people, including Noah, who preached the truth.
text: Nnee biak, u din ki nyik yaayoo ni tingbouŋ niib na. U din te ki nyume dii tingbouŋ maŋ, ki kpii binba kur ki tiinɔ na, ŋaan tinn Nowa, wunba din mɔɔntir Yenweiu po, ki din tinn u ŋaateeb bannlore ki pukin u paak.

ref: God punished the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah by burning them to ashes, and this is a warning to anyone else who wants to sin.
text: Ŋɔɔ Yennu bia din biir doi nba yi Sodom nan Gomora, ki te ki bi dii muu ki kab kpant tanpent, ki kpii doi maŋ ni niib kura. Linba nee tee siara ki want biitdamm wa tan sa dat bi tuba biaŋinba.

ref: Lot lived right and was greatly troubled by the terrible way those wicked people were living. He was a good man, and day after day he suffered because of the evil things he saw and heard. So the Lord rescued him.
text: Lot, jaŋanɔ maŋ, din be bi doo nie, ki laat bi toonbiit, ki gbia ba piak bi mɔbonbiit biaŋinba. Ba din tuun toonbiit yoo kur biaŋinba na, li din biir u par ni bonchiann, kimaan u din tee niŋanɔe.

ref: This shows that the Lord knows how to rescue godly people from their sufferings and to punish evil people while they wait for the day of judgment.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu mi biaŋinba ki u sa fat niib nba tiinɔ, ki nyinnib muksuk ni, ŋaan dat biitdamm tuba mɔkmɔk nan daar nba ki u tan sa bu bi buut na.

ref: The Lord is especially hard on people who disobey him and don't think of anything except their own filthy desires. They are reckless and proud and are not afraid of cursing the glorious beings in heaven.
text: Niib nba waa bi gbanant loonbiit ki tuun bonchonchonn toona, ki sian yudamm, Yennu tan sa dat li damm tuba ki gar wa tan sa dat nileeb tuba biaŋinba nawa. Faak wannteeb maŋ nba be i sinsuuk ni na, bi tee tubkangbata damme, bi ki tiin sɔɔ. Bi sukii malakanbae ki ki mɔk jaŋmaanii.

ref: Although angels are more powerful than these evil beings, even the angels don't dare to accuse them to the Lord.
text: Malakanba mɔk paŋ nan yikoo ki gariba, ŋaan bi ki yeen biit ki jiint bi po ti Yomdaanɔ boori.

ref: These people are no better than senseless animals that live by their feelings and are born to be caught and killed. They speak evil of things they don't know anything about. But their own corrupt deeds will destroy them.
text: Li nibooru ki mi malakanba nba be biaŋinba, ŋaan sukiib. Niib maŋ ki mɔk dudukiti; bi tuun linba ki bi loon, ki naan nan muuk ni bonkobibiit nba ki niib tuu soorib ki kpib nee. Bi mɔŋ toonbiite sa mantik biirib;

ref: They have done evil, and they will be rewarded with evil. They think it is fun to have wild parties during the day. They are immoral, and the meals they eat with you are spoiled by the shameful and selfish way they carry on.
text: bi toonbiit nyɔɔt sii tee biite. Bi lek tuun bi gbanant loonbiit yeenin ni. Bi-i be i ni yoonba, bi tee kaman nan jakint nba tuu taab chinchenn paak biaŋinba nee. Bi-i di jeet nani yoonba, ŋaan bi lek dukii bi toonbiit poe.

ref: All they think about is having sex with someone else's husband or wife. There is no end to their wicked deeds. They trick people who are easily fooled, and their minds are filled with greedy thoughts. But they are headed for trouble!
text: Bi-i la poochonchonn, bi ki nyikit u bandakiri, ki bia kan nyik bi biiti. Bi kpann niib nba ki mi Yennu sɔnu fanue ki boontib. Bi para ni, bi tumii nianchiauŋo, ki te ki Yennu mɔtomm be bi paak.

ref: They have left the true road and have gone down the wrong path by following the example of the prophet Balaam. He was the son of Beor and loved what he got from being a crook.
text: Bi ki saak ki waa sɔnu nba tooki, ŋaan tokii sɔnganu; bi waa Beor bija Balaam nba din waa sɔnu nba nee. U din loon toonbiit nyɔɔte,

ref: But a donkey corrected him for this evil deed. It spoke to him with a human voice and made him stop his foolishness.
text: ki Yennu din kpaanɔ u toonbiit paaka, ki u boŋ din pak nanɔ nan nisaarik kunkɔr ki kpaanɔ a wun nyik u waat toona.

ref: These people are like dried up water holes and clouds blown by a windstorm. The darkest part of hell is waiting for them.
text: Niib maŋ booru tee kaman nan bunbunn nba kaa nyun nee; bi tee kaman nan sayuŋ nba ki wouŋ tuu gaanɔ nee. Yennu tan sa jiib ki lub bunbɔnchiauŋ ni, mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: They brag out loud about their stupid nonsense. And by being vulgar and crude, they trap people who have barely escaped from living the wrong kind of life.
text: Bi piak karinbaanii barii nba ki mɔk nyɔɔte a bin kpann niib nba yabit bi mɔŋ a bin nyik biitdamm, ki kpannib a bii tuun gbanant loonbiit kii tuun bonchonchonn toona,

ref: They promise freedom to everyone. But they are merely slaves of filthy living, because people are slaves of whatever controls them.
text: ki beerib a bi tan sa la mɔŋ-fatu, ŋaan ki bi mɔŋ tee biit daaba, kimaan bonsiar nba nyann nirɔ, li bont tuu dia nirɔ maŋ daabsin ni.

ref: When they learned about our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they escaped from the filthy things of this world. But they are again caught up and controlled by these filthy things, and now they are in worse shape than they were at first.
text: Niib nba din mi ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia, wunba tee ti Tinntɔɔ, ki poŋ din nyik biit nba be tingbouŋ nna na, li biit maŋ-i jiki taŋŋi jen ki nyannib, bi joontu tan sii bi ki gar bi pinpiik nba din tee biaŋinba nawa.

ref: They would have been better off if they had never known about the right way. Even after they knew what was right, they turned their backs on the holy commandments they were given.
text: Bi-i bo-i kii mi popeenn binbeŋ, li bo sii sɔ nan ba bannir ŋaan ji ki sak barŋanii nba ki bi din gbat na.

ref: What happened to them is just like the true saying, “A dog will come back to lick up its own vomit. A pig that has been washed will roll in the mud.”
text: Bi tuun gbeent barjokit nba yet biaŋinba nee a “Bɔke tuu ŋmat ki di u mɔŋ siit,” ki “Doorik-i wuur nyun ŋaan u tuu ŋmat ki gbar u mɔŋ nan bakire.”

ref: what God's prophets said would happen. You must never forget what the holy prophets taught in the past. And you must remember what the apostles told you our Lord and Savior has commanded us to do.
text: N loon ki yii tian mɔmaan nba ki Yennu sɔkiniinbaŋamm din pak li po yaayoo ni na, kii bia tian sennii nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ, wunba tee ti Tinntɔɔ na nba din pak, ki timm nba tee u toomiinba din beti na po.

ref: But first you must realize that in the last days some people won't think about anything except their own selfish desires. They will make fun of you
text: Sinsinn, n loon ki yii tian nan nisiab nba waa bi para loonbiit tan sii be dajoontii ni, ki tan sii sarikiti,

ref: and say, “Didn't your Lord promise to come back? Yet the first leaders have already died, and the world hasn't changed a bit.”
text: ki yeen a, “Yisa Masia nba din senn mɔsonn a u tan sa jen na, ŋɔɔ lia-aa? Ti baanba nba poŋ din kpowa nan dinna, bonsiar kur kpan tee kaman nan la poŋ tuu tee biaŋinba dunia pinpiik ni nee.”

ref: They will say this because they want to forget that long ago the heavens and the earth were made at God's command. The earth came out of water and was made from water.
text: Li niib ki saak bin tiar nan Yennu din yet mɔmaame ki nan sanpaak nan tiŋ yaayoo ni; ki din nyinn tiŋ na nyun ni ki te ki li be nyun na sinsuuk ni,

ref: Later it was destroyed by the waters of a mighty flood.
text: ki bia din te ki nyun dii tingbouŋ maŋ ki li biir.

ref: But God has commanded the present heavens and earth to remain until the day of judgment. Then they will be set on fire, and ungodly people will be destroyed.
text: Ŋaan ŋɔɔ Yennu mɔmaame bia lek sii dia sanpaak nan tiŋ nba be mɔtana na mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki u tan sa te ki li kura-n di muu, ki biir biitdamm, kimaan li daar u tan sa bu bi buut ki biirib.

ref: The Lord isn't slow about keeping his promises, as some people think he is. In fact, God is patient, because he wants everyone to turn from sin and no one to be lost.
text: Nisiab dukii a li tan sa yukir nan ti Yomdaanɔ-n tun mɔsonn nba ki u din senn na, ŋaan li ki tee nna; li tee a u mɔk sukuru nani; u ki loon ki sɔɔ-n tan mann kɔɔ muu ni, u loon ki sɔɔ kur-n nyik u biite ki la tinnu.
