ref: The day of the Lord's return will surprise us like a thief. The heavens will disappear with a loud noise, and the heat will melt the whole universe. Then the earth and everything on it will be seen for what they are.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ daar maŋ baaru tan sii tee kaman nan nanyukɔɔ baaru nee. Li daar maŋ, ki sanpaapo sa gbenn nan fujaann; ki muu nba di bonchiann tan sa di sanpaapo bonsiar kur, ki tingbouŋ na nan bonsiar kur nba be leŋ tan sa di muue ki gbenn.

ref: Everything will be destroyed. So you should serve and honor God by the way you live.
text: Ki bont na kur nba tan sa di muu nna na, i dukin sɔɔ li ŋan ki yii tee niboor bee mani? Li ŋan ki yii mɔk binbenyeeŋ, kii tiin Yennu,

ref: You should look forward to the day when God judges everyone, and you should try to make it come soon. On that day the heavens will be destroyed by fire, and everything else will melt in the heat.
text: kii guu Yennu daar maŋ, ki yabir i mɔŋ paak ki daar maŋ-n baar yian. Li daare ki sanpaapo tan sa kab ki biir, ki muu na tonu tan sa nann sanpaapo bonnankaa.

ref: But God has promised us a new heaven and a new earth, where justice will rule. We are really looking forward to this!
text: Ŋaan ti guu yoo nba ki sanpaak paann nan tiŋ paann tan sii be kaman nan Yennu nba din senn mɔsonn nba na, ki popeenn toona kuukɔɔ sii be leŋ maŋ.

ref: My friends, while you are waiting, you should make certain the Lord finds you pure, spotless, and living at peace.
text: N naa walonkai, ya guu bona maŋ na paak, yii yabir i mɔŋ paak kii mɔk parmaasir, nan binbenyeeŋ, ki daa mɔk biit Yennu boori.

ref: Don't forget that the Lord is patient because he wants people to be saved. This is also what our dear friend Paul said when he wrote you with the wisdom God had given him.
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ nba di sukuru ki daa ki jen mɔtana na, u loon ki yin bann nan u teen niibe yaak a bin la tinnu sinsinn na ki wun jen. Ti naa bilonkak Pɔɔl mun daan sɔb gbounsiɔk ki turi ki beti linba nawa, kaman nan Yennu nba turɔ yan ki u dia biaŋinba.

ref: Paul talks about these same things in all his letters, but part of what he says is hard to understand. Some ignorant and unsteady people even destroy themselves by twisting what he said. They do the same thing with other Scriptures too.
text: U gbant kur ni, u din sɔb ki piak Yennu daar maŋ po labaare, ki wanti li labaar. Labasia be u gbant na ni ki paar nan nirɔ-n bann li paak, ki nisiab nba ki mɔk yan ki bia ki mɔk ba see siami, bi jikitir ki kpintir faake, kaman nan ba tuu kpintir Yennu gbouŋ maŋ paak bolia ni biaŋinba na. Li paake te ki Yennu tan sa dat bi tuba.

ref: My dear friends, you have been warned ahead of time! So don't let the errors of evil people lead you down the wrong path and make you lose your balance.
text: N naa walonkai, i poŋ mi ba tee biaŋinba. Yimm nba waa barmɔnii na, yi bann i yan ni man, ki daa waa biitdamm faak sɔnii na, ŋaan yabir ki daa baa i sinsee boa.

ref: Let the gift of undeserved grace and the understanding that come from our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ help you keep on growing. Praise Jesus now and forever! Amen.
text: Yi ŋamm gaar ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia wunba tee ti Tinntɔɔ na sommir ki pukin man, kii tumii u mɔmaan na. Ŋɔɔe yen dontir ki jikit saa nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: The Word that gives life was from the beginning, and this is the one our message is about. Our ears have heard, our eyes have seen, and our hands have touched this Word.
text: N sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teeni a n wanni wunba ki bi yiu Yennu Mɔb nba teen niib manfoa na poe. Yoo nba ki dunia din tan piint namu na, ŋaan sɔɔ ki Mɔb maŋ poŋ bee. Mɔmaan maŋ nba din sik tingbouŋ na ni yoonba, ki ti gbiintir u pinpauk, ki bia laatɔ nan ti ninbina ki goriiɔ, ki ti nii bia siitɔ,

ref: The one who gives life appeared! We saw it happen, and we are witnesses to what we have seen. Now we are telling you about this eternal life that was with the Father and appeared to us.
text: ki u din fiit ki ti set lau, ki mɔɔntir li siara ki teeni ki jikit saa manfoor nba kaa gbennu po. Manfoor maŋe din be ti Baa Yennu peŋ, ki ti Baa maŋ jiiu ki fiitɔ ki wannit.

ref: We are telling you what we have seen and heard, so you may share in this life with us. And we share in it with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text: Linba ki ti la ki bia gbate, ki ti mɔɔntir ki teeni na, a yin fit lian taan nant mɔtaauk ni. Barmɔniie setik, ti mɔtaauk ni, ti taant nan ti Baa Yennu nan u Bija Yisa Masia,

ref: We are writing to tell you these things, because this makes us truly happy. God Is Light
text: ki ti loon ki ti kur-n ŋamm la parpeenn bonchiann. Li paake teen ki n sɔbin teeni na. Yentu po

ref: Jesus told us God is light and doesn't have any darkness in him. Now we are telling you.
text: Tɔn, barii nba ki ti din gbat ŋɔɔ Yisa Masia boor ni ki mɔɔntir ki teeni na, li tee nnee: Yennu tee yentue, ki bunbɔnn kaa u ni.

ref: If we say we share in life with God and keep on living in the dark, we are lying and are not living by the truth.
text: Ki ti-i yaa a timm nan Yennu mɔk mɔtaauk nan leeb, ŋaan ki bunbɔnn be ti ni, ŋann ti fa faake ti mɔi ni, ki ti toona nee set want nan ti fa faak.

ref: But if we live in the light, as God does, we share in life with each other. And the blood of his Son Jesus washes all our sins away.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki ti waa yentu sɔnu yoo kur, kaman nan Yennu nba be yentu ni biaŋinba na, ŋann ti mɔk mɔtaauk nan ti leeb, ki u Bija Yisa sɔme yentir ti para ni biit kur.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we are fooling ourselves, and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Tɔn, ti-i yaa a ti ki mɔk biitie, ŋann ti kpann ti mɔŋe, ki barmɔnii kaa ti ni.

ref: But if we confess our sins to God, he can always be trusted to forgive us and take our sins away.
text: Ŋaan ti-i yɔɔ nyint ti toonbiit ki teen Yennu, tii mi nan u sii nyikit chabit ti toonbiit, ki bia yentir ti para ni biit kur, kimaan u tee barmɔnii daanɔ ki bia tee popeendaanɔ.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we make God a liar, and his message isn't in our hearts.
text: Ti-i yaa a ti ki tuun toonbiitie, ŋann nna ti teenɔ faak daanɔe na, ki u maan maŋ kaa ti ni.

ref: My children, I am writing this so you won't sin. But if you do sin, Jesus Christ always does the right thing, and he will speak to the Father for us.
text: N waas, n ki loon ki yii tuun toonbiiti. Li paake te ki n sɔbin ki teeni na, ŋaan li-i tee ki sɔɔ tun toonbiit, ti mɔk sindantinnɔ ti Baa Yennu boor ni, ki ŋɔɔe tee Yisa Masia, wunba tee popeendaanɔ na.

ref: When we obey God, we are sure we know him.
text: Ti-i dia Yennu sennii, ŋanne sa wann nan ti set miu,

ref: But if we claim to know him and don't obey him, we are lying and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: ki li-i tee ki sɔɔ yet a “N miu,” ŋaan bia ki dia u sennii, li yɔɔ tee faak daanɔe, ki barmɔnii kaa u ni.

ref: My dear friends, I am not writing to give you a new commandment. It is the same one you were first given, and it is the message you heard.
text: N walonkai, ma sɔbin loomm sennu nba ki teeni na, li ki tee sennpaanu kaa, ŋaan li tee sennu nba ki i din dɔŋ gbia ki li yukir na poe, ki sennkper maŋe tee Yisa maan nba ki i din gbia na.

ref: But it really is a new commandment, and you know its true meaning, just as Christ does. You can see the darkness fading away and the true light already shining.
text: Ŋaan mɔtana li bia daa tee kaman nan sennpaann nee ki n sɔbin ki teeni, kimaan yoo kpintire, ki bunbɔnn yentir, ki niib nunii po piint yentir nan Yisa barŋanii. Sennu nee ki Yisa binbeŋ ni din want nan mɔnii, ki i binbeŋ ni mun bia want nnee.

ref: If we claim to be in the light and hate someone, we are still in the dark.
text: Li-i tee ki wunba yaa a u nɔɔk ni mɔk yentu, ŋaan nân u naa bik, ŋann nna bunbɔnn lek daa be u ni;

ref: But if we love others, we are in the light, and we don't cause problems for them.
text: ki daanɔ nba loon u naa bik, u nɔɔk ni mɔk yentue, ki li paak, siar kaa u ni ki sa te ki wun tun biiti.

ref: If we hate others, we are living and walking in the dark. We don't know where we are going, because we can't see in the dark.
text: Ŋaan wunba nân u naa bik, bunbɔnn be u ni, ki u benu ni mun bia bɔne, ki bunbɔnn maŋ dɔkin u numm po, ki u ki laat wa saa siaminba po.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because your sins have been forgiven in the name of Christ.
text: N waas, Yennu nyik chabi i biita, kimaan Yisa Masia sann ni; li paake te ki n sɔbin ki teeni na.

ref: Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you have defeated the evil one.
text: N chanbaanba, i mi Yisa nba poŋ din yɔɔ be mɔkmɔk nan mɔkmɔk na; li paake te ki n sɔbin ki teeni na. Naasimm, i nyann Sintaanii, bonbiiuk daanɔ nawa; li paake te ki n sɔbin ki teeni na.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because you have known the Father. Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you are strong. God's message is firm in your hearts, and you have defeated the evil one.
text: N waas, ya mi ti Baa Yennu na, li paake te ki n sɔbin ki teeni na. N chanbaanba, ya mi Yisa nba poŋ din yɔɔ be mɔkmɔk nan mɔkmɔk na, li paake te ki n sɔbin ki teeni na. Naasimm, ya mɔk paŋ, ki Yennu mɔmaan bia be i ni, ki i nyann Sintaanii, bonbiiuk daanɔ maŋ na, li paake te ki n sɔbin ki teeni na.

ref: Don't love the world or anything that belongs to the world. If you love the world, you cannot love the Father.
text: I daa loon tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb sɔnu. I bia daa loon tingbouŋ na bona. Li-i tee ki daansɔɔ nba loon tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb sɔnii, u ki loon ti Baa Yennu.

ref: Our foolish pride comes from this world, and so do our selfish desires and our desire to have everything we see. None of this comes from the Father.
text: Niib mɔk niauŋ nan bin tun bi mɔŋ loomm, ki bia nian bona nba ki bi laat na paak, ki bia mɔk parbifaant, ŋaan linba na kur lek tee tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb niauŋo; li ki nyii ti Baa Yennu boor ni kaa.

ref: The world and the desires it causes are disappearing. But if we obey God, we will live forever. The Enemy of Christ
text: Tingbouŋ na tan sa gbenn, ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb niauŋ na mun bia sa gbenn, ŋaan wunba tuun Yennu yanbɔɔtoona, ŋɔɔe tan sii be mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Yisa Masia datai po

ref: Children, this is the last hour. You heard that the enemy of Christ would appear at this time, and many of Christ's enemies have already appeared. So we know the last hour is here.
text: N waas, dunia gbennu yoo baat. I poŋ mi gbat a Masia datasɔɔ sa baar; ki mɔtana, Masia datasiab bonchiann poŋ baar na, ki ŋanne te ki ti set bann nan dunia gbennu yoo baat.

ref: These people came from our own group, yet they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But they left, which proves they did not belong to our group.
text: Masia datai maŋe nyii ti ni; ŋaan bi ki tee ti yabi. Bi-i bo-i tee ti yabie, timm nan ŋammii bo sii lakin be; ŋaan bi nyikita, ki li want nan bi kur ki tee ti yab setik nan mɔnii.

ref: Christ, the Holy One, has blessed you, and now all of you understand.
text: Yisa Masia, popeendaanɔ nee, sikin u Seyeeŋ i paak, ki u te ki i kur mi wannu nba took.

ref: I did not need to write you about the truth, since you already know it. You also know that liars do not belong to the truth.
text: Tɔn, li ki tee a i ki mi wannu nba took kaa te ki n sɔbin ki teeni na, ŋaan li tee ya poŋ mi wannu maŋ na paak, ki bia mun mi nan faak wannu fɔk nan barmɔnii wannu.

ref: And a liar is anyone who says Jesus isn't truly Christ. Anyone who says this is an enemy of Christ and rejects both the Father and the Son.
text: Ki ŋmee tee faak daanɔ? Li tee wunba yaa a Yisa ki tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia wunba ki u din tumɔ tingbouŋ na ni nae. Ŋɔɔe tee Yisa dataak maŋ, ki yêen ti Baa Yennu nan u Bija Yisa mun biak.

ref: If we reject the Son, we reject the Father. But if we say we accept the Son, we have the Father.
text: Daanɔ nba yêen Yennu Bija Yisa, ti Baa Yennu ki be nanɔ, ŋaan wunba piak ki yeen a u teen yada nan Yisa tee Yennu Bijae, ti Baa Yennu be nanɔ.

ref: Keep thinking about the message you first heard, and you will always be one in your heart with the Son and with the Father.
text: Ya din sint gbat Yisa maan nba pinpiik ni na, yii dia nna man fanu. Li-i tee ki i dia maan maŋ, i sii gaa ti Baa Yennu nan u Bija Yisa paake,

ref: I am writing to warn you about those people who are misleading you.
text: N sɔbin linba na ki teeni ki chekinti ki jikit saa niib nba yaa bin kpanni na poe;

ref: But Christ has blessed you with the Holy Spirit. Now the Spirit stays in you, and you don't need any teachers. The Spirit is truthful and teaches you everything. So stay one in your heart with Christ, just as the Spirit has taught you to do. Children of God
text: ŋaan yimm ŋarin, Yisa sikin u Seyeeŋ maŋ i paaka. Seyeeŋ maŋ nba biar be i ni na, i ji kii loon ki sɔɔ-n wanniini, kimaan Seyeeŋ maŋ mɔŋe wanti bont kura, ki u wannu maŋe tee barmɔnii wannu, ki li ki tee faak wannu kaa. Li paak, yii gaa Yisa paak man kaman nan Seyeeŋ maŋ nba wanti biaŋinba na.

ref: Children, stay one in your hearts with Christ. Then when he returns, we will have confidence and won't have to hide in shame.
text: Tɔn, n waas, yii gaa Yisa paak man. Li-i tee ki i yɔɔ gaa u paakie, ti sii mɔk parcheenn, ki ti kan di fei u boor yoo nba ki u tan sa jen na.

ref: You know Christ always does right and everyone who does right is a child of God.
text: I mi nan Yisa Masia tee popeendaanɔe; li paake, ki i mun bann nan binba kur tuun popeenn toona, bi mun tee Yennu waase.

ref: Think how much the Father loves us. He loves us so much that he lets us be called his children, as we truly are. But since the people of this world did not know who Christ is, they don't know who we are.
text: Lek gotir man loonbooru nba ki ti Baa Yennu mantik loont na. U loomm maŋ mantik ji sa yabite, ki u jiit ki teenit u waas, ki ti set mun tee u waase. Ŋaan tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb ki mi ta tee niboorsiab nba, kimaan bi ki mi Yennu.

ref: My dear friends, we are already God's children, though what we will be hasn't yet been seen. But we do know when Christ returns, we will be like him, because we will see him as he truly is.
text: N walonkai, mɔtana ti tee Yennu waase, ŋaan li lek daa ki wann yent nan ta tan sii be biaŋinba. Ŋaan ta daa mi linbae na, a Yisa-i taŋŋi jeni, ti tan sa la wa set tee biaŋinba, ki li paak, ti tan sa kpint ki naan nan ŋɔɔ nee.

ref: This hope makes us keep ourselves holy, just as Christ is holy.
text: Ki bia jenna, binba kur daan u jenu po maŋ, ŋamme dia bi mɔŋ fanu, kaman nan wa be fanu biaŋinba na.

ref: Everyone who sins breaks God's law, because sin is the same as breaking God's law.
text: Binba kur tuun biit, ŋamme biir Yennu sennu, kimaan toonbiit tumu biir Yennu sennue.

ref: You know Christ came to take away our sins. He isn't sinful,
text: I mi nan Yisa Masia din sik baar tingbouŋ na ni a wun nyinn niib biite; ki biit kaa u ni.

ref: and people who stay one in their hearts with him won't keep on sinning. If they do keep on sinning, they don't know Christ, and they have never seen him.
text: Ki wunba kur gaa u paak, u ki tuun biiti. Ŋaan wunba tuun biit, u daa ki bann Yisa nba tee wunba, ki bia ki miu.

ref: Children, don't be fooled. Anyone who does right is good, just like Christ himself.
text: N waas, i daa saak ki sɔɔ-n kpanniini; daanɔ nba tuun popeenn toona, ŋɔɔe tee popeendaanɔ, kaman nan Yisa nba tee popeendaanɔ na.

ref: Anyone who keeps on sinning belongs to the devil. He has sinned from the beginning, but the Son of God came to destroy all that he has done.
text: Sintaanii poŋ tee biitdaanɔe piinu niwa nan mɔtana, ki daanɔ nba tuun biit, ŋɔɔe tee Sintaanii yɔɔ. Ŋaan ki Yennu Bija Yisa sik baar a wun biir Sintaanii toona.

ref: God's children cannot keep on being sinful. His life-giving power lives in them and makes them his children, so they cannot keep on sinning.
text: Binba kur tee Yennu waas nyii bi Baa Yennue, ki ki tuun biiti. Bi ki fit tuun biiti, kimaan ba gaar bi Baa Yennu binbeŋ na paak.

ref: You can tell God's children from the devil's children, because those who belong to the devil refuse to do right or to love each other. Love Each Other
text: Ŋaan binba kur ki tuun popeenn toona, bi ki tee Yennu waas kaa. Ki bia jenna, binba kur mun bia ki loon bi naa waasi, bi bia ki tee Yennu waas kaa. Ŋannii want binba tee Yennu waas nan binba tee Sintaanii waas. Ta sii loon ti leeb biaŋinba

ref: From the beginning you were told we must love each other.
text: Ya din dɔŋ gbat Yisa maan nba pinpiik ni na tee nnee: a tii loon ti leeb man.

ref: Don't be like Cain, who belonged to the devil and murdered his own brother. Why did he murder him? He did it because his brother was good, and he was evil.
text: Li ki ŋan ki tii be kaman nan Keenn nba din be biaŋinba na; ŋɔɔ din tee bonbiiuk daanɔ Sintaanii yɔɔe, ki din kpii u waarɔ. Bee paake te ki u din kpiiu? U din la u waarɔ tuun toonŋana, ŋaan ki u yar bi.

ref: My friends, don't be surprised if the people of this world hate you.
text: Tɔn, n naa waas, li-i tee ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb nami, i daa te ki li teei bakitnauŋu.

ref: Our love for each other proves we have gone from death to life. But if you don't love each other, you are still under the power of death.
text: Ta loon ti naa walees na, ŋannii want nan ti nyii kuun paŋ ni, ki be manfoor nba kaa gbennu ni. Ŋaan daanɔ nba ki mɔk li loonbooru, ŋɔɔ ŋarin biar be kuun paŋ nie.

ref: If you hate each other, you are murderers, and we know murderers do not have eternal life.
text: Ki bia jenna, wunba mun nân u naa bik, u tee nikpiiruko; ki i mi nan manfoor nba kaa gbennu kaa nikpiiruk ni.

ref: We know what love is because Jesus gave his life for us. This is why we must give our lives for each other.
text: Gotir man, Yisa nba din gaar ti paak ki kpo nee wantit loomm nba tee biaŋinba; ki li mun kpaa talase ki tii nyint ti manfoa ti naa waas paak.

ref: If we have all we need and see one of our own people in need, we must have pity on that person, or else we cannot say we love God.
text: Daanɔ nba mɔk linba jaŋitɔ, ŋaan la ki u naa bik kɔŋ siar, ki u baka kaa u ni, Yennu loomm kaa daanɔ maŋ ni.

ref: Children, you show love for others by truly helping them, and not merely by talking about it.
text: N waas, ti loomm maŋ daa be ti mɔi ni kuukɔɔ kaa; see ki lii tee barmɔnii loomm nba sommit niibe. Biaŋinba ki ti sii mɔk parcheenn Yennu boor

ref: When we love others, we know we belong to the truth, and we feel at ease in the presence of God.
text: Li-i tee ki ti tuun nna, ŋanne ki ti sa bann nan ti waa barmɔnii wannu, ki bia sii mɔk parcheenn Yennu boor.

ref: But even if we don't feel at ease, God is greater than our feelings, and he knows everything.
text: Li-i tee ki ti dudukit wannit nan ti mɔk biit, ŋaant man ki ti tiar nan Yennu chee ti dudukit ki bia mi barii kura.

ref: Dear friends, if we feel at ease in the presence of God, we will have the courage to come near him.
text: Tɔn, n walonkai, ti dudukit-i kii wannit nan ti tun bonkpetiri, ŋann ti mɔk parcheenn Yennu boor.

ref: He will give us whatever we ask, because we obey him and do what pleases him.
text: Ki bia jenna, ti-i dia u sennii ki tuun linba pent u parie, ŋannii sa te ki u teent linba ki ti meiɔ na.

ref: God wants us to have faith in his Son Jesus Christ and to love each other. This is also what Jesus taught us to do.
text: Ki nee tee u sennu maŋ, a ti teen u Bija Yisa Masia yada kii loon ti leeb kaman nan wa din sennit sennu biaŋinba na.

ref: If we obey God's commandments, we will stay one in our hearts with him, and he will stay one with us. The Spirit he has given us is proof that we are one with him.
text: Binba kur dia u sennii maŋ, ŋamme gaa u paak, ki u mun bia be bi ni. Ŋannii teen ki ti bann nan u yɔɔ be ti ni, kimaan u Seyeeŋ be ti ni.

ref: Dear friends, don't believe everyone who claims to have the Spirit of God. Test them all to find out if they really do come from God. Many false prophets have already gone out into the world,
text: N walonkai, i daa tee nisiab kur nba yaa a bi mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋ na maan yada, ŋaan bikintib man, ki got laan bi sei maŋ nyii Yennu boor ni amii lee, kimaan faak sɔkiniinba bonchiann nba kpann niib na poŋ baar na ki yat lin niib ni.

ref: But when someone doesn't say this about Jesus, you know this person has a spirit that doesn't come from God and is the enemy of Christ. You knew this enemy was coming into the world and now is already here.
text: Ŋaan wunba ki seek piak u ni, ki u yaa a u ki teen yada nan bi mar Yisa nisaarike, ŋɔɔe ki mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋi. Li daanɔ seeke tee Yisa dataak seek na. Bi din poŋ beti nan Yisa dataak maŋ sa baar na, ki mɔtana u poŋ set baar tingbouŋ na niwa.

ref: Children, you belong to God, and you have defeated these enemies. God's Spirit is in you and is more powerful than the one who is in the world.
text: N waas, yimm tee Yennu yabe, ki Yennu Seyeeŋ be i ni, ki u mɔk paŋ ki gar Sintaanii wunba be tingbouŋ na ni. Ŋanne te ki i nyann faak sɔkiniinba maŋ.

ref: These enemies belong to this world, and the world listens to them, because they speak its language.
text: Ŋamme tee tingbouŋ na yab. Li paak te ki bi piak tingbouŋ na niauŋ po, ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb fiin bi po.

ref: We belong to God, and everyone who knows God will listen to us. But the people who don't know God won't listen to us. This is how we can tell the Spirit that speaks the truth from the one that tells lies.
text: Ŋaan timm ŋarin tee Yennu yabe; ki binba mi Yennu, ŋamme fiin ti maan po. Ŋaan binba ki tee Yennu yabi, bi ki fiin ti maan po. Ŋanne sa te ki ti sa bann wunba mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋ nba want barmɔnii wannu, nan wunba mɔk Sintaanii seek nba want faak wannu. Yennu tee loomm

ref: My dear friends, we must love each other. Love comes from God, and when we love each other, it shows we have been given new life. We are now God's children, and we know him.
text: N walonkai, tii loon ti leeb man, kimaan loomm tee Yennu yare, ki binba mɔk loomm, ŋamme tee Yennu waas ki bia miu.

ref: God is love, and anyone who doesn't love others has never known him.
text: Yennu tee loomme, ki binba ki mɔk loommu, bi ki miu.

ref: God showed his love for us when he sent his only Son into the world to give us life.
text: Yennu din sikin u Bijayemmir tingbouŋ na ni a ti la manfoor u paak, ki nee fiit wannit nan Yennu set loont.

ref: Dear friends, since God loved us this much, we must love each other.
text: N walonkai, Yennu nba loont biaŋinba na, li kpaa talas ki tii munii loon ti leeb nna.

ref: No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is truly in our hearts.
text: Sɔɔ kaa ki mi la Yennu nan u ninbina, ŋaan ti-i loon ti leebie, Yennu sii yɔɔ be ti ni, ki ti sii mɔk loomm nba ki u teent na nan barmɔniie.

ref: God has given us his Spirit. This is how we know we are one with him, just as he is one with us.
text: Ŋɔɔe bia sikin u Seyeeŋ ki turit, ki ŋanne te ki ti bann nan ti gaa u paak, ki u mun bia be ti ni.

ref: God sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. We saw his Son and are now telling others about him.
text: Ki linba pukin biak, ti din laat u Bija, ki mɔtana ti mɔɔntir nan u Baa Yennu din sikinɔ tingbouŋ na ni, a wuu tee tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb Tinntɔɔ.

ref: God stays one with everyone who openly says Jesus is the Son of God. This is how we stay one with God
text: Wunba yaa a u teen yada nan Yisa tee Yennu Bija, ŋɔɔe ki Yennu be u ni, ki u mun bia gaa Yennu paak.

ref: and are sure God loves us. God is love. If we keep on loving others, we will stay one in our hearts with God, and he will stay one with us.
text: Ki ti mɔŋ mɔŋ mun bann Yennu nba loonit biaŋinba, ki bia teen yada nan u set loonit. Yennu tee loomm, ki daanɔ nba yɔɔ mɔk loomm nna, ŋɔɔe gaa Yennu paak, ki Yennu mun bia be u ni.

ref: If we truly love others and live as Christ did in this world, we won't be worried about the day of judgment.
text: Ki bia jenna, li-i tee ki ti waa loomm sɔnu nan barmɔnii, Yennu barbuut daar-i taŋŋi baar, ti kii mɔk jijeeti, kimaan Yisa binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba na, ti binbeŋ mun tee nnee tingbouŋ na ni.

ref: A real love for others will chase those worries away. The thought of being punished is what makes us afraid. It shows we have not really learned to love.
text: Ti-i mɔk loomm, jijeet sii kaa. Li-i tee ki loomm mantik be nan mɔnii mɔnii, jijeet kan la yaak kii be ti ni, kimaan tubdatu paake ki ti tuu jee. Li-i tee ki nirɔ jee jijeeti, u ki mɔk loomm nan barmɔnii.

ref: We love because God loved us first.
text: Yennue sint loonit; ŋanne teen ki ti mun mɔk loomm.

ref: But if we say we love God and don't love each other, we are liars. We cannot see God. So how can we love God, if we don't love the people we can see?
text: Daanɔ nba yaa a u loon Yennu, ŋaan bia nân u naa bik, u tee faak daanɔe. Li-i tee ki u ki loon u naa bik nba ki u laatɔ nan u ninbina, nlee ki u sii fit loon Yennu nba ki u ki laatɔ nan u ninbina?

ref: The commandment that God has given us is: “Love God and love each other!”
text: Ki Yisa din senn sennu ki yet a wunba loon Yennu, see ki wuu biakii loon u naa bik mun.

ref: If we believe Jesus is truly Christ, we are God's children. Everyone who loves the Father will also love his children.
text: Binba kur tee yada nan Yisa tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia na, li yabe tee Yennu waas. Ki daanɔ nba loon nisɔɔ, u bia sii loon nirɔ maŋ waase.

ref: If we love and obey God, we know we will love his children.
text: Li-i tee ki ti loon Yennu ki tuun linba ki u sennii nba want biaŋinba, ŋanne sa te ki tin bann nan ti loon Yennu waas;

ref: We show our love for God by obeying his commandments, and they are not hard to follow.
text: ki li-i biakii tee ki ti dia u sennu, ŋanne want nan ti loonɔ. Ki bia jenna, u sennu maŋ ki paari,

ref: Every child of God can defeat the world, and our faith is what gives us this victory.
text: kimaan binba kur tee Yennu waas, ŋammii fit nyant tingbouŋ na ni jinjammii. Linba te ki ti nyant tingbouŋ na jinjammii na tee ti yada teenue.

ref: No one can defeat the world without having faith in Jesus as the Son of God. Who Jesus Is
text: Ki ŋmee sii nyant jinjammii maŋi? Wunba teen yada nan Yisa tee Yennu Bija.

ref: Water and blood came out from the side of Jesus Christ. It wasn't just water, but water and blood. The Spirit tells about this, because the Spirit is truthful.
text: Ŋɔɔ Yisa Masiae tee wunba din baar nan nyun nan sɔn; u din ki baar nan nyun kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan u din baar nan sɔn mun biak. Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ tee siaradaanɔ, kimaan Seyeeŋ na tee barmɔnii daanɔ.

ref: In fact, there are three who tell about it.
text: Tɔn, binba tee siaradamm maŋ tee banntaae;

ref: They are the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and they all agree.
text: ki damm maŋe tee Yennu Seyeeŋ, nan nyun maŋ, nan sɔn maŋ; ki bi banntaa maŋ lakin taan mɔyomm ki tee siara.

ref: We believe what people tell us. But we can trust what God says even more, and God is the one who has spoken about his Son.
text: Nnee ki Yennu teen siara u Bija Yisa po, ki u siara maŋ chee nirɔ yar. Ti-i tee nirɔ siara yadaie, ŋann nna ti sii tee Yennu siarae yada ki gar nirɔ yar.

ref: If we have faith in God's Son, we have believed what God has said. But if we don't believe what God has said about his Son, it is the same as calling God a liar.
text: Wunba teen Yennu Bija maŋ yada, ŋɔɔe mɔk Yennu siara maŋ u par ni; ki wunba ki teen u siara maŋ yada, u teenɔ faak daanɔe na, kimaan u ki teen yada nan siara nba ki Yennu teen u Bija po na, a li tee mɔnii. Manfoor nba kaa gbennu po

ref: God has also said he gave us eternal life and this life comes to us from his Son.
text: Yennu siara maŋ tee nnee, a u turit manfoor nba kaa gbennu, ki manfoor maŋ nyi u Bija Yisa nie.

ref: And so, if we have God's Son, we have this life. But if we don't have the Son, we don't have this life. Knowing about Eternal Life
text: Wunba gaa Yennu Bija na paak, ŋɔɔe mun mɔk manfoor maŋ; ŋaan wunba ki gaa Yennu Bija maŋ paaki, u ki mɔk li manfoori.

ref: All of you have faith in the Son of God, and I have written to let you know you have eternal life.
text: N sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen yimm nba teen Yennu Bija yada, kimaan n loon ki i bann nan i mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

ref: We are certain God will hear our prayers when we ask for what pleases him.
text: Tɔn, ti mei Yennu nan parcheenn, kimaan ti mi nan li-i tee ki ti meiɔ linba ki u loon wun turit na po, tii mi nan u gaan ti miaru.

ref: And if we know God listens when we pray, we are sure our prayers have already been answered.
text: Ti-i mi nan u set gaan ti miaru, tin bann nan ti poŋ gaar linba ki ti meiɔ li po nawa.

ref: Suppose you see one of our people commit a sin that isn't a deadly sin. You can pray, and this person will be given eternal life. But the sin must not be one that is deadly.
text: Sɔɔ-i laat u naa bik ki u tuun biit nba kan baar nan kuumi, li daanɔ-ii mei Yennu u paak, ki Yennu-n chab tur biitdaanɔ maŋ manfoor, kimaan u chabit manfoa ki teen binba tuun biit nba ki tee kuun toonni. Ŋaan toonbisiar be, ki tee kuun toonn. N ki yaa a yii mei Yennu kii teen binba tuun biit maŋ booru kaa.

ref: Everything that is wrong is sin, but not all sins are deadly.
text: Toona kur nba ki ŋan lek tee biite, ŋaan toonbisai be ki kan baar nan kuumi.

ref: We are sure God's children do not keep on sinning. God's own Son protects them, and the devil cannot harm them.
text: Ki ti mi nan damm nba nyii Yennu ni, bi ki tuun biiti, kimaan Yennu Bija Yisa guub, ki bonbiiuk daanɔ Sintaanii kan teemm siari.

ref: We are certain we come from God and the rest of the world is under the power of the devil.
text: Ki ti bia mi nan timm Masiaweira tee Yennu yabe, ŋaan tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb kur nba ki tee Masiaweira na be Sintaanii nuu ni.

ref: We know that Jesus Christ the Son of God has come and has shown us the true God. And because of Jesus, we now belong to the true God who gives eternal life.
text: Ti mi nan Yennu Bija Yisa din sik tingbouŋ na ni ki tan turit yan a tii bant barmɔnii Yennu. Ta gaa nan u Bija Yisa Masia na paak, ŋanne bia te ki ti gaa nan u Baa Yennu paak; ki ŋɔɔe set tee barmɔnii Yennu, ki bia tee daanɔ nba teent manfoor nba kaa gbennu.

ref: Children, you must stay away from idols.
text: N waas, nyint i mɔŋ man nan pata ni.

ref: From the church leader. To a very special woman and her children. I truly love all of you, and so does everyone else who knows the truth.
text: Min Jɔɔnn nba tee Masiaweira saakɔɔ nee, sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen fin nba tee n niipoo nba ki Yennu gannɔ na, nan a waas. N looni nan barmɔnii, ŋaan li bia ki tee min kuukɔɔ kaa looniini. Binba kur mun saak Yennu barmɔnii maŋ wannu, bi mun bia looni nnee.

ref: We love you because the truth is now in our hearts, and it will be there forever.
text: Min nan ŋamme looni, kimaan ti kur nba taa barmɔnii wannu maŋ paak, ki barmɔnii wannu maŋ set sii yɔɔ be ti ni mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu.

ref: I pray that God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son will be kind and merciful to us! May they give us peace and truth and love. Truth and Love
text: Ti Baa Yennu nan u Bija Yisa Masia sommir nan bi ninbatinu nan bi parmaasir sii be ti paak, nan ta waa barmɔnii wannu nan loomm sɔnu biaŋinba na. Loomm sɔnu po

ref: I was very glad to learn that some of your children are obeying the truth, as the Father told us to do.
text: N daan mɔk parpeenn bonchiann nan ma daan la a wasiat nba ki bi waa barmɔnii wannu nba ki ti Baa Yennu sennit sennu na.

ref: Dear friend, I am not writing to tell you and your children to do something you have not done before. I am writing to tell you to love each other, which is the first thing you were told to do.
text: N niipoo, n meia, ŋaant ki ti kur-ii loon ti naa walees. Linba na ki tee sennpaanu kaa ki n sɔbani, ŋaan li tee sennu nba ki ti din dɔŋ gbia li po pinpiik niwa mɔkmɔk nan dinnae.

ref: Many liars have gone out into the world. These deceitful liars are saying Jesus Christ did not have a truly human body. But they are liars and the enemies of Christ.
text: Tɔn, kpinkpannii damm bonchiann poŋ baar ki yat saan siar po kur tingbouŋ na niwa, binba yaa a ŋamm ki teen yada a bi mar Yisa ki u tee nisaarik na. Li nibooru tee kpinkpannii damme, ki bia tee Yisa datai.

ref: So be sure not to lose what we have worked for. If you do, you won't be given your full reward.
text: Li paak, i mi man kii tan kɔŋ linba ki ti poŋ yabir i paak na, ki i tan sa la i nyɔɔt kur Yennu boor ni.

ref: Don't keep changing what you were taught about Christ, or else God will no longer be with you. But if you hold firmly to what you were taught, both the Father and the Son will be with you.
text: Daansɔɔ nba ki gaan Yisa Masia wannu bonŋann ŋaan pukii u mɔŋ yar, Yennu ki be nanɔ, ŋaan wunba saak wannu maŋ ki ki nyikiti, ti Baa Yennu nan u Bija Yisa Masia kur be u ni.

ref: If people won't agree to this teaching, don't welcome them into your home or even greet them.
text: Li-i tee ki daansɔɔ nba baar i boor ni, ŋaan ki want Yisa Masia wannu maŋ nba tooki, i daa gaanɔ i ŋei ni, ki bia taan daa foontɔ gbaa,

ref: Greeting them is the same as taking part in their evil deeds. Final Greetings
text: kimaan wunba foontɔ, u mɔŋ taant daanɔ maŋ biit poe na. Chabitii

ref: I have much more to tell you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. I want to come and talk to you in person, because this will make us really happy.
text: N mɔk mɔbona bonchiann ki loon ki n sɔb ki turi, ŋaan n ki loon ki n sɔb gbouŋ ni kaa ki turiini, ŋaan n mɔk dindann nan n sa baar ki gɔni, ki tin pak nan leeb mɔi ni, ki li-i tee ki li fit teen nna, li sa maŋ ti para ni bonchiann.

ref: Greetings from the children of your very special sister.
text: A niipoo wunba ki Yennu gannɔ na, u waas foonta.

ref: From the church leader. To my dear friend Gaius. I love you because we follow the truth,
text: Min Jɔɔnn nba tee Masiaweira saakɔɔ nee, sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen n yɔɔk fin Gaius, fin nba ki n nian nan mɔnii.

ref: dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit.
text: N yɔɔk, n mei Yennu a paak nan fii mɔk laafia, ki a bont kur-n somm fanu kaman nan a seek nba be fanu Yennu boor biaŋinba na.

ref: It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth.
text: N mɔk parpeenn bonchiann nan ti naa wasiat nba daan baar nna, ki tan betin a po na, nan biaŋinba ki a waa Yennu barmɔnii wannu na, ki a benu nee bia want li sɔnu nan mɔnii.

ref: Nothing brings me greater happiness than to hear that my children are obeying the truth. Working Together
text: Siar ki penn n par ni ki mantik baar ma gbat a n waas waa wannu nba took na. Yisa mɔɔntdamm sommir po

ref: Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before.
text: N yɔɔk, fa sommit ti naa waas nba tee saamm gbaa biaŋinba na, a tuun linba ŋan.

ref: They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve.
text: Ba tuu tan baar Masiaweira lakinu ni yoonba, bi tuu tiar a burchintuk maŋ po. Li paak, bi-i tuu kii baar a boor ni, ki a bia ŋammit bi sɔnii maŋ ki sommitibie, ŋann a tuun bonŋanne na. I diab bonŋann kimaan bi tee Yennu yabe.

ref: When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower.
text: La tee Yisa sann paak nee ki bi lin ki mɔɔntir u maan, ŋaan ki gaan siar binba ki tee Masiaweira na boor ni.

ref: We must support people like them, so we can take part in what they are doing to spread the truth.
text: Li paak, timm Masiaweira ŋarin, li kpaa talase nan tii gaan li nibooru saantuk. Ŋanne sa te ki timm nan ŋamm-ii taa Yisa barmɔnii wannu mɔɔntii. Jɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Diotrefes na po

ref: I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us.
text: N poŋ mi sɔb barsai nna ki tur Masiaweira nba be a boor ni na, ŋaan ki Diotrefes yêen n mɔb, kimaan u loon wun pur u mɔŋ nijaanne.

ref: So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
text: Li paak, mi-i taŋŋi baar, n sa fiit linba kur ki u tun, nan bonbisiar nan faak nba ki u pak ki biirit na. Ŋaan biit nba ki u tuun maŋ na, li lek daa ki gboo u numm gbaa. Ti naa waas nba tuu tan baar u boor ni, u bia tuu ki gaanibi, ki damm nba tuu loon bin gaar ti naa waas maŋ saantuk, u bia tuu ber nyinnib Masiaweira lakinu maŋ nie.

ref: Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
text: N yɔɔk, daa tokii sɔɔ biiti, ŋaan kii tuun linba ŋan. Wunba tuun bonŋann, u tee Yennu yɔɔe; ŋaan wunba tuun biit, u ki bann Yennu. Jɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Demetrius na po

ref: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true. Final Greetings
text: Sɔɔ kur piak Demetrius; ki u benu nee bia want nan u waa barmɔnii wannu; ki ti mɔŋ mun piakɔ; ki a mun mi nan ti set tee barmɔnii damme. Chabitii

ref: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink.
text: N bia mɔk maan bonchiann ki loon ki n pak ki tura, ŋaan n ki loon ki n sɔb gbouŋ ni kaa ki turani.

ref: I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
text: Ŋaan n mɔk dindann nan n tan sa baar ki la-a daa ŋannlelee na ni, ki tin tan pak nan leeb mɔi ni.

ref: I pray that God will bless you with peace! Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
text: Parmaasir-ii be a paak. Ti yɔɔsinba foonta. Ti yɔɔsinba nba mun bia be a boor ni na, a foont bi sɔɔ kur nan u sann yenɔ yenɔ.

ref: From Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and the brother of James. To all who are chosen and loved by God the Father and are kept safe by Jesus Christ.
text: Min Juud wunba tee Yisa Masia toontunnɔ, ki bia tee Jems naa bik nee sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen niib nba ki ti Baa Yennu gannib ki loomm, ki Yisa Masia guub na.

ref: I pray that God will greatly bless you with mercy, peace, and love! False Teachers
text: Yennu-n tini ninbaauk ki turi parmaasir nan loomm bonchiann. Faidamm labaar

ref: My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people.
text: N walonkai, n ninbinn daan mɔnn nan ki n sɔb gbouŋ ki turi ki saan gaan tinnu nba tee ti kur yar na po; ŋaan mɔtana li ji kpaa talas ki n sɔb ki kpaani ki yin yabir i mɔŋ ki dia yada labaar nba ki Yennu din tur u niib na fanu, ŋaan daa saak ki sɔɔ-n lebitir,

ref: Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
text: kimaan nisiab nba ki tiin Yennu na din kɔɔ i sinsuuk ni woonin nie, ki lebit ŋɔɔ Yennu ninbatinu labaar a bii tuun bi gbanant loonbiit; bi ki saak Yisa Masia mɔbi; ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔe tee ti yudaanɔ nan ti Yomdaanɔ. Li yukira ki biitdamm maŋ tubdatu poŋ sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni na.

ref: Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them.
text: Ma yaa n beti linba na, i poŋ gbat li kura, ki mir, ŋaan n loon ki n tiari li poe; ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu din fat u niib ki nyinnib Ijipt tiŋ ni, ŋaan li poorpo u din kpii bi siab nba din ki teenɔ yada na.

ref: You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment.
text: Nnee biak ki malakanba nba din ki dia yaak nba ki Yennu din turib na nan li sɔnu na, ki bia din ki biar be bi binbeboor sanpaapo, ki Yennu baamm bant ki lub sarik bootsunsuŋ nba bɔn ni, ki guu dagbeŋŋir nba ki u sa bu bi buut na.

ref: We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
text: Nnee biak ki doi nba ki bi yi Sodom nan Gomora na nan digbana nba kpiar na niib din tuun bonchonchonn toona ki bia waa niauŋ nba bi bonchiann, ki bi laat tubdatu nba tee muu ki li di yoo nba kaa gbennu; ki li tee kpaanii ki tur sɔɔ kura.

ref: The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels.
text: Nibooru maŋ mun bia daa mɔk dudukit nba ki bi tuun ki biir bi gbanant, ki bia tee tubkangbata, ki bia sukii Yennu malakanba.

ref: Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
text: Ŋaan malakanba saakɔɔ nba ki u sann yi Maikel na, ŋɔɔ nan Sintaanii nba din kɔn Moses gbanant paak na, ŋaan Maikel mɔsɔɔ din ki sukir Sintaanii, ŋaan din betɔ a “Ti Yomdaanɔ sa wanna yan.”

ref: But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings.
text: Ŋaan niib na ŋanin sukii bont kur nba ki bi ki bann li paake, ŋaan tuun linba kur ki bi loon kaman nan bonkobit nba ki mɔk dudukit na, ki biir bi mɔŋ.

ref: Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
text: Parbiir be bi paak; kimaan bi wei Keenn sɔnbiiuk nawa; bi bia tee kpinkpannii damm likirii paak kaman nan Balaam nba din tee biaŋinba na; ki bia tuun kaman nan Kora nba din tun ki ki sak Yennu mɔb ki tan kpo na; bi mun tan sa kpo nnee.

ref: These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit.
text: Bi tee baritee i mɔtaauk jaamm dinu ni, ki lakin di nan leeb, ki ki tiin Yennu, ki mɔk gɔrit ki dint bi mɔŋ kuukɔɔ. Bi tee kaman nan sanpagbant nba ki baat nan saak ŋaan ki wouŋ da nee; ki bia tee kaman nan tiinii nba ki loon li lomm yoo na, bi kpoe ki sa tɔr, kimaan bi jiina kur fikita ki bi koor.

ref: Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
text: Bi tee kaman nan mɔkgbeŋŋir nyunpana nba fi ki pukir nan jakint nee. Bi bia tee kaman nan ŋmaabira nba tuu ŋabir ki lin nee; ki bunbɔnchiauŋ nba kaa gbennu be ki guub.

ref: Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said: Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels
text: Enok wunba paa Adam yoo paak munnlore ni na din sɔkin ki yet bi po a “Ti Yomdaanɔ nan u malakanbaŋamm tusa tusa baat

ref: to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
text: a wun bu nifosaarik kur buut, ki biir binba kur ki tiinɔ, ba tuun toonbiit, ki ki saak u mɔb biaŋinba na; nan biitdamm kura, ba piak mabiit kur ki biirɔ biaŋinba na, ki bi baka kaa nanɔ.”

ref: These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want. More Warnings
text: Bi tee bunburimii damme; bi numm ki mi gboo; ki bi tuun bi mɔŋ loonbiit, ki mɔk parbifaant bonchiann, ki korin niib a bin la siar.

ref: My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: N naa walonkai, i tian man maan nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia toomiinba din yet na.

ref: They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God.
text: Bi din yet a “Dajoontii daa maŋ ni, niib nba waa bi mɔŋ gbanant loonbiit na tan sa fiir ki sarikiti.”

ref: And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
text: Li niboorue baat nan bɔkitu i sinsuuk ni; bi gbanant loomm kuukɔɔe diab, Yennu Seyeeŋ kaa bi ni.

ref: Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray.
text: N naa walonkai, yii chee i para man Masia labayeen nba ki i teenir yada na paak, kii mei Yennu u Seyeeŋ paŋ ni,

ref: And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life.
text: kii tee niib nba ki Yennu loomm, kii guu yoo nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sa turi manfoor nba kaa gbennu, u ninbatinu paak.

ref: Be helpful to all who may have doubts.
text: Binba dudukit laŋ na, yi tinib ninbaauk, ki soorib

ref: Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds. Final Prayer
text: ki nyinnib Sintaanii muu ni; ki binba mɔk bi loonbiit dɔk na, yi tinib ninbaauk, ki bia ŋmat nan jaŋmaanii bi boor, i daa nakii bi biit boori. Yenpaku

ref: Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
text: ŋɔɔ nba tee Yen-yenɔkɔɔ ki tinnit ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak na, u yen paku nan yent nan paŋ nan bont kur yudant, yoo nba din gar na, nan mɔtana, nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John.
text: Maan nba ki Yisa Masia din fiit na sɔb gbouŋ na nie. Yennu din turɔ maan maŋ a wun wann u toontunna linba sa teen mɔtana. Masia din tun u malaka u toontunnɔ Jɔɔnn boor ni, a wun betɔ maan maŋ kur.

ref: And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
text: Ŋanne ki Jɔɔnn din fiit ki wann linba ki u din la na kur ki li tee barii nba nyii Yennu boor ni, nan linba ki Yisa Masia din fiit ki wannɔ na.

ref: God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
text: Wunba karin sɔkinii mɔmaan nba sɔb gbouŋ na ni, parpeenn tee u yare, ki binba gbia mɔmaan maŋ ki saakir, parpeenn tee bi yare mun, kimaan yoo pera ki linba kur sa tun na lin tun. Masiaweira nba bɔkit ki be digbana ŋannlore na labaar

ref: He lets us rule as kings and serve God his Father as priests. To him be glory and power forever and ever! Amen.
text: ŋaan te ki ti tee u naan ni niib nba tee manteeb, a tin tun u Baa Yennu toonn; ŋɔɔe yen paku nan paŋ mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

ref: Look! He is coming with the clouds. Everyone will see him, even the ones who stuck a sword through him. All people on earth will weep because of him. Yes, it will happen! Amen.
text: Gotir man, ŋɔɔe be sanpagbant ni ki baat na. Sɔɔ kur ninbina tan sa lau, nan binba din ŋmu-u na gbaa. Yoo nba ki u tan sii baat na, tingbouŋ na niib kur tan sa bui u paak. Mɔmɔmm, li sa teen nnawa.

ref: The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!” A Vision of the Risen Lord
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu wunba mɔk paŋ kur, ki be, ki din be, ki bia tan sii be yoo nba kaa gbennu, ŋɔɔe yeen a “Mine tee bont kura pinpiik nan li gbennu.”

ref: I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus.
text: Tɔn, min Jɔɔnn wunba tee i naa bik ki taan nani ki di fara nan sukuru Yisa paak, ma din lin ki mɔɔntir Yennu maan, nan Yisa barŋanii, ki bi din jiin ki saan nanin Patmos tiŋ nba ki nyun lintir na ni.

ref: On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
text: Ki lasir dasiar ki Yennu Seyeeŋ din fiir n ni, ki n gbat kunkɔsaakar ki li naan nan kikaak na ki piak n poorpo,

ref: The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
text: ki betin a n sɔb linba ki n laat na ki teen gbouŋ ni ki nakin ki tur Masiaweira nba be digbana ŋannlore: binba be Efesus nan Simirna nan Pegamum nan Tiataira nan Saadis nan Filadelfia nan Laodisea na.

ref: When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands.
text: Ki n ŋmant ki yaa n got wunba piak ki teenin na; ma ŋmant yoo nba na ki n la sarima fita ŋannlore ki li see.

ref: There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest.
text: Ki sɔɔ be li sinsuuk ni ki naan nan nisaarik Bik na, ki lia liatfouk ki li tan siit u taabira, ki sarima gbann boob u nyaauk ni,

ref: His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire.
text: ki u yut peen kaman nan gungunt na, ki u ninbina naan nan mupeenu na,

ref: His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall.
text: ki u taa nyirii kaman nan kut nba ki bi tuu naantir muu ni ki li nyirii biaŋinba na, ki u kunkɔr fu kaman nan siɔk kpenu ni nyun nba tuu fu biaŋinba na.

ref: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
text: Ki u kub ŋmaabira ŋannlore u nuudiitu ni, ki jukbanjiak nba di mɔleemii tant nyii u mɔb ni, ki u numm po yeen kaman nan yennu nba tuu yeen yensuuk ni na.

ref: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said: Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last,
text: Ma lau yoonba ki n baa dɔɔr u tɔɔnn kaman nan kpeemɔ na. Ki u jii u nuudiitu ki yii n paak, ŋaan yet a “Daa tiin jaŋmaanii; mine tee bont kura pinpiik nan li gbennu;

ref: and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead.
text: ki bia tee footɔɔ na, ki din kpo, ŋaan mɔtana n fiira ki sii fo yoo nba kaa gbennu, ki bia mɔk kuun nan kpeentiŋ paŋ;

ref: Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things.
text: a li paak a sɔb linba ki a la mɔtana, nan linba be mɔtana, nan linba tan sa tun yoonbawa;

ref: I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
text: a ŋmaabira ŋannlore nba ki a la n nuudiitu ni na, nan sarima fita ŋannlore na woonin ni paak tee nnee: ŋmaabira ŋannlore nee tee n weira nba be digbana ŋannlore na malakanba, ki fita ŋannlore na mun tee n weira maŋ bujir munnlore maŋ.”

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus: I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
text: Ki wunba ki n lau na betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Efesus doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba dia ŋmaabira ŋannlore u nuudiitu ni na ki bia somm sarima fita ŋannlore sinsuuk ni na boore:

ref: I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars.
text: ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i toona nba tee biaŋinba na, nan ya yabir ki tuun toonŋana nan sukuru biaŋinba, ki bia mi nan i ki loon nibiit i sinsuuk ni, li paake ki i din bikin binba tuu yaa a bi tee n toomiinba ŋaan ki ki tee na, ki i bann nan bi tee faidamme.

ref: You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
text: N mi nan i mɔk sukuru ki la fara n paak, ŋaan i ki nyikimi.

ref: But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to.
text: Ŋaan i biit tee nnee: i ji ki loonin kaman nan ya din dɔŋ loonin sinsinn biaŋinba na.

ref: Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand.
text: Tiat man ya din tuu be biaŋinba na ŋaan ji tan lebit ki nyikir na. Nyikin man i biit maŋ ŋaan kii tuun ya poŋ tuu tuun biaŋinba sinsinn ki li ŋan na; li-i tee ki i kan nyik i biiti, n sa baar i boor ki jii i fitir na li boor, ŋaan beri.

ref: But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
text: Ŋaan ya tuun linba ki li ŋane na; i ki loon toona nba ki Nikola niib tuun na, kaman nan n mɔŋ nba ki loon toona nba ki bi tuun na.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. I will let everyone who wins the victory eat from the life-giving tree in God's wonderful garden. The Letter to Smyrna
text: “Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na. “Wunba nyann nyannu maŋ, ŋɔɔe ki n sa turɔ sɔnu ki wun di manfoor tilɔɔna nba be Yennu boor, siaminba ki bi yir Paradais na.’ ” Masia labaar nba tee Simirna teeb yare na

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
text: Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Simirna doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba tee bont kura pinpiik nan li gbennu na boor ni; ŋɔɔe din kpo ki tan bia fiir kuun ni na;

ref: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
text: ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i fara po, ki bia mi nan i tee nandamme, ŋaan mɔmɔmm, Yennu boor ni, i tee mɔkitae; ki n mi nan nisiab piak ki biir i sana, ki bia yaa a bi tee Juu teebe, ŋaan ki ki tee nna; bi tee Sintaanii niibe.

ref: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
text: I daa tiin jaŋmaanii nan fara nba baat i paak na. Gotir man, Sintaanii sa te bin soor i siab ki kɔɔmm dansarik ki bikimm, i bia tan sa la fara ki saa dian daa piik; ŋaan yii teenin yada mɔkmɔk nan i kuun, ki n tan sa pi-i piinii nba tee manfoor.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death. The Letter to Pergamum
text: “Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na. “Wunba nyann nyannu maŋ, u kan la kuun nba pukin munnlee na.’ ” Masia labaar nba tee Pegamum teeb yare na

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
text: Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Pegamum doo ni na malaka, a ki yet a “Labaar na nyii wunba mɔk jukbanjiak nba di mɔleemii na boor nie:

ref: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
text: ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi nan siaminba ki i be na tee siaminba ki Sintaanii naangbouŋ na be, ki n mi nan i set dia n sann; yoo nba ki bi din tan kpii Antipas nba din tee n siara daanɔ nan barmɔnii, i mɔŋ doo nba ki Sintaanii be na, li yoo maŋ gbaa ki i ki yêt n sɔnu.

ref: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
text: Ŋaan mɔmɔmm, i mɔk biit ŋannlee nnae ki n sa wanni: siab be i sinsuuk ni ki dia Balaam sennu; ŋɔɔe din wann Balak wa sa teen biaŋinba ki te ki Israel teeb-n tun biit na. U din te ki bi dii pata nant, ki bia te ki bi chomm nan leeb.

ref: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
text: Li mun bia tee nnee i sinsuuk ni, ki i siab mun waa Nikola niib maŋ wannu.

ref: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
text: Li paak, yi nyik i biit maŋ; li-i kii tee nnai kaa, n sa baar mɔtana nan jukbanjiak nba tantir ki nyi n mɔb ni na, ki kɔn biir li nibooru.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
text: “Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na. “Wunba nyann nyannu na, ŋɔɔe ki n sa turɔ woonin ni jeet nba ki bi yir mana na ki wun di, ki n bia sa turɔ tanpeenn nba ki sanpaann sɔb li paak ki sɔɔ ki mir na, see fin nba gaar na kuukɔɔ.’ ” Masia labaar nba tee Tiataira teeb yare na

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
text: Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin ki be Tiataira doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii Yennu Bija nba ki u ninbina naan nan mupeenu na, ki u taa bia nyirii kaman nan nabint nba tuu nyirii biaŋinba na boor nie;

ref: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
text: ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba, ki bia mi i loomm, nan i yada teenu, nan i toona, nan ya mɔk mintir biaŋinba, ki bia mi nan i toonŋana tumu pukii ki gar i sinsinn toona na.

ref: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
text: Ŋaan i biit tee nnee: ya sak poo nba ki bi yiu Jesebel ki u yaa a u tee Yennu sɔkinii, ki ji taan ki be i boor ni ki kpann n toontunna ki wantib a bii chomm nan leeb kii di pata nant na.

ref: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
text: N turɔ yoo a wun lebit u binbeŋ na, ŋaan ki u ki saak a wun nyik u chonchonsin na.

ref: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
text: Li paak, n sa jiiu ki lu-u fara gado paak nan binba tuu lakin chomm nanɔ na kur; li-i kii tee ki bi nyik biit nba ki bi lakin tuun nanɔ na, n sa kɔɔmm fargbeŋŋir ni,

ref: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
text: ki kpi u weira kura; ki n weira kur sa bann nan mine tee wunba mi niib dudukit nan bi loomm nba tee biaŋinba, ŋaan sa tur sɔɔ kur u toona nyɔɔt nba tee biaŋinba.

ref: Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands.
text: “Ŋaan i siab nba be Tiataira ki ki waa poo maŋ sɔnii na, ki bia ki waa linba ki siab yir Sintaanii woonin ni labaar na, n yeen yimme a n kan jinni jikpiata.

ref: But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
text: Yii dia man linba ki i mɔk na fanu mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki n tan sa baar na.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
text: “Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.’ ”

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
text: Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Saadis doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba mɔk Yennu Sei ŋannlore nan ŋmaabira ŋannlore na boore: ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i toona nba tee biaŋinba, ki bia mi nan niib dukii a i fo, ŋaan i ki fo.

ref: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
text: Li paak, fiit man ki yabir i binbeŋ nba biar na a li daa kpenn ki gbenti, kimaan toona nba ki i tuun na, li ki gboot Yennu ninbina.

ref: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
text: Li paak, yii tian man wannu nba ki i din gbat ki waa-r na. Yii diar man ŋaan nyik i toonbiit tumu na; ŋaan li-i tee ki i ki fiir, n sa baar i boor ni kaman nan nanyukɔɔ nba tuu baar biaŋinba nee, ki i kan bann n baaru yoo.

ref: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
text: Ŋaan i siab be Saadis ki bi para ni ki mɔk jakinti. Ŋamme sa lia tapeenii ki min nan ŋamm sii tɔk, kimaan ba ŋan nann na paak.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
text: Wunba nyann nyannu maŋ na, u tan sa lia tapeenii na, ki n tan kan kpeen u sann manfopaana damm gbouŋ na ni, ki n tan sa yet n Baa nan u malakanba na paanu ni a li daanɔ tee n yɔɔe.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Philadelphia
text: “Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.’ ” Masia labaar nba tee Filadelfia teeb yare na

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
text: Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Filadelfia doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba tee Popeendaanɔ, ki bia tee Barmɔnii daanɔ na boore; ki u dia David peenu na, ki li-i tee ki u loot gann, sɔɔ kan fit loonni; ki li-i biakii tee ki u loonn, sɔɔ kan fit looti:

ref: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
text: ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i toona nba tee biaŋinba, ŋaan gotir, n loot gann ki turi, ki sɔɔ kan fit loonni. N mi nan paŋ nba ki i mɔk na, li waar, ŋaan nanna kura, i bia lek dia n wannu na, ki ki nɔi nan i ki mimi.

ref: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
text: Nnara, binba tee Sintaanii niib, faidamm nba yaa a bi tee Juu teeb ŋaan ki ki tee na, n sa te ki bin baar ki tan gbaan i tɔɔnn po ki bann nan n set looni.

ref: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
text: Kimaan ya sak n mɔmaan ki mɔk mintir na paak, n sa dia-i bonŋann ki i tan kan kɔɔ bonbiir nba baat tingbouŋ na kur paak a lin tan bikin tingbouŋ na niib na.

ref: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
text: N baate mɔtana. Yii dia man linba ki i mɔk na fanu ki sɔɔ tan kan fat i nyɔɔt maŋi.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
text: Wunba nyann nyannu na, n sa teenɔ Yennu diiuk ni jaantarik, ki u ji kan nyi leŋi. Ki n bia sa sɔb Yennu sann u paak, ki sɔb Yennu doo sann u paak, ki bia sɔb n mɔŋ sanpaann na u paak. Yennu doo maŋe tee Jerusalem paann nba sa nyi Yendɔuŋ ni Yennu boor ni na, ki tan sik tiŋ na ni.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Laodicea
text: “Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.’ ” Masia labaar nba tee Laodisea teeb yare na

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
text: Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Laodisea doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba sann yi Mɔnii na boore; ki u tee siara daanɔ nba tee barmɔnii daanɔ ki tee siara fanu; ki bia tee linba kur ki Yennu nann na yudaanɔ:

ref: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
text: ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i toona nba tee biaŋinba; i ki tôni, i bia ki mei, ŋaan n bo loon ki yii tee li yenne;

ref: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
text: ŋaan ya ki tôn ki bia ki mei na, n sa siiri pia teriku.

ref: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
text: Yimme yeen a i tee mɔkitae ki mɔk linba kur ki i loon, a ki siar kan pɔtiini, ŋaan ki ki mi ya mɔk parbiir nan ninbaauk biaŋinba. I tee nandamme, ki somm yana, ki tee jɔɔnii.

ref: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
text: Li paak, n kpaaniie a yi daa sarima nba tee sarinmɔnna n boor ni ki set la mɔkint, ki bia daa tapeenii ki dɔkin i fei na, ki bia daa kpan ki baŋ i ninbina na kii laat.

ref: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
text: Binba kur ki n loomm na, n kpaamm kpaanii ki daar bi tuba. Li paak, nyikin man i biit, ki daa jerimi.

ref: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
text: Nnara, n see tammɔb na ni ki kpaan gann; wunba gbat n kunkɔr ki loot gann maŋ, n sa kɔɔ u boor ni, ki min nan ŋɔɔ sa lakin ki di.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
text: Wunba nyann nyannu na, n sa turɔ yaak ki wun tan lakin kar nanin n naangbouŋ na paak, kaman nan ma nyann nyannu kur ki lakin kar nan n Baa Yennu u naangbouŋ na paak biaŋinba na.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
text: “Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.’ ”

ref: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
text: Li poorpo ki n bia la Yendɔuŋ ni gann ki li loot, ki n gbat kunkɔr nba ki n poŋ gbat na ki li bia piak ki teenin ki naan nan kikaak nba tuu mɔ biaŋinba na; ki kunkɔr maŋ betin a n do nna ki wun wannin linba sa tun nanna poorpo.

ref: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
text: Li taakpaak ni ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na fiir n ni, ki n la ki sɔɔ kar naangbouŋ paak Yendɔuŋ ni,

ref: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
text: ki u numm po nyirii kaman nan tana nba ki bi yi jaspa nan kanelian na, ki naatuuruk bɔɔn ki lint naangbouŋ na, ki naatuuruk maŋ tee kaman nan bonbɔnwuwur na.

ref: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
text: Ki naangbant piinnlee nan ŋanna dɔɔ ki lint naangbouŋ na. Ki saakab piinnlee nan banna lia tapeenii ki yir sarima fokirii ki kar naangbant piinnlee nan ŋanna maŋ paak.

ref: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
text: Ki sanyikintii nyi naangbouŋ maŋ ni nan fuut nan satianii. Ki fita ŋannlore lakin di naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn, ŋanne tee Yennu Sei ŋannlore na.

ref: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
text: Ki siar naan nan mɔkgbeŋŋir ki dɔɔ naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn ki tee kaman nan mianu nba nyirii biaŋinba na. Ki bonfoa banna be leŋ ki lint naangbouŋ na, ki mɔk ninbina bonchiann bi numm po nan bi poorpo kur.

ref: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
text: Sinsinn bonfoor na naan nan yambɔr nee, ki wunba waa bannlee na mun naan nan naajak na, ki wunba waa banntaa na numm po naan nan nifosaarik numm po na, ki wunba waa banna na mun naan nan kanbayuktuk nba yuuk na.

ref: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
text: Ki bonfoa banna na kur mɔk kpinkpant ŋannloob-loob, ki bia mɔk ninbina bonchiann bi gbanant kur po. Ki yennu ni nan nyiɔk kur bi ki nyikit yanii yinu. Bi yiin a, “Popeendaanɔ, popeendaanɔ, popeendaanɔ tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nba mɔk paŋ kura, ki din be, ki bia be nan mɔtana, ki bia tan sii be yoo nba kaa gbennu na.”

ref: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
text: Bonfoa banna na-i taŋŋi teen wunba kar naangbouŋ na paak ki be mɔkmɔk nan mɔkmɔk na niipoouk nan dontir nan paku,

ref: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
text: “Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu, li jaŋ nan tin jianta, ki tura nibaakir nan pakuwa, kimaan fine din nan bont kura. A loomm paake ki a naauŋ maŋ kur be, ki mɔk manfoor.”

ref: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
text: Ŋanne ki n bia la ki sɔɔ kar naangbouŋ na paak ki dia gbanpɔɔbir u nuudiitu ni, ki gbouŋ maŋ mɔk sɔbnii li numm po nan li poor po, ki bi din jii tiik yiit ki tabin li mɔi boa ŋannlore ni.

ref: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
text: Ki n la malaka nba mɔk paŋ ki u mɔɔntir nan kunkɔsaakar a “Ŋmee jaŋ nan wun pɔbit gbanpɔɔbir na ki tabit li yiiti?”

ref: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Ŋaan ki sɔɔ kaa Yendɔuŋ ni amii tingbouŋ na ni amii kpeentiŋ ki sa fit pɔbit gbanpɔɔbir na ki got li nɔɔk ni.

ref: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Li yoo ki n bui nan ninnyuut kimaan sɔɔ nba kaa ki jaŋ nan wun pɔbit gbanpɔɔbir na ki got li nɔɔk ni na.

ref: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
text: Ŋanne ki saakab na yenɔ yetin a n daa bui, a n got wunba nyii Juda maaru ni ki u sann yi Juda Yambɔr na, wunba tee David yaaboongbeŋŋir ki nyann bonsiar kur na, ŋɔɔe sa fit ki tabit yiit ŋannlore na ki pɔbit gbanpɔɔbir maŋ.

ref: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
text: Ki n la Pebik ki u see naangbouŋ na boor ki bonfoa banna na nan saakab na see ki lintɔ; ki Pebik na tee kaman nan pebik nba ki bi kpiiu ŋaan ki u fiir kuun ni na, ki u mɔk yina ŋannlore, nan ninbina ŋannlore linba tee Yennu Sei ŋannlore nba ki u tumm tingbouŋ na kur ni na.

ref: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
text: Ki Pebik na saan ki saa gaar gbanpɔɔbir na daanɔ nba kar naangbouŋ na paak u nuudiitu ni.

ref: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
text: Ki bi yiin yanpaanii ki teen Pebik na ki yeen a, “A set jaŋ nan fan gaar gbanpɔɔbir na ki tabit li yiit ŋannlore na, kimaan a din kpoe ki jii a sɔn ki daa niib ki tur Yennu; niib maŋ nyii nibooru booru kur ni, nan mabooru kur ni, nan maaru kur ni, nan tingbouŋ na yent kur ni.

ref: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
text: A bia te ki bi tee a naan ni niib binba tee manteeb, a bin tun Yennu toona; ki bi sii yen tingbouŋ na teeb.”

ref: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
text: Li poorpo biak ki n got ki la malakanba tusa tusa tusa, ki bia gbat bi kunkɔra, ki bi ŋaa lint bonfoa banna na nan saakab na nan naangbouŋ na.

ref: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
text: Ki n gbat ki bi yeen nan kunkɔsaakar a “Pebik nba ki bi din kpiiu ki u fiir na, ŋɔɔe jaŋ nan wun gaar paŋ nan mɔkint nan yan nan nyannu nan nibaakir nan dontir nan paku.”

ref: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
text: Ki linba kur ki Yennu din nan na, linba be sanpaapo, nan linba be tingbouŋ na ni, nan linba be kpeentiŋ, nan linba be mɔkgbeŋŋa ni, n gbat bi kur yeen a, “Paku nan nibaakir nan dontir nan paŋ kur-ii be wunba kar naangbouŋ na paak na nan Pebik na paak ki jii saa yoo nba kaa gbennu.”

ref: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
text: Ki bonfoa banna na sak ki yet a “Mɔmɔmm.” Ki saakab na kur gbaan Yennu nan Pebik na tɔɔnn ki jiantirib.

ref: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
text: Li poorpo ki n la ki Pebik na tabit yiit ŋannlore na yenn, ki n gbat bonfoa banna na yenɔ kunkɔr ki li tee kaman nan satianii na ki yiin a “Baat.”

ref: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
text: Ki n got ki la tanpeeŋ, ki wunba jak u paak na dia tadaauk, ki bi turɔ fokirik, ki u nyii, ki tee paŋ daanɔ, a wun kɔn ki nyann bonsiar kur.

ref: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Ki Pebik na bia tabit yiit nba pukin ŋannlee na, ki n gbat bonfoor nba pukin bannlee na kunkɔr ki u yiin a “Baat.”

ref: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
text: Ŋanne ki n la tanmɔŋ nyii ki set, ki bi tur wunba jakɔ na yaak a wun nyinn parmaasir tingbouŋ na ni ki te ki niib-ii kpi bi leeb; ki bi bia kubinɔ jukbanjagbeŋŋir.

ref: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
text: Ki Pebik maŋ bia tabit yiit nba pukin ŋanntaa na, ki n bia gbat ki bonfoor nba pukin banntaa na yiin a “Baat.” Ki n bia la ki tanbɔŋ see leŋ, ki wunba jakɔ na dia linba ki bi bikii bona na u nuu ni.

ref: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
text: Ki n gbat fuut ki li tee kaman nan kunkɔr na ki nyii bonfoa banna na ni ki yeen a “Diŋmanbaŋ tee daar paauk, a ki karwɔntaa ŋmanbaŋ munntaa mun tee daar paauk; a ŋaan daa biir kpan nan daan na.”

ref: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Ki Pebik na tabit yiit nba pukin ŋanna na; ki n gbat kunkɔr ki li nyii bonfoor nba pukin banna na, ki u yiin a “Baat.”

ref: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
text: Ŋanne ki n la tanpeenwuwur, ki wunba jakɔ na sann tee Kuun; ki kpeentiŋ mun waa u poor; ki Yennu turib yikoo a bin kpi tingbouŋ na niib bɔkitu kann taar munna yenn, a bin kpib nan jukbanjiak nan kon nan yiaru, ki bia kpib nan tingbouŋ na bonkobibiit.

ref: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
text: Ki Pebik na bia tabit yiit nba pukin ŋaŋŋmu na, ki n la ki binba ki niib din kpiib kimaan ba din dia Yennu maan, nan barŋanii nba ki bi din mɔk na paak, ki bi be binbintir na nyakir ni,

ref: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
text: ki yikin sanpaapo ki yeen a, “Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu, fin nba tee popeendaanɔ ki bia tee barmɔnii daanɔ, a li sa yukir nleewa ki a tan bu tingbouŋ na niib na buut ki dat bi tuba, kimaan ba kpiit na paaki?”

ref: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
text: Ŋanne ki bi tur bi kur liatfopeenii, ŋaan yetib a “I toontunleeb nan i naa waas nba ki niib tan sa kpib kaman nan ba din kpii yimm biaŋinba na, yin foi waan kii guu yoo nba ki bi tan sa kpib ki gbenn.”

ref: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
