text: Ki n la Pebik na ki u tabit yiit nba pukin ŋannloob na; ki tiŋ jek bonchiann, ki yennu bɔnn chimm, ki ŋmaamunn mɔnn kaman nan nyumɔnt na.

ref: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
text: Ki ŋmaabira nyii sanpaapo ki baa tiŋ ni kaman nan wouŋ nba tuu fikit ki niŋ kinkaŋ ki u lɔɔna tuu baa biaŋinba na.

ref: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
text: Ki sanpaak pɔbin pɔbin kaman nan pɔɔbit na ki bot, ki jɔɔt kur nan gbingbana kur chaat chaat siaminba ki li tuu be na.

ref: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
text: Ŋanne ki tingbouŋ na kpanbara, nan nijaana, nan lanjimanba yudamm, nan nitarinyikaa, nan panii damm, nan sɔɔ kur, binba tee daaba, nan binba yen bi mɔŋ, ki bi kur chiar chiar ki kɔɔ tankɔrit nan jɔlɔnt ni,

ref: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
text: ki yeen a jɔɔt nan tana na-n baa bi paak, a ki wunba kar naangbouŋ paak na kan lab, a ki Pebik na mun kan lab, nan u wutoor nba doo bi paak na,

ref: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
text: kimaan wunba kar naangbouŋ na paak na nan Pebik na wutoor dagbeŋŋir daar maŋ baar na, ki sɔɔ kaa ki sa set bi wutoor maŋ tɔɔnn po.

ref: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
text: a bi daa biir tingbouŋ na nan mɔkgbeŋŋa na nan tiinii na see ki bi tan dɔk Yennu toontunna bi yugbia paak sinsinn.

ref: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
text: ki yeen a, “Mɔmɔmm, paku nan dontir nan yan nan niipoouk turu nan nibaakir nan paŋ nan yikoo tee Yennu yare mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.”

ref: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
text: Li poorpoe ki n got ki la niburchiauŋ ki bi kaa kanni, ki nyii tingbouŋ na yent kur ni, nan nibooru booru kur ni, nan maaru kur ni, nan mabooru kur ni, ki lia liatfopeenii ki see naangbouŋ na nan Pebik na tɔɔnn po, ki dia dia biabfaat bi nii ni, ki yikin nan kunkɔsaakara ki yeen a,

ref: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
text: “Tinnu nyi ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nba kar naangbouŋ na paake, ŋɔɔe nan Pebik na.”

ref: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
text: Ki malakanba na kur set ki lint naangbouŋ na, nan saakab na, nan bonfoa banna na, ki baa funfaba naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn ki jiantir Yennu,

ref: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
text: Ki saakab na yenɔ boin a “Ki binba lia liatfopeenii na tee lamme, bi nyii lee?”

ref: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
text: Ki n jiin ki yetɔ a “Chanbaa, fine mi, ŋaan min ki mi.” Ki u yetin a, “Niib na nyii fargbeŋŋir na nie, ki wuur bi liatfɔkit na Pebik na sɔn ni ki li ji peen,

ref: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
text: a li paake ki bi see Yennu naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn ki tuun teenɔ nyiɔk nan yennu ni u jiantu diiuk na ni, a ki ŋɔɔ Yennu nba kar naangbouŋ na paak na sii be namm, ki u maasu sii dɔɔk bi paak;

ref: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
text: a ki kon nan nyunyukiru kan mi soorib, ki yentonu amii tongbeŋsiar kan mi ki toŋ bi gbanu,

ref: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
text: a ki Pebik nba be naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn na sii guub, ki chiamm saan namm nyunbuur nba teen manfoor na ni, a ki Yennu sa fiar bi ninnyuut kur.”

ref: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
text: Pebik na nba tabit yiit nba pukin ŋannlore na, li yooe ki Yendɔuŋ ni ŋmin surimm, ki li yann waan.

ref: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
text: Li poorpoe ki n la malakanba bannlore nba see Yennu tɔɔnn na; ki u turib kikai ŋannlore.

ref: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
text: Ki n bia la malakaganɔ ki u dia sarima seŋ ki saan saa set binbintir na tɔɔnn. Ki Yennu turɔ wubit bonchiann a wun pukin Yennu niib kur miaru na ni ki jii paan sarima binbintir nba see naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn po na, ki tur Yennu.

ref: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
text: Ki wubit na munyuut nan Yennu niib na miaru nyii malaka nba see Yennu tɔɔnn na nii ni ki do sanpaapo.

ref: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
text: Ki malaka na jii sarima seŋ na ki mɔɔr muu binbintir na paak ki gbeen seŋ na ki kpaar mɔɔn tingbouŋ na ni. Ki saak tian, ki fuut fu, ki sanyikintii nyikii, ki tiŋ damm. Kikai ŋannlore labaar

ref: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
text: Ki malakanba bannlore nba dia kikai ŋannlore na teen siir nan bin peb kikai na.

ref: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
text: Sinsinn malaka na nba dɔŋ pebir u kikaak na, ki sakaana nan muu ki li ŋmat nan sɔn ki mɔ tingbouŋ na ni, ki tingbouŋ na bɔkitu taar munntaa na yenn dii muu, ki tiinii bɔkitu taar munntaa mun dii muu, ki mɔmaasa kur dii muu.

ref: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
text: Malaka nba pukin bannlee na nba pebir u kikaak na, ki bi lu siar ki li naan nan jɔgbeŋŋir na ki di muu, ki tan baa tingbouŋ na mɔkgbeŋŋa kur ni, ki mɔkgbeŋŋa na bɔkitu munntaa yenn kpant sɔn,

ref: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
text: ki linba be mɔkgbeŋŋa nyun na ni bɔkitu taar munntaa na yenn kpo kpo, ki ŋarint nba paa mɔkgbeŋŋa nyun na paak na, ki li bɔkitu kann taar munntaa yenn kur biir.

ref: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
text: Malaka nba pukin banntaa na nba pebir u kikaak na, ki ŋmaabir gbeŋŋir nba di ki naan nan muu na nyii sanpaapo ki tan baa mɔka nan nyunbua nba be tingbouŋ na ni li bɔkitu taar munntataa yenn ni.

ref: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
text: Ki nyun na bɔkitu taar munntaa yenn kpant ton; ki niib bonchiann nba nyuu nyun na kpo, kimaan nyun na nba ton na paak.

ref: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
text: Malaka nba pukin banna na nba pebir u kikaak na, ki yennu nan ŋmaarik nan ŋmaabira li chian yenni taar munntaa na ni yenn yenn bɔnn. Ki yennu nan ŋmaarik nan ŋmaabira yentu na li chian yenni taar munntaa bɔnn. Ki yentu nba tuu yeen yennu ni na, ki li chian yeŋ taar munntaa ni ji ki yeeni, ki ŋmaarik nan ŋmaabira mun ji ki yeeni, ki nyiɔk bɔnnu mun bɔkit li biaŋ ki mun bɔnn nna.

ref: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
text: Li yoo ki n got ki la kanbagbeŋŋir ki u yuuk sanpagbant ni, ki yikin nan kunkɔsaakar a “Parbiir be, parbiir be, a malakanba banntaa nba biar na-i taŋŋi pebir kikai na, ki li mɔnii na bui, parbiir tan sii be tingbouŋ na niib paak.”

ref: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
text: Ki malaka nba pukin baŋŋmu na pebir u kikaak na, ki n la ki ŋmaabir nyii sanpagbant ni ki tan baa tingbouŋ na ni; ki bi turɔ bootuk nba sumii bonchiann ki kaa terik na li peenu.

ref: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
text: Ki ŋmaabir na loot bootuk na, ki munyuut nyii leŋ ki tee kaman nan musaakau munyuut na, ki li munyuut na dɔkin yennu, ki tingbouŋ na bɔnn.

ref: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
text: Ki tɔɔrii nyii munyuut na ni ki sik tingbouŋ na ni, ki Yennu turib tonu kaman nan nanii nba mɔk tonu tingbouŋ na ni biaŋinba na.

ref: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
text: Ki Yennu yetib a bi daa biir mɔɔt nan bonburkara nan tifaatii, a ŋaan mukis niib nba ki mɔk Yennu dɔkit bi yugbia paak na;

ref: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
text: ki u turib mɔb a bi daa kpi niib maŋ, a ŋaan te ki bin di fara ki saa dian ŋmaarii ŋaŋŋmu, ŋaan bi-i jun nirɔ li tonu sii tee kaman nan naŋ tonu nee.

ref: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
text: Li yoo maŋ, li nibooru sa lon kuun ŋaan kan la, bi bia tan sii loon bin kpo, ŋaan kuun sa chiar ŋaan nyikibe.

ref: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
text: Ki tɔɔrii na tee kaman nan taanii nba tuu teen siir a bin took tɔb nee; ki bi yir linba naan nan sarima fokirii na, ki bi numm po naan nan nifosaarik numm po na,

ref: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
text: ki bi yut naan nan poob yut na, ki bi nyana naan nan yambɔra nyana na.

ref: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
text: Ki bi lia naagbankɔŋŋa ki li naan nan kut na ki dɔkin bi para paak, ki bi kpinkpant fu fuut kaman nan tɔb taanii nba tuu teen siir ki daar torit ki tookit tɔb na.

ref: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
text: Ki bi mɔk joa nan lɔbii ki li naan nan nanii yar na, ki lɔbii maŋ mɔk paŋ ki sa mukis nifosaarii ki saa jaŋ ŋmaarii ŋaŋŋmu nae.

ref: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
text: Ki malaka nba guu bootsunsuŋ maŋ tee bi bat, ki Hiibru maan ni bi yiu Abadon, ki Griik maan ni bi mun yiu Apolion.

ref: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
text: Sinsinn parbiir na gara, ŋaan parbiir munnlee biar ki waa poor. Kikaak nba pukin ŋannloob ni labaar

ref: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
text: Ki malaka nba pukin bannloob na pebir u kikaak na, ki n gbat ki kunkɔr nyii sarima binbintir nba see Yennu tɔɔnn po na, li mɔgbanjoontii ŋanna na ni,

ref: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
text: ki kunkɔr na yet malaka nba pukin bannloob ki dia kikaak na a wun loot malakanba banna nba ki bi lorib mɔkir nba ki bi yir Yufretes ni na ki lub;

ref: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
text: ki bi lootib ki lub; Yennue din diab ki teemm siir daar nba ki bi sa kpi nifosaarii bɔkitu taar munntaa na yenn;

ref: By listening, I could tell there were more than
text: ki n gbat ki bi yeen lanjimanba nba tee tanjakira na kann, ki li tee tusbamm-bamm.

ref: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
text: Ki muu nan munyuut nan sarwuutaa nba nyi taanii na mɔi ni na kpii nifosaarii bɔkitu taar munntaa na yenn.

ref: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
text: Taanii maŋ yikoo be bi mɔi ni nan bi joa nie, ki bi joa na naan nan wai na ki mɔk yura ki bi dia junt niib.

ref: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
text: Ki niib nba biar ki bonbiir maŋ ki kpiib na din ki nyik ba jiantir bi mɔŋ bonninana na; bi bia jiantir narinbiit nan chicherii nan tana nan pata nba ki nyɔi, ki bia ki gbia, ki bia ki sommi.

ref: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
text: Niib maŋ bia ki nyik bi nikpinu, nan bi jabaa bunu, nan bi bonchonchonn toona, nan bi nanyuk toona.

ref: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
text: Li yoo ki n bia la malakasɔɔ ki u nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki siik, ki yoo sanpagbouŋ kaman nan chinchenn na, ki naatuuruk bɔɔn ki lint u yur paak, ki u numm po naan nan yennu na, ki u taa naan nan jaantarii nba di muu na.

ref: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
text: Ki u dia gbanpɔɔbik u nuu ni ki li pɔbit, ki u diitu taar tɔɔ mɔkgbeŋŋir paak, ki u gaŋ taar mun see tiŋ ni;

ref: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
text: ki u tian nan kunkɔsaakar kaman nan yambɔr nba tuu yikin beet biaŋinba na. Wa tian gbenn yoonba na, ki satianii ŋannlore mun tian ki jiin.

ref: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
text: Satianii maŋ poorpo, ki n teen siir a n sɔb, ŋaan gbat kunkɔsiar ki li nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki yet a n bɔr satianii ŋannlore nba yet maan nba na lii be n ni, ki daa sɔbini.

ref: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
text: Malaka nba ki n lau ki u see mɔkgbeŋŋir na paak nan tiŋ ni na donn u nuudiitu sanpaapo,

ref: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
text: ki por nan Yennu nba yɔɔ be mɔkmɔk nan mɔkmɔk na, wunba din nan sanpaak nan tingbouŋ na nan mɔkgbeŋŋa nan bona nba kur be li ni na, ki yet a li ji kan yukiri;

ref: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
text: a ŋaan malaka nba pukin bannlore na-i taŋŋi pebir u kikaak na daar nba, Yennu tan sa jii woonin ni maan ki tun nann toonn, kaman nan wa din yet ki tur u sɔkiniinba binba tee u toontunna biaŋinba na.

ref: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
text: Ki kunkɔr nba ki n gbat ki li nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki piak teenin na bia piak ki teenin a n saan ki saa gaar gbanpɔɔbik nba pɔbit ki be malaka nba see mɔkgbeŋŋir nan tiŋ ni na nuu ni.

ref: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
text: Ŋanne ki n saan malaka na boor a n gaar gbanpɔɔbik maŋ, ki u chabin a n gaar ki ŋman; a ŋaan li sii tonne n nɔɔk ni, ŋaan n mɔb ni ŋanin li sii man kaman nan siat nee.

ref: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
text: Ŋanne ki n gaar gbanpɔɔbik maŋ ki ŋman, ki li man kaman nan siat na n mɔb ni, ŋaan la sik n poor ni, li ji tonne.

ref: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
text: Ŋanne ki bi betin a “Li kpaa talas nan fan ŋamm ki sɔkin Yennu maan ki tur nibooru bonchiann nan tingbouŋ na yent bonchiann nan mabooru bonchiann nan kpanbara bonchiann.”

ref: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
text: ŋaan daa bikii li dundɔuŋ yabinti, kimaan n jii ŋanne ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na, ki bi tan sa ŋmaa ki lint Yennu doo ni ŋmaarii piinna nan ŋannlee;

ref: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
text: Siara damm bannlee nee tee Olif tiinii ŋannlee nan fita ŋannlee nba see ti Yomdaanɔ wunba yent tingbouŋ na tɔɔnn po.

ref: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
text: Ki sɔɔ-i taŋŋi loon wun turib daŋ, muu sa nyi bi mɔi ni ki tan di bi datai maŋ; ki wunba kur loon wun turib daŋ, nnee ki bi sa teenɔ wun mun kpo.

ref: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
text: Bi bia mɔk yikoo ki sa soor saak, ki saak kan baa yoo nba ki bi mɔɔntir Yennu maan na, ki bia mɔk yikoo ki sa te ki nyun-n kpant sɔn, ki bia sa fit te ki farbiiuk-n baa tingbouŋ na ni yoo kur nba ki bi loon.

ref: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
text: Yoo nba ki bi tan sa gbenn bi siara toona na, bonkobsiɔk nba be bootsunsuŋ nba kaa terik na sa nyi ki tookib nan tɔb, ki nyannib ŋaan kpib.

ref: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
text: Ki bi gbanant tan sii dɔɔ dogbeŋŋir sɔnjouk ni, siaminba ki bi din kpaa ti Yomdaanɔ dapunpunn paak na. Yennu pur doo maŋ Sodom amii Ijipt.

ref: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
text: Ki niib nba nyii maaru kur ni, nan nibooru booru kur ni, nan mabooru kur ni, nan tingbouŋ na yent kur ni, tan sa tikir ki got bi gbanant damuna ŋanntaa nan dajinn, ki tan kan chab ki bin pii bi gbanant maŋi.

ref: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
text: Tingbouŋ na niib sii mɔk parpeenn bonchiann niib bannlee na kuun paak, ki bi sa jerin ki pi bi leeb piinii, kimaan bee paaki, sɔkiniinba bannlee na te ki tingbouŋ na niib dii fara bonchiann.

ref: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
text: Ki daa ŋanntaa nan dajinn na nba gar, ki foon nba teen manfoor na nyii Yennu boor ki baar kɔɔb, ki bi manfoor jen, ki bi fiir set; ki jaŋmaanii bonchiann soor binba lab na.

ref: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
text: Li yoo na ki sɔkiniinba bannlee na gbat kunkɔsiar ki li nyii sanpaapo ki yetib a “Don man nna na.” Li yooe ki bi kɔɔ sanpagbouŋ ni ki doo Yendɔuŋ ni, ki bi datai na see ŋaan gorii.

ref: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
text: Li taakpaak ni ki tiŋ damm, ki dogbeŋŋir maŋ chianu taar piik ni yenn biir, ki niib tusa ŋannlore kpo tiŋ maŋ dammu ni, ki jaŋmaanii soor binba biar na, ki bi donn Yendɔuŋ ni Yennu sann.

ref: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
text: Gotir man, parbiir nba pukin munnlee na gara, ŋaan parbiir nba pukin munntaa na sa baar mɔtana. Kikaak nba pukin munnlore na labaar

ref: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
text: Li yoo ki malaka nba pukin bannlore na pebir u kikaak na, ki kunkɔsaakara yeen Yendɔuŋ ni a “Tingbouŋ na naan kur kpant ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nan u Niganntɔɔ Masia yara, ki u sa diab mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki mɔk gbennu.”

ref: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
text: ki yeen a “Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu, wunba mɔk paŋ kur, wunba bia be, ki din yɔɔ be na, ti teena niipoouk, kimaan a jii a pangbeŋŋir ki dii tingbouŋ na naan na.

ref: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
text: Binba ki waa Yisa nee jia wutoor, ki a mɔŋ wutoor mun doo. Ki yoo baar nan ki a bu binba kpo na buut, ki pa a toontunna nba tee a sɔkiniinba na, nan a niib kura nba jiantira na, binba tee nijaana nan nitarii kur, ki yoo bia baar nan ki a biir binba biir tingbouŋ na.”

ref: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
text: Ŋanne ki Yenjiantu diiuk nba be Yendɔuŋ ni na loot, ki n la lakir nba ki Yennu sennu be li ni na, diiuk na ni. Ki sanyikintii nyikintir, ki satianii tian nan fuut, ki tiŋ damm, ki sakaana nba gbeŋ bonchiann baa.

ref: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
text: ki poo maŋ mɔk poor ki bui matyiaru.

ref: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
text: Ŋanne ki nyinleer bia dɔkit sanpaapo; ki waakperbiiuk nba tee bonmuŋ see ki mɔk yura ŋannlore nan yina piik; ki naanfokirii ŋannlore yir u yura ŋannlore na paak.

ref: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
text: Ki u jii u joor ki dat ŋmaabira na bɔkitu ŋanntaa ni yenn ki nyinnir sanpaapo ki lur tingbouŋ na ni. Ŋanne ki u ji see poo nba mɔ matyiaru na tɔɔnn, ki guu a wun mar gbenna, ki wun ji ŋman sanpantik na.

ref: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
text: Ki poo maŋ tan mar bonjabik wunba yaa wun tan dia nibooru kur nan paŋ; ki bi nyiiar bik na ki saan nanɔ Yennu boor, u naangbouŋ paak.

ref: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
text: Ki poo na mun chiar ki saan muuk ni siaminba ki Yennu teen siir ki guunɔ na, ki sii dintɔ ki saa dian daa tusir nan kobiinnlee nan piinnloob.

ref: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
text: Ŋanne ki tɔb fiir Yendɔuŋ ni. Ki malaka wunba ki u sann yi Makel, ki ŋɔɔ nan u malakaleeb kɔn ki tookit waakperbiiuk na nan u mun yaa malakanba na;

ref: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
text: ki bi nyann waakperbiiuk nan u malakanba na, ki ki chabib a bii be Yendɔuŋ ni;

ref: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
text: bi jii waakperbiiuk na ki nyinnɔ; u poŋ tuu tee waauk nba tuu beewa, ki u sann yi Sanbiirtɔɔ amii Sintaanii, ki tuu kpann tingbouŋ na teeb kur na. Ki bi nyinn ŋɔɔ nan u malakanba ki lub tingbouŋ na ni.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
text: Li yoo ki n gbat kunkɔsaakar Yendɔuŋ ni, ki li yeen a “Mɔtana, ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu tinnu, nan u paŋ, nan u naan, nan u Niganntɔɔ Masia yikoo baar na, kimaan bee paake? bi nyinn Sintanii na Yendɔuŋ ni; ŋɔɔ Sintanii tee wunba din biir Masiaweira ki teen Yennu yoo kur na;

ref: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
text: a Pebik na sɔn nan u barŋanii nba ki bi mɔɔntir na paake ki bi nyann Sintanii na; ki bi ki tiin bi manfoor na ki sak nan kuum-i baarib, bi sa gaar.

ref: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
text: Li paak, yimm nba be Yendɔuŋ ni na, i mɔk man parpeenn. Ŋaan tingbouŋ na niib nan binba be mɔkgbeŋŋa ni ŋanin, parbiir be bi paak, kimaan Sintaanii sik bi paak nan wutogbeŋŋir, wa mi nan u yoo ji ki yab na paak.”

ref: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
text: Waakperbiiuk na nba bann nan bi jiiu ki lu tingbouŋ na ni na, ki u ji wei poo nba mar bonjabik na a wun soorɔ.

ref: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
text: Ki Yennu jii kanbagbeŋŋir kpinkpant ŋannlee ki tur poo na a wun yukir ŋaan nyik waakperbiiuk na, ki saan muuk ni kii saa kii be siaminba ki bi sii dintɔ bina ŋanntaa nan binjinn.

ref: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
text: Ki waakperbiiuk na puut nyunpana poo na paak kaman nan mɔkir ni nyun na, a nyun na-n diu;

ref: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
text: ŋaan ki tiŋ somm poo na ki yaat ŋaan jiin mubin nyun nba nyii waakperbiiuk mɔb ni na paak.

ref: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
text: Ki waakperbiiuk na wutoor doo nan poo na bonchiann, ki u saan a wun saa kɔn nan poo na booru nba biar ki waa Yennu sennu ki dia Yisa barŋanii na,

ref: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
text: Ki n la ki muuk ni bonkobuk nyii mɔkgbeŋŋir ni, ki mɔk yina piik nan yura ŋannlore, ki naanfokirii piik yir u yina piik na paak; ki sanbiiuk nba sukii Yennu sann na sɔb u yura na paak.

ref: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
text: Ki muuk ni bonkobuk nba ki n la na naan nan naamuuk na, ki u mɔk taa-nyargbeŋŋa, ki u mɔb naan nan yambɔr mɔb na. Ki waakperbiiuk na tur muuk ni bonkobuk na u mɔŋ paŋ nan u naangbouŋ nan u yikogbeŋŋir nba yab.

ref: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
text: Ki n la muuk ni bonkobuk na yuyenn ki li naan nan bi kɔtɔ ki kpiiu na, ŋaan ki u lek paaka; ki li ji bakit tingbouŋ na teeb kur, ki bi ji waau.

ref: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
text: Ki niib ji jiantir waakperbiiuk na, kimaan wa jii u paŋ ki tur muuk ni bonkobuk na paak. Ki niib na bia jiantir muuk ni bonkobuk na ki yeen a “Ŋmee paŋe tee kaman nan muuk ni bonkobuk na paŋ na? A ki ŋmee sa fit kɔn nan muuk ni bonkobuk maŋi?”

ref: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
text: Ki u piin ki piak sukii Yennu nan u sann nan siaminba ki u be na, nan binba kur be Yendɔuŋ ni.

ref: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
text: Ki u bia chabɔ yaak a wun kɔn nan Yennu niib ki nyannib, ki bia chabɔ yikoo ki u sa yent maaru kur nan nibooru kur nan mabooru kur nan tingbouŋ na yent kur nba be.

ref: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
text: Niib kur nba be tingbouŋ na ni, ki bi sana ki sɔb manfoor gbouŋ ni yoo nba ki dunia daa din kaa na, bi sa jiant muuk ni bonkobuk na. Manfoor gbouŋ na tee Pebik nba ki bi din kpiiu na yare.

ref: If you have ears, then listen!
text: Li paak, wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint.

ref: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
text: Wunba ki Yennu senn nan u sii tee daabir, u set sii tee daabire. Ki wunba dia jukbanjiak ki kpi u leeb, bi mun bia tan sa jii jukbanjiake ki kpiu. Li paak, li ŋan ki Yennu niib-ii mɔk mintir ki teenɔ yada fanu.

ref: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
text: Li poorpo ki n bia la muuk ni bonkoblɔɔ ki u nyii tiŋ ni ki mɔk yina ŋannlee nba naan nan Pebik yina na, ki u piak ki u kunkɔr naan nan waakperbiiuk kunkɔr na;

ref: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
text: ki see muuk ni bonkobsinsinn na tɔɔnn po, ki jii muuk ni bonkobsinsinn maŋ yikoo nba yab na, ki mukis tingbouŋ na teeb kur a bii jiantir muuk ni bonkobsinsinn nba la daŋ nba yaa lin kpiu ki u tan paak na.

ref: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
text: Ki bonkobuk nba pukin bannlee na bia tun bakitnauŋ nyina, ki te ki muu nyii sanpaapo ki sik tiŋ na niib ninbina ni.

ref: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
text: Ki Yennu nba chabɔ ki u tuun bakitnauŋ nyina na muuk ni bonkobsinsinn tɔɔnn na, ki u kpann tingbouŋ na teeb, ki betib a bin maa patir ki lii naan muuk ni bonkobuk nba la jukbanjiak daŋ ŋaan tan ki kpo na.

ref: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
text: Ki Yennu chab muuk ni bonkobuk nba pukin bannlee na yaak ki u foont ki teen patir nba naan nan muuk ni bonkobsinsinn na, ki patir maŋ tan fit piak ki te ki bi kpi binba ki sak a bin jiantɔ na.

ref: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
text: Ki muuk ni bonkobuk maŋ bia mukis niib kur, binba tee nitarii nan binba tee nijaana, mɔkita nan fara damm, daaba nan yentinmɔŋ damm, a wun dɔk bi niidiitu amii bi yugbia paak;

ref: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
text: li-i tee ki dɔk na kaa nirɔ paaki, u kan fit daa siar amii ki kɔi siari; ki dɔk na tee muuk ni bonkobuk na sann amii u sann kann.

ref: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
text: Linba na tee yanfantir yare. Wunba kur mɔk bannu yan, wun dukin bann muuk ni bonkobuk maŋ kann biaŋ. Kimaan bia, kann maŋ paak tee jasɔɔ sanne. Li kann tee kobii ŋannloob nan piinnloob nan ŋannloob.

ref: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
text: Ki n gbat ki kunkɔr nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki fu kaman nan sutuk ni nyun na, ki bia naan nan satiangbeŋŋa nba tuu tian biaŋinba na, ki bia naan nan bafaara nba tuu faa bi biat ki li mɔ biaŋinba na.

ref: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
text: Ki niib tuskobik nan tuspiinna nan tusa ŋanna maŋ see naangbouŋ nan bonfoa banna na nan saakab na tɔɔnn po ki yiin yanpaanii nba ki sɔɔ ki fit tumii, see niib maŋ nba ki Yennu gannib tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb ni ki tinnib na kuukɔɔ.

ref: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
text: Ŋamme tee jab nba dia bi mɔŋ fanu ki ki chommi, ki ki mi dɔɔ nan poo gbaa; ŋamme bia waa Pebik na ki saa siaminba kur ki u saa; ŋamme tee binba ki Yennu gannib nisaarii sinsuuk ni a bin teen sinsinn teeb nba ki ŋɔɔ Yennu nan Pebik na gaarib.

ref: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
text: Faak bia ki nyi bi mɔi ni; bi tee popeendamme. Malakanba banntaa labaar

ref: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
text: Ki n bia la malakasɔɔ ki u yuuk sanpaapo ki dia barŋanii nba kaa gbennu ki mɔɔntir ki teen tingbouŋ na teeb: nibooru kur nan maaru kur nan mabooru kur nan yent kur niib nba be tingbouŋ na ni;

ref: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
text: ki yet nan kunkɔsaakar a “Yii tiin Yennu kii biakii dontir u sann, kimaan yoo nba ki u sa bu niib buut na baara; yii jiantir ŋɔɔ Yennu nba din nan sanpaak nan tingbouŋ na nan mɔkgbeŋŋa nan nyunkpana kur na.”

ref: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
text: Ki malaka nba pukin bannlee na waa sinsinn yɔɔ na ki yet a, “Dogbeŋŋir Babilɔnn na baawa, a u din te ki nibooru kur tun bonchonchonn toona; li paak, ki Yennu wutoor doowa.”

ref: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
text: Ki malaka nba pukin banntaa na wei ki yet nan kunkɔsaakar a “Wunba kur jiantir muuk ni bonkobuk na nan u naauŋ na, ki mɔk bi dɔk u yugbiar paak amii u nuu ni,

ref: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
text: li daanɔ sa la Yennu wutoor nba tee kaman nan dapaaruk nba ki u mɔɔn ki teen daŋmanbaŋ ni na, li daanɔ bia tan sa la fara muu nba ki sarwuutaa di nann na ni, Yennu malakanba nan Pebik na tɔɔnn po.

ref: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
text: Ki bi muksuk maŋ munyuut sa do sanpaapo nan yoo nba kaa gbennu; bi bia kan la foon yennu ni ki boi nyiɔko. Ŋamme tee binba jiantir muuk ni bonkobuk na nan u naauŋ na, ŋaan mɔk u dɔk nba tee u sann bi paak na.”

ref: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
text: Li paak, li ŋan ki Yennu niib nba saak u mɔmaan na ki teen Yisa yada na-ii mɔk sukuru.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
text: Ki n bia gbat kunkɔr ki li nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki yeen a n sɔb maan na, a “Mɔtana ki jikit saa, parpeenn be damm nba kpo ti Yomdaanɔ sɔnu ni na paak.” Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na sak a “Mɔmɔmm, bi sa la foon bi toongbeŋŋa paak, kimaan bi toona nyɔɔt nee waab.” Tingbouŋ na jaanu labaar

ref: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
text: Ki n bia gokit ki la sanpagbanpeeuŋ, ki sɔɔ kar li paak ki naan nisaarik bik, ki yir sarima fokirik, ki dia gɔtuk nba di u nuu ni.

ref: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
text: Ki malakasɔɔ nyii Yenjiantu diiuk ni ki yeen nan kunkɔsaakar ki teen wunba kar sanpagbouŋ na paak na a “Jiin a gɔtuk na ki jaan tingbouŋ na, kimaan jaanu maŋ yooe na kimaan li kur beera.”

ref: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
text: Ki wunba kar sanpagbouŋ na paak na jii u gɔtuk na ki jaan tingbouŋ na.

ref: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
text: Ki malakasɔɔ mun nyii Yenjiantu diiuk nba be Yendɔuŋ ni ki mun dia gɔtuk nba di.

ref: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
text: Li yoo ki malaka nba tee muu saakɔɔ na nyii binbintir na boor ni ki yeen nan kunkɔsaakar ki teen malaka nba dia gɔtuk nba di na a “Jiin a gɔtuk na ki sia tingbouŋ na kpaab tilɔɔna na, kimaan lɔɔna maŋ kur mɔnna.”

ref: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
text: Ki malaka na jii u gɔtuk na ki siar tingbouŋ na tilɔɔna na, ki jii lɔɔna maŋ ki teen gbiouk nba ki bi ŋmakii lɔɔna li ni ki nyun nyi na. Gbiouk na tee Yennu wutogbeŋŋir nyinn nae.

ref: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
text: Ki bi ŋmakir lɔɔna na doo na kpiŋ, ki sɔn nyii siaminba ki bi ŋmakii lɔɔna na ki tan pukir, ki li fɔkint saan nan maar kobiinnlee nawa, ki li sumiiu sumii ki saa baar taamm yur fɔkinta.

ref: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
text: Li yoo ki n la bakitnauŋ nyinn nba gbeŋ Yendɔuŋ ni, ki malakanba bannlore dia farbiit booru taar munnlore nba biar poor ki tee Yennu wutoor nba biar poor na.

ref: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
text: Ki n la siar ki li naan nan mɔkgbeŋŋir na ki yeen kaman nan mianu na, ki ŋmat nan muu; ki n bia la binba kɔn ki nyann muuk ni bonkobuk na nan u naauŋ na nan u sann kann na, ki bi see ki kpia mianu mɔkgbeŋŋir na ki dia kɔnâ nba ki Yennu turib na;

ref: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
text: ki bi yiin yaŋ nba ki Yennu toontunnɔ Moses din tuu yiin na, nan Pebik na yaŋ a “Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nba mɔk paŋ kur, a toona tee toonjaana nba yaar ŋaan litit, a sɔnii took ki bia tee barmɔnii. Fine tee nibooru kur Kpanbar.

ref: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
text: Ti Yomdaanɔ, ŋmee be ki kan tiina ki bia kan dont a sanni? Kimaan fin kuukɔɔe tee popeendaanɔ. Nibooru kur tan sa baar ki jianta, kimaan a barmɔnii toona nyii paanu niwa.”

ref: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
text: Li poorpoe ki n bia la ki Yenjiantu diiuk nba be Yendɔuŋ ni na ki li loot, ki Yennu lanbouŋ na be leŋ.

ref: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
text: Ki malakanba bannlore nba dia fara booru ŋannlore na nyii jiantu diiuk maŋ ni ki lia tapeenii nba kaa jakinsiati, ŋaan jii sarima chinchenlora ki lor bi nyaat ni.

ref: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
text: Ki bonfoa banna na yenɔ jii sarima senii ŋannlore ki tur malakanba bannlore na; ki Yennu nba be nan yoo nba kaa gbennu na wutoor gbee li senii ni,

ref: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
text: ki Yennu yentsaakar nan u paŋ te ki munyuut gbee jiantu diiuk na, ki sɔɔ ji ki fit kɔɔ leŋ nan yoo nba ki farbiit booru ŋannlore nba ki malakanba bannlore dia na-n tan gbenn.

ref: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
text: Li poorpo ki n bia gbat ki kunkɔsaakar nyii jiantu diiuk na ni ki piak teen malakanba bannlore na, a bin jii senii ŋannlore nba gbee nan Yennu wutoor na ki kpaar mɔɔn tingbouŋ na ni.

ref: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
text: Ki sinsinn malaka na saan ki saa kpaar linba be u seŋ ni na ki mɔɔn tingbouŋ na ni, ki nara nba bi ki yiar bonchiann soor binba mɔk bonkobuk na dɔk, nan binba jiantir li naauŋ na.

ref: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
text: Ki malaka nba pukin bannlee na mun kpaar linba be u seŋ ni na ki mɔɔn mɔkgbeŋŋa ni, ki nyun na kpant nyumɔnt kaman nan kpeemɔ sɔn na, ki bonfoa nba kur be mɔkgbeŋŋa nyun na ni kpo.

ref: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
text: Ki malaka nba pukin banntaa na mun kpaar linba be u seŋ ni na ki mɔɔn kpenii nan nyunbua ni ki li nyun na kpant sɔn.

ref: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
text: Li poorpo, ki n gbat malaka nba tee nyun saakɔɔ na yeen a, “Ti Yomdaanɔ, Popeendaanɔ, wunba be, ki bia tuu yɔɔ be na, a buut na bunu tee linba took sɔnue,

ref: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
text: kimaan niib kpii a niib nan a sɔkiniinba, ki a jii sɔn ki turib a bin nyu. Ŋanne jaŋ namm.”

ref: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
text: Ki n bia gbat ki kunkɔr nyii binbintir na boor ki yeen a, “Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nba mɔk paŋ kur, a buut na tee mɔmɔmme, ki tee linba took sɔnu.”

ref: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
text: Ki malaka nba pukin banna na kpaar linba be u seŋ ni na ki mɔɔn yennu paak ŋaan chabɔ yaak a li tonu na-n ŋamm funn niib ki yekitib nan muu na.

ref: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
text: Ki yennu na ŋamm yekitib nan muu na; ki bi ji sukii Yennu wunba mɔk fara na paŋ na sann, ŋaan ki bi lek bia ki sak nan bin lebit bi dudukit kii teenɔ nibaakiri.

ref: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
text: Ki malaka nba pukin baŋŋmu na kpaar linba be u seŋ ni na ki mɔɔn bonkobuk na naangbouŋ paak, ki bonkobuk maŋ naan yent kur kpant bunbɔnn, ki niib junt bi nyana tont na paak,

ref: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
text: ki bia sukii Yennu nba be Yendɔuŋ ni na, kimaan bi tont nan bi nara na paak, ki bia ki sak nan bin nyik bi toonbiit toona na.

ref: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
text: Ki malaka nba pukin bannloob na mun kpaar linba be u seŋ ni na ki mɔɔn mɔkir nba ki bi yir Yufretes na ni, ki li nyun na fɔɔr ki tur sɔnjouk, ki kpanbara nba be yendopo na gar poot.

ref: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
text: Ki n la narinbiit banntaa ki bi naan nan pɔnta na ki nyii waakperbiiuk na, nan bonkobuk na, nan faak siara sɔkinii na mɔi ni.

ref: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
text: Bi tee narinbiit nba tuun bakitnauŋ toonae ki lin tingbouŋ na ni kpanbara kur boor, ki tikiib a bin took tɔb na, ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu wunba mɔk paŋ kur na dagbeŋŋir daar maŋ.

ref: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
text: Tɔn, Yisa yet a “Nnara, n baat kaman nan nanyukɔɔ nba tuu baar biaŋinba nae. Parpeenn be daanɔ nba ki gɔɔnt ŋaan nyɔi na paak, ki lia u tiat ki ki somm yanni, ki niib kan la u fei.”

ref: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
text: Ki narinbiit na tikir kpanbara na ki saan namm siaminba ki Israel teeb yi Amagedon na.

ref: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
text: Ki malaka nba pukin bannlore na mun kpaar linba be u seŋ ni na ki mɔɔn wan ni; ki kunkɔsaakar nyii naangbouŋ nba be Yenjiantu diiuk ni na ki yet a “Li tuma.”

ref: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
text: Ki dogbeŋŋir na bɔkit taar munntaa, ki nibooru kur digbana baa, kimaan Yennu tiar dogbeŋŋir Babilɔnn niib na biit po, ki u wutogbeŋŋir nba do bi paak biaŋinba na, ki u dat bi tuba bonbiir.

ref: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
text: Digbana nba ki nyun lintir na, ki li kura mirint, ki jɔɔt kura mun bia mirint.

ref: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
text: Li yoo ki malakanba bannlore nba dia senii ŋannlore na yenɔ baar n boor ni ki betin a “Baat, a man wanna poochonchonbiiuk nba kar kpenii nyun paak na buut bunu nba tan sii tee biaŋinba;

ref: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
text: a ki tingbouŋ na kpanbara chomm nanɔ, ki tingbouŋ na niib nyuu u bonchonchonn toonn maŋ ki yib.”

ref: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
text: Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na sik n paak ki malaka na jiin ki saan nanin muuk ni, ki n la poo ki u jak muuk ni bonkobmɔŋ paak, ki sanbiit nba sukii Yennu sann na sɔb lint muuk ni bonkobuk maŋ gbanant kur po, ki bonkobuk maŋ mɔk yura ŋannlore nan yina piik.

ref: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
text: Ki poo na lia tamɔnt, nan sarima tiat, nan tana nba nyirii bonchiann, ki dia sarima seŋ nba ki u bonchonchonn toonn jakint nan linba nubir Yennu boor na gbee leŋa;

ref: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
text: ki sann nba ki sɔɔ ki mi li paak sɔb u yugbiar paak a “Dogbeŋŋir Babilɔnn, poochonchona nan tingbouŋ na bona nba nubir Yennu boor na naa.”

ref: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
text: Ki n la ki poo na nyuu popeendamm sɔn nan Yisaweira nba ki bi kpiib na sɔn ki yib. Ma lau nna maŋ ki li bakitin bonchiann.

ref: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
text: Ki malaka na boin a bee ki li yaar ŋaan litimi, a u sa wannin poo na nan bonkobuk nba ki u jak ki li mɔk yura ŋannlore nan yina piik na paak nba tee biaŋinba:

ref: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
text: a bonkobuk nba ki n la na daan fo sianyoo, ŋaan ji ki fo, ŋaan sa nyi bootsunsuŋ nba kaa terik na ni ki do, ki sa saan siaminba ki u sa biir na boor; a ki niib nba be tingbouŋ na ni ki bi sana ki sɔb manfopaann teeb gbouŋ ni yoo nba ki dunia daa din ki piin na, sa got bonkobuk na lin bakitib, kimaan bee paake? bonkobuk na din fo, ŋaan ji ki fo, ŋaan bia tan sii fo;

ref: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
text: a ki linba na loon yan nan bannue; a yura ŋannlore na tee jɔɔt ŋannlore nba ki poo na kar li paak nee;

ref: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
text: a ki bia tee kpanbara bannlore, ki baŋŋmu kpo, ki wunba pukin bannloob na tee kpanbar mɔtana, ki yenɔ daa ki dii naan; a ŋaan u-i taŋŋi dii naan, u yoo na kan yukiri;

ref: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
text: a bonkobuk nba daan fo ki ji tan ki fo na pukin kpanbara bannlore na ni ki tee banniin, a ki mun tan sa saan siaminba ki u sa biir na boor;

ref: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
text: a ki yina piik nba ki n la na tee kpanbara piik, ŋaan bi daa ki dii naan na, ŋaan ŋamm nan bonkobuk na tan sa lakin di naan ki sa yukir yoo waaminna,

ref: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
text: ŋaan bi piik na tan sa taan mɔb ki jii bi paŋ nan bi yikoo na ki tur bonkobuk na,

ref: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
text: ki bi tan sa kɔn nan Pebik na, ki Pebik na sa nyannib, kimaan u tee yomdamm kur Yomdaanɔ, nan kpanbara kur Kpanbar, ki u poorpoweiteeb mun tee binba ki u yiinib ki gannib, ki bi bia tee yadadamm.

ref: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
text: Ŋanne ki malaka na bia betin a nyun nba ki n la ki poochonchonn kar li paak na tee nigbouŋ nba tee nibooru booru nan maaru nan mabooru kur niibe;

ref: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
text: a yina piik nba ki n la na, ŋamm nan bonkobuk na sii nân poochonchonn na, ki fat linba kur ki u mɔk na ki tennɔ gbananbin, ki bia pat u gbanant ki ŋman, ŋaan joou muu,

ref: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
text: kimaan Yennue te ki bi loon bin tun u loomm ki taan mɔyomm ki jii bi naangbouŋ ki tur bonkobuk na mɔkmɔk nan ŋɔɔ Yennu maan maŋ nba tan sa gbee;

ref: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
text: a ki poo nba ki n la na mun tee dogbeŋŋir nba tee tingbouŋ na kpanbara yudaanɔ.

ref: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
text: Li poorpo ki n bia la malakasɔɔ ki u mɔk yikoo bonchiann ki nyii Yendɔuŋ ni, ki u yentu ŋamm yent ki lint tingbouŋ na lokir kur po,

ref: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
text: ki u yeen nan kunkɔsaakar a “Dogbeŋŋir Babilɔnn na baawa, li set baawa, ki ji kpant siaminba ki narinbiit kur nan nɔɔnbiit nba mɔk sinsint na boore.

ref: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
text: Kimaan booru kur poŋ nyuu u nianbiiuk daan nawa, ki tingbouŋ na kpanbara chomm nanɔ, ki tingbouŋ na kpinkpenta kpant mɔkita u nianbiiuk nba mantik yabit na.”

ref: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
text: Ŋanne ki n bia gbat kunkɔsiar ki li nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki yeen a “N niib, nyint i mɔŋ man u boor, ki daa lakin ki tuun toonbiit nanɔ, ki u biit maŋ tubdatu daa taabitiini,

ref: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
text: kimaan u biit na doo bonchiann ki baar sanpagbanta, ki Yennu tian u biit maŋ po.

ref: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
text: Tɔn, bin mun teen ki jiinɔ kaman nan wa tuu tee biaŋinba na, ki pa jiinɔ u biit pann taar munnlee, ki dat u tuba bonbiir, kimaan u te ki niib tun toonbiit bonchiann;

ref: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
text: a ki te wun la muksuk nan parbiir a ki lin jaŋ nan wa din tuu be u yama ni ki dont u mɔŋ biaŋinba na, ki yeen u par ni a ŋɔɔ poobate kar na, a ki li ki tee a u tee pakɔɔki, a li paak a u kan mi la parbiiri.

ref: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
text: Li paak, fara sa baar u paak dasiar, ki yiaru nan parbiir nan kon sa soorɔ, ki muu bia sa diu; kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nba bu nanɔ na tee yabint daanɔe.”

ref: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
text: Tingbouŋ na kpanbara nba chomm nanɔ ki di u mant na, bi-i taŋŋi la u muu dinu munyuut na, bi tan sa bui ki fabin u paak.

ref: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
text: Bi tan sa tin jaŋmaanii u fara na paak, ki see banfɔkira ki ji yeen a “Aba, dogbeŋŋir na, aba, Babilɔnn, doo nba mɔk paŋ na, a tubdatu baar yiama.”

ref: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
text: Ki tingbouŋ na kpinkpenta tan sa bui ki fabin u paak, kimaan sɔɔ nba tan sii kaa ki sa daa bi tiat na paak,

ref: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
text: sɔɔ ji kan mi ki daa bi tiat nba tee sarima, nan nabint, nan tanchincheena, nan taŋant, nan gbaknii, nan agogo chinchena, nan daat nba mantik fan, nan linba ki bi jii loobuk nyana ki naan, nan bona nba ki bi dia daat nba yaa daauk paak ki naan na, nan kut, nan bont nba kur fan,

ref: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
text: nan kakaduro, nan gasinbunn, nan tulaarii, nan mir, nan frankinsen, nan daan, nan kpan, nan yon, nan dii, nan nei, nan pei, nan taanii nan li torit, nan daaba nan nisaarii manfoa.

ref: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
text: Ki kpinkpenta na yeenɔ a “Tiat nba ki a bo loon fii mɔk na, nan a mɔkint na, nan taŋant na gara, a ji kan mi ki la.”

ref: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
text: Ki kpinkpenta nba kpentir ki tan la gor doo maŋ ni na tan sii see banfɔkira ki mɔ ŋaan fabin, kimaan bi tiin jaŋmaanii u fara na paak.

ref: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
text: Bi sa bui ki fabin ki yeen a “Aba, dogbeŋŋir nba naan nan poo nba gbab chinchenŋana nan agogo chinchena nan sarima nan laanii nba daauk paar na,

ref: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
text: yoo waaminna sinsuuk ni ki a mɔkint na kur biir.” Ki binba tee ŋarint yudamm, nan binba kɔɔ ki saa bi saanu na, nan ŋarinkanna, nan binba kur tuun mɔkgbeŋŋa ni, ki bi ji see banfɔkir,

ref: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
text: ŋaan yikin, ba la ki muu diu ki li munyuut do sanpaapo na, ki bia yeen a “Ti ki mi la dogbeŋŋir na booru;”

ref: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
text: ki bi finii tant ki pukin bi mɔŋ paak, ki bui ŋaan fabin ki yeen a, “Aba, dogbeŋŋir nba ki niib mɔk ŋarint ki lin mɔkgbeŋŋa ni ki kpentir ki laat gor leŋ na, ki li taan baa ki biir yoo waaminna nɔɔk ni.”

ref: The heavens should be happy with God's people and apostles and prophets. God has punished her for them.”
text: Yendɔuŋ ni teeb, nan Yennu niib, nan u sɔkiniinba, nan Yisa toomiinba, i mɔk man parpeenn, kimaan Yennu bu dogbeŋŋir maŋ buut ki biirɔ ki turi.

ref: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
text: Ki malakapaarɔ tɔkir tann nba gbeŋ kaman nan naagbeŋŋir na, ki lur mɔkgbeŋŋir ni, ŋaan yet a “Bi tan sa wokit dogbeŋŋir Babilɔnn na ki lu-u nnee wun biir, ki sɔɔ ji kan mi ki lau,

ref: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
text: ki ji kan mi ki gbat ki bafaara faa biat, amii ki yan-yinna yiin yanii, amii ki yerpebira peeb yerii, amii ki naatunpebira peeb naatuna doo maŋ ni; ki toontunnɔ bia kan mi ki be leŋi, ki sɔɔ bia kan mi ki gbat naar fuut leŋi;

ref: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
text: a fitir yentu ji kan yent doo maŋ ni; a jakunt nan pookɔɔnt ji sii kaa leŋ biaki. Doo maŋ ni kpinkpentae tuu tee tingbouŋ na paarib, ki doo maŋ niib bia lin dia nyɔku ki kpann tingbouŋ na niib kur;

ref: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
text: a Yennu sɔkiniinba, nan u niib, nan binba ki niib kpiib tingbouŋ na kur ni na, a bi sɔn be Babilɔnn maŋ paak, li paak ki Yennu dat bi tuba.”

ref: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
text: Linba na poorpo, ki n gbat fuut Yendɔuŋ ni ki li tee niburchiauŋ paku, ki bi yeen a “Pakin Yennu man; a tinnu nan dontir nan paŋ tee ŋɔɔ Yennu yare;

ref: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
text: a ki u buut ŋan ki bia tee mɔmɔmm; a ki u bu poochonchonbiiuk nba biir tingbouŋ na nan u chonchonsin na buut; a ki dat u tubir, wa kpii ŋɔɔ Yennu toontunna na paak.”

ref: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
text: Ki niburchiauŋ na bia yikin ki yeen a “Pakin Yennu man. Doo nba joo na munyuut do sanpaapo nan yoo nba ki sɔɔ kan mi ki lar jik.”

ref: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
text: Tɔn, ki kunkɔsiar nyii naangbouŋ na boor ki yet a “Pakin Yennu man, yimm kur nba tee u daaba, yimm nba tiinɔ, nijaana nan nitarii.”

ref: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
text: Ŋanne ki n bia gbat kunkɔsiar ki li fu kaman nan niburchiauŋ nba tuu piak ki fu na, ki bia fu kaman nan sutuk ni nyun na, ki bia fu kaman nan satiangbeŋŋa fuut na ki yet a “Pakin Yennu man, kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nba mɔk paŋ kur na dii naan kura.

ref: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
text: Ŋaant man ki tii kpamm kii mɔk parpeenn kii dont u sann ki turɔ nibaakir, kimaan yoo na baara ki Pebik na loon wun kɔɔn u poo, ki sarkpeŋ poŋ teen siira.

ref: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
text: Ki Yennu tur sarkpeŋ na chinchenŋann nba nyirii ki yeen bonchiann, a wun gbab.”

ref: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
text: Ki malaka na yetin a n sɔb a “Binba ki bi yiimm ki bi baar Pebik maŋ pookɔɔnt jaamm ni na, parpeenn tee bi yare.” Ki malaka maŋ bia yetin ki pukin a “Maan na tee Yennu maame, ki tee mɔmɔmm.”

ref: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
text: Li yoo ki n gbaan u tɔɔnn a n jiantɔ. Ki u yetin a n daa tee nna kaa, a u tee min nan n naa waas nba dia Yisa barŋanii na toontunlɔɔe, a n jiant Yennue. Yennu sɔkiniinba nba sɔkint ki yeen na, bi piak Yisa poe. Tanpeeŋ jakirɔ labaar

ref: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
text: Li poorpo ki n la ki Yendɔuŋ loot, ki tanpeeŋ see leŋ, ki wunba jakɔ na sana tee Yadadaanɔ nan Barmɔndaanɔ; ki u bu buut nan li barmɔnii ki bia tookit tɔbii nan barmɔnii;

ref: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
text: ki u ninbina naan nan mupeenu na, ki u yir naanfokirii ki li yaba u yur paak, ki sann sɔb u paak, ki sɔɔ ki mi sann maŋ paak see u kuukɔɔ.

ref: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
text: Ki liatir nba ki u lia na far nan sɔn; ki sann nba ki bi yiu na tee a “Yennu Mɔmaan.”

ref: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
text: Ki Yendɔuŋ ni tɔb niib jak tanpeenii, ki lia chinchenŋana nba peen ki ki mɔk jakinti, ki waau.

ref: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
text: Ki jukbanjiak nba di bonchiann tant ki nyii u mɔb ni, ki u sa jiir ki kɔn nyann nibooru kur, ki yentib nan paŋ, ki sa ŋmakirib siaminba ki bi ŋmakii tilɔɔna na, ki li tee Yennu wunba mɔk paŋ kur wutochiauŋ na.

ref: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
text: Ki sansiar sɔb u liatir nan u pann paak a “Kpanbara kur Kpanbar, yomdamm kur Yomdaanɔ.”

ref: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
text: Ŋanne ki n la malaka ki u see yennu ni ki tian ki yi nɔɔnii nba yuuk sanpaapo a bin tikir ki baar Yennu jaangbeŋŋir na ni,

ref: You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
text: ki tan di kpanbara, nan lanjimanba yudamm, nan paŋ damm, nan taanii nan tanjakira gbanant, nan niib kur, binba tee daaba nan binba tee yentinmɔŋ damm, nan binba tee nijaana nan binba tee nitarii kur gbanant.

ref: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
text: Ki n la muuk ni bonkobuk na, nan tingbouŋ na kpanbara nan bi tɔb niib, ki bi tikir a bin kɔn nan wunba jak taamm maŋ paak na nan u mun yaa tɔb niib.

ref: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
text: Ki bi soor muuk ni bonkobuk na, nan u faak sɔkinii nba daan tuu tee bakitnauŋ nyina u tɔɔnn po ki kpann binba mɔk muuk ni bonkobuk maŋ dɔk na, nan binba daan jiantir u naauŋ na; bi soor muuk ni bonkobuk maŋ nan u faak sɔkinii maŋ na ki bi fo, ki lub bootuk nba gbee nan muu ki sarwuutaa di nann na ni.

ref: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
text: Ki wunba jak taamm ki jukbanjiak tant nyii u mɔb ni na, ki u jii jukbanjiak maŋ ki kpii binba biar na, ki nɔɔnii dii bi gbanant ki tan gboo.

ref: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
text: Ki n la naangbant, ki Yennu tur binba kar li paak na yaak a bin bu buut. Ki n bia la binba ki bi din pot bi yura na sei; ba din mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii nan Yennu mɔmaan paak ki bi kpiib; bi din ki jiant bonkobuk na, amii li naauŋ na, amii bi bia din ki gaar bonkobuk maŋ dɔk bi yugbia paak amii bi nii ni. Bi fiir kuun nie ki lakin dii naan nan Masia bintusir. Nee tee sinsinn kpeemm fiiru.

ref: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
text: Binba dɔŋ sinsinn kuun fiiru na ni, bi tee popeendamm, ki parpeenn bia tee bi yare; kuun nba pukin munnlee na ki mɔk bi yikoo; bi sii tee Yennu nan Masia manteebe, ki sa lakin di naan nan Yisa bintusir. Binba nyann Sintaanii labaar

ref: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
text: ki u sa nyi ki kpann nibooru nba be tingbouŋ na kur na ni, binba ki bi yib Gɔk nan Magɔk na. Sintaanii sa tikir bi kura ki bin took tɔb; bi kann tee kaman nan mɔkgbeŋŋir tanbiinii kann nee.

ref: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
text: Ki bi nyii ki tia ki lint lokir kur po, ki tant ki saa bia lint Yennu niib nba be sian na, nan doo nba ki Yennu mantik loon na. Ki muu nyii Yendɔuŋ ni ki tan diib.

ref: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
text: Ki malakanba jii Sintaanii nba kpann tingbouŋ na niib na, ki lu-u bootsunsuŋ nba gbee nan muu ki sarwuutaa di nann na ni, siaminba ki bi dɔŋ lu bonkobuk na nan u faak sɔkinii na. Leŋe ki bi sa di fara yennu nan nyiɔk mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Buut nba joont labaar

ref: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
text: Ki n bia la naan gboungbeŋŋir nba peen bonchiann, ki sɔɔ kar li paak; ki tingbouŋ nan sanpaapo mir bot u tɔɔnn, ki sɔɔ ji kan mi ki lab.

ref: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
text: Ki n la binba din kpo na, nijaana nan nitarii na, ki bi see naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn po; ki sɔɔ pɔbitir gbant, ki bia pɔbit gbanleer nba ki bi sɔb manfopaann damm sana ki teen leŋ na. Ki Yennu bu binba kpo na buut, ki li waa nan ba tuu tee biaŋinba bi toona ni na, ki li sɔb gbant maŋ ni.

ref: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
text: Ki mɔkgbeŋŋa nyinn kpeemm nba be nyun ni, ki kuun nan kpeentiŋ mun nyinn binba kpo ki be bi ni na. Ki Yennu bu bi buut kaman nan ba tun biaŋinba na.

ref: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
text: Ki bi jii kuun nan kpeentiŋ ki lub bootuk nba ki muu gbee leŋ na ni. Kuun nba pukin munnlee nee tee muu bootuk maŋ.

ref: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
text: Ki wunba sann din ki sɔb manfopaann damm gbouŋ na ni, bi sa lu-u bootuk maŋ nba ki muu gbee leŋ nie.

ref: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
text: Ki n bia la sanpaapo paann nan tingbounpaann. Ki sanpaapokper na, nan tingbounkper na mirint; ki mɔkgbeŋŋa ji kaa.

ref: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
text: Ki n gbat ki kunkɔsaakar nyii naangbouŋ na boor ki yeen a “Nnara, Yennu ŋaak ji be nifosaarii boore, a ki ŋɔɔ Yennu sii be namm, ki bi sii tee u niib, a ki ŋɔɔ Yennu mɔŋ sii be bi boor ki sii tee bi Yennu,

ref: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
text: ki sa fiar bi ninnyuut kur bi ninbina ni; ki kuun nan parbiir nan mɔnii nan yiaru ji sii kaa, kimaan bonkpera na kur ji gara.”

ref: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
text: Ki wunba kar naangbouŋ maŋ paak na yet a “Nnara, n tee bont kur bonpaann nee.” Ŋanne ki u bia betin a n sɔbir, a kimaan mɔbona na tee mɔniie ki tee linba ki yada be li ni.

ref: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
text: Ki u bia yetin a “Li poŋ tuma. N tee Alfa nan Omegae; li paak tee piinu nan joontu. Binba ki nyunyukiru mɔkib na, n sa pib manfoor nyun nba bunii ki nyi nyunkpana ni na ki bin nyu yann;

ref: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: a ki wunba nyann nyannu na, u sa gaar linba na n boor, ki n sii tee u Yennu, ki u mun sii tee n bik.

ref: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
text: Ŋaan jaŋmaanii damm, nan yadakanpɔɔna damm, nan biitdamm, nan nikpiirit, nan joonbaarit, nan jabaabura, nan patjiantdamm, nan fai damm kur, bi binbeboor tee bootuk nba gbee nan muu ki sarwuutaa di nann nee; ŋanne tee kuun munnlee na.” Jerusalem paann labaar

ref: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
text: Li yoo na ki malakanba bannlore nba daan dia senii ŋannlore ki li gbee nan farbiit ŋannlore nba waa poor na yenɔ betin a n baar ki wun wannin Pebik na pooyookir na.

ref: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
text: Ŋanne ki Yennu Seyeeŋ sik n paak ki malaka na jiin ki doo nanin jɔyirinn paak, ki wannin Jerusalem nba tee Yennu doo na, ki li nyii Yendɔuŋ paak Yennu boor ki siik,

ref: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
text: ki nyirii nan Yennu yentsaakar na, ki bia nyirii kaman nan tanŋann nba ki bi yir jaspa na, ki nyɔkit kaman nan mianu na.

ref: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Ki doo maŋ mɔk joongbeŋ-foot; ki joona maŋ mɔk gana piik nan ŋannlee, ki malakanba piik nan bannlee guu gana maŋ, ki Israel booru piik nan bannlee na sana sɔb gana piik nan ŋannlee maŋ paak.

ref: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
text: Ki gana ŋanntataa be joona ŋanna na paak, ki ŋanntaa be yendopo, ki ŋanntaa be diitu po, ki ŋanntaa be gaŋ po, ki ŋanntaa be yenbaapo.

ref: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
text: Doo maŋ fiakir see tana piik nan ŋannlee paake, ki Pebik na toomiinba piik nan bannlee na sana sɔb tana piik nan ŋannlee na paak.

ref: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
text: Ki malaka nba piak ki teenin na dia sarima patu nba ki bi dia ki bikii na, a wun bikin doo maŋ nan li tammɔi nan li joona na.

ref: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
text: Ki li mɔk joona ŋanna, ki joona maŋ fɔkint kur tee yomm. Ki malaka na bikin doo na nan u patu, ki li yarimu fɔkint tee maar tusir nan kobiinnlee, ki li yarimu fɔkint nan li sanpaapo fɔkint nan li yarimu kur tee yomm.

ref: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Ki joona na maa nan tana nba ki bi yi jaspa nee, ŋaan ki doo na mɔŋ tee sarima ki nyirii kaman nan mianu na;

ref: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
text: N ki la Yenjiantu diiuk doo maŋ ni; kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu wunba mɔk paŋ kur, nan Pebik na, ŋamme be leŋ ki niib jiantirib.

ref: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
text: Ki doo maŋ ki loon ŋmaarik amii yennu yentu leŋi, kimaan Yennu yentsaakar nee tee li yentu, ki Pebik na mun tee li fitir.

ref: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
text: Ki tingbouŋ na nibooru sii be li yentu ni ki somm; ki tingbouŋ na kpanbara sa kɔɔ nan bi mɔkint ki bir leŋ.

ref: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
text: Doo maŋ tammɔi ki loo, kimaan nyiɔk kaa leŋ.

ref: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
text: Ki tingbouŋ na nibooru sa kɔɔ doo na ni nan nibaakir nan paku ki bir leŋ.

ref: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
text: Ki linba tee jakint, nan wunba tuun fei toonn, nan fai damm, kan kɔɔ doo maŋ ni, see binba sana sɔb Pebik na manfopaann teeb gbouŋ ni na kuukɔɔe sa kɔɔ doo maŋ ni.

ref: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
text: Li yoo ki malaka na wannin manfoor nyun mɔkir na, ki li puuk nyi Yennu nan Pebik na naangbouŋ na ni, ki nyirii kaman nan mianu na,

ref: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
text: ki puuk ki gaar doo na sɔnjosinsuuk ni, ki tiik nba ki bi yir manfoor tiik na see mɔk-kpinii ŋannlee na kur po ki kook saawa; ki tiik maŋ lon lɔɔna booru piik nan ŋannlee, ki binn nɔɔk ni ki li loon taar piik nan ŋannlee, ŋmaarik kur ni li loone; ki li faat na tee nisaarii tebuk ki tur nibooru kur.

ref: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
